FPMT MASTERS PROGRAM — ORNAMENT  
 
The Explanation  Ornament of the Essence   
along with  
(i) the Root Text of the Treatise of Quintessential 
Instructions of the Perfection of Wisdom:  
Ornament for Clear Realization  
and 
(ii) the  Commentary Clear Meaning  
 
  
Root Text  [Ornament for Clear Realization]  
by the Protector Venerable Maitreya  
 
Commentary [ Clear Meaning]  
by Acarya Haribhadra 
 
Explanation  [Ornament of the Essence]   
by Gyel -tsab Dar -ma Rin -chen  
 
  
Translated by Toh Sze Gee  
first edition 2008 —2009 
revised January 2015

i Note by the Translator  
 
• The Tibetan texts mainly used here  are the 1996 editions published by Drepung Los eling 
Library Society.  
• Throughout the translation, “ROOT TEXT”  refers to Ornament for Clear Realization 
(Abhisamayalamkara )1 by Maitreya ; “COMMENTARY” refers to Clear Meaning  
(Abhisamayalamkara -nama prajnaparamitopadesa -sastra -vivrti , Sphutartha)  by Haribhadra;  
and “EXPLANATION” refers to Ornament of the Essence  (Rnam bshad snying po’i rgyan)  
by Gyel -tsab. 
• The translations  are done by referring the trans lations of Ornament for Clear Realization and 
Clear Meaning by Venerable Geshe Jampa Gyatso, Venerable Thubten Jampa, and Venerable 
Thubten Tsultrim (George Churinoff), as well as materials from the FPMT Masters Program 
1998 -2004 taught by Venerable Geshe Jampa Gyatso.  
• The bulk of Maitreya’s  Ornament for Clear Realization  is contained in eight chapters that 
elucidat e in detail  the eight categories  and the seventy topics  characterizing them . In addition 
to the eight chapters, the root text, as presented in the Tibetan tradition, begins with eighteen 
introductory stanzas,  which  can be likened to an index for understanding the  content of eight 
chapters, and ends with two concluding stanzas describing the sets of six and three 
summarized meaning s. The root text  has been numbered to attempt to reflect this structure. 
For example, “0.17” refers to stanza 17 of the introductory section, “3.1” stanza 1 of chapter 
3, “8.40” stanza 40 of chapter 8, and “9.2” stanza 2 of the concluding section. 
• To aid in following this structure of the eight categories and the seventy 
(10+11+9+11+8+13+4+4) topics, before the start of the translation of the three texts, the 
definienda, definitions, and boundaries of the eight categories and seventy topics a ccordin g to 
Je-tsun Cho -kyi Gyel -tsen’s Seventy Topics  (Don bdun cu) have been  provide d in tables.  
• To highlight the presence of the root text, its words are set in bold when they appear in the 
Commentary or Explana tion that follows immediately.  
• And to highlight the presence of the Commentary , its words are set in bold when they appear 
in the Explanation that follows immediately.  
• Words singled out for elucidation in the explanation are underlined for easy identification.  
• Where a definition is given  in Gyel -tsab’s text, its definiendum is underlined in order to 
highlight it.  
• Numbering and words in square brackets and as well as the footnotes in the English translation 
have been added for the sake of clarification and do not appear in the original Tibetan 
composition s.  
• The word “until” (Tibetan: bar du ) appears very frequently in the translation and is to be 
understood as meaning “up to and including.” For example, “the second ground until the fifth 
ground” refers to  the second  ground , the third ground , the fourth  ground, and also the fifth 
ground. An example of an expression in which the upper endpoint is not included is: “from 
the Mahayana path of accumulation until just prior to the end of the continuum.”  
• “The end of the continuum” (Tibetan: rgyun mtha’ ), referr ing to the end of the continuum of 
the ten grounds, is the occasion just before one attains buddhahood.  
• A “knower” (Tibetan: shes pa) is that which knows, and  throughout this text it refers to the 
direct agent that knows, that is, a consciousness, rather than a person. It is used in a similar way to words like “computer,” “blender,” “lighter,” “eraser,” “dryer,” “wrapper,” “rejoinder,” 
“pacemaker,” and “mixer,” which do not necessarily refer to persons.  
• The expression “sutra says …” indicates a citation from some sutra wh ose title  has not been 
explicitly identified by the text.  
• An English –Tibetan glossary can be found at the end of the translation .
                                              
1 The full title is stated and explained at the beginning of the text. See below.

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
iv CATEGORY 1: Exalted Knower of All Aspects ( 10 Topics)  
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Mahayana 
conventional 
generated mind A Mahayana special main mental knower that 
arises concomitant with an accompanying aspiration observing complete enlightenment for 
others’ welfare and is distinguished by abiding in 
a class of paths that serves as the entrance to 
the Mahayana paths.  exists from the Mahayana path 
of accumulation until the buddha 
ground  
2. Mahayana 
instruction  Mahayana speech that teaches a method for 
attaining the object of striving of the Mahayana 
generated mind.  exists from not having entered a 
path until the buddha ground  
 
3. Mahayana path 
of preparation 
(mutually inclusive 
with “Mahayana 
partial 
concordance with 
definite 
discrimination”)  A Mahayana mundane path that abides in a 
class of clear realizations that arise subsequent 
to the completion of the partial concordance with 
liberatio n and is concordant with a clear 
realization of the truth.  exists only on the Mahayana 
path of preparation  
4. Lineage abiding 
in the nature, the 
support of Mahayana 
achievings  The common locus of that which is the dharmata 
in the continuum of a bodhisattva and that which acts as the actual supporting basis of Mahayana achievings.  exists from the path of 
accumulation until the end of the 
continuum  
5. Object of 
observation of a 
Mahayana achieving  
(mutually inclusive with “object of 
knowledge”)  A basis for the elimination of superimpositions 
by a Mahayana achieving.  exists with respect to whatever 
is an established base  
6. Object of intent 
of a Mahayana 
achieving  
(mutually inclusive 
with “buddha”)  The final result that is the object of intent for 
which one engages.  exists only on the buddha 
ground.  
7. Achieving of 
armor  
(mutually inclusive 
with “a 
bodhisattva’s 
exalted knower”)  A bodhisattva’s yoga conjoined with an 
extensive attitude desiring to practice inclusively the six perfections, generosity and so forth, in 
their entirety within each of the six perfections.  exists from the Mahayana path 
of accumulation until the end of 
the continuum  
8. Achieving of 
engagement  A bodhisattva’s yoga practicing an application in 
some type of topic of Mahayana cause and 
effect by means of mainly joyous effort.  exists from the heat level of the 
Mahayana path of preparation 
until the end of the continuum  
9. Achieving of 
accumulation  A bodhisattva’s yoga that surpasses the 
middling level of supreme mundane quality of 
the Mahayana path of preparation and below by 
way of being conjoined with the two extensive 
accumulations and issue s forth great 
enlightenment, its result.  exists from the great level of the 
supreme mundane quality of the Mahayana path of preparation 
until the end of the continuum  
10. Achieving of 
definite 
emergence  A yoga of the pure grounds that undoubtedly 
issues forth an exalted knower of all aspects.  exists on the three pure grounds  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
v CATEGORY 2: Knower of Paths (11  Topics)   
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Branch  of a knower of 
paths  A special excellent quality  conjoined with 
great compassion that is included in the 
cause, entity, or result of a knower of paths.  exists from having  awaken ed 
the Mahayana lineage until the 
buddha ground  
2. Knower of paths 
knowing hearers’ 
paths  
(Mutually inclusive with 
“a Mahayana arya’s 
exalted knower  abiding 
in a class of hearers’ 
realizations”)  A Mahayana  arya’s  exalted knower that —by 
means of being conjoined with the three, the 
generat ed mind, dedication, and the wisdom 
realiz ing emptiness —abides  in a class of 
clear realizations of what is to be known in 
order to take care of  trainees who are hearer 
lineage- bearers.  exists from the Mahayana  path 
of seeing until the buddha 
ground  
3. Knower of paths 
knowing solitary 
realizers’ paths  
(Mutually incl usive with 
“a Mahayana arya’s 
exalted knower  abiding 
in a class of solitary 
realizers’ realizations”)  A Mahayana  arya’s  exalted knower that —by 
means of being conjoined with the three 
attributes —abides  in a class of clear 
realizations of what is to be known in order to 
lead trainees who are solitary realizer  
lineage- bearers.  exists from the Mahayana  path 
of seeing until the buddha 
ground  
4. Mahayana path of 
seeing  A Mahayana  clear realization of truth 
conjoined with the wisdom directly realizing 
emptiness in the continuum of the person 
who possesses it  in his continuum .  exists only on the Mahayana  
path of seeing 
5. Function of a 
Mahayana  path of 
meditation  A benefit  attaine d through the force of 
cultivating the Mahayana path of meditation 
that is the method for  attaining it.  exists from the second instant 
of cultivating the Mahayana 
path of meditation until the 
buddha ground  
6. Mahayana path of 
meditation of belief  A Mahayana  subsequent clear realization 
convinced that the Mothers are the source of 
the three welfares .  exists from the first ground  
until the end of the continuum  
7. Benefit of a Mahayana  
path of meditation of 
belief  An excellent quality attained through the 
force of cultivating the path of meditation of belief that is the method for attaining it.  exists from the second instant 
of cultivating the path of meditation of belief until the 
buddha ground  
8. Path of meditat ion of 
dedication  A conceptual Mahayana  subsequent clear 
realization —apprehending a sound 
generalit y and meaning generalit y as suitable 
to be mixed—that acts to transform one’s own or others’ roots of virtue into a branch of 
complete enlightenment.  exists  from the first ground  
until the end of the continuum  
9. Path of meditation of 
rejoicing  A conceptual Mahayana  subsequent clear 
realization —apprehending sound generalit y 
and meaning generalit y as suitable to be 
mixed —that cultivates joy in one’s own or 
others’ roots of virtue.  exists from the first ground  
until the end of the continuum  
10. Path of meditation of 
achieving  
(mutually inclusive with “a path of meditation of 
purity ”) An uncontaminated Mahayana  subsequent 
clear realization that establishes the effect 
that is  the final realization to be attained .  exists from the first ground 
until the tenth ground  
11. Path of meditation of 
purity  An uncontaminated Mahayana  subsequent 
clear realization that establishes the effect 
that is  the final abandonment to be attained .  exists from the first ground 
until the tenth ground  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
vi CATEGORY 3: Knower of Bases (9 Topics)   
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Knower of paths not 
abiding in cyclic existence 
through know ing 
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
knower of paths knowing 
hearer s’ paths ”) A Mahayana  arya’s exalted knower abiding 
in a class of realizations  that stops the 
extreme of cyclic existence relative to a 
conventional base.  exists from the Mahayana 
path of seeing until the 
buddha ground  
2. Knower of paths not 
abiding in peace through 
compassion  
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
Mahayana arya’s exalted 
knower that abides  in a 
class of realizations  of 
special method ”) A Mahayana  arya’s exalted knower abiding 
in a class of realizations  that stops th e 
extreme of peace relative to a conventional 
base.  exists from the Mahayana 
path of seeing until the 
buddha ground  
3. Knower of bases that is 
distant from the resultant 
Mother  
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
knower of bases of the 
discordant class ”) A knowe r of bases devoid of great 
compassion and bound by the conception 
of true existence.  exists from the Hinayana  
path of seeing until the 
Hinayana path of no-
more -learning  
4. Knower of bases that is 
near to the resultant 
Mother  
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
knower of bases of the 
antidotal  class ”) An exalted knower in the continuum of a 
Mahayana arya abiding in a class of 
Hinayana realization s that is conjoined with 
great compassion and the wisdom directly 
realizing emptiness.  exists from the Mahayana  
path of seeing until the 
buddha ground  
5. Knower of bases of the 
discordant class  
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
knower of bases bound by 
the conception of true 
existence in the continuum 
of a Hinayana  arya” ) A knower of bases devoid of the two, 
special me thod and wisdom.  exists from the Hinayana  
path of seeing until the 
Hinayana path of no-
more -learning  
6. Knower of bases of the 
antidotal class  
(Mutually inclusive with “a 
knower of bases in the 
continuum of a Mahayana 
arya”) An exalted knower in the continuum of a 
Mahayana arya abiding in a class of  
Hinayana realization s that is  conjoined with 
the two,  special method and wisdom.  exists from the Mahayana  
path of seeing until the 
buddha ground  
7. Application  of a 
bodhisattva  indicated in 
the context of the knower 
of bases  A bodhisattva’s yoga that cultivates an 
antidote to either (i) erroneous adherence 
to the entity and attributes of the mode of subsistence of conventional bases or (ii) 
erroneous adherence to the entity and 
attributes of the mode of subsistence of the 
ultimate.  exists from the Mahayana 
path of accumulation until 
the end of the continuum  
8. Equality of an application  
of a bodhisattva indicated 
in the context of the 
knower of bases  A bodhis attva’s yoga conjoined with the 
wisdom that stops adherence to the 
divisions of bases, definitions, definienda, 
objects, and object -possessors as truly 
existent.  exists from Mahayana  
path of accumulation until 
the end of the continuum  
9. Path of seeing explicitly 
indicated in this context  A Mahayana  clear realization of the truth 
that directly realizes freedom from 
elaboration that is explicitly indicated in this 
context.  exists only on the 
Mahayana path of seeing  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
vii CATEGORY 4:  Application in Complete  Aspects (11 Topics)  
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Knower -aspect  of 
the antidotal class 
explicitly indicated 
in this context  An exalted knower that is able to 
overcome its discordant class.  [exists on all paths]  
2. Principal 
application  
explicitly indicated 
in this context  A bodhisattva’s yoga conjoined with 
the wisdom that is a union of calm 
abiding and special insight observing 
emptiness that is explicitly indicated in 
this context.  exists from the heat level of the 
Mahayana path of preparation until the 
end of the continuum  
3. Excellent quality  of 
a Mahayana  
applica tion A benefit  attained through the force  of 
cultivating a Mahayana application that 
is the method for  attaining it.  exist from the Mahayana  path of 
accumulation until the buddha ground  
4. Fault  of an 
application  A mara’s action  that interferes with the 
production, abidance, or completion of 
an application.  exists from not having entered a  path 
until the seventh ground  
5. Yoga of the path 
perfection of 
wisdom  A bodhisattva’s yoga conjoined with a 
union of calm abiding and special insight that is the method for  attaining 
a resul tant perfection of wisdom that is  
its object of attainment.  exists from the Mahayana  path of 
accumulation until the end of the 
continuum  
6. Mahayana partial 
concordance with 
liberation explicitly indicated in this 
context  A clear realization of the doctrine in the 
continuum of a bodhisattva skilled in accomplishing an exalted knower of all 
aspects in his continuum.  exists only on the Mahayana  path of 
accumulation  
7. Mahayana partial 
concordance with definite 
discrimination 
explicitly indicated 
in this context  A Mahayana  clear realization of the 
meaning explicitly indicated in this context mainly qualified by the quality 
of method.  exists only on the Mahayana  path of 
preparation  
8. Bodhisattva sangha 
who has attained a 
sign of 
irreversibility  A bodhisattva who has attained any of 
the forty -four signs, such as having 
turned away from manifest  adher ence  
to forms and so forth as truly existent.  exists from the heat level of the path of 
preparation until the end of the 
continuum  
9. Application of the 
equality of cyclic existence and 
peace  A yoga of the pure grounds  that has 
exhaustively  destroyed the opportunity 
for a manifest conception of true 
existence to be produced on the 
occasions of subsequent attainment by 
means of  being conjoined with the 
wisdom directly realizing  cyclic 
existence and peace to be empty of 
true existence.  exists on the three pure  grounds  
10
. Application of pure 
land A yoga of the pure grounds in which a 
root of virtue, such as a prayer to 
accomplish the special land where one 
will attain enlightenment, has become 
a powerful potency  in one’s continuum .  exists on the three pure gr ounds  
11
. Application of 
skillful means  A yoga of the pure grounds that 
spontaneously accomplishes exalted 
activities by means of having pacified 
coarse exertion.  exists on the three pure grounds  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
viii CATEGORY 5: Peak Application (8 Topics)   
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Peak 
application of 
the heat A first Mahayana  partial concordan ce with definite 
discrimination  that has attained any of the twelve  
signs of having attained  peak application.  exists only on the heat level of 
the Mahayana path of 
preparation  
2. Peak 
application of 
the peak A second Mahayana  partial concordan ce with 
definite discrimination that has attained the sixteen 
thorough increases of merit, such as that exceeding 
that of making offerings to buddhas equal in number  
to the sentient beings of the billionfold world 
systems .  exists only on the peak level of 
the Mahayana path of 
preparation  
3. Peak 
application of 
the 
forbearance  A third Mahayana  partial concordance with definite 
discrimination that has attained stabili ty in (i) the 
wisdom that is a complete similitude of the three exalted knowers and (ii) indivisibility  from others ’ 
welfare.  exists only on the forbearance 
level of the Mahayana path of 
preparation  
4. Peak 
application of 
the supreme 
mundane 
quality  A fourth partial concordance with definite 
discrimination that has attained an always abiding 
mind with respect to infinite meditative stabilizations, 
on account of  having ripened the potency to 
generate the Mahayana path of seeing that is its 
result . exists only on the supreme 
mundane quality level of the Mahayana path of preparation  
5. Peak 
application of 
the path of 
seeing  A Mahayana  clear realization of the truth abiding in 
a class of direct antidote to the seeds of the 
conceptions that are objects to be abandoned by 
the path of seeing.  exists only on the Mahayana  
path of seeing 
6. Peak 
application of the path of 
meditation  A Mahayana  subsequent clear realization abiding in 
a class of direct antidote to the seeds of the 
conceptions that are objects to be abandoned by 
the path of meditation.  exists only on the [ Mahayana ] 
path of meditation  
7. Uninterrupted 
peak application 
(Mutually 
inclusive with “an exalted 
wisdom of the 
end of the 
continuum” ) A bodhisattva’s final yoga —conjoined with a wisdom 
highly transformed from the Mahayana path of 
accumulation that collectively cultivates the aspects 
of the three exalted knowers —that acts to directly 
produce an exalted knower of all as pects  that is its 
result.  [exists at the end o f the 
continuum]  
8. Perverse 
achieving that 
is to be 
eliminated 
indicated here  That which is included in a seed of a conception or 
a manifest conception apprehending the two truths 
as not suitable to be subsumed as a single entity 
indicated here.  exists from not having entered  a 
path until the seventh ground  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence          Definienda, Definitions, Boundaries and Topics of the Eight Categories  
ix CATEGORY 6: Serial Application (13 Topics)  
 Definiendum2 Boundaries  
1. Serial application of the p erfection of generosity   
exists from the Mahayana path of 
accumulation until just prior to the 
end of the continuum  2. Serial application of the p erfection of ethics

CATEGORY 7: Instantaneous Application (4 Topics)  
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Non-ripened 
instantaneous 
application  A bodhisattva’s yoga that serves as a direct antidote to the knowledge 
obscurations by means of being conjoined with a wisdom such that, if 
one uncontaminated non-ripened quality is actualized, those concordant 
in type with it are also actualized in the shortest instant in which an 
action can be completed.   
exists only at the 
end of the 
continuum  
2. Ripened 
instantaneous application  A bodhisattva’s yoga that serves as a direct antidote to the knowledge 
obscurations by means of being conjoined with a wisdom such that, if 
one uncontaminated ripened quality is actualized, those concordant in 
type with it are also actualized in the shortest instant in which an action 
can be completed.  
3. Characterless 
instantaneous 
application  A bodhisattva’s yoga that serves as a direct antidote to the knowledge 
obscurations by means of being conjoined with the wisdom directly 
realizing emptiness.  
4. Non-dual 
instantaneous 
application  A bodhisattva’s yoga that serves as a direct antidote to the knowledge 
obscurations by means of being conjoined with the wisdom directly 
realizing the emptiness of apprehendeds and apprehenders being 
different substances.  
CATEGORY 8: Resultant Truth Body (4 Topics)  
 Definiendum  Definition  Boundaries  
1. Nature body  A final sphere possessing the two purities.  
[The two purities are:  
1. purity with respect to inherent stains  
2. purity with respect to adventitious stains]  exists only on the 
buddha ground  
2. Complete 
enjoyment body  A final form body possessing the five certainties.  
[The five certainties are:  
1. certainty of abode: he resides only in Akanistha (Not Low)  
2. certainty of body: he is adorned with the clear and complete signs and exemplificati ons 
3. certainty of retinue: he is surrounded only by bodhisattva aryas  
4. certainty of Dharma: he teaches only the Mahayana Dharma 
5. certainty of time: he remains as long as cyclic existence is not empty]  
3. Emanation body  A final form body distinguished by not possessing the five certainties.  
4. Exalted wisdom 
truth body  An exalted knower that is final insight with respect to modes and 
varieties.  
 
Exalted activity of an  
exalted wisdom truth 
body  A very positive excell ent quality arisen from an exalted wisdom truth 
body (that acted as the empowering condition) that is its cause.  exists from not 
having entered a 
path until the 
buddha ground

1 The Explanation:  Ornament of the Essence  
Respectfully I always pay homage at the feet of the excellent venerables posses sing great 
exalted  compassion.  
Ornament of the Essence 
An Explanation of Clear Meaning , a Commentary on  
The Treatise of Quintessential Instructions of the Perfection of  
Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization 
 
The path traversed by tens of millions supreme scholars, 
The supreme system, the way of Perfection [g one-beyond]  
Is clarified by your kindness;  
I bow down to the venerable guru.  
 
Having seen exactly the essential points of the path of Muni’s complete teachings  
And gained mastery over them,  
You instructed migrating beings with loving- kindness ; 
I bow with  my crown at the feet of the gurus. 
 You have seen all the meaning of the profound and extensive, the modes and varieties, 
without exception  
And come under the influence of exalted compassion;  
I pay homage to the lineage gurus,  
The excellent Regent [Maitreya], Manjugosha, and so forth. 
 
By connect ing the boundaries that are definite in sequence and  
Taking the essence of the meeting points of the root text and explanation,  
Not seeing the realization of even a mere portion of this system ,  
I generated a little interest in uncovering this text. 
 
Those who do not possess the eyes of stainless reasoning and  
Are separated from the leaders for the blind, the excellent spiritual guides  
Desire to enter the city of the omniscient victors  
But are resigned to poorly enter wrongful paths. 
 
Although this profound path is very difficult to realize,  
Since I have familiarized with it in all lifetimes, and  
For the sake of repaying kindness out of gratitude to the deities and gurus,  
I shall explain it. Thus listen respectfully with single -pointedness!  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
2 Here, all of us have obtained leisures and endowments that are very difficult to find, and are 
greatly meaningful when found. At this time, effort for the sake of gain, honor, and the like 
of simply this life —without setting up well the basis that is the temporal and final aims that 
are strongly desired by beings from the next life and beyond, which surpasses the system of the elderly mundane beings —is like winnowed chaff devoid of grain. Having understood 
that doing so does not transcend the behavior of animals, we should make effort in taking 
the essence of our leisures and endowments well. 
 Due to the deeds of turning the wheels of Dharma in three stag es by the Buddha 
Bhagavan [Shakyamuni] who acts solely to benefit all migrating beings, the one called Acarya Arya Asanga subsequently engaged in the systems which have been determined 
with extensive collections of scripture and reasoning by the beings for whom the path (that 
the aryas of the three times have gone and will subsequently go on) along with its result 
have become valid, and understood their meanings. He came to understand that there is no 
supreme method to please the victors other than proper p ractice by means of the three types 
of precious trainings. Also, he acted exceptionally with regard  to many previous victors and 
generated vast roots of virtue. Even now he is being taken care of by the protector buddhas 
and bodhisattvas and his strength o f intelligence surpasses that of others. He accomplished 
well prayers to take responsibility for the teachings of the Subduer Buddha Bhagavan and it was prophesized that he would comment on the complete intention of the definitive and 
interpretable sutras sets. He saw that trainees of future generations will view the Mahayana 
sutra sets as though they are an ocean and will become like travelers who have lost their 
way; if even the relation between earlier and later words is difficult for them to understand, what need is there to mention about them understanding the profound supreme meanings? 
Being aware that the points of the tathagatas’ secrets are hidden phenomena even for 
himself, he thought, “If I were to please the excellent Regent [Maitreya] who posses ses eyes 
for which the secrets of the speech of the Muni are not hidden phenomena, I shall achieve my goal.” By engaging in practice, he beheld [Maitreya] directly. In dependence on the 
Bhagavan [Maitreya]’s miraculous powers, he went to Tushita [Pure Land ], and having 
heard the Dharma, fully realized the intention of the scriptures. 
 For the sake of taking care of future generations of trainees in Jambudvipa [Continent], 
he bestowed also the gifts of excellent Dharma, the five great treatises: the two type s of 
Differentiation
3, the two types of Ornament4, and the Great Vehicle Treatise on the Sublime 
Continuum (Mahayanottaratantrasastra ). 
 With regard  to that, in general the Bhagavan’s scriptures taught two modes of 
differentiating between the interpretable  meaning and the definitive meaning, these being 
taught in (i) the Teachings of Akshayamati Sutra (Akshayamatinirdeshasutra ), the King of 
Meditative Stabilizations Sutra ( Samadhirajasutra), and so forth and (ii) the Sutra 
Unraveling the Thought ( Samdhinirm ocanasutra).  
 According to the first, (a) the teaching that all phenomena are empty of being established 
by way of their own character is indicated as definitive meaning, while (b) teaching the person, aggregates, and the like by means of various words and  syllables is indicated as 
interpretable meaning. The latter sutra set teach es that imputational natures are not 
                                             
 
3  These are (1) Differentiation  of Phenomena and the Final Nature of Phenomena  
(Dharmadharmatavibhaga) and (2) Differentiation  of the Middle and the Extremes (Madhyantavibhaga).  
4  These are (1) Ornament for the Great Vehicle Sutras (Mahayanasutralamkara) a nd (2) Ornament for 
Clear Realization (Abhisamayalamkara ). 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
3 established by way of their own character, whereas other- powered natures and thoroughly 
established natures are established by way of their own  character; (i) the teaching that all are 
not established by way of their own character, and (ii) the teaching that all are  established 
by way of their own character are taught to be interpretable meaning , and the  teaching 
through clearly differentiating the boundaries between what is and what is not established 
by way of their own character is taught to be definitive meaning. Following the former 
sutras, the Protector Nagarjuna effortlessly established that the second sutra as interpretable 
meaning throug h opening well the chariot tracks differentiating between the interpretable 
meaning and the definitive meaning.  
 With respect to that, Acarya Arya Asanga followed the two types of Differentiation  and 
the Ornament for the Great Vehicle Sutras (Mahayanasutra lamkara ), and relied upon the 
mode of differentiating between the interpretable and the definitive in the latter sutra set. Mainly he opened well the chariot tracks of Cognition -Only ( Vijñaptimatra ) and also 
commented upon the intention of the Hinayana  sutra sets. The Differentiation  of Phenomena 
and the Final Nature of Phenomena (Dharmadharmatavibhaga ) taught (i) phenomena —
truly existent other- powered natures where apprehendeds and their apprehenders
5 appear as 
dual, the basis establishing cyclic existence —and (ii) dharmata (the final nature of 
phenomena) —the support for attaining liberation through observing which and 
familiarization with it, the emptiness of apprehendeds and their apprehenders as being different substances. 
 Differentiation  of the Middle and the Extremes  (Madhyantavibhaga), having taken truly 
existent dualistic appearances as the basis, made differentiation of the three characters, and 
presented the basis, path and results of the common and uncommon vehicles. 
 The Ornament for the Great Ve hicle Sutras ( Mahayanasutralamkara), without refuting 
truly existent dualistic appearances, beginning from the method for awakening the Mahayana lineage, extensively determined the method for the bodhisattva himself 
proceeding specifically to higher and hi gher realizations and the method for taking care of 
other trainees. These three texts teach a mere ultimate relative to the mentalities of the trainees; they do not clarify the ultimate that is the emptiness of inherent existence of all 
phenomena.  
 Followi ng these three texts, Acarya [Asanga], in accordance with the modes in which the 
interpretable and the definitive have been differentiated in the Sutra Unraveling the Thought 
(Samdhinirmocanasutra), opened well the chariot tracks of Cognition -Only in 
[comp ositions] such as the five Treatises on the Grounds
6 and the two Summaries7. He also 
taught in his Hearers’ Grounds (Sravakabhumi ) the teaching regarding a section of the 
Hinayana  basket on the manner in which rhinoceros -like solitary realizers and Mahayan a 
lineage -bearers collect the accumulations for a hundred eons and three countless eons 
[respectively] on the path of accumulation and then pro gress  through  the heat level of the 
path of preparation and above in a single session.  
                                              
5 “Apprehendeds ” refers to objects while “apprehenders” refer to object -possessors, usually the minds 
apprehending the objects.  
6 (1) Grounds of Yogic Practice  (Yogacarabhumi ), (2)  Compendium of As certainments 
(Nirnayasamgraha), (3) Compendium of Bases  (Vastusamgraha), (4)  Compendium of Enumerations 
(Paryayasamgraha), and (5)  Compendium of Explanations (Vivaranasamgraha ). 
7 (1) Summary of Manifest Knowledge  (Abhidharmasamuccaya), and (2) Summary of the Great Vehicle 
(Mahayanasamgraha).  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
4  The Great Vehicle Treatis e on the Sublime Continuum ( Mahayanottaratantrasastra ) 
explained that (i) those who have ripened their continua  through familiarization with the 
modes of Cognition -Only should be taught later and those Mahayana lineage -bearers of 
supreme faculties should be taught right from the very start, and that (ii) the extensive, 
medium, and brief [Perfection of Wisdom ] Sutra s and the Matrix of the One -Gone- Thus8 
Sutra (Aryatathagatagarbhanama- mahayansutra ) clearly teach there is no difference in the 
object of observation of the path that must definitely be realized —the emptiness of true 
existence of all phenomena, the ultimat e truth free from all elaborations —even for those 
who attain the enlightenment of hearers and the enlightenment of the solitary realizers. Acarya Asanga clarified its intention exactly as it is in its commentary, whereby you should 
not think of him as divi ded from the Protector Nagarjuna, the opener of the chariot tracks 
who relied upon the first mode for differentiating between the interpretable and the definitive, because he is a follower of [Nagarjuna] himself. The assertion that Sublime 
Continuum was co mmentated upon in the manner of Cognition -Only by the Acarya is 
incorrect, because  it seriously contradicts (a) the proof that there is one final vehicle and (b) 
the extensive determination of subtle emptiness  in that commentary . 
 In this treatise Ornament for Clear Realization ( Abhisamayalamkara), the final view of 
emptiness is taught again and again. However, the main object to be determined is the stages of clear realization of the three lineage -bearers; it clearly reveals the way to practice 
by means of the three: definite entity, definite number and definite sequence —the hidden 
meaning of the three, the extensive, medium, and brief [Perfection of Wisdom ] Sutras  
abiding within.   Regarding this explanation of the Ornament for Clear Realization  along wit h its 
commentary, there are three meanings:  
 
1. Meaning of the title  
2. Meaning of the text  
3. Meaning of the conclusion

1A2. Explanation of the title  
“Perfection of wisdom ” is the root sutras, path , and result. It will be explained along with its 
divisions in the section on summarized meanings  [outline 2B2B below]. “ Quintessential 
instructions ” causes the meaning of the sutras to be easily understood. Since it possesses the 
two excellent qualities of cure and protection, it is a “ treatise .” Since it approaches (mngon 
du phyogs pa) or realizes directly (mngon sum du rtogs pa ), it is “ clear realization ” (mngon 
par rtogs pa ). Just as an excellent form —a natural ornament —decorated with bracelets and 
the like —beautifying ornaments —when seen appearing in a clarifying mirror and the like 
generates joy in beings; likewise, the excellent corpus of the Mothers  [the Perfection of 
Wisdom  Sutras]—a natural ornament —adorned with the seventy topics —the beautifying 
ornament —appears in this clarifying treatise, generating joy in the wise, therefore it is an 
“ornament .” Since it proclaims the name, it says “ called ” [which is rendered as a colon in 
the title]. Due to commentating on the meaning of the root [treatise ], it is a “ commentary .”

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
6 EXPLANATION  
“Bundle ” is the name given to many parts of a text summed together.  
1B2 Translator’s homage 
ROOT TEXT  
I pay homage to all buddhas and bodhisattvas. 
 
EXPLANATION  
“I pay homage to all buddhas and bodhisattvas ” is stated by the lotsāwa (translator) for 
the sake of being able to accomplish many temporal and final desired aims.  
2 Meaning of the text  
2A. Embarking on the explanation  
2B. Setting forth the explanation itself  
2C. The way of completing the explanation  
2A. Embarking on the explanation  
2A1. Expression of wo rship and promise to explain  
2A2. Proving that [Haribhadra] is suitable to compose the commentary  
2A3. Discarding arrogance , and the reason for generating enthusiasm  
2A1. Expression of worship and promise to explain  
COMMENTARY  
To the perfection of wisdom  Respectfully I pay homage  
In order to thoroughly reveal its versified Ornament   
As serving as an ornament of all.  
 
EXPLANATION   
Respectfully I pay homage . To what? To the textual, path, and resultant perfection of 
wisdoms . By mentioning the textual, the  other two will also be established. However, it 
should be taken to be all three here. For the sake of what purpose? In order to thoroughly 
reveal  by means of this commentary the versified ornament of the Mothers  as serving as 
an ornament of all  three Moth ers. 
 There is a purpose for making a promise to explain at the beginning of composing the 
commentary; it is because it is for the sake of completing the composition. There is a purpose for making an  expression of worship; it is because it is for the sake of being able to 
accomplish many temporal and final aims. Since the four, purpose and so forth, are indicated by implication, the subject —this commentary:  
• has a subject matter, because it reveals the meaning of the sutras and root treatise;  
• has a purpose, because it is for the sake of understanding, in dependence on it, that the root treatise serves as an ornament for the three Mothers;  
• has an essential purpose, because in dependence on that purpose, one will eventually attain omniscience; a nd 
• has a relationship, because the three properties are such that each latter one is related to the former one.
9 
                                              
9  Note that this is a statement expressed in typical syllogistic format, con sisting of subject, predicate, and 
sign.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
7  
To summarize the essence, this stanza teaches that the excellent door for entering the 
teachings is going for refuge. When the Buddha Jewel in cluded within the resultant 
perfection of wisdom and the Dharma Jewel included within the path perfection of wisdom are indicated as objects to which we go for refuge, we will also understand the arya Sangha 
who practice them properly as a refuge. Hence, from the causes —which are the two: (1) 
conviction that the Three Jewels have the ability to protect us, and (2) fear of the suffering of cyclic existence —generate a mind of going for refuge that is not merely words. 
 Understand that the composers of treatis es set forth an expression of worship at the 
beginning for the sake of making known that going for refuge should come first at the 
beginning of any Dharma activity that is performed, such as hearing, contemplation, and 
meditation. The ways of putting into practice the meaning of the treatise will be explained 
from time to time. Since I wish to compile them and compose a separate text , look there on 
how to practice.
10 
2A2. Proving that [Haribhadra] is suitable to compose the commentary  
2A2A. Proving that [Ha ribhadra] possesses the outer condition of quintessential instructions of the spiritual 
guide  
2A2B. Proving that [Haribhadra] possesses the inner condition of wisdom realizing all the meanings of the 
treatise  
2A2C. Thus [Haribhadra] is suitable to compose the commentary  
2A2A. Proving that [Haribhadra] possesses the outer condition of quintessential instructions of the spiritual 
guide  
There are four subsections.  
 
OPINION: Is there or is there not a commentary done by a previous acarya ? If there is, 
[Haribha dra’s commentary] would be a repetition. If there is not, he does not possess the 
quintessential instructions. Hence he is not suitable to compose it. 
RESPONSE: Quintessential instructions that are dependent on the commentaries of 
previous acarya s do exist. Since his is a commentary [composed] as an ornament of all 
Mothers , there is not the fault of repetition.  
2A2A- 1. The way Acarya Asanga composed an exposition  
COMMENTARY  
By having generated deep affection out of affection for migrating being s,  
Arya Asanga, himself a refuge,  
Having listened to the Invincible Protector,  
Composed an exposition of the great treatises. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Composed an exposition of what? Of the great treatises —the Sutras —since  they satisfy 
the etymology .11 By who? It w as by Arya Asanga . By his excellent qualities: (i) the perfect 
                                              
10 In the collected works of Gyeltsab, we find a text entitled “Opening the Door of the Supreme Vehicle: How

’byed ces bya ba bzhugs so) and another entitled “ How to Practice the Commentaries on the Eight Clear 
Realizations as well as the Seventy Topics” ( mngon par rtogs pa brgyad don bdun cu dang bcas pa’i grel pa

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
8 attitude, compassion, having generated deep , that is, exceptional, affection out of 
affection for the  migrating beings  of the five continua, and (ii) perfect application, himself 
being a refuge  for others from suffering, he listened to the Invincible Protector .  
 It is suitable for Asanga to compose an exposition on the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras , 
because, having fully listened to the meaning of the words of the Mother Sutras  from the 
Venerable  [Maitreya] , he understood them. Those who propound that the two brothers 
[Asanga and Vasubandhu] are same in view simply should contemplate in detail the 
commentary [by Asanga] on  the Great Vehicle Treatise on the Sublime Continuum 
(Mahayanottaratantrasastra ). 
2A2A- 2. The way Vasubandhu  unraveled  
COMMENTARY  
Vasubandhu, a root and branch of the purpose of migrating beings,  By taking his belief as foremost,  
Thoroughly unraveled through correctly depending  
On objects of knowledge as being  the inner.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Thoroughly unraveled. What? It is as above. By who? It was by Vasubandhu , since he 
served as a root and branch of high status and definite goodness, the purpose of 
migrating beings . Where? In the text commentary on the Twenty  Thousand Stanza 
Perfection of Wisdom Sutra12. How? In the manner of the Mind -Only (Cittamatra), through 
correctly  depending on objects of knowledge, such as forms, as being the nature of the 
inner . 
 
QUALM: Did he explain it correctly?  
RESPONSE: [He explain ed it] by  taking his belief as foremost due to adherence to the 
emptiness of duality as truly existent. However, the essential meaning is to abide in the 
middle free from extremes, in variance with that. 
2A2A- 3. The way Arya [Vimukti] revealed  
COMMENTARY  
Also , he who is called Vimukti13,  
Who is included among the aryas,  
Having seen that taken as not being taken,  
Thoroughly revealed with a mind abiding in the middle.  
EXPLANATION   
Thoroughly revealed. What? The Mother Sutras  and the Ornament. Understand  it below. 
By who? He who is called Vimukti, who is included among the aryas, since he had 
attained the path of seeing. “ Also ” refers to the fact that the previous two [Asanga and 
Vasubandhu] were not exhaustive. Where? In a  commentary  on the Twenty Thousand

9 Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra.14 How? Abiding in the essential meaning, which is the

 The reason for his comment ating in this wa y is his having seen  the essential meaning

2A2A- 4. The way Honorable Vimuktisena expounded  
COMMENTARY  
After that, Vimuktisena,  
Who abided on the ground of faith,  
Not finding all the treatises,  
Expounded in accordance with himself.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Expounded. By who? Vimuktisena, who abided on the ground of faith, the path of 
accumulation. The time was after  that composed by Arya [Vimukti]. Where? In a  
commentary  on the Twenty Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra .15 How? Not 
finding  complete teachings on the stages of the eight clear realizations by means of all the  
extensive, medium, and brief treatises , he did it through correlating it only with the Twenty 
Thousand Stanza Perfection of W isdom Sutra  in accordance with  whatever he himself  
found.  
2A2B. Proving that [Haribhadra] possesses the inner condition of wisdom realizing all the meanings of the 
treatise   
COMMENTARY 
In that way, scholars illuminated  
All of these treatises  
Not found by just some;  
These which I found in that way  are amazing.  
 
EXPLANATION  
“In the way explained above, the four scholars illuminated  some of the meanings of the 
words. All of these extensive, medium, and brief treatises  (where the meaning of the 
intention has not been  found exactly by just some , such as Vasubandhu, who was 
renowned as being like the second Buddha ,) completely reveal the final essential meaning 
and the stages of clear realization. These which I found in that way commenta ted on  by 
the Ornament are amazing. ” This statement indicates that he is fortunate and possesses 
special wisdom. Understand that this also abandons the fault of repeating the previous 
commentaries.

10 2A2C. Thus [Haribhadra] is suitable to compose the commentary   
COMMENTARY  
Very d ifficult to find by those like them,  
What this profound path is like,  
Found through the power of the Buddha,  
Is suitable to be analyzed by scholars.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Very difficult to find , since it is not found even by those like  the scholars explained abo ve, 
what this profound path—the stages of the eight clear realizations and the meaning of emptiness —is like, found through the power of the blessing of the Buddha, is suitable to 
be analyzed by other scholars  equal to me, due to possessing special fortune, for the sake 
of repaying kindness out of gratitude to the Buddha .  
 By way of the modes of explanation of the four acarya s, [Haribhadra] demonstrates that 
he possesses the quintessential instructions of uninterrupted excellence, beginning from the perfect ly complete Buddha. Furthermore, they were not found completely in dependence on 
a single commentary; rather, some parts were found by depending on other commentaries 
and from the strength of exalted compassion, where requests to the buddhas have been made  
for a long time. For the sake of making known that progression along the path in stages through having found all the stages of the Mahayana path is contingent upon pleasing the 
virtuous spiritual guide, the root of the path, Acarya [Haribhadra] set forth [the above 
stanzas]. Thereby, trainees of future generations should also make effort in properly relying 
upon the spiritual guide.   The four lines “In that way, …” [under outline 2A2B] indicate that [Haribhadra] 
understood [the Ornament] as the ornament o f all three Mothers in dependence on the 
blessings of the virtuous spiritual guide and, due to possessing the special wisdom realizing 
all the meanings of the words, Acarya [Haribhadra] himself generated great joy. The last 
four lines [under outline 2A2C] indicate the promise of being suitable to compose the 
commentary due to fulfilling, without exception, all the outer and inner conditions for 
composing the commentary. Hence, this Acarya is suitable to compose the commentary, 
“because of having accumulated  without exception all the outer and inner conditions for 
composing it.”  
 Therefore, if those who wish to listen to and explain the Dharma are not able to tame 
their minds by going for refuge to the Three Jewels and they do not rely upon their virtuous 
spiritual guides in a non -erroneous manner as explained above, then it will be as if the gods 
have descended as demons, and they will not be able deposit latencies of the path. Thus we 
should be careful about this topic.  
2A3. Discarding arrogance , and the r eason for generating enthusiasm   
COMMENTARY  
Although not my object of utilization in all ways,  
Because it accords with achieving merits,  
I generated enthusiasm, As I desire to benefit myself and others. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
11 EXPLANATION  
Although , except for that gained through the strength of the blessings of the virtuous 
spiritual guide, the meaning of the Perfection of Wisdom  [Sutras] is not freely my—
Haribhadra’s —mind’s object of utilization  in all ways, I generated enthusiasm  in 
composing a commentary to the Ornament. For what purpose? As I desire to benefit 
myself and others  by means of depositing latencies and serving as a reminder for myself, 
and also generating realizations in others who possess the Mahayana lineage. I am able to benefit myself and others in dependence on it, because composing such a commentary is an 
excellent method that accords with achieving the merits  of generosity and the like for the 
sake of an exalted knower of all aspects.  
 Like it has been said in [Dharmakirti's] Commentary on [Dignaga's] 'Compendium of 
Valid Cognition'  (Pramanavarttikakarika ) that “A method that accords with the relationship 
is speech that acts to express the aims of beings,” such a treatise that completely reveals the 
method for high status and definite goodness is an excellent method that accomplishes those 
aims.  
 The Acarya [Haribhadra] himself composed this commentary by drawing well from the 
paths of reasoning of the stages of the Mahayana path. Hence the words above are 
composed to discard arrogance; there has been no misdeed.

2B1A. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY  
Arya Maitreya clearly shows himself to accord with the excellent conduct, whereby the 
exalted wisdom of individual knowledge having definitely held tha t “Lucidity in the object-
possessor, the perfection of wisdom, is the principal cause of attaining all goodness,” for the 
sake of others to generate lucidity in order that they will by implication engage the 
Bhagavati —the source of matchless and unfathomab le precious qualities —for the moment, 
at first, he pays homage to the Mothers, preceded by expressing the excellent qualities as they are. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Arya Maitreya clearly shows himself to accord with the excellent conduct, whereby , 
from among the many procedures in the activity of composition, for the moment, at first, 
he pays homage to the Mothers —the  purpose of his own welfare. The purpose of others’ 
welfare is for the sake of other  trainees to generate  irreversible lucidity . The purpose of 
that is in  order that they will by implication engage in it due to having generated strong 
interest, even though they may not be urged directly, saying “Engage in it!” In what do they 
generate faith and engage? In the cause, the Bhagavati , which serves as the source  of many 
unsurpassable excellent qualities, the source  that accomplishes the goals of the four 
Children of matchless and unfathomable precious qualities . How? By means of being  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
12 preceded with expressing the excellent qualities as they are , such as leading to peace 
[see stanza 1 of the root text below].  
 
QUALM: What purpose is there in causing [the trainees] to engag e in dependence on faith?  
RESPONSE: Having established with valid cognition that  “Lucidity in the object-
possessor of the perfection of wisdom is the principal cause of attaining all goodness, 
temporal and final ,” he intended also other trainees to attain enlightenment in dependence 
on faith. Which valid cognizer established it? It was realized by the exalted wisdom of 
individual  knowledge  of Arya Maitreya’s meditative equipoise and definitely held  by the 
subsequent attainment induced by that.  
 Those who  assert that the Venerable [Maitreya] does not seek his own welfare and the 
like have not made  grueling detailed effort in the M ahayana texts. In terms of the Venerable 
being someone who has gained clear complete  buddhahood, [the assertor] failed to 
differentiate between the common and uncommon occasions. In terms of him as a 
bodhisattva, if he were  not to see the need for the trut h body (dharmakaya) in regard  to fully 
completing his own welfare, then, being satisfied with solitary peace in regard  to his own 
welfare, he would not be able to  reverse the grasping to it, and without reversing that, the 
definition of the Mahayana genera ted mind16 [of enlightenment] would not be complete ; 
this is because interest in one’s own welfare, the truth body, would not be needed on the 
occasion of a tenth -grounder [based on the assertion that Maitreya is a bodhisattva abiding 
on the tenth ground wh o does not seek his own welfare]17. The division of a buddha’s 
exalted body into the two —an exalted body of one’s own welfare and an exalted body of 
others’ welfare —would also be unnecessary. 
 To summarize the essence and indicate it, the purpose of Venerab le [Maitreya] 
composing the expression of worship here is that faith precedes all, like it has been said: 
“Generate the preliminary of faith like a mother…” [in the Jewel Lamp Sutra ] and “Firmly 
stabilize the root of faith” [in Santideva ’s Compendium of Trainings  (Siksasamuccaya )]. If 
one has strong faith in the Mahayana texts as well as their path and result, then, by means of the strong aspiration induced by it, one will engage in the three —hearing, contemplation, 
and meditation —and so forth. Having see n that if one does not have faith at the beginning , 
one will not generate a genuine mind practicing the Mahayana path for the sake of the next 
life and beyond, it is intended that, “Those who wish to engage the path should at the 
beginning generate irreversible faith in (i) the Buddha Jewel, which is the final result, (ii) 
the Dharma Jewel, which is included within the three exalted knowers, and (iii) the Sangha 
Jewel, who practices it properly.”  
 With respect to the qualm, “By what measure is one considered to be skilled in the 
practice of the stages of the path of clear realization?”, it is indicated below that one needs 
to be skilled in the five objects, faith and so forth. Also, the uncommon Mahayana going for 
refuge is taught by “Faith observin g the buddhas and so forth,” [4.33a below]. I do not take 
the explanation of the meaning of the words in the passages below as principal ; rather, I 
shall explain  how one should practice in dependence on the quintessential instructions of Je 
Rinpoche [Lama Tsongkhapa], the entity of all the buddhas of the three times.

2B1B -2A. Definiteness in sequence 
It is incorrect to claim that “This is explained in accordanc e with the sequence of the sutras’ 
setting ” as it has been said in the Treatise Precious Lamp , because the expression of 
worship and the setting are correlated by neither of the two —Acarya [Haribhadra] and Arya 
[Vimuktisena]19—and an explanation of the sett ing is given by the Acarya elsewhere. 
 
OPINION: The knower of bases is praised first for the sake of making hearer lineage -
bearers enter the three vehicles successively. RESPONSE: You do not realize who the intended trainees of this treatise are, because h ere 
it is for the sake of making solely the Mahayana lineage -bearers enter by means of hearing 
and so forth after having generat ed faith in the Mothers; if all three lineage -bearers are 
present in the intended trainees of this treatise, then it must become  a treatise common to the 
vehicles. 
 
OPINION: It is for the sake of making trainees enter the three vehicles successively.  
RESPONSE: This is also not the meaning, because, although such a trainee is a possibility in general, the time has arrived for reveal ing the stages of engaging in the path for a person 
definite in the Mahayana lineage here, and there is not the slightest need for indicating the

15 way of entering through the hearers’ path at first by means of the very occasion of the

 Thus in the context of indicating the stages of the eight clear realizations in the section on

Commentary
20, it has been said that “engaging in by way of the knower of all itself…” As 
taught, [the expression of worship] is indicated in accordance with the stages for the 
intended trainees of the Ornament themselves to enter the path. Further, if your mentality 
has not been well -trained in the stages of the path of the being of middling capacity 
determined in the third chapter, then fully qualified great compassion cannot be produced, 
and in that case, a qualified Mahayana generated mind [of enlightenment] as well  as a 
knower of paths and the like will not be produced. Hence it is for the sake of making known 
that, in order to generate the stages of the path of the being of great capacity in one’s 
continuum, you must first train in the mentality of the being of mid dling capacity. There is 
plenty to be described regarding these points; I will explain them from time to time, and 
also explain how to practice the stages of the path of the Ornament separately [in another 
text].  
2B1B -2B. Definiteness in number  
Simply just  these stages of the path (that are to be practiced by a Mahayana person who 
wishes to take care of trainees who are  all three lineage -bearers) will be ideal, because they 
are neither too many nor too few. Those who wish to take care of hearer lineage -bearers and 
[also] wish to complete one’s own path should first cultivate the mentality in common with the being of middling capacity, as explained above.  
2B1B -2C. Meaning of Mothers and Children  
Since the exalted knowers existing earlier produce the aryas e xisting later and accomplish 
the goals of peace and so forth, understand that the Mothers and the Children are objects produced and producers [where, respectively, the Mothers are the producers and the 
Children the objects produced]. 
2B1B -2D. Identifying t he three exalted knowers which are the objects of praise  
As explained in the summary of the sutra question and answer section below, the knower of 
bases also bears the aspects of the sixteen [aspects of the four noble truths], impermanence 
and so forth. T hrough being illustrated  by that, the other two [the knower of paths and the 
knower of all aspects] should be understood. The explanation immediately below on how all three exalted knowers bear the aspect of not being produced is for the sake of identifyin g an 
isolate of the Mothers. A s it has been explained that “Mother is wisdom and father is 
method,” since it has been described that “The common cause which is the factor of wisdom 
is the m other, while the uncommon cause which is the factor of method is th e father,” 
having asserted the identification of an isolate of the Mothers, it indicates that the principal wisdom is that very wisdom realizing selflessness; it is not indicated that the Mothers and 
the exalted knowers are pervaded

QUALM: How is lucidity generated?  
RESPONSE : [Having definitely apprehended that]25 “The perfection of wisdom, the 
nature of the three exalted knowers of all, that having the three modes  of the p ath, is 
without doubt,”  lucidity in their entity is generated, and having definitely apprehended 
that “Their giving birth to  the four aryas, the buddhas and so forth is without doubt ,” 
lucidity in their ability is generated.  
 Since those of sharp faculty g enerate faith relying upon reason, the not seeing of damage 
itself is the reason; it is inappropriate to cite the scriptural passage of the expression of 
worship as the reason, because those of sharp faculty accept [a scriptural passage] as proof 
through the meaning of the scriptural passage  being free [from the three contradictions]  by 
way of  analysis26, but that is untenable in this context. 
 
OPINION: This indicates how those of sharp faculty generate faith; it does not demonstrate 
even slightly the reason  for generating faith. 
RESPONSE: Your assertion is incorrect. By the hearing of that stanza acting as a condition, none of the possibilities —(i) those of sharp faculty do not generate lucidity, (ii) they 
generated it in the absence of causes and conditions , or (iii) although there are causes and 
conditions, they are not indicated here —are tenable. And it is very easy to understand that it 
contradicts the meaning of this text.  
In the context of those of sharp faculty, if we were to assert that it is establi shed by valid 
cognition that, having established well by means of valid cognition that the three —bases, 
paths, and aspects —are not produced, and by familiariz ing with their meanings, there exists  
the state of an arya who directly realizes the meaning of th e object of familiarization, what 
fault would there be?

18 OPINION: In that case, there would be contradiction with the explanation that, due to
