QUALM: Wh ich  valid cogni zer establishes that the three —bases, paths, and aspects —are 
not produced?  
RESPONSE: It is first established on the occasion of the path of accumulation by means of 
a valid cogni zer that is supported on signs, such as “because of being free of an 
entityness of one or many”  and the reason of the diamond fragments. By familiarizing  
with their meanings, one will attain the knower of paths and the exalted knower of all 
aspects (or omniscience).  
 Although the two later exalted knowers realize that the three types of phenomena are 
devoid of  truly existent production, a knower of bases that is distinguished by realizing 
subtle selflessness of phenomena is not explicitly indicated in this context
27. Nevertheless, 
the explanation that “it knows that bases are not produced” should be taken to mean that it knows [they] are not produced by a s elf of persons, for the sake of identifying an isolate of 
the Mothers.  
 Some people, having generated the basis of mistakes  through these , appear to also assert 
the perva sion of  the realization of selflessness with respect to the knower of bases and the 
knower of paths. They fail to understand that the very heavy burden of contradictions will 
weigh upon them for a long time. They assert that the knower of bases explicitly indicated 
by the early line [of the stanza]
28 exists only  among Hinayanists, and , while asserting that it 
does not differ in meaning  from the explanation here , explained that to mean ‘From the sign 
“being free of one or many,” it is established that bases are devoid of  truly existent 
production ;’ and r egarding t he assert ion that there are hearer aryas who realize subtle 
selflessness of phenomena , it appears  that they take it  like seeing a bad omen.  
 Although there are many reasons that are accepted by the Middle -Wayers, just the reason 
“being free of one or many”  shall be explained. Regarding this, there are:  
 
2B1C -2A1B -1. Identifying the predicate of the negandum that is asserted by Acarya [Haribhadra]  
2B1C -2A1B -2. The reason that refutes it in terms of other bases  
2B1C -2A1B -1. Identifying predicate of the negand um that is asserted by Acarya [Haribhadra]   
For example, suppose something such as a pebble or a twig  appears as a horse or an 
elephant at the time of emanation by an illusionist. If that very appearance were to exist from the side of the pebble, the twig , and the like without being posited through the force of 
a mistaken awareness, then, since it would be an imprint deposited by previous types similar 
to them, it would also have to be seen by those who eyes were unaffected. Likewise, if the 
person and the  aggregates were established from the side of the objects’ own uncommon 
mode of subsistence without being posited through the force of appearing to an awareness, 
                                             
 
27 The knower of bases explicitly indicated in this context belongs to the continua of learner h earers and is 
pervaded by not realizing the selflessness of phenomena.  
28 This refers to the root text 0 .1a. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
19 then they would be able to withstand analysis by a reasoning that analyzes the ultimate, and 
would have to be seen by an arya’s meditative equipoise, and so forth. Therefore, that which 
is established from the side of the object’s uncommon mode of subsistence without being posited through the force of appearing to an awareness is the predicate of t he negandum (the 
property that is to be refuted). 
2B1C -2A1B -2. The reason that refutes it in terms of other bases  
2B1C -2A1B -2A. Stating the sign  
2B1C -2A1B -2B. Proving the modes  
2B1C -2A1B -2A. Stating the sign  
If it is not established as a truly existent one  or many, then it is pervaded by not being truly 
existent, as in the example of a reflection. “The three —bases, paths and aspects —are not 
established as truly existent one nor many” is a reason of non -observation of the pervader.  
2B1C -2A1B -2B. Proving the modes  
2B1C -2A1B -2B1. Proving the property of the subject  
2B1C -2A1B -2B2. Proving the pervasion  
2B1C -2A1B -2B1. Proving the property of the subject  
2B1C -2A1B -2B1A. Proving as free of being truly existent one  
2B1C -2A1B -2B1B. Proving as free of being truly existent many 
2B1C -2A1B -2B1A. Proving as free of being a  truly existent one  
The subject —it—is not established as truly existent one, because of having parts. The sign 
is established by way of direct  cognition in terms of a basis of dispute, such as a vase.  In  
terms of a basis of dispute that is not established by way of direct  cognition, it is established 
in dependence on the two: (1) a valid cognizer refuting the possibility of a partless object of 
knowledge, and (2) a valid cognizer ascertaining that having parts and not having parts are 
direct contradictions.  
 Although three valid cognizers are needed to prove its pervasion, [only the valid 
cognizer]  refuting  a common locus between the sign and the predicate of the negandum is 
difficult to realize. Therefore , to express it, if a common locus —between (i) having parts, 
and also (ii) being  established as truly existent one —were established, then, although the 
two—the parts and the whole —appear as different entities with respect to mode of 
appearance, they would have to exist as a single entity with respect to the mode of 
subsistence, because, otherwise, having parts would be impossible.  
 Although they appear as different entities with respect to the mode of appearance, they 
are established as a single entity with respect to the mode of subsistence; although the false mode of subsistence is not contradicted, the mode of subsistence that is established as true is 
contradicted. Also, the subject —the whole and the many parts —it follows that they do not 
exist differen tly, because, if they were to exist differently, that would have to be seen by an 
arya’s meditative equipoise, but they are not seen to be different by an arya’s meditative 
equipoise.  
 If you accept that, then: the subject —the whole —it follows that it is many, because it is 
established and it is not different from the many parts. If you accept that, it is damaged by 
the valid cognizer that comprehends it as one. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
20  The subject —the many parts —it follows that it is one, because it is established and not 
different from the whole. The assertion is damaged by the valid cognizer of 
conventionalities that comprehends it as many.  
 In dependence on these, it has been  refuted that the many parts and the whole are not 
different. By refuting that, the sign of the consequence is refuted. Also, by refuting a 
component, the composite is refuted, whereby it is incorrect that  a later side of the sign  has 
been refuted ; we must assert that the s ign is refuted by refuting the earlier. I t has been 
refuted that i f the parts and the whole were to exist differently, that would be seen by an 
arya’s meditative equipoise, whereby a common locus between ‘having parts’ and ‘established as truly existent on e’ is refuted. 
2B1C -2A1B -2B1B. Proving as free of being truly existent many   
The subject —the three, bases, paths, and aspects —are not established as a truly existent 
many, because truly existent one does not exist.  
 QUALM: Since it has been proven above that a truly existent one is not established, why is 
the non-existence  of a truly existent one stated as a sign here?  
RESPONSE: It is a requisite to prove the pervasion of this sign. Its sign is established in dependence on the two: (1) refuting partlessness, and (2) it be ing necessarily established as 
a partless one if it were established as a truly existent one. 
2B1C -2A1B -2B2. Proving the pervasion  
Although three —(1) a valid cognizer ascertaining a basis of characteri zation  of the sign, (2) 
a valid cognizer ascertaining that the predicate of the probandum and the predicate of the 
negandum are directly contradictory, and (3) a valid cognizer refuting a common locus 
between the sign and the predicate of the negandum —are needed, since the first two are 
easy to realize, the last is as follows. When the valid cognizer (ascertaining one and many to be mutually exclusive direct contradictions in terms of a basis, a reflection) realizes the 
infeasibility of three possibilities
 between one and many, there is an ascertainment by way 
of realizing the infeasibility of three possibilities between truly existent one and many in 
terms of the basis, a reflection. 
 
OPINION of someone who has not trained in the presentation of reasoning: The 
ascertainment that an existent is either one or many eliminates the superimposition holding that something truly existent is not pervaded by being either truly existent one or many. 
RESPONSE: In that case, the valid cognizer ascertaining that product i s pervaded by being 
impermanent must eliminate the superimposition holding that the product sound is not 
pervaded by being impermanent sound, whereby the inferential cognizer supported on that 
sign would not newly eliminate the superimposition. There is pl enty to be said, but I will 
leave it for the time being.  
2B1C -2A2. From that, generating the aspiration which seeks  
COMMENTARY   
Also through lucidity in those, also both persons who strongly desire their excellent 
qualities develop great respect in that and the scriptures based upon that for the purpose of 
holding and so forth in all ways.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
21 EXPLANATION  
There is special purpose in generating faith in the Mothers, because a lso through lucidity 
in those Mothers , also both sharp -faculty and dull -faculty trainees of the Ornament, 
persons who strongly desire their excellent qualities , to achieve the goals of the four 
aryas and so forth , (i) develop great respect in that perfection of wisdom  and the brief, 
medium, and extensive  scriptures based upon that  existin g as the  subject matter and (ii) 
generate the aspiration which seeks. For what purpose?  For the purpose of generating the 
wisdoms of the three —hearing, contemplation, and meditation —holding the words and so 
forth in all ways  regarding that which is together with the complete subject matter.  
2B1C -2A3. From that, how to attain the result after having entered  
COMMENTARY 
From that, through the stages of generating the knowers which arise from hearing and so 
forth, supreme goodnes s will be attained. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Generating great seeking is together with a special purpose, because from generating that,  
supreme goodness —unsurpassable enlightenment —will be attained  in dependence on 
that. It is not that it will be attained immediately  after having generated the seeking, because 
one must attain it  through the stages of generating the three knowers which arise from 
hearing and so forth. Through these explicitly indicating t he process of giving rise to  the 
stages of the path in the mental  continuum of Venerable [Maitreya], implicitly the reverse is 
implied.  
2B1C -2B. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
Therefore, lucidity in the Bhagavati is the principal cause of attaining all goodness.  
EXPLANATION   
The subject —faith of lucidity in the Bhagavati —is the principal cause of attaining all 
goodness, because it has already been shown to be so by means of a valid cognizer that 
ascertains cause and effect . 
2B1C -3. Dispelling arguments  
OPINION: Is the praise to the perfection of wisdom at the beginning of composing the treatise meant [as a praise] of all, or of only one portion? In the first case, the number [from 
among the eight clear realizations  that is actually praised] would have been too few, since 
the four applications and the truth body are not prai sed. In the second case, it would have 
been sufficient to praise any of the three, or the principal exalted knower of all aspects 
alone. So what is the purpose of mentioning the three together as objects of praise?  
RESPONSE: We do not assert the latter pos ition; we accept the former. Further, there is a 
special reason and purpose for explicitly mentioning the three themselves, whereby the 
number would not have been too few.  
 
COMMENTARY  
Regarding that, the three exalted knowers of all themselves include th e eight [clear 
realizations] ; those also are the perfection of wisdom, and also by them, all aims are 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
22 fulfilled in a manner that will be explained. Having kept the aforesaid in mind, [Maitreya] 
praised the perfection of wisdom by means of the three exalted  knowers of all themselves.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding that, there is a reason for the praise by means of the three exalted knowers, because (i) Venerable [Maitreya] wished to praise all eight [clear realizations] , and (ii) 
having kept the  inclusion (where the three exalted knowers of all themselves include the 
eight categories ) in mind, he  praised the perfection of wisdom by means of the excellent 
qualities of the three exalted knowers of all themselves. 
 There is a purpose for praising the Mothers by means of the three exalted knowers, 
because it will be known that “ (i) those  eight categories which are included within the three 
exalted knowers also  are the complete  perfection of wisdom, and (ii) also by the three 
exalted knowers, all desired aims of the aryas are fulfilled in a manner that will be 
explained. ”  Since the eight are subsumed in the three, those eight are asserted also to be 
objects of praise.  
 The way in which three includes eight is as follows. Explanations such as ‘the more 
extensive includes t he smaller,’ ‘nature includes the occasions,’ and ‘the three exalted 
knowers that are the includers and the three exalted knowers that are the objects included 
are different’ cannot withstand analysis by reasoning, whereby they are left aside for the 
time being.  
 The meaning is that if one were to practice the three exalted knowers completely, then 
one would be practicing all the eight [clear realizations]. Therefore, having seen that the complete three exalted knowers bear the excellent qualities that are  the desired objects, it is 
impossible not to see all the eight [clear realizations]  in that way; having wished to praise 
the three exalted knowers, it is impossible not to wish to praise the eight clear realizations. 
The purpose of indicating overtly just  the three exalted knowers is so that it is made known 
that what are asserted to achieve the goals of the three lineage -bearers are individual 
unmixed paths of the three. 
 In summary, if it is one of the eight clear realizations, it is pervaded by being an  aspect 
that is an object of cultivation of one of the exalted knowers, and, through practicing in that manner, three includes eight.  
2B1C -4. Meaning of the words  
2B1C -4A. Praise by means of individual qualities 
2B1C -4B. Having summarized, paying homage  
2B1C -4A. Praise by means of individual qualities 
The first of the three is as follows. 
2B1C -4A1. The way in which the knower of bases leads to peace  
COMMENTARY   
Fully knowing that all bases are not produced is that which makes those desiring nirvana —
the hearers, and also the solitary realizers, placed in that class, whose life activities are 
superior by merely a little, indicated by a sequence —attain the two types of nirvana with the 
characteristic of being together with the aggregates and without the agg regates. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
23 EXPLANATION  
“Is that which makes… attain.”  By who? By those desiring the result of nirvana for their 
own welfare —the hearers, and, not only them, also the solitary realizers. How are the 
solitary realizers indicated —by what words? They are impli citly indicated by being placed 
in that class  of hearers. They are further indicated  by a sequence of words  that describe 
the excellent qualities of the knower of bases. Although indicated by being placed in the 
class of hearers, it is not that they do not  differ [from hearers], because their life -activities 
are superior by merely a little, such as revealing Dharma to others without sound, having 
abandoned the conception of apprehendeds, and not relying upon the quintessential 
instructions of a master in th e last existence. By what method? By fully knowing that all 
bases are not produced by a self of persons. 
 What is the result? T he two types : (1) nirvana that is  together with the aggregates of 
suffering, the result of past karma and afflictions, even thoug h afflictions have been 
exhaustively abandoned, and (2) nirvana with  the characteristic of being without the 
aggregates. It is incorrect to assert that here nirvana without remainder is a nirvana where 
the continuity of matter and cognition has been severe d, (i ) because that is only an 
imputation by those of the hearer schools, and (ii ) because the time has arrived for the 
Acarya himself to make a presentation of the lower nirvana drawing  from the paths of 
reasoning.  
2B1C -4A2. The way in which the knower of  paths achieves the aims of the world   
COMMENTARY  
The very entity realizing that all paths are not produced is that which causes bodhisattvas —
those enacting the welfare of migrating beings as long as samsara exists —to achieve the 
aims of all migrating beings.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The method, the very entity realizing that all paths are not  truly produced, is that which 
causes the persons who are the support, bodhisattvas —those enacting the welfare of 
migrating beings as long as samsara exists —to achieve  the result , the aims of all 
migrating beings  who are the three lineage -bearers.  
 
2B1C -4A3. The way in which the exalted knower of all aspects turns the wheels of Dharma  
COMMENTARY  
Possessing that which comprehends that all aspects are not produced, the b uddhas —bearers 
of exalted bodies which are perfectly restrained, principal of the lords who bear yoga —as 
antidotes to deeds, turn all the wheels of Dharma without exception in all aspects. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Turn.  By who? By the buddhas —bearers of exalted bodie s which are perfectly 
restrained  from discordant classes of the three doors , lords who bear the yoga of the union 
of calm abiding and special insight, and principal since they have gained mastery in being 
perfect lords of Dharma compared to the hearers and  solitary realizers and have gained 
mastery in exalted words . Through what method? Through possessing that exalted knower 
of all aspects  which comprehends  that all aspects that are the causes, results and entities 
of phenomena are not produced. What is the  result?  As antidotes to the  84,000 afflicted 
deeds  of the trainees , they turn all the wheels of Dharma that are in the nature of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
24 84,000 aggregates of Dharma without exception, all aspects that are the complete aspects 
of the subject matter, the method s.   
 QUALM: With respect to that, all of however much Dharma there is —(i) scriptures that 
have been taught by the Bhagavan for the sake of severing the discordant classes in the continuum of the trainees, and (ii) clear realizations —is the wheel of Dharma , but why is it 
called “wheel?”  
RESPONSE: The precious wheel of a wheel -turning king transfers progressively to this and 
that other place and becomes victorious over the discordant classes through the force of that. Likewise, [the wheel of Dharma] also tra nsfers to the continua of the trainees and becomes 
victorious over the discordant classes, such as attachment. Hence, because it is concordant with the wheel in terms of properties, it is called “wheel.”

together for ease of understanding:

2B1C -4A3A. Explicit teaching by way of the conventions of names of the three levels of the wheels of Dharma 
occurring in the Sutra Unraveling the Thought (Samdhinirmocanasutra ) 
In the Sutra, [Paramarthasamudgata says to the Buddha]30: 
 
Initially, in the are a of Varanasi in the Deer Park called “Sage’s Propounding,” 
the Supramundane Victor thoroughly turned a wheel of doctrine for those 
engaged in the Hearer Vehicle, fantastic and marvelous which none —god or 
human —had previously turned in a similar fashion in  the world, through 
teaching the aspects of the four noble truths. Furthermore, that wheel of doctrine 
thoroughly turned by the Supramundane Victor is surpassable, affords an 
occasion [for refutation], requires interpretation, and serves as a basis for 
controversy.   
 Based on just the naturelessness of all phenomena and based on just the absence 
of production, the absence of cessation, quiescence from the start, and naturally 
passed beyond sorrow, the Supramundane Victor turned a second wheel of 
doctrine f or those engaged in the Great Vehicle, very fantastic and marvelous, 
through the aspect of speaking on emptiness. Furthermore, that wheel of doctrine turned by the Supramundane Victor is surpassable, affords an occasion [for 
refutation], requires interpret ation, and serves as a basis for controversy. 
 However, based on just the naturelessness of phenomena and based on just the 
absence of production, the absence of cessation, quiescence from the start, and

25 naturally passed beyond sorrow, the Supramundane Vic tor turned a third wheel 
of doctrine for those engaged in all vehicles, possessed of good differentiation, 
fantastic and marvelous. This  wheel of doctrine turned by the Supramundane 
Victor is unsurpassable , does not afford an occasion [for refutation], is of 
definitive meaning, and does not serve as a basis for controversy.  
 
Many differing assertions of the great charioteers have arisen regarding the meaning of this 
sutra set. Acarya Arya Asanga, for the sake of taking care of the many Mahayana lineage -
bearers, opened the chariot tracks of Cognition -Only. According to that , he  thus 
differentiated between the interpretable and the definitive of the three wheels from this sutra 
set and accepted literally the teaching on three final vehicles. Further, he explained the three —extensive, medium, and brief Perfection of Wisdom Sutras —the basis of 
characterization  of the second wheel, as being of interpretable meaning in the following 
manner. In reply to a question on the interpretable and the definitive by the Bodhisattva Paramarthasamudgata, the Teacher says:  
 
Imputational natures are  not established by way of their own character and the 
two—other -powered natures and thoroughly established natures —are established 
by way of their own character. Also, thinking of these three modes —that (i) 
imputational natures are character- non-natures, (ii) other- powered natures are 
production -non-natures and ultimate -non-natures, and (iii) thoroughly established 
natures are ultimate -non-natures —all phenomena are explained to be natureless.  
 Like it has been said, taking as the basis of thought the three  types of modes of 
naturelessness of the three natures, it has been taught that “All phenomena are natureless.” Hence the Mother Sutras are not literally acceptable. Nevertheless, although being 
interpretable meaning, they are the principal sutra sets dete rmining extensively the 
emptiness that is included within thoroughly established natures, which are unchanging objects of observation of non -conceptual wisdom. Intended trainees of those [Mother 
Sutras] realize the complete thought without needing to rely on other sutra sets 
commentating on the thought, and do not hold them to be literally acceptable. Lineage -
bearers who need to be shown through a sutra such as the Sutra Unraveling the Thought (Samdhinirmocanasutra) by clearly discriminating their thought a re Mahayana lineage -
bearers, but they are of lower faculty than the former [trainees who realize the complete 
thought without needing to rely on other sutra sets]. Although the two great charioteers of 
the Middle Way and Cognition -Only are not alike with regard  to the differences between the 
interpretable and the definitive, they are similar in asserting that [the Mother Sutras] are the basis of characterization  of the second wheel explained here.  
 Just understand the first wheel explained here. A Tibetan a sserted that “Taking that 
which is the final wheel as definitive meaning, as its bases of  characterization, there are 
these very sutra sets, the Matrix of the One -Gone -Thus Sutra 
(Aryatathagatagarbhanamamahayansutra), and so forth.” Also, someone propounded that 
“Those sutras indicate that (i) established ultimate truth are self- powered permanents, and 
(ii) imputational natures and other- powered natures are clung to and posited by a mind that 
has made mistakes, and do not have the sli ghtest existence by way of their own nature.”  In 
particular, the assertions regarding the latter being indicated by the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras to be the principal subject matter lacked even the ability to discriminate the coarse 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
26 meaning of the sutras  and the commentaries on their thought. It is taught in th is very sutra 
set that other- powered natures and thoroughly established natures are truly existent, and that 
there are three final vehicles. The teaching that all phenomena are empty of true existen ce, 
that stains are adventitious and that there is one final vehicle in the Matrix of the One -Gone -
Thus Sutra, the Lion's Roar of Shrimaladevi Sutra (Shrimaladevisimhanadasutra ), and so 
forth, have been well established by the Great Vehicle  Treatise on the  Sublime Continuum 
(Mahayanottaratantrasastra ). Hence, if one were to reflect upon whether or not their 
meanings are the same, in the commentary on  the Great Vehicle  Treatise on the Sublime 
Continuum , the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras are also pointed to as reference regarding the 
way of realizing the meaning of the mode of abidance, and from that only self- emptiness is 
indicated. Also, know that the assertion —that the teachings on ultimate true existence in the 
Matrix of the One -Gone -Thus Sutra  are comment ated upon by the Great Vehicle  Treatise on 
the Sublime Continuum —is just a  contradiction. Since they appear to be topics of extremely 
coarse wrong conceptions, I will not elaborate more beyond just this. 
 Further, it is incorrect for someone to hold the Sutra Unraveling the Thought  in its 
entirety to be the basis of characterization  of the mentioned proximate word, “ This … is 
unsurpassable” [cited above], because the other questions and answers in which individual dissimilar questions were posted to the Teac her by ten bodhisattvas who are lords of the ten 
grounds in that [ Sutra ] have no relation to the basis of characterization  of “This.” Hence the 
answer in response to the question on interpretable meaning and definitive meaning asked 
by the Bodhisattva Para marthasamudgata is just those very words spoken by the Teacher. 
Summarizing the meaning of the answer spoken by the Teacher regarding this sutra cited 
above, Paramarthasamudgata said in brief, “I have understood thus.”  
  It is clear that those who have in verted  the bas es for characterization of the  sutras , 
having retained  the conventions of interpretable and the definitive of the middle and final 
wheels, depended only upon the words, without even slightly analyzing the meaning. 
According to the analysis of the meaning comment ated upon by Acarya Arya Asanga in the 
commentary of Great Vehicle  Treatise on the Sublime Continuum , and the commentaries on 
the thought of Acarya Nagarjuna by Acarya Buddhapalita and Acarya Chandrakirti, the 
Sutra Unraveling the Thought  was asserted not to be literally acceptable and to be of 
interpretable meaning, while the second wheel explained here, the Mother Sutras , was 
accepted to be of definitive meaning. The teachings on impermanence and so forth in the 
first wheel are also in terpretable meaning, but those teaching the subtle emptiness that are 
the selflessness es of persons and phenomena are definitive meaning. It is asserted that at the 
time when the first wheel of Dharma was turned, the group of five [former ascetic 
companions who became the first disciples of the Buddha], 80,000 gods, and so forth also 
directly  realized that very subtle selflessness. In [Nagarjuna’s] Sixty Stanzas of Reasoning 
(Yuktisastikakarika ) and Precious Garland of Advice for the King  (Rajaparikatharatnavali ), 
it is also proven that the teaching on this very subtle selflessness is a Hinaya na scriptural 
basket, and that even those trainees must realize just that in order to attain the result of foe -
destroyer.  
 Some —in dependence on having heard the mere repetition of that said of the great 
beings, that Acarya Bhavaviveka and so forth, having  at first familiarized with the methods 
of realizing the meaning of emptiness based upon the ways of the Autonomists (Svatantrikas) for a long time, would comprehend subtle emptiness as soon as their continua 
ripen —propound that “It is impossible for heare r aryas to realize subtle selflessness” . They 
are like a fox competing with an elephant in strength. Therefore, those who wish good for 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
27 themselves should be careful about this topic, because leisures and endowments will quickly 
disintegrate and also the fu lfillments of this life are insignificant in essence.   
 The bodhisattva Kamalashila said [in the extensive commentary to the Seven Hundred 
Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra ] that very imputation by others that the person, 
aggregates, and so forth are trul y existent is an imputational nature; and its  absence  of even 
the slightest nature is taught in the Sutra Unraveling the Thought to be the meaning of 
imputational natures being character- non-natures. When refuting other- powered natures to 
be truly existent , as an auxiliary, thoroughly established natures are also refuted to be truly 
existent. Thus, he asserted that, for the sake of proving that the very teaching that all phenomena are not truly existent in the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras is definitive meani ng, 
the Sutra Unraveling the Thought  pointed to the texts of [the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras].  
[2B1C -4A3B. Indication by means of the three levels of leading trainees occurring in the Questions of King 
Dharanishvara (Dharanishvararajapariprchchhasutra) a lthough the conventions of names have not been 
taught explicitly]  
The Questions of King Dharanishvara ( Dharanishvararajapariprchchha- sutra ) does not 
speak of the conventions of the three wheels, but in the analogy of the way a jewel is 
cleaned by a skillfu l jeweler, it takes three levels of guiding trainees after they have been 
adopted: “(1) Having produced despondency towards cyclic existence through a disillusioning discussion of impermanence, suffering, selflessness and uncleanness at first, 
[the Tathaga ta] cause s sentient beings who bear the cause of various natures to be entered 
into the subduing dharma of the aryas. (2) And after that, make them realize the modes of 
the tathagatas through discussion of emptiness, signlessness and wishlessness. (3) And then 
cause them to be entered into the place of the tathagatas, through discussion of the wheels of 
irreversible Dharma, discussion of the completely pure three wheels.”  
QUALM: What is indicated by this?  
RESPONSE: It indicates the stages of proceeding on the path of both of those trainees : 
those who enter the three vehicles progressively and those who are definite in the Mahayana 
lineage. If it is someone definite in the Hinayana lineage, then, his continuum having been ripened with the first level, is fre ed from [cyclic] existence with the second level, and led to 
the Mahayana path by means of the third level. Also if it is  someone definite in the 
Mahayana lineage, his continuum is ripened with the first level, as before, and then he 
practices the meaning of the second level. Following that, the way of practicing by fully 
upholding that very wisdom realizing emptiness with the extensive factor of method is indicated by the third level.  
QUALM: The selflessness of the first level is a coarse selflessness, bu t that indicated by the 
second level is very subtle. It is incorrect to meditate  on that  at the second level, because the 
factor of method must precede the wisdom realizing emptiness.  
RESPONSE: There is no fault, (i) because, as taught in the commentary of Ornament of the 
Middle Way , sharp -faculty Mahayana lineage -bearers must proceed to the realization of 
emptiness prior to generating the mind [aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment, and (ii) because these levels are taught also in terms of the main train ees. According to these 
[levels], the Matrix of the One -Gone- Thus Sutra  should also be taken as a basis of 
characterization  of this third level, because that is very clearly the meaning of the passage 
that reveals the purpose at the conclusion of the first  chapter of the Great Vehicle  Treatise 
on the Sublime Continuum

QUALM: Do assertors of these meanings of whatever is disputed actually exist? Even if 
they do not, [this dispute] anticipates someone  abiding within [such] doubts. In the first case 
[where their assertors actually exist], the dispute is either about the Mother Sutras or about 
the treatise. In the first case [where the dispute is about the Mother Sutras], since emptiness 
is extensively determ ined in the sutras by having held the three exalted knowers and so forth 
as the bases of attributes, there would not be doubt wondering, “ Are only the bases taught?” 
In the second case [where the dispute is about the treatise], has [the assertor] seen the 
complete treatise , or has he merely seen the expression of worship ? If he has not seen it 
even in the slightest, then [his claims  that] t he Acarya [explained  that homage]  is paid in 
that way would be irrelevant .  
 In the first case [where the assertor has seen the complete treatise ], why does the [assertor] 
dispute, despite knowing what subject matter has been taught? In the second case [where the assertor has merely seen the expression of worship ], who is the person who generates such a 
doubt as soon as Ve nerable [Maitreya] has finished writing the mere stanza of the 
expression of worship? When seeing praise to the Mothers by means of the three exalted 
knowers, there is not the slightest certainty that the complete corpus  of the treatise must 
accord with ho w the expression of worship has turned out. If there is, then, having already 
realized the three exalted knowers have been determined  [as the subject matter], wondering, 
“What has been taken as the subject matter?”  would be a question of an unintelligent 
person. Thus it is meaningless to create such an objection and reply in this treatise. Even if 
there is no one who makes such an assertion, if it is set forth by the Acarya for the sake of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
29 eliminating possible doubts in other possibilities, since it contrad icts the Great 
Commentary,  what it is like must be described and, also, from where this objection ensued 
must be described.  
 
RESPONSE: True indeed, but saying that  “He composed a treatise that took the three 
exalted knowers as the subject matter” is an imp licit dispute regarding the promise [to 
compose the text ]can hardly be correct, because asking “What is the subject matter?” while 
ascertaining such is a childish question. For, even implicitly formulating the promise [to 
compose the text ] as “He composed a treatise in accordance with the excellent qualities of 
the objects of praise” is uncertain to be related.  
  Therefore, if there actually is a person who makes objection in this way, then, having 
ascertained by means of a correctly assuming consciousness what are determined with 
respect to  the three exalted knowers, he must at first determine their three objects of 
observation in order to determine them, since, without realizing their objects of 
observation —the three, bases, paths and aspects —there is no realization of three object -
possessors. Hence, the basis giving rise to the statement “there will be a fault that will be explained below,” as well as the doubt, is the expression of worship. Also in that case, the 
time has arrived to provide the answer to the question of those with probing minds; it is 
difficult to eliminate the arising of such doubts of those with probing minds in dependence 
on the statement of the expression of worship.  
 Hence the principal qualm —the dispute that it follows that there wo uld be no purpose in 
composing the Ornament for Clear Realization  when all the three exalted knowers are taken 
as the subject matter, since it repeats the Sutras —occurs just in making the connection of the 
[statement of] purpose -and-relationship. In order to eliminate its possibility, there is this 
very rejection of the dispute regarding taking the parts singly as the subject matter. Hence, 
this is clearly the meaning of the Acarya’s anticipation of those who abide in the previous 
position. Also, this does not contradict the Great Commentary ; it means the flinging of these 
repudiations that are indicated in the context of dispute here if an assertion like this were to 
arise. Those abiding in doubts thinking, “ Like it has be en explained here, there would be 
these faults if the parts were only indicated singly; if one were to assert in that way, that would be true, but I do not assert such,” are taken up by the Acarya.  
 Having reflected upon these objection and replies ensuing  from the expression of 
worship, he gathered them into a topic of dispute and answer regarding the expression of 
worship. It is clear that the “beings of low intelligence”
32 and so forth explained in the 
dispute and answer regarding the purpose -and-relation ship are manifestation s of not having 
realized the meaning well. For the sake of proving that there is a purpose for composing the 
Ornament for Clear Realization , having rejected the doubts of lack of purpose, it is very 
clear that he revealed the general presentation of the three exalted knowers, the subject matter of the treatise. It will also be explained that it is incorrect to take as individual the topics of (i) the expression of worship and (ii) the purpose -and-relationship in earlier and 
later dispu te and answer.

If the treatise Ornament for Clear Realization, preceded by homage and praise in that way, 
includes anything whatsoever from among the bases, antidotes, and aspects:

30  
EXPLANATION   
Therefore, if one were to investigate, when com posing the treatise Ornament for Clear 
Realization  preceded by homage and praise in that way,  whether it includes anything 
whatsoever —from among  (i) the bases  for the sake of generating the wisdom that discerns  
phenomena, (ii) antidotes  for the sake of aba ndoning the discordant classes, and  (iii) 
aspects  for the sake of knowing all objects of knowledge without exception —as the subject 
matter, [one would see that] all of these are inappropriate. 
 
COMMENTARY  
In the case of the first position, effort is meani ngless; in this Perfection of Wisdom, there is 
no occurrence of any basis not mentioned in the treatises with the characteristics.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In the case of the first position, effort to compose this treatise is meaningless , because it 
repeats the Abhidharma; for, in this Perfection of Wisdom  Ornament for Clear Realization  
like that, there is no occurrence of any basis not mentioned in the treatises with the  
characteristics  that determine the specific and general characteris tics of phenomena, the 
Abhidharma (Manifest Knowledge) treatises.  
 
COMMENTARY  
In the case of the second, also because of including merely the completely pure properties, 
by not including the thoroughly afflicted properties, that this is the antidote to wh at will not 
be understood.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In the case of the second  position, also because of including merely  the antidotes —the 
completely pure properties —as the subject matter, by not including the objects of 
abandonment —the thoroughly afflicted properties —as the subject matter, that this  
antidote is the antidote to what object of abandonment will not be understood  in 
dependence on this treatise.  
 
COMMENTARY   
In the case of the third, also because of including merely some aspects without bases, as the meaning is not realized because it does not explain even slightly, the meaning is not 
realized. Will the aforesaid not become the thought of others?   
EXPLANATION  
In the case of the third  position , are the aspects taught by being together with the bases, or 
are they taught without relating to the bases? In the first case, there will be just that fault of 
repeating the Abhidharma. In the latter case, also because of including merely some 
aspects without bases  as the subject matter, it follows that it does not e xplain even slightly 
the requisite meaning for beings seeking the object of engagement , as the meaning related 
to the bases that are aggregates characterized by impermanence, selflessness, and so forth is 
not even slightly realized  in dependence on this treatise. If you accept that, then it follows 
that composing this treatise is meaningless, because it does not explain the requisite 
meaning even slightly  to beings. Therefore, composing the treatise is without purpose . Will 
the aforesaid not become the thou ght of others  with probing minds ?

COMMENTARY  
It is not that. Why? Since all clear realizations are included within the three exalted knowers 
of all, just according to the order of the hearers and solitary realizers, the bodhisattvas , and 
the unsurpassable buddhas, because they are being included also within the three positions together.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —Ornament for Clear Realization —it is not that there is fault by means of 
including only any one of the bases, antidotes, and aspects singly as the subject matter. 
Why? Because all the three —bases, antidotes, and aspects —are determ ined to be the 
subject matter by it; they are being included as its subject matter (by way of [their]  manner 
of characterizing the three exalted knowers) also within  the three positions of the bases, 
antidotes, and aspects  together. 
 There is a reason for determining the three exalted knowers; it is because of desiring to 
determine all the aryas’ clear realizations, since all clear realizations are included within  
the knower of bases, the knower of paths, the exalted knower of all aspects, and the three 
exalted knowers of all, just according to the order of all the clear realizations of the 
hearers and solitary realizers, the bodhisattvas , and the unsurpassable buddhas.  
 
OPINION: The clear realizations of the four aryas are included as the expressed  subject 
matter, because the three exalted knowers are included as the expressed  subject matter. 
There is pervasion, because the clear realizations of the four aryas are included within the 
three exalted knowers. 
RESPONSE: What is propounded above is posited by someone who does not understand 
the classification of engagement through exclusion of the expresser.  
 
Regarding (i) the three —bases, antidotes, and aspects —and (ii) the three exalted knowers, 
in this context, in dependence on one, the other exists as an ob ject to be characterized; if we 
combine the bases of attributes and the attributes, they would be called “the instances of the exalted wisdom realizing the two selflessnesses (as well as their retinues) upon the bases, 
the four noble truths,” and if they w ere separated into individuals, they would be known as 
“the three —bases, antidotes, and aspects.”  
 
OPINION:  Regarding that, also in this context, a past Tibetan scholar, without realizing the 
occurrence of the dispute (that it follows that composing the treatise will be without purpose if all the three were included as the subject matter), through making the connection of the 
purpose -and-relationship, having thought that the dispute and answer regarding that are 
implicitly indicated here, stated the dispute  as, “It follows that there are three bodies of the 
treatise if all the three were included”  and the reply as, “Since the circumstance of a single 
person attaining enlightenment is indicated, there would not be three bodies.” Well , the 
three exalted knowe rs would be the clear realizations of only one person.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
32 RESPONSE: Although they are determined as the circumstance of one person, it has been 
explained that, “The three exalted knowers are the clear realizations of individual persons; it 
is thus.” That [pos ited above] is very incorrect, (i) because the rejection of the dispute of the 
parts singly is an ordinary one, and (ii) because —that it follows that composing the treatise 
is without purpose if all the three are included —is the principal dispute, whereby it is 
inappropriate to explain it as the implicitly indicated meaning of this commentary.  
  
Therefore, these rejections of dispute are for the sake of (i) establishing a general 
presentation of the subject matter of the t reatise and (ii) proving that there is a purpose for 
composing the Ornament for Clear Realization .

The reason why the clear realizations of the four aryas are included within the three exalted 
knowers: 
 
COMMENTARY  
Thus, from the medium Mother of the Victors : “Subhuti, the knowers of all are of hearers 
and solitary realizers; the knowers of paths are of bodhisattvas; the exalted knowers of all 
aspects are of Tat hagata Foe -destroyer Samyak Sambuddhas (the One -Gone- Thus, Foe -
Destroyer, Perfectly Complete Enlightened Ones).”  
 
EXPLANATION  
Thus, from the medium Mother of the Victors , having called out, “Subhuti,” [it is said 
that] the knowers of all , (from between (i) the support of presentation and (ii) the support 
of generation, in terms of the first ,) are clear realizations of hearers and solitary realizers; 
the knowers of the paths,  (as before, in terms of the support of presentation that 
individually discriminates the class of clear realization ,) are clear realizations  of 
bodhisattvas; the exalted knowers of all aspects , (according to both the supports of 
presentation and generation ,) are clear  realizations of Tathagata Foe -destroyer Samyak 
Sambuddhas (the one -gone -thus, foe -destroyer, perfectly complete enlightened ones). 
 OPINION: Knowers of bases exist in the continua of only hearers and solitary realizers, and 
knowers of paths in  the continua of only bodhisattvas.  
RESPONSE: This assertion is not at all the thought of the treatise because of the following. 
The Great Commentary says, “The subject matter that is explained, the meaning of sutras 
such as the Arya Eight Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom , the nature of accomplishing 
the mind of enlightenment, the instruction s, and so forth are all differentiations by way of  
divisions into eight chapters .”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
33  Furthermore, this has been taught many times also in the commentary in the cont ext of 
saying “No basis ; no going;  no production” [4.30d] and also in the commentary on the truth 
body. Thus, taking the eight principal subject matters of the eight chapters, the ten topics 
which characterize the exalted knower of all aspects, as the basi s of the path, their occasions 
divided into eight, they should be practiced by Mahayana persons. And also in this small 
commentary [Haribhadra’s Clear Meaning ], the natures of the eight clear realizations are 
taught to be objects of experience by bodhisatt vas. According to you [who expressed the 
above opinion], one would have to assert that the knower of bases was not indicated as the 
principal subject matter of the third chapter. The commentary on the truth body also says, 
“The exalted knower of all aspect s and.”33 There is contradiction with the explanation that 
the term “and” indicates the knowers of paths and the knowers of bases and so forth.  
 OPINION by someone of the past: Although both a knower of bases and a knower of paths 
are generated in a bodhisattva’s continuum, they are of different entities , and all three 
exalted knowers in a buddha’s continuum are generated as different entities. Further, an 
exalted knower of all aspects directly knows all aspects without exception, but they are not 
known by the other two exalted knowers of a buddha. Even though a knower of paths 
directly knows the general characteristics of paths, they are not known by a knower of 
bases. Hence, a knower of bases (belonging to the divisions into three exalted knowers) solely knows bases, and a knower of paths [belonging to the divisions into three exalted 
knowers] solely knows bases and paths. Therefore, three exalted knowers are only of 
different entities.  
 
OPINION by someone else regarding that: If an exalted knower of all aspects does not 
realize bases, this contradicts that it realizes the aspects based upon them . If it does realize 
bases, then the position that it is not of one entity with a knower of bases will degenerate. 
 
RESPONSE: It is difficult for the [above]  repudiation to become correct, but a buddha 
never has conceptions or mistaken non -conceptual [minds], whereby we must assert that a 
knower of bases in the mental continuum of a buddha is an unmistaken non -conceptual 
[mind]. In that case, if a basis appears , the aspect must also appear to it. An awareness that 
does not see it (even though it appears directly) does not exist in a buddha, because he has 
exhaustively abandoned all seeds of mistakes. It must be asserted that even a buddha’s eye 
consciousness kno ws all objects of knowledge, as taught by Acarya Haribhadra, whereby it 
is difficult to posit that the three exalted knowers of a buddha are of different entities.   
 
QUALM: What is the explanation that knowers of bases are clear realizations of hearers and  
solitary realizers in this context like?   RESPONSE: It boils down to merely different supports of presentation (i) because knowers 
of bases are necessary in order for hearer and solitary realizer aryas to achieve mere peace, and they are able to achieve that merely by means of the knowers of bases; (ii) because 
bodhisattvas must know the three paths in order to generate the exalted knowers of all aspects, and they are able to achieve that merely by means of those [knowers that know the 
three paths]; and (iii) since the exalted knowers of all aspects are definitely needed in order

34 to turn all the wheels of Dharma without exception. Thus, there are different presentations 
of the dissimilar imprints of the individual exalted knowers.  
2B2A -1B2A -2. Extensive e xplanation  
First of the three subsections:  
2B2A -1B2A -2A. How the clear realizations of hearers and solitary realizers are included within the knowers of 
bases  
2B2A -1B2A -2A1. Question 
COMMENTARY 
"Bhagavan, on account of what are the knowers  of all ,34 [those] of hearers and solitary 
realizers ?" 
 
EXPLANATION  
"Bhagavan, on account of what  reason and purpose are the knowers  of all , the clear 
realizations of hearers and solitary realizers ?" 
2B2A -1B2A -2A2. Reply  
COMMENTARY 
"Subhuti, all are merely these: however ma ny outer and inner phenomena there are. Hearers 
and solitary realizers also know them; they are not, however, by all paths; they also do not 
in all aspects. Because of that, [those] of the hearers and solitary realizers are said to be the 
very knowers of a ll." 
 
EXPLANATION  
Having called out, “Subhuti,” all  bases are merely these: however many outer and inner 
phenomena , the twelve sources,35 there are. Hearers and solitary realizers also  directly 
know them  distinguished by the sixteen [aspects of the four no ble truths], impermanence 
and so forth, whereby they have the knowers of bases; they are not, however, definite 
emerging by all  three paths , whereby they do not have the knowers of paths; they also do 
not directly realize all aspects, whereby they do not have the exalted knowers of all aspects. 
Because of that, the clear realizations of the hearers and solitary realizers are said to be  
included within the very knowers of all.  
 This sutra shows that hearers and solitary realizers have knowers of bases, but not 
knowers of paths and exalted knowers of all aspects. However, this does not even slightly 
indicate that the other two persons36 do not have the knowers of bases. 
2B2A -1B2A -2B. How the clear realizations of bodhisattvas are included  within the knowers of paths  
2B2A -1B2A -2B1. Question  
COMMENTARY 
"Bhagavan, on account of what are the knowers37 of the aspects of the paths [those] of 
bodhisattvas?"

Advice to those possessing pure wisdom: 
 
COMMENTARY  
"Subhuti, bodhisattvas sho uld generate all paths: (i) that which is the path of the hearers, (ii) 
that which is the path of the solitary realizers, and (iii) that which is the path of the buddhas; 
and they should know  all paths.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
"Subhuti, bodhisattvas should first generate  in their continua all the three: (i) that which 
is the path of the hearers, the exalted wisdom realizing the selflessness of persons; (ii) 
that which is the path of the solitary realizers, the exalted wisdom realizing apprehendeds 
and their appreh enders as empty of being different substances; and (iii) that which is the 
path of the buddhas,  the path realizing all phenomena as being devoid of  true existence; 
and then they should  directly know38 all paths.”  
 
COMMENTARY  
“They should also fully complet e those, and also perform the activities  of those paths.  But, 
without  having completed prayers, without  having ripened sentient beings, and without  
having purified the buddha land, they do not actualize the perfect end. Because of that, those of bodhisattv as are ‘the knowers of aspects of paths.’”  
 
EXPLANATION  
“They should also fully complete those  three paths by means of abandonment and 
realization , and also perform the activities of those three paths , the activities of ripening 
sentient beings in that wa y. Because of that, the clear realizations of bodhisattvas are 
called the very knowers of aspects of paths."  
 The subject —bodhisattva aryas —(i) possess pure wisdom, because they are skilled in 
generating in their continua the complete classes of abandonmen t and realization of the 
three paths, and (ii) with respect to the latter, possess pure method, because they are skilled 
regarding the proper and improper times for actualizing the perfect end.  
 QUALM: Should  they also perform all the activities of the three paths?  
RESPONSE: They do not actualize the perfect ends of those three paths.
 “By way of those 
three paths” [rather than “of those three paths”] having emerged, explanation that the text is 
corrupt does not seem to be taken as erroneous.

QUALM: Is it [that they do not actualize the perfect end] at all times?  
RESPONSE: In terms of the emanation body (nirmanakaya ), one makes a promise for the 
sake of trainees of a special land of the emanation body, such as the Victor Amitabha 
(Infinite Light), who promi sed, “I will not attain enlightenment until there is no one who 
does not possess the five clairvoyances in the land where I become enlightened.” And in 
terms of the complete enjoyment body (sambhogakaya), a promise is made that until one 
has completed the ability for only Mahayana aryas to occur in one’s retinue, one will not 
attain enlightenment.  
 Not having completed prayers  wishing thus [as explained in the previous paragraph] , 
not having ripened sentient beings —who are like ripened boils ready to be la nced ,39 
having ripened as many sentient beings as there are countless sentient beings (in each retinue of as many displays of exalted bodies as there are in a billion lands simultaneously) 
who are placed on the arya path —or not having purified the buddha land  by means of 
fully completing roots of virtue that are the causes accomplishing the special lands of a 
complete enjoyment body and lands of an emanation body such as Sukhavati (Blissful Land 
of Amitabha Buddha), they do not actualize the perfect end  in a manner of abiding in 
absorption in the sphere of peace unless they are roused from it, and the like.   Explaining that the criterion for having completed one’s prayers is that one is able to 
effortlessly spontaneously accomplish others’ welfare while bein g set in meditative 
equipoise on dharmata (the final nature of phenomena) becomes [like] saying, “Do not 
actualize the truth body as long as you have not attained the truth body,” whereby a topic of 
doubt does not exist. Taking the criterion for ripening s entient beings to be completing 
trainees of the bodhisattva’s path is also similar to the former and is only a probandum (object to be proven).  
 Further, someone who claims that only a pure land is seen with respect to a buddha’s 
self-appearance and an imp ure land is posited from the trainees’ other- appearance does not 
know how to posit a faultless exalted wisdom knowing varieties
40 that is included within a 
buddha’s own continuum. Saying that is only a wrong conception that has failed to understand the pres entation of reasoning that “ By reversing  what is seen by an omniscient 
mind, one is caused  to understand that it does not exist at all.” [For,] if the appearance of an 
impure land does not dawn to a buddha’s self- appearance, then an impure land would not b e 
directly seen by a buddha and it must definitely become ascertained as non -existent, since 
an implicit realization is also untenable.  
 Hence, the earlier [regarding completed prayers] is as explained above. The middle one 
[regarding ripened sentient bein gs], being able to place the numberless sentient beings (in 
the retinue of each display of as many exalted bodies displayed in a billion lands) in the state of an arya with each stanza, (i) is also contingent upon doing so in a single time, 
simultaneously, becoming effortless and spontaneous (since one is unable to do that as long 
as there is conceptual motivation), and (ii) relies upon entering the absorption in the sphere of peace, in a manner of not arising again, after having abandoned the levels of lat encies of 
ignorance and uncontaminated karma. It is implicitly indicated that, when such trainees 
have already been placed in the ripening paths (from between the two —ripening paths and 
liberating paths), then one actualizes the perfect end. The criterion of purifying the land is to

40  “Varieties” refers to conventional truths, as opposed to “the modes,” which refers to ultimate trut hs. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction

2B2A -1B2A -2B2B. Decisive analysis  
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -1. Identifying the modes of generality and exception  
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -2. Explaining the meaning of the teaching that bodhisattvas complete the abandonments 
and realizations of hearers and solitary realizers  
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -3. Having identified the p erfect end, explaining how it is actualized  
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -1. Identifying the modes of generality and exception  
As terminologies of generality and exception are terminologies that are only  overt , it is 
incorrect to apply them to what is indicated implicitl y. In terms of what is indicated 
explicitly in this context, stemming from the generality, “They should also fully complete 
those, and also perform the activities  of those paths” [from the commentary under 2B2A -
1B2A -2B2A], if one wonders whether they compl ete all activities of the paths, then there 
comes the exception, “They should  not actualize the perfect ends of those three paths” [from 
the explanation under 2B2A -1B2A -2B2A]. Since this is a small topic of doubt, I will not 
elaborate more beyond just this . 
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -2. Explaining the meaning of the teaching that bodhisattvas complete the abandonments and 
realizations of hearers and solitary realizers 
OPINION: The realizations of the hearers and solitary realizers are completed on the first ground, but  the abandonments are completed on the eighth ground. The abandonment of the 
abidance of the afflicted mentality
41 and all afflictive obscurations without exception is the 
reason for the latter [that abandonments are completed on the eighth ground].  
RESPONSE:  That is not th e assertion of the Acarya, for he made divisions of the objects of 
abandonment of the path of meditation of the three realms into the sixteen afflictions in the Great Commentary [on the Eight Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom ]. And the Great 
Commentary  says, “ By way of division of the completely purified phenomena on the paths 
of seeing and meditation, [ the paths of seeing and meditation ] are presented as the ten 
grounds, Very Joyous and so forth .” Hence, it is explained that affl ictive obscurations even 
exist on the occasion of the tenth ground.  
 SOMEONE, h aving refuted that system, propounds : Both realizations and abandonments 
are completed on the first ground. The meaning of having completed abandonment is that the afflictive ob scurations that bind one to cyclic existence are abando ned even from the 
first ground, but the dormant afflictive obscurations are not abandoned even by hearer and 
solitary realizer foe -destroyers, because they take a body of the nature of mental  
conscious ness from the cause that is the latencies of ignorance. Therefore, both realizations 
and abandonments are completed on the first ground.”  
RESPONSE: Someone who propounds that is only propounding an erroneous presentation 
of the objects of abandonment and a ntidotes, as well as the way of abandoning the objects 
of abandonment, (i) because that the hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers have not 
exhaustively abandoned the seeds of the afflictive obscurations has  not been taught  by any 
                                             
 
41  This is one of the eight collections of consciousnesses asserted by the Mind -Only School following 
Asanga.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
38 of the great chariot eers, and has been well refuted and (ii) because that is also incorrect by 
way of reasoning.  
 Thus, since the seeds of acquired afflictive obscurations would not have been 
exhaustively abandoned on the path of seeing of the hearers and solitary realizers, it would 
be inappropriate to posit learner arya persons to be the Sangha Jewel, because, according to 
you, immediately after the path of seeing has been attained, the seeds of doubt regarding the 
perfect objects of refuge would not have been exhaustively abandoned, through  the path of 
seeing. If you say that it [the reason] is not established, then it contradicts [your statement 
above] that afflictive obscurations have not been exhaustively abandoned by hearer and 
solitary realizer foe -destroyers, and [you r] stating it as the reason for the taking of a body  of 
the nature of mentality in dependence on the levels of latencies of ignorance is irrelevant, because the “levels of latencies of ignorance” that have been explained —which are present 
in the continua of hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers, as well as in bodhisattvas who 
have attained mastery —are taken to be knowledge obscurations, not afflictive obscurations, 
by the great charioteers. 
 
OPINION: We also assert that dormant afflictive obscurations  are knowledge obscurations. 
RESPONSE: This is even more greatly amazing, because knowledge obscurations are inappropriate in any way as objects of abandonment in the analysis of whether or not the 
abandonments of hearers and solitary realizers have been c ompleted on the first ground. 
Therefore, you should not, like enthroning a fox as the king, enter into the Buddha’s 
teachings and repudiate them. Hence, [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand
42 
teaches:  
 
Furthermore, it has been taught, “That which is a knower and an abandoning of 
someone —ranging from stream -enterers to solitary realizers —is a forbearance of 
a bodhisattva who has attained forbearance with regard  to the dharma that is not 
produced.” What is  forbearance? It is the forbearance of dharma knowledge with 
respect to sufferings43. From what is this known? If it is different from  another 
path, then it is because lineage, power and accumulation will be superior. For, the 
actions  of a forbearance of a bodhisattva subsume all actions  of the realizations44 
of others without exception. That which is a knower and an abandon ing of them 
is a forbearance of [such a bodhisattva]; with respect to the statement, ‘ The 
criterion of realization is not just this ,’ it is another enumeration .  
 
According to the meaning said above, the explanation has been taught to mean that, 
“Having intended that the actions  of a forbearance of dharma knowledge with respect to 
sufferings of a bodhisattva on the path of seeing subsume al l functions of the realizations of 
the selflessness of persons of hearers and solitary realizers without exception, it signifies 
that the abandonments and realizations of hearers and solitary realizers are subsumed in the 
forbearance of a bodhisattva; this  does not signify that all the ability of a realization of 
selflessness of persons without exception is complete on the path of seeing.” Hence, it is not 
                                              
42 This refers to Vimuktisena’s Commentary on the Twenty -five Thousand (Abhisamayalamkara -vrtti ). 
43  This is one of the sixteen instants of for bearance and knowledge of dharma and subsequent knowledge 
with respect to four noble truths.  
44  Rtog pa  emended to read rtogs pa in accordance with the explanation below.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
39 [Vimuktisena’s] assert ion that both the abandonments and realizations of hearers and 
solitary realizers, or either of them is complete on the first ground. Then what?  
 If [a bodhisattva]  wishes to exhaustively abandon those indicated to be the objects of 
abandonment —the afflictive obscurations in the case of the hearers and solitary realizers, 
and the uncommon knowledge obscurations of the conceptions of apprehendeds of the 
solitary realizers —, then, by intending that [a bodhisattva] ha s the ability that is able to 
abandon them on the path of seeing, it is explained that the actions  of a forbearance of a 
bodhisattva subsumes all actions  of the realization s of others. If the reason for the presence 
of such ability lies in another path mentioned  above [in Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  
cited above] —the  exalted wisdom  direct ly realiz ing all phenomena as being empty of tru e 
existence —being superior to that of hearers and solitary realizers, then the reason “because 
of being superior to the hearers and solitary realizers by means of possessing the Mahayana 
lineage, very sharp powers of fa ith and so forth, and the vast accumulation of merit, in 
addition to that” is stated.  In dependence on the teaching in the commentary on the Great Vehicle Treatise on the 
Sublime Continuum (Mahayanottaratantrasastra ) that the very knower of paths that is 
included within non -conceptual exalted wisdom arises naturally on the eighth ground, a 
great lama translator says that the paths of the hearers and solitary realizers are generated in a manner of reflections  on the eighth ground, not before, because there is the misgiving  of 
them falling into becoming hearers or solitary realizers [before the eighth ground].  
 QUALM: How is “What is forbearance? It is the forbearance of dharma knowledge with respect to sufferings” [in Illuminating the Twenty Thousand cited a bove] explained?  
RESPONSE  [from the great lama translator] : It indicates the object to be known —the 
forbearance that is included within the path of the hearers; it does not indicate the knower—
the uncommon forbearance of a bodhisattva. Thus, it is asserted that the abandonments and realizations of hearers and solitary realizers are completed on the eighth ground.  
 The meaning of this citation from Illuminating the Twenty Thousand is as follows: if 
someone, such as a bodhisattva abiding on the forbea rance level of the path of preparation, 
were to principally make effort in abandoning the seeds of the afflictive obscurations, then 
at the time  of having attained the path of seeing , he would have been  able to exhaustively 
abandon the afflictive obscurati ons and to attain the liberated path in which they have been 
abandoned. Hence, it indicates the ability and force of the path in a manner of examining a 
possibility of attain ing it  if someone abiding on the dharma forbearance of the Mahayana 
path of seeing  were to also make effort in that way, as explained before. Not like that , (i) the 
exhaustive abandonment of the seeds of afflictive obscurations (by a bodhisattva arya who 
has not previously traversed the Hinayana paths) and (ii) its liberated path  would not arise 
until he has attained buddhahood. This also is the assertion of the Acarya  [Haribhadra] .  
2B2A -1B2A -2B2B -3. Having identified the perfect end, explaining how it is actualized  
QUALM: With regard  to that, what is the perfect end? How is it actualiz ed?  
RESPONSE: It is assert ed that (i) b y taking the perfect end to be mere thusness, the 
meaning of actualizing it is taken to be placed in meditative equipoise in a manner of not 
rising again, and (ii) by taking the cessation that is a dharmata  free from  stains to be the 
perfect end, attaining that is to actualize it.  These assertions woul d mean “One should  not 
actualize buddhahood until buddhahood is attained,” whereas it is difficult for th em to be 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
40 correct. This is because, although referring to the nirvana of severance45, they contradict the 
implied teaching that one should actualize  it after having completed [prayers], ripened 
[sentient beings] and purified [one’s buddha land]. 
 Someone makes a distinction between being able or not being able to (i) place  in 
meditative equipoise on mere thusness in a manner of not rising, and (ii) set  in meditative 
equipoise on the thusness free from stains by way of not rising. This claim is suitable as an 
object of laughter.  
 Therefore, these instruction s are instr uctions mainly for persons definite in the Mahayana 
lineage; further, the instructions are to begin from generating the mind [aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment, with the exception, “Do not actualize the perfect end on any 
occasion where the three [properties]
46 have not been completed.” In terms of ordinary 
disciples, the hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers, having arisen from the sphere of 
peace, are  instructed  to begin from generating the mind [aspiring] towards the Mahayana, 
with the excepti on, “Do not actualize the perfect end from now onwards in any interval 
where the three [properties] have not been completed.” Understand that if it is impossible for anyone to be able to actualize the perfect end during that time, explaining such 
instructi ons on this occasion would be  without purpose.  
 QUALM: In that case, what is the perfect end? How is it actualized?  
RESPONSE: In general, even thusness is the perfect end. When arya bodhisattvas have 
actualized the perfect end, they must be in direct med itative equipoise on thusness. 
However, here it is the very sphere of pacification of pervasive compounded suffering. The 
setting in absorption in the sphere of peace by hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers (for 
up to an eon in a manner of not risin g again in reliance on attitude, due to having been 
captivated by the bliss of peace prior to entering absorption) is the meaning of having actualized the perfect end in terms of Hinayana lineage -bearers themselves. Such trainees 
definite in the Hinayana l ineage, even if given the previously explained instructions right 
from the start, will also degenerate from the lower lineage. Having risen from the sphere of peace, the person who has generated a mind [aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment will 
enter ab sorption on that again and again through the force of previous habituation. 
However, discerning people should understand that they lack even the mere stain of giving 
up the Mahayana generated mind [of enlightenment].  
 According to  the assertions of the gre at charioteers who accept that true cessations are 
ultimate truths, the perfect end indicated in this context of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras 
should only be explained to be solely thusness. The assertion of this Acarya who composed 
the commentary, as ex plained above, is also the assertion of Acarya Dharmamitra. In view 
of the expression “bearing the support of not actualizing suchness,”

41 of that, cite the passage with the reason of not indicating the path of meditation48 have
