QUALM: Are bodhisattvas  who have not previously traversed the Hinayana path able or not 
able to actualize the perfect end before completing the three properties? What is it like  if it 
is taken that  they actualize it?  
RESPONSE: In asserting that they actualize it, if they are abl e to do so immediately after 
having attained the Mahayana path of seeing, what need to mention about the eighth -
grounders? However, the misgivings regarding seventh -grounders and below actualizing it 
is smaller; the misgivings regarding eighth -grounders is great er, whereby, having attained 
the eighth ground, the v ictors rouse them from cessation. That also is merely examining a 
possibility ; having attained the eighth ground, how can it be possible to actualize the perfect 
end before completing the three pro perties? This means that, if they were to actualize it, 
then, the mind having been well induced by the bliss of pacification of pervaded compounded suffering upon thusness, they would enter absorption for an eon or more in the 
sphere of peace. How can there be the misgiving that, although the exception is to actualize the perfect end after having completed the three [properties], due to captivated by that 
peace , they will come under its influence?  
 Hearers and solitary realizers who have risen from the sph ere of peace and entered the 
Mahayana actualize the perfect end from time to time, nevertheless this is nothing but mere 
habituation; the impelling of the thought, “I shall place myself in meditative equipoise on 
this for a long time” does not even slightl y exist. Since these topics are very difficult to 
realize, it is only appropriate for discerning people , without being decisive in contexts 
where the generation of doubt is appropriate, to analyze them well.  
2B2A -1B2A -2C. How the clear realizations of buddhas are included within the exalted knower of all aspects  
2B2A -1B2A -2C1. Question  
COMMENTARY 
“Bhagavan, on account of what are the exalted knowers of all aspects49 [those] of Tathagata 
Foe-destroyer Samyak Sa mbuddhas (the one- gone -thus, foe -destroyer, perfectly complete 
enlightened ones)?”  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Bhagavan, on account of what are the exalted knowers of all aspects [those] of 
Tathagata Foe -destroyer Samyak Sambuddhas (the one -gone- thus, foe -destroyer, 
perfectly complete enlightened ones)?”  
2B2A -1B2A -2C2. Reply 
COMMENTARY  
“Subhuti, phenomena are strongly expressed by (i) whatever aspects there are, (ii) whatever 
marks there are, and (iii) whatever signs there are. The Tathagatas comprehend those 
                                              
48 For more information related to this phrase, refer to the following outline near the end of chapter three: 
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3B. Decisive analysis 
49 sarvvākārajñatā  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
42 aspects, those marks, and those signs ; because of that , [those] of the Tathagata Foe -
destroyer Samyak Sambuddhas are the exalted knowers of all aspects.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
Having called out, “ Subhuti,” phenomena —ranging from forms to the exalted knowers of 
all aspects —are strongly expressed by (i) whatever aspects there are , the entities, (ii) 
whatever marks there are , the results, and (iii) whatever signs there are , the causes. The 
Tathagatas comprehend those aspects designating conventions and so forth —because of 
that reason , [those] of the Tathagata Foe -destroyer Samyak Sambuddhas are the exalted 
knowers of all aspects .   
2B2A -1B2B. Stating the proof from the brief Mother  
COMMENTARY  
Similarly, also in the brief it is taught, “also those who wish to train in the ground s of 
hearers” and so forth. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The way the clear realizations of the four aryas are included within the three exalted 
knowers is taught in the medium Mother . Similarly, it is taught also in the brief, the Eight 
Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra , because  there it is taught that “also those 
who wish to train in  the grounds of hearers train in the Mothers ” and so forth.  
2B2A -1B2C. Making referral to the proof from the extensive Mother  
COMMENTARY 
It is extensively taught also in the exte nsive [ Mother ]. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It is taught also in the extensive , because it is extensively  taught, “also those who wish to 
train in the grounds of stream -enterers …” 
2B2A -1B3. Explaining the meaning of the passages  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding that, the  knower of a ll (i-ii) bears the support of impermanence and so forth, with 
respect to phenomena, forms and so forth ; and (iii) bears the abandonment of confusion as a 
self, the result.   
 
EXPLANATION   
Regarding that indication of the three exalted knowers, the  knower  of all  possesses three 
attributes, because it :  
(i-ii) bears the support of the sixteen aspects, the aspect of impermanence and so forth 
(with respect to phenomena that are the objects of observation, forms and so forth) ; and  
(iii) bears the abandonment of confusion as a self  of persons , the result that is a  
separation . 
  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
43 COMMENTARY  
The knower of paths (i) is definite emergence by way of all vehicles ; (ii) bears the support 
of not actualizing50 suchness ; and (iii) bears the function of gathering sentient beings not 
gathered and so forth, the result.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the  knower of paths —possesses special method and wisdom, because it :  
(i) is the wisdom of definite emergence by way of all  the three  vehicles ;  
(ii) is the method which bears the support of not actualizing suchness at an improper 
time; and  
(iii) bears the function of gathering sentient beings not gathered and so forth, by way of 
the result of function (action) of beings.  
 
COMMENTARY  
The exalted knower of all aspects (i -ii) bears the support of being devoid  of the production 
of all phenomena ; (iii) bears the result of: (1) not discontinuing the welfare of sentient 
beings reaching the limits of space; (2) (a) realizing the perfect end; and (b) abandoning the 
connection of latencies. 
 
EXPLANATION  
An exalted knower of all aspects  has culminated the two welfares, because it:  
(i-ii) bears the support  of the aspect of the lack of ultimate production of all phenomena  
that are the objects of observation;  
(iii) bears  the result of: (1) others' welfare , not discontinuing the welfare of sentient 
beings reaching the limits of space ; and (2) one's own welfare, (a)  directly realizing the 
perfect end  in a manner of not rising again, and  (b) abandoni ng connection to a later 
similar type of  latencies  of non- afflicted ignorance.  
 
2B2A -1B4. Summary   
COMMENTARY 
Therefore, this statement, “The Ornament for Clear Realization , since it includes all the 
bases, antidotes, and aspects, completely reveals the clear realizations” is correct.  
 
EXPLANATION  
All the clear realizations of the four aryas are included within the three exalted knowers.  
Therefore, this statement, “ The subject —the Ornament for Clear Realization —
completely reveals the clear realizations  of the four aryas in their entirety ” is correct, 
since it includes all the bases, antidotes, and aspects.

50  Gsum emended to read sum.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
44 2B2A -2A. Having set forth a qualm, making a connection  
QUALM: When determining all the bases, antidotes, and aspects, it is necessary to 
extensively determine the three exalted knowers. In that case, since the Perfect ion of 
Wisdom Sutras are repeated, there would be no purpose in composing the Ornament for 
Clear Realization .  
 
COMMENTARY 
In order to eliminate the qualm of beings of low intelligence: "Did not the Bhagavan, bearer 
of exalted compassion desiring to benefi t the sentient beings with faith in the extensive, 
medium, and brief, clarify all meanings of the Perfection of Wisdom, and the stages of the 
eight clear realizations in the various Mothers  of the victors ? What significance is there for 
the Bhagavan One Vi ctorious Over the Non -victorious Aryas  to show those again?", 
through generating doubt  tending towards the factual , the branches of engaging —the subject 
matter, the purpose, the purpose of even the purpose, and the internal relationship of his own treatise —are taught. 
 
EXPLANATION   
This is the branch of eliminating qualm  in order to prove that there is a special purpose for 
composing the Ornament for Clear Realization by explicitly setting forth here the rejection 
of the principal qualm. In order to elimin ate the qualm of those of low intelligence  who 
do not understand the feature of uncommon purpose s of the Sutras and the treatise, the 
subject matter, the purpose, and the purpose of even the purpose  are explicitly 
indicated , and the internal relationship of Maitreya’s own treatise is  taught.  What is the 
qualm like?  "What significance of purpose  is there  for the Bhagavan One Victorious 
Over the Non -victorious Aryas to show those stages of the eight clear realizations 
again? There is none."  
 The reason for the wrong conception is as follows. Whether in terms of those of sharp, 
middling or dull faculties, did not the Bhagavan, bearer of exalted compassion desiring 
to benefit the sentient beings with faith in the extensive, mediu m, and brief, clarify all 
meanings of the Perfection of Wisdom? He already did. Where? In the various  three 
Mothers  of the victors . What are the meanings? The stages of the eight clear realizations.   
Having eliminated the qualm, there is a need to set fort h a statement of the purpose -and-
relationship, because, it is a branch of the method of engaging in this treatise . By setting 
forth a statement of the purpose -and-relationship, one will engage, because , through 
generating doubt tending towards the factual  in dependence on this statement, thinking 
that there is probably a purpose, one engages.  
 The former qualm  is erroneous  and forceful , whereby one does not hold it to mean 
“Having  eliminat ed doubt,  generating doubt .” In brief, it indicates the branch of engaging, 
where doubt tending towards the factual is generated regarding the four, the purpose and so 
forth, which contradicts the doubts tending towards the non -factual: (1) thinking that there is 
probably no purpos e of accomplishing the temporal and final welfare of beings in 
composing this treatise, (2) even if there is a purpose, that is not the object of seeking of 
beings, (3) even if it is, that purpose cannot be accomplished, and (4) even if it can be 
accomplis hed in general, it is unrelated to the method shown here.

There is not the fault of repeating the Mother Sutras in composing this treatise…

…because ‘realizing easily’ the meaning of the Mother Sutras in dependence on this is the 
purpose of composing this treatise . There is a purpose for easily realizing the meaning of 
the Sutras, because, in dependence on it, the intelligent bodhisattvas will directly see the 
meaning o f the sutras of the nature of the ten Dharma practices , such as the generated 
mind [of enlightenment], which characterize an exalted knower of all aspects. It is not that there is no method to directly see the meaning of the sutras, because it  will become manifest 
through having cultivated  it by placing it in mindfulness with hearing and contemplation. 
This treatise possesses a special meaning that is to be realized, because it is directly seen by the exalted knower of all aspects, and  the path  which procee ds there, differentiated by 
way of the ten topics and eight sections , that which is explained in these  three Mothers  by 
the Teacher, is  determined as the subject matter.  The subject —the stages of the eight clear 
realizations —are not completely experienced by any person, because they are  not 
completely experienced by others , such as non -Buddhists, hearers and solitary realizers . 
By the first two lines indicating the entity of the subject matter, then one [line indicating] the distinction, then three [indicat ing] the essential purpose, and the last two [indicating] the 
purpose, the relationship is i mplied.  
2B2A -2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. The exalted knower of all aspects, the path of the buddhas 
themselves, is taken intent ly to close ly characterize all the clear realizations. The Bhagavan, 
through the three types of miracles, subsequently taught to all migrating beings, by he himself, whatever is shown as the very subject matter in the  three types of the Mothers  of 
the victors  togethe r. The expresser and the subject matter expressed are made related by 
way of manner of a method and the object to be achieved by that method. That not 
experienced through the levels with the knowers arisen from hearing and so forth by those 
who have  not fa miliarize d with the selflessness of all phenomena —non -Buddhists and the 
others, those separated from attachment and so forth; having placed well all the meanings of 
the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras without exception, the natures of the eight clear 
realizati ons—the mind of enlightenment and the deeds of achiev ings, generosity and so 
forth —in the consciousness of mindfulness that arose from the latencies which were 
deposited by very clear experiences by only the bodhisattvas who set out, without 
depending upon anyone, to achieve the fulfillment of their own and others' welfare; 
“through the levels of realizing the Very Joyous ground and so forth, the characteristic of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
46 realizing the sphere of dharma progressing everywhere, they will be actualized in all 
aspects”  is strongly desired for the sake of the purpose of even the purpose .  
 That the meanings of the Perfection of Wisdom —the mind of enlightenment and the 
characteristics of the achieving s and so forth —will be easily realized by the trainees is the 
purpose of  composing the treatise.  
 
EXPLANATION  
2B2A -2C1. Purpose  
2B2A -2C2. Essential purpose  
2B2A -2C3. Subject matter  
2B2A -2C1. Purpose  
There is a purpose in composing this treatise; that the meanings of the Mothers will be 
easily realized by the intended traine es of the Ornament  is the purpose of composing the 
treatise. What will be easily realized?  The meanings of the Perfection of Wisdom Sutras  
differentiated into the stages of eight clear realizations by taking the ten topics —the mind 
of enlightenment and the  characteristics of the achievings  and so forth—as the basis. 
2B2A -2C2. Essential purpose  
The aim that is  strongly desired for the sake of the purpose of even the purpose  is the 
essential purpose. It is not that there is no essential purpose because, in de pendence on this 
purpose [explained above], “ through the levels of realizing and attaining  the Very Joyous 
ground and so forth, the characteristic of realizing the sphere of dharma progressing 
everywhere  to all property -possessors, all the stages of clear realizations will be actualized 
in all aspects ” will be attained. 
 What is the method that will manifest the essential purpose? Having placed well in the 
consciousness of mindfulness , it will be actualized later. What is placed? All the meanings 
of the Per fection of Wisdom Sutras without exception —(i) the generated mind, the 
support of the deeds, the ten topics which characterize the exalted knower of all aspects,  
[which are]  the mind of enlightenment and generosity and so forth,  (ii) the deeds of 
achiev ings that practice the deeds, and (iii) the summarized result that is the object of 
attainment.  Further, in terms of the principal objects to be determined by the eight chapters, 
they are divided into the natures of the eight clear realizations .  
 What is the feature of the consciousness of mindfulness? Having contemplated well, 
through reasoning, the meaning of the stages of the clear realizations that has been heard, it 
is a mindfulness that arose from the latencies which were deposited by very cl ear 
experiences  that is able to induce an ascertaining consciousness in accordance with how it 
has been asserted. Who, having experienced it with hearing and contemplation at the 
beginning, will eventually actualize it?  It will be experienced by only the bodhisattvas 
who set out and begin without depending upon anyone , since they definitely engage in 
such a path to achieve the fulfillment of their own and others' welfare  and do not rely 
upon others. The assertion —that the principal object to be determined o f the third chapter is 
the knowers of bases in the continua of hearers and solitary realizers themselves —is well 
refuted by these [explanations].  
2B2A -2C3. Subject matter  
Whatever is shown as the very subject matter in the  three types of the Mothers  of th e 
victors  together is the subject matter of the treatise. Who showed it? The Bhagavan, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
47 through the three types —the miracle  of magical emanation, the miracle  of expressing all 
[known by the] exalted minds, and the miracle  of subsequent demonstration—
subsequently taught  the paths of the three vehicles to all migrating beings by he himself. 
What is the subject matter? It possesses a special subject matter. An exalted knower of all 
aspects is, through direct realization of which [the subject matter], the path of the buddhas 
themselves  which engage in an exalted knower of all aspects, ranging from the generated 
mind to the achievings. Through division into eight sections , having taken that as the basis, 
the ten topics which closely characterize all the eight clear realizations are taken 
intently  by the Teacher. In brief, [the subject matter is] the eight clear realizations.   
 The distinction  of the subject matter is as follows:  that not experienced through the 
levels of the knowers arisen from hearing and so forth by those who have not 
familiarized with the selflessness of all phenomena  free from all extremes —non-
Buddhist  followers such as Simha, Vishnu, and Brahma, and the others , the hearers and 
solitary realizers sepa rated from attachment and so forth . There is a reason for it not 
being experienced by them, because the expresser —the Mother Sutras as well as the 
Ornament—and the subject matter expressed —the eight clear realizations —are made 
related by way of manner  of a method and  accomplisher of that method.  There is 
pervasion, because they [non -Buddhists, hearers and solitary realizers] do not perform any 
of the three —hearing, contemplation, and meditation, in regard  to the very extensive 
scriptural baskets and the tre atises that commentate  on their thought. The Mahayana is 
superior, due to its greater object of observation. It is also explained that the Acarya, in this 
passage, clarified the internal relationship . 
 Hence, the entity of the subject matter is as follows. The Great Commentary says that 
“With respect to that, by indicating the result, bodhisattvas who seek enlightenment are made to fully understand all aspects, such as the generated mind, whereby the exalted 
knower of all aspects is expressed first.” “The p ath of the exalted knower of all aspects” 
[0.2a] is taken to indicate that the path that proceeds to an exalted knower of all aspects is 
principal. Know that the expression of worship and the purpose -and-relationship must be 
correlated with the two other s ummarized meanings , which will be explained below.
51

2B2B -1. Making a connection  
2B2B -2. Setting forth the statement  
2B2B -3. Summarized and explained  
2B2B -4. Reason for not unraveling them extensively  
2B2B -1. Making a connection  
Since the relationship was not explicitly indicated by the root text, having asserted the elimination of the qualm, the relationship was mentioned at the outset.  
 
COMMENTARY 
Thus, having indicated the relationship and so forth , in order to make trainees realize easily, 
and also having seen it as easy to explain by not mixing up the completely classified 
meaning of the treatise , the presentation of the corpus of the Perfection of Wisdom is taught, 
through indicating briefly and extensively by fifteen verses .

48 EXPLANATION  
Thus, having indicated52 the relationship and so forth, the presentation of the corpus of 
the Perfection of Wisdom is taught; for the purpose in regard  to the listeners is in order to 
make  listener trainees realize easily  the levels of extens ive explanation of the branches, 
and in regard  to the explainers, [for them] to do it also having seen it as easy to explain by 
not mixing up the completely classified  concise meaning of the treatise  with the levels of 
extensive explanation of the branches . A summarized meaning  of the corpus has indicated 
it briefly and extensively by fifteen verses .

EXPLANATION  
The three —extensive, medium, and brief textual Perfection of Wisdom —are objects to be 
determined. By what? By this treatise. How? They are perfectly , completely and non -
erroneously explained by the divisions of the expresser into eight chapters, and also the 
divisions of the categories , which are the meaning of the subject matter expressed, in to 
eight clear realizations .

2B2B -2A1B -1. Stating the assertion of the Acarya 
The Great Commentary  says:  
 
Thus, because of realizing the exalted wisdom of the exalted knower of all 
aspects through the stages of generating the knowers bearing the nature of 
hearing, contemplation and meditation at first, it goes to the other side —that is, 
the side of excellence —whereb y it is paramita [usually rendered as “perfection,” 
literally meaning  “that gone to the other side”]. 
 
Further, it has gone to the other side that is wisdom, the characteristic of discerning  
phenomena that is called “ prajña,” [usually rendered as “wisdom,”  literally meaning  
                                              
52  Bsnyad  emended to read bstad in accordance with the commentary above.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
49 “excellent knowing”] [whereby] it is prajñaparamita  [usually rendered as “perfection of 
wisdom,” literally meaning  “that gone to the other side that is wisdom”].  
 Although a buddha bhagavan’s illusion -like exalted wisdom of non -duality is the actual 
[perfection of wisdom], the texts that are the collections of words and statements and the 
paths that bear the characteristics of seeing and so forth, due to being concordant with the 
attainment of that [exalted wisdom], are imputed “perfecti on of wisdom.”  Acarya Dignaga 
also explained that:  
 
Due to the possessing the meaning  of the object to be achieved ,  
The perfection of wisdom ;  
That non-dual exalted wisdom [of] the Tathagatas ,  
 The paths and texts are termed that. 
 Therefore, [Acarya D ignaga ] definitely held
 that “ By way of being related to that, all three 
aspects  free from being reflections are also perfections of wisdom ”; it is in terms of that 
other than the principal . It is not just those , because that is in terms of all three 
enlig htenments, since [all  three enlightenments] would not occur if this [perfection of 
wisdom] were absent.53 In addition, [the Great Commentary ] explains that:  
 
“Also, those who wish to train in the grounds of hearers should listen to this Perfection of Wisdom ” and so forth will be explained.  
 It means that only a buddhas’ exalted wisdom distinguished by either the three or the four 
attributes
54 is the actual perfection of wisdom; the path perfection of wisdom and textual 
perfection of wisdom are imputations.  
 With regard to the differences between the actual and the imputed, Acarya Dignaga’s text 
was cited, but it is not identical in every way [to the explanation here], [because] “illusion -
like” is mentioned by asserting [an exalted wisdom] distinguished from a  truly existent 
knower of emptiness of duality. “By way of being related to a Mahayana  person who is the 
support, all three perfections of wisdom free from being the reflections of perfection of 
wisdom are also perfections of wisdom” is the meaning of “that definitely held” by Acarya Dignaga.  
 
QUALM: Why is it that only the three —Mahayana  texts, paths, and results —are indicated?  
RESPONSE: These are in terms of the principal objects to be determined
; they are not 
exhausted in just these, because if the p aths of the hearers and solitary realizers have not 
been determined, the Mahayana  texts and paths would also not occur, where by they are 
determined in terms of the paths of all three enlightenments.  
                                              
53 Annotated in accordance with a statement in the next outline: “Since all three enlightenments would not 
occur if this perfection of wisdom wer e absent, the paths of all three are indicated. ” 
54  These are alluded to in Dignaga’s explanation above. In accordance with page 118 of the Ganden Jangtse

attribute of entity: being an exalted wisdom; and (4) the attribute of being free from the object of negation: 
being empty of true existence, l ike an illusion.

50 2B2B -2A1B -2. Refuting the wrong explanations given by ot hers  
OPINION: Because of this statement, “in terms of that other than the principal,” that in 
terms of the resultant perfection of wisdom, the principal among the three perfections of 
wisdom , is indicated.  
RESPONSE: Your assertion is a wrong explanation. For this text by Acarya Dignaga explicitly indicated the three —Mahayana  texts, paths and results. The proof is indicated by 
the passage:  
 
The three Mahayana  dharmas —free  from being (i) a reflection of the path 
perfection of wisdom, an exalted wisdom realiz ing impermanence and so forth 
that is bound by the conception of true existence, (ii) a reflection of the resultant 
perfection of wisdom that is to be attained by merely that, and (iii) a reflection of 
the textual perfection of wisdom in which mainly those  are being determined —is 
the perfection of wisdom.  
 For this is also the meaning of the statement “It is the principal object to be determined;” 
there is no qualm regarding indicating the resultant perfection of wisdom as principal. Since all three enlight enments would not occur if this perfection of wisdom were absent , the paths 
of all three are indicated. Where? The statement “ Also those who wish to train in the 
grounds of hearers…’ will be explained  below ,” and also [it is indicated in the context of] 
“the cause of attaining the three vehicles together.”
55 
 The two —the textual and path perfections of wisdom —are imputed perfections of 
wisdom. The reasons for the imputation are (i) because [the textual perfection of wisdom] 
indicates the resultant perfection  of wisdom as the subject matter, and (ii) because [the path 
perfection of wisdom] is the method for attaining it. The purpose of the imputation is to 
make known that those who wish to attain the result must hear and contemplate upon the 
texts and practice  the paths. They are not the actual, because they lack the culmination of 
wisdom.  
 Someone’s assertion —that a perfection of wisdom is pervaded by being a wisdom —does 
not differentiate between etymology and engagement  of the term . Because , the perfection of  
generosity and so forth in the mental continuum of a bodhisattva are made by this [wisdom] 
to be gone to wisdom , whereby they satisfy the etymology “ gone to wisdom ” (“perfection 
of wisdom”) . 
 
Hence, the textual perfection of wisdom is  the collections of t exts, the expresser that mainly 
determine s the Mahayana  paths along with their results. The path perfection of wisdom is 
the paths in the mental continuum of a bodhisattva that are explicitly conjoined with the 
wisdom realizing emptiness  and the method of great compassion. The resultant perfection of 
wisdom is the culminated exalted wisdom distinguished by the four attributes, as explained above.

51 2B2B -2A2A. General presentation of the path  
2B2B -2A2A -1. Positing the tradition of the previous acaryas

2B2B -2A2A -3. Our own system  
r2B2B -2A2A -1. Positing the tradition of the previous acaryas  
OPINION 1: The three exalted knowers are determined to be the general ity clear 
realizations that are to be generated in the continua of all aryas of the three times. The four 
applications and the truth body are indicated to be mere paths that are to be generated in the 
continua of present intended trainees of the Ornament. Hence they are asserted to be 
generality and instances, and to be broadly and narrowly pervasive.  
 OPINION 2: It has been said that, “The th ree exalted knowers, like a sword placed on a 
plain, are devoid  of boundaries, whereas the four applications and the truth body, like a 
sword held in the hand, have boundaries posited.”   
However, this is not much different from the former [position].  
 
OPINION 3: It has been said that, “The chapters of the three exalted knowers principally 
indicate the objects to be determined by way of hearing and contemplation, whereas the four applications and the truth body indicate the stages of the path of practice by  way of the 
wisdom arisen from meditation.”  
 
OPINION 4: Someone deprived of anything to say asserts that the first three chapters 
indicate view; the middle four, cultivation and conduct; and the last, the result.  
 OPINION 5: Taken in the system of solely paths:  
• an exalted knower of all aspects is to be cultivated on the occasions of the paths of 
accumulation and preparation ;  
• a knower of paths from the first to the sixth grounds ; 
• a knower of bases on the seventh ground ; 
• the four applications are cultivated sequentially on the three pure grounds ; and  
• the truth body is generated on the buddha ground.  
 
ACARYA DHARMAMITRA: Having progressively generated the paths of the hearers and 
the paths of the solitary realizers indicated on the occasion of the second chapter, and the 
Hinayana  paths indicated in the third and fourth chapters, the result of foe -destroyer is 
actualized and one absorbs in the sphere of peace. Then, rising from that, one generates in the continuum the first and second generated minds indicated on the occasion of the first 
chapter, and the partial concordance with liberation indicated on the occasion of the fourth 
chapter; this is taken to be engaging in the Mahayana  path.  
2B2B -2A2A -2. Examining whether  they are correct or incorrect  
It is difficult to posit most of those  [as being correct]. It is impossible for aryas of the three 
times, who have generated in their continua a knower of paths and an exalted knower of all aspects, not to have generated in t heir continua the four applications and the truth body , 
(i) 
because, even though [the eight categories] have been determined here, this indicates paths 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
52 of all three times,  there does not exist even slightly a valid cognizer of definite 
discrimination that individually discriminates  that “this indicates paths of all three times ,” 
and “this indicates paths of only someone in the present,” and (ii) because even if others 
were to posit what is opposite in meaning to that, it would be extremely difficult to 
demo nstrate damage. 
 The second system is also incorrect because, no matter in which person’s continuum they 
are generated, one must posit definite boundaries with respect to the eight clear realizations; 
otherwise, the explainers and listeners of the path wou ld become totally confused, since 
they would not know the sequence of the paths in which first this is cultivated and afterwards that is cultivated.  
 The third system —[which asserts that] having determined something by way of hearing 
and contemplation, one  meditates on something else unrelated to that at the time of 
meditation —is extremely vile; it would become unrelated, just like consistently  showing a 
horse where to race, and then taking somewhere else to be the race tracks. Therefore, one 
must practice by meditating on just that which has been determined by way of hearing and 
contemplation, because, without having determined by way of hearing and contemplation that very meaning that is to be meditated upon, one’s meditation would become erroneous. 
 For, if it is necessary beyond doubt for the treatise to individually indicate those two [(1) 
the objects to be determined by way of hearing and contemplation, and (2) the stages of the 
path of practice by way of the wisdom arisen from meditation], then even if it is asserted 
that the remaining five chapters indicate the determination by way of hearing and contemplation, whereas the first three chapters indicate practice by way of meditation, there would not be even the slightest damage that can be demonstrated. For if the person who 
practices the four applications does not practice [the topics] ranging from the generated mind to the achievings and so forth, then this contradicts the practice of the four    
applications; and if he does practice them, then he must  practice them as indicated on the 
occasion of the first chapter, whereby it is very inappropriate to delineate as separate the 
passages for hearing and contemplation and the passages for practice.  
 This is because it seems that there are many followers w ho, as signs of not having gained 
a comprehensive understanding regarding these points, despite having learnt the scriptural 
baskets well their entire lives, at the time of generating a desire to henceforth perform 
practice , do not even know any of the nam es imputed to the practices . For it seems that even 
at the time when those who have formed a little understanding perform hearing and contemplation, as signs that they have taken the path only as an object of observation of 
awareness without generating it into the entity of awareness, even explainers become weary 
due to explaining and listeners also probably become weary due to listening.  
 It is easy to realize that the fourth system is incorrect; it obviously does not understand  
even the general presentati on of the treatise.  
 The fifth system appears to be that posited as our own system in the brief commentary
56 
composed by the Acarya, but [it is not], because a specific  refutation of it is made below in 
this commentary, Clear Meaning.  Without understanding the teaching on how 
misconceptions regarding the sequence are refuted, even the explanation called “ passage  
indicating the three paths  as imputation al [rather than fully qualified] ” can appear correct.  
                                              
56 This refers to the Commentary on the Difficult Points of the Verses Gathering the Precious Qualities of the 
Bhagavan composed by Haribhadra.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
53  The last system [posited by Acarya Dharmamitra] should be analyzed because, if one 
were to assert that “ Intended trainees of the Ornament must definitely engage the Mahayana  
path preceded by the Hinayana  path,” then, even though there may be some of dull faculty 
who discard the shortcut that proceeds to  omniscience and instead engage in the long path, 
this is impossible for the intended trainees of this treatise. If one were to consider the 
possibility of a person who engages progressively in the paths of the three vehicles, then 
[this should also be ana lyzed] because, in the context of indicating the stages of the path of a 
person who is definite in the Mahayana  lineage, it would be inappropriate to discard that 
and indicate something else that is unrelated.  
 Further, someone , while asserting  that the first three chapters indicate the objects of 
engagement of a Mahayana  person, the middle four the yogas that engage in them, and the 
last the result that has been engaged, also assert s that a knower of bases [exists] only in 
hearers and solitary realizers a nd that buddhas do not have a knower of paths ; this  is just 
something he should reflect upon.  
2B2B -2A2A -3. Our own system  
As indicated in the section on the purpose -and-relationship, easily realizing all the eight 
clear realizations by means of hearing and  contemplation is the purpose. I t has been taught 
that one traverses the ten grounds through practice by meditating on that which has been 
taken as the object of mindfulness by means of hearing and contemplation, therefore, since 
the meaning that is to be determined by a Mahayana  person by means of hearing and 
contemplation is indicated by all the eight chapters , [and]  since that very meaning that has 
been determined by means of hearing and contemplation is practiced by means of meditation, know that the mo de of practice is also indicated by all the eight chapters. 
 The Great Commentary says, “The nature of achieving the mind of enlightenment, the 
instruction s, and so forth are all differentiations due to divisions of the eight sections .” 
Taking those very t en topics that characterize an exalted knower of all aspects as the basis 
of the path, it is differentiated by means of the eight sections , the eight clear realizations. 
Further, one practices the path for the sake of achieving that very aim  of the promise  by 
means of the generated mind. The “practice of the achievings” is presented and great compassion must precede the generated mind. In order for that [to occur], one must first train in the attitude of a being of middling capacity, the path of the sixteen  [attributes of the 
four noble truths], impermanence and so forth, which has been determined in the third chapter. In dependence on that, [one generates] the generated mind [aspiring] towards 
supreme enlightenment. With respect to that very path directly realizing impermanence and so forth that is motivated by that [generated mind] and conjoined with the wisdom directly 
realizing emptiness, indicated on the occasion of the second chapter, one should also apply the modes of knowing hearers’ paths and, likewi se, knowing solitary realizers’ paths. When 
both paths that stop the extremes of cyclic existence and solitary peace are directly known 
by a bodhisattva, they are called “knowers of bodhisattvas’ paths,” and by means of being 
paths distinguished by diligen tly initiating  joyous effort in those, they receive the 
convention “achievings.”  
 Those very achievings cultivate the full complete aspects of the three exalted knowers. 
Furthermore, t hat very yoga that acts to destroy the afflictions and conceptualizations 
through a union of the two —calm abiding and special insight —is called “application.” That 
itself is divided into four applications by means of occasion and mode of cultivation. The 
achievings which are of the same entity as the applications are also divided into four. In 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
54 order to practice the Mahayana  path, one must first initiate joyous effort in the extensive 
attitude. In dependence on that, an extensive practice of the applications wi ll arise. In 
dependence on extensive attitude and application, one will be able to gather extensive 
accumulations of the causes of omniscience. Hence, the achievings are divided into four by 
way of their ability to undoubted ly give rise to  an exalted knowe r of all aspects . Also, with 
respect to that, for the sake of the three objects of intent,57 one must first observe the eleven 
objects of observation.58 
 Although lineage is explained in the context of the path of preparation, the Mahayana  
lineage must be aw akened prior to generating the generated mind in one’s continuum. In 
order for that [to occur], you must understand the methods for examining which lineage is possessed, like they have been explained in Hearers’ Grounds (Sravakabhumi ), and the 
signs of having awakened the lineage, like  they have been explained in the  Ornament for the 
Great Vehicle Sutras ( Mahayanasutralamkara) and the Bodhisattvas’ Grounds 
(Bodhisattvabhumi). Thus, in accordance with the stages of leading trainees, you must lead 
them by way of lineage before leading them to great compassion.  
 In the context of the instructions for the achievings, even though nothing other than the 
isolates of the ten topics
59 has been taught, you must receive instructions on all the paths of 
the three vehicl es as well as their results, without exception. Due to meditating upon the 
meaning of emptiness on the occasion of the path of accumulation, the initial boundary 
where it transforms into a wisdom arisen from meditation, the path of preparation, is 
indicate d. The presence of other special purposes regarding that will be explained. Since 
how one practices the stages of clear realizations will be explained separately [elsewhere], 
here I will not elaborate more beyond just that.

0.4c The e xalted knower of all aspects, …  
 
EXPLANATION   
The definition [of an exalted knower of all aspects ] is: a final exalted wisdom directly 
realizing the ten topics —generated mind and so forth.  
 That an exalted knower of all aspects of this [context] is posit ed by means of merely 
knowing the two truths is not an assertion of the Acarya; that the corpus of the general 
Mahayana  path dawn s as an object of awareness through indication  of the objects of 
realization of that exalted knower of all aspects that is one’s  object of attainment  is the 
purpose .

55  The object of characterization  is: “ exalted knower of all aspects .” The basis of 
characterization  is: in general, in terms of divisions, from between the two exalted 
wisdoms —(1) knowing all aspects of objects of knowledge, and (2) directly knowing the 
ten topics of principal cause and result —here it is the latter. The boundaries are: it exists 
only on the buddha ground.  
2B2B -2A2B -1B. Knower of paths  
ROOT TEXT  
0.4c …the knower of paths, 
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition [of a knower of paths ] is: a Mahayana  person’s clear realization conjoined 
with a wisdom directly realizing emptiness in the continuum of the person who possesses it 
in his continuum.   
 Someone’s assertion of “an exalted wisdom directly realizing the empti ness of true 
existence of the three paths” as the definition of a knower of paths is incorrect. A knower of paths knowing hearers’ paths would not directly realize the sixteen [aspects of the four 
noble truths], impermanence and so forth, whereby it follow s that it would be inappropriate 
for a clear realization (that is to be passed through, having been viewed by knowing and 
seeing by a bodhisattva) to be generated at the time of the Mahayana  path of seeing; it 
follows that the first of the three knowers of paths would not be cultivated  for the sake of 
taking care of the hearer lineage -bearers ; if that very direct realization of impermanence and 
so forth directly realizes freedom from elaboration, then it follows that the stains of the conception of the two truths as different entities would be exhaustively abandoned on a 
learner’s path; it follows that meditative equipoise and subsequent attainment would be of 
one entity, and so forth.  
 Furthermore, with respect to a knower of paths, it follows that the grea t compassion that 
stops the extreme of peace would  not included within a knower of paths. And if even the 
compassion of non- observation in a bodhisattva’s mental continuum does not directly 
realize emptiness, then the assertion that a knower of paths is pe rvaded by it would only be 
incorrect. 
 The object of characterization  is: “knower of paths .” The bases of  characterization are: 
all clear realizations of Mahayana  aryas that are conjoined with a wisdom directly realizing 
emptiness, such as a knower of path s knowing hearers’ paths. The boundaries are: it exists 
from the Mahayana  path of seeing until the buddha ground.  
2B2B -2A2B -1C. Knower of bases  
ROOT TEXT  
0.4d  Then, knower of all, 
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition [of a knower of bases ] is: an exalted wisdom —conjoined with a wisdom 
directly realizing the selflessness of persons in the continuum of a person who possesses it 
in his continuum —that abides in a class of hearers’ clear realizations. The object of 
characterization  is: “then, knower of all.” The bases  of characterization are: all that directly 
realize the sixteen [aspects of the four noble truths], impermanence and so forth, and abide 
in that class. The imputation  of the convention “antidotal knower of bases”  to the exalted 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
56 wisdom directly realizing em ptiness of true existence free of elaborations is not present in 
either of the Acarya’s great or small commentaries. The boundaries are: it exists in all arya 
persons.

meditation observing emptiness. The object of characterization  is: “gone to the peak.” Its 
divisions are four when condensed, and 173 when extensively [enumerated]. The 
boundaries are: from the heat level of the Mahayana  path of preparation until  the end of the 
continuum of the ten grounds.

aspects of the three exalted knowers, cultivates them progressively. The object of 
characterization  is: “ serially.”  It has thirteen divisions. The boundaries are: from the 
Mahayana  path of accumulation until just prior to the end of the continuum.

2B2B -2A2B -3. The truth body  
ROOT TEXT  
0.5d Truth body: …  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition [of a truth body ] is: a final result attained by the strength of cultivating the 
aspects of the three exalted knowers. The object of characterization  is: “truth body.” When 
divided, there are four exalted bodies. The boundaries are: only on the buddha ground.  
 
Condensing the enumeration of these:  
 
ROOT TEXT  
0.5d … these are the eight. 
 
EXPLANATION  
“These are the eight.” With respect to that, the way in which the number of the three 
exalted knowers is definite should be known at the outset by all who seek mere liberation and who seek omniscience. Meditating on the bases that are objects to be determined by 
means of the sixteen aspects of the four noble truths, impermanence and so forth, is definitely necessary in order to attain the result of foe -destroyer of hearers and sol itary 
realizers; one is able to attain the result of foe -destroyer through merely that. Even a 
bodhisattva must first cultivate the sixteen aspects prior to cultivating a knower of paths; he 
too must know such bases in order to (i) take care of hearer line age-bearers and (ii) generate 
disillusionment for all of cyclic existence himself. Without that  [knowing of bases] , these 
will not arise. In order to attain the convention, “ a bodhisattva who knows well the path 
proceeding to omniscience,” and to accomplis h the welfare of the entire world, he must 
know the paths of the three vehicles. Without that, he will not be able to do so. An exalted 
knower of all aspects is definitely necessary in order to turn the complete wheels of Dharma 
for all three lineage -beare rs. Without that, he will not be able to do so. Hence, even from the 
point of view of accomplishing the goals of the four aryas, the exalted knowers are definite in number as three, because the exalted knowers that are objects to be practiced by 
mahayanists are definitely three, and nothing more than those is needed.  
 Acarya Arya [Vimuktisena] said, “[That] is explained to be the meaning of ‘a hearer’s 
path passed through, having been viewed by knowing and seeing by a bodhisattva, and is 
the cause of attain ing the three vehicles together…”
60 From that, it seems  that there is 
neither a knower of bases nor a knower of paths in ordinary beings.  
 The number of the four applications is definite. In order to destroy all the afflictive and 
knowledge obscurations wit hout exception, one must cultivate the complete aspects of the 
three exalted knowers. Since that is  subsumed under the two modes —(i) cultivation for the 
sake of attaining control over the familiarization , and (ii) cultivation that is the culmination 
of the  highest familiarization —there are the first two applications , and  in order to actually 
attain the truth body that is clearly  and completely enlightened with respect to all 
phenomena in one instant, when one takes to mind a single [aspect], such as impermanence, 
                                              
60 See the explanation of stanza 1.10.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
58 on the occasion of its application, without having to exert oneself in taking to mind the 
others, all aspects of the three exalted knowers must dawn as objects of the mind ; since one 
must act to sequentially familiarize with those [aspects] through exertion previously in order 
for that to occur, the branches of the attainment of stability are definite as the two later 
applications. Thus the applications are definite as four. The final result of these is called the 
“truth body.”  
 The sequence of the eight clear realizations is definite. It is explained in the section on 
making a connection at the Truth Body [explanation] of the Great Commentary [on the 
Eight Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra] : 
 
“At that time when those who take to mind the thorough abandonment of deceit 
and dissimulation, and so forth, through the stages that have been explained: (i) will subsequently co gnize with an exalted knower of all aspects that all 
phenomena are like illusions with respect to this perfection of wisdom; (ii) will 
thoroughly  analyze with a knower of the aspects of the paths; (iii) will engage 
with a knower of all; (iv) will realize w ith a complete clear realization of all 
aspects; (v) will intend with a clear realization of peak; (vi) will comprehend with a serial clear realization; (vii) will closely realize with an instantaneous 
clear realization; and (viii) will also cultivate with  a clear complete enlightened 
truth body, at that time it will not be difficult for these bodhisattvas to fully 
completely gain: (a) all excellent qualities of the nature of the truth body; (b) a 
buddha land with the characteristic of the complete enjoymen t body; (c) the 
unsurpassable quality of a buddha, the self- entity of the emanation body; along 
with (d) the exalted activities” —is the meaning of the words. 
 
Therefore, the three exalted knowers are in the order of explanation.  
 Since what is explained he re is an explanation of the stages of practice of a Mahayana  
person, at the beginning, he first meditates on the sixteen aspects, then generates a knower of paths, and after that, he will directly realize the ten topics [of an exalted knower of all 
aspects ]. The first two applications are in the order of generation; the later two applications 
are also in the order of generation. The first and third applications are in the order of generation  too. The three exalted knowers and the four applications are in th e order of 
explanation. The clear realizations are definite as eight, the objects to be cultivated by the four applications are definite as the aspects of the three exalted knowers, the way of 
cultivating them is definite as the four applications, and the result is definite as the truth 
body.

0.6b Four types of branches of definite discrimination,  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of a Mahayana  branch  of definite discrimination  is: a Mahayana  clear 
realization generated subsequent to a partial concordance with liberation that is included 
within the level of utilizing belief61. When divided, there are four types .  
                                              
61  Generally, generated minds are divided into four levels. They are: (1) the level of engagement through 
belief, (2) the level of pure extraordinary thought, (3) the level of ripening, and (4) the level of the 
abandonment of obscurations. The level of engagement through belief covers the path of accumulation until 
the path of preparation; the second, the level of pure extraordinary thought, covers the first until the seventh 
grounds; the third, the level of ripening, covers the three pure grounds; and the fourth, the level of the 
abandonment of obstructions, the buddha ground.

2B2B -2B1A -2. How they characterize an exalted knower of all aspects  
Although this is slightly explained in the modes in which the result is characterized by the  
cause, the Acarya’s assertion is as follows. The Great Commentary says:  
It is very clear that the object -possessor is characterized by the object; also the 
purpose of that is the direct realization of these ten topics —the nature of that 
exalted knower of all aspects that is the object of seeking.  
 When that is explained, it is indicated that the way of realizing them is to generate the mind 
[aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment at first, and then, through having experienced by 
way of the stages of the four achievings, to eventually manifest the three great ones. Hence, 
by merely indicating the ten topics that are to be directly realized [by] an exalted knower of all aspects, the corpus of the general Mahayana  path will dawn as an object of awareness, 
whereby it is for the sake of knowing the nature of the result that is the object to be attained.

62 2B2B -2B1A -3. Ascertaining the sequence  
Although the support of the achievings ha s been indicated  in the middle  from the point of 
view  of purpose, in terms of th e order of generation, first one awakens the lineage, generates 
the mind [aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment, hears instructions on the achievings, 
and produces  in the continuum the achieving of the wisdom arisen from meditation that 
observes emptines s by practicing the meaning of the instructions ; in order for that to occur, 
one observes the eleven objects of observation, and, for the sake of the three great objects of 
intent, through practic ing the stages of practice by way of the stages of the four achievings, 
one attains an exalted knower of all aspects directly realizing the ten topics.

Of skilled bodhisattvas is explained like that. 
 
EXPLANATION  
A knower of paths  that is a clear realization of skilled bodhisattva  aryas is explained like

the paths of all three vehicles , then one is included within the ranks of [those knowing well 
the path proceeding to omniscience] ; know that even though meditating on just a trifling 
path will also become a cause of omniscience, it is a long path.

66 not abiding in cyclic existence . Having eliminated the qualm wondering, “What are the 
paths that stop them?,” when the bodhisattvas ’ paths of seeing and meditation were 
indicated to stop the extremes of cyclic existence and solitary peace prior to these, saying “a 
knower of bases not abiding in solitary peace through compassion” is a wrong application of 
conventions, since (i) the meaning that is to be characterized and  (ii) the two meanings that 
act to characterize —are attributes of a knower of bases and a knower of paths 
[respectively].  
 
ROOT TEXT  
0.11b Through compassion, not abiding in solitary peace;  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of a knower of paths not abiding in solitary peace through compassion  is: 
a Mahayana  arya’s clear realization abiding in a class that stops the extreme of solitary 
peace relative to conventionalities.  
 Both of these exist from the first ground and above. It is incorrect to link “through 
know ing” [in the root text 0. 11a] only to a wisdom realizing emptiness, because its meaning 
is taught in the Great Commentary : “Seeing the faults of cyclic existence with wisdom.” 
The faults of cyclic existence are seen with a knower of conventionalities; it c annot be seen 
with a reasoning consciousness  observing the ultimate.

divisions of being devoid of or not being devoid of special method and wisdom. Doubts 
regarding these two characterizing qualities are also eliminated in the chapter on the knower 
of paths.  
2B2B -2B1C -1C. Divisions of the discordant and antidotal cla sses  
ROOT TEXT  
0.12a Discordant and antidotal classes;  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
67 EXPLANATION  
That very knower of bases is divided into (i) a knower of bases of the discordant class and  
(ii) a knower of bases of the antidotal class , by means of (i) being devoid of special method 
and wisdom and (ii) being conjoined with them. The assertion that the former is a 
conception of true existence should be discarded, since it does not know the presentation of 
reasoning and is not skilled in the stages of traversing the paths. The subject —a hearers’ 
path of seeing —it follows that is a conception because it is a knower that is a conception of 
true existence. If you accept that, then, since it lacks clear appearance, your assertion that it 
acts as a direct antidote to the seeds of afflictive o bscurations will degenerate. Furthermore, 
it follows that it is a wrong conception because it is the above [i.e., a conception of true existence ]. If you accept that, then, since it is mistaken with respect to its object of 
adherence, there would not be th e slightest difference between a conception of a self of 
persons and what is or is not to be practiced. That very knower of bases conjoined with 
special method and wisdom is explained to be “ a knower of bases of the antidotal class ,” but 
neither Arya [Vimu ktisena] nor Haribhadra used that convention “antidotal knower of 
bases” for an exalted wisdom directly realizing emptiness.

2B2B -2B1C -1D3. Result of an application of a knower of bases   
ROOT TEXT  
0.12c Hearers and so forth, path of seeing:  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of an explicitly indicated path of seeing  is: a clear realization of the truth that 
directly realizes freedom from elaboration. By way of those nine topics, that very knower of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
68 bases bearing the aspects of impermanence and so forth is indicated through being 
characterized by means of both explicit indication and implicit indication. A clear 
realization to be passed  through, having been viewed by knowing and seeing, that is 
included within a bodhisattva’s path of seeing is implicitly indicated by the explicitly indicated path of seeing.  
 From between the two —(1) a clear realization that is an object to be known and (2) a 
clear realization that is an object to be actualized —in the continuum of a bodhisattva, the 
first, although existing on all three paths, is a clear realization (that is merely viewed by knowing and seeing, and is an object to be passed through, having  been viewed by knowing 
and seeing), like  it has been explained.  
 The latter need not be definitely cultivated in order to attain mere liberation  and are  paths 
that need to be definitely cultivated as antidotes to the knowledge obscurations , [because] 
they are special method and wisdom. Hence, when indicating a clear realization (that is 
merely viewed by knowing and seeing and is an object to be passed through, having been 
viewed), it is necessary to indicate the path of seeing; it is inappropriate to indic ate the path 
of meditation that  is of a similar type to it. If  the principal learner’s path that is  actualize d 
for the sake of the path that is to be actualized  is indicated , the path of meditation must be 
indicated, and the path of seeing must also be ind icated as its cause. Therefore, the path of 
seeing explicitly indicated here is [ the clear realization ] that acts to pass through , and that 
indicated implicitly is the clear realization that is an  object to be passed through.

0.12d Such are asserted to be knowers of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Such clear realizations of hearers, solitary realizers, and so forth who are the support of the 
presentation are asserted to be knowers of all. These are explained in this way simply 
through the force of the metrics of composition; since the path of seeing is the last characterizing topic, its meaning is explained to be “Path of seeing, of hearers and so 
forth .”

2B2B -2B2A -1A1A. Divisions of the modes of apprehension of the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom  being 
cultivated  
ROOT TEXT  
0.13a Aspects …  
 
EXPLANATION   
When  the instances  of the modes of apprehension  of the aspects of the applications  are 
divided, there are 173. Taking the aspects  indicated here as objects to be characterized and 
supplementing them with their characteristics is a case of not having understood the 
meaning of “[Those] called  ‘aspects’ are the characteristics” [4.1b]. For, they are explained 
in the commentary of Arya [Vimuktisena] to indicate the antidotal characteristics; they are 
not explained to be the characteristics of the aspects. If that were to be the case, then the 
contextual meanings of all the three —bases, antidotes, and aspects —explained in the 
treatise wou ld become lost . 
2B2B -2B2A -1A1B. Application of meditative equipoise  
ROOT TEXT  
0.13a … along with applications;  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of a principal application  explicitly indicated here  is: a yoga that is a union 
of calm abiding and special insight realizing that the object, object -possessor, and the 
three —bases, paths, and aspects —are empty of true existence and free from the 
elaborations.  Having taken what is explicitly indicated here to be a yoga that is a union of calm abiding 
and special insight  realizing emptiness, the assertion that it exists from the path of 
accumulation is one which is unskilled in the stages of the path, because such a path must 
definitely be attained on the heat level of the path of preparation.  
 By way of the two -fold divi sion—into (1) objects to be cultivated, the aspects, and (2) 
that which acts to cultivate, the applications —having taken the 173 aspects as the objects of 
observation, the bases for eliminating the superimpositions of the applications, taking the 
cultivati on of all of them (by collecting  them together in the emptiness of true existence) as 
the way of cultivating an application in complete aspects is very incorrect. 
 This is because an application in complete aspects must cultivate all 173 aspects by way 
of a union of calm abiding and special insight, having caused them to dawn as modes of 
apprehension of its  aspects. Since these will be explained in the section on the meaning of 
the words, I will not elaborate more beyond just this. The words “ along with ” [in 0.13a] 
indicate the persons who are the supports cultivating the applications and gathering roots of 
virtue. The division of applications into twenty  is a division of the excellent qualities that 
are attained on that occasion; they are not divisions of the meditative equipoise alone.  
 With respect to that, the “applications” indicate a union of calm abiding and special 
insight, whereby a union of calm abiding and special insight observing varieties exists even 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
70 on the path of accumulation. However, th at which observe s modes is as explained above [to 
be attained on the heat level of the path of preparation]. 
2B2B -2B2A -1A1C. Excellent qualities and faults of the applications being cultivated  
ROOT TEXT  
0.13b Excellent qualities and faults…  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of an excellent quality  of an application  is: a benefit attained through the 
force of cultivating that [application]. When divided, there are fourteen. The definition of a 
fault of an application  is: that acting as a hindrance to the productio n or abidance of an 
application. When divided, there are forty -six. 
2B2B -2B2A -1A1D. Characteristics that characterize the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom  
ROOT TEXT  
0.13b … along with characteristics;  
 
EXPLANATION  
The definition of a yoga of the path  perfection of wisdom  is: a bodhisattva’s activity of a 
union [of calm abiding and special insight] that serves as the method for attaining a resultant 
perfection of wisdom. When divided, there are ninety -one. Its boundaries are: from the 
Mahayana  path of accumulation until the end of the continuum.

2B2B -2B2B -2. Instantaneous application  
ROOT TEXT  
0.17bcd … Clear complete  enlightenment  
In a single instant, 
By way of characteristics: fou r types. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
74 EXPLANATION  
Joining the [brief] indication and the [extensive] explanation, “ Clear complete  
enlightenment in a single instant,” is stated, and when divided, by way of the 
characteristics  of dissimilar isolate demarcations, there are four types  of different isolates 
with respect to a single entity.   
 I do not think that the assertion that it exists on the seventh ground and above  is the 
intention of the Acarya. Since even the end of the continuum cannot be a partless instant of time, [it exists fo r] the shortest instant in which an action is completed.  
 If we were to analyze the characteristics of the modes of cultivation of the last two 
applications, it seems they are as follows:  at the time of serial cultivation, on the occasion of 
the tenth gro und prior to the end of the continuum, if one were to directly realize a quality 
such as impermanence —through the force of previous familiarization, even without needing 
great exertion —all the aspects of the three exalted knowers, without exception, would 
become objects of direct perception in a single series without mix -up in sequence, by way of 
being with a little exertion. However, know that even at various points [during the process 
itself ], a little focused exertion is necessary. 
 I think that someone’s explanation that a serial application only progressively cultivates 
the thirteen topics [characterizing a serial application] has cast away the contextual 
meaning, because one needs to gain control over familiarization of the 173 aspects. 
Although one must cultivate the thirteen topics without mixing up the sequence in its 
appropriate mode, know that most of the thirteen are branches that act to stabilize the serial 
activities subsequent to the cultivation of the 173 aspects during meditative equi poise. 
 On the occasion of subsequent attainment, one recollects the Three Jewels, enhances the 
mind of giving, observes the intention of abandonment, and so forth.  
 On the occasion of cultivating an instantaneous application, as in the case of the analog y, 
“passing like a person’s watermill,” when an uncontaminated quality becomes manifest, 
without having to focus on the rest, they will manifest directly in a single series without 
mix-up in sequence.   
 That is the time of the path of application of the e nd of the continuum. As for the time of 
the uninterrupted path, know that from the state of being placed in meditative equipoise on emptiness, one transfers to the first instant of an exalted knower of all aspects, by means of 
that clear realization immedi ately becoming the nature of powerful potency. At that time, 
that very single entity of awareness places  in meditative equipoise on emptiness , in a 
manner of not rising as long as cyclic existence exists. At the time of that itself, one directly 
perceives all the varieties of phenomena as one would a amalaki  fruit placed upon one’s 
palm. Since the modes of the perfection of wisdom are difficult to realize at all times —and 
not simply on this occasion —they are only to be realized through having pleased the 
spiritual guide for a long time. 
2B2B -2B3. Explaining the corpus of the truth body  
ROOT TEXT  
0.18ab Nature along with complete enjoyment, Likewise the other, emanation,  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
75 EXPLANATION  
(i) A nature  body  that is a sphere possessing the two purities66, along with  (ii) a complete 
enjoyment body  distinguished by the five certainties and, likewise , that other  from the 
two—(iii) a supreme emanation  body , a final form body not distinguished by the five 
certainties —are the results that are objects to be obtained. Throug h that, know also their 
individual definitions. 
 
ROOT TEXT  
0.18cd Truth body along with the activities  
Are perfectly described as being of four types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Joining the [brief] indication and the [extensive] explanation, “Truth body” is stated. An 
assertion of the Acarya took them to be the words joining the [brief] indication and the 
[extensive] explanation. However, it is asserted that there is not the fault of not having 
engaged the meaning of the words of the treatise, even though the fourth  exalted bod y has 
been indicated by this [0. 18cd ]. Hence, the definition of a realization exalted wisdom truth 
body  is: a knower that is final insight relative to the modes  and varieties. The phenomena 
that are a  truth body along with the activities are perfectly described as being of  four 
types. 
 Having refuted that the meaning of “in reliance on an exalted wisdom and so forth as the 
very objects of buddhas and so forth” (in the context of the summarized meaning s of this 
[category] below) is that exalted wisdom emerges from within exalted wisdom and so forth, 
there is the explanation that it means that “(i) the twenty -one sets of uncontaminated exalted 
wisdom are posited as the exalted wisdom truth body, and (ii) the final body adorned with 
the signs and exemplifications is posited as the complete enjoyment body.” Both these 
explanations are cases of not having seen the Acarya’s commentaries in detail, because, in 
relation to this, the Great Commentary taught that it means that the exalted wisdom truth 
body  and the two form bodies have arisen from the accumulation of exalted wisdom, have 
arisen from the accumulation of merit, and have arisen from factors of both. 
2B2B -3. Summarized and explained  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. By way of the first two stanzas, because of being summarized into 
the eight categories, it was indicated in brief. By way of thirteen stanzas, through being 
summarized into the topics of those, it was explained extensively. Thus, because o f having 
been summarized and extensively explained, it has been well explained.  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to the fifteen stanzas present, the subject —by way of the first two stanzas  
[0.4 and 0.5]—the corpus was indicated in brief, because the corpus was su mmarized  
merely into the structure  of the eight categories. The subject —by way of thirteen 
remaining stanzas  [0.6–0.18]—the corpus was explained extensively, because it was 
explained through being summarized merely into the structure  of the seventy topics of 
                                              
66 The two purities are the purity  with respect to inherent stains and the purity with respect to adventitious 
stains.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                     Introduction  
 
76 those  eight [categories]. Thus, it has been well explained by way of the fifteen [stanzas], 
because of having been summarized and extensively explained in the above manner.  
2B2B -4. Reason for not unraveling them extensively  
COMMENTARY  
Fearing it would become the fault of repetition, the stanzas of the summarized meanings  
were not unraveled; by the very composition of the treatise, all of the treatise that will be 
explained (such as “ The generated mind is [ desire for perfect complete enl ightenment ] for 
others’ welfare”) will explain it, whereby I thought to explain it by thoroughly explaining 
those.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the stanzas of the summarized meanings  —were not unraveled  in detail 
prior to the extensive explanation of the branc hes which will be explained. The reason is 
because if they were unraveled in that manner, I fear it would become a commentary on 
the extensive explanation of the branches and there would be the fault of repetition,  
whereby they were not unraveled. There wo uld be repetition [if the stanzas of the 
summarized meanings  were unraveled], because, by the very composition of the treatise, 
all the words of the root text of the extensive explanation of the treatise which will be 
explained (such as “The generated mind  is [desire for perfect complete enlightenment] 
for others’ welfare”  [1.1a b below]), will explain this corpus. There is pervasion, because, 
in order to explain all of the words of the root text of the extensive explanation, I thought to 
explain this corpus  by thoroughly explaining those ; they will be explained.  
 
OPINION: Since the consideration of the fault of repetition in the brief indication and the 
extensive explanation is unrelated, the rejection of the dispute of this context is incorrect. 
RESPONSE: This is a manifestation of not having realized the meaning.  
OPINION: A commentary on the extensive explanation of the branches will repeat the root 
text, because it will extensively explain the corpus.  
RESPONSE: Those who are able to completely comprehend the meaning in dependence on 
the mere words of the root text do not need it; it is composed for the sake of those who are 
not able to do so.

Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
2B2C -1A. Explaining the object of seeking, the exalted knower of all aspects

2B2C -1A3. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -1A1. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY  
Having indicated all the summarized meanings  in that manner—since bodhisattvas desire to 
attain enlightenment accordingly, as it is necessary to attain the exalted knower of all 
aspects because it is the result —it is asserted that the exalted knower of all  aspects is 
explained first by means of thoroughly explaining the stanzas that summarize it. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Having indicated all the summarized meanings  in that manner, it is asserted that the 
exalted knower of all aspects is explained first, stating “genera ted mind and so forth 
taught…” There is a reason for first explaining an exalted knower of all aspects, as it is 
necessary  for trainees who desire to directly realize all the stages of clear realizations 
without exception to eventually attain the exalted k nower of all aspects . It is necessary, 
because it is the  principal result that is the object of seeking. That [an exalted knower of all 
aspects is the principal result that is the object of seeking] follows, since  the intended 
trainees of the Ornament, bodhisattvas, desire to attain  unsurpassable enlightenment in 
accordance with  the buddhas of the past and the present who have gone there.  
 
OPINION: Although it is appropriate to explain that at the beginning, why are merely [its 
topics ,] generated mind, instruction s, and so forth explained?  
RESPONSE: It is appropriate to explain those, because it is asserted that, by means of 
thoroughly explaining the stanzas that summarize the exalted knower of all aspects, the 
object -possessor is extensively characterized by the objects.  
 The explanation (by way of applying to all below what is similar to this [instance of] 
making of connection) is a presentation whose confusion generates amazement, whereby 
scholars should not make effort, because the need to explain that t he characteristics of an ox 
are conglomerated objects such as a dorsal hump, folds of flesh at the nape, drooping hide at the neck (dewlap), and so forth is inappropriate for trainees of this treatise for the time 
being.

2B2C -1A2A -1A. Supports who generate the  Mahayana generated mind  
In regard to the support who generates the wishing mind [of enlightenment], even desire 
realm gods, nagas, and so forth who are inclined to the Mahayana  and desire to attain 
buddhahood generate it. As for the explanation in [Asanga ’s] Bodhisattvas’ Grounds  
(Bodhisattvabhumi) and [Atisa’s] Lamp for the Path to Enlightenment  (Bodhipathapradipa ) 
that the support of the engaging mind of enlightenment must be individual liberation (pratimoksa) vows
67, this is in terms of the special suppo rt, because it also exists in the 
continua of some bodhisattvas who bear the support of desire realm gods and the support of 
the upper realms [form and formless realms].  
2B2C -1A2A -1B. Cause   
[Santideva ’s] Compendium of Trainings  (Siksasamuccaya ) explains the four modes of 
generating the mind [of enlightenment]: (1) having seen the exalted body of a buddha, one wonders, “What if I were to attain such [a body]?”; (2) seeing and hearing about the 
inconceivable strength of buddhas and bodhisattvas, and so fort h; (3) not being able to bear 
the decline of the Mahayana  teachings; and (4) not being able to bear sentient beings 
tormented by suffering. The explanation that it is generated from the four causes, four 
conditions and four forces is a branch of settling u pon the causes and conditions. Although 
the other three are mere generated minds that seek buddhahood, what is explicitly indicated here is a fully qualified principal generated mind that acts as the support of deeds; hence it 
must definitely be motivated by a compassion that cannot bear sentient beings’ suffering 
and wishes to free them from it.
