79 2B2C -1A2A -1C1. Definition  
The definition [of the Mahayana  generated mind ] is: a special mind that is concomitant with 
the aspiration seeking perfect complete enlightenment for others’ welfare. Know from the 
stages of the Ornament for Clear Realization, composed by Jetsun Losang Dragpa 
Palzangpo [Lama Tsongkhapa], how you should train in the attitudes seeking others’ welfare and seeking enlightenment, and so forth, in order to generate this.  
2B2C -1A2A -1C2. Divisions 
[The divisions are] wishing and engagi ng minds.  
 
2B2C -1A2A -1C3. Differences between wishing and engaging  
[Kamalashila’s] Stages of Meditation  (Bhavanakrama) says: 
 
The seeking at the beginning, “May I become a buddha in order to benefit those 
migrating beings” is the mind that wishes. From th en onwards, having upheld 
vows, that having engaged in the accumulations is the mind that has engaged. 
 
[Santideva ’s] Engaging in the Deeds of a Bodhisattva (Bodhisattva caryavatara , 1.16) gives 
analogies of the two:  
 
As is understood by the particularities  
Of desiring to go and going,  
So the wise should understand respectively  
The particularities of these two.  
 Having taken as the basis the thought, “I shall go to the city,” there are two [possibilities] of 
being and not being actually conjoined with activi ties of the body, such as striding onwards. 
Likewise, while the general definition of the generated mind does not have the attribute of 
engaging, the divisions into the two [possibilities] of being and not being actually conjoined 
with the activity of the practice of deeds is the meaning of the above  passage. The assertion 
that there is no wishing mind at the time of training in the deeds after taking the engaging vows is not the meaning of those [passages]. Moreover, even after having upheld the 
engaging v ows in that manner, there are two modes of engagement of the mind with respect 
to training in the deeds. The continuous  presence of a mindfulness and aspiration , that
 seeks 
omniscience (at a time when not only is one motivated merely by the generated mind, but an 
enhanced intention to give also dawns) is the meaning of being actually conjoined with the activity of the practice of deeds. When such is absent, then it is not actually conjoined [with 
the activity of the practice of deeds]. Therefore, it is obvi ous that there is no fault in 
asserting that a wishing mind exists from the path of accumulation —and even after having 
attained a ground —because even though one directly realizes the meaning of emptiness 
after having attained a ground, there will occur ple nty of occasions where the generated 
mind is not actually conjoined with [the activity of the practice of deeds].  
 Although the two —the engaging vows and the engaging mind —are contradictory [in the 
sense of being mutually exclusive], know that the generat ion of that [engaging mind comes 
about] through only the power of upholding the vows. The engaging mind exists from the path of accumulation until the buddha ground. What has been explained here are the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
80 divisions of the conventional mind of enlightenment; they are not divisions of the ultimate 
mind of enlightenment. The difference between the two is as follows. An intention for the 
conventional exalted body (without intending for the mere ultimate mode of abidance of 
unsurpassable enlightenment) is the form er. An intention for only the ultimate mode of 
abidance of enlightenment, in a manner of water placed in water, is the latter; it exists having attained the path of seeing and onwards; it does not exist in ordinary beings.  
2B2C -1A2A -1D. Benefits 
There are the temporal benefits of (i) purifying causes of bad migrations accumulated 
previously and severing their future continuity, (ii) newly accumulating causes of good 
migrations and those accumulated previously not having a limit to their exhaustion, and (iii ) 
becoming worthy of being paid homage by the world, including gods. Eventually, one will 
attain unsurpassable enlightenment and so forth.

COMMENTARY 
The entity of the generated mind itself, as well as objects of obs ervation:  
 
EXPLANATION  
The entity of the generated mind itself, as well as its objects of observation:  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1B. Root text   
ROOT TEXT  
1.1ab  The generated mind is desire for Perfect complete enlightenment for others’ welfare.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The Mahaya na generated mind  indicated here  is the definiendum. The definition is : a 
special mind concomitant with the desire for perfect complete enlightenment for the sake 
of liberating other  sentient beings from all suffering. 
 Having explicitly indicated the meaning here to be the Mahayana  generated mind, 
indicating it with the expression “desire” is for the sake of a special purpose. The meaning s 
of the two —the desire  of others’ welfare implied by merely this name and the desire  of 
enlightenment —are implicitly indicated here. Further, that is for the sake of making known 
that the desire for others’ welfare is necessary as a cause of the generated mind, and the desire for enlightenment is necessary as a companion. These will be e xplained. Hence, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
81 desire. For what? Unsurpassable enlightenment. For the sake of what? “For others’ 
welfare.” Hence, [from between] the two —enlightenment which is the object of seeking and 
others’ welfare —enlightenment will occur first, but know that the at titudes seeking those 
two must be generated in reverse sequence [i.e. the attitude seeking others’ welfare should 
be generated before the attitude seeking enlightenment].  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C. Commentary  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -1. Meaning of the words  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2. Dispelling objection  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -1. Meaning of the words  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. The characteristic of the generated mind [thinking], “Having achieved buddhahood, for others’ welfare I shall make effort in accordance with their 
fortunes” which desires perfect complete enlightenment for others’ welfare is of two types: 
wishing and the very entity of engaging.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the generated mind thinking, “For  the sake of others’ welfare, having 
achieved buddhahood myself at the beg inning, I shall make effort in accordance with 
the fortunes  of the three lineage -bearers” —is of two, because the characteristic of desire  
for perfect complete enlightenment for others’ welfare is of two types : generated mind 
wishing  to engage and the very entity of engaging . 
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2. Dispelling objection  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2A. Objection  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2B. Reply  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2A. Objection  
COMMENTARY  
OPINION: The very desire for perfect complete enlightenment —the aspiration for virtuous 
qualities seek ing that —is a mental factor68, but is not the generated mind a mind69 that 
arises bearing the appearance of superior objects? How will it be the generated mind?  
 
EXPLANATION  
OPINION: The subject—the aspiration which seeks (that is the desire for perfect 
comp lete enlightenment) —how will it be the generated mind? It follows that it will not, 
because it is a mental factor. There is pervasion, because the desire for enlightenment —the 
aspiration for virtuous qualities seeking that—is a division of the individually- object -
ascertaining [mental factors]70 included within the aggregate of compositional factors71, but 
                                              
68 What is usuall y translated as “m ental factor ” literally means “arisen from mind.” 
69 “Mind” in this context refers to a main mind or a primary consciousness.  
70 According to Asanga's Compendium of Knowledge (Abhidharmasamuchchaya), the 51 m ental f actors can 
be subsumed under the  six groups:  
(1-5) omnipresent mental factors, feeling and so forth  
(6-10) object -ascertaining mental factors, aspiration and so forth  
(11-21) virtuous mental factors, faith and so forth  
(22-27) root afflictions, attachment and so forth  
(28-47) se condary afflictions, belligerence and so forth  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
82 is not the generated mind a mind that arises bearing the appearance of superior  
objects , the two welfares , as objects ?  
 Regarding the meaning o f this objection, those who are mixed -up about just the words 
say, “ One posit s that the generated mind is a desire, but one  does not accept the 
consequence flung, that it follows that a desire is not the generated mind.” With respect to 
the thought of the objector, if he objects that a desire is posited as the definition of the 
generated mind, then, since the thought, “That desire —the basis of characterization  in 
which the definition has engaged —must be asserted as the generated mind,” would arise if 
one is an analytical person, how can there be an opportunity for one not accepting [that]? It 
is not that the Acarya composed the treatise after having stated the objection in which that 
was not accepted, but was deprived of a reply to that. Since the pervasion of that 
consequence was also accepted by the Acarya, the damage to fact in that reply is just that. With respect to the  claim  that “That indicates that minds and mental factors are different 
substances ,” the pervasion that those that are contradictory are necessarily different 
substances cannot be proven even by a pure  being .  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2B. Reply  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2B1. Designation by the name “desire for others’ welfare”  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2B2. Designation by the name “desire for enlightenment”  
2B2C -1A2A -2A1C -2B1. Designation by the name “desire for others’ welfare”  
COMMENTARY 
It is true. However, in this [context] when there exists the characteristic of aspiration for virtuous qualities —seeking —the mind of enlightenment is generated, whereby the result is 
indicated by the cause. In order to make known that “all the virtuous qualities of 
bodhisattvas who seek in that manner will increase,” since it is based upon designation, 
there is no fault. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It is true  that whatever is a mental factor is not the generated mind . However, the subject —
in this context of indicating the definition of the generated mind —since the generated mind  
is based upon designation by the name “desire,”  there is not the fault of asserting a 
common locus [between a mental factor and  the generated mind], because it is a designation 
by way of the three attributes being complete.  There is a reason for the designation, because the result is indicated by designating it 
with the name of the cause. The aspiration seeking others’ welfare and the Mahayana  
generated mind will become cause and result, because when there exists the seeking of 
others’ welfare with the characteristic of aspiration for the  virtuous qualities  of 
separating all sentient being s from suffering , there is pervasion that the mind of 
enlightenment is definitely generated  after that ; if that does not come first, the fully 
qualified generated mind indicated here would not be produced.  
 There is a purpose for designating it by the name  “seeking others’ welfare,” because it is 
for the sake of making known that “all the virtuous qualities included within the 
generated mind (that is the support for deeds) and the deeds in the continua of bodhisattvas 
                                                                                                                                                     
 
(48-51) changeable ment al factors, regret and so forth  
71 The aggregate of compositional factors is one of the five aggregates which make up a person. It contains 
all the mental factors, with the exception of feeling and discrimination.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
83 who seek others’ welfare  in that manner  will increase.”  The damage to fact is just that 
explanation above. 
 Explaining that the aspiration which seeks in this context is the seeking of enlightenment 
is a case of (i) not understanding even slightly the presentation of the stages of the path of the Perfection [Vehicle], and, (ii) having determined the basis for designation and the 
designated phenomenon here, not understanding what to do.  
 Therefore, to give a little description here, that one must realize emptiness prior to 
generating the mind [aspiring] towards supreme enlightenment applies to those of sharp faculty, from among the two types of trainees; there is no such certainty for thos e of dull 
faculty, but even they must become bearers of the essence of compassion  from the very time 
of having initially generated the mind. 
 Hence, for the sake of making known that the generated mind indicated here must 
definitely be preceded by great co mpassion, it is designated by the name “seeking others’ 
welfare.” If the generated mind induced by that fully qualified compassion is present, the desire to train in the deeds will arise naturally, and if that is present, all the virtuous qualities 
that are the causes of omniscience will increase; it is so designated also for the sake of 
making that known.  
 QUALM: You would have in your palm all phenomena if you were to have in your palm 
one phenomenon; what is the one [phenomenon]?  
RESPONSE: “Compassion.” Like it has been taught, “since it is important at the three —
beginning, middle and end —for those who desire to engage in the Mahayana  path, you 
should cultivate compassion at the beginning, and, without letting it degenerate, if you increase it more and mo re to full qualification, the generated mind and the deeds will 
become fully complete.” Understand that, for the sake of making that known to those who 
are skilled in the sequence of the Mahayana  path, the generated mind here is designated by 
the name “asp iration that seeks others’ welfare.” There is much to be expressed in 
dependence on this, but I will not elaborate more.

As one type, prayer seeks; it is the very desire for perfect complete enlightenment. 
Indicating the generated mind that acts together with it as that term is in order to make known that “in that manner, the mind possessing prayer is generated in bodhisattvas.”  
 
EXPLANATION

seek, it is also designated by the name “aspiration,” whereby the fault of asserting a 
common locus does not exist. The prayer (in which one has produced hope for the result) 
seeks; further, here  it is the very desire for perfect complete enlightenment. The 
subject —the generated mind —there is a reason for designating it by the name “aspiration 
that seeks enlightenment,” because it acts together with it by means of the five 
concomitances72. Indicating it as that term is purposeful, because it is in order to make

that they  are expressed briefly and extensively  in all the three —extensive, medium and

also indicated in the other two.

the Great Commentary explained that:

of Wisdom ] Sutra.

Twenty -Five Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom .

2B2C-1A2A -2A2B -2A. Explaining the two observed objects  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Perfect complete enlightenment and others’ welfare are also briefly and extensively indicated in the three types of Mothers  of the Victors together, without 
contradic ting the meaning of the Sutra , by way of statements that earnestly /intent  indicate 
fully: “the Perfection of Wisdom” and “regarding all —generosity and so forth —the act of 
generosity, the giver, the recipient, and so forth are to be established as not observed,” bearing the meaning  that causes understanding; and “ [those who desire to set ] all sentient 
beings  [in] nirvana  in accordance with the fortunes” and “those who desire to set the miserly 
and so forth in generosity and so forth, should accomplish this very Perfection of Wisdom” 
and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Since the observed objects of the Mahayana  generated mind are also indicated in the 
extensive and the brief, as in the middle Mother , the observed objects of the generated 
mind —perfect complete enlightenment and others’ welfare —are also briefly and 
extensively indicated in the three types of Mothers  of the Victors together , because they 
are also indicated without incompleteness in the two —the extensive and the brief—without 
contradicting , that is, agreeing with , the Twenty -Five Thousand Stanza [Perfection of 
Wisdom] Sutra , exactly in accordance with the meaning of the Sutra . It is not that there is 
no statement indicating that exact accordance in the two —the extensive and the brief —
because it is  indicated by: (i) the statement in the middle Mother  bearing the meaning that 
causes understanding  by having taken as the subject matter the meaning of enlightenment 
that is indicated in the brief indication and extensive explanation; (ii) the statement i n the 
extensive Mother that earnestly indicate s fully the meaning of the brief indication and 
extensive explanation on others’ welfare that was indicated in the middle Mother ; and (iii) 
the statement in the  Eight Thousand Stanza [Perfection of Wisdom Sutra ] that “Subhuti, a 
bodhisattva mahasattva is based upon the Perfection of Wisdom; be confident!” Understand 
that it is wrong to say that “This would become saying ‘It is indicated that there is no 
contradiction with the three Mothers together also in the t hree types of Mothers  of the 
Victors  together.’”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
86  
QUALM: How is the meaning of the middle Mother intent ly indicated?   
RESPONSE: The briefly indicated meaning of enlightenment is indicated by “ Sariputra , 
with respect to this, Bodhisattva Mahasattva, those who desire to attain clear complete  
enlightenment in all ways in regard to all phenomena should strive in the Perfection of Wisdom,” and the extensively explained meaning of enlightenment by “ Sariputra , with 
respect to this… regarding all the six perfectio ns—generosity and so forth —the act of 
generosity, the giver, the recipient, and so forth are to be established as not observed ” 
in the other two [ Mothers ]. Although this appears to be only appropriate as an explanation 
of the condensation of the varieties of words of the middle Mother , it suffices as a 
condensation of the varieties of meaning.  
 Clear complete  enlightenment in regard to all phenomena is the entity of enlightenment. 
The teachi ng that the method for [attaining] it is, in general, the training in the perfection of 
wisdom, is a brief indication by means of method; and teaching it to be training in all six perfections by means of being conjoined with the wisdom realizing emptiness is an 
extensive explanation by means of method.  
 Further, others’ welfare is briefly indicated by means of method in the middle Sutra,  by 
the passage: “Bodhisattva Mahasattva, all those sentient beings existing in the world 
systems numbering the grains of sand of the banks of the Ganges River of the lands of the 
ten directions, who desire to thoroughly  pass beyond sorrow in the sphere of nirvana that is 
without remainder of the aggregates, should train in the Perfection of Wisdom.” Condensing 
its meaning, i t is said, “ Those who desire [to set]  all sentient beings [in] nirvana  exactly 
in accordance with the fortunes of  the three lineage -bearers  [should accomplish  this very 
Perfection] of Wisdom.”   And others’ welfare is extensively explained by means of meth od with “Bodhisattvas 
[who desire to set] miserly sentient beings in generosity,” and so forth. Condensing its meaning, it is said, “Those who desire  to set the miserly and so forth in generosity and 
so forth should accomplish this very Perfection of Wisdo m” and so forth.  
 Someone, having taken the statement of intent  to refer to the treatise, explains that “they 
are briefly and extensively indicated by it.” These are words lacking relation, because it would become saying, “they are briefly and extensively indicated also in the treatise in the 
three Mothers together.” The explanation in the context of the classifications [of the 
generated mind] in the extensive explanation of others’ welfare and enlightenment is 
explained in that [treatise], but it is not th e meaning of this context. That the brief is 
explained in the words of the root text that indicate the definition, and the extensive in the words of the root text of the classification —is clearly a gibberish explanation. Others’ 
welfare is the nirvana in  others’ continua; the method for [attaining] that is indicated 
extensively and briefly.

88 2B2C -1A2A -2B3A -1. Extensive explanation  
COMMENTARY 
Those explained by [the root text] to be concomitant with:  
(1) aspiration, (2) attitude, (3) exceptional resolve, (4) application, (5) generosity, (6) ethics, 
(7) patience, (8) joyous effort, (9) concent ration, (10) wisdom, (11) skillful means, (12) 
prayer, (13) power, (14) the perfection of exalted wisdom, (15) clairvoyance, (16) merit and exalted wisdom, (17) dharmas in harmony with the class of enlightenment, (18) compassion 
and special insight, (19) retention  and confidence, (20) a festival of dharma, (21) the one 
path traveled, and (22) the truth body, are respectively like:  
 
(1) the earth, (2) fine gold, (3) the new moon, (4) fire, (5) a great treasure, (6) a jewel -mine, 
(7) a great ocean, (8) a vaj ra, (9) the king of mountains, (10) medicine, (11) a virtuous 
spiritual guide, (12) a wish -granting gem, (13) the sun, (14) a pleasant Dharma melody, (15) 
a great king, (16) a treasury, (17) a highway, (18) a mount, (19) a geyser, (20) a pleasant 
sound, (21) the flow of a river, and (22) a cloud, respectively through:  
 
(1) being a property that is the basis of all wholesome qualities, (2) unchanging until 
enlightenment, (3) fully increasing all virtuous qualities, (4) burning the firewood of 
obscurations t o the three exalted knowers of all, (5) satisfying all sentient beings, (6) being a 
property that is the support of precious excellent qualities, (7) being undisturbed by the 
descending of all undesirables, (8) through firm conviction, being inseparable, (9) being 
unmoved by distracting objects, (10) thoroughly pacifying the diseases of afflictive and 
knowledge obscurations, (11) not forsaking the welfare of sentient beings on any occasion, 
(12) accomplishing the results of wishes accordingly, (13) fully ri pening trainees, (14) 
teaching the Dharma that makes trainees aspire, (15) accomplishing others’ welfare through unobstructed might, (16) being like a treasury of many collections of merit and exalted wisdom, (17) being that which all aryas traveled and wi ll travel, (18) through not falling 
into either samsara or nirvana, progressing easily, (19) through retaining heard and unheard dharmas, inexhaustible, (20) pleasantly proclaiming to trainees who desire liberation, (21) 
activities of others’ welfare being  not different, and (22) being suitable to always display 
abiding in the abode of Tushita and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Those twenty -two generated minds explained by [the root text]:  
 
(1) The subject —the generated mind concomitant with aspiration—is like the  earth , 
because, through inducing all paths, it is a property  that is the basis of all wholesome 
qualities . 
 
(2) That concomitant with attitude is like fine gold , because it will not degenerate in 
attitude until enlightenment. The sutra passages for these [first two] are as in the brief 
indication and the extensive explanation on enlightenment. 
 
(3) That concomitant with extraordinary resolve is like the new moon  because it will  
increase  all virtuous qualities, such as the close placements of mindfulness, w ithout 
degeneration. Sutra says, “ Sariputra , a great bodhisattva should abide in the Mother, fully 
completing the thirty -seven harmonies with enlightenment.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
89  
(4) That concomitant with application  that cultivates the similitudes of the three exalted 
knower s is like fire , because it burns  the manifest firewood of the obscurations to the 
three exalted knowers of all. Sutra says “Due to desiring to pass through having viewed by 
knowing and seeing a knower of all.”  
 
(5) That concomitant with generosity is like a great treasure , because it satisfies all 
sentient beings with resources. Sutra says, “Those who desire to set miserly sentient beings 
in generosity…”  
 
(6) That concomitant with ethics is like a jewel -mine , because it acts as a property that 
supports prec ious excellent qualities . Sutra says, “… the immoral…”  
 
(7) That concomitant with patience is like a great ocean  because it is unperturbed  by 
the descent of all undesirables  of harm and suffering. Sutra says, “… those bearing 
harmful intent…”  
 
(8) That con comitant with joyous effort is like a vajra because, through firm conviction 
in the path, it is inseparable73 from the Mahayana . Sutra says, “… the lazy…”  
 
(9) That concomitant with concentration is like the king of mountains  because, through 
firm meditative stabilization, it is unmoved by distracting objects. Sutra says, “… those 
whose minds are distracted…”  
 (10) That concomitant with the perfection of wisdom is like medicine  because it 
thoroughly pacifies the diseases of af flictive and knowledge obscurations . Sutra says, 
“… those with corrupt wisdom…”  
 (11) That concomitant with skillful means is like a virtuous spiritual guide  because, 
through the four immeasurables, the method for not discarding sentient beings, it does no t 
forsake the welfare of sentient beings on any occasion . Sutra says, “… those who are 
skillful in the means of even just one virtuous generated mind…”  
 (12) That concomitant with prayer is like a wish -granting gem  because, through the five 
clairvoyances, the method for having power over results, it accomplishes the result of 
wishes accordingly . Sutra says, “Accomplishing enlightenment, the exalted body of a 
buddha…”  
 
(13) That concomitant with power is like the sun  because, through the four means of 
gathe ring [disciples], it fully ripens trainees . Sutra says, “Through the food, drinks, 
clothing, and the like of a great bodhisattva, the wishes in the minds of sentient beings are 
fully completed…”

(19) That concomitant with retention and confidence is like a geyser  that spouts water

it is inexhaustible . Sutra says, “That which has been taught, is being taught , and is to be 
taught by all the buddhas of the ten directions…” Retention causes one to hold, and 
confidence causes one not to know exhaustion.

light of the body darkness and what is dark among the worlds in the lands of each of the ten 
directions, sentient beings who are born there are caused to hear the sounds of the Thre e 
Jewels .”

through having attained the ability that subdues all trainees in common, the activities of 
others’ welfare are not different. Sutra says, “In the realms of th e infinite worlds in each 
of the ten directions, sentient beings who are blind, deaf, insane, naked, thirsty, hungry, and 
so forth…”

COMMENTARY  
In that way, through “Further, earth, gold, moon, fire,” and so forth, the twenty -two 
generated minds are explained.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Through the similes of “Further, earth, gold, moon, fire,” [1.2a]  and so forth, the 
twenty -two generated minds are explained  because, as explained above, [the twenty -two 
generated minds] are meanings that are objects to be characterized by similes and they are 
implied by means o f concordant property in that way.

that will enter into the first ground; (5 -14) the ten after that are included within the te n 
grounds, Very Joyous and so forth, and bear the objects of utilization of the paths of seeing and meditation; (15 -19) the five after those are included within the distinguished paths; and 
(20-22) the three generated minds after those are included within buddha ground by means 
of preparation, actual, and conclusion.  
 
EXPLANATION   
Regarding those, (1 -3) the first three  generated minds are included within the 
beginner's level , because they are respectively included within  the small, middling, and 
great path of accumulation themselves ; (4) the one  possessing preparation after those is 
included within the path that will enter into the first ground , because it is included 
within the Mahayana  path of preparation; (5 -14) the subject —the ten after that—are  
respect ively included within the ten grounds, Very Joyous and so forth , because they 
bear the objects of utilization of the paths of seeing and meditation  that are the entities 
of the ten grounds; (15 -19) the five —possessing the six clairvoyances and so forth —after 
those are included within the distinguished paths, because they are included within the 
three pure grounds; and (20- 22) the  last three generated minds after those are included 
within buddha ground,  because they are respectively included by means of the 
preparation, actual, and conclusion of buddha ground.

2B2C -1A2B -1A. Makin g a connection  
2B2C -1A2B -1B. Explaining the meaning  
2B2C -1A2B -1A. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY 
Having expressed the divisions incidentally, instructing in order that the bodhisattvas who 
have generated the initial mind of enlightenment and so forth in that manner, who seek in 
exact accordance at the time, generate the mind of enlightenment and accom plish the 
qualities propelled by it; and in order to strongly increase the excellent qualities attained for 
the purpose of completely safeguarding them: the instructions.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Since the divisions of the generated mind are a subsidiary topic of the  common topic, 
having expressed them incidental to the definition and the object s of observation taught, 
after that : the instructions. The subject —the receivers [of instructions], the bodhisattvas 
who have generated the three, the initial mind of enlighten ment and so forth in that 
manner —instructing  Mahayana  instructions [to them] by a supreme emanation body is 
purposeful, because it is  in order to generate the mind of enlightenment not attained 
before  and to accomplish the qualities propelled by it, such a s calm abiding and special 
insight  included within perfect application , and in order to strongly increase the excellent 
qualities  previously  attained for the purpose of completely safeguarding them  without 
degeneration.  
 [The explanation above] is in terms  of those who have obtained the meditative 
stabilization of the stream of Dharma from the great level of the path of accumulation, who 
are pervaded by being able to directly hear instructions from a supreme emanation body 
because, in general, if even those  who have not entered a path can directly hear Mahayana  
instructions from [a supreme emanation body], what need is there to mention about [those who can directly hear Mahayana  instructions from a supreme emanation body] existing 
from the small level of the  path of accumulation? The special receivers [of instructions] are 
bodhisattvas who seek in exact accordance at the time  of hearing the instructions . 
 The cause of hearing instructions, the meditative stabilization of the stream of Dharma, is 
a meditative stabilization that is a union of calm abiding and special insight  that has 
generated a special ability to directly hear instructions from a supreme emanation body, due to having purified most of the coarse obscurations to directly seeing the buddhas and 
hearing the Dharma from them by those on lower paths. Its causes are having collected the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
93 accumulations for many eons, pure view and ethics, possessing lineage , much hearing and 
having abandoned most of the four hindrances of lineage75.

2B2C -1A2B -1B1A. Root text  
ROOT TE XT  
1.4 The achievings, the truths, 
The Three Jewels, Buddha and so forth,  
Non-adherence, tirelessness, 
Thoroughly upholding the path,  
 
1.5 The five eyes, the six excellent qualities of 
Clairvoyances, and the path of seeing and  
That called “meditation”: instructions are to be  
Known as the nature of ten. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —Mahayana  instructions —are to be known as the nature of ten, because 
they range from the entity of the achievings themselves through the instructions on the path 
of meditation. From  between the two —the supports of the achievings and the supported —
the former is instructions on the Three Jewels, Buddha and so forth , while regarding the 
latter, the bases for the attributes and the attributes.  
 With respect to the first [the bases for th e attributes], the instructions on the aspects and 
the objects of observation are instructions on the achievings  and the truths . With respect to 
the attributes, there are instructions on eliminating discordant classes and instructions on 
achieving excellen t qualities. With respect to eliminating the manifest [discordant classes], 
the instructions on the causes of stabilizing, increasing and not degenerating the achievings are the instructions on non -adherence, tirelessness,  and thoroughly upholding the 
Maha yana  path. With respect to achieving [excellent qualities], the instructions on the 
causes of engaging independently and quickly completing the result are the instructions on 
the five eyes  and the six excellent qualities of clairvoyances . To eliminate the seeds that 
are objects of abandonment, there are instructions on the path of seeing  and path of  
meditation .

COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding achieving the divisions  of the mind of enlightenment 
which were explained, instructing on achieving training: "Through not passing beyond the 
conventional76 and ultimate truths, not in common with the hearers and so forth, one should 
engage in a manner of not observing."  
 
EXPLANATION   
Instructing on achieving.  Achieving what? Instructing on achieving the d ivisions  of the 
attitude, the mind of enlightenment, and the applications propelled by that, generosity and 
so forth, which were explained  previously.  
 How are they achieved?  Because of instructing by way of  training : "One should 
engage  through (i) the practice not passing beyond the stages of the path based upon the 
conventional  truth s, having established by valid cognition the achiever, the person who is 
the support, the objects to be practiced, the results, and so forth as they have been explained , 
and, (ii) the practice [not passing beyond] the stages of the path based upon the ultimate 
truth s, by realizing all of those to be empty of true existence, in a manner of not 
observing the four qualities of practice with respect to the Mothers , for the s ake of all 
sentient beings’ accomplishing nirvana and the truth body not in common with the hearers 
and so forth." If the meaning of not passing beyond the mode of realizing the conventional  
to be false and illusory is to be explained, you should know it t o be as before. 
 “Sariputra , at the time when a bodhisattva mahasattva practices the Perfection of 
Wisdom, while the bodhisattva himself exists, one does not perfectly subsequently see the 
bodhisattva …” indicates the instructions on the entity of the achie vings themselves, because 
they are  instructions to achieve without  passing beyond the stages of the path based upon 
the two truths with respect to the generated mind and the phenomena indicated on that 
occasion.  “Sariputra , if in77 this Jambudvipa, Bhiksu Sariputra …” indicates the 
instructions on the uncommonness of the entity of the achievings themselves, because they 
are instructions to not take to mind Hinayana  for the welfare of oneself alone.

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
95 2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1A1. Definitions  
The definition of an ultimate truth  is: an object to be realized by way of vanished dualistic 
appearance  (by a direct valid cognizer explicitly realizing it) that is not to be realized in the 
aspect of an affirmative phenomenon.  
 The definition of an obscurational truth78 is: that which abid es in the class of objects to be 
realized by way of being together with dualistic appearance by a valid cognizer explicitly 
realizing it.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1A2. Meaning of the names  
Due to abiding in the perspective of an unmistaken, excellent object -possesso r, an arya’s 
meditative equipoise, it is called “ultimate truth” [literally, excellent -meaning truth].  
 Due to being a truth  in the perspective of an erroneous object -possessor, an awareness 
that is a mind79 of conventionalities, but a falsity with respect  to measure of abidance, it is 
called “obscurational truth.” Further, it abides in the perspective of this obscurational awareness that obscures the explicit seeing of suchness, such as a sense consciousness. 
Hence, the terms “an obscurational awareness” a nd “a truth in its perspective” are 
explained.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1A3. Divisions of the bases of  characterization  
Besides mere dissimilar ways of expressing an ultimate truth, divisions that are made by way of entity do not exist. Regarding the ultimate, categ orical  ultimates are inferential 
reasoning consciousnesses, w hile non -categorical ultimates are the aryas’ meditative 
equipoise which sees the ultimate.  Know that when obscurational truth is divided, also there are two real and unreal . 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1A4.  Investigating one and different  
If the two truths were to be one, there would be the four faults taught in Sutra Unraveling 
the Thought ( Samdhinirmocanasutra ), but even if they were to be different entities, there 
would be the four faults taught in that. Hence they are one entity but different isolates.80 
 Just understand the other explanations in the commentaries of the Acarya and the Arya in 
relation to this point. With respect to an eighth -grounder, if the meaning of “by stopping 
nirvana,” which bears an ulterior intention , is to be slightly explained: in relation to the 
generated mind, “with respect to a knower of all, passing through, having  viewed by seeing” 
was explain ed, and “due to desiring to pass beyond the levels of a youth” was taught.  
 Therefo re, from among the two —(1) passing through, having seen, and (2) passing 
beyond, having abided—the first is passing through the hearers’ and solitary realizers’ 
grounds by a bodhisattva on the occasion of the path of seeing, having viewed them by 
knowing and seeing. The second refers to an irreversible eighth -grounder; the meaning of 
passing beyond that is: in the manner of examining a possibility on the eighth ground, if one is not roused by the buddhas, due to immediately severing the continuity of migrat ing 
beings’ welfare, one will enter absorption in the uncontaminated sphere; but, due to being 
                                             
 
78 Although usually translated as “conventional truth,” here it is given a literal rendition so as to bring out its 
etymology.  
79 Bsam pa  
80 The Tibetan word " ldog pa" translated here as "isolate" literally means "reverse" and indicates a double 
reverse or dou ble negation. For more information on this topic, see Daniel E. Perdue's Debate in Tibetan 
Buddhism  (Snow Lion Publications, 1992).  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
96 roused by the buddhas, even if one enters absorption continuously on the eighth ground, one 
passes beyond actualizing it. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B. Instructions on the objects of observation of those [achievings]  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1. Meaning of the g eneral ity  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2. Meaning of the branches  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1. Meaning of the g eneral ity 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1A. Definite number of the four truths  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1 B. Definite sequence 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1C. Bases of  characterization  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D. Valid cognizers which ascertain those 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1A. Definite number of the four truths  
The principal objects to be determined by those seeking liberation are definite as the two 
classes which are included within samsara and nirvana. With respect to samsara, they are 
also definite as two: (1) the cause that binds, true origins, and (2) the result that is bondage, 
true sufferings. With respect to the completely p ure, they are also definite as two: (1) the 
cause that is the method liberating from samsara, true paths, and (2) the entity of the result that is liberation, true cessations. Hence, the causes and results of entering into and turning 
away from samsara are  definite as four.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1B. Definite sequence 
In terms of the order in reality, true origins and paths are objects to be indicated first, and 
sufferings and cessations are objects to be indicated later. Here they are to be indicated 
according t o another sequence corresponding to the order of the object -possessor clear 
realizations, in this manner, that is —true sufferings, origins, cessations and paths. You must 
at the outset generate an awareness that wishes to be separated from the general and 
particular drawbacks of samsara by means of having contemplated them well because, 
without being preceded by that, it will be impossible to generate the desire to abandon their causes.  
  Once the wish to be separated from sufferings has been generated, you  investigate the 
origins, asking: “Am I able or not able to separate from these? Do these have causes or not? 
Further, are the causes permanent or impermanent?” For, you see that you cannot abandon 
the entity of suffering itself in the way one pulls  out a thorn [embedded in one’s flesh]. If 
you were to investigate in this manner, then by comprehending that the two —karma and 
afflictions —are its causes, you will generate the mind wishing to abandon them. Thus the 
origins are indicated second. Once you see tha t you are able to abandon the origins, you will 
see that you can put a stop to suffering. Then, since you will see that you can actualize the 
cessations in which suffering has been stopped, the cessations are indicated third. Since you 
will see that actual izing those is contingent on having relied on and familiarized with the 
paths, the true paths are indicated fourth.  
 
QUALM: Since first seeing that one is able to stop suffering is pervaded by seeing that one 
is able to actualize the cessation of sufferin gs, cessations should be indicated next. Further, 
having seen that one is able to abandon origins, one must see that one is able to stop sufferings, whereby origins should be indicated before sufferings. Since seeing that one is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
97 able to abandon origins is contingent upon realizing selflessness, that itself should be 
indicated at the beginning. Hence the sequence of the Four Truths explained is not definite. 
 
RESPONSE: I shall explain. In general, for any activity, embarking upon it is contingent 
upon seeing its benefits, and turning away from it upon seeing its drawbacks. Hence, by 
having contemplated the general and particular drawbacks of samsara, having see n that 
samsara is like a fire pit, one will generate at first a mere awareness that desires to separate from it; one does not understand that one is able to separate from it. Then, secondly, one 
sees with valid cognition that one is able to abandon origins . Once that is seen, one will see 
that one is able to actualize cessations, whereby it is appropriate for origins to be indicated second, and cessations third. That paths are to be indicated last is as [explained] above. 
Therefore when a path of seeing obs erving the Four Truths is generated in a yogi’s 
continuum, it is also generated in agreement with that sequence.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1C. Bases of  characterization  
Present feelings of pain in the kidneys and the like are suffering of suffering. Feelings relishing tastes due to excessive craving for pleasant food are suffering of change. That 
which is a contaminated aggregate that is an imprint deposited by karma and afflictions is 
all-compounded suffering. All of these are true sufferings. All contaminated ka rma and 
afflictions are true origins. A separation in which sufferings have been stopped is a true cessation. A wisdom directly realizing selflessness is a true path.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D. Valid cognizers which ascertain those 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D -1. Identif ying the sixteen wrong conceptions  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D -2. Identifying the sixteen aspects of the truths contradicting those  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D -1. Identifying the sixteen wrong conceptions  
• The wrong engagements with respect to sufferings are the four apprehensions of 
cleanness , happiness, permanence and self.  
• The wrong engagements with respect to origins are the two —the apprehension of 
sufferings as causeless, and the apprehension of the di scordant as causes. With respect to 
the latter, there are: the apprehension of [suffering] as being produced by only a single 
cause; the apprehension of [suffering] as being produced due to others, such as Isvara, 
first having an idea; and the apprehension  of the nature as permanent, but its states 
changeable.  
• The wrong engagements with respect to cessations are: the apprehension of liberation as 
utterly not existent, the apprehension of some contaminated attribute as liberation, the 
apprehension of some a ttribute of suffering as sublime liberation, and the apprehension 
of the exhaustion of suffering as existing, but reversible.  
• The wrong engagements with respect to paths are: the apprehension of the path to 
liberation as utterly not existent, the apprehen sion of the familiarization of selflessness 
as being unsuitable to be a path, the apprehension of merely some attribute of concentration as the path to liberation, and the apprehension of a path (that acts as a 
bearer of the quality of overcoming suffering ) as not existent. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B1D -2. Identifying the sixteen aspects of the truths contradicting those  
The subject —the suffering of suffering —is: 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
98 (1) impermanent, because it is produced intermittently;  
(2) suffering, because it is under the control  of karma and afflictions;  
(3) empty, because it is devoid of a supervisory self that is a different entity, and  
(4) selfless, because it is not established as an entity of independent self.   
 
The aspects of origins are as follows. The subject —contaminated  karma and craving —are:  
(1) the aspect of cause, because they are the root of suffering, its result;  
(2) origin, because they produce all types  of that [suffering] again and again; and  
(3) strong production, because they produce them forcefully.  
The subjec t—craving for [contaminated ] existence —is:  
(4) the aspect of condition, because it is the cooperative condition of suffering, its result.  
 The aspects of cessations are as follows. The subject —the separation that is an utter 
exhaustion of suffering throug h the force of antidotes —is:  
(1) the aspect of cessation, because it is a separation in which suffering has been abandoned;  
(2) the aspect of pacification, because it is a separation in which afflictions have been 
abandoned;  
(3) the aspect of sublimeness , because it is a liberation that is benefit and happiness; and  
(4) the aspect of definite emergence, because it is a liberation that bears the quality of 
irreversibility. 
 
The aspects of paths are as follows. The subject —the wisdom directly realizing 
selflessness —is: 
(1) the aspect of path, because it is a path that proceeds to liberation;  
(2) the aspect of suitability, because it acts as the direct antidote to afflictions;  
(3) the aspect of achievement, because it is an exalted wisdom  directly realizing the final 
mode of abidance of the mind;
81 and  
(4) the aspect of deliverance , because it is an antidote that  causes one to have the quality  of 
sufferin g being not returnable . 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2. Meaning of the branches  
The first of four:  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2A . Instructions regarding true sufferings  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding suffering, “The emptiness of forms and so forth that are results and the 
Perfection of Wisdom are one nature in the entity of thusness.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —“Sariputra , with regard to this, when a bodhisattva mahasattva strives with 
respect to the emptiness of forms, he strives…” —are instructions for the sake of stopping 
adherence to objects to be known as truly existent regarding suffering, because they are 
instructions that make known tha t “The emptiness of contaminated forms and so forth 
that are results, and the Perfection of Wisdom directly realizing that  [emptiness]  are one 
nature in the entity of thusness , the emptiness of true existence.”  
                                              
81 Compare this with the presentation in “ 2B2C -1B2B -1A3A -3. The aspects of true paths.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
99 2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2B. Instructions regarding t rue origins  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding origins, as emptiness and forms and so forth that are causes  are just not different, 
“Forms and so forth are not bearers of the qualities of origination, cessation, the thoroughly 
afflicted or the completely pure.”  
 
EXPLANA TION  
“Do not perfectly subsequently see forms as bearing the quality of production or bearing the 
quality of cessation…” are instructions regarding origins,  because they instruct that 
“Forms and so forth  included within  contaminated causes  do not newly  exist truly as 
bearers of the qualities of origination, cessation, the thoroughly afflicted or the 
completely pure.” They do not exist truly as those, as emptiness and forms and so forth 
that are causes are just not different ultimately. This is for the sake of refuting true 
existence with respect to origins, the objects to be  abandon ed.   
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2C. Instructions regarding true cessations  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding cessations, “emptiness is free from production and cessation, the thoroughly 
afflicted and the completely pure, decrease and increase and so forth; from form does not 
exist, production of ignorance does not exist, cessation of ignorance does not exist, buddha 
does not exist, until enlightenment does not exist.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Sariputra , emptiness is not produced, not ceased, not thoroughly afflicted, and not 
completely pure,” are instructions regarding cessations, because they instruct that 
“emptiness , the freedom from adventitious stains, is free from truly existent characteristics 
of compounded phenomena, production and cessation, the thoroughly afflicted and the 
completely pure  objects of abandonment and antidotes , occasions of decrease an d 
increase , causes and results, and so forth; with respect to it, truly existent characterizing 
attributes are never established anywhere.”  It follows that true existence is never established 
anywhere because, from a generality of the thoroughly afflicted,  form , does not exist ; a 
particularity  [of the thoroughly afflicted ], production of ignorance, does not exist; a 
generality of the  completely pure, cessation of ignorance, does not exist; a particularity  [of 
the completely pure ], the realization buddha , does not exist; until the abandonment 
enlightenment does not exist ultimately. This is for the sake of refuting true existence with 
respect to cessations, the objects to be actualized. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -1B2D. Instructions regarding true paths  
COMMENTARY 
Regardi ng paths, “The perfection of generosity and so forth, oneself or inner emptiness and 
so forth, outer emptiness and so forth, the past limit and the future limit —mutually 
possessing all and established as just not being not possessed.” Indicating [all the a bove four 
statements] instructs about the truths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Sariputra , when a great bodhisattva practices the Perfection of Wisdom, he does not 
perfectly subsequently see himself ‘striving’ or ‘not striving’ in generosity…” are 
instructions regarding path,  because they instruct that “The perfection of generosity and 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
100 so forth, the bodhisattva himself, the object of cultivation and that which cultivates are not 
truly existent, whereby they are not possessed; the negation is not truly existent, whereby it  
is not that they are not possessed. Realize that.”  
 “Regarding emptiness, one does not practice by way of emptiness; it is a yoga path by 
way of emptiness…” are instructions regarding  suitability and achievement , because they 
instruct that “(i) The medita tive stabilizations that takes the three —inner emptiness and so 
forth —as objects, and (ii) the three —the emptiness of outer objects and so forth— do not 
exist truly as objects and object -possessors. That negation itself also does not exist truly.”  
 “With re spect to forms, one does not and will not practice the future  limit…” are  
instructions  regarding  deliverance , because they instruct that “mutually possessing the 
past limit, samsara  and the future  limit,  nirvana (where one definitely emerges from and  
where  one definitely emerges to) is not truly existent , and since that very absence  of true 
existence is also not truly existent, they are  established as just not being not possessed.  
These are instructions for the sake of stopping adherence to paths, the objects to be relied 
upon, as truly existent.

2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -2A1. Identifying the uncommon Three Jewels  
[Maitreya’s] Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum  (Mahayanottaratantrasastra ) 
[1.5] says:  
 
Uncompounded, spontaneous,  Not realized by others’ conditions,  
Possessing knowledge, loving -kindness,  and ability  
Is the very Buddha possessing the two welfares.  
 
Like it has been said, that is the Buddha Jewel bearing the nature of the three exalted bodies, 
the fulfillment of the two welfares. Further [1.10]:  
 
That which is inconceivable, without the two, without conception, Pure, clear, and of the antidotal class,  
That which is and that by which there is separation from attachment;  
That bearer of the characteristics of the two truths is the Dharma. 
 
Like it has been said, that is the Dharma Jewel bearing the characteristics of the two truths 
of the completely pure [class]. Also [1.14]:  
 
Because of pure inner exalted wisdoms  
Seeing modes and varieties,  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
101 The assembly of irreversible intelligent ones  
Just possesses  unsurpassa ble excellent qualities.  
 
Like it has been said, that is the Sangha Jewel who are irreversible aryas possessing the 
excellent qualities of knowledge and liberation. I will not elaborate more beyond this. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -2A2. General presentation of refuge  
Furthermore, it is like it has been taught in that [Maitreya’s Mahayana Treatise on the 
Sublime Continuum ( Mahayanottaratantrasastra ), 1.19]:  
 
Due to the teacher, the teaching and the disciples,   
In terms of the three vehicles and  
Those who believe in th e three takings,  
The three refuges are presented.  
 
Here, in general, the purpose of presentating the three refuges in the scriptures is indicated. 
In terms of: (i) the buddha that is an object to be attained in the continuum of Mahayana  
lineage -bearers who  have entered a path, which should be known as a resultant refuge, and 
the buddha that has already been accomplished, which should be known as a causal refuge; 
(ii) the causal refuges of the other two lineage -bearers; and (iii) those who believe in taking 
the buddhas to be supreme, such as making offerings to them and honoring them, even 
though they have not entered the path —the Buddha Jewel is presented as a refuge.  
 In terms of: (i) the resultant refuge of solitary realizer lineage -bearers  who have entered a 
path, those who seek in their continua nirvana without relying on others’ sounds, at the time 
of the last existence and , (ii) those who believe in taking the excellent Dharma to be 
supreme, even though they have not entered the path —the Dharma Je wel is presented as a 
refuge.  
 In terms of: (i) the resultant refuge of hearer lineage -bearers  who have entered a path , 
those who seek nirvana that is attained through following others’ sounds at the time of the last existence, and (ii) those who believe in taking the Sangha to be supreme, even though 
they have not entered the path —the Sangha Jewel is presented as a refuge.  
 Even though one may not seek them as objects to be attained in one’s continuum, 
understand that even the Dharma and Sangha Jewel for w hom they have become refuges are 
causal refuges upon which one relies. In dependence on the presentation of the three Mahayana  uncommon refuges above, the three causal refuges and the two resultant refuges 
of the other two lineage -bearers will also become understood. However, this indicates the 
purpose of the general presentation of the three refuges in the scriptures. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -2A3. Difference between conventional and ultimate refuge   
That [Maitreya’s Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum  
(Mahayanottaratantrasastra ), 1.20] says:  
 
Because it will be abandoned, because it bears the quality of being deceptive , 
Because of absence and because it is together with fear, The two types of Dharma and the assembly of aryas  
Are not supreme, everlasting refuges. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
102 Regarding that, the meanings of ultimate and conventional refuge are as follows. The 
refuge, as in the case of those definite in the hearer lineage, that —(i) in the perspective of its 
attitude, seeks merely the pacification of the suffering of cy clic existence, and holds that it 
is not necessary to proceed beyond that, and (ii) in actuality, does not complete the 
traversing of the path —is the meaning of a conventional refuge. That which —(i) in the 
perspective of attitude, holds to completing the t raversing [of the path], and (ii) in actuality 
completes it —is the meaning of an ultimate refuge; there is no person who wishes to know 
the proof of the scriptural Dharma and the like as not being the final truth body, whereby it is only inappropriate . Hen ce, even what is a Mahayana  scriptural Dharma is not a final 
refuge; it is merely an object to be relied upon as a method for clearly realizing the meaning. Once the final clear realization of the meaning has been attained, since it is not necessary as 
a method for attaining that which is not yet attained, it is only an object to be abandoned  
like a boat. Further, the general conclusion here is a branch of establishing the pervasion; it 
is not the principal meaning. 
 A wisdom directly realizing selflessness  in the continuum of a bodhisattva arya (i) has 
not exhausted all stains, even though it appears as though it has separated from all stains in the perspective of seeing, and (ii) has not exhausted all stains in entity, even though the 
pollution of stains h as not entered its entity, whereby it bears the quality of being 
deceptive , a falsity, the combination of appearing to be that and being empty of that. Hence, 
it is indicated to not be an ultimate refuge. Having said that, know that the assertion —that 
this indicates that a compounded phenomenon is pervaded by not being an ultimate 
refuge —is a vile wrong conception that denies that a buddha’s true paths and two types of 
form bodies are ultimate refuges; it should be cast faraway.    Also, with respect to the  modes of the hearers’ vehicle, a hearer’s nirvana without 
remainder is not an ultimate refuge, because it is strongly distinguished by a mere absence  
of suffering s and origins [of sufferings] . That implies that the fear that is  the obscurations to 
knowledge has not been exhausted ; some ignoramus, having discarded the timely occasion 
to state a proof for that not being an ultimate refuge in our system, formulated a 
consequence and explained that the sign and the predicate are assertions of those of hearer 
schools. Another explained that it means non -existence at the final time , stated a non -
affirming negative as the reason, and so forth. Their making presentations despite not 
having realized the meaning will cause the Buddha’s teachings to become stained. T hus, it 
will be marvelous to ignore them.   The mode of the hearers is a  mode of vehicle accepted  even in our system; it is not a  
mode of assertion of lower tenets. The analogy of a butter- lamp that has died out means that 
when a hearer has entered the sph ere of peace, even appearances in which dissimilar 
meanings are slightly observed have vanished; it does not mean that the continuity of matter and cognition has been severed, as is asserted by some Buddhist schools. Even the 
compounded signs of a hearer foe -destroyer is together with the fear that is the knowledge 
obscurations, as if the knife of a executioner has been raised. 
 Therefore, [Maitreya’s Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum  
(Mahayanottaratantrasastra ), 1.21] says:  
 
Ultimately, the refuge  of migrating beings  
Is solely the Buddha,  
Because the Muni bears the truth body;  
Because [He] is also the culmination of the assembly. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
103  
Like it has been said, only the Buddha who bears the nature of the three exalted bodies is an 
ultimate refuge.  
2B2C -1A2B-1B1B -2A4. How to go for refuge   
Having understood well the excellent qualities of the Three Jewels and the stages to be trained in of one who has gone for refuge in them that have been taught in [Asanga's] 
Compendium of Ascertainments (Viniscaya -samgr ahani) and so forth, you yourself should 
contemplate well upon the shortcomings of samsara in general and the shortcomings of the bad migrations in particular, in dependence on the two causes —(1) recollecting the fear, and 
(2) knowing that the Three Jewels  have the ability to protect from the fear. Then tame your 
mind [by] going for refuge such that you entrust yourself in the Three Jewels from the 
bottom of your heart, after which train properly in the trainings of refuge.  
 In that manner, by having entere d into the exalted compassion of the v ictors, your 
excellent qualities will increase more and more, whereby you will achieve the essence with 
regard to the support of leisures and endowments; professing to make effort in hearing, 
contemplation and meditati on without being able to tame your mind [by] going for refuge 
will bring very little result. What is explicitly indicated here is a presentation of the three Mahayana  uncommon refuges. The objects of going for refuge will be indicated below with 
“Faith obs erving the buddhas and so forth” [4.33a].

2B2C -1A2B -1B1B-2B1. Instructions on the Buddha Jewel  
COMMENTARY 
Concerning buddha, since buddha and enlightenment are just the same character, with 
regard to not observing the exalted knower of all aspects, a defining quality that acts to 
[characterize] buddha, by not  connecting forms and so forth, “know the equality between 
the object of observation and the observer,” and…  
 
EXPLANATION   
“A bodhisattva, at the time of practicing the Perfection of Wisdom, does not act to connect 
an exalted knower of all aspects to form ; he does not perfectly, subsequently see form 
itself…” are instructions for the sake of abandoning adherence to the Buddha as truly existent, because they instruct that “a final exalted wisdom knowing the equality between  
the object of observation and the observer in being empty of true existence is the 
Buddha Jewel .” That follows, because they instruct that “ with regard to not observing an 
exalted knower of all aspects as [existing] ultimately, there is no object to be adh ered to, 
by not connecting and  connecting  forms and so forth— object and object -possessor, 
definition and definiendum, and sphere and cognizer—ultimately.”  
 Instructions on an exalted knower of all aspects will serve as instructions on the buddha, 
because a n exalted knower of all aspects is a defining quality that acts to characterize 
buddha. Do not observe an exalted knower of all aspects as [existing] ultimately, since 
buddha and enlightenment possessing the two purities are just the same character  in 
being empty of true existence .

104 2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -2B2. Instructions on the Dharma Jewel  
COMMENTARY 
Concerning Dharma, “All phenomena which are included within those including all bases, 
antidotes, and aspects included within the three exalted knowers of all have n o entitiness,” 
and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Do not connect, saying ‘The thing form exists;’ do not connect, saying ‘Things do not 
exist’…” indicates instructions on the Dharma,  because it instructs that “All phenomena 
which are included and summed up within those including all instances82 that are bases, 
antidotes, and aspects included as objects within the three exalted knowers of all have 
no entitiness  ultimately.”  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -2B3. Instructions on the Sangha Jewel  
COMMENTARY 
Concerning sangha, excluding the foe -destroyer who is included within the Buddha Jewel, 
the eight: the seven great beings, through instances of abiders in the result and approachers, together with the solitary realizers; through instances of the dull -faculty and so forth, 
complete in number a s twenty. “Engage the irreversible bodhisattva arya learners as just not 
produced.” Imparting these passages are instructions on the Three Jewels. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Imparting these passages, “Engage the irreversible bodhisattva arya learners as just 
not produce d ultimately,” are instructions on the sangha, because they are instructions for 
the sake of stopping adherence to the Sangha Jewel as truly existent companions who 
practice the path . 
 
QUALM: Further, how many types of that Sangha are there?  
RESPONSE: The subject—the eight: the seven great beings , through  instances of three 
abiders in the result and  four approachers, together with the solitary realizers83—are 
suitable to be divided into many because, through instances of the dull -faculty and so 
forth , there are twenty in number  explained. 
 QUALM: Do [they] not become the eight approachers and abiders?  
RESPONSE: The Mahayana foe- destroyer  is excluded  from the Sangha Jewel of this 
context, since it is included within the Buddha Jewel . It is for the sake of stopping 
adherence to the Three Jewels as refuges ultimately.

105 2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3A1. Instructions on the causes of not dege nerating the achievings  
 COMMENTARY  
Since some will adhere to being diligent  towards the meaning of what was explained by just 
having initiated  joyous effort, due to a mere agreeable state of body and so forth, 
instructions on not adhering84 which indicate  that the body and so forth are devoid of  
entitiness, and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Great bodhisattva, the practice of the Perfection of Wisdom with body, speech and 
mind…” are instructions on not adhering  to negative activities of the three doors, because 
they are instructions  which  indicate  “Realize that the body and so forth are devoid of  
entitiness  ultimately.” It is appropriate to instruct in that manner because, since some of 
dull-faculty will adhere to diligently  practicing  the meaning of what was explained on 
the occasion of the generated mind previously  by just having initiated  joyous effort  
through application (if they ha ve not familiarized with the meaning of emptiness), it must be 
abandoned. Owing to what  cause [will they adhere]? It will be produced due t o a mere 
agreeable state of body and so forth attached to ordinary activities .

…although one has familiarized for a long time, if the desired aim is not accomplished, since those bearing fearful natures will become thoroughly upset, the instructions on the 
absence  of weariness
85 that indicate that clinging to form and so forth until perfect complete 
enlightenment is merely not existent, and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
 “Great bodhisattva, at the time of practicing the six perfections, do not cling to forms; do 
not cling to feelings…” are instructions on the absence  of weariness  at the time of 
pract icing, because they are instructions that  indicate  “Realize that clinging to form and 
so forth until  perfect complete enlightenment as  truly existent  is merely not existent.” It 
is appropriate to instruct in that manner, because although one has familiariz ed with the 
achievings for a long time, if the desired aim is not accomplished accordingly , since 
some of  dull- faculty  with fearful natures will become thoroughly upset, it must be 
eliminated.

“Sariputra , a great bodhisattva possessing such exalted wisdom, as many grains of sand on

the buddhas who reside in the ten directions and so forth, since , thinking “How can I be 
able to do that?”, the mind will become discouraged , it must be eliminated.
