2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3B1. Instructions on the five eyes: the causes of indep endently engaging in the achievings  
COMMENTARY 
…teaching that “(1) An eye of flesh, (2) a divine eye, which arose from maturation, (3) a wisdom eye, (4) a dharma eye, and (5) a buddha eye —respectively , the object -possessor 
that (1) ascertains things individually, (2) [knows] the death -transference and birth of all 
sentient beings, (3) does not conceptualize with respect to all phenomena, (4) realizes all 
arya persons, and (5) is clearly and  completely enlightened in all aspects of all 
phenomena —are established as merely one in thusness” instructs on the five eyes, and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
Based upon “Sariputra , a bodhisattva mahasattva sees a hundred yojanas87 with an eye of 
flesh…,” instructions on the five e yes are indicated, because they teach that “ The objects 
and object- possessors of the five eyes  are established as merely one  taste in thusness.”  
Having indicated the ability and power to see the objects of the five eyes conventionally, 
their absence  of nat ure ultimately is indicated. 
 With regard to them, respectively, (1) an eye of flesh is an object- possessor that 
ascertains things individually,  seeing subtle and coarse forms in the billionfold world 
systems ina hundred yojanas;  (2) a divine eye, which ar ose from maturation  of previous 
virtuous contaminated karma, is an object- possessor that knows exactly  the death -
transference and birth of all sentient beings; (3) a wisdom eye  is an object- possessor 
that does not conceptualize  true existence with respect to all phenomena, directly  
realizing the absence  of true existence;  (4) a dharma eye  is an object- possessor that 
realizes all arya persons  of sharp and dull faculties;  and (5) a buddha eye  is an object-
possessor that is manifestly, completely enlightened i n all aspects of all phenomena, 
respectively . 
 (1) An eye of flesh exists from the path of accumulation, (2) a divine eye from the path of 
preparation, (3) a wisdom eye from the path of seeing, (4) a dharma eye from the 
subsequent attainment of the path of seeing, and (5) a buddha eye from the buddha ground, 
its similitude existing from the eighth ground.  
 If you were to possess these five eyes:  
• with the eye of flesh, you would know exactly where the spiritual guides teaching 
Dharma, your disciples, and so forth are abiding within the billionfold world systems;  
                                              
87 An Indian measure of distance, the yojana is about 7  km. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
107 • with the second, [you would know] where your disciples die, transfer and are reborn;  
• with the third, you would directly know the meaning of the mode of abidance, the method 
for subduing trainees. 
• with the fourth, you would know well the particularities of the faculties of the trainees 
who are objects to be assisted and taught the Dharma; and  
• even when having merely attained a similitude of the fifth, through knowing most of the 
varieties of objects of knowledge, you would be urged from within towards the 
achievings and would independently engage in the achievings without dependency upon 
others. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3B2. Instructions on the six clairvoyances: the causes of quickly completing the achievings  
COMMENTARY 
… indicating that “The clairvoyances of (1) magical emanation, (2) divine ear, (3) knowing others’ minds, (4) recalling previous states, (5) divine eye that arises from manifest 
compositional action, and (6) knowing the extinction of contamination s: (1) moves the earth 
and so forth, (2) hears faint and other sounds abiding in all the world systems, (3) thoroughly knows others’ minds, such as those with attachment, (4) recalls many past lives 
of oneself and others, (5) sees all forms, and (6) abando ns afflictive and knowledge 
obscurations —are to be realized as merely pacified from the beginning,” instructs on the six 
clairvoyances, and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
“A great bodhisattva who practices the Perfection of Wisdom will attain the perfection of clairvoyance ; he experiences many types of magical emanation…” instructs on the six 
clairvoyances, because it instructs by way of indicating how the six clairvoyances arise in the continuum conventionally and that they “ are to be realized as merely pacified from 
the b eginning  ultimately .” 
 With regard to them:  
• the clairvoyance of magical emanation  possesses special power, because it is that 
concomitant with meditative stabilization and wisdom which is able to move the earth , 
transform into one, emanate into many, and so forth ; apply similarly to those below:  
 
• the clairvoyance of divine ear  possesses special power, because it is that 
concomitant with meditative stabilization and wisdom , which has as its empowering 
condition a transparent physical material ( included in t he level of equipoise arisen 
through the force of meditation ) that hears faint and other loud sounds abiding in all the 
world systems ; 
• the clairvoyance knowing others’ minds  [possesses special power, because] it is 
that concomitant with meditative stabiliz ation and wisdom which directly, thoroughly  
knows others’ minds, such as those  free from being with  attachment ; 
• the clairvoyance recalling previous states  [possesses special power, because it is 
that concomitant with meditative stabilization and wisdom] which recalls  many, such as 
a hundred or a thousand, past lives of oneself and others , through the force of 
experience;  
 
• the divine eye that arises from the  manifest compositional action  of cultivating a 
concentration in this life is a transparent physical material  (included within a level of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
108 equipoise arisen from meditation ) which sees all  subtle and gross forms  that are remote 
or not remote ; the clairvoyanc e [of divine eye that arises from the manifest 
compositional action ] [possesses special power, because] it is a mental consciousness 
concomitant with the two qualities [meditative stabilization and wisdom] supported on its 
empowering condition;   
• the clair voyance of the exhaustion of contaminations  possesses special power, 
because it is that concomitant with the two qualities [meditative stabilization and 
wisdom] which is a method abandoning the afflictive and knowledge obscuration s, 
and directly knows the separations that are the abandonments.  
 If you were to possess these six clairvoyances, you would directly hear even the faint and 
loud sounds revealing the Dharma in the infinite world systems. You would directly know 
the teachers and disciples with whom you were related in past lives and also where they are 
in the present. Having caused trainees to excellently [appear] before you  through magical 
emanation, you examine well whether or not these vessels have attachment and so forth in 
their continua. By rev ealing the methods for attaining liberation that you directly know, you 
will quickly complete the achievings in your own continuum. Through completing those, you will swiftly complete omniscience. 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C. Instructions on eliminating the seeds th at are objects of abandonment  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C1. Instructions on the antidotes to the acquired objects of abandonment  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2. Instructions on the antidotes to the innate objects of abandonment  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C1. Instructions on the antidotes to the acquired objects of abandonment  
COMMENTARY 
…indicating that “With respect to the path of seeing, the very entity of the sixteen instants 
included within the four truths —the nature of forbearances and knowledges of dharma and 
of subsequent knowledge —a yogi realizing all phenomena to be devoid of entitiness, like an 
illusionist, he, bearing a body without adherence to all phenomena, performs thorough 
meditation on the very direct antidotes to the objects to be abandoned by that,” instructs on 
the path of seeing, and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Subhuti, the bodhisattva mahasattvas, based on the Perfection of Wisdom, generate confidence” instructs on the path of seeing, because it instructs by way of indicating 
“perform  thorough  meditation on the very direct antidote s to the  acquired that are the 
objects to be abandoned by that  path of seeing .” What is the path of seeing?  The very 
entity of the sixteen instants , the object -possessors  included within the four truths , the 
objects —the nature of forbearances and knowledges of dharma and of subsequent 
knowledge [dharma  forbearance , dharma  knowledge, subsequent  forbearance , and 
subsequent  knowledge ]. By which person? By a yogi realizing all phenomena to be 
devoid of  truly existent enti tiness. Further, like an illusionist, he, bearing a body without 
adherence to all phenomena as truly existent, meditates. 
 The path of seeing, as well as the way of generating it, will be explained below. In this 
context, just understand the explanation in  [Vimuktisena’s]  Illuminating the Twenty  
Thousand  on the reason spoken by Subhuti to indicate the path of seeing.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
109 2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2. Instructions on the antidotes to the innate objects of abandonment  
COMMENTARY 
Because —just as, due to the compounded and t he uncompounded being merely the same  
entity, one is unable to realize them as mutually different —it observes non -differently from 
the property that was made direct by the path of seeing which was explained, since seeing 
and meditation do not have [types] that are different, presenting the fully -qualified path of 
meditation does not exist. However, that [abandons] by the nature of dependent -arising. 
“Perform thorough meditation as the direct antidote to the objects to be abandoned by that,” 
instructs on the  path of meditation, 
 
EXPLANATION  
“Subhuti, in the absence of compounded sphere, one is unable to impute uncompounded 
sphere …” instructs on the path of meditation by means of simile, because it instructs that 
“Perform thorough meditation  as the direct antidote to the innate objects of 
abandonment that are the objects to be abandoned by that path of meditation.”  
 
With respect to that, there are:  
 
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2A. The nature of the path: the object to be meditated upon  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2B. Dispelling objection  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2A. The nature of the path: the object to be meditated upon  
Thoroughly presenting the uncontaminated fully -qualified  path of meditation that is a 
different type from the path of seeing which directly realizes dharmata does not ex ist, since 
seeing and meditation that are direct equipoise on dharmata do not have types  that are 
different. They do not have types that are different, due to the compounded—true paths 
directly realizing selflessness —and the uncompounded—their separation f rom seeds of 
stain —being  merely the same  entity , since, it would follow that they [the true paths] are 
not free from stains if they [the true paths and their separation from seeds of stain] were 
different entities; because —just as one is unable to impute  them as mutually different 
entities —also that path of meditation directly observes the property (that was made 
direct by the path of seeing ) that was explained  above to be non-different from 
dharmata. For, since dharmata has no differences that are dissimil ar types by means of 
entity, the earlier and later paths that are direct meditative equipoise upon it also have no 
differences that are dissimilar types from the point of view  of entity. This is in turn because 
both directly observe thusness, the negation of the object of adherence of the innate 
conception of true existence. However, know that the dissimilar superimpositions that are 
objects to be removed are removed individually.  
 
In this context:  
 
SOMEONE FROM THE PAST: Although the meditative equipoises  of the ten grounds do 
not differ with respect to the seeing of dharmata, there are differences in the seeing of the 
truth body .  
RESPONSE: If one were to assert that a true cessation were  an ultimate truth, then that 
would be appropriate, but if it were an obscurational truth, then the direct comprehension of 
an obscurational truth by that very exalted wisdom of someone on a learner’s path that 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
110 directly realizes dharmata would transgress the path of reasoning; it would follow t hat 
meditative equipoise and subsequent attainment would become one entity on a learner’s 
path, it would follow that the stains of apprehending the two truths as different entities 
would be exhaustively abandoned, and there would not be the slightest fault  in a 
bodhisattva’s having brought about the perfect end without having completed [prayers], ripened [sentient beings] and purified [his buddha land].  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1B -3C2B. Dispelling objection  
OPINION: If those two paths do not have different characteristi cs, then they would also not 
have difference with regard to the ability to abandon the objects of abandonment. 
RESPONSE: Indeed, the two are not different in type. However, it is not that they do not 
have difference with regard to the ability to abandon th e objects of abandonment because, 
even though the seeds of the innate [objects of abandonment] are not abandoned by the 
earlier path, that later path, through the force of having greatly advanced in familiarization 
compared to the former, by the nature of dependent -arising , is able to abandon the seeds 
of the innate [objects of abandonment]. For example, even though an inferential valid 
cognizer realizing that sound is impermanent realizes the non -existence of the object of 
adherence of the innate conceptio n of sound as permanent, it harms the manifest acquired 
[objects of abandonment]; it does not harm the innate [objects of abandonment].  
2B2C -1A2B -1B1C. Summary   
COMMENTARY 
Thus, concerning the Perfection of Wisdom that  is the mind of enlightenment and the  very 
entities of the phenomena entailed by that, all of these —(1) not observing, which are the 
aspects of achievings; (2) the four truths of the aryas, which are the objects of observation of those; (3) the three refuges, which are the support; (4) non-attachment , which is the cause 
of distinguished progress; (5) absence of weariness , which is the cause of irreversible 
progress; (6) thoroughly upholding the path, which is the cause of not progressing by way of other vehicles; (7) the five eyes, which are t he causes of progressing without dependency on 
others; (8) the six clairvoyances, which are the causes of thoroughly completing an exalted knower of all aspects; and (9 -10) the paths of seeing the truth and meditation, which are the 
causes  that complete —are indicated in the context of instructions. Since , by only just these , 
all aims will become fulfilled, the instructions are of ten types.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Thus, the Mahayana  instructions  indicated here are definite as ten types . All of these  ten 
are indicated in the context of instructions, since by only just these  ten, all desired aims  
of beings seeking omniscience will become fulfilled , that is, completed. 
 QUALM: The other paths that will be explained below would be unnecessary. 
RESPONSE: Do not hold that position because, while a Mahayana  path that is not 
subsumed under them is not possible, since not all the divisions that are instances are 
indicated in these, they must be realized from elsewhere. This is just like, while a thing
88 
that is not subsumed under product is not possible, the term expressing “product” does not 
necessarily indicate all things.

111  Concerning the practice of the Perfection of Wisdom in which  the ten paths  are 
included within the stages of the Mahayana  path, the mind of enlightenment a nd the very 
entities of the phenomena entailed by  and conjoined with that, the instructions that 
instruct on —(1) “not observing  what is not in common with the hearers , and, what does not 
pass beyond the two truths, which are the aspects of the modes of cultivation, (2) the 
objects of observation of those  achievings;”  (3) “the support  of achievings;”  (4) 
“distinguished  higher and higher achievings;”  (5) “irreversible from the practice of the 
achievings;”  (6) “having degenerated from the Mahay ana, by way of other vehicles  of the 
hearers and solitary realizers;”  (7) “due to independently engaging in the achievings, by way 
of others ; (8) “due to quickly  completing the accumulations, all aspects ;” and (9- 10) the 
paths of seeing and meditation whic h are the causes that complete the achievings by 
means of exhausting all of the two obscurations —are ten . 
 The instructions by way of having qualified each of the ten instructions by the realization 
of emptiness are for the sake of (i) indicating that, in general, all Mahayana  paths need to be 
conjoined with that wisdom, and (ii) making known that, in particular, by meditating upon 
most of the achievings bearing the aspect of conventionalities during subsequent attainment, 
and emptiness during meditative eq uipoise on the occasion of the path of accumulation, the 
partial concordances with definite discrimination that will be explained below —the 
wisdoms arisen from meditation that observe emptiness —will be generated.

2B2C -1A2B -1B2A -1A. Presentation of the results  
Regarding the results of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue, the mode o f a trainee -in-virtue is a 
mode in which one is set in the ultimate training in virtue by way of having abandoned the 
afflictions; further, regarding an uninterrupted path , an abandonment is its  result that is a  
separation , and a liberated path is its  result concordant with its cause. Thus these are called 
“result s of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue.” Also, due to the particularities of occasion, four 
results have been  taught in the sutras. The reason for positing them is that there are three 
objects of  abandonment: (1) the three entwinements that are hindrances to embarking on 
liberation, (2) the five partial concordances with the bottom most , and (3) the five partial 
concordances with the upper. With respect to these:  
• the first result is presented by wa y of having abandoned the first;  
• the middle two results by way of having abandoned [respectively] most of the second and 
all of the second without exception; and  
• the result of foe -destroyer by way of having abandoned the last.  
In dependence on which leve ls are they attained?  
• The first two results are attained in dependence on the preparations of the first concentration;  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
112 • if one attains the third result serially or by way of having largely separated from 
attachment previously, the mind support is similar to  the above; if one has separated from 
attachment previously, it is supported on any of the actual concentrations;  
• with respect to the fourth result, if one attains it in the two lower realms [desire and form realms], it is supported on any of the six level s of concentrations; if it is attained in the 
formless realm, it is supported on any of the first three formless absorptions.  
In dependence on which—the path of seeing or the path of meditation —are they attained?  
• The first result is attained only by way of the path of seeing;  
• if the middle two are attained serially, then they are attained by way of the path of meditation; if one has largely separated from attachment previously, or one has separated 
from attachment with respect to the desire realm previousl y, then they are attained by 
way of the path of seeing; and  
• the last result is attained in dependence on the path of meditation, because it must be 
attained through having exhaustively abandoned the objects to be abandoned by the path 
of meditation of the  Peak of Existence.   
By which —a mundane path or a supramundane path —are they attained?  
• The first and the last results are attained only by way of a supramundane path;  
• the middle two are attained after [having attained] the path of seeing; if that person has 
familiarized with practice predominant in calm abiding , then they are attained by way of 
a mundane path bearing the aspects of peace and coarseness.  
In dependence on which support are they attained?  
• the first three are attained in dependence on a desi re realm support;  
• the fourth is attained on a support of all three realms.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B2A -1B. Presentation of the persons  
With respect to persons who are stream -enterers, the first of the four, the definition of the 
first from between the two, approacher and abider [i.e. the definition of an approacher to 
stream -enterer ] is: a person who strives for the sake of attaining the result distinguished by 
having abandoned the three entwinements. When divided, there are two:  
(1) followers of faith, and  
(2) followers of Dharma.  
 The boundaries are: from a single -session path of preparation onwards, as long as one has 
not attained the liberated path included within the result of stream -enterer. With respect to 
abiders in the result of stream -enterer, there are:  
(1) those who are serial, and  
(2) those who are simultaneous.  
 With respect to first, the definition [of a serial abider in the result of stream -enterer ] is: a 
person (abiding in the result distinguished by having abandoned the three entwinements ) 
who does not abandon simultaneously by way of aspects , the objects to be abandoned by the 
path of meditation of the three realms. The three entwinements are: (1) the view of the 
transitory collection, (2) the view holding bad modes of conduct as supreme, and (3) doubt.  
 When divided, differentiated by faculty, there are the two: believers -by-faith and 
attainers -by-seeing; differentiated by application, there are the two: mere and special abiders 
in the result —those who abide in that very result, who  do not strive and who strive for the 
sake of the special result. Know the two —differentiation through faculty and differentiation 
through application —similarly for the two middle results. When differentiated by way of 
the attribute of rebirth, there are two: m aximum -seven -timers and those born from class to 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
113 class.  The boundaries are: from the sixteenth instant of the path of seeing through abiding 
on the uninterrupted path that abandons the sixth affliction of the desire realm that is an 
object to be abandoned  by the path of meditation.  
 With respect to the second, those who are simultaneous, the definition [of a simultaneous 
abider in the result of stream -enterer ] is: a person [ abiding in the result distinguished by 
having abandoned the three entwinements ] who abandons simultaneously by way of 
aspects, the afflictions that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the three realms. The afflictions are abandoned in dependence on the preparation of the first 
concentration, Not Unable, (i) becau se one is able to abandon all the afflictions of the three 
realms in dependence on this, and (ii) because the other levels are not appropriate as the mind support for abandoning the objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the 
desire realm. Further, they are abandoned by only a supramundane path, because a mundane path is unable to abandon the objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the Peak 
of Existence. 
 They are abandoned by way of simultaneously abandoning the nine great of the great 
objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the three realms [until] simultaneously abandoning the nine small of the small. The reason for such ability is that this [simultaneous 
abider] is of sharper faculty than the others, whereby without  having differentiated the 
aggregates —forms and so forth —of the three realms as being of individual realms and 
levels, he familiarized with them collectively , thinking, “All of these are impermanent, 
suffering and selfless.”  
 It is inappropriate to posit simultaneity with respect to the two middle results; at the time 
of abandoning the sixth affliction of the desire realm, it is impossible to abandon the sixth object to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the Peak of Existence; at the time of 
abandon ing the ninth affliction of the desire realm, it is impossible to abandon the ninth 
[object to be abandoned by the path of meditation] of the Peak of Existence. Hence it is posited only with respect to the first and the last results. When divided, there are:  
(i) the mere abiders in the result of stream -enterer and  
(ii) special abiders in the result of stream -enterer.  
 From between these two, the latter [special simultaneous abider in the result of stream -
enterer] is an approacher to the result of foe -destroyer. The boundaries [of a simultaneous 
abider in the result of stream -enterer] are: from the sixteenth instant of clear realization of 
the truth through abiding on the uninterrupted path that abandons the small of the small 
objects to be abandoned by the  path of meditation.  
 With respect to the second, the once -returners, the definition of the first from between the 
two, approacher and abider, [i.e. the definition of an approacher to once -returner
] is: a 
person who strives for the sake of attaining the re sult of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue 
distinguished by having largely separated from attachment.  When divided, there are two:  
(i) serial approacher to the result of once -returner, and  
(ii) leaper approacher to the result of once -returner.  
The boundaries are: from  a serial approacher striving in the application that abandons the 
first object to be abandoned by the path of meditation of the desire realm after ha ving 
attained the path of seeing, through abandoning the sixth. A leaper approacher is  like a 
stream -enterer; the difference is that he has abandoned either the sixth, seventh or eighth 
object to be abandoned by the path of meditation prior to the path of seeing. 
 The definition of the second, an abider in the result of once -returner , is: a  person who 
abides in the result of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue distinguished by having largely 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
114 separated from attachment. When divided, differentiated by the way of attaining the result, 
there are the two:  
(i) serialist and  
(ii) leaper.  
A one -obsta cle89 special abider in the result of once -returner has abandoned the seventh or 
eighth affliction of the desire realm, has not abandoned the ninth, and attains nirvana after 
having taken one rebirth. He has attained the powers that are uncontaminated antid otes to 
these objects of abandonment. The boundaries are: for a leaper, from the sixteenth instant of 
clear realization of the truth, and, for a serialist, from the liberated path (in which the sixth 
has been abandoned), until  the uninterrupted path that a bandons the ninth object to be 
abandoned by the path of meditation of the desire realm.  
 With respect to the third, the non -returners, the definition of the first from between the 
two, approacher and abider, [i.e. the definition of an approacher to non -returner ] is: a person 
who strives for the sake of attaining the result of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue 
distinguished by having abandoned the five partial concordances with the bottom. Its divisions are similar to those of a once -returner. The boundaries for an approacher to non -
returner who has separated from attachment previously are similar to those of a leaper 
approacher to once -returner; the difference is that he has abandoned some of the 
afflictions —ranging from the ninth of the desire realm until the ninth of Nothingness —prior 
to the path of seeing. As for a serial approacher, [the boundaries are:] from striving to 
abandon the seventh affliction of the desire realm, until  striving to abandon the ninth, after 
the path of seeing. 
 The definition of the second, an abider in the result of non -returner
 is: a person who 
abides in the result of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue distinguished by having abandoned the 
five partial concordances with the bottom.  The five partial concordances with the bottom 
are: (1 -3) the three entwinements, (4) aspiration for the desire realm, and (5) harmful intent. 
When divided, differentiated by the manner of attaining the result, there are the two:  
(i) serialist non -returner and  
(ii) non -returner who has separated from attach ment previously.  
When differentiated by the manner of relying upon arya qualities, there are the two:  
(i) non -returner who transforms over lives in the desire realm, and  
(ii) [non -returner] who does not transform in a life in the desire realm.  
The first  attains the state of non -returner by relying upon an arya path in continuous rebirths 
in the desire realm ; he attains nirvana in the life he attains [the state of] non -returner  
Pacifying in that present life means actualizing nirvana in the life where one  attains the path 
of seeing. The second attains the state of non -returner in that life where he attains the  path 
of seeing. With respect to that, there are :  
(i) one who proceeds to the form realm,  
(ii) one who proceeds to the formless realm,  
(iii) one who pacifies in that present life, and  
(iv) one who actualizes with a body.  
The definition of the first [ an abider in the result of non -returner who proceeds to the form 
realm ] is: a person who attains the state of non -returner in that life where he attains the path 
of seeing and takes rebirth in the form realm. With respect to its divisions, (1) the first of 
five—one who attains nirvana in the intermediate state —has abandoned the entwinement of

115 rebirth in the form realm and, due to not having ab andoned the entwinement of 
establishment, actualizes nirvana on the support of an intermediate existence of the form 
realm. (2) The second —one who attains nirvana upon rebirth —due to not having abandoned 
both entwinements, having actualized the path when j ust having established the birth 
existence of the form realm, attains nirvana. (3) The third —one who attains nirvana by 
being with compositional action —having actualized the path with great exertion in order to 
be like the others, attains nirvana. (4) The fourth —one who attains nirvana by being without 
compositional action —having actualized the path without great exertion through the force 
of familiarization, attains nirvana. 
 (5) With respect to the fifth —one who transfers to the upper—its definition is: a n abider 
in the result (distinguished by having abandoned the five partial concordances with the bottom) who bears the lineage of attaining nirvana on the support of either Akanistha  or the 
Peak of Existence, having passed through rebirth in the form realm . Its divisions are:  
(i) a lineage -bearer who proceeds finally to Akanistha and  
(ii) a lineage -bearer who proceeds finally to the Peak of Existence.   
With respect to the first [an abider in the result of non -returner who is a lineage -bearer who 
proceeds  finally to Akanistha ], there are three:  
(i) lineage -bearers who are leapers,  
(ii) [lineage -bearers] who are half- leapers, and  
(iii) [lineage -bearers] who die and transfer to all other abodes, excluding Great 
Brahma. 
The second [an abider in the result of non -returner who is a lineage -bearer who proceeds 
finally to the Peak of Existence] is not reborn in the pure places, due to not having 
meditated on the causal alternating concentrations. Persons who meditate on alternating 
concentrations are of two typ es: non -returners and foe -destroyers. With respect to the latter 
[foe-destroyers], those of dull faculty meditate [on alternating meditations] in order to 
distance themselves from the obscurations relishing the taste of meditative stabilization, 
while thos e of sharp faculty meditate in order to abide in the happiness of that present life. 
The first [non -returners] meditate also in order to be reborn in the pure places.  
 On what minds are they supported? They are initially meditated on by being supported on 
a mind of the fourth concentration, since it is the best serviceable mind; subsequently, they 
are also supported on other minds. Further, one enters absorption upon an uncontaminated 
fourth concentration by way of a single application, then a contaminated one, then an 
uncontaminated one —the small, alternating three [concentrations]. Through that mode, 
alternating six, nine, twelve and fifteen [concentrations] are the middling, great, very great, and tremendously great. That very karma that projects [a rebirth] into the fourth 
concentration that was accumulated at the time of an ordinary being previously, due to one 
having meditated on the pure fourth concentration, transforms into the cause for being 
reborn in a pure place. Hence there is no t the fault that it would follow that, despite having 
seen the truth, one newly accumulate s karma that projects one into cyclic existence. 
 The definition of the second [an abider in the result of non -returner who proceeds to the 
formless realm ] is: a person who attains no n-returner in that life where he attains the path of 
seeing and bears the lineage of actualizing nirvana on a support of the formless realm 
without taking rebirth in the form realm. When divided, there are four, excluding one who 
attains nirvana in the int ermediate state.   
 The third, a non -returner who pacifies in that present life, actualizes nirvana in that very 
life of attaining the path of seeing. The fourth, a non -returner who actualizes  with a body, is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
116 an arya learner who possesses the eight liberat ions. The boundaries, for a serialist, are from 
the liberated path in which the ninth affliction of the desire realm has been abandoned, until 
abiding on the uninterrupted path that abandons the ninth [affliction] of the Peak of 
Existence, and for a leaper, from the sixteenth instant of the clear realization of the truth.  
 Regarding the fourth, a foe -destroyer, the definition of an approacher [to foe -destroyer ] 
is: an arya person who strives for the sake of attaining the result of the mode of a trainee -in-
virtue that is distinguished by having abandoned the five partial concordances with the 
upper. When divided, there are two:  
(i) serial and  
(ii) simultaneous approachers to foe -destroyer.  
If he is a non -returner, then [the boundaries] are: from striving i n the application that 
abandons the remaining objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation, until the 
uninterrupted path which abandons the ninth affliction of the Peak of Existence. If he is a 
stream -enterer, then the boundaries are posited to be sim ilar to those for a special 
simultaneous stream -enterer. 
 In regard to the second, the definition [of an abider in the result of foe -destroyer ] is: a 
person who abides in the result of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue that is distinguished by 
having abandon ed the five partial concordances with the upper. The five partial 
concordances with the upper are: (1 -2) attachment of the form and formless realms, (3) 
excitement, (4) pride, and (5) ignorance. Its divisions, when it is divided by way of the mode of aband oning the objects of abandonment, are the two:  
(i) serial foe -destroyers, and  
(ii) simultaneous foe -destroyers.  
And when divided by means of faculty, there are the six:  
(1) those who bear the property of degeneration,  
(2) those who bear the property of suicide, 
(3) those who bear the property of subsequent protection, 
(4) those who bear the property of unmoving from that state,  
(5) those who bear the property of worthy of thorough realization, and  
(6) those who bear the property of unwavering. 
The boundaries are: from the knowing of exhaustion and absence of production.  
2B2C -1A2B -1B2A -2. Meanings that are objects to be characterized  
The bases of the divisions are simply irreversible learner bodhisattva aryas. The way of dividing them into twent y is as follows: “Those who transfer to the upper, the  five, from 
proceeding to Akanistha  until proceeding finally to the Peak of Existence,” which are 
explicitly indicated by the texts of the Acarya  Arya [Vimuktisena], are counted as one of the 
twenty. Th us, by adding the four —the eighth, the two mere abiders in the two middle results 
and the approacher to foe -destroyer—to the sixteen explicitly mentioned by the text, twenty 
are explained. 
 According to Acarya  [Haribhadra]:  
• there are five related to stream -enterers: the four explicitly mentioned, supplemented by 
the mere abider in the result of stream -enterer;  
• there are three related to once -returners: the two explicitly mentioned, supplemented by 
the mere abider in the result of once -returner;  
• there are five related to non -returners: the explicitly mentioned approacher and four who 
attain nirvana;  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
117 • although those who proceed to Akanistha  were explicitly mentioned, having taken them 
as the bases of division, there are three who transfer to the upper who are differentiated 
from them; although a certain translator said that “there are two: one who destroys 
attachment of the form realm and proceeds to the Peak of Existence, and one who pacifies 
in that present life and actualizes with a body,” in the case of t his new translation, even 
though one who proceeds finally to the Peak of Existence  Akanisthaand one who destroys 
attachment of the form realm were explicitly mentioned, without counting them as 
divisions, they are subsumed into one meaning and taken as a b asis of division.  
 By differentiating one who pacifies in that present life and one who actualizes with a body 
into two, and supplementing it with an approacher to foe -destroyer, the text makes it up to 
twenty by way of the seventeen explicitly mentioned, supplemented by three. Without 
counting mere believers -by-faith and attainers -by-seeing in the enumeration, [the text] 
explicitly indicates those bearing the name of approachers to both middle results.  
 It seems that, some arrogant one, holding to the lat ter system (from between the two: the 
assertion that these are fully qualified stream -enterers and so forth, and the assertion that 
they are imputed), flung what is only a counterfeit repudiation, and laughed at the slightly wounded tradition of another wi thout sensing his own head splitting; I shall explain this 
slightly on the occasion of the meaning of the words.  
 A previous scholar said the following regarding the Acarya : If he had explained the 
modes of the Mahayana , then it would be incorrect to appl y them to one who is serial in the 
stages of abandoning the afflictions; if [he had explained] just the modes of hearers, then the 
presentation of proceeding to the Peak of Existence would be incorrect; the stages of 
abandonment of the afflictions would be  incorrect as reasons for making divisions of the 
Mahayana  sangha; and the names “stream -enterers” and so forth would be incorrect in 
meaning, because it is impossible for a bodhisattva of sharp faculty who is skilled in means 
to previously abandon only the afflictions of the desire realm, since he simultaneously 
abandons the small of the small objects of abandonment of the three realms. It would have been better if he  had not behaved like someone shooting an arrow without having seen what 
the target was. T here is plenty to be said about these; since those (with neither discernment 
nor diligent energy ) who desire to engage in th e stages of clear realization are asserted to 
have been cared for, I will not elaborate more.

frequently in the sutras, two interceding stanzas :   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
118 2B2C -1A2B -1B2B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
1.6 Dull and sharp faculties;  
The faithful and the attainers -by-seeing; from class to class;  
One obstacle; intermediate state and upon rebirth,  
Acting and not acting; pro ceeding to Akanistha , 
 
1.7 Three leapers; pro ceeding finally to the Peak of Existence, 
Destroying attachment of the form realm,  
Pacifying in that present life, ac tualizing with a body;  
And the rhinoceros are the twenty.  
 
EXPLANATION  
[Haribhadra’s] Eight Chapters90 states that the sum of the twenty explicitly mentioned by 
the text, the eighth, the first three abiders in the results, and the approacher to foe -destroy er 
is twenty -five. Further, in terms of the eighth , it is said [in sutra], “ Bhagavan, where did the 
bodhisattva abiding by way of the practice of the perfection of wisdom die and transfer from, and became reborn here? After dying and transferring from here , where will he be 
reborn?” Similarly, [passages] regarding the two followers [of faith and dharma] also appear 
in individual sutras. Fearing wordiness, I have not written them down.  
 The subject —the person abiding on the uninterrupted path of dharma forbe arance on the 
Mahayana  path of seeing —is divided into two types, because there are those of dull and 
sharp faculties ; similarly, there is one bearing the name “stream -enterer” who is supported 
implicitly. [Indicated] by “ the faithful and the attainers -by-seeing,” there are two bearing 
the name “approacher” by way of the two middle [results]. Indicated by “ from  class to 
class, ” there are two bearing the name “special abider in the result of stream -enterer” and 
one bearing the name “mere abider in the result of once -returner” implicitly indicated. 
Indicated by the name “special” is “ one obstacle .” And indicated by the name “non -
returner” are four bearing the names “attaining nirvana in the intermediate state, upon rebirth,
91 with compositional  action and with no compositional  action .” In regard to those 
who bear the name “ progressing to Akanistha ,” there are three leapers . Taking as the 
basis of division “ destroying attachment of the form realm”  that is imputed as 
“progressing finally to the Peak of Existence ,” there are the two —pacifying in that 
present life and actualizing with a body —and that bearing the name “ rhinoceros  solitary 
realizer.” Thus there are twenty .

ethics and modes of conduct as supreme are exhausted, the view of the transitory collection 
is not exhausted. Since even the innate view of the transitory collection is explained to be an 
entwinement, it should not be t aken that “the three entwinements are exhausted.” (4 -5)

(10-13) After that, there are  four types with respect to the presentation called  “non-
returners,”  because there are four types called: attaining nirvana in the intermediate 
state, in that they attain a liberated path in which any of the afflictive obscurations have 
been abandoned upon the support of the intermediate existence of the form realm ; attaining 
nirvana upon rebirth in the form realm ; completely attaining nirvana , in that they attain a 
liberated path in which any of the afflictive obscurations have been abandoned with 
manifest compositional action, and without manifest compositional action  of endeavor . 
 Someone propounds that, on these occasions of (i) having abandoned the entwinement of 
birth but not having abandoned the entwinemen t of establishment, and (ii) not having 
abandoned the entwinement of birth, even though the causes of being born into the 
intermediate existence and the birth existence of the form realm are retained, “bodhisattva 
aryas do not take rebirth through the forc e of karma and afflictions.” Know that this is 
simply contradiction.  
 Yet another person, having taken a bodhisattva (abiding on the liberated path in which 
the ninth affliction of the desire realm has been abandoned) as an imputed non -returner, 
asserts th at his abiding in the result of the mode of a trainee -in-virtue that is distinguished 
by having abandoned the five partial concordances with the bottom, is our system. This is an assertion that incurs all three circles
92 for the sake of disparaging himself.  Also, does 
“having abandoned the ninth affliction of the desire realm” refer to having abandoned 
merely the manifest afflictions or the seeds? In the first case, presenting those on the path of 
seeing (who have attained an actual concentration prior to th e Mahayana  path of seeing) as 
sangha bearing the name “non -returner” indicated here would not contain contradiction. If it 
were to refer to the seeds, then that would become an assertion outside of this system, since 
the Acarya  asserted that a bodhisattva arya who has not traversed the Hinayana  path 
exhaustively abandons the seeds of the ninth object to be abandoned by the path of 
meditation that are both [afflictive and knowledge] obscurations with the uninterrupted path 
of the end of the continuum  of a sentient being.

(14-16) After that, those very bodhisattva aryas, actua lizing a liberated path in which any 
of the afflictive obscurations has been abandoned upon the support of Akanistha , proceed 
finally to Akanistha ; there are three types other than the five held in the previous 
enumeration, because there are three who transfer higher: (1) by leaping —having taken 
rebirth in Brahma Type at first, discarding the others and taking rebirth in Akanistha  from 
there;  (2) half-leaping —from Brahma Type, having taken rebirth in any of the three lower 
levels of the pure places, taking rebirth in Akanistha  from there;  and (3) death -
transference in all abodes —from Brahma Type, having progressively passed through [the 
various levels] until Akanistha , attaining a liberated path in which any of the afflictive 
obscurations have been abandoned  upon the support of Akanistha . 
 Some ignoramus, adhering to merely what is overt in the sutras, asserted that if a 
bodhisattva arya who has attained the fourth concentration were to take rebirth in Brahma 
Type, he would degenerate from the three higher co ncentrations. This is extremely 
inappropriate, (i) because it is impossible for a bodhisattva arya to degenerate from a concentration, and (ii) because, since it has been explained that “the branches are 
thoroughly exhausted,” even though he has attained t he fourth concentration, due to having 
thoroughly exhausted the potency to take rebirth through the force of the karma of pure concentration, it will be impossible for him to be born in cyclic existence through the force 
of that.  
 Therefore, even those wh o take rebirth in the form realm do so through the force of 
compassion and prayers. Concurring the conventions with those of the hearer sangha that are overt in the sutras is: (i) for the sake of causing some who are not definite in the hearer 
lineage to e nter the Mahayana ; (ii) for the sake of stopping someone’s deprecation that “The 
Mahayana  sutra sets are not taught by the Buddha, since they do not have a presentation of 
the approachers and abiders;” and (iii) for the sake of leading hearers who have ari sen from 
the sphere of peace to the Mahayana , saying “With merely that nirvana, you have not fully 
completed what is to be attained; you must act to attain non -abiding nirvana.”

2B2C -1A2B -2. Explaining the start of generating the wisdom arisen from meditation observing emptiness 
through meditating on the meaning of the instructions  
2B2C -1A2B -2A. Meaning of the  general ity

2B2C -1A2B -2A1. Support  
The body supports of men and women of three [human] continents as well as gods of the 
desire realm are the support of body [for generating a path of preparation]. Although not the 
support for actually generating the supreme mundane quality level, apparentl y some beings 
of the form realm also generate the heat and peak levels.  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2. Entity  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2A. Definition 
2B2C -1A2B -2A2B. Divisions  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2C. Mode of generation  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2D. Meaning of the individual divisions  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2A. Defin ition 
[The definition of a path of preparation  is:] a Mahayana  mundane path (subsequent to the 
completion of a partial concordance of liberation) that is concordant with  the clear 
realization of truth. It is not limited to solely being a wisdom arisen from  meditation, 
because there are many cases of wisdoms arisen from hearing or contemplation. 
2B2C -1A2B -2A2B. Divisions  
The divisions of occasions are the three: small, middle, and great. The divisions of entity are 
the four: heat, [peak, forbearance and supreme mundane quality].  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2C. Mode of generation  
On the occasion of the path of accumulation, although a fully qualified application in 
complete aspects that completely practices all the types of aspects of the three exalted 
knowers without excep tion exists, and a wisdom arisen from meditation that observes 
impermanence and suffering, as well as a wisdom arisen from meditation that has meditated on unattractiveness and so forth exist, there is no wisdom arisen from meditation that 
observes emptine ss because, when that [wisdom arisen from meditation that observes 
emptiness] is initially generated, one transfers to the path of preparation.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
123 QUALM: What are the differences among the three types of wisdom observing emptiness?  
RESPONSE: At the time when a wisdom arisen from hearing or contemplation observes 
emptiness, which is a mere negation of elaborations of true existence, although a generality 
of the object dawns well, the object and the object -possessor appear as though un related due 
to being together with very coarse dualistic appearances. However, when a wisdom arisen from meditation observes it, although there are dualistic appearances, due to having 
overcome the coarse dualistic appearances as in the previous two cases, it appears as if one 
has penetrated the object. And since it seems to  approximat e the transform ation  in a manner 
of water placed in water, the dawning of the meaning generality of emptiness in such a 
manner is described as “dharmata appearing clearly.” Ne vertheless this is not a clear 
appearance that is free from conceptions.  Furthermore, through the force of analyzing well with reasoning the meaning of 
emptiness from within the state of meditative equipoise in calm abiding , from that juncture 
in which on e is able to induce the bliss of pliancy of the body and the mind, one attains the 
fully qualified special insight observing emptiness, and is said to have attained a union of 
those. In spite of that, you should know that the mere union of calm abiding and  special 
insight exists even in those who are on the path of accumulation, as well as in some non -
Buddhists. When one initially generates such a union of calm abiding and special insight 
realizing emptiness, one is said to have “generated the heat level of the path of preparation.” 
Even though one is able to ascertain the presence of dualistic appearances through the force 
of experience even while being in meditative equipoise at that time, since one is not able to 
ascertain it in such a manner at the time of attaining the forbearance level of the path of preparation, it has been explained in [Asanga’s] Hearers’ Grounds, “ Dualistic appearances 
seem to have vanished,  but it is not that  dualistic appearances have vanished.”  
2B2C -1A2B -2A2D. Meaning of the indiv idual divisions  
It is very difficult to differentiate among the four levels of path of preparation by means of (i) the apprehended -objects ’ absence  of nature being small, middling and great clear 
appearances, and (ii) their apprehender- subjects ’ absence  of nature being complete clear 
appearance, because one is placed in the meditative equipoise on their emptiness of true 
existence by simultaneously withdrawing all phenomena that are apprehended -objects and 
their apprehender- subjects during meditative equipo ise, and there is no difference with 
respect to their meaning -generalities of dharmata dawning clearly.  
 Therefore, the ability to abandon the conception of true existence of apprehended -objects 
becomes more and more outstanding on the occasions of the fir st two levels of the path of 
preparation. Hence, at the time of having generated the forbearance level of the path of preparation, due to having weakened the coarse potency of even the manifest innate 
conception of true existence of apprehended -objects, wh en arising from meditative 
equipoise , the generality of appearances of phenomena that are apprehended -objects, such 
as forms, dawns well, like a reflection in a mirror. And at the time of having generated the 
supreme mundane quality level, apprehender- subjects also appear in that manner through 
the strength of meditation. The stages unfold in this way through the force of the degree of difficulty and ease of abandoning the adherence to the apprehended -objects and 
apprehender -subjects as truly existent. 
 It is like it has been posited (from the side of what has been abandoned) in the Ornament 
for the Great Vehicle Sutras ( Mahayanasutralamkara): “At that time, the distractions to 
apprehended- objects will be abandoned,” and “In this way, at the end of that, the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
124 distractions to apprehender- subjects will be abandoned.” The claim that “Differentiation is 
made on the basis of whether or not clear ap pearance of exalted wisdom is present” is one 
that is clearly deprived of anything to say.  
2B2C -1A2B -2A3. Analysis of the awareness and knower  
With respect to such a path of preparation, it is inappropriate to assert that, at the time of 
meditative equipoi se, it is free from conception that apprehends a sound -generality and a 
meaning -generality with respect to dharmata as being suitable to be mixed, because (i) 
dharmata is a hidden phenomenon for ordinary beings and (ii) such a non -conceptual mind 
cannot po ssibly comprehend a hidden phenomenon. If it were mistaken with respect to 
dharmata, then it would also be incorrect for it to eliminate manifest superimpositions, 
because it would be a non -conceptual wrong consciousness. And if it were not mistaken, 
then it follows that even the seeds that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing 
would have been abandoned, because it would be non -conceptual and unmistaken with 
respect to dharmata. Therefore, such a meditative equipoise on the path of preparati on, 
having transformed into a wisdom arisen from meditation through the strength of having 
familiarized the continuum with a subsequent cognizer realizing emptiness, is of the entity 
analyzing the clearly appearing meaning -generality of emptiness. Some ign oramus asserted 
that the classifications of the three wisdoms are inferential cognizers based upon the sign of 
freedom from one and many.

Beginners, having obtained the instructions in that manner, since the branches of definite 
discrimination will arise, the branches of definite discrimination.  
 
EXPLANATION

instructions by having obtained the instructions in that manner ; since the branches of 
defini te discrimination will arise, the branches of definite discrimination —the path that 
accords with the generation of the path of seeing after it —will be explained.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B1B. Root text  
1.8 By way of object of observation, aspect, 
Cause, and tutor,  
The heat level and so forth of bodhisattva protectors –  
In accordance with their nature  
 
1.9 Relying on possessing four conceptions,  
The small, middling and great — 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
125 Are superior 
To that of the hearers along with the rhinoceros -like. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Overtly, the middle four lines [1.8 cd—1.9ab] indicate the meaning of the bases for the 
attributes, the first two lines [1.8 ab] the attributes, and the last two [1.9 cd] those to whom 
they are superior. In addition, the distinction of being [ great,]  middling, and small is 
indicated by implication as an attribute in this commentary. This is also clearly taught in 
Acarya  Arya [Vimuktisena]’s commentary.  
 Thus, the subject —the heat level and so forth of bodhisattva protectors —are 
superior to  the paths of preparation of the hearers along with the rhinoceros -like because 
of being superior by way of object of observation, aspect, cause, and tutor,  and by way 
of being supported on possessing four conceptions in accordance with their nature and 
the small, middling, and great. The reason for the small , middling , [and great]  serving as 
an attribute is that enhancing and familiarizing more and more with the full complete corpus 
of the Mahayana  path is an unsurpassable attribute. According to what is overt in the 
commentary, the sixth  line [1.9 b] refers to the bases for the attributes. The remaining 
meanings of these will be explained below.

…arisen from mundane meditation, the root of virtue called “transformed into heat;” after

mundane quality.” And the divisions of the occasions (due to progressively generating 
“small” and so forth, generating the three —beginning, middle, and end —in a single 
continuum, or due to the particularities of persons of dull faculties and so forth related 
to bodhisattvas  of different continua)—t he small, middling, and great—are superior. 
Alternatively, they are correlated with the attributes and explained.   
 
COMMENTARY 
The very entity of the attribute of observation to be explained is the observation of the 
properties of the four truths; due to being the very antidote to viewing phenomena, engaging 
the aspects of absence  of adherence and so forth; the entities that are the causes of realizing 
the three vehicles together; through the very characteristics of skill in means of the spiritual 
guide, possessing the tutor; the four types of conceptualization of apprehendeds and apprehenders, which are the objects to be abandoned by the paths of seeing and 
meditation —arising in relation to the manner that will be explained.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Why are they supe rior? The very entity of the attribute of observation to be explained  
below  is the observation of the properties of the four truths  distinguished by the sixteen 
attributes, impermanence and so forth, because they are held as the bases for eliminating 
super impositions; that meditative equipoise of the path of preparation engages the aspects 
of absence of adherence  as truly existent  and so forth, due to  being the very direct 
antidote to viewing phenomena,  in which apprehended- objects and apprehender- subjects 
are adhered to as truly existent ; the causes of the special results are superior due to being 
powerful potency, because they are  the special entities that are the causes of the path of 
seeing that directly realizes the three vehicles together ; a bodhisattv a on the path of 
preparation possesses  the special tutor , because of being directly taken care of by the 
supreme emanation body , the special spiritual guide  through  the very characteristics of 
skill in the means of revealing  the complete Mahayana  path; they are  distinguished by the 
four types of conceptualization of apprehendeds and apprehenders, which are the 
objects to be abandoned by the paths of seeing and meditation  included within the peak, 
because the four are distinguished by  arising  in taking th orough affliction  in relation to the 
manner which will be explained  below. The Great Commentary  says: 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
127  
Thus these partial concordances with definite emergence  should be expressed 
based upon the thoroughly afflicted, exactly in respective order, by way of t he 
four types of conceptualization of apprehendeds and apprehenders, which are 
objects to be abandoned by the paths of seeing and meditation included within 
the clear realization of peak, which will be explained below.  
 Therefore, since the conceptions ind icated in this context are taught to be abandoned by the 
peak application, which will be explained below, the explanation that the paths of those occasions exist only on the occasions of the pure grounds is well refuted. Also, regarding 
the mode of possess ing conceptualization, since it has been taught that they possess in 
taking thorough affliction, the assertion that they possess the entity of meditative equipoise 
has transgressed the paths of reasoning. Does “the heat level and so forth possessing the 
four conceptions in respective order” mean that they possess seeds, or that they possess the 
manifest [conceptions] in respective order? It mean s abandonment in that order? The first is 
incorrect because the seeds of all four conceptions are possessed at the  time of the heat level 
of the path of preparation. In the case of the second, are [the heat level and so forth, and the 
manifest conceptions] simultaneous or not? If they are simultaneous, are they one [entity] or 
different entities? That they are one ent ity is incorrect, because the two —(1) the conception 
of apprehendeds and apprehenders as truly existent and (2) the realization of emptiness of 
true existence —have directly contradictory modes of apprehension in observing a single 
observed object. Simultan eous different entities is also inappropriate, because it is incorrect 
for two manifest conceptions of different entities to be produced together in one continuum. 
For it would follow that there are many continua [with respect to] a single person. Even if 
they are not simultaneous, [it is also inappropriate], because there is no reason for the four 
levels of the path of preparation to individually possess four conceptions. Further, it is inappropriate to make differentiations by way of whether or not the fo ur phenomena of 
apprehendeds and apprehenders are realized as being devoid of  true existence, because all 
phenomena are realized as being empty of true existence, even at the time of the heat level 
of the path of preparation.  
 OPINION: If one were to realize the emptiness of true existence of only apprehendeds, one 
would be observing a trifling [form of] emptiness. 
RESPONSE: That is a great fault, because whether or not one realizes pervasive emptiness 
is posited to whether or not one completely severs the  coarse and subtle elaborations of true 
existence; it is not posited by way of the extensiveness or narrowness of the property -
possessors. Otherwise, a bodhisattva on the path of seeing would not directly realize 
pervasive emptiness, because he has not directly realized all property -possessors. 
Furthermore, it is incorrect to differentiate the conceptions of apprehended -objects and 
apprehender -subjects by means of the object of observation, because “conceiving 
apprehended- objects” and “conceiving apprehende r-subjects” explain the modes of 
apprehension of the aspects; they do not explain the observed objects of the conceptions. 
 
OPINION: It is also incorrect that the four levels of the path of preparation abandon the four 
conceptions sequentially, because whe n the heat level of the path of preparation realizes that 
all phenomena that are apprehended -objects and apprehender- subjects are empty of true 
existence, it is incorrect that the superimpositions that are the conceptions of apprehended -
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
128 objects as truly ex istent are severed, but the conceptions of true existence of apprehender-
subjects are not severed.  
RESPONSE: With respect to this, someone’s assertion that the path of preparation and the 
manifest conceptions of this are one entity is inappropriate, becaus e one would need to 
assert that that there is a common locus between the two, direct benefit and harm to  the peak 
application of the path of seeing by the supreme mundane quality level of the path of 
preparation that realizes that apprehended -objects and a pprehender -subjects are devoid of  
true existence . And that [supreme mundane quality level] and the two adherences to 
apprehended- objects and apprehender- subjects as truly existent would not be the object 
directly harmed and the harmer that are based upon c ontradictories which do not abide 
together. In that case, if even the supreme mundane quality level of the path of preparation 
itself would need to  be specifically abandoned , then “Bodhisattvas must accomplish it with 
effort for many eons through being ornamented by infinite collections , but t hey should 
abandon it through effort” are senseless words.  
 OPINION: In the way  a swan separates milk that has been mixed in water, although they are 
one entity, they are to be differentiated into individuals. RESPONSE: Understand that no matter how great the potency of the cause that generates as 
a single entity may be, the cause for generating (i) a valid cognizer realizing sound is 
impermanent and (ii) the manifest superimposition that apprehends sound as permane nt as a 
single entity does not even exist slightly.  
 Hence, as in the meaning of the partial concordance with definite discrimination 
possessing the conceptualizations cited above, since that has been taught to be possession in 
taking thorough affliction, it is to possess [those conceptualizations] as objects of 
abandonment. If it were to refer to possessing seeds, then since all possesses all, the meaning of possessing sequentially would not be possible.  
 Regarding the meaning of “ possession in taking tho rough affliction,”  hearers seeking 
mere pacification act to abandon afflictive obscurations specifically
; since otherwise they 
would not attain the result that is desired, they make effort for the purpose of that. Likewise, bodhisattvas take the conceptual izations that apprehend apprehended -objects and 
apprehender -subjects as truly existent to be the main objects to be abandoned specifically
; 
since otherwise they would not attain omniscience, the desired result, they make effort for the sake of that. This i s the meaning of possession in taking thorough affliction, because it is 
said, “The afflictions of the bodhisattvas are the conceptualizations.”  
 For otherwise, it would be incorrect for their conceptualizations to be afflictions, and 
another meaning of ta king [those] as afflictions would also be incorrect. By way of the 
essential point of the degree of difficulty and ease of abandoning the four 
conceptualizations, on the occasion of the heat level, in merely weakening the conceptions 
of apprehended- objects  as truly existent in regard to the objects of abandonment, with 
respect to the antidotal class there is no degeneration of the conceptions of apprehended -
objects as truly existent. Thus, although there is no difference in all phenomena being 
realized as e mpty of true existence, due to the differences in the force of the length of time 
of familiarization and the increase of excellent qualities, since the four manifest 
conceptualizations will progressively become weaker on those four occasions, that is the 
meaning of possession by that manner of the manifest [conceptualizations] being the objects 
of abandonment.

because  it occurs in the tradition  of the great trail -blazers who made the presentation of 
the hearers’ vehicle that  the roots of virtue of “transformed into heat” and so forth of 
those hearers and solitary realizers : obs erve the four truths, the nature of the properties 
of the definitions of purified suffering and origins, included [respectively] within “that

 QUALM: What are the purified cessations and paths in which the aspects of the attributes have been abandoned like?  
RESPONSE: When one initially comprehends the objects to be known and objects to be 
abandoned, the purified  bases of characterization of sufferings and origins, one will 
subsequently be able to comprehend the existence of merely true cessations (in which 
sufferings and origins are ceased) and true paths, which act to attain that. After that, one will 
comprehend  that one will be able to actualize the cessations in which sufferings are ceased 
and, in order to do that, true paths are to be relied on for a long time in the continuum, so having taken cessations and paths which have been previously realized as the bas es for the 
attributes, one meditates by way of making distinctions of the eight attributes.

COMMENTARY  
The bodhisattvas’ partial concordances with definite discrimination, through the strength of 
skillful means, obse rve the properties of the four truths, however possible: (1) by means of 
cause on some, (2) by means of result on some, (3) by means of the very entity on some, and (4) by means of the aspect of dharmata on some. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
130 EXPLANATION  
The subject —the bodhisattvas’ partial concordances with definite discrimination —
observe the properties of the four truths, however  possible  the modes of apprehension 
that are  their aspects are, because of observing the four truths through the strength of 
skillful means  in practicing th e complete path: (1) by means of cause on some  occasions 
of middling forbearance, (2) by means of result on some  occasions of small supreme 
mundane quality, (3) by means of the very entity  of the two truths on some occasions of 
small forbearance, and (4) by means of the aspect of dharmata on some  occasions that 
are the nine others. It is said that “For they observe by means of the aspect of dharmata 
during meditative equipoise, and by means of entity, cause, and result during subsequent 
attainment.”  This i s because the individual occasions are posited from the point of view  of 
the mentality of proximate  trainees; otherwi se, all would observe all.

COMMENTARY

EXPLANATION  
Know that, like the former  path of preparation of the hearers , they also always observe 
the four truths by way of other aspects , impermanence and so forth . This is because they 
must train well in  the mentality of the beings of middling capacity for the sake of destroying 
the glory of afflictions, generating disillusionment regarding  cyclic existence, and taking 
care of Hinayana  lineage -bearers on that occasion.

2B2C -1A2B -2B1C -2D. Dispelling objection 
COMMENTARY 
Why? This is solely a dissimilar presentation; because of that, repudiations based on the 
other v ehicles should not be expressed at any time.  
 
EXPLANATION  
OPINION: If one were to observe the four truths by way of infinite aspects, this would contradict [Vasubandhu’s] Treasury of Manifest Knowledge ( Abhidharmakosa) [7.12c], 
which says “The stainless do  not have aspects other than the sixteen.”  
RESPONSE: Repudiations based on the other vehicles  that do not meditate on that other 
than the sixteen aspects of the four truths should not be expressed , not just on this 
occasion, but also  at any time , before or later. Why? Because this  practice of the complete 
aspects of the four truths  is solely a presentation  (dissimilar  from the former) that takes 
the Mahayana  scriptural baskets as valid with respect to Mahayana  lineage -bearers.

1.10 The objects of observation are impermanence and so forth,  
Bearing the support of the truths.   
The aspect is stopping adherence of those and so forth.  
It is the cause of attaining the three vehicles together.  
 
EXPLANATION

are the objects of observation of the small heat level of the Mahayana  path o f preparation 
because they are the bases for eliminating the superimpositions of it . Apply [the sign] 
similarly to all the remaining objects of observation.  
 The subject—the exalted wisdom of the small heat level which stops adherence, 
exaggerated abidanc e, and so forth with respect to the sixteen attributes of those four truths  
as truly existent —is the aspect of the small heat level because it is an exalted wisdom of the 
small heat level that stops the superimpositions in the wrong class with respect to 
phenomena that are its objects of observation. Also apply this similarly to the remaining 
objec ts of observation.  
 Sutra says, “ Sariputra , when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, 
adherence, exaggerated abidance, and knowing all, with respect to the ‘impermanence,

result because it is the cause of the Mahayana  path of seeing that will attain the three 
vehicles  together . Apply this also to those below. Sutra says “Also those who wish to train 
in the grounds of hearers should listen to this very perfection of wisdom.”

COMMENTARY 
Regarding the objects of observation of the middling level, since, due to the entitiness  of 
dharmdhatu, impermanence and so forth and emptinesses do not have their own entitinesses , 
“impermanence and so forth and emptinesses are mutually one nature.” Regarding the 
aspect, that which is “not taking forms and so forth as one’s own” by negating the 
entitinesses  is that.  
EXPLANATION  
Those distinguished by impermanence and so forth and emptinesses being mutually one 
nature are the objects of observation of the middling [peak] level.  
 The subject —impermanence and so forth and their emptinesses —are pervaded by not 
having their own entitinesses  ultimately, due to the very entity of dharmdhatu, 
emptiness of true existence. That which is  the exalted wisdom of the middling [peak] level 
realizing not taking forms and so forth as one’s own ultimately by negating the truly 
existent entities  themselves, is the aspect.  
 
COMMENTARY  
Regard ing the objects of observation of the great level, due to the very absence  of 
entitinesses , “those forms and so forth not seen as signs that are blue and so forth.” 
Regarding the aspect, since wisdom thoroughly differentiates phenomena perfectly, 
“definite ly realizing all things as not observed.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
Due to the very absence of entitinesses  ultimately, those forms and so forth not seen  as 
ultimately established signs that are blue and so forth are the objects of observation of 
the great [peak] level . The exalted wisdom definitely realizing all things  by way of not 
observing the three circles to be truly existent is the aspect. The objects to be investigated 
do not exist truly, since  the wisdom  analyzing the ultimate thoroughly differentiates  
[them] perfectly  in that manner.
