2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2C2A. Explanation  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding the object of observation of the small level, since  the 
entitiness of characteristics and the characteristics of the entitiness  are just same  in 
emptiness, “just being devoid of  the entitiness  of forms and so forth.” Regarding the aspect, 
in the perspective of a being bearing the object of observation, “fo rms and so forth, just 
without the thing , the entitiness .”   
 
EXPLANATION  
Forms and so forth distinguished by just being devoid of  the entitinesses  ultimately are 
the object s of observation of the small forbearance level. The entity does not exist 
ultimate ly, since the entitinesses  of characteristics , characterizing by th ese, and the 
characteristics of the entitinesses , these, the object s to be characterized  are just same  in 
emptiness  ultimately. The realization of “the entitiness of forms and so forth”  in the 
perspective of a being bearing  the object of observation  who adheres to true existence, 
even though  forms and so forth are just without the thing  ultimately, is the aspect of the 
small [forbearance].  
 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding the object of observation of the middling [forbearance] level, since just not 
produced  by nature, with respect to forms and so forth, “birth does not exist and nirvana 
does not exist.” Regarding the aspect, by only having realized the self- entity of all 
phenomena, “body and so fort h pure in all ways.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to forms and so forth , birth  in samsara does not exist [ultimately] and 
nirvana  does not exist ultimately is the object of observation of the middling . Samsara 
and nirvana do not exist ultimately, since they are just not produced  by nature  ultimately. 
The exalted wisdom of the middling forbearance level, by only having familiarized through 
having realized that the self- entity of all phenomena  is not established ultimately, 
realizing that three doors, body and so forth, the noble signs, land, and so forth will be 
attained pure in all ways , is its aspect.  
 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding the objects of observation of the great, since specific and general characterist ics 
are untenable, “all phenomena just being devoid of  signs.” Regarding the aspect, due to only 
the nature, since just separated from the support of signs of forms and so forth, “not 
believing  through attention to the believed , and not knowing through att ention to thusness.”   
 
EXPLANATION  
Since specific and general characteristics are untenable  ultimately, all phenomena just 
being devoid of  signs are the objects of observation of the great [forbearance] level. The 
exalted wisdom of the great forbearance —realizing (i) not believing  signs  through 
attention to the believed  that is realized by way of the meaning -generality of emptiness of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
139 true existence, the support of signs, and (ii) not knowing  the signs through attention to 
thusness  that is directly realized —is its aspect, [because]  the support of specific and 
general signs of forms and so forth is pervaded by being just separated from  true 
existence , due to  only being the nature  of not existi ng ultimately.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2C2B. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
In that way , because of forbearance toward the great phenomen on due to not going to bad 
migrations, “transformed to forbearance,” possessing objects of observation and aspects, is 
of three types.  
 
EXPL ANATION  
In that way,  because of forbearance by way of having separated  from manifest fear  
toward the great phenomen on, the phenomenon that is not produced  due to not going to 
bad migrations  through (i) having attained small forbearance towards the phenomen on 
that is not produced and (ii) having attained non -analytical cessation with respect to rebirth 
in bad migrations , it is explained in the Great Commentary in this [context], “one who has 
attained forbearance does not fall into the bad migrations.” Hence,  the teaching that is 
given by the Acarya  that “bodhisattvas do not act to generate forbearance”  is to be 
contemplated, because it has been taught that bodhisattvas realize forbearance.  
 This also does not contradict sutra, which says “one who has  attained forbearance does 
not go to the bad migrations,” because [that sutra passage is taught with] the intention that “[one who has attained forbearance does not go to the bad migrations] through the force of 
karma.” Know that this [explanation] is defi nite in this manner.  
  Regarding the meaning of “Thus, by the directive of the Bhagavan, Sariputra  and others, 
carrying Sundari, went to the hell realm is explained to be another position,” the Acarya  
says that such an explanation does not refute that of Acarya  Vasubandhu; it refutes the 
system of the hearers’ school set forth in the root text and [self- ]commentary of 
[Vasubandhu’s]  Treasury of Manifest Knowledge ( Abhidharmakosa). It refutes someone 
from that school who asserts that rhinoceros -like solitar y realizers and bodhisattvas generate 
the heat level of the path of preparation and beyond in a single session, whereby the taking of rebirth in the bad migrations through the force of the bodhisattvas’ compassion occurs on 
the occasion of the path of accu mulation, but it is impossible for them to go [to the bad 
migrations] beyond that [path of accumulation] and, in particular, upon attaining 
forbearance, not just Hinayana  persons, but also bodhisattvas do not take rebirth in bad 
migrations. This also does not contradict the Abhidharma Sutra: it means that one is not 
born in the bad migrations through the force of karma upon having attained forbearance; it is not that one does not go there for others’ welfare. This is because not only do 
bodhisattvas attain forbearance, it is taught in the Vinaya scriptural basket that “Arya 
Sariputra  and others, carrying Sundari, went to the hell realm.” Know that this attaining of 
the heat level and above in a single session was taught in the Hinayana  scriptural baskets by 
the Bhagavan himself for the very purpose of causing trainees to enter [the path].

1.15cd Meditative stabilization, the very activity of that, 
Prediction and has extinguished clinging,   
 
1.16ab The three, mutually in the same entity,  
The m editative stabilization  that does not conceptualiz e—

object of observation of the small [supreme mundane quality] level; and spontaneous 
engagement in the activity of that meditative stabilization is the aspect. The speci al cause 
of hidden prediction , the middling supreme mundane quality,  forms and so forth that are 
devoid of  entitinesses  ultimately  are its objects of observation; the exalted wisdom that has  
extinguished  coarse  clinging  to the three circles as existing ult imately  is its aspect. The 
three  circles mutually in the same entity  in the nature of dharmata is the object of 
observation [of the great  supreme mundane quality];  the meditative stabilization  of the 
great supreme mundane quali ty that does not conceptualize  any limit is its aspect.  
 Respectively, sutra says, “Regarding this, the meditative stabilization of the great 
bodhisattvas, all phenomena are not produced;” “By which meditative stabilization will the objects of utilization of all meditative  stabilization be experienced? That is called the 
‘meditative stabilization going heroically;’” “ Sariputra , those great bodhisattvas abiding by 
way of these meditative stabilizations are also predicted to unsurpassable complete 
enlightenment by those previ ous tathagatas;” “I do not perfectly subsequently see 
meditative stabilization; I do not hav e a clinging mind thinking ‘I am in absorption; I shall 
set in meditative equipoise; I have set in meditative equipoise;’” “ Sariputra , there is no thing 
called ‘other perfection of wisdom,’  ‘other meditative stabilization,’  and also ‘ other 
bodhisattva; ’” and “Venerable Subhuti, does the son of the lineage discriminate in that 
meditative stabilization? I say, Venerable Sariputra , there is no discriminating.”

141 2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2D2. Commentary  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2D2A. Explanation  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2D2B. Summary  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2D2A. Explanation  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding the object of observ ation of the small [supreme mundane 
quality] level, “Meditate on the absence  of production of all phenomena and on the 
meditative stabilization of going heroically and so forth.” Regarding the aspect, “By the 
power of one’s prayers, merit, exalted wisdom, and the dharma dhatu , spontaneously, 
exactly in accordance with the fortunes in all the world systems, the engag ement  in the 
activity of meditative stabilization.”   
 
EXPLANATION  
“Meditate on the absence of production of all phenomena and on the meditative 
stabilization of going heroically and so forth .” The explicitly indicated meditative 
stabilization and forms and so forth of inseparable entity in suchness are the objects of 
observation of the small  supreme mundane quality. The exalted wisdom of the small 
supreme mundane quality realizing the engag ement in the activity of meditative 
stabilization spontaneously, exactly in accordance with the fortunes  of trainees in all 
the world systems  when one has bec ome enlightened, is the aspect. One engages in exalted 
activities spontaneously by  (i) the force of one’s  previous prayers , the two collections of 
merit and exalted wisdom, and (ii) the power of the  pure and just inconceivable 
dharma dhatu . 
 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding the object of observation of the middling, the buddhas make prediction to the 
yogis who possess perfectly engaged meditative stabilization, “This is dharmata.” 
Regarding the aspect, since all conceptualizations are incorrect, in regard to realizi ng the 
self-entity of a bodhisattva’s meditative stabilization, “a consciousness thinking, ‘I am 
placed in equipoise’ and the like will not arise.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
The buddhas make prediction of unsurpassable enlightenment  to the yogis who possess 
meditative  stabilization perfectly engaged  upon meditation on the absence  of nature of 
apprehended- objects and apprehender -subjects, “This is dharmata.” Hence, the special 
cause of prediction and forms and so forth of inseparable entity ultimately are the objects of  
observation of the middling [supreme mundane quality] level. When a bodhisattva’s meditative stabilization  of middling supreme mundane quality places in equipoise in order 
to realize  and attain self- entity , “a consciousness that apprehends the three circl es as truly 
existent, thinking, ‘I am placed in  equipoise  now’ and the like  will not arise,”   since all 
manifest conceptualizations that are apprehensions of true existence are incorrect. The 
exalted wisdom of the middling supreme mundane quality that does  not conceive the three 
circles as truly existent is  the aspect. 
 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding the object of observation of the great, “meditative stabilization by way of dharmata, the bodhisattva, the perfection of wisdom, and the three meanings being mutually 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
142 one entity.” Regarding the aspect, since all phenomena just are not existent, “meditative 
stabilization that does not conceptualize is the excellent method.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —meditative stabilization by way of dharmata  which is empty of true 
existenc e, the bodhisattva, the perfection of wisdom  that is the object of meditation, and 
the four truths distinguished by the three meanings being mutually one entity —are the 
objects of observation of the great supreme mundane quality level because they are the 
bases for eliminating the superimpositions regarding those. The subject —the exalted 
wisdom of the great supreme mundane quality realizing that meditative stabilization  that 
does not conceptualize  ultimate  existence  is the excellent method  for attaining 
buddhahood —is its aspect because it is an exalted wisdom of the great supreme mundane 
quality that acts as the antidote to the wrong superimpositions regarding the attributes of its 
objects of observation. [T hey] are not established as an object of investigation in meditative 
stabilization on suchness, since all phenomena just are not existent ultimately.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -2D2B. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
Thus, because of being the supreme of all mundane  qualities, “supreme quality,” possessing 
objects of observation and aspects, is of three types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Thus,  because of being the supreme of all mundane  qualities  since an uncontaminated 
exalted wisdom will be directly produced , [it is called] “supreme quality.” Pos sessing 
objects of observation and aspects, [it] is of three types . 
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3. Dispelling objection about the mode of expression  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3A. Objection  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3B. Reply  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3A. Objection  
OBJECTION: Since these four paths are  concordant causes of the clear realization of the 
truth, through observing the four arya truths one must realize emptiness and so forth, 
qualities that are their attributes. Hence, even at the time of indicating these with terms, since one will not realiz e them if the objects of observation —the four truths —and the 
aspects —the qualities that are attributes in terms of the four truths —are not indicated, it is 
appropriate to indicate them explicitly in that manner. However, at the time of indicating the 
objec t of observation of the small heat level… [sentence continues in Explanation below]  
 
COMMENTARY 
Except for “The objects of observation are impermanence and so forth, bearing the support of the truths,” although the objects of observation and aspects are o bjects to be expressed by 
expressing the special quality -bearer and qualities, the objects of observation and aspects 
are expressed in all cases by expressing the special qualities…  
 
EXPLANATION  
[continuing from Explanation  above] … except for “The objects of observation are 
impermanence and so forth, bearing the  aspect of the truths ” [1.10ab, in the context of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
143 the small heat level], when indicating the objects of observation and aspects  of the 
remaining ones, it is inappropriate that they are expressed in all cases by  only expressing 
the special qualities  because, it is appropriate for the  special objects of observation  and 
aspects  to be objects  whose qualities are to be expressed  in terms of that  quality -bearer . 
 
Although [the above paragraph] is not conne cted to an explicit passage of objection, there is 
no contradiction in explaining the meaning in this manner.  
In this context:  
OPINION: “The first two lines [1.10ab] that indicate the objects of observation of the small heat level explicitly indicate that  the objects of observation of the small heat level are the 
four truths, whereby the term that expresses the qualities —‘of that’—imply that the 
remaining objects of observation and aspects are different in entity from the four truths. 
Thus, one would not r ealize that the remaining ones are based upon the four truths.” When 
refuting such an explanation to be the meaning of the objection, it is extremely clear that the 
above  repudiation is irrelevant: “It follows that it is inappropriate for the statement ‘Th e 
remaining ones are expressed in all cases by expressing the qualities’ to occur, because the objection is about the remaining ones being expressed by both types of terms [expressing 
the quality -bearer and the qualities].”  
 
Further, it is appropriate tha t the terms that indicate the remaining objects of observation 
and aspects express that (i) the objects of observation are based upon the four truths, and (ii) 
all the objects of observation and aspects are qualities of those [four truths]. Without being 
expressed in this manner, how is the reply “There is not the fault of the quality -bearer not 
being understood by expressing the remaining ones with terms that express qualities” and 
answering in response, “Since the two types of terms do not have different objects to be 
expressed, the quality -bearer is understood,” not contradictory? Think about it! If the two 
types of terms do not have different objects to be expressed, then it is contradictory for the terms indicating the remaining objects of observation a nd aspects to express only the 
qualities, and not express the quality -bearer. 
 Also, if one asserts that, from among the many qualities with respect to sound that exist, 
only impermanence is known, then at the time of expressing “impermanence of
 sound,” 
saying that “Because impermanence is implied to be the quality of sound through having 
eliminated other isolates with the sixth case [genitive particle ‘of’], the other attributes have 
been removed and ‘the quality expressed,’” is clearly declaration that is  motivated by one of 
the three poisons, while one’s awareness has not even slightly turned towards the modes of 
the two types of terms, because it is erroneous declaration.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3B. Reply  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3B1. There is not the fault of  not realizing that due to being based upon the quality -
bearers, the four truths not having been explicitly expressed  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3B2. Although it was not explicitly expressed in that manner, it should be understood 
through implication  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
144 2B2C -1A2B -2B2A -3B1. There is not the fault of not realizing that due to being based upon the quality -bearers, 
the four truths not having been explicitly expressed  
Although the remaining objects of observation and aspects were not explicitly expressed to 
be based upon the four truths, i t follows that there is not the fault of not realizing that they 
are based upon them, [sentence continues in Explanation below]  
 
COMMENTARY 
… because “Except for removing and not removing other attributes, there is not any difference in meaning regarding t hese” occurs in the system.  
 
EXPLANATION  
[continuing from Explanation  above] … because “ Regarding the two types of terms 
expressing the qualities and the quality -bearer that rely on a single basis, except for 
removing  and not removing  other attributes, there is not any difference in meaning 
regarding these” occurs in the system  of the great trailblazer. 
 Further, when expressing “the body of an upper grinding stone” and “the head of 
Drajen,” one is made to understand that the body and the head are the base s for the 
attributes, and the upper grinding stone and Drajen are the qualities that are the attributes. 
Similarly, [Dharmakirti's] Commentary on [Dignaga's] 'Compendium of Valid Cognition'  
(Pramanavarttikakarika ) explains, “That taken as a branch of the general,” whereby when 
expressing “form of a vase,” one is made to understand that form is the basis for the 
attribute and vase the attribute; when expressing “beautiful form,” if one generates the doubt wondering “Of what?”, one is made to understand [tha t it is the beautiful form of a vase] 
through [the beautiful form] being attributed by the vase. 
 To express the terms of the quality and the quality -bearer that express a single isolate as 
an example, at the time of expressing “impermanent  sound ,” one is made to understand that 
the sound is the basis for the attribute and impermanence is the attribute, whereas  if one 
expresses 'impermanence of sound' one is made to understand that impermanence is the 
basis for the attribute and sound is the attribute, whereby although there is no difference in 
both the former and latter terms that express “sound” expressing the single isolate of sound, it is as though the quality and the quality -bearer which [respectively] removes and does not 
remove the other attributes are individually understood as objects of the awareness. Hence, at the time of expressing “impermanence of sound,” taking that to indicate that 
impermanence is the attribute is clearly a case of not having realized the meaning of any of 
the great textual sys tems.  
 It is clear that [the objection presented above] is an objection regarding whether or not 
the word “truths” is present in this [context], because it has been taught that the Acarya  
Arya [Vimuktisena] explicitly indicated the objects of observation and aspects that 
presented the truths even on the occasion of the objects of observation and aspects of the 
small peak level.
93 If, not being so, the objection concerns  whether or not suffering, 
impermanence, and so forth have been explicitly indicated, the n it would be appropriate for 
the reply, “It is incorrect not to explicitly indicate the aspects of the truths in the context of 
all the remaining objects of observation and aspects, because they were explicitly indicated 
in the context of the small peak l evel,” to occur, but it did not occur that way.

COMMENTARY 
In one aspect, although  expressed differently in the perspective of the met ered stanzas, 
because the possession of the stopping of adherence and so forth will become separation 
from the affirmative and the negative in suchness, solely those included in the truths, 
sufferings and so forth are the objects to be observed and aspect. Also know those below 
similarly. 
 
EXPLANATION  
In one aspect  of the reply, only the realization with respect to  the truths, sufferings and so 
forth is the object to be understood as the aspect  of the small heat level, because the exalted 
wisdom of the small heat level realizing the possession of the stopping of adherence  as 
truly existent, exaggerated abiding, and so forth , with respect to the quality -bearers, the 
four truths is its aspect. Also know those aspects of middling heat and so forth below 
similarly.   Solely those  realized as the truths, sufferings and so forth are the objects to be 
observed of the middling heat level because the four truths distinguished by separation 
from the affirmative and the negative in suchnes s become  the objects of observation of 
the middling heat level. Also know those objects of observation of the great heat level and so forth  below similarly.   
 OPINION: This is not the meaning of “ preparation
94” in the path of preparation on the 
occasion below, because clear realizations will arise in a single continuity of similar type, 
whereby it is unsuitable that, when doubts have been eliminated with respect to the former, 
doubts will also be eliminated with  respect to the later in return, for that reason is similar 
also for the Mahayana  path of preparation indicated on the occasion below. Otherwise, the 
expresser will not be engaging through exclusion.  [RESPONSE:] This [explanation] explicitly indicated here summarized the meaning of 
“The aspect is stopping adherence of that and so forth” [1.10c] and “Freedom from 
assembly and establishment of forms and so forth” [1.11 a]. Although
 the remaining objects 
of observation and aspects are established to be based u pon the four truths —the support as 
well as quality -bearer—there is a reason for not explicitly expressing “bearing the support 
of the truths” with respect to the remaining ones, because it boils down to them just being 
expressed in a different manner of ex pression  in the perspective of the metered stanzas  
of the Ornament. “Although” is a word that summarizes the absence  of contradiction.

146 2B2C -1A2B-2B2B -1A. Identifying the generality  
From among the three types of conceptions in general —an incorrect indicative conception, 
a mind bearing a rough aspect, and a conception apprehending [a sound -generality and a 
meaning -generality] as suitable to be mix ed—here it is the last.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -1B. Analyzing whether they bear the aspect of negative or affirmative phenomena  
It is not correct to assert that [the conceptions] are solely conceptions of negative 
phenomena because conceptions of affirmative phenom ena are indicated both in this context 
and in the context of peak application.  
 It is not correct to assert that [the conceptions] are solely conceptions of affirmative 
phenomena, because  all the principal conceptualizations of apprehendeds and apprehenders 
included within the knowledge obscurations  in this and the later context must be indicated 
as being of two [possibilities]. Otherwise, one would not comprehend to what these are antidotes.   
 Someone who claims that “If the conceptions indicated here were conceptions of 
negative phenomena, there would be repetition of  the attributes that are the aspects” asserts 
that whatever is a conception of a negative phenomena indicated here is only a realization of 
apprehendeds and apprehenders as being devoid of  true existence, and does not know the 
classification of the objects of abandonment and antidotes. For, one must take even the adherence to the emptiness of true existence of apprehendeds and apprehenders as truly 
existent as a conception of a negative phe nomenon, and that is also a conceptualization 
included within the knowledge obscurations indicated in this context. The explanation of “conception of apprehendeds” and “conception of apprehenders” acts to characterize just a 
mere section; it is not that al l conceptions that are objects of abandonment are exhaustively 
similar with them in the mode of apprehension, because even adherence to the hearers’ 
paths and result as truly existent objects to be turned away  are conceptions that are objects 
of abandonment indicated here. Furthermore, they are merely indicated through summarizing them here. Know that they are mutually inclusive with those indicated in the 
context of peak application.  
 In addition, like  it has been taught that “Since the object of negation does not exist, it is 
clear that there is nothing to correctly negate,” know that both —(1) the apprehension of the 
aggregates as truly existent, and (2) the apprehension of the negation (in which the objects of adherence of that [apprehension of the aggreg ates as truly existent] are negated) as truly 
existent —are conceptualizations that are objects of abandonment. Through characterization 
by that, you should know that both [types of conceptions] bearing the aspects of negative 
and affirmative phenomena exis t. It is easy to realize that it is inappropriate to take the 
meditative equipoise of the path of preparation to be a conception that is an object of 
abandonment. 
 The meaning of the teaching given by the Acarya  Arya —that whatever is a first type of 
concep tualization of apprehendeds is the second —is as follows. He taught that if it is an 
adherence to apprehendeds as truly existent (through observing merely the thoroughly 
afflicted and completely pure apprehendeds), then it is pervaded by being a conception of 
completely pure apprehendeds. However, the assertion that he taught that a conception of thoroughly afflicted apprehendeds is pervaded by being a conception of completely pure 
apprehendeds is without relation. Hence, “general support bearer” and “suppor t bearer that 
is a mere thing” are explained.  
 Now it is explained in this manner:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
147  
1. Identification of the generality, conceptions of apprehendeds and apprehenders  
2. Explanation in particular the conceptions of apprehenders  
1. Identification of the gene rality, conception of apprehendeds and apprehenders  
With regard  to apprehendeds and apprehenders, although there are many modes, here, like  
asserted by the great Lama Lotsawa, the objects of utilization have been taken to be the 
apprehendeds, and the utilizers to be the apprehenders; the former is subsumed under the 
thoroughly afflicted class and subsumed under the completely pure class, whil e the later is 
subsumed under persons and subsumed under knowers and cognizers. From among the two, each is further divided into nine each by means of the objects of observation. Hence, 
adherence to objects of utilization as truly existent and adherence to  utilizers as truly 
existent is the meaning of conception of apprehendeds and conception of apprehenders.  
2. Explanation in particular the conceptions of apprehenders  
QUALM: What is the meaning of a substantially existent person and an imputedly existent  
being? If they were taken to refer to a self- sufficient substantial existent and that imputed to 
the collection of aggregates as a being [respectively], since an apprehender observing the 
first is an afflictive obscuration, it would be incorrect for it to be a conception of 
apprehenders that is a knowledge obscuration, and since an apprehender of the latter 
apprehends the meaning established by valid cognition, it would be incorrect for it to be a 
conception that is an object of abandonment. 
  Further, if p ersons that are able and not able to perform a function are taken to be 
substantially existent  and imputedly existent , it would be unreasonable to refute an 
apprehender of the former, because if it were refuted, it would become deprecation. Since 
an appreh ender of the latter is refuted even by a valid cognizer of conventionalities, it need 
not be refuted by way of observing the meaning of the mode of abidance.   
 Also, with respect to taking a person that is truly existent and [a person] that is empty of 
true existence like an illusion [respectively] to be a substantial existent and an imputed 
existent, it would become impossible for a conception apprehending an imputed being 
indicated here to be an innate [object of abandonment ]. 
 Hence, what is the differe nce between the substantially existent  and the imputedly 
existent  like?  
 
RESPONSE: There are two types of apprehen ders of [persons] as substantial ly existen t: (i) 
the apprehension of a person as being able to perform a function, and (ii) the apprehension of [a person] as self- sufficient substantially existent . The object of adherence of the latter 
being  refuted through the force of experience exists in aryas, not in ordinary beings. Hence, 
it has been taught that the apprehension of substantial existence be ars the support of 
ordinary beings whereas the apprehension of imputed existence bears the support of aryas. 
However, know that in general both [the apprehension of substantial existence and the apprehension of imputed existence] bear the support of both [ ordinary beings and aryas].  
 A manifest innate apprehension of imputed existence, which is an apprehension in which 
the collection of aggregates is imputed as a being, exists even in ordinary beings who have 
not entered the path. It is permissible to take  either a person as being able to perform a 
function or as  self-sufficient substantially existent ; through obse rving [the apprehenders of] 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
148 both of those, adhering to them as truly existent utilizers are [the conceptions of] 
apprehenders of substantially ex istent persons .  
 And through observing [the apprehender of] that imputed to the collection of aggregates 
as a being, adhering to it  as before  [as a truly existent utilizer] is a conception of an 
apprehender of an imputed being . Through observing [the apprehender of] a being who  is 
empty of true existence like an illusion, apprehending it as a truly existent utilizer is a 
conception of an apprehender of a imputedly existent person . Know  that it is of two types, 
the acquired and the innate. If these  two were discriminated by means of the object of 
observation, ridiculous consequences would follow.  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -1C. The mode of becom ing an attribute  
The mode of becoming attributes is possession in taking [them as] thorough afflictions as 
explained ab ove95 . Further, if one is able to abandon these conceptions by taking them as 
principal objects of abandonment, that will become a superior attribute of the path.  
However, saying that “These conceptions, due to being possessed as objects of 
abandonment , have been taken as attributes, whereby it follows that it is  superior” is a case 
of being obscured by counterfeit reasoning; it follows that hell sentient beings would be superior due to their greater suffering, because that is their attribute.  
 Also, when  these conceptions are abandoned by the path of preparation, flinging the 
consequence that the objects of abandonment are not definite in number as two would bring 
about ridiculous consequences; does the meditative equipoise of the path of preparation 
aban don or not abandon the manifest conceptions indicated here? In the latter case, it 
follows that the superimposition that apprehends sound as permanent would not be damaged even slightly by the inferential valid cognizer realizing that sound is impermanent. In the 
first case, the [absurd] consequence that the objects of abandonment would not be definite in number as the two —objects of abandonment by the path of seeing and objects of 
abandonment by the path of meditation —would become one’s own.  
 
OPINION: Sinc e the antidotes that abandon the seeds are definite as two, in general, the 
objects of abandonment are definite as two.  RESPONSE: That “the seeds are abandoned by the path of preparation” is not propounded 
even by others. Hence, the principal hindrances t o achieving enlightenment which is the 
object that is desired are these [conceptions]. Their seeds are being abandoned on the occasions of the two later paths. On this occasion, [the path of preparation] has been taken 
as attributed by acting to achieve en lightenment by way of having abandoned with effort 
some of the manifest [conceptions]. 
 How many meanings are there? In terms of the general presentation that will be 
explained, “attribute of the conceptions” has been explained. However, the antidotes that  
abandon the seeds are induced by its  own power, and since even the manifest conceptions 
can be abandoned by itself, this is the attribute of ability that is the path’s own entity. Analyzing whether or not the seeds of the conceptions and the manifest conc eptions are 
possessed in the entity of the path of preparation is a small point of doubt. The meaning of the explanation that they are possessed in respective order has already been explained 
above. Saying that “If the attribute of the conceptions is like that, then there would be no 
difference with the attribute that is the aspect” would be similar to saying that “If sound is 
                                             
 
95 See 2B2C -1A2B -2B1C -1A above.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
149 impermanent, sound would become an attribute.” Someone, having differentiated the 
conceptions of this [context] into seeds [of the c onceptions] and manifest [conceptions], 
while asserting that seeds are possessed during meditative equipoise and the manifest are possessed during subsequent attainment , stated contradictory aspects as the reason for their 
not being mutually the same entit y with the path of preparation. Since the contradiction is 
very easy to realize, I have not made effort to refute it.

1.17  By way of basis and antidotes to that, 
The c onceptions of apprehendeds are of two types.  
By way of particularities of confusion and aggregates and so forth, 
They have individually nine types.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The basis of division of the  conceptions of apprehendeds is of two types, by way of the 
divis ions into that bearing the support of the thoroughly afflicted basis  and that bearing the 
support of  the antidotes to that. The two have individually nine types  of bas es of division, 
by way of particularities  that observe confusion that is ignorance and that observe 
aggregates and so forth.

2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -2B2B -1A. Conceptions of thoroughly afflicted apprehendeds  
COMMENTARY 
Bearing the support of the thoroughly afflicted basis, (1) ignorance, (2) the aggregates of 
form s and so forth, (3) adherence to na me and form, (4) attachment to the two extremes, (5) 
not knowing the thoroughly afflicted and the completely pure, (6) not abiding on the arya path, (7) the objects of observation, (8) the self and so forth, (9) generation of purity and so 
forth: conceptua lizations of apprehendeds, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —[regarding] conceptualizations of apprehendeds bearing the support of the 
thoroughly afflicted basis —there are nine conceptualizations of truly existent 
apprehendeds that are objects of utilization —be cause there are [nine] in that way by means 
of the objects of observation, objects of observation with respect to the generalities and 
particularities of true sufferings and true origins. There are divisions into two by means of 
observing generalities, bec ause there are the two adherences:  
(1) to the generality of afflictions, afflicted ignorance , and  
(2) to the generality of suffering s, the contaminated aggregates of form s and so forth  
– as truly existent objects of utilization.  
 
Sutra says :  
(1) “Sariput ra, these phenomena do not exist in such a way as strongly attached  to by 
childish  ordinary beings ,” and   
(2) “Sariputra , because of inner emptiness [and so forth] until the entit iness  of non-things,  
form does not exist.”  
 With regard  to the  particularities of afflictions, there are four objects of observation, 
because there is adherence to truly existent objects of utilization by way of:  
(3) having observed adherence to name and form , craving for a n erroneous  result ,  
(4) attachment to the two extremes  of permanence and severance , craving for a n 
erroneous  object of knowledge,   
(5) observing non- faith, not knowing the thoroughly afflicted and the completely pure  as 
objects to be discarded and to be adopted [respectively], and  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
152 (6) having obs erved laziness, not abiding in a manner of strong enthusiasm on the arya 
path.  
 
Sutra says:  
(3) “Having investigated those phenomena which do not exist in that way, they adhere to name and form.”  
(4) “Those adherences to phenomena conceive the two extremes which do not exist.”  
(5) “Do not know and do not see ‘thoroughly afflicted form’; do not know and do not see 
‘completely pure form’.”  
(6) “They do not abide; how do they not abide?” “They do not abide in the perfection of generosity.”  
 
With regard  to the particularities of suffering s, there are three objects of observation, 
because there is adherence to truly existent objects of utilization by way of having observed:  (7) the objects of observation  that are contaminated feelings, the three fruitions of comfort, 
discomfort, and neutrality,  
(8) the supports of feelings, the self, others, and so forth, and  
(9) generation of purity  and so forth when the nature of suffering feelings is understood, 
by way of disillusionment due to it; this is because when one is disillusioned due to feelings, 
the cessation which is the separation from suffering feelings, as well as the path that 
proceeds to that, are induced.   
 Sutra says:  
(7) “When a great b odhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, he does not observe and 
does not perfectly subsequently see the perfection of wisdom. Train in that way.”  
(8) “Because of being the very pure, one does not observe and does not see the self.”  
(9) Sariputra , the very pure is without generation, does not arise, and  is without manifest 
application.”  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -2B2B -1B. [Conceptions of completely pure apprehendeds]  
COMMENTARY 
Bearing the support of antidotes, (1) the aggregates, (2) entrances of generating, (3) classes, 
(4) birth, (5) emptiness, (6) the meaning of the Perfections, (7 -9) the paths of seeing, 
meditation, and no -more -learning: conceptualizations of apprehendeds, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —conceptualizations of apprehendeds  bearing the support  of antidotes —are 
of nine types, because there are five observing completely pure objects of knowledge, one observing deeds, and three observing entity. The first sign [that there are five observing completely pure objects of knowledge] is established beca use there is adherence as before 
by way of observing: four included within objects of knowledge that are varieties —(1) the 
completely pure aggregates, (2) entrances of generating  included within the sources, the 
empowering condition , (3) classes  included w ithin the constituents, the substantial cause , 
and (4) completely pure birth  of the reverse process of [the twelve links of] dependent -
arising —and (5) the mode, emptiness. 
 
Sutra says:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
153 (1) “One who is not skilled in means conceptualizes form and adheres to  it.” 
(2) “One conceptualizes the eye and adheres to it.”   
(3) “One conceptualizes the constituents of the eye, form, and eye primary consciousness 
and adheres to them.”  
(4) “One conceptualizes ignorance and adheres to it.”  
(5) “One conceptualizes emptine sses, from inner emptiness until  the emptiness of the 
entitiness  of non-things , and adheres to them.”  
 
The middle sign [that there is one observing deeds] is established, because, having observed 
(6) the meaning of the Perfections  that are deeds, one adheres to them as being like that. 
Sutra says, (6) “One conceptualizes the six perfections and adheres to them.”  
 The last sign [that there are three observing entity] is established because, having observed 
the completely pure entiti es—(7-9) the paths of seeing, meditation, and no -more -
learning —one adheres to them as truly existing objects of utilization. Sutra says:  
(7) “One conceptualizes the thirty -seven harmonies with enlightenment and adheres to 
them.”  
(8) “One conceptualizes the  four concentrations, the clairvoyances, the immeasurables, and 
the formless absorptions and adheres to them.”  
(9) “One conceptualizes the ten powers of the tathagatas, [and so forth] until omniscience and adheres to them.”  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -2B2B -2. Conceptio ns of apprehenders

COMMENTARY 
Bearing the support of substantially exist ent persons, (1) an independent self, (2 -4) a self 
that is unitary, a cause, a viewer and so forth, (5) the thoroughly  affliction ed, (6) separation 
from attachment, (7) seeing, (8) meditation, and (9) th e support which enacted the welfare: 
conceptualizations of apprehenders, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —conceptualizations of apprehenders bearing the support of substantially 
existent persons  that adhere to apprehenders as truly existent utilizers —are of ni ne types , 
because there are : (1) one object of observation, an apprehender of substantial existence that 
is its object of adherence, and (7 -9) eight divided by way of object of adherence. 
 
The first [that there is one object of observation, an apprehender of substantial existence that 
is its object of adherence] is established, because, having observed (1) an apprehender of an 
independent self, it adheres to it as a truly existent utilizer. Independence is the mode of 
apprehension of that apprehender of substantial existence, the observed object; it is not the 
mode of apprehension of a conception that is a knowledge obscuration, because [otherwise,] 
it follows that [the conceptualization of apprehenders] would be an afflictive obscuration. 
Sutra says, “ Sarip utra, do not closely observe the self; likewise, the sentient being, the life 
force...” The latter sign [that there are eight divided by way of object of adherence] is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
154 established, because there are three in terms of the generalities, the objects of observ ation, 
the apprehenders of substantial existents, and five divisions in terms of the particularities.  
 The first [that there are three in terms of the generalities, the objects of observation, the 
apprehenders of substantial existents] is established, beca use, having observed the 
apprehenders of an independent self as  (2-4) a self that is  unitary, a cause, a viewer , a 
knower , and so forth,  they adhere to them as truly existent utilizers. Respectively, the 
apprehenders of the aggregates, sources, and constit uents as an independent self are the 
three observed.   
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(2) “ Sariputra , do not closely observe form [and so forth] until do not closely observe the 
primary consciousness.”  
(3) “Do not observe the eye; likewise, until mentality.”  
(4) “Do not observe the eye consciousness with respect to the eye and form.”  
 
The latter [that there are five divisions in terms of the particularities] is established, because, 
having observed (5) an apprehender of the thoroughly  affliction ed such as ignorance as an 
independent self and  (6-9) an apprehender of separation from mundane attachment, 
seeing, meditation, the powerful support which enacted the welfare  as an independent 
self, and so forth, they adhere to them as truly existent utilize rs. 
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(5) “Do not observe dependent -arising.”  
(6) “Do not observe the first concentration [and so forth] until  the formless absorptions.”  
(7) “Do not observe the noble truths.”  
(8) “Do not observe the eight types of liberation and the nine final absorptions.”  
(9) “Do not observe the ten powers [and so forth] until  do not observe omniscience.”  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -2B2B -2B. Conceptions of apprehenders of imputed existen ce 
COMMENTARY 
Bearing the support of imputedly -existing beings, (1) the aggregates, (2) the sources, (3) the 
constituents, (4) dependent -arising, (5) the completely pure, (6 -9) paths of seeing, 
meditation, distinction, and no -more -learning: conceptualizations of apprehenders.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —conceptualizations of appr ehenders  that, having observed apprehenders  
bearing the support of imputedly -existing beings,  adhere to them as truly existent 
utilizers —are of nine types, because there are (1 -3) three divisions in terms of the 
generalities, the objects of observation, th e apprehenders that are their object of adherence, 
and (4- 9) six divisions in terms of particularities.  
 The first [there are three divisions in terms of the generalities, the objects of observation, 
the apprehenders that are their object of adherence] is established because they observe (1 -
3) the apprehenders of the aggregates, the sources and the constituents as beings. 
Respectively, sutra says:  
(1) “Subhuti, what do you think of that? Even form is other. Do you think illusion is also 
other?”  
(2) “Even t he eye is other. Do you think illusion is also other?”  
(3) “Even the eye, form, and eye consciousness are other. Do you think illusion is also other?”

155  
The latter [there are six divisions in terms of particularities] is established because they, 
having observed the apprehenders of:  
(4) [the twelve links of] dependent -arising, ignorance and so forth,  
(5) the completely pure  thirty -seven harmonies with enlightenment  
(6-9) paths of seeing, meditation, distinction, and no -more -learning as beings, adhere 
to the m as truly existent utilizers. 
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(4) “Even illusions are other. Do you think dependent -arisings are also other?”   
(5) “Even those until the harmonies with enlightenment are other. Do you think illusions are 
also other?”  
(6) “Even emptiness, signlessness, and wishlessness are other. Do you think illusions are 
also other?”  
(7) “Even illusions are other. Do you think the concentrations and formless absorptions are 
also other?”  
(8) “Even illusions are other. Do you think all emptiness es are also other?”  
(9) “Even the ten powers of the tathagatas and so forth until the eighteen unmixed qualities 
of the buddhas are other. Do you think illusions are also other?”  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2B -2C. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
The four conceptualizations and the four partial concordances of definite discrimination as 
such, as enumerated, are possessed.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The four conceptualizations and the four partial concordances of definite 
discrimination as such, as enumerated, are possessed  as objects of abandonment and 
antidotes that are abilities and destroyers.

2B2C -1A2B-2B2C -1. Making a connection by showing the purpose of other sequences  
OPINION: It is inappropriate to explain the tutor later in the extensive explanation, because 
this is discordant with the sequence in the brief indication [2B2C -1A2B -2B1].  
 
COMMENTAR Y

again at the end, an interceding stanza:   
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a purp ose for having taught the tutor  in the brief indication prior to the 
conceptions, because it was indicated in accordance with the easy joining of the stanza.  
Even though it has been taught earlier in the brief indication, there is a purpose for 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects

2B2C -1A2B -2B2C -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
1.19 Undaunted minds and so forth,  
Teaches the absence  of entitiness and so forth,  
Discards the discordant classes of that, 
Is in all ways the tutor. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalted wisdom in the continuum of a bodhisattva on the path of 
preparation that is not separated from the attention to an exalted knower of all aspects, that 
is skilled in the means of undaunted and unafraid minds and so forth  regarding the 
meaning of profound emptiness , and discards the discordant classes of that [exalted 
wisdom ], the attention of hearers and solitary realizers and any adherence to true 
existence —is in all ways the inner tutor  of the bodhisattva on the path of preparation, 
because it is a clear realization of that [bodhisattva] that acts to stop the extremes of cyclic 
existence and solitary peace. 
 Sutra says, “When a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, although he 
individually conceptualizes with a mind possessing an  exalted knower of all aspects, 
thinking, ‘Form is impermanent,’ he does not observe it,” and “Subhuti, when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom he meditates on the perfection of wisdom 
and observes it by way of  being separated from the attention possessing an exalted knower 
of all aspects.”  
 The subject —a buddha’s supreme emanation body who teaches all Dharmas, the 
absence  of entitiness ultimately and so forth  to that [bodhisattva on the path of 
preparation] and also reveals the path in which one discards  the discordant class of that  
virtuous spiritual guide, maras, negative companions, and the like —is the  outer tutor , 
because he is a virtuous spiritual guide who is the empowering condition that acts to instruct that [bodhisattva on the path of preparation] on the fully complete method and wisdom.  
 [Sutra says,] “Subhuti, furthermore, who is a virtuous spiritual guide  of a great 
bodhisattva? He teaches Dharma to that [great bodhisattva] by way of observing ‘form is impermanent’ and, except for omniscience, does not allow the bodhisattva to dedicate all 
the roots of virtue to the grounds of hearers and solitary realizers,” and “A negative 
companion of a great bodhisattva is one who separates him from the Perfection of 
Wisdom.”  
2B2C -1A2B -2B2C -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. With  the skillful means of undaunted and unafraid minds and so 
forth, in accorda nce with the mentality , teaching  the selflessness of all things and so forth, 
not possessing qualities of the discordant class, miserliness and so forth, since he is  the 
virtuous spiritual guide, is the tutor. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
157 EXPLANATION  
An emanation body —with the skillful means leading on the gradual path of undaunted 
and unafraid minds and so forth regarding the meaning of profound in a manner of gentle 
terminology as when  the king’s beautiful wife has died , in accordance with the mentality  
of the trainees , teaching the  selflessness  of persons and phenomena of all things and so 
forth , teaching the generated mind [of enlighten ment] and the deeds of the six perfections, 
revealing those who cause one to abandon the Mahayana text and path as negative 
companions, teaching not  possessing qualities of the class discordant  to the six 
perfections , miserliness and so forth —is the tutor  of a bodhisattva on the path of 
preparation, since he is the virtuous spiritual guide teaching the complete Mahayana path 
to him .

2B2C -1A2C -1A1A. Stating the Mind -Only  position and analyzing whether it is correct o r incorrect  
Sutra says, “That lineage of the bodhisattvas  is that which , having passed  down from 
beginningless time, has come , is obtained naturally , and is the attribute of the six sources .” 
Interpreting  the meaning of that passage, the Proponents of Mind -Only  say that  if a basis -of-
all is not posited, [lineage] is the potency that is suitable to produce the realizations of any 
of the three vehicles  when the uncontaminated seed that exists upon the six inner sources 
meet with conditions. However, if a basis -of-all is posited, when those very uncontaminated 
seeds existing on the basis -of-all are not nourished by co nditions, they are called ‘lineage 
abiding in the nature .’  They,  nourished by the conditions of hearing, will become special, 
called the ‘dev elopmental lineage.’ 
 
• That which is nourished by the condition of hearing profound and extensive Dharma is called the Mahayana lineage;  
• that which is nourished by the condition of hearing the hearers’ Dharma is called the hearers’ lineage;  
• that which is nourished by the condition of hearing merely the Dharma that apprehendeds 
are devoid of  nature, and below, is called the solitary realizers’ lineage;  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
158 • that which is definite in the cause of the realizations of the individual vehicles by way of 
entitiness is  called the definite lineage;  
• that which is suitable to be transformed into the cause of any of the vehicles by conditions is called the indefinite lineage;  
• that which is unsuitable to be nourished by the condition of hearing is called the severed lineage. 
 OPINION: Regarding this, the Acarya Arya [Vimuktisena] says, “[The subject] —such an 
uncontaminated seed —it follows that it is not a lineage abiding in the nature, because the 
meaning of that is not complete.” The reason is not established; it has the mea ning of acting 
as the cause of aryas’ realizations.  RESPONSE: Well, you cannot counter the consequence that it follows that even that perfect 
adoption due to conditions is a lineage abiding in the nature. 
 
Alternatively, it has been taught, “The lineages of those are imputed as lineage abiding in 
the nature, whereby it does not ha ve the same meaning as taking dharmata as the lineage.” 
The meaning of that, is that the presentation of lineage [above] is a presentation of merely the system of the Cognition -Only in [Maitreya’s]  Ornament for the Great Vehicle Sutras 
(Mahayanasutralamkara ); the teaching of that very pure nature, the dharmata of sentient 
beings’ minds in this treatise is explained to be the Middle Way system. Hence the 
convention of the actual nam e of ‘lineage abiding in the nature’ with respect to that 
uncontaminated seed included within compounded phenomena is incorrect. If one were to 
assert that as the lineage here, since it is the lineage that arose from perfect adoption, one 
should know that when it is imputed the convention ‘lineage abiding in the nature,’ it is the 
convention of an imputed name.  
2B2C -1A2C -1A1B. Our own system 
From between the two, l ineage abiding in the nature in our own Middle Way system  and 
lineage arising  from correct  adoption due to conditions , the first is that which is suitable to 
transform into a truth body; it also is the dharmata that is the emptiness of true existence of sentient beings’ minds. The truth body which it transforms into is the very pure nature that 
is devoid of all stains. However it is not taught that the pure nature of sentient beings, 
having transformed, change into a buddha’s meditative equipoise.   The existence of a potential upon sentient beings’ minds such that it is suitable for one to 
separate  from stains and suitable for one to generate aryas’ realizations is established by 
reasoning. That [potential] itself is deposited by the condition of hearing and is to be 
nourished by the condition of hearing. Therefore, the “lineage arising  from correct  
adoption” is just that which is suitable to transform into a form body when its transformation is culminated. It is what is posited to be the lineage abiding in the nature by 
the Proponents of Mind -Only . Acarya Arya [Vimuktisena] asserts that it is the imputed 
lineage abiding in the nature, and the fully qualified developmental lineage.   
 That very lineage abiding in the nature which was previously explained acts as the 
support for the realizations of the individual vehicles, and, since it is suitable to transform 
into the support for the realizations of any vehicle, temporally there are three lineages and 
one applies the convention “ indefinite lineage.”  However, we do not assert a lineage that is 
severed forever, because, in the commentary on the Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
159 Continuum (Mahayanottaratantrasastra), sutra was cited and the severed lineage was 
proven to be an explanation bearing an intention.  
 Further, that very [commentary by Asanga on the Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime 
Continuum  (Maha yanottaratantrasastra )] says:  
 
Because hatred for the Mahayana Dharma is the cause of those bearing wrong 
view and craving [not attaining enlightenment for a long time], the teaching that 
“Those bearing wrong view and craving bear the property of never att aining 
nirvana” is taught with the intention with respect to other times for the sake of overcoming hatred for the Mahayana Dharma. Never ever becoming pure is not 
fitting, because, with the intention that that which is suitable to be pure exists in 
all se ntient beings without distinction, the Bhagavan taught that:  
 
Although beginningless, possessing an end,  
The bearer of the property of permanent purity by nature, Is not seen, concealed by a cover on the outside since beginningless time,  
Just like a conce aled golden statue. 
 Its meaning is as follows:  
 
A. Basis in [Buddha’s] thought  for the explanation of a severed lineage 
B. Purpose for the explanation of a severed lineage 
C. Damage to the literal   
A. Basis in [Buddha’s] thought  for the explanation of a severed lineage 
The basis in [Buddha’s] thought  is that the path to liberation will not be generated in the 
continuum for a long time —innumerable eons. 
B. Purpose for the explanation of a severed lineage 
Since hatred for the Mahay ana Dharma is the cause for those bearing wrong view and 
craving not attaining enlightenment for a long time, [the explanation of a severed lineage] 
was taught for the sake of overcoming hatred for the Mahayana Dharma. 
C. Damage to the literal  
It is unreas onable that sentient beings will never purify stains, (i) because the lineage that is 
pure by nature exists in all sentient beings, and (ii) because the buddhas’ exalted activities engage all sentient beings. For, with even rebirth as a fortunate one who a ttains the state of 
high status being the buddhas’ exalted activity, it is impossible for there to be a sentient 
being who has never attained high status even once. 
 There are other extreme positions:  
• because even though the stains will be purified, the st ains in the continu a of some sentient 
beings are permanent, 
• because even though they are impermanent, the method for destroying them is not 
possible,  
• because even though it is possible, nobody knows the method, 
• because even though someone knows it, there i s some sentient being for whom it is not 
possible to generate [a mind] seeking it,  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
160 • because even though someone knows it, a person who cares for [sentient beings] with 
loving- kindness is not possible,  
• because it is not possible for some inferior sentient be ings to generate disillusionment due 
to suffering, 
• even though it may be generated, [sentient beings]  will not become an object of exalted 
compassion of the buddhas, and so forth.  
You should know that there is no sentient being for whom (i) stains are not suitable to be abandoned, and (ii) antidotes are not suitable to be generated, from among the reasonings 
refuting these extremes. 
 With respect to that very potential —for which the obscurations of sentient beings are 
suitable to be abandoned and supramunda ne paths are suitable to be generated —imputed 
with the convention “developmental lineage,” there are divisions into the three lineages, an 
indefinite lineage, and so forth. Further, you should know that this is in terms of temporal 
occasions; relative to t he final, all sentient beings bear the buddha lineage.  
 Explaining that [Asanga’s] commentary on the Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime 
Continuum (Mahayanottaratantrasastra) taught merely the Mind -Only  system is inapt; that 
has already been extensively expla ined in its Explanation . In that commentary, as proof for 
the buddha lineage existing in all sentient beings, the Abhidharma Sutra is cited:  
 
The sphere of beginningless time  
Is the state of all phenomena. 
Because it exists, all migrations and  
Even nirvana  will be attained. 
 In other texts that commentate on the Mind -Only  system, Acarya Asanga takes that as proof 
for the existence of the basis -of-all which is different in entity from the six collections [of 
primary consciousnesses]. Therefore, if that were  the proof for demonstrating the lineage 
abiding in the nature, it would be inappropriate to posit it as the proof for the other.  
 
OPINION: Although [you] assert that all sentient beings are bearers of the tathagata 
essence, lineage -bearers of that [tathag ata essence] are not asserted by the Proponents of 
Mind -Only . 
RESPONSE: Propounding that is simply proceeding to just what one has indicated. For those who lack the power to  discern  the texts that are assertions of the great trailblazers  
clearly without mi xing them up, it will be wonderful for them to be neutral with respect to 
discriminating the supreme texts.  
2B2C -1A2C -1A2. Divisions  
The general divisions of lineage have already been explained. The lineage that is explicitly 
taught here is of thirteen ty pes by way of divisions of the occasion. The conventional 
support of the achievings is bodhisattvas. That also is of thirteen types by way of divisions 
of the occasion. Through observing and meditating on that very emptiness of true existence 
of the six in ner sources, aryas’ realizations will be generated, hence it is called “lineage.” 
When familiarization with that becomes more and more outstanding, also the potential of 
the developmental lineage explained above will become more and more outstanding, hence  
it is called “lineage that transforms from development.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
161 2B2C -1A2C -1A3. Dispelling objection  
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
QUALM: What precedes the generated mind [of enlightenment], the partial 
concordances with liberation and definite discrimi nation, and so forth is that 
[lineage]. Hence, is lineage not to be explained only first? Why is it explained in 
this manner?  
 RESPONSE: That is true. However, that is the sequence in reality . Having 
explained the result first, the cause is indicated later, whereby this is the sequence of explanation. Hence there is no fault.  
 The meaning of that is as follows. Having first awakened the lineage, after that, one 
generates the mind [of enlightenment]; that is the sequence of the cause and result in reality . 
When the generated mind [of enlightenment], the partial concordance with liberation, and so 
forth are generated in one’s continuum, one will be able to realize the existence of lineage in 
one’s continuum, whereby the explanation here is made by means of th at sequence. 
 [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand says:  
 
QUALM: If the lineage abiding in the nature exists in all sentient beings, why is 
it that there are still infinite creatures possessing lineage who have not attained 
nirvana, even thoug h infinite buddhas have come?  
RESPONSE: It is because of possessing the four hindrances of lineage: predominance of afflictions, being held by negative companions, being deprived 
of necessities of life, and being under the control of other sentient beings . The 
teaching here is that one separates from the four hindrances of lineage in dependence on the Mahayana path of preparation, and, through observing and 
familiarizing with the lineage abiding in the nature by way of the wisdom arisen 
from meditation, on e will become empowered to attain the aryas’ qualities, hence 
it is presented from the path of preparation.  
2B2C -1A2C -1A4. The manner of leading accordingly  by way of lineage 
The method for examining the interests of the trainees at the beginning is as follows. Like it 
has been explained in Hearer’s Grounds, having caused them to hear the scriptural baskets 
of the Hinayana and Mahayana, when leading them to their goals , one wil l realize the level 
of their faculty. Since lineage  is distinguished by the accumulations [of merit and exalted 
wisdom] and the purification [of negativities and obscurations], one leads them by way of 
the stages of the path of the three —the beings of smal l, middling and great capacities. 
When, for the sake of generating Mahayana realizations, they have engaged in gathering 
great causes of accumulation and have purified well the faults that hinder the generation of 
the path by way of many avenues, prior to generating the mind [aspiring] for the Mahayana, 
at the time of initially gaining firm interest in the extensive dharma —joy in meditating on 
great compassion, engaging in extensive generosity, guarding extensive  ethics , joy in 
cultivating patience, and so forth —they are said to be “abiding in the Mahayana lineage,” 
and those persons are even explained to be “bodhisattvas who have not generated the mind 
[of enlightenment].”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
162  From there, if they have generated the foundational attitudes of the beings of smal l and 
middling capacity, when at the beginning they have trained well in the attitudes seeking 
others’ welfare and seeking unsurpassable enlightenment and made these firm, cause them 
to generate the mind [aspiring for] supreme enlightenment. After that, ha ving examined 
whether or not they are able to train in the infinite deeds of the bodhisattvas, when one sees that they will be able to guard the bodhisattva vows without becoming sullied by the root 
downfalls which contravene them, cause them to uphold the  engaging vows.  
 Due to having been persistent in the paths of hearers and solitary realizers for many 
lifetimes in the past, they [may] initially generate disillusionment that cannot bear the 
suffering of cyclic existence, and produce strong [aspiration]  seeking pacification of that at 
the beginning. When it seems unsuitable for the time being to lead them to the practice of the extensive dharma, lead them through those very paths [of hearers and solitary realizers].  
 Since it is necessary to indicate th e method for initially awakening the lineage of that 
path regardless of which path they enter, you should understand the manner of leading them by way of lineage at the beginning. Intending that, Acarya [Haribhadra] explained in [his] 
Great Commentary, “Th at is the sequence of meaning.” Know the two paths by way of the 
beings of small and middling capacities also as branches of both (i) the method for awakening the lineage and (ii) engagement by way of the stages of the path once the lineage 
has been awaken ed.

COMMENTARY 
In one who possesses the achievings, the partial concordance of definite discrimination which has been explained, the path of seeing, and so forth, furthermore, will arise. Thus, 
what serves as the support of the achievings:  
 
EXPLANATION  
Without awakening the Mahayana lineage, one will not engage in the generated mind [of 
enlightenment], and even if one were to engage in it, it would not be firm. Hence, it is 
necessary to awaken the lineage. In one who possesses lineage that i s suitable to give rise 
to the achievings, the partial concordance of definite discrimination which has been  
explained, the path of seeing which will be explained, and so forth, furthermore, will 
arise. Indeed the Mahayana path of accumulation is also supp orted on the lineage, but it is 
not indicated, because (1) the context of nourishing the lineage continually is the main one 
and (2) [the Mahayana path of accumulation] is not separated from all hindrances of 
lineage. Thus, I shall explain the lineage that  serves as the support of the achievings:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
163 2B2C -1A2C -1B1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
1.20 The six qualities of realization,  
Antidotes, abandoning,  
Fully reaching  those,  
Wisdom along with compassion,   
 1.21 That not in common with the learners, 
The stages  of others’ welfare,  
And exalted wisdom engaging without exertion – 
Know their support as lineage. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1-6) The dharmata that is  the support of the six qualities of realization that are with stains 
is called “lineage.” Indicating the support of:  
• heat, sutra says, “Subhuti, the tracks of a bird in the sky do not exist and are not 
observed. Likewise, the meaning of the word ‘bodhisattva’ does not exist and is not 
observed.”  
• peak, sutra says, “Abiding in the perfect end does not exist and is not observed.”  
• forbearance, sutra says, “Abiding in the form of a being who is an illusion does not 
exist and is not observed.”  
• supreme mundane quality, sutra says, “The meaning of the words ‘Practicing the 
inner emptiness of a being who is an illusion’ does n ot exist and is not observed.”  
• path of seeing, sutra says, “The meaning of the words with respect to the form of a 
tathagata foe -destroyer perfectly complete buddha does not exist and is not observed.”  
• path of meditation, sutra says, “The meaning of the w ords ‘no generation’ does not 
exist and is not observed.”   
 
(7-8) Abandoning  by way of approaching the generation of the antidotes , the uninterrupted 
paths , and approaching the cessation of the seeds that are the objects of abandonment , (9) 
the liberated paths , which fully reach  those  objects of abandonment , (10) wisdom along 
with compassion  which stop the extremes of cyclic existence and solitary peace , (11) that 
not in common with the learners , the hearers and so forth , (12) the achievings of the 
stages  of others’ welfare, and (13) exalted wisdom engaging  in the two types of objects 
of knowledge, modes and varieties, without exertion —know their support as lineage.  
 Respectively, sutra says:  
• “When the sun shines brightly, abodes of darkness do not exist and are not 
observed.”  
• “When the fire of the eon blazes, abodes of all types of compositional factors do 
not exist and are not observed.”  
• “With respect to the ethics of a tathagata foe -destroyer perfectly complete buddha, 
abidance of bad ethics do not exist and are not observed.”  
• “Abidance in the light of the sun and the moon does not exist and is not observed.”  
• “Abidance in the light of hearers, solitary realizers, constellations, stars, precious 
gems, and lightning does not exist and is not observed.”

exist and is not observed.”  
• “Abidance in the light of a tathagata foe -destroyer perfectly complete buddha does 
not exist and is not observed.”
