COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1 -4) For the time being, at the beginning, the mundane partial 
concordances of definite discrimination. (5 -6) After that, the supramundane path of seeing 
and path of meditation. (7 -8) After that, by the force of generating those, like throwing ou t 
of a thief and shutt ing of the door —the simultaneous generation and the cessation of the 
antidote and the discordant class. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1-4) The subject —for the time being  by way of serial indication , at the beginning, the 
dharmata of the mundane partial concordances of definite discrimination —is their 
support, because it is the dharmata of those which will be enhanced more and more through 
observing and meditating on it . (5-6) After that, the dharmata that is the support of the 
supramundane path of seeing  which newly realizes emptiness by way of direct cognition  
and path of meditation which familiarizes with that already realized is called “lineage .” 
Do not think that it indicates only the path of meditation of the seventh ground and below; 
the mere path of meditation is indicated.  
 (7-8) After that, the way in which the objects of abandonment are abandoned by the 
uninterrupted path of those paths are indicated as follows. By the force of generating the uninterrupted paths included within those  paths of seeing and meditation, the exhaustion of 
the seeds that are the corresponding objects of abandonment occurs simultaneously, like the 
simultaneous  throwing out of a thief and shutting of the door —the simultaneous  
approach to the generation of the anti dote, the uninterrupted path, and the approach to the 
cessation of the discordant class, the seeds that are the corresponding objects of 
abandonment  [occur] . This indicates the mode of abandoning the objects of abandonment 
by the uninterrupted paths of bot h paths; do not think that it is correct to apply it only to the 
uninterrupted path of the eighth ground. 
 If we were to express the way in which an uninterrupted path destroys the objects of 
abandonment in an example , when the uninterrupted path, the dharma forbearance of [true] 
sufferings of the Mahayana path of seeing is generated, the direct objects to be damaged, the 
seeds that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing , are ceased. The two —
approach to the generation [of the uninterrupted pa th] and approach to the cessation [of the 
seeds that are the objects of abandonment] —are simultaneous. That very uninterrupted 
arisal of what is similar to a previous type acts as a bearer of the property of [the object of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
165 abandonment] being unsuitable to be generated henceforth, by the force of the uninterrupted 
path. The start of that path in which a true cessation (which has come to be the bearer of the property of [the object of abandonment] being unsuitable to be generated by way of the imprint of the antidote) has been attained is called a “liberated path.” It is generated by the uninterrupted path.  
 At the time of the uninterrupted path, the corresponding object of abandonment does not 
exist and bears the property of [the object of abandonment] being  unsuitable to be 
generated. However, this is due to incomplete conditions and is not the attainment of an analytical cessation. Inferring from this, even though there is not even a mere particle of subtle stains of negative states at the time of the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuum of a sentient being of the tenth ground, do not take it that “they have been exhaustively abandoned.” For, the path (that has come to bear the property of [the object of abandonment] being unsuitable to be generate d on account of the imprints being deposited 
by the direct antidote) has not been attained. If you respond that the sign is not established, then it follows that cause and result would be simultaneous.  Someone, having accepted that the direct object to be  damaged does not exist at the time 
of the uninterrupted path of the path of seeing, asserted that subtle stains of negative states exist at the time of the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuum of a sentient being; this brings together the tenets  propounding abandonment by that which will be generated 
and propounding abandonment by that which will cease. Know that those who assert also 
that the first instant of an exalted knower of all aspects acts to directly damage [the subtle 
stains of negative  states] are not skillful  in the modes of the objects of abandonment and the 
antidotes.  
 Another person, without determining the meaning of the teaching from the [Asanga's] 
Compendium of Knowledge (Abhidharmasamuchchaya) which says: ‘It is not because the  
past has ceased…,’ asserts that, although the three modes of abandonment of the manifest [afflictions] are incorrect, the seeds that are the objects of abandonment and their direct 
antidote, the uninterrupted path are simultaneous. This is also inconclusive, because, in that 
case, one would have to assert that also illumination and darkness that are the direct object to be damaged and the damager would be simultaneous, since that [illumination and darkness] acts as an analogy for that [assertion].  
 Therefo re, it is not that the uninterrupted path abandons the direct object to be damaged, 
the seeds that are the objects of abandonment, (i) like a vase that has been destroyed by a hammer, which is a past [phenomenon] at the present time. It is also not abandoned (ii) like 
making an offering of what has not been generated at the present time. It is also not 
abandoned (iii) in a manner of ceasing simultaneously what exists at the present time, like dzos
96 bound together that are thrown down into a precipice. 
 Well, how is it then? In dependence on having familiarized with the antidotal class in the 
past, by means of (i) simultaneous approach to the generation of the antidote and approach to the cessation of the final similar class of the seeds that are the obje cts of abandonment, 
and (ii) simultaneous generation of the antidote and the cessation of that, having come to 
bear that property of the objects of abandonment being unsuitable to be generated is the 
meaning of having caused damage [to the objects of aband onment].  
 Hence, I do not see this passage  explained as  the modes of abandoning the objects of 
abandonment by both the uninterrupted path and liberated path as the meaning. You should 
                                             
 
96 A dzo ( mdzo ) is the offspring of a bull and a fem ale counterpart of a yak.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
166 know that, having thought that throwing out a thief and shutting the do or are not 
simultaneous, claiming that it explains the intention of this [passage] is inappropriate. 
 
COMMENTARY 
(9) After that, by way of not observing those, conceptualizations which possess generating and ceasing those have been abandoned. (10) After that, by the force of previous prayers and skillful means, generosity and so forth, wisdom and method with the characteristic of not abiding in samsara or nirvana. (11) After that, by generating those, qualities  not in 
common with those of hearers and so forth. (12) After that, in accordance with the 
mentality , by means of ulterior intention toward causing them to enter and so forth, the 
characteristic of setting in the paths of the three vehicles: the stages  of the others’ welfare.  
 
EXPLANATION 
(9) After th at, the dharmata which is the support of the liberated path  in which 
conceptualizations (that are conceptions of true existence which possess as their objects 
the existence of the generating and ceasing of those objects of abandonment and antidotes)  
have been abandoned ( by way of familiarizing with not observing those  
conceptualizations of objects of abandonment and antidotes as truly existent) is called 
“lineage.” (10) After that, the support of wisdom and method with the characteristic of 
not abiding in the extreme of samsara or the extreme of nirvana (by the force of 
previous prayers , thinking, “May I enact others’ welfare effortlessly,”  and skillful means 
of having trained in the six perfections , generosity and so forth ) is called “lineage,” and is 
attained from the eighth ground.   
 (11) After that, by generating those  method and wisdom, dharmas not in common 
with  those of  hearers and so forth,  the method for ripening sentient beings —the four 
individual correct knowledges —are attained from the ninth ground. (12) After that, the 
subject —a tenth -grounder —possesses the achieving of the stages of others’ welfare , 
because he is a bodhisattva who has attained  equality with a buddha, spontaneously 
accomplishing others’ welfare with the  characteristic of setting in the paths of the three 
vehicles in accordance with the mentality  of trainees, by means of ulterior intention 
toward causing them to enter  and so forth,  and by means of the definitive meaning .  
 With respect to that, there are:  
 
1. The difference between interpretable97 [meaning  sutras] and definitive  [meaning  sutras] 
2. The difference between [sutras] bearing intention and [sutras of] ulterior intention  
1. The difference between interpretable [meaning sutras] and definitive [meaning sutras]  
Like it has been taught in the King of Meditative Stabilizations Sutra ( Samadhirajasutra) 
and the Teachings of Akshayamati Sutra  (Akshayamatinirdeshasutra), sutra sets that 
explicitly indicate the meaning of emptiness, where the  meaning of the mode of abidance 
does not need to be explained through having been interpreted over and above them, are definitive in meaning; and (i) the sutras that indicate that compounded phenomena are impermanent and so forth, although literally acceptable, where the meaning of the mode of abidance needs to be explained through having been interpreted over and above them, and 
(ii) the sutras that are not suitable to be held literally are interpretable in meaning. With 
respect to the latter, there are four ulterior intentions.  
                                             
 
97 grangs  emended to drang according to page 372 of Sera Jhe’s edition of General Meaning .  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
167  (1) Regarding the ulterior intention with respect to the characters , for instance, whether 
the three natures have the attribute of being established or not established as truly existent 
has been taught in the Sutra Unraveling the Thought (Samdhinirmocanasutra ). (2) The 
ulterior intention regarding causing to enter, is such as the teaching that a self of persons exists for the sake of other [non -Buddhist] schools and some of our own [Buddhist] schools. 
(3) Concerning  the ulterior in tention regarding antidote , there are  four bearers of intention s. 
(3a) For example, with the intention of the three samenesses, the Teacher [Buddha] taught “I, at that time, was the Buddha Vipashvin.” (3b) The intention regarding other meaning  is 
[such as that in the teaching] “The father and the mother are to be killed.” (3c) The intention regarding other time  is [such as that in the teaching] in which it has been taught that one will 
be reborn in Sukhavati [Amitabha Buddha’s pure land] by simply making prayers to be reborn there as an antidote for those who are lazy. (3d) The intention regarding the mentality of persons  is [such as that in the teaching] that deprecates generosity and praises 
ethics as an antidote to complacency with merely generosity. (4) The ulterior intention regarding conversion  is [such as that in the teaching] “Know the essence in that which is 
devoid of essence,”  which converts poor syllables that are widely renowned into those 
which reveal an unrenowned profound meaning.  
2. The difference between [sutras] bearing intention and [sutras of] ulterior intention  
Although similar in being not being literally acceptable, that in which the basis of [Buddha’s] thought is taken as principal is that which bears intention , while that  in which 
the intention
98 is taken as principal is the ulterior intention . 
 If you wish to extensively know the distinctions between that definitive in meaning and 
that interpretable in meaning, understand them from [Lama Tsongkhapa] Je Rinpoche Losang Dragpa’s Treatise Differentiating Interpretable and Definitive Meanings: Essenc e 
of Eloquence .

2B2C -1A2C -1B1C -2. Summary 
COMMENTARY 
Only the entitiness99 that is the sphere of qualities (dharmadhatu) of the thirteen kinds of 
bodhisattvas due to the particularities of different occasions of the qualities of the 
achievings, that serves as the basis of the qualities which have been explained is indicated as 
“lineage.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
Only the entitiness that is  the sphere of qualities  (dharmadhatu)  in the continua of the 
thirteen kinds of bodhisattvas is indicated as “lineage”  in this context, because only that 
is the sphere  that serves as the basis of  the qualities  of the achievings  which have been 
explained. It is appropriate to divide bodhisattvas into thirteen, due to the particularities of the thirteen different occasions of the qualities of the achievings . Although the thirteen 
are posited by means of dissim ilar functions and so forth, it is not that the thirteen are solely 
of different entities.

2B2C -1A2C -1B2B -1A. Decisive analysis  
With respect to that, in general in this Ornament for Clear Realization, three vehicles are 
posited by way of faculty in relation to t he view. (1) The best view in the great vehicle, the 
view of the Middle Way (Madhyamaka), (2) the middling view in the middling vehicle, the view of the Mind -Only ( Cittamatra), and (3) the last view in the small vehicle, the common 
view of the selflessness  of persons, are posited. However, they are not definite; even in the 
case of suchness being present in all three views, there is no contradiction that there are 
three levels of faculties by means of whether or not suchness is quickly penetrated. Because 
the inability to abandon adherence to consciousness as truly existent has been indicated as 
an attribute of the inferior, one is able to realize the existence of a view higher than that, therefore it is inappropriate to explain the Ornament for Clear Realiz ation  as Mind -Only . 
 Furthermore, the Acarya Arya Vimuktisena also made the presentation that hearers and 
solitary realizers in this context also have the realization of the selflessness of phenomena. 
His Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  says : 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
170 With regard to that, the conceptions and the conceptualizations adhere to things 
and their signs. Because they are not existent, know them without attachment. 
Not being existent is the very nature of all phenomena. Hence, on account of this, 
dharmadhatu itself is th e cause of the aryas’ qualities. Thus it is indicated that 
“The lineage abiding in the nature is the support of the achievings.”  
 
It explains [that dharmadhatu is] the emptiness of true existence which is the absence of 
existence in the way apprehended by the attachment that adheres to things and their signs as 
truly existent. 
 With respect to that, the objection is stated: If dharmadhatu were  the lineage, then all 
sentient beings would abide in the lineage, because in general dharmadhatu abides in all. [The objector] thinks that abiding in the lineage refers to the lineage of the occasion of the 
path.  
 In reply to that, it has been taught that —since, whatever, when observed, will become the 
cause of the aryas’ qualities is expressed thus as the lineage —there is no ridiculous 
consequence with respect to it. The meaning is that one does not abide in the lineage of the occasion of the path by way of the mere presence of dharmata; if one observes dharmata and 
meditates on it by way of the path, at the time when it becomes the special cause of the 
aryas’ qualities, then it is posited as the special lineage. 
 “Even in that case, since dharmadhatu has no divisions, it is inappropriate for the lineages 
of the three vehicles to be different.” In reply to that, it is i ndicated that “Lineage is 
expressed as different by way of divisions of the supported qualities , the path that acts to 
observe.”  
 The support refers to the object of observation here, and the supported the [path] that acts 
to observe . The [path] that acts  to observe exist s even [in] the two vehicles of hearers and 
solitary realizers. In order to observe dharmata, it must be established in the perspective of 
the awareness; without severing true existence in the perspective of the awareness, 
emptiness of tru e existence will not be established in the perspective of the awareness; and 
if that is not established, dharmata will not be established in the perspective of the 
awareness. Furthermore, initially it must be ascertained in terms of one property -possessor 
basis, whereby it is indicated that, through observing the outer and inner property -
possessors, observation of their dharmata, the absence  of true existence, exists even in 
hearers and solitary realizers.  
 If taken like that, since even a realization of th e meaning of suchness exists in solitary 
realizers, solitary realizers are not pervaded by being unable to abandon adherence to the consciousness as truly existent. Hearers too must be divided into two [types], those who 
realize suchness and those who do n ot. 
 Even if one thinks, “We are not disputing different lineages of the three vehicles being 
inappropriate; we are arguing that the divisions of the thirteen lineages are inappropriate,”  
this is incorrect. [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  says: 
 
OPINION: It is as said [in sutra], “Manjushri, if dharmadhatu is only one, thusness is just one, and the perfect end is just one, then how does one impute 
vessel and non -vessel to it?”  
 
It is taught in the other sutras that “Since dharmadhatu has no divisions, how should one examine whether someone is a vessel or non -vessel for the Mahayana Dharma?” Through 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
171 such, one makes objections as before; it is inappropriate to argue about whether o ne is a 
vessel or non -vessel for the Mahayana Dharma if lineage is taken to be thirteen [types]. 
What is explained in the commentary also accords with that explained by the Arya in this 
context. This passage of the reply also condenses that very meaning of the reply taught in 
[Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand that there is not the fault of a ridiculous 
consequence.   
 In both [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  and [Haribhadra’s] Great 
Commentary , as the reason for positing dharmadhatu as the lineage of all three vehicles, 
“All persons who are aryas are thoroughly distinguished by the uncompounded” is cited. It proves the statement from the Vajra Cutter Sutra ( Vajracchedika ), “The buddhas and all the 
dharmas revealed by them do not exist.” The meaning is that all persons who are aryas of the great and small vehicles are posited by way of directly realizing the uncompounded ultimate in which phenomena are not established in suchness.  
  The presenta tion of the hearers’ path has been made in terms of the coarse selflessness of 
persons on most of the occasions at the beginning and the end. Even having done so, with 
respect to the Hinayana [trainees] which are to be cared for, there are two —those who 
become and those who do not become a vessel for the profound. With respect to that, the 
latter [those who do not become a vessel for the profound] is by far the majority, and when 
one has familiarized with the common realization of selflessness, through quic kly ripening 
the continuum, one will become a vessel of the profound path. Hence mostly that path is 
frequently taught. The presentation of the both of them is also abundant in other texts. For 
example, it is just as the Mind -Only  view is taught as a means  to ripen the continuum of the 
Middle -Way view.  
 Although it is not asserted that it is necessary to directly realize emptiness definitely in 
order to attain the hearers’ path of seeing in the way  asserted by the Middle -Way 
Consequentialists, there are man y Middle -Way Autonomists who assert that there are hearer 
aryas who also directly realize the meaning of emptiness. 
 While teaching that a path that realizes the profound meaning exists in hearers in all the 
earlier and later portions of this treatise may not have been agreeable, I have arranged it. For 
those who  have not pleased their spiritual guide over a prolong ed period , the various 
assertion of the great trailblazers abiding unmixed like lotus petals ; separated from the 
mental strength of discriminati on, they  have been diligent in achieving just gain, honor and 
reputation of this life. Regarding the assertions that the realization of the profound meaning 
exists in Hinayana aryas, it appears that there have been many cases of taking them like 
seeing som e bad omen. Those who wish good for themselves should possess 
conscientiousness with respect to this topic even later.

COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. In the way  observed by way of the stages of realization of t he 
hearers’ vehicle and so forth, accordingly, for the sake of realizing the aryas’ qualities, by 
means of presenting dharmadhatu as the entity of the cause, the convention as being just the 
lineages is imputed.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The thusness that is together with stains is the buddha lineage. It follows that there is no 
fault that the classification of the three lineages is inappropriate; in the way  thusness that is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
172 dharmadhatu is  observed by way of the stages of realization of dissimilar attributes of the 
accumulations and faculty of the hearers’ vehicle, the solitary realizers’ vehicles, the 
bodhisattvas’ vehicle  and so forth, accordingly, due to observation for the sake of 
realizing their respective  aryas’ qualities  in just the manner observed , by means of 
presenting dharmadhatu as the entity of the object of observation that is the cause, the 
convention of the three persons as abiding in their respective lineage, and of their dharmata 
as being just the lineages  of the three vehicles  is imputed. 
 The meaning of the statement is as follows. With respect to positing that persons of all 
three vehicles observe thusness and that the thusness that is together with stains is the lineage, it follows that there is no t the fault that the three individual lineage -bearers and the 
classification of the three individual lineages are  inappropriate, because even though all 
three vehicles observe thusness, due to difference in the accumulations and faculty, it becomes the object of observation that is the cause of dissimilar realizations of the greater 
and smaller vehicles, whereby the divisions of the three lineage -bearers and the three 
lineages are correct. Differentiating by way of differences in observing subtle and coarse 
selflessness on this occasion degenerates from the  contextual meaning of the reply to the 
objection.

how to posit composite and continuity; it is obvious that, through the force of that, only 
wrong conceptions regarding also the presentation of the groun ds and paths will be 
produced.

EXPLANATION  
The “objects of observation of achievings” are indicated in general by way of:  (1-3) objects 
to be adopted, objects to be discarded , and neutral objects, and are of eight divisions by w ay 
of (4-5) path s, (6-7) objects of abandonment and antidotes, (8 -9) truths, and  (10-11) results.  
They are the objects of observation of the Mahayana achievings, because they are the bases 
for eliminating the superimpositions of those [ achievings ].  
2B2C -1A2C-2B2. Decisive analysis  
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary in this context says:  
 
OPINION:  By way of those intentions thinking, “The constant purity 
dharmadhatu is the object of observation  of the very exalted wisdom of non -
duality ,” since it is the very c onstant purity, how is it to be expressed as 
proceeding higher and higher to distinguished purity? It has been said that “ In the 
way the water constituent, gold, and space are pure, it is asserted to be pure.”  
RESPONSE: In that case, because [thinking] “ Purity  is the exalted wisdom of 
suchness” is adherence to the antidotal class, adherence to the discordant class is 
implied. Hence, since the conceptualizations of the discordant and antidotal 
classes have not been abandoned, the consequence of partial purit y would follow.  
QUALM: According to your position, since it has been said, “Subhuti, the object 
of observation of an exalted knower of all aspects is non -thing,” what is the final 
object of observation like?  RESPONSE: There is no fault, because a thing th at accompanies reasoning does 
not exist, thus non- thing is expressed as being a real conventionality; the very 
realization gradually of th at which obscures the nature of suchness as illusory is 
purity.  
 
A. Incorrect meaning of this  [passage from the Great Commentary] as posited by others  
B. Our system   
A. Incorrect meaning of this [passage from the Great Commentary] as posited by others  
A1. The stated assertion of others is not the assertion of the other party 
A2.Incorrect presentation of our system  
A1. The stated assertion of others is not the assertion of the other party 
QUALM: It has been said that “Since the Proponents of Mind- Only  have asserted that only 
the thusness that is the emptiness of apprehended -objects and their apprehender- subjects 
being different substances is the object of observation of an arya’s meditative equipoise, that 
is refuted.” What is the meaning of that?  
RESPONSE: [It means that] “It is not asserted by the Proponents of Mind -Only  that—
having taken other -powered natures as the object of observation, and the emptiness of 
apprehended- objects and their apprehender- subjects being different substances as the 
aspect —other- powered natures’ emptiness of the apprehended -objects and their 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
175 apprehender -subjects being different substances is directly seen by104 an arya’s meditative 
equipoise.” If you say that, then you have not understood well their system, because there is 
no great charioteer of the Mind -Only  who ha s made such an assertion, and because if I were 
to explain the assertion of Cognition -Only in the Seven Treatises , it has been established not 
[just] once that the two —the direct damager and the superimposition that is to be 
damaged —are identical in the object of observation that is the basis; you would be asserting 
that “It is not possible to directly realize that, to an other- powered nature, apprehended -
objects and their apprehender- subjects appear to be of differe nt substances but are not 
established in the way they appear,” and even if it were to exist, you would be asserting that 
it is realized by a direct cognizer of an ordinary being.  
 
OPINION: Although the objects  of a conceptual reasoning consciousness  appear in that 
manner as the basis of characterization  and the attributes, since other- powered natures do 
not appear to a  seer that is an arya’s meditative equipoise, other- powered natures are not 
realized by a direct cognizer to not be established in the way th ey appear, and only thusness 
is observed. 
RESPONSE: Also in our system, does sprout appear to the seer that is a meditative 
equipoise directly realizing that a sprout does not exist truly? It follows that only the 
emptiness of true existence would be direc tly realized, without the sprout being realized by 
a direct cognizer to not exist truly.   
QUALM: Sprout appear s to it.  
RESPONSE: It follows that there would be even superimpositions to the seer that directly 
sees the apprehender of true existence as empt y of true existence; there will be refutation 
below. 
A2. Incorrect presentation of our system  
If you were to think, “Since, in our system, it is asserted that the property -possessor, such as 
a sprout, appears to the seer that is a meditative equipoise, we do not have a similar fault of 
refuting the  assertion  of the Proponents of Mind -Only ,” then this is seriously inappropriate 
as an assertion of the Middle -Wayers; when that property -possessor appears to a seer that is 
the path of seeing directly realizing suffering as empty of true existence, does it appear in a 
manner of water placed in water, or does it appear dualistically?  
 In the first case, the consequence that it follows that [suffering] is an ultimate truth 
cannot be countered. In the second case, s ince an arya’s meditative equipoise must also 
appear in that manner to [a seers] that sees it  as empty of true existence, you cannot counter 
the consequence that the meditative equipoise itself is a different substance from itself.  
 QUALM: Further, to that path of seeing, does suffering appear differently [from its 
emptiness of true existence], and does ultimate truth appear in a manner of water placed in 
water, or is ultimate truth realized implicitly ? 
RESPONSE: In the first case [wh ere suffering appears differently  and ultimate truth appears 
in a manner of water placed in water], to a seer that is a bodhisattva arya’s meditative 
equipoise, the modes of appearance of the two  types of objects of knowledge —modes and 
varieties —appearing dissimilarly transgresses the path of reasoning, because, otherwise, one 
must assert that meditative equipoise and subsequent attainment are one entity on that 
                                             
 
104 Gi emended to read gis in accordance with the explanation below.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
176 occasion. In the second case [where ultimate truth is realized implicitly ], what is that 
explici tly realized [by the realization which] realizes ultimate truth implicitly?  
 
OPINION: It explicitly realizes that very property -possessor, suffering.  
RESPONSE: The consequence that it follows that [suffering] would be an ultimate truth cannot be countered even more so. This is because there is no Middle -Way Consequentialist 
or Autonomist who asserts that compounded property -possessors appear to a seer that is a 
meditative equipoise of a arya learner. 
 
QUALM: What is the explanation of “an awareness to which  an illusory nature appears” 
below like?  RESPONSE: It is very clear that it is indicated to be the illusory meditative equipoise itself, because it has been taught in that way on many occasions in [Haribhadra’s] Great 
Commentary .  
B. Our system  
We accept that all consciousnesses in the continua  of ordinary beings, such as sense 
consciousnesses that are apprehenders of apprehendeds, are other- powered natures. We 
assert that , to sense consciousnesses, even though apprehended -objects and their 
apprehender -subjects appear to be different substances, they are seen by an arya’s 
meditative equipoise to not be established in the way they appear. It is asserted that its  
objects do not appear [to] sense consciousnesses , and unchanging thoroughly established 
natures a ppear in a manner of water placed in water. Nevertheless we assert that they  are 
directly seen  to not be established in the way they appear [to] sense consciousnesses, and we 
do not assert that they are not seen  [to not be established in the way they appea r to sense 
consciousnesses ]. Hence, the meaning of the assertion that the constant purity that is 
dharmadhatu is the object of observation of the non -conceptual exalted wisdom is that.  
 With respect to that, the assertion that (i) other- powered natures whi ch are the bases that 
are objects of observation, and (ii) the unchanging thoroughly established property -
possessors are truly existent is refuted by the Middle -Wayers , [because] a lso at the time 
when other -powered natures are observed during the occasions  of subsequent [attainment] 
by the aryas, it follows that purity would not become enhanced (in the manner of mistaken appearances and the conceptualizations that apprehend apprehended -objects and their 
apprehender -subjects as different substances becoming weaker), because the mistaken 
appearances and the conceptualizations have previously entered into the nature of other-
powered natures, and that state of entry is truly existent, and with respect to the truly 
existent, enhancement does not exist. It follows  that purity would not become distinguished 
in meditative equipoise, that is, the seeds would not be purified more and more.   
 
PROPONENTS OF COGNITION -ONLY : Even though water, gold, and space are pure by 
nature, they can be gradually purified of dirt, rus t, and so forth. Likewise, stains are also 
gradually purified by antidotes. 
RESPONSE: That is incorrect in  your system. The purity suchness is the emptiness of 
apprehended- objects and their apprehender- subjects being different substances. Since it  is 
truly existent, it is inappropriate for it to abandon the discordant classes , [because] i t is 
asserted that it  has entered into the nature of other- powered natures in which apprehended -
objects and their apprehender- subjects appear as different substanc es, and other- powered 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
177 natures themselves are also truly existent. Hence in that case: the subject —other- powered 
natures —it follows that apprehended -objects and their apprehender- subjects appearing to 
you as different substances are  established in the way t hey appear, because they appear in 
that manner and they are truly existent. There is pervasion, because there is no occasion in 
which that which is embraced by falsity abides as truly existent. If you accept that, then it 
follows that you would not abandon the conception that apprehends apprehended -objects 
and their apprehender -subjects as different substances which is asserted to be a knowledge 
obscuration, because an awareness that is unmistaken with respect to its object of adherence 
is faultless. Therefore, if you assert that the object of comprehension of the exalted wisdom 
of meditative equipoise is truly existent, then it follows that utmost purity cannot be 
asserted, since you must assert by implication that it is inappropriate to abandon the 
discord ant classes that are the objects of abandonment. 
 
OPINION: Since you assert that “The object of observation of an exalted knower of all 
aspects is a non -thing,” the subject —the emptiness of true existence of a sprout —it follows 
that it is truly existent, b ecause it exists for a reasoning  [consciousness ] that analyzes 
finality. You asserted the sign. If you accept that, then it follows that you will incur the fault 
that was explained earlier. There is pervasion, because if something were to exist for that 
ultimate awareness, then it must exist with respect to that ultimate, in which case it must 
exist ultimately.  
RESPONSE: We do not incur fault; even though the emptiness of true existence of a sprout 
exists for a reasoning  [consciousness ], we do not assert t hat it is truly existent, because even 
that negation is empty of true existence and false, abiding like an illusion. Thus it is as it has 
been explained in [Jnanagarbha’s Two Truths ], “Since the object to be negated does not 
exist, it is clear that there i s no correct negation.”  
 It is very clear that  it means that “Even though conceptual and non -conceptual reasoning  
consciousnesses are asserted to be ultimates, they are asserted to be real conventionalities; 
they are not asserted to be truly existent”. What follows from these will be explained on the 
occasion of the achieving of accumulation. Following from that, [Haribhadra’s] Great  
Commentary  says, “Therefore, what serves as the principal nature is the lineage; what 
serves as the principal transformation is the object of observation, thus that both are both is 
not refuted.” The meaning of the passage boils down to understanding whic h is the great 
principal or the small principal. The meaning of the latter [“that both are both is not refuted”] is that it becomes the lineage abiding in the nature by means of observing the six 
inner sources as being empty of true existence, without bein g separated from the property - 
possessor; it is inappropriate for the property -possessor to be abandoned. It means that the 
basis upon which superimpositions are to be eliminated in the context of the objects of 
observation is principally a conventionality ; even thusness serves as an object of 
observation.

178 2B2C -1A2C -2C1. G eneral indication  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. For the time being at the beginning, in general, (1 -3) virtue, non -
virtue and the unspecified, respectively the mere manner of the trainee -in-virtue, killing and 
so forth, and the unspecified actions o f body and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
A bodhisattva, for the time being at the beginning, analyzes initially: (1 -3) “In general, 
from among these phenomena, what are the three —virtue that is to be adopted, non -virtue  
that is to be discarded, and the unspecifi ed that are to be set aside in neutrality?” When he 
analyzes in this manner, he will realize respectively the mere manner of the trainee -in-
virtue, killing and so forth, and the unspecified actions of body and so forth are objects 
to be adopted, objects to  be discarded and neutral objects . The subject —those three —are the 
objects of observation of the achievings, because they are bases for eliminating the 
superimpositions of those [ achievings ].  
2B2C -1A2C -2C2. Individual explanations  
COMMENTARY

so forth, the remaining, respectively: (4) the five aggregates related to all childish beings, 
(5) the four concentrations included within all arya beings,  
 
EXPL ANATION

division , by just four pairs of divisions of the two aspects, mundane an d so forth, 
respectively , he thinks : (4) the subject —the five aggregates  included within mundane 
paths which are  related to  all childish beings —are mundane, because they are qualities 
that are familiar with respect to cyclic existence since beginningless t ime; (5) the subject —
the four  uncontaminated concentrations included within the continua  of all arya 
beings —are supramundane, because they are antidotes to craving, qualities that are 
unfamiliar with respect to cyclic existence.  
 
COMMENTARY 
(6) the five a ppropriated aggregates which are not antidotes to the view of a self, (7) the four

which are contingent upon causes and conditions, (9) thusness not relying upon  causes,  
 
EXPLANATION  
Then, he examines, “For the sake of abandoning the discordant classes, what are the objects 
of abandonment? What are the uncontaminated antidotes?” When he has examined them, he 
thinks: (6) the subject —the five appropriated aggregate s—are contaminated, because they 
are produced from the root that is the view of the transitory collection, and they are not a 
class of antidote  to the view of a self;  (7) the subject —the four uncontaminated close 
placements of mindfulness, antidotes to vie wing that  self—are  uncontaminated, because 
they are  the direct antidote s to the seeds that are the objects of abandonment. With respect 
to the analysis by way of dividing the uncontaminated itself into the two factors of cause and result, when he has analy zed in this manner, he thinks:  (8) the subject —true paths based

179 upon the desire realm and so forth, which are  contingent upon causes and conditions —

COMMENTARY

EXPLANATION  
Then, when he analyzes, “What are the results to be attained?” [he thinks]: (10) the 
subject —the four concentrations which arise in the continua  of aryas of all three 
vehicles —are common excellent qualities, because they are excellent qualities which arise 
in the continua  of all arya beings  of all three vehicles;  and (11) the subject —the ten 
strengths —are uncommon excell ent qualities, because they are excellent qualities  which 
arise  only in the continua  of perfect complete buddhas. Stating “ property -possessors” is 
for the sake of making known that they are the support of all the excellent qualities of the 
buddhas without exception.

ROOT TEXT  
1.25 The three —the mind, supremacy of all sentient beings,  
The abandonment, and the realization — 
By105 three greatnesses,  
Know that the objects of intent of the self- arisen are these.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Know that the objects of intent are these. Of whom are they? They are of  bodhisattvas 
imputed with the name  “self-arisen ” since they will transform in a self- arisen manner. By 
means of what? By three greatnesses. Wh at are they ? The three —(1) the great mind that 
enacts the  supremacy of all sentient beings, and (2 -3) the abandonment and the great 
realization  which are one’s own welfare . 
2B2C -1A2C -3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. By means of fully kno wing the exalted knower of all aspects in all 
ways, because they engage in the achievings  in that manner—that is, in terms of (1) the 
great mind of the supremacy of every sentient being, all sentient beings, (2) the great 
abandonment, and (3) the great rea lization —due to possession of three greatnesses, know 
that the objects of intent of the buddhas who will arise, the bodhisattvas themselves as three.  
EXPLANATION  
Know that the subject—those of the buddhas who will arise, the bodhisattvas 
themselves —the objects of intent as being of three  types, because there are three objects 
of intent due to possession of three greatnesses . There are three because they engage in 
the achievings in that manner, that is, in terms of the three final objects to be attained. 
What are the three? (1) The great mind , the great exalted compassion of the buddhas, the 
desire that “ sentient beings ,” all of them are caused to become buddhas, (2) final 
abandonment, the great abandonment , and (3)  final realization, the great realization . 
 By means of what will they become buddhas? They will arise by means of fully 
knowing the exalted knower of all aspects in all ways  which is characterized by the ten 
topics that act to characterize an exalted knower of all aspects. The bodhisattvas’ enga ging 
in the achievings , similar in terms of properties to a king engaging in battle, has the purpose 
of the three objects of intent: abandonment of all the discordant classes without exception, attainment of all the objects to be attained without exception , and enactment of the  
supremacy of  one and all.

without practice by way of the applications in generosity and so forth being principal, then, 
since [the deeds of generosity and so forth] exist even previously prior to entering the path, 
the answer to this question is inopportune; even if one were to describe it as the occasions of familiarization again and again thro ugh contemplating the very meaning that has been 
determined, there is not even the slightest reason for the practice of the deeds of generosity 
and so forth not being principal then.  
 Further, it is very clear that [claiming that] (i) the activities of practice of the three 
exalted knowers are applications in the practice of view and also (ii) the practice of the four applications [are applications in] the practice of deeds is a case of being deprived of 
anything to say. This is because there is not even t he slightest reason for the view and the 
deeds with respect to the two [the activities of practice of the three exalted knowers and the 
practice of the four applications] to be principal or not, and because there is not the slightest 
activity of practice o f the bodhisattvas (that has taken the three exalted knowers as object s) 
that is not an activity of practice of any of the four applications. 
 Further, even if you were to assert that “The four applications are the practice of view, 
and the activities of p ractice of the three exalted knowers are the practice of deeds,” what is 
the point of propounding anything to you? As to whether there is difference in indicating or 
not indicating the view as principal (in the first three chapters and the four chapters  that 
indicate the four applications), wipe your eyes well and realize it! You simply have not seen even slightly the divisions of the very practices of the three exalted knowers into six also on  
the occasion of summarizing  the meaning  of the Ornament into six below

 OPINION OF A PAST SCHOLAR: [The activities of practice of the three exalted knowers 
and the practice of the four applications] refer to (i) whether or not one is focused on the 
beginning of the six perfections, generosity and so forth, a nd (ii) whether or not the six 
perfections are conjoined with the characteristics of individual ascertainment.  
RESPONSE: Even taking them as those cannot bear analysis. If that refers to whether or not 
there is activity of contemplation of the practice of the six perfections, then it follows that 
this first achieving would be an achieving prior to entering the path of accumulation, because, having initially attained the achieving of armor, there is activity of the practice of 
contemplation which observes t he six perfections. If that is accepted, then with respect to 
the question regarding what the entity of the achievings is, there would be contradiction with the division of it into two. If it refers to whether or not the six perfections, generosity 
and so forth are attained in the continuum, then this would contradict the four applications 
posited on a later achieving, because a bodhisattva attains the application in complete 
aspects from the very beginning of having entered the path.   Hence, most people h ave presented the meaning of the commentary erroneously through 
having taken the contradictory mode of expression of the terms to be a contradiction in fact.

 Well, what is it like then? In the first three chapters, having taken the three exalted

From between the two, question and reply, the question is as follows:  
 
COMMENTARY 
QUESTION: Thus, having expressed what serves as the support of th e achievings and so 
forth, what is the self- entity of those?  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUESTION: Thus, having expressed what serves as the support of the achievings , the 
objects of observation, the objects of intent, and so forth, what is the self- entity of those  
achie vings?  
The reply is as follows. 
 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding (i) those bearing the support of virtuous qualities in general with respect to the 
objects that are the three exalted knowers of all, and (ii) the four types of clear realizations, 
manifestly and compl etely realizing all aspects and so forth, the activities which bear the 
support of each clear realization and the six perfections are the achievings.   
 
EXPLANATION  
On the occasions of indicating (i) the achievings which were indicated as object -possessors,  
those bearing the support of virtuous qualities in general with respect to the aspects of 
the three exalted knowers of all  with the nature that individually ascertain the three  
aspects of the three exalted knowers in the first three chap ters through attributes of 
dissimilar expressers , [the aspects] taken as the objects, by being included within the 
exalted knowers , without being clearly indicated by means of possessing the six perfections, 
except armor, and (ii) the four types of clear r ealizations, manifestly and completely 
realizing all aspects and so forth, the activities of practice which  were indicated as  
bearing the support of each clear realization and the six perfections, are the two 
achievings.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
183  With respect to the entity of ach ievings, there are two, because, by means of dissimilar 
modes of demonstration, there are: (1) the achievings in which the three exalted knowers 
have been taken as objects in general, and (2) the achievings that have been indicated as 
possessing the six perfections. When taken in accordance with fact, the subject —the 
Mahayana path of accumulation —is an achieving of the objects, the exalted knowers, 
because it is an activity of practice of meditation having taken some aspect of the three 
knowers as its objec t. 
 That subject [—the Mahayana path of accumulation —] is an achieving of the 
applications, because it is an activity of practice that is conjoined with either attitude or 
application possessing the six perfections. 
2B2C -1A2C -4A2. Division into four by mea ns of the bases of  characterization  
2B2C -1A2C -4A2A. Meaning of positing the achievings as the entity of joyous effort  
2B2C -1A2C -4A2B. Differences among the four achievings  
2B2C -1A2C -4A2A. Meaning of positing the achievings as the entity of joyous effort  
It has been explained that:  
 
At the beginning, from the joyous effort of armor,  The joyous effort of applications, strongly engaging properly, and  
The joyous effort that is undiscouraged, undisturbed, and not complacent.  
 
Hence, there are: (i) the joyous effort of armor, (ii) the joyous effort of engaging in the 
practice of the applications, (iii) the joyous effort that is undiscouraged regarding all of the 
Mahayana qualities, and is undisturbed by discordant classes, and (iv) the joyous effort that 
is not complacent.  
 It is taught that in this manner, [the achievings] are distinguished by joyous effort. Even 
though one may have generated the mind aspiring for supreme enlightenment, if one does 
not diligently initiate joyou s effort through effort in the practice of the achievings, it will 
merely be a noble thought; one will not be able to attain unsurpassable enlightenment. 
However, it is inappropriate to assert that if something is the entity of the achievings, it is 
pervad ed by being joyous effort, because the dissimilar patterns of the awareness abide 
unmixed like the petals of the lotus.  
2B2C -1A2C -4A2B. Differences among the four achievings  
At first one must have the extensive attitude of practicing all the causal and re sultant 
Mahayana qualities. Without being preceded by that, there will be no real practice of the extensive achievings, so bad systems [asserting that] at the time when two persons —one 
who has done much learning of the scriptural baskets and one does not k now it at all —later 
generate a desire to perform the achievings, neither much or little practice will arise  
[respectively for those two persons] will occur.  
 From having been preceded by the extensive attitude, extensive practice by way of the 
application s will arise. From having been preceded by the extensive attitude and 
applications, extensive accumulations will become complete. In dependence on that, 
omniscience will arise definitely without doubt. Hence, with respect to the practice of the 
achievings that act to attain omniscience, four are necessary; since more are not needed, the 
achievings are definite as four. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
184  The purpose of being skilled in the practice through condensing the meaning of those 
occurs in the context of “Joyous effort in the objects  of utilization, generosity and so forth,” 
[4.33 c]. The sequence of initial generation is definite in that way; since the activities of 
extensive attitude exist even at the end of the continuum, know that the achieving of armor 
exists from the path of accu mulation until that [end of the continuum].   
 The explanation that this passage [above] only indicates the boundaries of the four 
achievings is incorrect, because the boundaries are not easily realized, since by indicating 
“It exists there,” its existing elsewhere is not negated. Even though it is ass erted that the 
boundary of the end of a sentient being’s continuum  has been indicated, one must explain 
that the sequence of generation has been indicated; it is incorrect to relate it to the 
boundaries. In [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  it is related to the citation from Acarya 
Asanga, because, although one can provide a little explanation if it were to be explained, it 
is difficult for the phrase “ Exactly in that manner ” [see commentary below] to be correct.  
 Regarding the meaning of that, it is indicat ed by implication that: (i) the indirect 
application with respect to the Mahayana path of seeing, the very entity of the path of accumulation is [the achieving of] armor, (ii) the actual generation and the actual 
application, the very entity of the path of preparation is [the achieving of] engagement, (iii) 
the direct application with respect to the path of seeing, the achievings from loving -kindness  
until retention included within the supreme mundane quality level, and the achievings 
included within the fi rst ground, and so forth, is the achieving of accumulation, and (iv) the 
achievings of the three pure grounds is the achieving of definite emergence. Hence you 
should explain that it indicates the divisions and the bases of  characterization of the four 
achievings. 
 
COMMENTARY 
Exactly in that manner , the characteristics of the achievings of the very entities of the paths 
of preparation, seeing, meditation, and distinction —armor, engagement, accumulation, and 
definite emergence.    
EXPLANATION  
Exactly in tha t sequence,  the subject —the achieving on the path of preparation —is the 
achieving of armor , because it is an achieving bearing the extensive attitude of practice 
possessing the six perfections. The achieving included within the path of seeing  is the 
achiev ing of engagement , because it is an achieving distinguished by joyous effort that 
directly engages the Mahayana , which destroys the discordant classes of the six perfections. 
The Mahayana path of meditation  of the seventh ground and below is the achieving of 
accumulation because it is an achieving distinguished by joyous effort which completes the 
extensive army of the two accumulations.  
 The characteristic of the achieving of the very entity of the path of distinction  of the 
eighth ground and above is the  achieving of definite emergence , because it is a yoga of the 
pure grounds that issues forth omniscience without doubt.

1.26 Because those individually include

Is explained exactly by way of six sets of six.  
 
EXPLANATION  
That which is the achieving of armor is explained . How? Exactly  as in sutra by way of 
six sets of six . The division into six sets of six is appropriate, because  each of those  six 
achievings of armor individually include the six types, generosity and so forth.  
 Sutra says, “When a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, he fully 
performs thos e full performances of generosity  with the attention to omniscience . The roots 
of virtue are further shared with all sentient beings and dedicated to unsurpassable complete enlightenment. This is a great bodhisattva’s  practice of the Perfection of Wisdom a nd the 
armor of the perfection of generosity which fully performs generosity.”

2B2C -1A2C -4B1C -2. Summary 
COMMENTARY 
Since in that way generosity and so forth individually are included within the perfections of 
gene rosity and so forth, there are thirty -six types through six sets of six. Nevertheless, 
because the six, generosity and so forth are concordant in property, the achieving of armor is of six types.   
 
EXPLANATION  
In that way there are thirty -six types of arm or through six sets of six when divided by 
way of isolates, since generosity and so forth individually are included within each of 
the perfections of generosity and so forth; the divisions by way of isolates are  like that.  
Nevertheless, the achieving of armor is of six types, because the six, generosity and so 
forth subsumed under the achieving of armor of generosity are concordant in property  in 
being  the armor of generosity .   
 Someone takes that to mean that the six wisd oms are concordant in property in being  
wisdom , due to the six generosities being concordant in property in being  generosity . This is 
the proof that there are thirty -six; it is a mistaken way of proving that the achieving of armor 
is definite as six, becau se otherwise one must assert that practice by way of condensing the 
six generosities is the achieving of armor of generosity. That is thinking of the two —(1) the 
armor that is included within the achieving of armor of generosity and (2) generosity —as 
being  one. 
 In the context of the ethics of bodhisattva vows, know that the intention of abandonment 
that stops even the seeking of liberation for the sake of oneself alone is an uncommon 
branch. With respect to the pratimoksa (individual liberation) vows, the intention to 
abandon harming others as well as the bases is an uncommon branch. Know the way in

general deeds composed by Je Rinpoche [Lama Tsongkhapa].

ROOT TEXT  
1.27 The concentrations and formless absorptions, generosity and so forth,  
Paths, love and so forth,  
Possessing non -observability, 
The purity of the three spheres, 
 
1.28 Objects of intent, six clairvoyances, 
An exalted k nower of all aspects – 
The achievings that engage in that manner  
Are to be known as ascending in the Mahayana. 
 
EXPLANATION  
This very engagement in the cause is engaging in the result. Nevertheless, [the achievings] 
are divided into nine types by way of o bject. The subject —the activities that  engage  and 
practice  in the manner  of (1-8) the eight included within paths: the  concentrations and 
formless absorptions, generosity and so forth,  included within the Mahayana paths, and  
(9) an exalted knower of all as pects included within the result —are to be known as 
ascending in the Mahayana, because they are achievings that engage higher and higher in 
the Mahayana included within cause and result.  Sutra says, “ Sariputra  asked, “Venerable Pourna, by just what does a great bodhisattva 
correctly engage and ascend the vehicle?” Pourna replied, “Venerable Sariputra , with regard 
to this, a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of generosity and is devoid of desire. He is devoid of non -virtuous qualities, negativities. He closely accomplishes and abides in the 
first concentration bearing joy and bliss which is generated from being with investigation, 
being with analysis, and isolation.”

188 2B2C -1A2C -4B2C -1. Individual meanings  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The concentrations and formless absorptions, the six perfections, 
generos ity and so forth, the paths of seeing, meditation, no -more -learning, and distinction, 
the four immeasurables, the possession of non -observation, the characteristic of perfectly 
abiding in the three pure spheres regarding all things, the objects of intent, and the six 
clairvoyances , and the exalted knowers of all aspects themselves —since all the qualities of 
the Mahayana are the very entities which subdue: the achieving of engagement, nine types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Since all the causal and resultant qualities of the Mahayana are the nine achievings of the 
very entities which subdue  by way of practice of the applications, the nine types of  
achieving of engagement are further subsumed into two, because they are definite as the 
two—(i) engagement in the causes, the Mahayana paths and (ii) engagement in their results . 
 The first [engagement in the causes, the Mahayana paths] is of three, because there are:  
(a) one activity of practice —engagement in the concentrations and formless 
absorptions —the mundane p aths included within the Mahayana support,  
(b) engagement in the supramundane paths, and  
(c) qualities that differentiate both of them [the mundane and supramundane paths] from the Hinayana.  
 The middle one [engagement in the supramundane paths] is of four, because there are:  
(a-b) the six perfections, generosity and so forth, which act to attain the fulfillment of 
realization, and the paths of seeing  and so forth , which act to attain the fulfillment of 
abandonment: one’s welfare,  
(c) the four immeasurable s, love and so forth, the method to accomplish others’ welfare, 
and 
(d) the possession of non- observation that realizes emptiness, which purifies others’ 
welfare.  
 There are three qualities that differentiate them from the Hinayana, because there are  the 
activity of practice with the characteristic of perfectly abiding through observing 
engagement in : 
(a) the three completely pure spheres in which the action, object and doer are realized to 
be empty of true existence regarding all things  of the six perfecti ons, 
(b) transformation into the purpose of the three objects of intent in which one dedicates 
roots of virtue to unsurpassable enlightenment, and  
(c) the six clairvoyances, the method for quickly completing the accumulations .  
 And [there is] engagement in the results , the exalted knowers of all aspects themselves. 
2B2C -1A2C -4B2C -2. Particular explanation of that which is difficult to realize  
Regarding the explanation of the concentrations and formless absorptions, there are:

189 2B2C -1A2C -4B2C -2A. Cause  
If one has not become skilled in the sequence and entity of calm abiding and special insight  
that are included within the preparations of the first concentration at the start, one  will not 
understand the general presentation of the mundane path. Therefore one should analyze 
these at the beginning, because, one must cultivate special insight  after having achieved a 
fully qualified calm abiding ; except for a mere similitude of specia l insight, it is impossible 
to attain a fully qualified [special insight ] prior to [achieving a fully qualified calm abiding ]. 
For, it has been explained in Bodhisattvacarya vatara  [Santideva ’s] Engaging in the Deeds 
of a Bodhisattva  (Bodhisattvacarya vatara, 8.4abc) that:  
 
Having understood that afflictions are completely destroyed  
By special insight  strongly endowed with calm abiding , 
First of all I should search for calm abiding . 
 
And Heap of Jewels Sutra (Ratnakutasutra) says:  
Abiding in pure ethics , attain meditative stabilization  
Having attained meditative stabilization, cultivate wisdom.  
 
And [Kamalashila’s] Stages of Meditation (Bhavanakrama ) says:  
 
Therefore, those who wish for the arising of a thoroughly pure exalted wisdom 
through having abandoned all obscurations should cultivate wisdom by abiding in calm abiding .  
 
The sequence for attaining [special insight ] at the beginning is definite in that way; 
afterwards, having already attained it, then there is no certainty [that calm abiding precedes  
special insight ], since having cultivated special insight  previously, subsequently one also 
cultivates calm abiding . Know that the assertion —that special insight  is attained prior to 
attaining a fully qualified calm abiding at the time of newly accomplishing it at the 
beginning —is motivated only by ignorance.  
 QUALM: [Asanga's]  Compendium of Knowledge (Abhidharmasamuchchaya) says: ‘Some 
attain special insight ; they do not attain calm abiding . They strive for calm abiding in 
dependence on special insight .’ Why is it taught in that manner?  
RESPONSE: It is not that they do not attain the calm abiding included within the 
preparations of the first concentration; they do not attain the calm abiding of the actual first 
concentration and above. Further, h aving directly realized the four truths in dependence on 
the special insight  included within the preparations of the first concentration, they achieve 
the calm abiding included within the actual first concentration. For, [Asanga’s] Actuality of 
Grounds (Bhumivastu ) says: 
 
Furthermore, one strongly understands sufferings and so forth until  paths 
perfectly as they are. The first concentration and so forth have not been attained. As soon as that [understanding] is present, the mind is set and one does not 
discriminate phenomena. In dependence on that very superior wisdom, one 
connects to a superior mind. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
190  
A certain person who has not learnt well the presentation of the path of Abhidharma does 
not understand  the sequence of the two —with respect to the  preparatio ns of the first 
concentration , after having initially achieved a fully qualified calm abiding , the attention of 
a beginner  and the beginner that purifies  afflictions . He holds that the initial preparation 
[starts] from the attention of individual knowledge  of character. He has not turned his mind 
to the two existing sequences of achieving calm abiding and special insight  with respect to 
what is included within a preparation of the first concentration. For, he has not even 
understood the meaning taught in [M aitreya’s]  Ornament for the Great Vehicle Sutras 
(Mahayanasutralamkara ) which says:  
 
 
Through familiarization with those [nine metal abidings that are included within 
the level of the Desire Realm] 
One does not engage in application [of the antidotes].  
Then, when one has attained great pliancy of body and mind  of that , 
It should be known that one has a n attention  [that is, calm abiding]. 
 
 “That” [mentioned above], the fully qualified calm abiding achieved by way of the nine 
steps of mental abidings is that taught in [Asanga’s] Hearers’ Grounds; the attention of 
individual knowledge of character must be achieved after it. For, in that way, [Asanga’s] Hearers’ Grounds says:  
  
A beginner at attention  is a beginner while not having attained an attention  with 
respect to making one -pointed [that is, while not having attained calm abiding] 
and until reaching the one -pointedness of mind  [that one has with calm abiding]. 
A beginner at purifying afflictions is undertaken with the attention of individual 
knowledge of the character of one who has attained a n attention [that is, calm 
abiding] and who wishes to purif y the mind of all afflictions , and is a 
familiarization with [that understanding]. 
 
And:  
 
Further, with respect to that, a yogi who strongly strives in order to separate from 
attachment of the desire realm subsequently attains the separation from the attachment to the desire realm by way of the seven types of attention.  
 
QUESTION: What are those seven?  
RESPONSE: The attentions of:  
(1) individual knowledge of character  
(2) arisen from belief (3) thorough isolation  
(4) joy -withdrawal 
(5) analysis  
(6) final application  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence        Chapter 1: The Exalted Knower of All Aspects  
 
191 (7) result of final application  
 
Hence, having generated the bliss of pliancy of the bo dy and mind by having initially 
achieved a single -pointed mind by way of the nine steps of mental abidings, a fully qualified 
calm abiding is achieved. After that, for the sake of abandoning the afflictions of the desire 
realm, one cultivates (1) the atten tion of individual knowledge of character and so forth.  
  The first attention, having viewed the desire realm in the aspect of coarseness and the 
first concentration in the aspect of peace, undertakes analysis by way of hearing and 
contemplation. Through the force of having performed analysis in this manner, by inducing 
the bliss of pliancy of body and mind, special insight  is initially attained; this is (2) the 
attention arisen from belief, which is a wisdom arisen from meditation.  
 Then, due to having f amiliarized with the meditative stabilization that is a union of calm 
abiding and special insight , the antidotes that abandon the three great and three middling 
afflictions of the desire realm are respectively (3 -4) the attention arisen from thorough 
isola tion and the attention of joy-withdrawal.  
 By having abandoned most of the afflictions in that way, when seeing that the manifest 
afflictions of the desire realm do not occur, one wonders, “Have I abandoned all the 
afflictions without exception?” Hence, for the sake of investigating whether or not the 
afflictions have been abandoned, one pays attention and individually investigates the signs 
of easy occurrence of the afflictions; this is (5) the attention of analysis.  
 By way of having familiarized with t he antidotes when one understands that a little 
[amount of those afflictions] has not yet been abandoned, the direct antidote that abandons 
the three small afflictions is (6) the attention of final application; (7) the attention of the 
result of final appl ication is the attainment of the actual first concentration.  
 Similarly understand the remaining levels by means of the eight attentions. Also, know 
that the first attention —a fully qualified calm abiding —is a branch of abiding that acts to 
separate from t he attachment to the lower levels.  
 This Acarya who composed the commentary does not accept a basis -of-all that is a 
different entity from the six collections [of primary consciousness es]. According to those 
who assert  it, the manifest afflictions of the desire realm (excluding the afflicted mentality) 
are destroyed by the preparations of the first concentration ; the other one  is not . 
[Vasubandhu’s] Thirty Stanzas  (Trimsikakarika ) explains: 
 
It is of that, wherever one is born,  
 Therefore, [the afflicted mentality ] is included in whichever level the support is. It has been 
explained that, with the exception of three occasions, the manifest [afflicted mentality ] is 
not ceased.  
2B2C -1A2C -4B2C -2B. Entity  
2B2C -1A2C -4B2C -2B1. Actual meaning
