230 2B2C -1B2A -2C1. Meaning of the words  
COMMENTARY 
Since “The presentation of the three vehicles bears an intention; it is not fully qualified” 
occurs in the system, all beings bear the finality of the unsurpassable perfect complete enlightenment. Because of that, those who have separated from attachment and even the other yogis are pervaded, since the knower of paths is an object to be cultivated in order to attain buddhahood.  
 
EXPLANATION 
The subject —hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers who have separated from the 
attachment of the three realms and even the other yogis who have not separated from 
attachment—are pervaded by lineage, since the knower of paths is an object to be 
cultivated in order to attain buddhahood . It is like that, because all sentient beings bear 
the progression to the finality of the unsurpassable perfect complete enlightenment.  
That is the case, since “The presentation of the three final vehicles bears an intention; it 
is not a fully qualified  definitive meaning that is literally acceptable ” occurs in the system  
of the great trail -blazers (or openers of the chariot tracks) Nagarjuna and Asanga .  
2B2C -1B2A -2C2. Decisive analysis  
2B2C -1B2A -2C2A. Proving that there is one final vehicle 
2B2C -1B2A -2C2B. Setting forth other systems that assert that there are three [final vehicles]  
2B2C -1B2A -2C2C. Analyzing whether or not there is a limit to samsara 
2B2C -1B2A -2C2D. Analyzing from which of the Mahayana paths the hearers and solitary realizers enter  
2B2C -1B2A-2C2A. Proving that there is one final vehicle 
In regard  to scripture, the Descent into Lanka Sutra  (Lankavatarasutra) says:  
 
Mahamati, those of the hearer vehicle are not liberated by the hearer’s vehicle; 
for them, it is the finality of the Mahayana. 
 
The White Lotus Sutra  (Pundarikasutra ) says:  
 
The three vehicles taught by the great sage is a skillful  means of the guides. 
There is one vehicle; it is not that there are two. For the sake of respite, three vehicles were taught.  
 The Glorious Lion's Ro ar of Shrimaladevi Sutra ( Shrimaladevisimhanadasutra ) says:  
 
This ‘thoroughly passing beyond sorrow’ is a method of the tathagatas.  
 Also, sutra says:  
Manjusri, if the tathagatas were to teach the Mahayana to some, the hearer’s vehicle and the solitary realizer’s vehicle to some, it follows (i) that the tathagatas’ exalted minds would be completely impure, (ii) that they would have the fault of adherence, (iii) that they would have trifling great compassion, (iv) that they would discriminate as different, and (v) that they would be masters who are stingy in regard to the Dharma. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
231 On account of these five reasons, it has been taught that the three lineage -bearers are set in 
the state that has been found by the Buddha himself.  
 In regard  to reasoning, the dharmadhatu pure by nature that is free from all elaborations 
of true existence exists in all sentient beings without any difference. Although dissimilar 
divisions of its entity do not exist even slightly, the very erroneous apprehension  of it, the 
root of all afflictions, the conception of true existence that apprehends the aggregates and so 
forth as truly existent, like the apprehension of a mottled rope as a snake, is an unstable root 
that arises adventitiously in the mental continuum, and is suitable to be separated from by its 
antidote. Hence, if the stains of all sentient beings are suitable to be separated from, it would be impossible for (i) the knowledge obscurations (in the continua of hearer and solitary 
realizer foe -destroyers who have arisen from the sphere of peace) to be unsuitable to be 
abandoned, and (ii) the activities of the buddhas who teach the methods for abandoning 
them to be discarded. Furthermore, reasonings that refute other extremes will be explained 
below.   
 
OPINION: That contradicts the teachings that there are three final vehicles.  
RESPONSE: There is no fault. For example, a ship captain who is skillful  in means 
emanates a city on an island in the ocean in order for the travelers, tired due to the long 
journey,  to relax; having relaxed, they gradually proceed to the island of jewels. Likewise, 
the teaching on the two nirvanas, which are likened to the emanated city, for the sake of 
respite, to those who are tired due to the passage of cyclic existence and do not  initially wish 
to enter the Mahayana path is a skillful  means of the tathagatas.  
 The teaching (in the Sutra Unraveling the Thought  and the like) given to some trainees 
who bear the Mahayana lineage, that there are three final vehicles, is intended and t aught for 
the sake of guiding those trainees who, although bearing the Mahayana lineage, are not 
suitable to be taught the profound path for the time being. Regarding the basis in [Buddha’s] 
thought , it is intended that one needs to lead those —who, tempora rily, are of dissimilar 
interests and dissimilar dispositions —through the paths of the individual vehicles. 
Regarding the purpose, it is for the sake of taking care of lineage -bearers who are to be led 
to the Mahayana path without refuting that the emptine ss of apprehendeds that are forms 
and their apprehenders being different substances is truly existent. Those which damage 
what is overtly stated are the reasonings that prove that all phenomena are empty of true 
existence and the reasonings below that refu te other extremes.  
2B2C -1B2A -2C2B. Setting forth other systems that assert that there are three [final vehicles]  
Proponents  of Cognition- Only assert that there are three final vehicles, in dependence on a 
literal reading of the Sutra Unraveling the Thought . The reason for asserting it this way is 
that, since the two —other- powered natures and thoroughly established phenomena —exist 
ultimately, the lineage, which is the uncontaminated seed in the continua of sentient beings 
rendering them suitable to be led on  the paths of the individual vehicles is something 
definite to the individual. However, since the lineage is definite to the individual ultimately, 
even though interests and thinking are suitable to transform slightly due to temporal 
conditions, [lineage] is definite to the individual ultimately by way of entitiness, whereby 
the final vehicle is also definite as three different individuals. I n this system , the explanation 
of someone having the property of never fully passing beyond sorrow  is taken to be  their 
system.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
232  Although it has been explained that there is one final vehicle, [Proponents  of Cognition -
Only] take it as a n interpret able meaning that is not literally acceptable. Regarding the bases 
in [Buddha’s] thought , [one  final vehicle] ha s been taught by intending the seven bases in 
[Buddha’s] thought:  
(1) there is no difference in the dharmadhatu of the three vehicles, which is like space,  
(2) the persons of the three vehicles are similar in realizing selflessness,  
(3) the liberations in which sufferi ngs and origins have been abandoned are similar,  
(4) since a lineage -bearer indefinite in the hearer [lineage] enters the Mahayana and 
becomes a buddha, even two different lineages enter the same path,  
(5) the tathagatas have practiced the deeds of bodhi sattvas from the beginning, and when 
some of those definite in the hearer lineage practice the deeds of bodhisattvas for some time, then give them up and attain nirvana, the tathagatas think, “By having previously practiced the deeds of a bodhisattva I att ained buddhahood”; likewise, by the power of the buddhas 
one gains just once a similar thought, “By having previously practiced the deeds of a bodhisattva I will attain buddhahood,”  
(6) emanated hearers show the manner of attaining nirvana by way of the h earer’s vehicle 
many times for the sake of trainees, and  
(7) there is none but one final  vehicle.  
 The purpose is to lead those who are indefinite in the hearer lineage to the Mahayana, and to 
take care of those who are indefinite in the Mahayana [lineage] so that they do not fall into 
other vehicles. Those which damage what is overtly stated are, in te rms of scripture, the 
Sutra Unraveling the Thought  and so forth, and in terms of reasoning, the reasonings that 
prove that other- powered natures and thoroughly established phenomena are truly existent. 
2B2C -1B2A -2C2C. Analyzing whether or not there is a li mit to samsara 
Questions are to be asked in this manner as follows:  
• If it were possible for all sentient beings to attain buddhahood, then later buddhas need only to enact the welfare of a small number of sentient beings, whereby others’ welfare 
would bec ome trifling and they would not fully complete the accumulation of merit.  
• When all sentient beings have become buddhas, [the buddhas] would not enact the 
welfare of even one sentient being, whereby others’ welfare would be interrupted and 
they would fall into the extreme of peace.  
 
• If it were not possible for all sentient beings to attain buddhahood then: (i) perfect 
complete buddhas who desire to place all sentient beings in the state that they themselves 
have found would lack the result of turning the w heel of Dharma by way of the twelve 
deeds until cyclic existence is empty, and (ii) even the seeking of the mere pacification of the suffering of cyclic existence of all sentient beings would not be the complete fully -
qualified great compassion of the Maha yana; there would be contradiction with the 
teaching that if the bhagavans were to not set all sentient beings in the state that they 
themselves have found, then there would be five types of damage
119, the consequence that 
[the bhagavans] are stingy in regard  to the Dharma and so forth. If all sentient beings 
were able to attain the pacification of the sufferings of cyclic existence, then one final 
vehicle would be well established, whereby their attainment of buddhahood would be 
                                              
119 See the sutra citation under the outline 2B2C -1B2A -2C2A.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
233 established effortlessly . If there are sentient beings for whom it is impossible to attain 
even mere liberation, then it would contradict the proofs by scripture and reasoning made 
by Acarya  [Haribhadra] and Arya [Vimuktisena] that [the teaching that there are sentient 
beings for whom it is impossible to attain even mere liberation] bears an intention and is 
not literally acceptable. Therefore, how this is so must be expressed. And if it is possible 
for all sentient beings to attain buddhahood, then also the explanation that there  is no 
limit to cyclic existence would be incorrect.   
 
With respect to this, there are:

2B2C -1B2A -2C2C -1. Refuting other systems  
OPINION: Let alone it being definite that al l sentient beings will attain buddhahood, you 
are not able even to prove that they are just suitable to attain buddhahood. In order to prove 
that the result is suitable to be produced, one has to state as the sign: “The collection of 
causes is complete.” B ut if the collection of causes of buddhahood were to be complete in 
all sentient beings, then it follows that it would be impossible for there to be a sentient being 
who has not entered the path to liberation. On account of that reason itself, there is no 
exhaustive limit to cyclic existence, for which one may say, “Prior to this point, there is a 
sentient being circling in cyclic existence, but from this point onwards, there is none.”  
 
RESPONSE: It is not obvious that the teaching that “The significance of the proof that there 
is one final vehicle is that all sentient beings who have entered the path will attain 
buddhahood” is a thesis that is established by reasoning. It is intended to propound the 
reasoning of definite pervasion by way of suitability to p roduce the result if the collection of 
causes is complete; it is not intended to mean that if the result is suitable to be produced, 
then the collection of causes is pervaded by being complete, because that contradicts 
reasoning.  
 This is because even thou gh a barley sprout is suitable to be produced from a barley seed, 
it is not established that the collection of causes is complete. Furthermore, are hearer arya s 
suitable to attain buddhahood or not?  
 In the first case [that they are suitable to attain budd hahood], it follows that the collection 
of causes of buddhahood would be complete. If you accept that, then it follows that they 
have entered the Mahayana path. Having accepted the sign s and the pervasion s of both the 
former and the latter consequence s, and having accepted the predicate of the latter 
consequence, [your position that] not all sentient beings have merely entered a path would 
degenerate.  
 In the second case [that they are not suitable to attain buddhahood], there would be a 
direct contradiction and it would contradict there being only one final vehicle. In addition, it 
is very clear that it is asserted that “You must  assert that if all sentient beings will attain 
buddhahood, then a time  when [all sentient beings] have attained buddhahood is po ssible.”

234 have attained buddhahood is impossible. If you assert that, if al l sentient beings will attain 
buddhahood, then a time when all sentient beings have attained buddhahood is pervaded by 
being possible, then —in the consequence “It follows that a time when all things120 have 
disintegrated is possible, because all things will disintegrate” —you are expressing a 
consequence in which neither a valid cognizer nor the asserted predicate can be demonstrated. What fault is there if you were to assert that? It would be an intense assertion 
to say that “If a time when all things have di sintegrated is possible, then it follows that a 
time when all things do not exist is possible.” You would have to assert that if a time when 
all things do not exist is impossible, then a time when things do not exist is impossible. It 
follows that prior to  a certain point, things exist, but from that point onwards, things do not 
exist, because a time when all things have disintegrated is possible.  
 RESPONSE: If the meaning of that is that it follows that from that point onwards things are 
impossible, then i t follows that you are able to prove that prior to a certain point blue exists, 
but from that point onwards blue does not exist, because a time when blue have 
disintegrated is possible. If you say that the sign is not established, then it follows that it i s 
permanent and is a thing , because it is impossible for it to have disintegrated after having 
been produced from its cause. Also with respect to a time when all things have disintegrated being impossible, there would be that consequence.  
 Regarding the as sertion, “It follows that a time when all sentient beings have attained 
buddhahood is possible, because they are  attain ing buddhahood,” the fault posited by 
someone that “It follows that all sentient beings are pervaded by having already died 
because all s entient beings are pervaded by dying” clearly demonstrates a very crude 
intelligence. With regard to the previous consequence, is it saying that “It follows that all sentient beings are pervaded by having already attained buddhahood”? Or that “It follows 
that a time when all sentient beings have attained buddhahood is possible”? Realize them 
well! It is clear that propounding that “All sentient beings will attain buddhahood; it is not 
definite that they will attain buddhahood” is an expression of not unders tanding reasoning. 
Has this ascertaining consciousness of yours that thinks, “All sentient beings will attain 
buddhahood” come through the force of valid cognition in your own continuum or not?  
 In the first case [this ascertaining consciousness has come t hrough the force of valid 
cognition], it follows that it is certain that all sentient beings will attain buddhahood, 
because you have ascertained through the force of valid cognition that all sentient beings 
will attain buddhahood.  
 
DISPUTANT: The pervasion is not established, because although it has been ascertained 
through the force of valid cognition, it is not certain in reality. 
 
RESPONSE: It follows that this ascertaining consciousness of yours that thinks, “All 
sentient beings will attain bud dhahood” is a wrong consciousness that is mistaken with 
respect to the object of adherence, because there is ascertainment by your awareness, 
thinking “They will attain buddhahood,” but there is not even the slightest certainty that 
they will attain buddha hood in reality.

235 DISPUTANT: The production of smoke by fire is established by valid cognition, but the

adverse conditions for their attainment of buddhahood would be impossible.

that analogy, if the production of smoke by fire is ascertained  through the force of valid 
cognition, it is contradictory that the production of smoke is uncertain. If it is not 
ascertained by valid cognition, then the meaning of the thesis is not established by valid cognition. While this is the case, you simply demo nstrate yourself to be very foolish when 
you say, “There is no correct proof whatsoever for whatever thesis posited by us who are the proposers of the thesis.”  
 Furthermore, it follows that it would be  uncertain that hearer and solitary realize r aryas 
will attain buddhahood, because hindrances that are adverse conditions for the attainment of 
buddhahood are possible. You accepted the pervasion. If you accept that, then this contradicts your own assertion that the final vehicle is definite as one. The meani ng of that 
passage from [Dharmakirti's] Commentary on [Dignaga's] 'Compendium of Valid 
Cognition'  (Pramanavarttikakarika ) is also not like that. This has already been extensively 
explained in its  Explanation. If it were not like that, but as you have asserted, then it follows 
that it is uncertain that a vase exists, because hindrances that are adverse conditions for the 
existence of a vase are possible. Hence, even though hindrances that are adverse conditions 
for its production are possible, what cont radiction is there that they are unable to act to not 
produce it now?  
 In the second case, if that ascertaining consciousness of yours thinking, “All sentient 
beings will attain buddhahood” has not come from the force of your valid cognition, then (i) it follows that it is inappropriate for you to propose the thesis that “All sentient beings will 
attain buddhahood,” and (ii) it follows that your subsequent cognizer thinking “They will 
attain buddhahood” does not engage the concordant meaning, because that t hey will attain 
buddhahood is not established by a valid cognition in your continuum. If you say that there is no pervasion, then you would necessarily be making the deprecation, “A correct sign of 
non-observation of the non -appearing does not exist.” In t hat case, you would necessarily be 
asserting that “Since the reasoning is similar also for other correct signs, there is no proof 
whatsoever for all hidden phenomena.”  
 Therefore, the difference between the skilled analytical ones and the confused ones li es in 
whether or not they know how to posit well the mode of establishing by valid cognition the meaning of a thesis that is asserted by themselves . If, having established by valid cognition 
that all sentient beings will attain buddhahood, it is not ascert ained that their attainment of 
buddhahood exists, then it would not be a positing of an existent, that observed by valid 
cognition. How can there be anything more unreasonable than propounding that “If their 
attainment of buddhahood exists, it is not certa in that they will attain buddhahood”? Is it 
certain or not that sentient beings will attain buddhahood  from the perspective of their 
reality ? In the latter case, it would contradict your assertion. In the first case, if it has been 
settled  that they will n ot attain buddhahood, it would contradict your assertion; if it has been 
settled that they will attain buddhahood, it would contradict [your assertion] that it is not

236 certain that they will attain buddhahood.If neither has been settled  from the perspective  of 
their reality , then the decision settled upon by you that they will attain buddhahood would 
be unrelated to them. 
 
Lacking the power to discriminate the stainless path of reasoning, and  
Lacking the instructions in which the teachings dawn as advice,  
You propound that “I am commentating on the Victor’s intention.”  
It would be wonderful if you leave the Buddha’s doctrine alone.  
2B2C -1B2A -2C2C -2. Presenting our system  
It has already been explained that, after having asserted that they will attain buddh ahood, 
not asserting that the attainment of buddhahood is certain contradicts reasoning, as 
explained above. Also, the statement “It is not established that all sentient beings will attain 
buddhahood” cannot bear analysis by reasoning. For, if the minds of all sentient beings are 
not pure by nature, then we must assert that a truly existent phenomenon is possible among objects of knowledge, whereby it would not be established by valid cognition that all 
phenomena are empty of true existence. There would be no way to posit ultimate truths. 
However, if their minds are established by valid cognition to be pure by nature, then [that all 
sentient beings will attain buddhahood] is established in dependence on that very reasoning 
that the stains ( that are the conce ptions of true existence as well as their imprints ) are 
adventitious and are suitable to be separated from with respect to the mind. Otherwise, we 
would have to assert that stains have entered the nature of the mind, just like the tirtika 
(non-Buddhist For der) practitioners.  
 OPINION: Although the above [stains] are established by valid cognition to be suitable to 
be separated from, it is not established that the antidotes that will destroy the stains are 
suitable to be generated in the continuum. 
RESPONSE : (1) Is that because there is no method for destroying the stains in the continua 
of sentient beings?  (2) Or is that because, although there is a method, there is no one who knows it?  
(3) Or is that because, although there is someone who knows it, generating [an aspiration] 
seeking that method is not possible at any time?  
(4) Or is that because, although that is possible, a person who knows the method teaching it 
motivated by loving- kindness is not possible?  
(5) Or is that because, although having been  taught, engaging in and cultivating that method 
is not possible?  
 
The first is not reasonable, because by having cultivated the wisdom realizing selflessness 
one is able to exhaust all stains. 
 The second is not reasonable, because a teacher who has culm inated the cultivation of the 
method, the wisdom realizing selflessness, for the welfare of sentient beings is well 
established by reasoning.  
 The third is not reasonable, because (i) it is not possible for sentient beings to not seek 
high status in dependence on being urged by the buddhas, (ii) even though for the time being sentient beings may not generate [a mind] seeking definite goodness, al l sentient 
beings possess the two types of buddha lineage, and (iii) since the [buddhas’] exalted 
compassion that wishes to place all sentient beings in buddhahood does not degenerate, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
237 [sentient beings] will generate disillusionment regarding cyclic existe nce for the time being 
and [an aspiration] seeking nirvana. This is also established in dependence on a correct sign 
that is a scripture that is pure by way of the three analyses. 
 The fourth is not reasonable, because when [an aspiration] seeking [high st atus and 
definite goodness] is generated from the side of sentient beings themselves, since the buddha bhagavans’ loving -kindness  towards all sentient beings as though they are their only 
child does not degenerate, they do not discard the activity of teach ing Dharma.   
 The fifth is not reasonable, because it is proven (in the root text of [Maitreya’s] 
Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum (Mahayanottaratantrasastra),  its 
commentary, and so forth) that at a certain time, due to the conditions for awake ning the 
lineage becoming complete, the lineage will be awakened.  
 Therefore, having asserted that a person —for whom it is impossible to attain 
buddhahood —is not possible, one should not assert that (i) it is not the case that all sentient 
beings will atta in buddhahood and (ii) it is not certain that they will attain buddhahood.  
 
DISPUTANT: It follows that a time when all sentient beings have attained buddhahood is 
possible because they will attain buddhahood.  
RESPONSE: It can only be accepted; the flingin g of the consequence that all sentient beings 
have already attained buddhahood has already been explained to be incorrect.  
 
DISPUTANT: Then, it follows that a time when there is no sentient being at all is possible, 
because all sentient beings’ having att ained buddhahood exists.  
RESPONSE: There is no pervasion, because there is the contradiction that you will not exist 
if your having attained buddhahood exists. The consequence that a time when there is no 
sentient being is possible can only be accepted, b ecause things are transitory. The 
consequence that it is possible that a time when there is no sentient being who is the object for enacting welfare by the buddhas is possible is accepted, as before [because things are 
transitory].  
 
DISPUTANT: It follows t hat a time when the continuity of sentient beings who are the 
objects for enacting welfare by the buddhas have been severed is possible, because all sentient beings’ having attained buddhahood exists.  
RESPONSE: The subject —an eighth ground bodhisattva —it follows that a time when his 
continuity have been severed is possible, because his having attained buddhahood exists.  
 Since the pervasions are similar in strength, all three are accepted. Realize well whether or 
not you think that —if it is a buddha it is pervaded by not being a sentient being. If examined 
closely, you did not make a difference between [the statements] “all sentient beings’ having 
attained buddhahood exists” and “all sentient beings have attained buddhahood.”    
DISPUTANT: If all sentient b eings’ having attained buddhahood exists, where will it exist 
and at what time will it exist?  
RESPONSE: In whose continuum does the perfect complete buddha —that is the object to 
be attained when you think “I will attain buddhahood for the welfare of senti ent beings” —
exist, and when does it exist?  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
238 DISPUTANT: When one has attained clear complete buddhahood it exists in one’s 
continuum; the object to be attained, buddhahood of a bodhisattva on the path of 
accumulation, does not exist in his continuum, becau se a cause and [its] result do not 
coexist.  
RESPONSE: Why is it unreasonable to propound in that way for the other [case ]? 
 
DISPUTANT: If cyclic existence had a beginning it would be causeless. Therefore, there is 
no beginning limit. But does an end limit exist or not?  
RESPONSE: Saying that “The mere generality cyclic existence has no future limit, but a particularity of cyclic existence has a future limit” is contradictory, because it is 
contradictory for a vase that is not possible among functioning thin gs to exist. Therefore, 
both the generality cyclic existence and a particularity of cyclic existence have future limits, 
because the significance of the teaching in a certain scripture —that a future limit to cyclic 
existence is not evident —is that childish  beings cannot conceive of a definite time, thinking, 
“Some sentient being s will attain liberation at just this time, not before or after.” As 
explained before, know that [specifying] a time when [all sentient beings] have attained 
liberation is impossible.  
 
Someone’s saying —that cyclic existence has no future limit but all sentient beings will 
attain buddhahood —is only a load of contradictions. Enough elaboration.

previously realized by way of direct cognition, then there would be no need [for them to generate the Mahayana path of seeing] since they have already realized emptiness and 
familiarized with it. Hence, they should enter from  the seventh ground or above.  
 However, in the case of [asserting that] hearers do not have the realization of selflessness 
of phenomenon, they must generate the Mahayana path of seeing which newly realizes 
emptiness by way of direct cognition. As a cause of that they must generate the Mahayana 
path of preparation that meditates on emptiness by way of a meaning -generality with a 
wisdom arisen from meditation. In order for that to occur, they must determine emptiness by 
way of hearing and thinking, whereby t hey enter from the Mahayana path of accumulation.

239 RESPONSE: That is very unreasonable. For, if it is said, “Further, in the case of asserting 
that hearers do not have the realization of selflessness of phenomen a, it follows that there is 
no need for them to generate a Mahayana path of seeing in the continuum; it is necessary to 
generate a Mahayana path of seeing for the sake of newly realizing selflessness that has not

familiarized with selflessness that has already been directly realized,” what reply can you

 DISPUTANT: The Mahayana path of seeing  is not distinguished by newly realizing mere 
selflessness by way of direct cognition; it is differentiated also by newly realizing subtle emptiness by way of direct cognition. Hence it must be newly generated. 
 RESPONSE: Even according to the former assertion [that hearers have the realization of 
selflessness of phenomen a], it follows that the Mahayana path of seeing must de finitely be 
generated in one’s continuum, because that path of seeing is distinguished by the two 
accumulations, the extensive accumulation of merit and the accumulation of exalted 
wisdom. The path of seeing that is included within the accumulation of merit must be newly 
generated in one’s continuum, and one must also generate, as its cause, the path of 
accumulation and the path of preparation.  
 
DISPUTANT: According to the former system , the accumulation of merit included within 
the Mahayana path is completed on the seventh ground and above, whereby there is no 
fault. 
 
RESPONSE: It follows that those who wish to quickly attain unsurpassable enlightenment 
should definitely first enter the hearer’s path, because (i) with blazing joyous effort they would be abl e to attain the result of a hearer foe -destroyer in three lifetimes and then they 
can enter [the Mahayana path] from the seventh ground without needing to generate in their continu a the sixth ground and below, and (ii) because [otherwise] they would have t o gather 
the accumulations for up to one countless great eon on the Mahayana paths of accumulation and preparation.  
 Therefore, in dependence on this [explanation above], understand that it is incorrect to 
assert that hearer foe -destroyers do not need to generate the Mahayana paths of 
accumulation and preparation and below, and enter at the minimum from the eighth ground, 
and the like. 
2B2C -1B2A -2C2D -2. Presenting our own system  
Therefore, even if hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers are taken to ha ve realized by 
way of direct cognition both of the subtle selflessnesses, when they enter the Mahayana they 
definitely enter from the path of accumulation, because, having initially determined well by 
way of hearing and contemplation the infinite divisions  of instances that are included within 
the accumulation of merit, they then familiarize with them by way of a mundane wisdom arisen from meditation for up to a countless eon, after which, they attain twelve sets of a 
hundred excellent qualities and so fort h which are included within the first ground.  
 Asserting that such [hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers] are ordinary beings when 
they abide on Mahayana paths of accumulation and preparation is a deprecation of arya 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
240 persons. Understand that [saying  that] what has been abandoned by a supr amundane path 
can return is a great wrong view that asserts that liberation is not trustworthy.   
 Furthermore, although [hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers  who have realized 
emptiness , and Mahayana aryas] d o not differ in the mere direct realization of dharmadhatu, 
know that the difference in their ability and power to destroy the discordant class and so 
forth is [like] the difference between Mount Meru  and a mustard seed. Therefore, you 
should know that, re gardless of whether hearers are asserted to have the realization of 
selflessness of phenomena or not, they enter [the Mahayana path] from the Mahayana path 
of accumulation.  
 In dependence on this [explanation], also know that in regard  to the two —someone 
definite in the Mahayana lineage and someone temporarily definite in the hearers’ lineage, who enter [the Mahayana path] from the path of accumulation at the same time —attaining 
unsurpassable enlightenment, there is a great difference in the Mahayana linea ge-bearer 
being very quick, and the other being very slow. I am able to describe the reason for that with infinite collections of reasoning, but fearing wordiness I did not write it down here.  
 Here the principal basis of debate is hearer and solitary rea lizer foe -destroyers who 
proceed to solitary peace. Understand that in proving that there is one final vehicle —as a 
proof that all sentient beings will attain buddhahood —the relationship is established as 
before.

COMMENTARY 
By way of  the generated mind  toward s enlightenment being  the very engagement to enact 
the welfare of sentient beings for as long as samsara exists, that “The afflictions  have not 
been abandoned in all ways”  is the nature.   
 
EXPLANATION  
That “The afflictions included within the contaminations of desire and existence121 have 
not been abandoned in all ways  with effort by taking them as pri ncipal objects of 
abandonment ” is the nature  of the knower of paths . Hence it is superior to the paths of 
hearers and solitary realizers, because, b y way of the generated mind toward s 
enlightenment being the very engagement to enact the welfare of sentient beings for as 
long as samsara exists,  the contaminations of desire and existence will serve as the 
branches of accomplishment of others’ welfare by bodhisattva aryas . 
2B2C -1B2A -2D2. Decisive analysis  
DISPUTANT: Although t he afflictions explained here are fully qualified ones, t hey are a 
cause for bodhisattva aryas to take rebirth , because [Arya Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the 
Twenty Thousand and [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
                                              
121 The contaminations of existence are the contaminations of the form and formless realms.  See stanza 5.2  of 
Vasubandhu’s  Treasury of Manifest  Knowledge  (Abhidharmakosha ).  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
241 They abandon the dormancies  included within ignorance and view, but not the 
contaminations of desire and existence, because they intentionally take rebirth in 
cyclic existence. 
 Nevertheless, it has been explained that by destroying the faults of the afflictions —the 
ability to make the mental continuum very unpeaceful, the tak ing of rebirth under the 
influence of afflictions, and so forth —[arya bodhisattvas] do not generate the fault of 
afflictions at any level.   
RESPONSE: That [afflictions are a cause for bodhisattva aryas to take rebirth] is 
contradicted by many scriptures a nd reasonings. Do bodhisattva aryas, not being able to 
take rebirth as they wish through the force of compassion and their prayers, need afflictions as an aid? Or is it that, even though they do not wish for the afflictions, due to fear that the 
self will be severed just before death -transference, craving for contaminated aggregates 
arise, and that [craving] aids the bodhisattvas to transmigrate?   
 The latter case [that craving for contaminated aggregates just before death -transference is 
an aid] contradic ts bodhisattvas not taking rebirth under the influence of afflictions. The 
former case [that bodhisattvas need afflictions as an aid] also contradicts that.  
 Furthermore, if they need afflictions as a cause for taking rebirth, then they have not 
abandoned  the sufferings of aging and so forth through the force of afflictions, whereby 
others’ welfare would be out of the question, since they are not able to enact even their own welfare as they wish. That would contradict many teachings, such as [Maitreya’s] 
Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum ( Mahayanottaratantrasastra ) which says:  
 
Aryas have abandoned from the root the sufferings of  
Sickness, aging, and death.  Rebirth through the force of karma and afflictions  
Does not exist for them.  
 
The meaning of the above passage [from the Great Commentary ] is not to show [that 
afflictions are] a cause for taking rebirth; one should know [that afflictions are] a branch of producing thousands of sons, by way of having taken the bodies of wheel -turning kings and 
so forth through the force of compassion and their prayers. Understand that, even when they take rebirth in the form realm, since they have extinguished the potency for taking rebirth 
through the force of the contaminated karma of a concentration, they do  not need 
[afflictions] as a cause for taking rebirth in the upper realms; once born there they take the 
contamination of existence as a branch of accomplishing others’ welfare. There is not the 
slightest need for afflicted ignorance, wrong views, and so f orth to be a branch of 
accomplishing others’ welfare. Therefore, [afflictions] included within the objects to be 
abandoned by the path of seeing have been abandoned exhaustively in them.  
 
DISPUTANT: In the  Sutra Requested by Sagaramati ( Sagaramatiparipriccha -sutra ), what 
are explained as the eight causes for taking rebirth
122 are imputed as afflictions. The y are 
                                              
122 According to the  Sutra Requested by Sagaramati ( Sagaramatiparipriccha-sutra ), these eight causes for 
taking rebirth are:  
1. Not being satisfied with seeking the accumulation of merit  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
242 designated [afflictions]  due to being similar in function to the afflictions , the causes for 
taking rebirth in ordinary beings .   
RESPONSE: That is  not correct. For, those [eight causes] are fully complete factors of 
method of a knower of paths, whereby there is no need to examine the qualm wondering, 
“Do bodhisattvas specifically abandon them?” here. If the afflictions explained here were  
designated  [afflictions], then also the afflictions that are to be specifically  abandoned by 
hearers’ paths would be designated   [afflictions] , because they [bodhisattvas] act to achieve 
their own enlightenment without having specifically  abandoned the afflictions, whereby 
[their paths] are shown to be superior in nature to hearers’ paths.   
 
Therefore, the afflictions [mentioned here] are fully qualified ones. Although they are the 
contaminations of desire and existence, they are not necessary as causes for taking rebirth; not only are the faults of the afflictions not generated, they become branches of 
accomplishing others’ welfare.  
 When those on the seventh ground and below accomplish [ a body of the nature of mind ] 
with effort, they are able to take a body of the nature of mind in dependence on the level of 
the latencies of ignorance and uncontaminated karma. However, know that spontaneous 
accomplishing [of a body of the nature of mind occurs] in hearer and solitary realizer foe -
destroyers, as well as in bodhisattvas who have gained mastery.

COMMENTARY 
After that, due to the actions bearing such a nature not actualizing the perfect end, and 
through wisdom and skillful means, fully gathering s entient beings not gathered and so 
forth, are deeds. 
 
EXPLANATION  
After that explanation on the nature , the function of the  knower of paths bearing such a 
nature is superior to the function of hearers’ path s, due to the deeds  of not actualizing the 
perfect end until one has fully completed prayers, fully ripened sentient beings, and 
completely purified the buddha land , and through the wisdom observing emptiness and 
the skillful means  of compassion and so forth, fully gathering sentient beings not 
gathered a nd so forth.

COMMENTARY 
Having described the support and so forth in that way, since it is ne cessary to complete all 
paths on the occasion of a knower of paths, hearers’ paths : 
 
EXPLANATION  
Having described the support and so forth in that way, it is appropriate to describe the 
mode of knowing hearers’ paths on the occasion of a knower of paths, s ince it is 
necessary to completely  cultivate all paths  of the three vehicles in order to attain an  
exalted knower of all aspects.  
2B2C -1B2B -1A2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.2 Regarding the mode s of a knower of paths,  
By means of not observing  
The aspects of the four noble truths  
These hearers’ paths are to be known.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding the mode s of completely cultivating  a knower of paths, the way to cultivate 
these hearers’ paths is  also to be known, because one must cultivate the sixteen aspects  
of the four noble truths  by means of being conjoined with the generated mind [ of 
enlightenment], dedication, and the wisdom not observing true existence. 
 The knower of paths knowing hearers’ paths which is explained here is an exalted 
wisdom directly realizing t he sixteen [aspects of the four noble truths], impermanence and 
so forth. These are to be meditated upon in order to take care of hearer lineage -bearers.   
 QUALM: Would it not become a knower of bases? Why is it called a knower of paths?  
RESPONSE: It is presented by means of being conjoined with the three attributes, not in 
terms of observing hearers’ paths and then meditating on their emptiness. An exalted 
wisdom directly realizing impermanence and so forth in the continuum of a bodhisattva on the path o f seeing is a knower of paths that knows hearers’ paths, and also a knower of 
bases. However, here at the time of terminological indicati on, it is indicated in a manner of 
being conjoined with three attributes. In the third chapter [of the Ornament], it is  indicated 
by way of the isolates of the realizations of impermanence and so forth, for the sake of 
passing through them having viewed them by knowing and seeing.

2B2C -1B2B -1A3A- 1A1. The aspects of true sufferings 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding that, in accordance with the sequence of true sufferings: 
(1) impermanence, (2) suffering, (3) emptiness, and (4) selflessness, the characteristi cs of 
those, are the four aspects of pacification , and   
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding that, in accordance with the sequence of the four aspects of true sufferings: 
(1) impermanence  due to being produced from conditions , (2) suffering  due to being under 
the control of karma and afflictions , (3) emptiness  due to being empty of a self that is 
another object , and (4) selflessness  due to its entity not being established as a self, are the 
four aspects of pacification  since the characteristics of those that are selfles s are not 
established as a self. Take what are overtly mentioned [in the commentary] as the theses and 
the explanation of their meaning as the signs. Within the explicit words of the sutra, the two 
latter aspects [empty and selfless] are indicated by way o f the aspect of enumeration  [of 
names] . According to that, it has been explained that the characteristic of empty is the 
aspect of being selfless, and the characteristic of that [being selfless] is the aspect of pacification .  
2B2C -1B2B -1A3A- 1A2. The aspects of true origins  
COMMENTARY 
The very entities of—(1) cause, (2) origin, (3) strong production, and (4) condition —of true 
origins, and the aspects of (1) disease, (2) boil, (3) pain, and (4) negativity, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The aspects of true origins  are correlated with the explanation by way of the enumeration 
in sutra as follows. The subject —craving —: 
• is the aspect of (1) cause , like a (1) disease, because it serves as the root of suffering  
• is the aspect of (2) origin , like a (2) boil, because it produces suffering again and again  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
245 • is the aspect of (3) strong production , like a (3) pain,  because it produces intense 
suffering  
• is the aspect of (4) condition , like the aspect of a (4) negativity, because it is a 
cooperative condition for fut ure rebirth.  
2B2C -1B2B -1A3A- 1B. The common aspects of true sufferings and true origins  
COMMENTARY 
Since “in order to be disillusioned, separate from attachment, and cease, there is entry” 
occurs —the characteristics of disillusionment of those individual true sufferings and true 
origins themselves: the very entities of the aspects of (1) the antagonist and (2) the bearer of the property of destruction; the entities of the objects from which attachment is to be 
separated: the aspects of (3) moving and (4) strongly disintegrating; and the self- entity of 
cessations: the aspects of (5) fear, (6) infectious disease, and (7) harm, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those true sufferings and true origins themselves —are to be observed as 
the characteristics of disillusionm ent towards individual  true sufferings and true origins, 
the entities of the aspects of (1) the enemy -like antagonist  due to being an opponent  and 
(2) the bearer of the property of destruction  due to acting to harm, since “in order to be 
disillusioned  regarding the two, true sufferings and true origins, there is entry  into the path 
of preparation” occurs. [The subject —those true sufferings and true origins themselves —] 
are to be observed as  the entities of the objects from which attachment is to be 
separate d, the aspects of (3) moving  due to not remaining for a second period of time, and 
(4) strongly disintegrating  without relying on conditions and causes arising later, since “in 
order to separate from attachment to true sufferings and true origins, there is  entry ” 
occurs. 
 [The subject —those true sufferings and true origins themselves —] are to be observed as  
the self- entity of cessations , the aspects of (5) fear  due to serving as a source of damage , 
(6) infectious disease  due to harm to the skull by rakshasa (cannibal spirits) and so forth , 
and (7) harm  due to lightning striking and so forth , since “in order to cease  true sufferings 
and true origins , there is entry” occurs  in the summary of the enumeration. 
 The three, dis illusionment and so forth, are [posited on] the occasions of the path of 
seeing, the end of the path of meditation, and [nirvana] without the remainder of the aggregates. By implication, entry for their sake is attained on the path of preparation, on the 
occasion of the path of meditation excluding the end, and on the occasion of [nirvana] with 
remainder. 
2B2C -1B2B -1A3A- 2. The aspects of true cessations   
COMMENTARY  
(1) The self- entity of the cessation of true cessations, selflessness, (2) pacification, (3) the 
nature of the aspect of sublim eness , isolation, and (4) the characteristic of definite 
emergence, the aspects of emptiness, signless, wishless, and being devoi d of manifest 
compositional action, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The aspect of the self- entity of the cessation of true cessations  is the aspect of (1) 
selflessness , because it is a separation in which the afflictions have been abandoned . [True 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
246 cessations ] is the aspe ct of (2) pacification,  because it is a separation in which sufferings 
have been pacified . [True cessations ] is the aspect of (3) isolation, the nature of the aspect 
of sublimeness , because it is a separation that is in the entity of cleanness  and happiness .  
 
Sutra says, “One who possesses the attention to the exalted knower of all aspects, by way of not observing it, takes to mind the cessation —of ignorance and so forth until aging and 
death, and the aggregates of suffering —as selfless, pacification, isolation, empty, signless, 
wishless, and being devoid of  manifest compositional action.” T he second aspect  is 
indicated by way of the entity, and the others by way of enumeration.  
 The subject—a separation with the characteristic of definite emer gence —is the 
aspects of (4) emptiness, signlessness, wishlessness, and being devoid of  manifest 
compositional action , because, respectively, (i) it is an abandonment where the manner 
through which one definitely emerges is free from the three —truly existen t entity, cause and 
result —and (ii) it is an abandonment in which what definitely emerges is free from truly 
existent manifest compositional actions  with exertion.   
2B2C -1B2B -1A3A- 3. The aspects of true paths 
COMMENTARY 
The aspects  of (1) path, (2) suitab ility, (3) achievement, and (4) deliverance —of true paths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —an aspect of true paths , the wisdom directly realizing selflessness —is the 
aspects of (1) path, (2) suitability, (3) achievement, and (4) deliverance , because, 
respectively , (1) it is a path that actually proceeds to nirvana, (2) it is suitable as the direct 
antidote to the seeds of afflictions, (3) it directly realizes the mode of abidance of the mind, and (4) it is a path that utterly extinguishes suffering . 
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(1) “One who possesses the attention to the exalted knower of all aspects, by way of not 
observing them, cultivates the close placements of mindfulness and so forth until the 
unmixed qualities of a buddha.”  
(2) “One practices the perfection o f generosity.”  
(3) “When one practices the Perfection of Wisdom one connects oneself to the Dharma by 
way of Dharma.”  
(4) “Koushika, that which is a mind of full dedication is not a mind.”  
2B2C -1B2B -1A3B. The manner of knowing them  
COMMENTARY 
By means of not observing entitiness, on the occasion of the knower of paths, bodhisattvas 
should fully know them in that way as being the hearers’ paths.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Bodhisattvas should fully know those paths explained above that are to be known in that 
way as bei ng the hearers’ paths. How?  By means of being conjoined with the generated 
mind [of enlightenment], dedication, and the wisdom not observing entitiness. When?  On 
the occasion of  the knower of paths  [when] the collection of causes of omniscience are 
shown .

ROOT TEXT  
2.3 With regard to the paths of arya hearers:  
Since forms and so forth are empty,  
Emptiness is indivisible, therefore  Heat; due to those not being observed,  
 2.4 It is asserted that it transforms in to the peak;  
By stopping the abiding on those  
By way of the permanent and so forth —the forbearance;  
Beginning from the ten grounds   
 
2.5 That extensively indicated as not abiding  
Transforms into  the supreme mundane quality —it is  explained. 
“Why?” Because the buddhas,  
By means of an exalted knower, do not see phenomena.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The direct knowing of the paths of arya hearers  must be preceded by its cause, its partial 
concordance with definite discrimination. The very realization of the sixteen, imperm anence 
and so forth, by way of meaning -generality (that is initially conjoined with the wisdom 
realizing emptiness by way of meaning -generality) is the heat; when increased, it is the 
peak . That conjoined with the attainment of a forbearance in which adherence to the 
permanent, the impermanent, and so forth as truly existent has been stopped is the 
forbearance . That conjoined with a wisdom that realizes that the apprehenders, the ten 
grounds are not truly existent objects to be abided upon is the supreme mu ndane  quality . 
Respectively, sutra says:    
• “Koushika, form is empty of form.”  
• “One should not abide on form by way of the observation of a bodhisattva.”  
• “One should not abide, saying, ‘Form is permanent’ by way of observing it.”  
• “Furthermore, a bodhisattva  should not abide on the first ground by way of observing it.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
248  
QUALM: Why  are they not truly existent objects to be abided upon ?  
RESPONSE: Because if phenomena were truly existing objects to be abided upon, the 
buddhas  must see that by means of a direct exalted knower, but they do not.   
 In this context, [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
SOME HEARER SCHOOL: With respect to a supreme emanation body 
demonstrating the manner of passing beyond sorrow, that is a passing beyond 
sorrow by way of severing the continuity of matter and cognition.  
 
There is much damage to this from scripture and reasoning. Whatever bears the absence of incomplete causes for its abidance does not bear the nature of its continuity of abidance 
being severed; for example, smoke a nd so forth, where the potentialized causes of 
abidance —fire, wood, and so forth —exist completely. Also the Buddha Bhagavan is one 
who bears the absence of incomplete causes of abidance. Having stated the sign above, 
when proving its property of the subjec t, extensive reasoning was demonstrated by means of 
the incorrectness of all of these following positions. W ould the Bhagavan’s incomplete 
causes for abiding  be: 
(1) due to him having exhausted the power to enact the welfare of sentient beings?  
(2) due to him being separated from great exalted loving- -compassion?  
(3) due to him degenerating from the power gained over the abidance of his compositional factor of life?  
(4) due to him having exhausted the potency of karma for his life power to abide?  
(5) due to  there being no sentient being to be subdued?  
 
Fearing wordiness, I will not elaborate more.  
2B2C -1B2B -1B3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Because the aggregates of forms and so forth themselves are empty of their own entitiness, emp tinesses are not mutually different, (2) as before, forms 
and so forth not observed, (3) “similarly, forms and so forth are neither permanent n or 
impermanent,” abidance by way of observing is stopped, (4) because of what? By the 
correctness of the sign of non-observation  by a valid being, “The tathagatas, having attained 
buddhahood in clear complete enlightenment, do not see phenomena,” not abiding in the 
Very Joyous ground and so forth— by these quoted aspects said to have been taught 
extensively, with resp ect to the objects of observation of the truths respectively, the partial 
concordance with definite discrimination is said to be generated. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The aggregates of forms and so forth and their emptinesses themselves are not mutually different ultimately, because they are empty of their own entitiness  ultimately; 
(2) forms and so forth do not exist ultimately, because, as before , if they were to exist 
truly, that would be suitable to be observed, but that is not observed; (3) similarly, abidance b y way of observing forms and so forth  as existing ultimately is stopped , 
because they are neither permanent n or impermanent  by way of existing ultimately, (4) 
the Very Joyous ground and so forth  are devoid of true existence as objects to be abided 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
249 upon; because of what? By the correctness of the sign of non -observation by a valid 
being , “Because, if they exist truly, that must be seen by the tathagatas, but the 
tathagatas, having attained buddhahood in clear  complete enlightenment, do not see 
phenomena  such as the ten grounds as objects to be abided upon ultimately,” not 
abiding —by these aspects said to have been taught extensively  in the sutras, through 
cultivation by observing the objects of observation of the truths respectively, the partial 
concordance  with definite discrimination is said to be generated . 
 What is explicitly presented here is the path of preparation that realizes emptiness. The 
cause of a knower of hearers’ paths, definite discrimination, is a mundane wisdom arisen 
from meditation observing the sixteen attributes, which is implied in this pa ssage.  
 OPINION: What is explicitly presented here repeats the path of preparation in the first chapter. 
RESPONSE: There is no fault because the purpose is dissimilar. The earlier [presentation] 
is for the sake of realizing that [the Mahayana path of prep aration] is superior to a hearer’s 
path of preparation. The [presentation] here is for the sake of making known that one is to meditate on the sixteen attributes of the four noble truths by being conjoined with the 
wisdom realizing emptiness. 
2B2C -1B2B -2. Knower of the nature of solitary realizers’ paths123 
2B2C -1B2B -2A. Meaning of the generality  
2B2C -1B2B -2B. The meaning of the branches  
2B2C -1B2B -2A. Meaning of the generality  
[Solitary realizers] have mild afflictions, an attribute of [their] lineage, where by they do not 
like disquiet and prefer to be alone. Compared to the bodhisattvas, they have little 
compassion and therefore do not engage in the extensive welfare of others. Due to being of 
middling faculty, they desire to attain enlightenment without com petition.  
 Their mode of training in the path is as follows. Some types of solitary realizers, at the 
time when they are ordinary beings, having pleased the buddhas who arise and collected 
merit for a hundred eons, attain the result of foe -destroyer in the  future life in which they 
attain the path of seeing. Some collect the accumulations for an indefinite period of time. Some, having completely generated the partial concordance with definite discrimination and 
below in the presence of a master, generate th e path of seeing in a future life, and then in the 
last rebirth attain the result of foe -destroyer.  
 There are two types of solitary realizers: those who rely on a master to generate an arya 
path and those who do not. In the life in which they attain the s tate of foe -destroyer, they 
definitely attain enlightenment without relying on a master; for, otherwise, they would be 
hearers. Know this extensively from elsewhere.

to describe the solitary realizers’ paths; but for the time being, the proof that they are 
superior  will be explained. If they are not different from hearers’ paths, it is unreasonable 
for them to be other. If there are differences, how are they superior to hearers’ paths? By 
what  reason ? In order to anticipate the qualms that wonder if their paths are other: 
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.6 Self- arisen because he realizes by himself; 
Also he does not need to be taught by others. 
The exalted wisdom of the rhinoceros -like 
Is clearly described as being more profound.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —a self-arisen foe-destroyer— does not need to be taught by others  in the life 
of his last existence, beca use in that life he realizes enlightenment by himself  without 
relying on a master.  
 Sutra says, “ Also , within the retinue, some sons of gods think this, ‘We will understand 
those languages of the yakshas and so forth when they are said, but we will not understand 
those explanations of the Perfection of Wisdom given by Arya Subhuti.’” [The significance 
of] “also” is that [solitary realizers] also do not definitely teach Dharma  to others by speech.  
 The subject —the exalted wisdom of the rhinoceros -like—is clearly described as 
being more profound that a hearer’s, because it cannot be realized by an effect sign that is 
speech teaching the Dharma as in the case of hearers, and it possesses profo und realization 
by way of teaching Dharma  with the body . 
 Sutra  says,  “The sons of gods think this, ‘Oh! The Elder  Subhuti cla rifies the Perfection 
of Wisdom; he teaches what is far more profound than the profound; he teaches what is far 
more subtle than the subtle.’ ”  
 Although the paths that will be explained below are  also cultivated  by bodhisattvas, do  
not think that it is contradictory to present the result in this context as that very [result] in 
the continuum of  solitary realizers. T he bodhisattvas ’ practice  of the path realiz ing that 
apprehendeds are devoid of nature  is for the sake of guiding  solitary realizer lineage -
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
251 bearers . The stages of the mode of travers ing their path and the mode of actualizing their 
result are mentioned as a branch of realization.   
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -3. Commentary 
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -3A. Diffe rence in traversing  the path  
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -3B. Difference in teaching the Dharma 
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -3A. Difference in traversing  the path  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. It occurs in the scriptures, “The hearers, having relied on being 
taught by others , will realize their own enlightenment; others also are caused to engage in 
virtue due to having been taught the articulated Dharma.” The solitary realizers, having 
performed manifest compositional actions previously in hearing and so forth, without 
relyin g on being taught by others, will clearly realize their own enlightenment by 
themselves. Because of that, it is not necessary for them to be taught by buddhas and so 
forth. This is one distinction.  
 
EXPLANATION  
It is not necessary for those  solitary realiz ers to be taught  enlightenment by buddhas and 
so forth  in the life of their last existence, because, without relying on being taught by 
others , they will clearly realize their own enlightenment by themselves  in that life. It will 
be so, since it will be so  due to the cause that is the solitary realizers having performed 
manifest compositional actions with respect to the path previously in hearing, prayers, and so forth. This is one distinction.  
 It is because they abide in the way  it occurs in the scripture s, “The hearers, having 
relied on being taught by others in the life of their last existence, will realize their own 
enlightenment; other  trainees also are caused to engage in virtue due to having been 
taught the articulated Dharma.”    
2B2C -1B2B -2B1A -3B. Difference in teaching the Dharma 
COMMENTARY 
If [hearers] cause the listeners to realize the mental prowess of the explainer by means of 
having taught the Dharma articulated in sound, then, they, through the force of exalted wisdom attained by themselves a nd so forth, by having taught the Dharma without sound, 
cause others to engage in the ten virtues and so forth. Because of that, exalted wisdom itself 
not realized of those is the second distinction.  
 
EXPLANATION  
From  the effect sign of speech, the exalted wisdom itself not realized of those solitary 
realizers is the second distinction  over the hearers by way of teaching the Dharma , 
because those  solitary realizers , through the force of exalted wisdom  attained by 
themselves  and through the force of p rayers  and so forth, by having taught the Dharma 
without sound, cause others to engage in the ten virtues , realizing selflessness, and so 
forth. There is pervasion, because hearers rely on  causing the listeners to realize the 
mental prowess , the ability of the mind  of the explainer by means of having taught the 
Dharma articulated in sound.

COMMENTARY 
Again, to anticipate the qualms that wonder how the Dharma is taught without sound : 
 
EXPLANATION  
Since the objection follows from other objections, again, to anticipate the qualms that 
wonder how the Dharma is taught without sounds:  
2B2C -1B2B -2B1B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.7 Whomever, whatever meanings, however – 
To one desiring to hear,  
The meanings  
Appear in that way even without sound.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Whomever trainees, whatever meanings that are to be realized, however the aspects —to one 
desiring to hear, the meanings appear in that way even without sound.  
 There is no contradiction that the solitary realizers teach Dharma even without sounds, 
because they are able to generate realizations in trainees as are appropriate , even without 
sounds.  
2B2C -1B2B -2B1B -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because one does not speak words without having investigated and without having analyzed, articulation is a distraction. Since it also disturbs the continuum
, 
bodhisattvas made prayers, “May  I teach the Dharma without articulation, by becoming a 
buddha in that way.” Therefore, they are similar to buddhas . Since, also in the context of 
solitary realizers, by the force of prayers and so forth, even without sound, in the 
consciousness of whoever  desiring to listen to whatever meaning in however an aspect, it 
will appear as only that meaning and that aspect, it is called “teaching Dharma without 
sound.” The aim of “sounds that teach Dharma” is this, “to strongly produce and generate in 
the conscio usness of the listener, the Dharma which is to be articulated in this manner.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
“Teaching Dharma without sound is not contradictory,” because, if the aim that is the 
purpose of “sounds that teach Dharma” is this, “to strongly produce and generate in 
the consciousness of the listener, the realization of the Dharma which is to be 
articulated in this manner,”  then that is present also in Dharma taught by solitary realizers 
without sound. That is present, since, also in the context of solitary reali zers, by the force 
of previous prayers and so forth, even without  the sound teaching Dharma, in the 
consciousness of whoever  trainee desiring  to listen to whatever meaning  of high status 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
253 and definite goodness in however an aspect, it will appear as only th at meaning and that 
aspect. 
 The reason for that is that bodhisattvas made prayers, “May I teach the Dharma 
without articulation, by becoming a buddha in that way”; solitary realizers also made 
prayers in that manner in the past. Therefore, they are simila r to buddhas.  There is a 
reason for solitary realizers making prayers in that way, since articulation  of words 
distracts  [them] from meditative stabilization, and it also disturbs the continuum  of 
meditative stabilization. The reason for that is because one does not speak words without 
having investigated  the entity and without having analyzed the attributes. Bodhisattvas 
accomplish previous prayers, “May I, without relying on articulation, be able to generate realizations in the continua of trainees when  I attain buddhahood.” The meaning of they 
being “similar to buddhas” is just that; it is not that buddhas teach in the absence of speech 
teaching the Dharma.  
 In addition, solitary realizers mostly abide in that manner; it is not that it is impossible fo r 
solitary realizers to give teachings with speech, because it has been taught in the  Vinaya 
Scripture  that [there are solitary realizers who] teach Dharma with speech, and because it 
has been taught in the Clear Lamp ( Pradipoddyotana- Namatika ) that “They mainly teach 
Dharma with the body.”  
 The reason for them mostly abiding in that manner is that, solitary realizers are not able 
to teach with articulation the Dharma without relying on investigation and analysis, in the way that buddhas are able to, whereb y it is said that it “disturbs the continuum” if they were 
to teach the Dharma with articulation.  
 
OPINION: Teaching the Dharma with speech and teaching it with body do not differ with 
respect to causing [others] to realize one’s mental prowess.  
RESPONSE: That is not correct, (i) because by way of displaying magical emanations with 
the body, trainees are able to realize merely one’s possession of magical emanation but they 
are unable to realize that one possess es a realization of selflessness, and (ii) because they are 
able [to realize that one possess es a realization of selflessness] from the effect sign of 
speech. 
 If [the display of magical emanations] were to definitely rely on a motivation thinking “I 
want to display magical emanations,” then the reason  for not teaching Dharma with speech  
[explained above] would  be a counterfeit [reason] . Hence, one is able to display magical 
emanations without relying on such a motivation, since the ability to produce realizations of 
high status and definite goodness in  the continua of trainees in dependence on seeing those 
[magical emanations] is the result of prayer.  
 In our system, it is simply incorrect to negate only the teaching of Dharma with the body 
without being able to posit even slightly the meaning of teach ing the Dharma.

EXPLANATION

2B2C -1B2B -2B2B. Root text  
2B2C -1B2B -2B2B -1. Meaning of the words  
2B2C -1B2B -2B2B -2. Decisive analysis  
ROOT TEXT  
2.8 Because it abandons the conceptions of apprehendeds as objects, 
Because it does not abandon apprehenders, and   
By a support, the path of the rhinoceros -like 
Is to be known as perfectly encapsulated. 
2B2C -1B2B -2B2B -1. Meaning of the words  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the path of the rhinoceros -like [solitary realizers]—is to be known as 
perfectly encapsulated due to  the possession of three attributes, (i) because it abandons 
the conceptions of apprehendeds , forms and so forth, as external objects , (ii) because it 
does not abandon the conceptions of apprehenders, consciousnesses apprehending forms 
and so forth, as truly existent apprehenders , and (iii) because it is to be known as being 
distinguished by being a support  that is supported by lineage.  
 It is also to be known by bodhi sattvas by way of being conjoined with the generated mind 
[of enlightenment], dedication, and the wisdom realizing emptiness. Respectively, sutra says:  
• “Form is not profound; it is not subtle. Why is that? The nature of form is not profound; it 
is not subtle,”  
• “Then those sons of the gods thought this, “Elder Subhuti, what kind of listening to the Dharma should we seek?” and,  
• “Then Subhuti said to those sons of the gods, “Sons of the gods, seek the listening of the Dharma which is like an illusion and li ke an emanation.”   
2B2C -1B2B -2B2B -2. Decisive analysis  
In this context, by way of the three attributes that have been indicated in sutra, both the objects to be generated in the continua of Hinayana persons and the objects to be generated 
in the continua of bodhisattvas have been indicated. Know them also in that way in this 
treatise. Now, the analysis of this:   
DISPUTANT: The views of solitary realizers and the  Proponents of Mind -Only  are not 
similar, because, even though the former have abandoned the c onception of apprehendeds, 
they adhere to apprehenders as truly existent; the latter realize apprehendeds and 
apprehenders as being devoid of  true existence.  
RESPONSE: You have not examined this closely at all. What is the meaning of a solitary realizer realizing apprehendeds as being devoid of  true existence?  
 
DISPUTANT: He realizes that those objects —property -possessing bases, forms and so 
forth, established by valid cognition —are devoid of  true existence. However, he does not 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
255 realize that the sense con sciousnesses that apprehend them and so forth are devoid of true 
existence. 
RESPONSE: That is very unreasonable, because, having realized that a property -possessing 
base does not exist truly, when one engages in analysis as to whether or not other property -
possessing bases exist truly, in dependence upon remembering the way in which the 
previous property of the subject and pervasion have been established by valid cognition, one 
definitely realizes that they  are devoid of true existence. For, otherwise, it follows that it 
would be impossible to realize that all property -possess ors are devoid of  true existence on a 
learner’s path. Therefore, Acarya  Aryadeva says in his  Four Hundred Stanzas  
(Chatushataka):  
 
Due to seeing the thusness of one thing,  
One sees the  thusness of all things.  
 DISPUTANT: Although they realize that forms and so forth do not exist as external objects, 
solitary realizers do not realize that there is no apprehender that is a different substance 
from them.  
RESPONSE: It is not possible for anyone, even someone with dull faculties, to have negated a form as being a different substance from a sense consciousness, but not have 
negated sense consciousness as being a different substance from form.  
 
DISPUTANT: Although forms have been negated as b eing external objects, there is no 
certainty that the apprehendeds included within the substance of consciousness have been 
negated as being different objects, because [Dharmakirti's] Commentary on [Dignaga's] 
'Compendium of Valid Cognition'  (Pramanavarttikakarika ) says: “The argument is similar 
for that,” indicating other reasonings that negate them. 
RESPONSE: There is no fault, because if the apprehendeds that rely upon the substance of 
consciousness have been negated as being different objects, it is imp ossible that the 
apprehenders have been not negated as being different objects. It is incorrect that tenets that, 
having negated external objects, do not negate the apprehendeds as being mere other objects 
differ in being higher or lower. 
 Hence, the meani ng of “[the path of solitary realizers] abandon s the conception s of 
apprehendeds ” [in 2.8a] is that it realize s that forms and so forth do not exist as a different 
substance from the sense consciousnesses that apprehend them. As for “[the path of solitary 
realizers] does not abandon apprehenders ” [in 2.8b] , not only do they not realize that 
consciousnesses, such as sense consciousnesses, are empty of true existence, they do not 
definitely need to familiarize with that realization in order to attain  the state of a foe -
destroyer of the middling vehicle.   Therefore, saying that “Since the view of solitary realizers indicated here and the view of 
the Proponents of Mind- Only  do not differ even slightly in being higher or lower, it is 
amazing that the tw o have similar tenets” is a joke for the clarifiers. The intention of 
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  is also to be understood to be only that. This Acarya  
[Haribhadra] asserts there are no external objects even conventionally, in agreement with 
the Propon ents of Cognition -Only. Even bodhisattvas meditate on the emptiness of 
apprehenders and apprehendeds being different substances, on the occasions of the first 
three [paths]. Although they pass through it having viewed them by knowing and seeing on 
the occa sion of the path of seeing, they are not diligent in mainly familiarizing with it on the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
256 occasion of the path of meditation; it is cultivated in the manner of an auxiliary. Therefore, 
know that it will transform into the non -dual instantaneous application at the time of the end 
of the continuum of a sentient being.   The assertion that it is possible for solitary realizers to realize the subtle meaning of 
emptiness has already been explained above. A knower of bases that is included within this realization has been presented with respect to solitary realizers, the support of the 
presentation, but it is also generated in the continua of bodhisattvas. Hence, the mode of knowing solitary realizers’ paths is that they are not to be comprehended as if one is look ing 
at visual form with eyes. Although solitary realizers observe the scriptural basket of hearers, due to the distinction in faculty, they realize the selflessness of phenomena.  
2B2C -1B2B -2B2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Concerning s olitary realizers’ paths: only to meditate on the truths 
which have been explained, and by having meditated on dependent -arising according to the  
actual, respectively to abandon and not abandon conceptualizations of objects of 
apprehendeds and apprehenders ; and by the attribute of the basis of the supported qualities 
included within the solitary realizer’s vehicle, to realize the qualities possessing 
distinction —are to be known by the bodhisattvas; they are not to be known in all ways. 
These are the paths o f the solitary realizers.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Solitary realizers’ paths  are to be directly known by bodhisattvas; they are not to be 
known in all ways  by way of actualization. These are the paths of the solitary realizers  
that are to be known . Distinguished by wh at are they to be known? Concerning solitary 
realizers’ paths: only to meditate on the sixteen aspects of the truths which have been explained,  (definite apprehension is the negative of non -possession); by having meditated 
on the forward and reverse proces ses of the twelve links of dependent -arising according 
to the actual modes of arisal and reversal , respectively to abandon conceptualizations 
that adhere to apprehendeds as external objects, and not abandon conceptualizations of 
objects that apprehend consciousness as truly existent ; and, by the attribute of the  
supporting basis that is supported  on lineage, the  dharmata  of the supported qualities 
included within the solitary realizer’s vehicle, to realize the q ualities possessing 
distinction. 
 That ve ry exalted wisdom which directly realizes apprehendeds as being devoid of  true 
existence is called a solitary realizers’ path. That which is present in the continuum of a 
solitary realizer is supported upon the dharmata of his six sources. That which is present in 
the continuum of a bodhisattva is supported upon his lineage abiding in the nature. Do not 
think that it follows that that very lineage in the continuum of a bodhisattva is the solitary 
realizers’ lineage. Although even hearers directly realize th e modes of entering into and 
turning away from cyclic existence and familiarize with them, they do not abandon the 
adherence to external objects. That they do not directly realize [the appearances of external 
objects] as mere mistaken appearances of the erroneous adherence to external objects is due 
to their having dull faculties as compared to the former [solitary realizers].

2.10 Through the emptiness of the inner and so forth,  
Because of not apprehending forms and so forth, forbearance.
