EXPLANATION  
Through the realization of emptiness of the inner and so forth, because of not 
apprehending forms and so forth  as external objects, forbearance . And that bearing 
the aspect of forms and so forth being devoid of  production  is supreme mundane  
quality . Respectively, sutra sa ys:  
• “Oh, Elder Subhuti, this does not contradict imputation. Showing dharmata also possesses 
profound wisdom”;   
• “Also, one should not train in order to increase forms”;  
• “Also, Subhuti said, “Venerable Sariputra , in this way there is no full apprehension as 
forms”; and  
• “Subhuti said, ‘Sariputra , when a bodhisattva mahasattva practices the Perfection of 
Wisdom in this way, he does not see the production of forms.”  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
258 It appears to be just suitable to explain the e xplicit presentation of the root text as explicitly 
indicating the four levels of the path of preparation which realize the emptiness of 
apprehendeds and apprehenders being different substances, whereby it is explained thus. 
The Commentary  has explained it  in a way similar to the previous path of preparation [in the 
first chapter].  
2B2C -1B2B -2B3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) “To clearly show that they are not contradictory with the 
dharmata of that imputed which arose from the terminology of phenomena that are forms 
and so forth, (2) to learn the meaning of forms and so forth being devoid of  decre ase and 
increase and so forth ultimately, (3) to not apprehend by the emptinesses of the inner, the 
outer, and so forth with respect to forms and so forth, because of the emptiness of entitiness, 
and (4) the absence  of production and cessation with respect  to forms and so forth.” With 
respect to the objects of observation that are the four truths respectively, by way of these abovementioned aspects, the partial concordance with definite discrimination is generated.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) By explicitly showing the  exalted wisdom of the heat level (which realizes that , in the 
way forms and so forth have been clearly  shown,  they are not contradictory with the 
dharmata of  that imputed which arose from the terminology of phenomena that are 
forms and so forth, which alt hough not existing ultimately, exist just conventionally), the 
heat level which by means of a meaning -generality realizes the emptiness of apprehendeds 
and apprehenders being different substances is shown implicitly. (2) By [explicitly] showing the peak le vel which realizes the meaning  of forms and so 
forth being devoid of  decrease and increase and so forth ultimately, in the way  it has 
been  learnt, the other peak level is shown implicitly. 
(3) By [explicitly showing] the forbearance level which does not ap prehend apprehendeds 
as truly existent by the very realization of the emptinesses of the inner, the outer, and so 
forth with respect to forms and so forth, because of the emptiness of entitiness  
ultimately, the other [forbearance level is shown implicitly] . 
(4) By explicitly showing the supreme mundane quality level which realizes the absence of 
production and cessation ultimately  with respect to forms and so forth, the other 
[supreme mundane quality level] is shown implicitly .  
 
By observing and meditating on the objects of observation that are the four truths 
respectively , by way of these abovementioned aspects  which have been explained, the 
partial concordance with definite discrimination is generated . There is a purpose for 
explicitly showing the four le vels of the path of preparation that bear the aspect of 
emptiness in this context, because it is for the sake of making known that the four levels of 
the path of preparation shown implicitly are cultivated by being conjoined with the wisdom 
realizing empti ness.

2B2C -1B2B -3B. Individual entities  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1. The path of seeing

2B2C -1B2B -3B1. The path of seeing  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1 A. Meaning of the generality

2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2. The supported path of seeing  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -1. The support of the path of seeing  
The body support is humans excluding those of [the northern continent] Unpleasant Sound, 
hermaphrodites and neuters, and the support of the gods of desire realm. It is not generated 
on the support of the upper realms, because [beings of the form and  formless realms] have 
weak despondency with regard  to the three realms.  
 Regarding the mind support, it is supported on an actual concentration. Although it is 
explained in [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary that the path of meditation is supported on a 
desire realm mind and on the Peak of Existence, I wonder if that is able to characterize the 
path of seeing.  
 The explanation in the Sutra on the Ten Grounds  that one attains the concentrations and 
clairvoyances on the occasion of the third ground indicates an attainment by the force of 
meditative equipoise, an uncommon attribute. For, the actual fourth concentration bearing 
the aspect of peace and coarseness has  definitely  been  attained by a bodhisattva on the 
occasion of the path of preparation; if that has  beenattained , there is no need to generate a 
supramundane path in dependence on a more difficult path.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2. The supported path of seeing  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2A. The entity of the path of seeing  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2B. Divisions  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C. Decisi ve analysis  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2A. The entity of the path of seeing  
[The entity of the path of seeing] is that concomitant with wisdom and meditative 
stabilization, [occurring] immediately after the supreme mundane quality level of the path 
of preparation, ne wly realizing emptiness free of elaboration by way of direct cognition, 
through observing the sixteen attributes of the four noble truths, and conjoined with all types of deeds, generosity and so forth.  
 That is from the point of view of the uncommon Maha yana path of seeing; the definition 
of a mere Mahayana path of seeing is: a Mahayana clear realization of the truth that is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
260 conjoined with a Mahayana exalted wisdom newly realizing emptiness by way of direct 
cognition in the continuum of the one [possessin g it]. There is also a path of seeing that 
directly realizes the sixteen, impermanence and so forth, on that occasion.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2B. Divisions  
There are eight forbearances included within the uninterrupted path which observe the dharmata of the four noble truths, and eight knowledges included within the liberated paths: 
sixteen differentiated by way of isolates.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C. Decisive analysis  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C1. How refutation [of others’ systems] and positing [of our system] is done by Acarya 
[Haribhadra] in the Great Commentary  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C2. Summarizing and indicating the assertion of Acarya [Haribhadra]  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C3. Analyzing the sequence in which the path of seeing that acts to pass through and the 
path of seeing that is to be passed through are generated  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C1. How refutation [of others’ systems] and positing [of our system] is done by Acarya 
[Haribhadra] in the Great Commentary  
DISPUTANT: The dharmatas of the four noble truths are realized by way of direct cognitio n with a single mind in one instant. Therefore, at that time, sufferings are known, 
origins are abandoned, cessations are actualized, and paths are cultivated. Thus, the clear realization that is an object upon which one acts with respect to the four noble  truths, or the 
resultant clear realization, is one instant. However, from the point of view of the sequential 
elimination of the errors with respect to each of the four noble truths, there are sixteen 
instants of individually realizing clear realization. Therefore, the presentations of the 
approachers and abiders are correct.  
RESPONSE: This is not the assertion of Acarya  [Haribhadra]. It is not correct even by way 
of reasoning, because (i) if true cessations were actualized at the time of newly realizing the 
dharmata of true sufferings, then it follows that true cessations would not need to be 
preceded by true paths, and (ii) it follows that the uninterrupted path and liberated path of a 
specific basis would arise simultaneously.  
 DISPUTANT: True cessatio ns will be realized [afterwards].  
RESPONSE: With respect to the two —the earlier and the later—the classification of either 
one or sixteen instants would be meaningless.  
 
ANOTHER DISPUTANT: [Dharmakirti's] Commentary on [Dignaga's] 'Compendium of 
Valid Cognition'  (Pramanavarttikakarika) says:  
 
A single self- entity of the object  
Is the nature directly cognized.  
Of any object to be investigated by valid cognition,  
What other unseen part is there?  
 
Rather than being the clear realizations which individually realize by way of the mode 
described above, it is one instant. This asserts that an uninterrupted path and a liberated path are produced in one instant, not sequentially, because, otherwise it wou ld not be right to 
fling to this [disputant above] the consequence that the presentation of the approachers and abiders is incorrect.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
261  
RESPONSE: That is also not the assertion of Acarya  Haribhadra because he asserts that the 
forbearances and knowledges are produced sequentially. Hence, Pramanavarttikakarika  
says:  
 
Due to seeing form of concordant qualities,  
The outside of a fish [appears] like the aspect of silver.  Due to this mistaken reason,  
Other excellent qualities are not connected.  
 
If it were l ike that, then since there are many erroneous superimpositions., although [the 
uninterrupted path of the path of seeing] is one instant by way of the force of reasoning, it is 
explained to be sixteen instants by way of isolates. Since the forbearances and knowledges 
are produced sequentially, it is taught that “The presentation of the approachers and abiders 
is correct.”  
 
This is what is asserted by Haribhadra. With respect to this:  
 DISPUTANT: Although all the parts are apprehended, from the point of view  of the ability 
to dispel superimpositions, all the sixteen instants only arise sequentially. Furthermore, the 
ability is attained sequentially by way of one session of meditative equipoise. 
 RESPONSE: This is not correct by way of reasoning. It means that  during subsequent 
attainment sixteen dissimilar ascertaining consciousnesses are generated sequentially; it does not mean that a valid cognizer that induces the ascertaining consciousnesses only 
arises sequentially.  
 This is like the case of the generati on of sequential ascertainments thinking “I have seen 
this place” due to a single direct cognizer even in ordinary beings.  
 Although [all the parts] are apprehended in every way, the explanation that the ability to 
dispel obscurations with respect to them is a valid cognizer does not mean that sixteen dissimilar valid cognizers are produced sequentially in one session of meditative equipoise 
of the path of seeing, because, otherwise it would be meaningless to differentiate [the path 
of seeing] from the vali d cognizer of an ordinary being.  
 The meaning of that is as follows. The reply is that, there is indeed a difference in 
whether or not they are able to abandon the seeds; in ordinary beings, there is a 
consciousness to which an object appears but is not as certained, such that ascertainment is 
not generated although an object has been apprehended by way of direct cognition; however, like it has been taught in [Dharmakirti's Commentary on [Dignaga's] 
'Compendium of Valid Cognition'  (Pramanavarttikakarika )] wi th respect to aryas that “The 
greatly intelligent ones, from seeing, ascertain all aspects,” a consciousness to which an object appears but is not ascertained is not possible in aryas. For, otherwise, the reply that 
“Due to the aspects having become cleare r after that, by that much their consciousness es 
become free from mistaken reasons; by way of just that they are superior,” would be 
meaningless. 
 Therefore, the uninterrupted path that is the dharma forbearance of true sufferings itself is 
generated in th e entity of the eight forbearances, and, by means of it having become valid 
with respect to the dharmata of the four noble truths, it is an uninterrupted path that 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
262 abandons the seeds that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing of all four 
noble truths. 
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C2. Summarizing and indicating the assertion of Acarya [Haribhadra]  
When the dharmata of the four noble truths which has not been directly realized before is 
newly realized in a manner of water placed in water, while it is reaso nable that one is afraid 
of this profound dharma which one  has never previously familiarized with in cyclic 
existence, one is unafraid, whereby it is called “dharma forbearance.” Therefore, there are 
four forbearances by way of isolates comprehending the d harmata of the four noble truths.  
 While it is reasonable that subsequently one is  afraid of that mind in which dharmata has 
become [like] water placed in water, one realizes it without fear, whereby it is called 
“subsequent forbearance.” There are the fo ur subsequent forbearances from the point of 
view of realizing the four object -possessors of the dharmata of the four noble truths, 
differentiated by way of isolates. The eight forbearances exist in the manner of being one 
entity but different isolates. At  that time, a true cessation that is free from adventitious stains 
has not been attained yet.  
 From the point of view of realizing the dharmata of the four noble truths, one is posited 
to realize the object -possessors of that. When one, without arising from that very meditative 
equipoise, attains a true cessation that is free from adventitious stains and generates a 
liberated path, the four dharma forbearances (differentiated by way of isolates) which 
realize the dharmata of the four noble truths, the fo ur subsequent forbearances which realize 
those very object -possessors [as being empty of true existence] , and the eight knowledges 
exist in a manner of one entity and different isolates. Hence the clear realization of truth of a 
single session of meditativ e equipoise is generated in the nature of sixteen instants of 
knowledges and forbearances in one instant. We do not assert in this context that the subsequent forbearances are generated subsequent to having generated the dharma 
forbearances. 
 The path of s eeing being one instant means that the meditative equipoise which is set in 
equipoise on emptiness is a single session, not that it is the shortest limit of time. Also the 
presentation of approachers at the time of abiding on the forbearances, and abiders in the 
result at the time of abiding on the knowledges is correct; at the time of the meditative equipoise, one abides in the nature of powerful ability to practice the yoga that includes the 
six perfections in their entirety in each of [the six perfection s] generosity and so forth, for, 
otherwise, one would not be able to destroy the seeds that are knowledge obscurations.  
 At the time of the subsequent knowledge of true origins, there is no manifest meditation 
on the four immeasurables, love and so forth. However, since these excellent qualities 
(which have been previously meditated on and will be meditated on again later) exist 
without degeneration in the continuum of that person, he does not have the fault of falling 
into the cessation.  
 In regard  to the  way of positing the number of instants of the path of seeing, I do not see 
any difference between this Acarya [Haribhadra] and what occurs in the root text and 
commentary of [Nagarjuna’s] Sixty Stanzas of Reasoning ( Yuktisastikakarika ). However, 
they differ drastically when it comes to whether or not a self- cognizer is asserted and so 
forth.  
 Apart from that, regarding those who do not assert that one realizes the dharmata of true 
origins when one  realizes the dharmata of true sufferings, I see them as a source of many 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
263 faults, such as having to assert that “an object of hearing”125 is a correct sign in the proof 
that sound is impermanent.  
 At the time of being set in meditative equipoise on emptiness, also the Mahayana 
generated mind is present in the continuum of that person, but how can it be necessary for there to be a manifest awareness thinking, “I will attain enlightenment for the bene fit of 
sentient beings”? Understand the other qualities in a similar way.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1A -2C3. Analyzing the sequence in which the path of seeing that acts to pass through and the 
path of seeing that is to be passed through are generated  
OPINION: Are the t wo—(1) the clear realization that is to passed through, which directly 
realizes the sixteen, impermanence and so forth, and (2) the path of seeing bearing the 
aspect of dharmata —simultaneous or sequential?  
 In the first case [they are simultaneous], when a single mind directly realizes both [the 
sixteen aspects and emptiness] by way of direct cognition, the exalted knowers of modes 
and of varieties would exist as a single entity. That they differ in being an explicit or 
implicit realization is incorrect, b ecause, otherwise, the same would follow on the occasion 
of the path of preparation.  
 In the latter case [they are sequential], if the path of seeing that is to be passed through is 
generated first, then a bodhisattva would need to sequentially abandon th e seeds of the 
acquired afflictions that are the two obscurations; if they are generated in opposite order to 
that [the path of seeing that acts to pass through is generated first], then, after having already 
generated the path of seeing that acts to pass through, one would have to subsequently 
generate a path of seeing that newly realizes the selflessness of persons, whereby it would 
be endless.  
 
RESPONSE: There is no fault, because, although that very path of seeing that acts to pass 
through realizes wit h direct cognition that the sixteen aspects are free from elaboration, it 
does not realize the sixteen aspects. Having exhaustively destroyed the seeds of the acquired afflictions that are the two obscurations by that itself, afterwards a manifest path of seeing 
that is to be passed through which directly realizes the sixteen aspects is generated. 
 The instants [of the path of seeing] explained above are in terms of meditative equipoise; 
as long as one has not been directly set in meditative equipoise on em ptiness in order to 
directly destroy the seeds of the innate afflictions that are obscurations to knowledge, one is 
to be known as being included within the path of seeing.

ROOT TEXT  
2.11 By way of four types of instants of the forbearances  
And knowledges with respect to the truths and truths,  
This path of seeing in regard to a knower of paths  
Is explained along with the benefits.  
 
EXPLANATION  
This path of seeing  in the context of a knower of paths is explained along with its 
benefits , because it is explained by way of  the benefits and four types of instants of the 
forbearances and knowledges with respect to the true  sufferings and origins and true  
cessations and paths.  
 Sutra says, “‘Venerable Sariputra , where should the Perfection of Wisdom be sought?’ 
Sariputra  said, ‘Koushika, the Perfection of Wisdom is to be sought in the Subhuti 
chapter.’”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -1B. Co mmentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. On the occasion of a knower of paths, (1 -2) “forbearance and 
dharma knowledge of dharma knowledge and (3- 4) forbearance and subsequent knowledge 
of subsequent knowledge,” that to be meditated on by bodhisattvas , possessing the four 
instants of forbearance and knowledge individually related to true sufferings and so forth, 
possessing excellent qualities of this and others, is described as “the path of seeing, the great 
benefit.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
That  in which emptine ss is to be newly meditated on  by way of direct cognition 
possessing excellent qualities of this life and other lives, such as ripening sentient beings 
on the occasion of a knower of paths, is described as “the path of seeing, the great 
benefit.”  
 This is  because the possession  of each of the four instants of forbearance of dharma 
knowledge and so forth individually related as object and object -possessor to the 
dharmata of true sufferings and so forth by bodhisattvas  is described as “the path of 
seeing bea ring great benefit.”

265 2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.12 Because in thusness and buddhas, 
Mutual support and supported do not exist,  
Not accepting categorization;  
The great; as well as valid cognizer does not exist;  
 2.13 Immeasurable; without extremes;  
One abiding on that definitely apprehends  Forms and so forth as the state of buddha;  
Not adopted, not discarded, and so forth;  
 
2.14 Love and so forth; emptiness;  
Attaining buddhahood itself;  
Thoroughly included in all the completely pure;  
Dispelling all anxieties and sicknesses;  
 
2.15 Pacifying apprehending nirvana;  
Being guarded by the buddhas and so forth;  
Not killing and so forth,  
The manner of an exalted knower of all aspects,  
 
2.16 Oneself abiding, thereby setting sentient beings;  
Generosity and so forth,  
Dedicating to complete enlightenment –  
These are the instants of the knower of paths. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The Great Lama Lotsawa, referring to the assertions of Acarya  Vimuktisena, explains that 
the entity of sixteen instants of knowledges and forbearances are indicated by the first four 
instants and the benefits by the remaining [instants]. He explains that the benefit of the first 
five instants is indicated by “ without extremes ”; the benefit of the dharma knowledge of 
true origins is indicated by “ One abiding on that definitely apprehends forms and so 
forth as the state of buddha”; the benefit of the subsequent forbearance of true origins is 
indicated by “ Not to be adopted, not to be discarded, and so forth ”; and the benefit of the 
subsequent knowledge [of true origins] is indicated by “ love and so forth”; the benefits of 
the four instants of knowledges and forbearances of true cessations are respect ively 
indicated by “ emptiness ” and so forth until “dispel all anxieties and sicknesses ”; the 
benefits of the four instants of knowledges and forbearances of true paths are respectively indicated by the four below.  
 It is asserted that these dissimilar aspe cts explained here are indicated to be aspects that 
are meditated on the occasion of the path of preparation; by meditating on them in that way, 
one generates the path of seeing, and one also attains these benefits during the occasions 
after that; the sixt een instants are characterized and indicated by them. Although these 
aspects are to be meditated on during the occasion of the path of preparation, these excellent qualities must definitely be possessed on the occasions of the sixteen instants of the path of 
seeing.

2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -2C1A. The aspects of [true] sufferings 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Through just the ultimate mode, there do not exist  the things that 
are the support and the supported regarding thusness and buddhas -  because of that, not 
asserting them as abiding by way of mutual categorizations and (2) through the entitiness of 
the dharmadhatu of forms and so forth, just great and (3) similarly, a valid cognizer of those 
just does not exist, and (4) like the former, through space just being devoid of  measure, they 
are devoid of  measure. The aforesaid like that are the aspects of true sufferings.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) Through just the ultima te mode , the exalted wisdom directly realizing the thusness  of 
true sufferings and buddhas does not assert them as abiding by way of  categorizations  
of mutual  support and supported ultimately, because  the things that are the support and 
the supported  do not exist in  them ultimately. By meditating in this way on the occasion 
the dharma forbearance of true sufferings of the path of seeing, one obtains the benefit of the firm exalted wisdom of a buddha in the future.  
 In short, the subject —the exalted wisdom o f dharma forbearance of true sufferings that 
directly realizes that (i) the thusness of true sufferings and (ii) the exalted wisdom that is its object -possessor are not established as the support and the supported ultimately —is the 
aspect of the dharma forbearance of true sufferings , because it is an exalted wisdom of 
dharma forbearance that acts as a direct antidote to the seeds that are the corresponding 
objects of abandonment of dharma forbearance. Know the rest similarly.   Sutra says: “In the thusness of forms, tathagatas are not observed; in the tathagatas, the 
thusness of forms is also not observed.”  
 
(2) Apply similarly as before to “ through  the entitiness of dharmadhatu , the emptiness of 
true existence ultimately with r espect to forms and so forth, its object -possessor, the 
Perfection of Wisdom is  just great.” (3) The subject —the exalted wisdom of subsequent 
forbearance of true sufferings that directly realizes that similarly, a valid cognizer  that 
comprehends those  sufferings ultimately  just does not exist—[apply as before]. (4) The 
exalted wisdom of subsequent knowledge of true sufferings that directly realizes that, like 
the former, through space just being devoid of  measure, those sufferings  being devoid 
of measure  ultimately is the aspect of that [subsequent knowledge of true sufferings]; the 
reason is as before [because it is an exalted wisdom of subsequent knowledge that has 
abandoned  the seeds that are the corresponding objects of abandonment of subsequent 
forbeara nce]. The aforesaid like that are the aspects of true sufferings.  
 
Sutra says:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
267 • “Because form is just great this Perfection of Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas is just 
great.”  
• “Koushika, because a valid cognizer with respect to forms does not exist, a vali d cognizer 
with respect to this Perfection of Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas does not exist.”  
• “Koushika, because forms are devoid of  measure, this Perfection of Wisdom of the great 
bodhisattvas is devoid of  measure.”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -2C1B . The aspects of [ true] origins  
COMMENTARY 
(1) Through the very  non-existence of entitiness in form s and so forth, the non- existence of 
the very extremes of permanence  and severance , (2) abiding in the Perfection of Wisdom , 
through the entit iness  of dharmadhatu, the definite apprehension of form s and so forth as the 
state of tathagata , (3) similarly, abiding in that, to meditate on all phenomena as neither  
adopted  nor discarded  and (4) to meditate on the four immeasurables of preliminary belief 
as being devoid of  entitiness. The aforesaid  like that  are the aspects of true origins . 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The exalted wisdom of dharma forbearance of true origins  that directly realize s the non -
existence of the very extremes of permanence and severance  and so forth through the 
very non- existence of entitiness  ultimately in forms and so forth included within 
contaminated causes, is the aspect of that [dharma forbearance of true origins ]; (2) through 
the entitiness of dharmadhatu, the definite apprehension of forms and so forth as  the 
nature of the state of tathagata , the emptiness of true existence , of an yogi who abides in 
the Perfection of Wisdom  of dharma knowledge of true origins , [is the aspect of that] 
dharma knowledge; (3)  the exalted wisdom of subsequent forbearance of true origins  that, 
similarly, abiding in that  continuity of the Perfection of Wisdom, meditates on all 
phenomena as neither adopted nor discarded ultimately and so forth; and (4)  the exalted 
wisdom of subsequent knowledge of true origins that, by meditating on the four 
immeasurables of preliminary belief as being devoid of  entitiness  ultimately, possesses 
the excellent qualities of meditation in that way, is the aspect of that [subsequent knowledge of true ori gins]. The aforesaid like that are the aspects of true origins. 
 Due to possessing the excellent qualities of the four immeasurables on the occasion of 
subsequent knowledge, the fault of falling into cessation is immediately blocked; they are 
neither medit ated on manifestly at the time of being set in meditative equipoise, nor 
meditated on by dividing meditative equipoise into earlier and later intervals. Sutra says:  
• “Koushika, because form is infinite, this Perfection of Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas 
is infinite.”  
• “Bhagavan, I will hold that which is not separate from this Perfection of Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas of ours as the state of tathagata.”  
• “Bhagavan, the mode of not accepting and not discarding this Perfection of Wisdom [of] great bodhisat tvas until omniscience.”   
• “By way of not observing all sentient beings, others, [they] meditate on love, compassion, joy, and equanimity, whereby humans and non- humans will not find a chance with 
respect to them.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
268 2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -2C1C. The aspects of [tru e] cessations 
COMMENTARY  
(1) The primordial entity of forms and so forth  is just empty of only nature, (2) to attain the 
state of tathagata, the result of the roots of virtue which equal dharmadhatu, (3) the antidotes 
being gathered in all ways within the  Perfection of Wisdom, and (4) by that itself, to 
strongly pacify outer and inner harms. The aforesaid like that are the aspects of true cessations. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The exalted wisdom of dharma forbearance of true cessations that directly realizes that  
the primordial entity of forms and so forth is just empty of only the nature  of true 
existence from the beginning, is the aspect of that [dharma forbearance of true cessations];  
(2) the dharma knowledge of true cessations that bears the benefit of attaining the state of 
tathagata, the result of the roots of virtue which equal dharmadhatu,  is the aspect of 
that; (3) the subsequent forbearance of true cessations which possesses the excellent quality 
of the antidotes, the completely pure class  being gathered in all ways within the 
Perfection of Wisdom  of subsequent forbearance of true cessations , [is the aspect of that], 
and (4) the  exalted wisdom of subsequent knowledge of true cessations, by that  subsequent 
knowledge of true cessations  itself, which possesses the excellent quality of strongly 
pacifying outer and inner harms,  is the aspect of that. The aforesaid like that are the 
aspects of true cessations. Sutra says:  
• “That is because those sons or daughters of the lineage thoroughly meditate on inner emptiness by way of not observing it.”  
• “Bhagavan, to what extent does a great bodhisattva hold...” until “...the vinaya with 
respect to the three vehicles is most amazing.”  
• “Bhagavan, to what extent when this profound Perfection of Wisdom is fully held, will the Perfections be fully held?”  
• “Koushika, likewise any son or daughter of the good lineage who holds the Perfection of 
Wisdom, wields it, and so forth will quickly pacify and obliterate those conflicting 
disputes and arguments by the power of the Perfection o f Wisdom.”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -2C1D. The aspects of [true] paths 
COMMENTARY 
(1) By only meditating on the absence  of entitiness, to pacify adherence to forms and so 
forth and to nirvana, (2) upon engaging in wisdom and skillful means, being guarded, protected and hidden by the buddhas, (3) by strongly desiring buddhahood, having abided in 
the exalted -knower of all aspects, oneself to abandon killing and so forth, to set others in 
that itself, and (4) by the desire to make generosity and so forth inexhaustible, one should 
dedicate to perfect complete enlightenment. The aforesaid like that are the aspects of true 
paths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The exalted wisdom of dharma forbearance with regard  to the path that  pacifies 
adherence to forms and so forth and to nirvana as truly existent by only meditating on 
the absence of entitiness  ultimately , is the aspect of that [dharma forbearance of true 
paths]; (2) that very exalted wisdom of dharma knowledge with regard  to the path that bears 
the benefit of being guarded, protected  and hidden by the buddhas  from the three 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
269 obstacles bearing the nature of action of the gods, evolutes of the elements and sickness  
upon that exalted wisdom of dharma knowledge  engaging in wisdom and skillful means, 
is the aspect of that, (3) the exalted w isdom of subsequent forbearance that bears the 
excellent quality that, by strongly desiring buddhahood, having oneself abided in the 
perfections, the cause of the exalted knower of all aspects, oneself to abandon killing 
and so forth, places others in that  itself, [is the aspect of that]  and (4) the subject—the  
exalted wisdom of subsequent knowledge with regard  to the path that possesses the 
excellent quality of dedicating to perfect complete enlightenment by the desire to make 
the virtues of generosity and  so forth inexhaustible —is the aspect of subsequent 
knowledge with regard  to its path, because it is an exalted wisdom of subsequent knowledge 
with regard  to the path that is the entity of a liberated path in which one has been directly 
liberated from the seeds that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing of paths . 
Alternatively, apply [the reason] exactly as explained above. The aforesaid like that are 
the aspects of true paths. Sutra says:  
• “Koushika, in that way all phenomena are totally pac ified [by] the Perfection of Wisdom 
and they are not increased.”  
• “The sons -of-the-gods of the billionfold world systems who hold this Perfection of 
Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas, wield it, and so forth are always guarded, protected, and hidden continuously.”  
• “I will abandon killing. I will also correctly urge others to abandon killing.”  
• “Koushika, in addition, when the great bodhisattvas practice the six perfections, whatever generosity that is performed will be shared with all sentient beings and fully  dedicated to 
unsurpassable complete enlightenment by way of not observing it.”

OPINION: Without explaining the meaning of the aspects by way of the passage which emphatically  characterizes the meaning of the verses here, a trifling characterization of the 
sixteen instants of the path of seeing was simply made. One shoul d similarly view the verses 
on the meaning of the partial concordance with definite discrimination which was explained

270 EXPLANATION  
OPINION: Without explicitly explaining the meaning of the aspects of the knowledges 
and for bearances of the path of seeing by way of words of the sutra that indicate those 
words and meanings of the passage  of extensive explanation which emphatically  
illustrates  and indicates  the meaning of the subject matter of the verses briefly indicating 
the instants here, by explicitly indicating the benefits that will arise during the subsequent 
occasions , a trifling illustration of the sixteen instants of the path of seeing was simply 
made. Not only that, saying “explained above and,” without explicitly exp laining the 
aspects that are to be meditated upon in those [sections], they were characterized and 
indicated by the subsequent deeds and so forth. The aspects are indicated in the context of 
the 173 aspects.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B1B -3B. Refuting it  
COMMENTARY  
OTHE RS: Because those did not show the stages of meditation, the stages of clear 
realization were not shown even slightly. Furthermore, why do you cite the meaning of the verse “By way of object of observation, aspect,” [1.8 a] and so forth?   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those  passages  “Because in thusness and buddhas,” [2.12a] and so forth, 
according to the Acarya and others  agreeing with him —it follows that the stages of clear 
realization were not shown even slightly  by them, because they did not explicitly show 
the stages of how to meditate  on the aspects that are the modes of apprehension of the clear 
realizations. Furthermore, there is the fault of not engaging the meaning of the words of 
the text: why do you cite the meaning of the verse “By way of object of observation, 
aspect,” [1.8 a] and so forth? It follows that citing it should be incorrect, because the 
passages on the partial concordance with definite discrimination in the first chapter do not 
indicate the aspects. Regarding the former [fault], the c onsequence that the title and the 
meaning of the treatise do not concord is flung.

2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.17 They are thorough pacification, bowing down to all,  
Victory over the afflictions,  
Not b eing overwhelmed by harm,  
Enlightenment, and just worship of the support.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are six actions of the path of meditation, because there are the first four results created by beings, a fifth result which is concordant with the cause, and a sixth 
environmental result.  
 Sutra says respectively:  
• “With this Perfection of Wisdom, the great bodhisattvas are thoroughly subdued in that 
way. To closely abide in order to not to have pride is a great amazement.”  
• “Bhagavan, therefore with this Perfectio n of Wisdom great bodhisattvas will thoroughly 
bow down and closely abide in order not to have pride.”  
• “Thus, daughters of lineage, in this way the weapons of attachment of those who for a 
long time practice the Perfection of Wisdom and one’s arrows of att achment will be 
destroyed.”  
• “Regarding that, someone will not be harmed by any of these: doused with poison, cast 
with spells, thrown into a fire pit, stabbed by weapons, given food or drink mixed with 
poison, and thrown into a river.”  
• “They do not observe all phenomena and attain unsurpassable perfect complete enlightenment, and will look upon all sentient beings.”  
• “With this very Perfection of Wisdom, the location becomes a support of worship of sentient beings.”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
(1) To act with self- power in the mind in all ways, (2) to bow down to all beings, the 
spiritual guide and so forth, (3) to overcome attachment and so forth, (4) just not being an object for subsequent accomplishment of harms by antagonists, (5) to a ccomplish perfect 
complete enlightenment, and (6) to take the place of support as the very thing to be 
worshipped: are the six types of actions. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
272 EXPLANATION  
(1) To act with  self-power with respect to generating higher paths in the mind in all ways, 
(2) by destroying all pride and arrogance, to bow down to all beings, the spiritual guide 
and so forth, (3) without coming under the control of afflictions, to overcome attachment 
and so forth instead , (4) just not being an object for subsequent accomplishment o f 
harms  of poisons, weapons, spells, and the like by antagonists, (5) to accomplish perfect 
complete enlightenment by one’s own strength, and (6) to take the place of support 
where a bodhisattva who has cultivated the path of meditation has stayed at as th e very 
thing to be worshipped: are the six types of actions.

COMMENTARY 
… the first path of meditation, “attention to belief”:  
 
EXPLANATION  
… the first path of meditation, “attention to belief”:  
 
Regarding the positing of these as contaminated, that which is with the conception which 
apprehends a sound -generality and a meaning -generality as suitable to be mixed is posited 
as being with contamination; these are not contaminateds that are included wi thin the 
objects of abandonment, (i) because it is that which a bodhisattva repeatedly generates with 
effort on the occasions of subsequent [attainment] of the path of meditation as a branch of 
completing the accumulation of merit, and (ii) because no arya  would generate an object 
that is to be abandoned by the path of meditation with effort.  
 If it were an object of abandonment, it must be taken as an object to be abandoned by the 
path of meditation; no one would [assert] a common locus between an object of abandonment of a Mahayana arya and a Mahayana path of meditation.  
 In [Asanga’s] Summary of Manifest Knowledge  (Abhidharmasamuccaya ), the 
contaminated is explained to be pervaded by being an object of abandonment; taking that as 
a proof is irrelevant, because that is a contaminated that is an affliction. If there were no 
conception which apprehends a sound- generality and meaning -generality as suitable to be 
mixed on the eighth ground and above, then there would not be the slightest need for hidden 
phenomena to be taken as object s.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1B -1A2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.18 Belief is to be known  As being of three types: one’s welfare,  
One’s and others’ welfare, and others’ welfare.  
Further, they are asserted to be individually  
 
2.19 Of three types: small, middling, and great. 
Moreover, they are of three types by way of the divisions:  
The small of the small and so forth. In this way, It is asserted to be of twenty -seven types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the Mahayana path of meditation of belief—is to be known as  being of three 
types , because there are the beliefs of one’s welfare, one’s and others’ welfare s, and 
others’ welfare . The subject —further, those three beliefs —are asserted to be 
individually of three types  each, because each has three : small, middling, and great. The 
subject —moreover, the small, middling, and great —are  each of three types  by way of the 
divisions:  the small of the small and so forth. In this way, belief is asserted to be of 
twenty -seven types .

274 2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1B -1A3. Com mentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The very observation of one’s, both, and others’ welfares, although 
being the support of the virtuous qualities which are seen in accordance with  how they are 
believed, from having mastered the path of meditatio n, also the entity which is not 
actualized at the beginning is of three types. Further those are each of three types by way of 
the particularities, the small and so forth. Moreover, those are individually of three types by 
way of the divisions, the small o f the small so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —also the entity of the Mahayana path of meditation of belief —is of three 
types , because there are three by means of the very observation of one’s, both, and 
others’ welfares. The subject —the Mahayana path of meditation of belief—there is a 
reason for not actualizing  it at the beginning  of the arya paths, because, although being 
the support of the Perfection of Wisdom itself, the object of belief, the virtuous qualities 
which are  seen in accordance with how the  Mothers are believed  as the source of the 
three welfares , it is a path that is attained from having mastered  the mundane  path of 
meditation  that is pure subsequent attainment. The subject —further those three beliefs —
are each of three types , each by way of  the particularities, the small and so forth. The 
subject —moreover, those, the small and so forth —are individually of three types  each,

In summary:  
COMMENTARY  
In this way, by means of three sets of nine, twenty -seven types of belief.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In this way, by means of  three sets of nine, twenty -seven types of belief. In general, an 
object of belief is a meaning that has been determined by scripture and reasoning. However, 
the objects of the pa th of meditation of belief are the textual, path and resultant Perfection of 
Wisdom.  
 Its entity is the faith of conviction and lucid faith, which are faiths attained by way of the 
conviction that the three types of Perfection of Wisdom are the source of t he three welfares, 
during the subsequent attainment that is attained from the force of meditating on the path 
Perfection of Wisdom during meditative equipoise.  
 In terms of (i) the occasion of not having abandoned the pride of I, (ii) the occasion of 
having realized that oneself and others are equal, and (iii) the occasion of engaging only in others’ welfare in all ways, it seems like the divisions have been posited on (i) the seven impure grounds, (ii) the eighth and ninth grounds, and (iii) the tenth grou nd. However, those 
are in terms of the principal ones; there is no contradiction in positing that they exist during all nine  subsequent attainments of the path of meditation.

ROOT TEXT  
2.20 On the occasions of belief  
In the Perfection s of Wisdom,  
By means of three sets of nine, they are asserted  
To be praised, eulogized, an d lauded.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —on the occasions of belief in the textual, path and resultant Perfection s of 
Wisdom  as the source of the three welfares —they are asserted to be praised, eulogized, 
and lauded, because they are taught to be praised, eulogi zed, and lauded  by means of three 
sets of nine. 
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1B -1B3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
On all occasions —the first, second , and third attentions to belief which engaged in the 
Perfection of Wisdom, with the characteristic of the qualities which are seen in accordance 
with how they are believed —respectively higher and higher by nine types individually, 
cause manifest joy and are asserted to be praised, eulogized, and lauded.  
 Thus these praises and so forth bear the characteristic of realizing facts  just as they are  in 
reality; they are not the entity of flattery.  
 
EXPLANATION  
They are asserted to be praised, eulogized, and laud ed. On which occasions? On all 
occasions —the first, second and third attentions to belief which engaged  with the aspect 
of conviction in the object of belief, the Perfection of Wisdom, with the characteristic of 
the qualities which are seen in accordance w ith how  the Mothers are believed  to be the 
source of the three welfares —by also causing manifest joy and  making praise and so forth 
by means of the later ones being superior, respectively higher and higher  compared to the 
former ones by nine types individu ally. 
 The subject —thus these praises and so forth —are not the entity of ingratiatory 
flattery  not abiding in accordance with fact, because the praises and so forth are made 
motivated by that bearing the characteristic of realizing facts just as they are i n reality  
according to how the excellent qualities exist.

2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1B -2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.21 The action of that  
Special thorough dedication is superior;  
That bearing the aspect of not observing;  The characteristic of not being erroneous;  
 2.22 Devoid; bearing the object of utilization that recollects  
The nature of the buddhas’ accumulation of merit;  
Together with means; signless;  
Rejoicing by the buddhas;  
 
2.23 Not included in the three realms;  
The other dedications are the three types  
Of small, middling, and great, 
The nature of being a source of great merit.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The action of that dedication of an arya on the path of meditation, the  special thorough 
dedication,  is superior to the other dedications —because it is an unsurpassable dedication 
that transforms all one’s and others’ roots of virtue into the branches of enlightenment. 
Sutra says, “A bodhisattva abides closely in order to subdue sentient beings and so forth; the 
things that are meritorious activities possessing rejoicing are  shared with all sentient beings 
and dedicated to unsurpas sable complete enlightenment by way of not observing them.”   
 The nature of dedication, the stopping of adherence —to (i) the substance that is to be 
dedicated, (ii) the mind that acts to dedicate, and (iii) the dedicator—as truly existent, is 
indicated by  “that bearing the aspect of not observing;  the characteristic of not being 
erroneous ; devoid. ” The stopping of adherence to one’s own virtue and the buddhas’ virtue 
as good or bad is indicated by “ bearing the object of utilization that recollects the natu re 
of the buddhas’ accumulation of merit.” The stopping of adherence to the virtue of the six perfections as good or bad is indicated by “ together with means .” The stopping of 
[adherence to] the three spheres with respect to all dedications as truly existe nt is indicated 
by “signless.” Having understood dependent -arisings as illusions conventionally, dedicating 
towards buddha ground and not dedicating towards the causes of cyclic existence are respectively indicated by the two lines “ Rejoicing by the buddha s; Not included in the 
three realms .” The other dedications are the three types  by means of types, because 
there are three types by means of small, middling, and great dedication with the nature of 
being a source of great merit. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
277 2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1B -2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) The special belief that has been  explained, (2) not observing, (3) 
not erroneous, (4) devoid, (5) recollecting  the nature of the tathagatas’ accumulation of 
merit, (6) together with skill in means, (7) sign less, (8) permitted by the buddhas, (9) not 
included in the three realms, and (10) small, (11) middling, and (12) great, giving rise to 
great merit. The attentions imputed with the aforesaid names in that way, respectively do 
not observe and observe: (1) u nsurpassable enlightenment, (2) the aggregates of ethics  and 
so forth, (3) the mind that acts to dedicate, (4) the thing possessing a self and so forth, (5) the virtues of the buddhas of the three times, (6) generosity and so forth, (7) signs, (8) all 
paths, (9) the desire realm and so forth, (10) the paths of action of the ten virtues and so 
forth, (11) stream -enterers and so forth, (12) to abide in unsurpassable enlightenment. With 
those possessing the action of causal entity  that show the path to sentien t beings who are to 
be subdued by the three vehicles, in order to make [virtues] inexhaustible for the benefit of 
all sentient beings, they make twelve types of dedication towards unsurpassable perfect 
complete enlightenment.  
 
EXPLANATION  
They make twelve  types of dedication towards unsurpassable perfect complete 
enlightenment; the purpose is in order to make [virtues] inexhaustible for the benefit of 
all sentient beings . They are dedications by means of being conjoined with special method 
and wisdom, beca use they are dedications (i) conjoined with the wisdom realizing [the 
corresponding objects] to not be observed  as truly existent, and that observe them as 
existing conventionally , [and] (ii) conjoined with those  methods possessing the action of 
causal entity that show the path to sentient beings who are to be subdued by the three 
vehicles  in accordance with their fortunes. 
 What are those twelve? Correlate the attentions imputed with the names in that way, 
[for instance]  (1) “ the dedication that has tak en as its object the  belief that has been  
explained, the special attention [superior] to the virtues of hearers and solitary realizers,”  
respectively  with being whatever dedication, [for instance] “is a dedication that does not 
observe (1) unsurpassable enlightenment as existing ultimately but observes it as existing 
conventionally.”  
 (2) The dedication that does not observe  the object to be dedicated is a dedication that 
does not observe (2) the aggregates of ethics  and so forth as existing ultimately but 
observes them [as existing conventionally].  (3) The dedication bearing the name “ not erroneous ” is a dedication that does not 
observe (3) the mind that acts to dedicate as existing ultimately but observes it [as 
existing conventionally]. Apply this [patte rn of correlation] also to the ones below.  
 The meaning is as follows. (4) The subject—the path of meditation of unsurpassable 
attention to dedication indicated here —is called “a dedication ultimately devoid  of a 
dedicator,” because it is a dedication tha t does not observe (4) the thing  that is the dedicator 
possessing a self  and so forth as existing ultimately but observes it as existing 
conventionally. Apply similarly to all of the above and the below.  
  (5) The dedication bearing the name “ recollecting the nature of the tathagatas’ 
accumulation of merit” is a dedication conjoined with the wisdom that does not observe (5) the virtues of the buddhas of the three times as existing ultimately but dedicates them 
towards enlightenment conventionally. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
278  (6) That bearing the name “ together with skill in means ” is a dedication that does not 
observe (6) generosity and so forth as existing ultimately but observes them as existing 
conventionally. (7) That bearing the name “ signless ” is a dedication that refutes ultima te (7) 
signs. (8) That bearing the name “ permitted by the buddhas ” does not observe  (8) all 
paths  as existing ultimately and dedicates them towards enlightenment conventionally. (9) 
That bearing the name “ not included in the three realms ” does not observe (9) the desire 
realm and so forth as existing ultimately and dedicates all roots of virtue towards 
enlightenment conventionally.  
 (10) The “ small dedication giving rise to great merit” is a dedication that does not 
observe the virtues of placing sentient beings of the billionfold systems in  (10) the paths of 
action of the ten virtues and so forth  as existing ultimately and transforms them into a 
branch of enlightenment conventionally. Correlate (11) “ the middling dedication giving 
rise to great merit” with  “virtues of placing sentient beings in [the states of] (11) stream -
enterers and so forth.” Correlate (12) “ the great dedication giving rise to great merit ” 
with “virtues of placing sentient beings (12) in unsurpassable enlightenment.”

2.24 By way of method and not observing,  
Rejoicing in roots of virtue  
Is described here as the meditation  
Of attention to rejoicing.  
 
EXPLANATION  
It is described  in this  context  as the contaminated meditation of attention to rejoicing. 
The lucid faith concomitant with mental happiness that cultivates joy in the roots of virtue  
of oneself and others by way of  being conjoined with the method  that realizes the roots of 
virtue as existing conventionally like illusions  and the wisdom that does not observe  them 
as existing ultimately is described as rejoicing.

COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. With a very joyful mind, through observi ng the roots of virtue (by 
way of method) as existing conventionally, by way of just not observing them as existing 
ultimately, they are the objects to be rejoiced in.   
 
EXPLANATION  
With a very joyful mind towards the  roots of virtue of oneself and others , through 
observing the roots of virtue (by way of the method  increasing virtues higher and higher ) 
as existing conventionally, by way of just being conjoined with the wisdom realizing that they are not observed as existing ultimately, they are the objects  to be rejoiced in. 
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B1C. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding that, the summary is this: attention to belief strongly composes merit like gold nuggets from a mine; attention to dedication makes the limbs of perfect complete 
enlightenment like ornaments by a goldsmith; attention to rejoicing attains the equality of 
the produced merit of oneself and others. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding that, the summary  of the functions of the three is this: attention to belief 
strongly composes  extensive merit newly like finding gold nuggets from a mine . The 
subject —attention to dedication —accomplishes special action, because it makes  virtues 
into the limbs of perfect complete enlightenment like  the transforming of the gold 
nuggets into ornaments by a goldsmith. The subject —attention to rejoicing  –with the 
previous predicate [accomplishes special action], because it attains the equality of the produced merit of oneself and others  as if oneself created the virtues that have been 
establishe d in others’ continua.

2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.25 The entit iness  of that is just supreme,  
Not strongly composing with respect to all,  
Bestowing without observing phenomena , 
The great aim itself.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The path of meditation of achieving is of five types, because there are those related to entity, 
distinguished result, functio n, temporal excellent qualities, and final excellent qualities.   
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) To see forms and so forth non- erroneously is the entit iness . (2) 
That others will not attain buddhahood is just su preme. (3) The application realizing the 
absence  of production, an attribute of all phenomena, does not strongly compose any. (4) To 
generate in the continua of yogis, phenomena are just not observed by  the paths which 
possess such entity itself and so forth , is to bestow. (5) Because of enacting the great aim, 
buddhahood, the great aim itself.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The subject —the exalted wisdom of meditative equipoise of the second ground —is the 
entitiness  of the path of meditation of achieving, because, as it says in sutra, “The 
Perfection of Wisdom is very pure, therefore it illuminates,” it is a subsequent clear realization that sees the mode of abidance of forms and so forth non- erroneously. (2) It is 
just the supreme  method , because, while buddhahood is  not attained by others , such as 
generosity that is not conjoined with it, it is attained through the force of being conjoined 
with it.  
(3) The application realizing the absence  of production, an attribute of all phenomena, 
does not strongly compose a ny as truly existent. Sutra says: “Furthermore, because forms 
and so forth until  omniscience are not to be strongly achieved, the Perfection of Wisdom is 
to be strongly achieved.”  (4) A path of meditation of achieving possesses special temporal result s, becau se it bestows  
special temporal result s in the continua of yogis. That follows, because it generates [special 
temporal result s] in the continua of yogis by means of being conjoined with the wisdom 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
281 realizing that phenomena are just not observed by the paths which possess attributes of 
such entity itself, result, action, and so forth.  
(5) It enacts the final result, the great aim itself, because it  enacts the great aim, 
buddhahood. Sutra says: “By what categorization is this Perfection of Wisdom great? 
Regardi ng the Perfection of Wisdom, forms are not great and are not small.”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2. The path of meditation of purity  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2A. Attribute of cause  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2B. Objects

characteristics of the very pure will be explained, by means of dispelling the doubt 
wondering what the causes of the generation and the non- generation of this are, which 
will be attained by fully holding to some  conducive conditions and forsaking some  
adverse conditions.

ROOT TEXT  
2.26abc  Devotion to the buddhas, generosity and so forth,   
And any skill in means  
Are the causes of belief in this. 
 
EXPLANATIO N 
These three are the causes of belief in this  path of meditation of purity: the outer condition 
of devotion to the buddhas, the inner condition s of generosity and so forth, and any skill 
in means  of union of calm abiding and special insight . 
 
Sutra says:  
• “Great bodhisattva, by devoting oneself to the countless buddhas of the world systems of 
the ten directions, rays of light will come forth and be generated.”  
• “When the first mind [of enlightenment] is generated, one practices the perfection of generosity.”   
• “Any great bodhisattva, having generated the first mind [of enlightenment] and held it closely, strives by way of not observing this profound mother.”

2.26d The causes of being bereft of qualities  
 
2.27 Are being oppressed by maras,  
Not believ ing in the profound Dharma,  
Adhering to the aggregates and so forth, and  
Being s eized by negative companions.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are four causes of being bereft of qualities  such as the path of meditation of 
achieving : being oppressed by maras, disbelief in the profound Dharma, adhering to 
the aggregates and so forth, and  being  seized by negative companions . 
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2A3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY  
“(1) To please the buddhas, (2) to complete the perfections of generosity and so forth, and 
(3) skill  in calm abiding ” are the causes for generation. “(1) Being harmed by maras, (2) 
not believing in the profound Dharma, (3) to hold to things, and (4) to keep company with non-virtuous companions” are the causes for non -generation.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The conducive conditions are the causes for  generating  a path of meditation of achieving: 
“(1) for the sake of fully completing the qualities of a buddha, to please the buddhas , (2) 
to complete the perfections of generosity and so forth, and (3) skill in calm abiding that 
has attained special meditative stabilization.”  
 There are four causes for not generating  a path of meditation, because there are the 
four:  (1) due to not having collected virtue in the past, being  harmed by maras, and so 
forth.

2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2B2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.28 The results are pure. Forms and so forth  
Are just pure. Because of what?  
Since those two are not different and  
They are not delineated, they are described as purity.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
283  
EXPLANATION  
If the results of the modes of a trainee -in-virtue  are pure  in being free from their 
corresponding stains, then  their objects, forms and so forth are  also just pure  in being free 
from those very stains . Because of what? Since those two purities (that are devoid of the 
one object to be free from) are not different by means of different entities of specific 
characteristics  and they are not delineated  by means of different types , therefore they are 
described as being one type of purity.  
 Sutra says: “Subhuti, that which is the purity of forms is the purity of results.”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2B3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. That which is a result of the modes of a trainee -in-virtue of arya 
persons, that which is the purity by way of separation from all discordant classes, is the 
purity of forms and so  forth, by way of the purities of the results and forms and so forth 
being distinguished  by having separated from adherence to forms and so forth as self. 
Because of what? The purities of those are not different and are not delineated, their specific 
and general characteristics are not different. Because of that, purity is described in that way.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for describing  the results and forms and so forth in that way  as being one 
type of purity  in being free of the one object to be free from, because their purities of being 
devoid of the one object to be free from  are not different by way of the entitiness of their 
specific and general characteristics not being different. Because of what reason ? 
Because the purities  of those  results and f orms and so forth are not different by way of 
entitiness and are not delineated as different types by valid cognition. That follows, 
because —by way of the purities  of the results , the liberated paths, and forms and so 
forth , their objects, being distinguis hed by having separated from the objects of 
abandonment of the corresponding liberated paths, the seeds of adherence and so forth to 
forms and so forth as a self of persons —they are pure in being devoid of the one object to 
be free from.  
 Hence, “That which is the purity of results itself is the purity of forms and so forth” is 
explained in sutra, because that which is a liberated path, a result of the modes of a 
trainee -in-virtue of arya persons  of the three vehicles  is the purity in being  free from also 
those very stains of their objects, forms and so forth, by way of separation from all its 
corresponding discordant classes, whereby it is explained  in that way.  
 The meaning is as follows. It is not indicated that when a yogi himself exhaus ts all stains, 
all his objects are devoid of all stains. If it were like that, there would be the consequence that when a being attains buddhahood, later ones would [attain buddhahood] without relying 
on the effort of meditating on the path.  
 Then what does it mean? When that very person becomes devoid of stains, his objects, 
forms and so forth also become devoid of those very objects of abandonment in his 
continuum. Therefore, when that very yogi observes forms and so forth, an appearance that 
is dissimil ar to the previous dawns, like  in the case of dependent- arisings appearing as 
illusions for a person who has realized dependent -arisings to be empty of true existence.

2.29 Because of the afflictive, the knowledge, and having diminished  
Those of the three paths, the purities of the learners,  
The rhinoceroses, and the v ictors ’ children.  
The buddhas’ is exceptional in all ways.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Hearers, solitary realizers, and bodhisattvas on the path of meditation have a purity that is 
the purity in being free126 of some stains, because hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers 
are devoid respectively of the afflictive obscurations and  the knowledge  obscurations that 
adhere to forms and so forth as external objects , and the latter [bodhisattvas on the path of 
meditation] have abandoned and diminished  some seeds that are obscurations of the three 
paths.  Those purities are called “ the purities of the learn ers, the rhinoceroses, and the 
victors’ children.”  
 The subject —the  purity of complete buddhas —is said to be “ exceptional in all ways,” 
because it is a final purity. Sutra says, respectively:  
• “These —the purity of attachment, hatred, and ignorance, and the purity of forms and so 
forth —are not dualistic and are not to be taken as dualistic.”  
• “By the purity of ignorance, the purity of compositional factors.”  
• “By the purity of the perfection of generosity, the purity of the perfection of ethics .”  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B-2B2B -2C3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because of having abandoned: (1) t he afflictions of attachment and 
so forth, (2) that and one part of the knowledge obscurations, the conceptualizations of 
apprehended objects, and (3) the three  obscurations of the paths of the three vehicles, they 
are respectively the purities of (1) hearers, (2) solitary realizers, and (3) bodhisattvas.  
 Because of having abandoned the afflictive and knowledge obscurations as well as their 
imprints in all ways, the exceptional purity arisen from dharmadhatu is asserted to be that of 
unsurpassable buddhas.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The abandonments in the continua of hearer and solitary realizer foe -destroyers are called 
“purities of (1) hearers and (2) solitary realizers ,” because they are separations in their 
continua in which  (1) the afflictive  obscurations  of attachment and so forth, and (2) that 
                                              
126 Ci rigs pa  emended to read ci rigs pas.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
285 afflictive obscurations  and one part of the knowledge obscurations, the 
conceptualizations of apprehended objects  in which forms  and so forth are adhered to as 
external objects , have been abandoned respectively. The abandonment in the continua of 
(3) arya bodhisattvas on the path of meditation is the purity of bodhisattvas , because it is a 
separation in their continua in which some  of (3) the three obscurations —the afflictive 
obscurations and the two knowledge obscurations —of the paths of the three vehicles, have 
been abandoned.  The subject —the exceptional purity  in being free from all adventitious 
stains, that very pure nature  arisen from dharmadhatu —is asserted to be the purity  of 
unsurpassable buddhas, because it is a separation in which the afflictive and knowledge 
obscurations as well as their imprints have been abandoned in all ways.

QUALM: Incidental to mentioning purity on the occasion of the knower of paths, what is 
the explanation of the purity of the buddhas and hearers and so forth respectively as being 
exceptional and the other like?  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: Incidental to mentioning purity on the occasion of the knower of paths, what 
is the explanation of the purity of the buddhas and hearers and so forth respectively as 
being exceptiona l and the other like ? On the occasion of explaining the purity of the 
bodhisattvas’ path of meditation, it is not time for the purities of the others.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D1B. Reply 
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D1B -1. Root text  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D1B -2. Commentary

ROOT TEXT  
2.30 On the nine levels, the antidotes to the stains,  
The great of the great and so forth,  
The paths that are the small of the small and so forth,  
Are pure.  
 
EXPLANATION  
By having made the divi sions of purity, which is the result, the divisions of a bodhisattva’s 
path of meditation, which are the causes that act to purify, are also established. Hence there 
is no fault that it is not time [to explain the purities of the others].  
 How are they [th e divisions of a bodhisattva’s path of meditation] established? The purity 
of buddhas is exceptional, whereas the purities of hearers and solitary realizers are not 
exceptional. The antidotes to the stains, that act to destroy  [stains] (from  the great of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
286 great, [until] the small of the small  and so forth conceptualizations of apprehendeds and 
apprehenders, the objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation that exist  on the nine 
levels  of the three realms), the paths that are the small of the small Mahayana path of 
meditation and so forth, are paths that act to purify  both obscurations . However, the paths 
of meditation127 of hearers and solitary realizers are not able to directly extinguish all stains.  
 Here [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary explains the two: (i) our system which, having 
taken the nine levels to be the nine levels of the three realms, teaches them to be the mind  
support of paths, and (ii) others’ system , which teach es (a) the explanation that the mind  
support are the nine levels as referri ng to the six concentrations and the first three formless 
absorptions, and (b) the explanation here of the nine, the desire realm and so forth, as 
referring to the path Perfection of Wisdom not abiding ultimately in the three realms.  
 It is said that the explanation that [the path  of meditation ] are not supported on minds of 
the desire realm and of the Peak of Existence is in terms of hearers who are not skilled in 
method. According to what is overtly stated in this commentary, it is clear that the nine 
levels have been taken as supports of the objects of abandonment.  
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D1B -2. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
Because, as entities of antidotes to the nine types of discordant class, the great of the great 
and so forth on the nine levels —the desire re alm, the concentrations and the formless 
absorptions —respectively the nine types of path, the small of the small and so forth, are the 
causes that completely purify all types and the other, they are said to be exceptionally pure 
and the other. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The purity of the complete buddhas is  said to be exceptionally pure, and the other , the 
purities of hearers and solitary realizers , not exceptionally pure, because, as entities of 
antidotes to the nine types of discordant class,  the great of the great obj ects to be 
abandoned by the path of meditation  and so forth, which exist in dependence on the nine 
levels —the desire realm, the four concentrations and the four formless absorptions —
respectively the nine types of Mahayana path  of meditation , the small of the small and so 
forth, are the antidotes that purify  all types of obscurations, and the other, the paths of 
meditation of hearer and solitary realizers aryas, are the causes that completely purify the 
trifling objects of abandonment.

COMMENTARY 
QUESTION: How is it exceptional?  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUESTION: How is the purity of the complete buddhas  exceptional?  
                                              
127 Sgom lam gyi  emended to read sgom lam gyis.
