287 2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
2.31 By having dispelled dispute regarding those,  
The path —by way of comprehenders and the objects to be comprehend ed  
Being just equal —is asserted to be  
The very antidote of the three realms.  
 
EXPLANATION  
DISPUTANT: In your system, it follows that impure other- powered phenomena are truly 
existent, because the objects to be damaged are established as the nine division s of objects 
of abandonment; it follows that real other- powered phenomena also are truly existent, 
because those which act to damage are established as the nine divisions of antidotes.  
 
RESPONSE: That is a dispute that holds the two truths as contradictory . An argument about 
the sequence of generation of the antidotes and the sequence of cessation of the objects of 
abandonment is an argument that is based upon the conventional.  
 This follows from the previous root text, “The buddhas’ is exceptional in all ways.” 
[2.29d]. The subject —a bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom of meditative equipoise of the path  
of meditation —is asserted to be the very antidote that purifies all the obscurations of the 
three realms  without exception, because, by means of having dispelled  dispute regarding 
those  sequences of the antidotes and the objects of abandonment, it is established to be the 
antidote to those objects of abandonment. That follows, because it directly destroys those 
objects of abandonment by way of realizing with direc t cognition that consciousnesses that 
are comprehenders and the objects to be comprehended are  just equal  in not existing 
ultimately.   
2B2C -1B2B -3B2B -2B2B -2D2C. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
By means of dispelling the dispute, “Regarding that, the antidotes that are the great of the great and so forth, the discordant classes that are the small of the small and so forth are 
reasonable,” through the analogy of a washerman’s prolonged effort to clean a subtle stain 
adhering to a cloth, because that exceptional path o f meditation itself which has been 
shown, consciousnesses and the objects to be known bearing the aspects of the three realms are just equal through just not observing them, is the very entity  of the antidotes to all, the 
purity of the buddhas is presented  as being exceptional.  
 
EXPLANATION  
DISPUTANT: The sequence of generation of the antidotes and the sequence of cessation of the objects of abandonment which have been explained are incorrect. Regarding that, in 
the way  a strong slayer is needed for a strong enemy, when the objects to be abandoned by 
the path of meditation are gradually ceased at first from the great of the great, it is correct 
for the antidotes  that are the great of the great path of meditation, and so forth  to be 
generated sequentially. In the way  a strong slayer is not needed for a weak enemy, in order 
to cease  the discordant classes that are the small of the small and so forth, the antidote 
that is the small of the small  is reasonable. Hence when ceas ing the small of the small 
object of abandonment of the path of meditation at the end, it is reasonable to generate the 
small of the small path of meditation at the end.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 2: The Knower of Paths  
 
288  (Alternatively, apply to the dispute: “It is correct to generate the antidotes from t he 
greater sequence, and it is reasonable to cease the discordant classes from the smaller 
sequence.”)  
 RESPONSE: That is an argument that is based upon the conventional. Like  it has been said 
in the commentary by Arya [Vimuktisena], arguing that if the objects of abandonment and 
the antidotes do not exist ultimately, then their sequence is incorrect is an argument about 
the two truths being contradictory.  
 The purity of the complete buddhas is presented as being exceptional , because that 
exceptio nal path of meditation  of the Mahayana aryas itself which has been shown is the 
very entity of the antidotes to all  obscurations. Even though the objects of abandonment 
and the antidotes do not exist ultimately, the sequences of the objects of abandonment and 
the antidotes are correct, because the path of meditation that has just been explained 
destroys the objects of abandonment by means of realizing with direct cognition that 
consciousnesses and the objects to be known bearing the aspects of the three rea lms 
are just equal in being empty of true existence through just not observing them as 
existing ultimately. Sutra says:  
• “Bhagavan, purity is not generated in the desire, form, and formless realms.”  
• “Bhagavan, complete purity is without seeking.”  
 
It fol lows that there is no fault that the sequence of cessation of the objects of abandonment 
and the sequence of generation of the antidotes which have been explained are incorrect, 
because the sequences of the objects of abandonment and the antidotes are esta blished by 
means of dispelling the dispute  which has been explained through the analogy of a 
washerman’s not needing effort for a long time to clean a coarse stain adhering to a cloth, 
but [needing] prolonged  effort to clean a subtle stain adhering to a cl oth.   
2B2C -1B3. Summarizing the chapter  
COMMENTARY 
The commentary on the second chapter from the Treatise of Quintessential Instructions 
of the Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization . 
 
EXPLANATION  
The explanation of the commentary on the sec ond chapter  from  the Ornament of the 
Essence,  which is an explanation of the commentary on the Treatise of Quintessential 
Instructions of the Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization . 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
289  
Chapter 3: The Knower of Bases  
2B2C -1C. Explaining the branch that purifies the knower of paths, the knower of bases  
2B2C -1C1. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
2B2C -1C2. Explaining the  text of the chapter  
2B2C -1C3. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -1C1. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
COMMENTARY  
Without fully knowing all bases, there is no full knowing of the paths well, therefore the 
knower of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for explaining the knower of all  after having explained the knower of 
paths, because, without fully knowing all bases , impermanence and so forth , there is no 
full knowing of the paths of the three vehicles well, therefore  it will be explained as a 
branch of the knower of paths.  
 
QUESTION: What is the knower of bases that is the main subject to be explained in this 
third chapter?  
• Is it the knower of bases bearing the aspects of the sixteen, impermanence and so forth, 
an object to be cultivated  also by  bodhisattvas?  
• Or, is it the knower of bases of the discordant class, since it has been explained that it is indicated for the sake of making known the corrupted causes of omniscience [propounded 
by] Acarya  Buddhajñana and so forth?  
• Or is it what has bee n imputed with the convention “antidotal knower of bases,” created 
by some for the realization that bases are devoid of nature?  
 
RESPONSE: That it is not the last [of the three cases] will be explained later on [under 
2B2C -1C2A -1C3B]. Although the knower of bases of the discordant class is also indicated 
for the sake of making known a corrupted knower of paths, the main subject to be explained 
is the first one itself. For, with respect to the qualm, “When a bodhisattva’s path of seeing 
and path of meditati on were previously indicated as paths that purify the two extremes, what 
creates the dissimilar imprints that stop the two extremes?”, “Through knowing, not abiding in cyclic existence; through compassion, not abiding in solitary peace; [0 .11ab]” the exalt ed 
wisdom realizing impermanence and so forth itself has been taught to be that which stops 
the extreme of cyclic existence. This very [exalted wisdom] is going to be explained here in 
the extensive explanation. [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says: 
 
QUALM : Since the paths of hearers and so forth have been explained on the 
occasion of a knower of paths, has the knower of all not been explained? Why is the knower of all especially explained again separately?  
RESPONSE: That is to be described. With respect t o that, it has been described 
that “‘Regarding the mode of a knower of paths, by means of not observing the 
aspects of the four noble truths, these hearers’ paths are to be known. [2.2]’ By 
way of the stages of meditating and so forth without observing [th e aspects], the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
290 paths of hearers and so forth are to be meditated on by bodhisattvas.” Hence, it is 
held that, in order to fully know all the bases (that are implied by the aspects, 
impermanence and so forth) without exception, the knower of all is especia lly 
explained separately. 
 
Thus, when that very bodhisattva’s path bearing the aspects of impermanence and so forth is 
posited as a knower of paths, it is to be understood as being conjoined with the three 
attributes. Further, the generated mind must be preceded by great compassion, which is 
contingent upon training well in the attitude of a being of middling capacity —
impermanence, suffering, and so forth.  
 Therefore, since it has been taught that a knower of bases is necessary prior to a knower 
of paths, the chapter on Special Deeds and so forth have been composed as a branch that 
acts to purify the knower of paths. Also, that very meaning implied and indicated by the 
sutra passage, “[Regarding this, does a bodhisattva mahasattva] pay attention to the 
impe rmanence of forms…” [under 2B2C -1B2B -1A3A] which was cited when explaining 
the nature of a knower of hearers’ paths previously, is emphasized and explained here.

ROOT TEXT  
3.1 Not abiding in the extremes of this side and that side,  
Not between these, 
Because it knows the times are just equal,  The Perfection of Wisdom is asserted. 
 
EXPLANATIO N 
These words of the root text have been explained by the commentary to [express] the near and distant paths. It has been explained that the reason itself is proven by the lines below.   
 The subject —the Perfection of Wisdom  that know s bases—is asserted  to be near to the 
continua of buddhas and bodhisattvas because it is an exalted wisdom that directly knows 
the phenomena of the three  times  are just equal  in being devoid of  the nature of true 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
291 existence. This implicitly indicates that it is distant from the hearers and solitary realizers, 
since they lack that reason.  
 It does not abide in the extreme of cyclic existence on this side, and the extreme of  
solitary peace on  that side, because it acts to stop those two extremes. It does not abide 
ultimately betwe en these  extremes, because it realizes emptiness. Although conventionally 
it abides in a middle that is neither of the extremes of cyclic existence and of solitary peace, 
this refutes that [the middle] between these exists ultimately. 
 Sutra says, “Bhagava n, the Perfection of Wisdom of the great bodhisattvas is not of this 
side, is not of that side, and also does not abide between them.”  
2B2C -1C2A -1A2. Commentary  
2B2C -1C2A -1A2A. The path that stops the extremes of existence and peace which is explicitly indicated  
2B2C -1C2A -1A2B. Indicating being separated from those paths of the hearers and solitary realizers which are 
implicitly indicated  
2B2C -1C2A -1A2A. The path that stops the extremes of existence and peace which is explicitly indicated  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Due to realizing that the phenomena of the three times are just equal in the aspect of being devoid of  production, that which is the Perfection of Wisdom is 
asserted to be near to the buddhas and bodhisattvas, since it does not ab ide in the extreme of 
samsara on this side through wisdom, and the extreme of nirvana  on that side through 
compassion —respectively the characteristics of permanence and severance —nor between 
them, does not abide in cyclic existence or solitary peace.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that which is the Perfection of Wisdom  that know s bases —is asserted to be 
near to the  continua of buddhas and bodhisattvas, due to directly realizing that the 
phenomena of the three times are just equal in the aspect of being devoid  of truly 
existent production . 
 The subject —the wisdom  in the continuum of a bodhisattva directly realizing 
impermanence and so forth (when dissimilar imprints are individually deposited on this path 
Perfection of Wisdom by the presence of many, the wisdom  realizing impermanence and so 
forth, great compassion, realization of emptiness, and so forth)—through  it, the extreme of  
permanence  (in which one transmigrates for a long time through the force of karma and 
afflictions included within the extreme of sams ara on this side ) is stopped, because it 
destroys the root of samsara.  
 The subject —the great compassion in the continuum of a bodhisattva —through  it, the 
extreme with the characteristic of severance  in which one attains the extreme of nirvana 
on that side  in dependence on having severed rebirths in cyclic existence is stopped, 
because, by taking rebirth again and again in cyclic existence through its force, it is a path 
that acts to achieve non -abiding nirvana.  
 Therefore, the subject —the Perfection of Wis dom that know s bases —does not abide in 
cyclic existence or solitary peace and does not abide  ultimately between them, since it 
stops the extreme s of cyclic existence and solitary peace relative to  the conventional, and, 
through realizing that cyclic existe nce and solitary peace are not truly existent, it does not 
abide ultimately between them.  
 Regarding the explanation by someone in this context —that what stops the extreme of 
cyclic existence relative to the conventional is the wisdom realizing emptiness —clearly is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
292 an expression of not having realized in detail the dissimilar modes of engagement of minds 
of the stages of the path, because (i) both Acarya [Haribhadra] and Arya [Vimuktisena] say 
that the wisdom sees the faults of cyclic existence, and (ii) th e faults of cyclic existence are 
not found by a reasoning  consciousness that comprehends the ultimate.   
  “This side” is [posited] relative to ordinary beings; it means that they are on this side, not 
on the other side which is the severance of cyclic existence. “The extreme of permanence” 
has the significance of engaging for a long time due to transmigrating through the force of 
karma and afflictions; it is not an extreme of a permanent thing. The extreme of severance is 
a nirvana that is attained not only by severing rebirth through the force of karma and 
afflictions, but also by severing rebirth through the force of compassion and prayers. The 
Great Lama Lotsawa said:  
 
The main knowers of bases are [those] of hearers and solitary realizers, but the 
main perfections of wisdom are [those] of bodhisattvas. Therefore, the knower of 
bases that is a reflection of [that in] the continu a of bodhisattvas is the main 
subject to be explained here. It is generated on the eighth ground, and is 
explained here to be  a branch of the knower of paths knowing all three paths. 
This has been taken in terms of attaining a knower of the three paths naturally without effort. The explanation that it knows impermanence and so forth in the 
previous context of the path of seeing indicates the objects of a knower of paths. 
 
Through this passage he wished to indicate the attribute of compassion. In that manner it is correct in uncommon terms, but it is difficult for the time of generating a knower of bases 
and so forth to be correct . 
2B2C -1C2A -1A2B. Indicating being separated from those paths of the hearers and solitary realizers which are 
implicitly shown  
COMMENTARY 
Since they are  indicated  as the reverse of the context of the  knower of all, because the 
knowing of the three times a s just equal does not exist in hearers and so forth, by way of 
being distant  from the perfect Perfection of Wisdom, because the Perfection of Wisdom 
which is merely realized by themselves is separated from compassionate kindness  and 
wisdom, by just observing the property and the lack of the property, they are to be known as 
abiding in samsara and nirvana.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is not the fault of not having presented the knower s of bases of hearers and solitary 
realizers in this context, since, as the reverse  of the context of the  knower of all  in the 
continuum of a bodhisattva explicitly indicated  in this context, they are  implicitly 
indicated . The knower s of bases of hearers and solitary realizers are presented on some 
occasion below, whereby [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary says:  
 
Some are shown by means of reverse, some by means of accompaniment.  
 
The subject —hearer  aryas, solitary realizers and so forth—abide by way of being distant 
from the perfect Perfection of Wisdom, because, due to adhering to the ex tremes of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
293 cyclic existence and solitary peace as truly existent, the knowing of the three times as just 
equal does not exist in them.  
 The subject —hearers who , for the time being , abide in a reflection of the Perfection of 
Wisdom  that know s bases,  which is merely realized in a trifling manner by hearers and 
solitary realizers themselves —abide in  the extreme of samsara, because they apprehend 
the mere property  of rebirth in cyclic existence as an object to be discarded categorically. 
That follows, be cause —since they desire to stop not only rebirth through the force of karma 
and afflictions, but also rebirth through the force of compassion and prayers —they are 
separated from compassionate kindness , a cause of taking rebirth in samsara. They are to 
be k nown as not abiding in the extreme of nirvana by just observing the lack of the 
property of rebirth in cyclic existence that is attained from severing mere rebirth in cyclic 
existence as an object to be adopted. That follows because, although they desire t o attain the 
severance of rebirth in cyclic existence and they have stopped rebirth through the force of 
karma and afflictions, they are separated from the  wisdom  that knows the method for 
accomplishing nirvana in dependence on taking rebirth in cyclic exi stence. (i) That they lack 
the wisdom realizing emptiness and (ii) correlation by reversing the above  two reasons —is 
not the meaning of this passage occurs in the teachings of the Foremost Being [Lama 
Tsongkhapa].  
2B2C -1C2A -1B. Proving that very reason  
2B2C -1C2A -1B1. Dispute 
2B2C -1C2A -1B2. Reply 
2B2C -1C2A -1B1. Dispute 
COMMENTARY 
DISPUTANT: Because it occurs in the system that “Whatever arises dependently, that itself 
is accepted as empty,” the knower of the three times as just equal only realizes bases. 
Furthermore, does it not exist in all? Why do hearers, bodhisattvas and so forth become 
distant and otherwise with respect to  the perfect Perfection of Wisdom?  
 
EXPLANATION  
DISPUTANT: Why  do hearers, bodhisattvas and so forth become distant and 
otherwise with respect to  the perfect Perfection of Wisdom by the difference of knowing 
or not knowing the three times as just equal ? It follows that it is incorrect, because a knower 
of the three times as just equal exists also in hearers and solitary realizers. That follows, 
because the knower of the three times as just equal only realizes the mode of abidance of 
bases ; furthermore, does  the realization of the mode of abidance of bases not exist in all  
aryas ? It does. It follows that the realization of the mode of abidance of bases exists in 
hearer and solitary realizer aryas, (i) because  it occurs in the scripture of the great 
trailblazer Protector Nagarjuna  that emptiness is [the meaning] of dependent -arising, 
“Whatever arises dependently, that itself is accepted as empty,”  in the Praise to the 
Supramundane, and (ii) because the realization  of the mode of abidance of dependent -
arising exists also in hearers and solitary realizers.  
  This is a dispute created by those who accept that the realizations of the selflessnesses of 
persons and of phenomena exist in hearer and solitary realizer aryas , in accordance with the 
indication by that citation that emptiness is the meaning of the term “dependent -arising.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
294  By way of the assertions of the Acarya, a reply is given by means of [saying that], 
although the citation indicates that dependent -arisings  are pervaded by being empty of true 
existence, that realization does not exist in hearers and solitary realizers.

2B2C -1C2A -1B2B. Commentary  
2B2C -1C2A -1B2C. Summary  
2B2C -1C2A -1B2A. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
3.2 That, by means of observing signs  
With incorrect means, is distant.  
That, with skill in means,  
Is explained as correctly near. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —hearer and solitary realizer aryas —that  Perfection of Wisdom that know s 
bases is distant from them, becau se they , by means of observing signs  of true existence 
with respect to bases , are conjoined with incorrect skill in the  means for generating the 
resultant Mother. That follows, because they are separated from a spiritual guide who 
teaches the mode of abida nce, and they also practice a reflection of the Perfection of 
Wisdom.    Sutra says: “Bhagavan, if one were to know by way of observing the perfection of 
wisdom of sons or daughters of the lineage of the bodhisattva vehicle who are not skilled in means, on e would discard this perfection of wisdom and become distant from it.”  
 The subject —bodhisattvas —there is a reason for explaining that Perfection of Wisdom 
as correctly near  to them, because they are conjoined with skill in the  means  for 
generating the res ultant Mother.  
 Sutra says: “Furthermore, Bhagavan, explaining well this perfection of wisdom, u ntil 
extracting it well is amazing.”  
2B2C -1C2A -1B2B. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. With regard  to the things emanated by a magician appearing, due to 
adherence to them as things by those who do not know the self- entity of those, they do not 
appear as being devoid of  the very entity. Likewise, because they are separated from skill in 
means, a spiritu al guide and so forth, since they realize things as possessing signs, the 
hearers and so forth who do not know the entity of things do not have the exalted wisdom of 
equality. Because of that, the Mother of the victors is distant from them.  
 In bodhisattvas bearing the non -erroneous instructions of a spiritual guide whom they 
have correctly pleased for a long time, who have eliminated the mistaken signs of adherence 
to things, due to skill in the means of generating a knower of hearing and so forth that is 
supported on the two truths, that very full knowing of phenomena, forms and so forth, is the 
full knowing of the three times as just equal. Because of that, they are correctly close to this Mother. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
295 EXPLANATION  
The Mother of the victors is di stant from those  hearer and solitary realizer aryas, because 
the hearers and so forth who do not know that the entity of things  is empty of true 
existence and free from elaborations do not have the exalted wisdom of equality of the 
three times. That follow s, since they realize things as possessing  the signs  of true 
existence. That follows, because, simply realizing the mere selflessness of persons, they 
are separated from skill in means, a spiritual guide  who teaches subtle selflessness and 
so forth . 
 It is not that there is no analogy for adherence in that manner, because, with regard to 
the things  that are horses and elephants emanated by a magician appearing, due to 
adherence to them as things  that are horses and elephants  by those who do not know the 
self-entity of those  that are illusions, they are not realized and do not appear as being 
devoid of  the very entity  of horses and elephants. Likewise,  they adhere to things as being 
truly existent. 
 The meaning of the analogy as explained above should be real ized by relating it to 
falsities that are renowned in the world, and further, it should be realized from having 
pleased the spiritual guide for a long time, because one adheres to signs with respect to things by not being conjoined with it, and it is taugh t that one will be distant from the 
Perfection of Wisdom due to just that.  The subject —those bodhisattva  aryas —are correctly close to this Mother , because 
they know that the three times are just equal. That follows, because they know that phenomena, forms  and so forth, are empty of true existence and free from elaborations. It 
follows that there is pervasion, because  that very full knowing of phenomena, forms and 
so forth,  as empty of true existence and free from elaborations is the full knowing of the 
three times as just equal. The former sign follows, because they have eliminated 
mistaken signs of adherence to  things  as truly existent, due to skill in the means of 
generating a knower of hearing and so forth that is supported on  skill in the divisions of 
the two truths . That follows, because they bear the non- erroneous instructions of a 
spiritual guide skilled in the divisions of the two truths whom they have correctly pleased 
for a long time.  
2B2C -1C2A -1B2C. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
Hence, they are distant due to incorrect means; they are not distant due to means. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The difference of the Perfection of Wisdom being near to or distant from the hearers and 
solitary realizers and the bodhisattvas is correct, because hearers and solitary realizers  are 
distant due to  incorrect skill in the means of meditating on the Mother; bodhisattvas are 
not distant due to skill in means.  
 The meaning of this is to be known in this way: if the two —those who are temporally 
definite in the hearers’ lineage and those definite in the bodhisattvas’ lineage —were to enter 
at the same time their respective paths, and accomplish their respective enlightenments with effort, know that the former would need a longer time to attain unsurpassable 
enlightenment. 
 
OPINION: A hearer’s enlightenment is attained in three lifetimes if it is accomplished with 
effort. Then, by entering from the seventh Mahayana ground and so forth, the time to attain 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
296 unsurpassable enlightenment will be very much shorter than that of the other [definite in th e 
bodhisattvas’ lineage].  
RESPONSE: In that case, if one wishes to quickly attain buddhahood, it would be 
appropriate to initially enter into a hearer’s path. Entry from a high path has already been 
refuted.128   
 
Therefore, although the attainment of a hearers’ enlightenment is quick —through the force 
of (i) not having familiarized with principally striving for others’ welfare and (ii) having 
single -pointedly familiarized with the mind that observes the faults of cyclic existence in the 
past—they attain the state of a foe -destroyer due to an intense awareness that contemplates 
the benefits of the happiness of pacification, after which they enter absorption in the sphere 
of peace for eons. Although they are urged by the light rays of buddhas and are awakened, 
they enter absorption in the sphere of peace again and again. And also, after having 
generated the mind towards supreme enlightenment, even though they know that they 
should not enter into the meditative equipoise of the sphere of peace for a long time, 
through the force of previous familiarity, they enter that state again and again. For this very 
reason, they are very much slower compared to those definite in the bodhisattvas’ lineage. There is a great difference of being near to  or distant from  the result  since they have to start 
collecting the infinite accumulation of merit from the beginning. Know that [the  path  of 
those who are definite in the Mahayana lineage ] is a quick path relative to that of those 
definite in the hearers’ lineage.

ROOT TEXT  
3.3 The emptiness of the aggregates, forms and so forth;  
Phenomena belonging to the three times;  
The harmonies with enlightenment, gener osity and so forth — 
The discrimination of the practice regarding them is a discordant class.  
 
EXPLANATION  
A path that is bound by  the discrimination of the practice —regarding  (i-ii) the objects of 
knowledge which are the bases: the emptiness in being devoid of  a self of persons of the 
aggregates, forms and so forth  included within modes , and phenomena belonging to the 
three times  included within varieties , and (iii) paths: the harmonies with enlightenment, 
generosity and so forth —as truly existent is a discordant class  that is a corrupted path for 
those definite in the bodhisattvas’ lineage, because it is a distant path for achieving the 
resultant Perfection of Wisdom . 
 Sutra says, “When those not skilled in means practice that called ‘the emptiness of 
forms’”... “the discrimination [of phenomena] until the practice regarding an exalted knower 
of all aspects.”  
 Someone asserts that a meditative equipoise of hearer and solitary realizer aryas is a 
conception of true existence. These are words unpleasant to the ears. It follows it would be similar to a conception of self of persons in not being able to destroy the seeds that are the 
afflictive obscurations, because it would be similar in being a wrong consciousness that is 
mistaken with respect to its objec t of adherence. If that is accepted, since that has been 
explained as “Holding that ‘Grasping and so forth will not be abandoned by the learners’ 
vehicle,’” if that propounder has bodhisattva vows, a root downfall will be incurred.  
 If it were not free from the conception which apprehends a sound -generality and a 
meaning -generality as suitable to be mixed, it follows that it would not be a yogic direct 
perceiver. If that is so, then, like a sense consciousness to which two moons appear, it would 
not be a path to liberation. Therefore, do not deprecate the hearers’ paths as well as their 
result. It is clear that the explanation of the meaning of the commentary in this way by this person muddled in words is [an explanation of someone] of very dull faculty. 
2B2C-1C2A -1C1A -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
By just observing all which are not things as things, phenomena of the three times, forms 
and so forth, (the entities of the contaminated, the uncontaminated and both,) to view the 
self which is thoroughly imputed by others and so forth as just empty and the discrimination of the achievings . Although it is the antidote to those, because it is an object to be 
abandoned by erroneous engagement, it is a discordant class.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the dharma forbearance of true sufferings of a hearer’s path of seeing that is 
bound by (i) the wisdom that directly  views the self of persons which is thoroughly  
imputed by others and so forth as just empty and (ii) the discrimination of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
298 achiev ings that realize only the close placement of mindfulness and so forth, while being 
bound by just observing all which are not truly existent things as truly existent things, 
phenomena of the three times, forms and so forth, (the entities of the contaminated in 
the continuum of an ordinary  being , the uncontaminated , the branches of enlightenment 
and so forth, and both) —is the antidote to its corresponding objects of abandonment, 
because it is a path that directly harms the seeds of acquired (thoroughly imputed) afflictive 
obscurations.   
 Although it is the antidote to the objects of abandonment of those hearers , it is a 
discordant class  that is a corrupted bodhisattvas’ path, because its generation in one’s 
continuum  is an object to be abandoned. That follows, because it is a path that is bound by 
erroneous engagement  towards the mode of abidance of things .  
 It is not indicated that it is to be abandoned by means of apprehending the opposite of the 
observed object and aspect of this path. If one  were to apprehend the opposite of those, there 
would be a wrong consciousness that is mistaken with respect to its object of adherence.  
 To be “bound by a conception of true existence” means that, when one investigates 
whether the bases and paths exist t ruly or not, one does not transcend adherence to them as 
truly existent; this is in terms of not having previously determined the mode of abidance of 
phenomena by way of hearing and contemplation. Since it is a long (distant) path relative to 
that of bodhi sattvas, entering it is to be abandoned.

meaning..

3.5ab  Because t he path of phenomena is  
Devoid of nature, it is the profound itself.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The final mode of abidance that is the path of phenomena is the profound itself, because 
it is dharmata that is devoid of nature . 
 Sutra says: “The perfection of wisdom is profound” and “Subhuti, because all phenomena 
are devoid of nature.”  
2B2C -1C2A -1C2B -1B2. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because of what? Since the types of phenomena are empty of just 
entitiness, it is the profound itself. Because of that, e ven observing the tathagatas is a 
discordant class.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Even observing the tathagatas  as an ultimately existent refuge is a discordant class, 
because  the mode of abidance of those phenomena is the profound that is the emptiness of 
true existence. Because of what?  That follows, since the types of phenomena are empty 
of just the entitiness  of existence in suchness.

ROOT TEXT  
3.5cd  The knowing of phenomena as being of one nature  
Abandons grasping.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalted wisdom that is the bodhisattva aryas’ direct knowing of 
phenomena as being of one nature in being empty of true existence —abandons grasping 
to results as truly existent, because it is the direct antidote to that.  
 Sutra says, “Furthermore, Subhuti, the nature of phenomena is not two; the nature of 
phenomena is simply one.”  
2B2C -1C2A -1C2B -2B2. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. All phenomena, forms and so forth, are only of one nature: “Like this, without nature.” The full knowing that knowers and the objects to be known are one in 
being just equal abandons grasping.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the path of seeing that directly and  fully knows that knowers and the 
objects to be known are one in being just equal in not being existent in suchness —
abandons grasping to results as truly existent, because it is the direct antidote to that.  
 It follows that the modes of a bidance of phenomena are of one taste, since the modes of 
abidance of all phenomena, forms and so forth, are only of one nature  and taste is the 
meaning of ascertaining  “Like this, without nature.”

3.6ab  Since seeing a nd so forth is refuted ,  
That is explained to be difficult to realize.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that ultimate truth —there is a reason for explaining it to be difficult to 
realize, since  it is to be realized by only a reasoning consciousness that analyzes t he 
ultimate;  realizing it by a valid cognizer of conventionalities seeing  forms  and so forth is 
refuted .  
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, that is like this; the Mother is not seen, not heard, not ascertained, 
not known, and not manifestly enlightened by anyone.” That is the reason for it being 
profound.

by an arya’s meditative equipoise; it is explained as that difficult to understand by a 
knower of conventionaliti es itself. Because of what? The latter follows, since that it is an 
object observed by all consciousnesses  of conventionalities, sense consciousnesses 
apprehending forms and so forth,  is eliminated  by valid cognition .

2B2C -1C2A -1C3B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
3.7 In that way, in the syste m of a knower of all, 
All the divisions without exception  
Of the discordant and antidotal class es 
Are to be known here as they have been explained.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
304 EXPLANATION  
All the divisions  without exception of a knower of bases of the discordant and antidotal 
class es in the system , that is, on the occasion of a knower of all, are to be known here as 
they have been explained in sutra, because they are to be known here in that way  
explained above.  
 What directly acts to stop the apprehension of true existence with resp ect to the bases, 
paths, and results is the wisdom that directly realizes emptiness. Nevertheless, the knower of 
bases is divided into two [types] —that of the discordant class and that of the antidotal 
class —because that very exalted wisdom knowing the six teen attributes is divided into two 
from the point of view of whether or not it is  conjoined with special compassion and the 
wisdom realizing emptiness; with respect to that very wisdom realizing emptiness, the 
convention “antidotal knower of bases” has never been taught anywhere, either above or 
below.  
 The perfection of wisdom that know s bases, that realizes impermanence and so forth, in 
the continuum of a bodhisattva does not act to directly damage the conception of true existence, yet it is called “a knower of bases of the antidotal class ” because of being 
conjoined with fully complete method and wisdom.  
 A knower of bases of the discordant class, as explained above, is not shown to be an 
object to be abandoned categorically; the significance that is to be known is that, if those who are definite in the bodhisattva lineage were to be led through that path, it would be a 
hindrance to attaining buddhahood quickly and would  be a corrupted path.   
 An ignorant person holds that the paths of the hearers and solitary realizers are 
hindrances to attaining buddhahood categorically. He is co nfused about the divisions of the 
path. I pay homage to anyone who is able to generate those very hindrances in the continuum! 
2B2C -1C2A -1C3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. On the occasion of a knower of all, in that manner by the way explained, respectively the classifications of the discordant class and the antidote of hearers, 
bodhisattvas, and so forth should be known.  
 
EXPLANATION  
On the occasion of a knower of all, respectively the classifications of (i) a knower of 
bases of the disc ordant class , a knower of bases in the continu a of hearer  aryas, and (ii) a 
knower of bases of the antidotal class , a knower of bases  in the continu a of bodhisattvas 
and so forth,  should be known, because they should be known in that manner  by the 
way expl ained above.

QUALM: Having described the discordant class and so forth in that way, what are the applications which meditate on them?  
RESPONSE: Applications: 
 
The applications are of ten types, because there are: four divisions by way of object, three 
by way of entity, two by way of action, and one by way of indicator e xample.  
2B2C -1C2A -2A2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
3.8 The applications that stop forms and so forth,  
The practice with respect to their impermanence and so forth, and  
The incomplete and the thoroughly complete, and  
The non- grasping to that and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The first [i.e. that there are four divisions by way of object] is established, because there are 
three objects of observation with respect to the conventional and one object of observation with respect to the ultimate. That follows, because there are four applications  by way of (i) 
stopping adherence to the bases of characterization, forms and so forth , as truly existent, 
(ii-iii) stopping the practice with respect to the non -grasping to the characterizing 
attributes, impermanence and so forth, and  the incomplete and the thoroughly 
complete  (as supports of of excellent qualities) as truly existent, and  (iv) stopping the 
practice that, having abided in the non -grasping to the ultimate as a truly existent object of 
observation, adheres to it as truly exist ent. Respectively, sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, with regard to this, when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of 
wisdom, if he does not utilize forms, he practices the perfection of wisdom.”  
• “Furthermore, if [a great bodhisattva] neither utilizes ‘form i s permanent’ nor ‘form is 
impermanent’, he practices the perfection of wisdom.”  
• “In addition, Subhuti, when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, if he neither utilizes ‘form is complete’ nor ‘form is incomplete’, he practices the perfect ion of 
wisdom.”  
• “Furthermore, when [a great bodhisattva] practices the perfection of wisdom, if he neither utilizes ‘grasping to form’ nor ‘not grasping to form’, he practices the perfection of 
wisdom.”   
 
ROOT TEXT  
3.9ab  Unchanging, the non -existence of the agent,  
The applications of the three types of difficult activities,  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
306 EXPLANATION  
There are three by way of entity, because there are three: the stopping of the adherence to 
the action, agent, and the result ant activities  as truly existent : (i) unchanging,  (ii) not being 
the agent , and (iii) the applications of the three types of difficult activities . The three 
difficult activities are the difficult activity of object of intent, the difficult activity of 
application, and the difficult activity of actio n. Respectively, sutra says:  
• “When a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, if the perfection of 
wisdom is presented, there are difficulties: the mind not becoming discouraged and 
distracted, undertaking this perfection of wisdom, and being irreversible from 
unsurpassable complete enlightenment.”  
• “Bhagavan, those who seek armor for the sake of sentient beings are those who seek 
levitation in space.”  
• “Bhagavan, by that enumeration, those who seek clear complete buddhahood in 
unsurpassable comp lete enlightenment for the sake of sentient beings seek the full 
freedom of space.”   
 
ROOT TEXT  
3.9cd  Due to attaining the results according to the fortunes,  
That asserted to be fruitful, and  
 
3.10a  That which is not contingent on others, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are two by way of action, because there are two: (i) that asserted to be fruitful due to attaining the results according to the fortune s by means of performing the activity that 
will produce the result , and (ii) that which is not continge nt on others  by means of 
possessing benefit. Sutra says:  
• “Tathagata, another bhiksu thinks this, ‘There is neither production nor cessation in any phenomena anywhere; I pay homage to the Bhagavati Perfection of Wisdom of 
designations of phenomena from the aggregate of ethics .’” 
• “Koushika, likewise, the great bod hisattvas who practice the perfection of wisdom will 
not accomplish the intentions of whatever guarding, protecting and hiding as they have intended, and will bear the fortune of tiredness.”  
 
ROOT TEXT  
3.10b Seven types of appearances are indicators. 
 
EXPLANATION  
While [they] are not established as truly existent by means of indicators , they are indicated 
(made known) by means of seven types of appearances.  
 Sutra says, “At any time, a great bodhisattva does not generate a clinging mind for 
dreams; he doe s not generate a clinging mind due to dreams; he does not generate a clinging 
mind with respect to dreams. Likewise, he does not generate a clinging mind with respect to 
[the rest] until  emanations. At that time the great bodhisattva will fully know all ph enomena 
as being like illusions, until  being like emanations.”  
2B2C -1C2A -2A3. Commentary  
2B2C -1C2A -2A3A. Th ose explicitly indicated  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
307 2B2C -1C2A -2A3B. Th ose implicitly indicated , the applications of hearers and solitary realizers  
2B2C -1C2A -2A3A. Those expli citly indicated  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) All phenomena, forms and so forth, (2) the impermanence and 
the emptiness es of those themselves, (3), the incomplete and the complete individually, (4) 
the non -grasping, (5) not changing into another , (6) the non -existence of the agent, (7) 
respectively, the difficult activities of the object of intent, the application, and the action, of 
the nature of the three exalted knowers of all, (8) by attaining the results according to the 
fortunes; non -depriv ation, (9) mere non -progression through the condition of others, (10)(a) 
complete transformation, (b) assembled together,  (c) contradicting, (d) conditions, (e) not 
transferred, (f) without support, and (g) not being the agent; by means of eliminating, the  
full knowings that accomplish with respect to the seven appearances to consciousness, the applications with respect to the various [objects] of the bodhisattvas are of ten types.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the applications  that stop the adherence to the various objects of the  
applications  of the bodhisattvas as truly existent —are of ten types , because there are full 
knowings —that  accomplish with respect to  the seven types of appearance by the entity 
and attributes of the bases —differentiated by means of el iminating  the conception of true 
existence. What are they?  
 
There are four divisions by way of object, because:  
• there are three that stop the adherence to —(1) the bases of characterization, all 
phenomena, forms and so forth, (2) the characterizing attribut es, the impermanence 
and the emptiness of those [forms and so forth]  themselves, (3) the attributes of the 
support, the incomplete and the complete individually as supports of excellent 
qualities, and varieties —as truly existent, and  
• the stopping of the adherence to —(4) the grasping and the non- grasping to modes as 
ultimate objects of observation —as truly existent.  
 
The realization of other- powered phenomena as being empty of true existence, like 
illusions, is complete as a support of excellent qualities , because , in dependence on it , one 
would be able to abandon discordant classes , and excellent qualities would increase more 
and more. The adherence to other- powered phenomena as truly existent is incomplete as a 
support of excellent qualities, because it hinders the generation of excellent qualities.  
 There are three divisions by way of entity because there are:  
• [the application] realizing that action, the Perfection of Wisdom, (5) does not change 
into another  through increasing or decreasing ultimately,  
• in terms of doer, [the application realizing] (6) the very non-existence of the agent  
ultimately, and  
• in terms of the result, respectively  the applications of (7) the  three types of difficult  
activities  that: 
(i) although not observing the object of intent , the exalted knower of all aspects of the 
nature of the three exalted knowers  of all, as existing ultimately, realizes the object of 
intent as existing conventionally,  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
308 (ii) although not observing the knower of paths of the time of the cause as existing 
ultimately, realizes the application  which is the cause of an exalted knower of all aspects as 
existing conventionally, and  
(iii) although not observing the knower of bases as existing ultimately, realizes it possesses 
the action  of being a method for takin g care of other trainees as existing conventionally.   
 
The subject —an application of a knower of bases indicated here —possesses a special action 
because, (8) by [causing them to]  attain the results according to  the fortunes  of the three 
types of lineage -bearers, it does not deprive  them [of the results] . It holds a special benefit, 
because (9) one possesses (i) the attainment of enlightenment not contingent on only others , 
but by one’s own strength, and (ii) not progressing through the conditions of others  due 
to oneself being able to protect, guard, and so forth.  
 (10) The subject —these contaminated dependent -arisings —do not exist truly:  
(a) because they are mere appearances that are complete transformations  of the latencies 
of adherence; for example, lik e a dream :  
(b) because they are appearances which are the assembling together  of causes and 
conditions; for example, like an illusion : 
(c) because they are appearances that are contradictory  to true existence; for example, like 
a mirage : 
(d) because they are appearances that are contingent on conditions ; for example, like an 
echo : 
(e) because they are appearances that are not transferred  from the aspect that deposits 
latencies; for example, like a reflection : 
(f) because they are appearances that are devoi d of a truly existent support ; for example, 
like a city of smell -eaters ; and  
(g) because they are appearances that are not established as a truly existent agent ; for 
example, like an emanation.  
 
The stopping of adherence to the seven appearances to consci ousness as truly existent in 
this way is the reason for being able to meditate on the entity, the attributes, and so forth of the bases by way of negating true existence.  
2B2C -1C2A -2A3B. Those implicitly indicated , the applications of hearers and solitary  realizers  
COMMENTARY 
Through having indicated  by means of accompaniment, implicitly, [that] of hearers and so 
forth are the reverse of what has been explained, applications.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The paths of preparation of hearers and so forth are the reverse of what has been 
explained, applications that cultivate without stopping the adherence to true existence, 
because, through having indicated  an explicit indication of the bodhisattvas’ applications  
by means of accompaniment, they are  to be known implicitly  in this way.  
 It is to be known in this way, because the application of the  knower of bases, the 
principal object to be explained here, is that very bodhisattva’s path of preparation bearing 
the aspects of the sixteen, impermanence and so forth, which is the cause for the clear 
realization that is to be passed through in the continuum of a bodhisattva on the occasion of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
309 the path of seeing. The path of preparation bearing the aspect of emptiness that is cultivated 
by having been conjoined with the wisdom realizing those bases as empty of true existence 
is a cause that is similar in type to that very path of seeing that acts to pass through, which 
will be explained below. That very path bearing the aspects of the sixteen, impermanence 
and so forth, is explicitly indicated  to be cultivated  by bodhisattvas through stopping the 
conception of true existence on the occasion of the path of preparation. Implicitly, it is 
indicated  that it is cultivated on  the paths of preparation of hearers and  solitary realizers 
without stopping the conception of true existence.

EXPLANATION

“forms as not permanent ” and so forth —are of sixteen, because there are (i) eight 
divisions by way of isolates included within uninterrupted paths, that, by way of observing the aspects of the four truths, directly and newly realize emptiness of true existence and 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
312 freedom from elaborations, and (ii) eight divisions by way of isolates included within 
liberated paths.

2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -1A1. The aspects of the forbearances and knowledges of sufferings 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because of being devoid of  entitiness, (1) since forms and so forth 
are free from being permanent and impermanent, neither permanent nor impermanent, (2) 
since it is free from being suffering and non -suffering, free from the extremes of 
permanence and severance, (3) because it is free from being empty and non -empty, purity, 
and (4) since the very entities of self and non -self are not [established], not produced and 
not ceased, and not the thoroughly afflicted and not the completely pure —and so forth, are 
the aspects of the true sufferings. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The subject —impermanent forms and so forth—are neither permanent nor 
impermanent ultimately, since they are free from being permanent and impermanent 
ultimately. That follows because  they are devoid of  entitiness  ultimately. The di rect 
realization in that manner is the aspect of the forbearances and knowledges of true 
sufferings. Apply in that manner also to all those below. The reason of being devoid of  
entitiness is also to be applied to all.   
(2) The subject —the aspect of suffering —is free from the extremes of permanence and 
severance  ultimately, since it is free from being suffering and non -suffering  ultimately.  
(3) The subject —sufferings’ emptiness of a self that is a different object —is purity  by way 
of nature , because it is free from being empty and non -empty  ultimately.  
(4) The subject —sufferings’ not being established in the entity of a self—is not produced 
and not ceased, and not the thoroughly afflicted,  grasping and so forth , and not the 
completely pure , faith and so fo rth, ultimately, since the very entities of self and non -self 
are not  established  ultimately.  
The exalted wisdoms that realize with direct cognition in this way are “the aspects  of the 
forbearances and knowledges of true sufferings .”  
 
Sutra says:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
313 • “Subhut i, the great bodhisattva Maitreya, upon attaining clear complete  buddhahood in 
unsurpassable complete enlightenment, will not teach the Dharma saying, ‘Form is 
permanent’ or ‘Form is impermanent.’”  
• “‘What Dharma will Maitreya teach upon attaining buddhahoo d?’ ‘He will teach the very 
pure Dharma saying, “Form is very pure.”’”   
• “Subhuti, because forms are completely pure, the Perfection of Wisdom is completely 
pure.”  
• “Subhuti, form’s absence  of production, cessation, being thoroughly afflicted and being 
completely pure is form’s complete purity.”  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -1A2. The aspects of the forbearances and knowledges of origins  
COMMENTARY 
(1) Because of the insubstantiality  of cause and non -cause, like space, (2) because of not 
possessing origins and non -origins, having abandoned all afflictions and secondary 
afflictions, (3) by non -relation with strong production and non -strong -production, liberated 
from full holding, and (4) because of complete l iberation from conditions and non -
conditions, not expressible by its entity, are true origins. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —karma and craving – : 
(1) do not exist ultimately, like space, because of the  insubstantiality , the absence  of 
ultimate  existence of caus e and non- cause  of sufferings, 
(2) have abandoned all  ultimately existent afflictions and secondary afflictions, because 
of not possessing ultimately existent origins and non- origins ,  
(3) are liberated from full holding  to ultimate existence, by non -relation with  ultimately 
existent strong production and non- strong -production  of intense sufferings, and   
(4) not expressible by its entity  ultimately, because of complete liberation  as existing 
ultimately from conditions and non- conditions  of sufferings;   
 
The new realizations with direct cognition in this way are  the aspects of the forbearances 
and knowledges of  true origins. Respectively, sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, furthermore, due to space being completely pure, the perfection of wisdom is completely pure.”  
• “Subhuti, due to space being unpolluted, the perfection of wisdom is completely pure.”  
• “Subhuti, furthermore, due to space not being held, the perfection of wisdom is 
completely pure.”  
• “Subhuti, due to conventionalities not existing, the perfection of wisdom  is completely 
pure.”  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -1A3. The aspects of the forbearances and knowledges of cessations 
COMMENTARY 
(1) Because of what? Not related with cessation and non -cessation; because of that, the 
meaning of true cessations articulated in words cannot  be imparted to others’ continua, (2) 
since pacification and non -pacification do not exist, it acts without an object of observation, 
(3) because of freedom from the sublime and the non -sublime, it has transcended the two 
extremes and is purity, and (4) be cause definite emergence and also non -definite emergence 
do not exist, all diseases are not produced, are true cessations.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
314  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the meaning of true cessations , a separation in which sufferings have been 
abandoned –:  
(1) cannot be imparted to the others’ continua  ultimately by being articulated in words; 
because of what? Because it is not related with cessation and non -cessation  ultimately; 
alternatively, apply “it cannot be imparted to others’ continua immediately by merely being 
articulated in words,”  
(2) it acts without a truly existent  object of observation , since pacification and non -
pacification  of suffering s do not exist ultimately, 
(3) has transcended the two extremes  of permanence and severance, and is purity  by way 
of nature, because the sublime liberation and the non- sublime are free from ultimate 
existence, and  
(4) possesses the benefit that all diseases are not produced, because  of having realized 
with direct cognition that definite emergence and also non -definite -emerge nce do not 
exist ultimately. 
 
The realizations in this way are  the aspects of the forbearances and knowledges of true 
cessations. Sutra says respectively:  
• “Due to space being inexpressible, the perfection of wisdom is completely pure.”  
• “Due to space being unobservable, the perfection of wisdom is completely pure.”   
• “Subhuti, due to all phenomena being not produced, ceased, thoroughly afflicted, and 
completely pure, the perfection of wisdom is completely pure.”  
• “Bhagavan, when any son or daughter of good li neage holds, clasps, reads, understands, 
and pays proper attention to this Perfection of Wisdom, Bhagavan, he or she will not have 
eye diseases.”  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -1A4. The aspects of the forbearances and knowledges of paths  
COMMENTARY 
(1) Because of freedom from paths and non -paths, severance of bad migrations, (2) because 
it is not mixed with those that are suitable or unsuitable, non -conceptualization of the 
method with the aim of actualizing the results, (3) by complete liberation from 
accomplishment  and non-  accomplishment, not related with the signs of all phenomena, and 
(4) by the non- existence of deliverance and non -deliverance, the characteristics of the 
entities of that to be expressed and that which expresses, regarding the two, that to be 
known and the term, without production of a knower, are true paths. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalted wisdom that realizes newly with direct cognition that paths are 
empty of true existence – :  
(1) possesses severance of bad migrations  such as the hells, because of realizing with 
direct cognition that paths and non- paths are free from  ultimate existence ,  
(2) is non-conceptualization of the method with the aim of actualizing the results  such 
as stream -enterers as ultimately existent, because  of directly realizing that it is not mixed 
with those that are suitable or unsuitable as antidotes to afflictions ultimately,   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
315 (3) not related with the signs of  true existence of all phenomena, by complete liberation 
from  achieving the accomplishment and non -accomplishment  of the non- erroneous 
measure of abidance of the mind ultimately, and  
 
The subject —the subsequent knowledge with respect to a Mahayana path of seeing —(4) is 
without  the production of a knower  that observes the two, the meaning that is to be 
known , that which is to be expressed, and the term, the characteristic of the entity that 
expresses , by way of being together with dualistic appearances, because it is an exalted 
wisdom distinguished by newly realizing with direct cognition the non- existence of 
delivera nce and non- deliverance  to nirvana ultimately.  
 The meaning is as follows. In the perspective of a direct realization of the ultimate, 
[phenomena] do not appear as if they are expressed by terms. This is the meaning of 
dualistic appearance having vanished. The explanation of the above has been made in that 
way in accordance with the measure of abidance of objects since it is easy to understand. The meaning should be applied to all in the way  it has been explained here. Respectively, 
sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, if one were to teach this precious perfection of wisdom, one would be 
completely liberated from the hells.”  
• “Subhuti, regarding this perfection of wisdom, phenomena are not produced, ceased, thoroughly afflicted, completely pure, to be fully held, and to be discarded.”  
• “Subhuti, this precious perfection of wisdom is unpolluted by any phenomenon.”  
• “Subhuti, when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, if he does not conceptualize in that way, does not observe in that way, and does not give rise  to thoughts 
in that way, he practices the perfection of wisdom.”  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -1B. Summarized and indicated  
COMMENTARY 
Those instants of a knower of all bearing such aspects are the path of seeing of bodhisattvas.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those instants of forbearances and knowledges of a knower of all bearing 
such aspects —are the path of seeing of bodhisattvas,  because they are their clear 
realizations of the truth .

The reverse  of that, that to be meditated on by the hearers in the aspects of impermanence 
and so forth, is the path of seeing with respect to the  knower of all. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The reverse of that bearing the aspect of freedom from  elaborations , that to be meditated 
on through newly realizing with direct cognition by130 the hearers in the aspects of 
impermanence , suffering, and so forth, is the path of seeing  that is to be passed through 
and is implicitly indicated  on the occasion of  the knower of  all. 
 Because of being such clear realizations of the truth, through this, both —(i) the path of 
seeing in the continua of hearers and solitary realizers, and (ii) the path of seeing that is to

2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3. The reason for not indicating  the path of meditation  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3A. Meaning of the words  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3B. Decisive analysis  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3A. Meaning of the words  
COMMENTARY 
Since hearers’ paths are objects to be fully known, not to be actualized, by bodhisattvas, the 
path of meditation is not indicated . 
 
EXPLANATION  
The path of meditation in the continu a of bodhisattvas that acts to familiarize  with those 
very sixteen, impermanence and so forth, which have been already newly realized with direct cognition by means of diligent familiarization is not indicated . If I were to indicate 
the purpose for that, it is  since,  apart from  being what will tra nsform into the clear 
realizations that are to be actualized by bodhisattvas, they are  not clear realizations to be 
actualized by bodhisattvas.  That follows, because hearers’ paths bearing the aspects of 
the sixteen, impermanence and so forth, are merely objects to be fully known  having been 
viewed by knowing and seeing by bodhisattvas, and are objects to be passed through having 
been viewed by knowing and seeing, for, the latter is set apart from the path of seeing by 
way of observation .  
2B2C -1C2A -3B2B -3B. Decisive analysis  
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  explains:  
 
QUALM: Why is there no separate explanation of the path of meditation for the 
knower of all, in the way  it has been indicated  for a knower of paths?  
RESPONSE: The excellences of hearers and  solitary realizers have been 
indicated  as those to be passed through having been viewed by knowing and 
seeing of bodhisattvas, whereby they are not to be actualized. Therefore, they are not indicated  with respect to bodhisattvas. Since acting to actualize  again and 
again that very meaning in accordance with how it is fully seen in hearers and so 
forth is the path of meditation, it is easy to understand, so it is not indicated . 
Know that the partial concordance with definite discrimination of bodhisattvas i s 
just that explained previously. It is held that it is clear that when hearers and so 
forth take the four noble truths as objects of observation by way of the aspects of 
impermanence and so forth, it is the heat level and so forth with the nature of 
conta minated meditation, therefore it is not taught.  
 
The path of preparation that is similar in type to this path of seeing that acts to pass through 
should be understood from the explanation in the first chapter; although the path of 
meditation that is simila r in type to this path of seeing that is to be passed through needs to 
be indicated  to hearers and solitary realizers, as it is easy to understand, it is not indicated  
here.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
317  In this context, the reason —given by someone to prove that this text does not in dicate  the 
path of meditation in the continua of hearers themselves —is not even slightly relevant, 
because it is taught in [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand that: 
 
Because for a knower of all , there is no path of meditation.  
 
Therefore it is  not right that there is no path of meditation in the continua of hearer aryas 
that diligently familiarizes with that bearing the aspects of impermanence and so forth. 
Otherwise, it would follow that even the implicitly indicated  path of seeing in this [co ntext] 
is only [generated] in the continua of hearers and solitary realizers; however, this is not right because it has been taught that it is a clear realization that is viewed by knowing and seeing 
by a yogic direct perceiver in the continua of bodhisatt vas and is to be passed through 
having been viewed.  
 The reason —given by someone else to prove that the path of meditation bearing the 
aspect of freedom from elaborations in the continua of bodhisattvas is not indicated  —is 
even more irrelevant tha n the p revious one, because that is clear from the reason explicitly 
stated in this text, and there is no reason to wonder why the path of meditation that is a clear 
realization and is a bodhisattvas’ object of actualization is not indicated  here.  
 DISPUTANT: Th at is not established. I wonder why, having explicitly indicated  the path of 
seeing here, the path of meditation is not indicated  after that —in the way  the path of 
meditation has been indicated  after the path of seeing in the second chapter?  
 RESPONSE: That is because the path of seeing that is explicitly indicated  here is indicated  
for the sake of understanding the path that acts to pass through, rather than the clear 
realization that is to be passed through; there is no purpose in  indicat ing the path of 
meditation that is similar in type to that to any person whether of the Mahayana or the 
Hinayana  here.  
 
Therefore, the following doubts have been eliminated:  
• When the path of seeing in the continuum of a bodhisattva bearing the aspects of 
impermanence and so forth is indicated , why is the path of meditation that familiarizes by 
means of diligently familiarizing with the continuity of a similar type to that not indicated ?  
• Why are the two —the path of seeing and the path of meditation —not indicated  with 
respect to the knower of bases that is to be known by bodhisattvas , in the way  the two —
the path of seeing and the path of meditation —are indicated  with respect to the knower of 
paths that is a bodhisattva’s object of actualization ?  
 Such a path of meditation is not indicated , since bodhisattvas themselves do not need to 
diligently familiarize with that path (i) in order to generate disillusionment for cyclic 
existe nce, and abandon the afflictive obscurations and knowledge obscurations, and (ii) in 
order to take care of the hearer and solitary realize r lineage -bearers; it is not that that very 
knower of bases do es not exist in the continua of bodhisattvas on the path  of meditation. 
They are able to take care of others by just generating the path of seeing.  
 The meaning of the teaching in [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  that—
the functions of the abandonments and realizations of hearers and solitary rea lizers  are 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 3: The Knower of Base s 
 
318 subsumed under the Mahayana path of seeing in this context —has already been explained 
before.131

2B2C -1C2B -1. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY 
Having shown extensively in that way, by summarizing all the meanings, as a conclusion of 
the three exalted knowers of all:  
 
EXPLANATION  
Having shown  the three exalted knowers extensively in that way  explained a bove, I set 
down a conclusion of the three exalted knowers of all . 
 There is a reason for setting down a conclusion of the three exalted knowers, because, by 
summarizing all the meanings  of the clear realizations, the meaning of the others will also 
be sum marized and realized. 
2B2C -1C2B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
3.16 Thus, these, also these,  
And also these: these three types  
Are the complete thorough presentation  
Of the three chapters. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Having shown the three exalted knowers extensively in that wa y, 
• the sutra statements beginning from “Subhuti, this Mother does not attain any quality” 
until “Not past, not future, not arising in the present,” until  the three condensed into one, 
these  nineteen lines which indicate the conclusion of a knower of bases;   
• also the statements “Subhuti, thus the Mother is not a means of transcending the three 
realms” and so forth, these  lines [which indicate] the conclusion of a knower of paths;  
• and also the statements “Subhuti, this Mother is not a means of bestowing and a means 
of discarding the qualities of the buddhas and so forth until  the qualities of the solitary 
realizers” and so forth, these  lines [which indicate the conclusion of an exalted knower of 
all aspects];  
the subject —these three types  of lines from sutra —are the complete thorough 
presentation  of the conclusion of the three chapters  that have taken the three exalted 
knowers as their subject matter above, because they are lines that present the conclusions of 
those three exalted knowers completely presented above.

2B2C -1C2B -3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. In the manner just explained, this exalted knower of all aspects, this 
knower of paths and this  knower of all, fully complete in that way the three types of the 
three chapters.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In the manner the three exalted knowers have been just explained extensively , they are 
summarized and shown. Sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, this Perfection of Wisdom does  not engage any phenomen on, does not correctly 
act to indicate, does not definitely act to indicate, does not thoroughly act to attain, and does not definitely act to attain,” these lines of the conclusion of an exalted knower of 
all aspects ; 
• “Bhagavan, wi th respect to this, no one will grasp to any phenomena,” and so forth, these  
lines of the conclusion of a knower of paths ; 
• “Subhuti, with respect to emptiness, one neither thoroughly engages nor turns away,” and so forth, these  lines of the conclusion of a  knower of all bases ; 
The subject —in that way the three types of sets of meanings of the lines of conclusions of 
the three chapters —fully complete the conclusions of the three exalted knowers, because 
they are lines from sutra that indicate the conclusions  of the three exalted knowers explained 
extensively above. 
 The explanation by someone that —the three chapters thoroughly present the three exalted 
knowers—is a wrong explanation of the meaning due to having encountered an incorrect text. What is explained  in the root text accords with the order that occurs in the medium 
Perfection of Wisdom Sutra , and this [order] accords with the order from the Eight 
Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra .

Chapter 4: The Application i n Complete Aspects  
2B2C -2A1. [The application of clear complete realization of] all aspects: the causes that act to gain  control  
2B2C -2A1A. Making a connection by st ating the relationship
