2B2C -2A1C. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -2A1A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
QUALM: Further, what is the difference between the three exalted knowers of all 
and the clear complete realization of all aspects?  
 
SOMEONE’S ASSERTION: The three exalted knowers of all individually 
ascertain the objects that are the aspects by way of the  aspects that have been 
explained, whereas the clear complete realization of all aspects is an object-
possessor of all aspects without exception.  
 ANOTHER SAYS: The three exalted knowers of all are presented as being fully -
qualified, whereas the clear com plete realization of all aspects is an applicator.  
 
SOME PEOPLE’S EXPLANATION: The clear complete realization of all 
aspects is distinguished by the positing of discordant classes and antidotal 
class es, whereas the three exalted knowers of all, due to bei ng the very aspect of 
pacification by nature, are not like that. 
 The meaning of that which has been explained above is to be understood in this context as 
follows. The clear complete realization of all aspects and the three exalted knowers (i) have 
dissimilar modes of indication by means of their objects, and (ii) have dissimilar modes of 
description by means of entity and by means of function. Having indicated the three exalted 
knowers in the first three chapters, there is not the fault of repetition in explaining the three 
exalted knowers here again, …  
 
COMMENTARY 
The complete knowing of the three exalted knowers of all is the clear complete realization 
of all aspects, since the three exalted knowers of all are cultivated , by means of further 
collect ing the knowers of all aspects, of paths, and of bases, for the sake o f gaining control . 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
321 EXPLANATION  
… because  the complete knowing of the three exalted knowers of all  that are objects to 
be cultivated in one’s continuum by means of individually ascertaining the aspects 
previously is explained in this chapter to be the clea r complete realization of all aspects,  
by means of further collecting  all the 110 aspects of the  exalted knower of all aspects, 
the thirty -six aspects of the knower of paths, the twenty -seven  aspects of the  knower of 
bases , and all aspects without exceptio n, whereby the modes of description and purposes 
are dissimilar. 
 There is a purpose for collect ing all the aspects of the three exalted knowers without 
exception and indicating them, because, since all the aspects of the three exalted knowers 
of all  without exception  are cultivated,  they are explained in that way. There is a purpose 
for cultivating all [these aspects] without exception, because it is for the sake of gaining 
control over the aspects of the three exalted knowers. Since both [the aspect s of the three 
exalted knowers indicated in the first three chapters and in this chapter] are to be known by way of hearing and contemplation, and are to be meditated upon, it is inappropriate in any way to delineate as separate the passages for hearing an d contemplation and those for 
practice.  
 
OPINION: It is sufficient to indicate just the later one. 
RESPONSE: It is taught that there are also other purposes, because, regarding the above 
predicate of the probandum that there is not the fault of  repetition , (i) [the indication] in the 
first three chapters is for the sake of making completely known the uncommon self- entities 
and characteristics of the three exalted knowers of all, and (ii) [the indication] in this chapter 
is for the sake of [making] known th at one cultivates all the aspects of the knowers of all 
aspects, of paths and of bases without exception through the application that is a union of 
calm abiding  and special insight . Further, the purpose for cultivation in this manner is for 
the sake of gai ning control [over these aspects].  
 (i) [The aspects of the three exalted knowers] are indicated in the first three chapters for 
the sake of cultivation through having understood the characteristics of the three exalted 
knowers, and (ii) they are indicated  in this chapter for the sake of cultivation through  having 
understood the applications that act to cultivate them. Therefore, know that delineating as 
separate the passages for hearing and contemplation and those for practice acts as a 
condition for the d ecline of the great texts [and] are words unpleasant to the ears.  
 Also, regarding the above predicate of the probandum that there is not the fault of 
repetition, (i) the explanation of the three exalted knowers is indicated in this [chapter on] 
the clear complete realization in all aspects for the sake of cultivating the antidotes by 
having understood the classifications of the discordant classes and the antidotes , and (ii) it is 
indicated in the first three chapters for the sake of cultivation by having fully understood the 
three exalted knowers of all that are distinguished by having pacified some objects of 
abandonment.  
 Having understood the classifications of the discordant classes and the antidotes, one 
cultivates the three exalted knowers of all by  means of collect ing the knowers of all aspects, 
of paths, and of bases through the application that is a union of calm abiding  and special 
insight . Thus a special purpose is established, because it is for the sake of gaining control 
over the cultivation o f the aspects of the three exalted knowers. 
 (a) Having individually indicated the aspects of the three exalted knowers in the first 
three chapters, indicating them here collectively
, (b) having indicated the uncommon 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
322 natures of the three exalted knowers i n the first three chapters, indicating the applications 
that practice them here, (c) having indicated the cultivation of all the aspects without 
exception for the sake of abandoning all the discordant classes without exception here, 
indicating the three ex alted knowers that are free from some of the discordant classes in the 
first three chapters —are simply dissimilar modes of description and purposes; one knows all 
of them by way of hearing and contemplation, and also meditates on them by way of the 
wisdom arisen from meditation. Therefore, delineating the passages for hearing and 
contemplation and those for practice as separate dissimilar sections are words that cause the 
degeneration of the Buddha’s teachings. Hence it is only suitable to discard them.  
  “For the sake of gaining control” and “cultivated” [in the commentary above] indicate 
that, even in order to attain mastery over cultivation and familiarization of the three exalted 
knowers that have been explained in the first three chapters, one must cult ivate them 
through the application that is a union of calm abiding  and special insight  for the sake of 
abandoning all the discordant classes without exception; it is not held that the meanings indicated here are for the sake of attaining mastery over famil iarization and those explained 
previously are for the sake of mere comprehension of hearing and contemplation, because a path that (i) cultivates through application any one aspect of the three exalted knowers that 
have been indicated in the first three ch apters and (ii) does not cultivate through application 
the aspects indicated here, and a path that is the reverse of that [a path that (a) does not cultivate through application any one aspect of the three exalted knowers that have been 
indicated in the first three chapters and (b) cultivates through application the aspects 
indicated here] are impossible.  
 Regarding the meaning of “collectively”  cultivati ng the aspects of the three exalted 
knowers, a previous Tibetan scholar said:  
 
The seventy -one aspects o f truths that bear the aspect of being selfless and the 
102 aspects of conventionalities that exist are the bases that are to be cultivated. 
A basis upon which superimpositions are to be eliminated, [through establishing 
it] as being devoid of  true existen ce, is, for instance, the aggregates. The meaning 
of collective  cultivation through collecting them is to collect the seventy -one 
aspects into the three common doors of liberation. However, the principal 
[meaning] is, having collect ed the entities of the 1 73 aspects into the three —(1) 
the liberation that is the emptiness door of liberation called “the application that stops abidance,” that realizes them to be empty of true existence and free from 
elaborations, (2) the signlessness door of liberation called “stopping application,” 
that stops adherence to object -possessors as truly existent, and (3) the 
wishlessness door of liberation that stops adherence to results as truly existent —
each of [the aspects] is  differentiated into the three latter ones, and the latter ones 
are divided into the three applications: the profound, the difficult to fathom , and 
the immeasurable. Therefore, cultivation through collect ing them into the three 
doors of liberation by way of the application that is a union of calm abiding  and 
special insight  in meditative equipoise is the meaning of collectively cultivating 
[the aspects of] the three exalted knowers. 
 
I do not think such an assertion can bear analysis. Does one perform cultivation by having taken even the aspects that are varieties from among the objects of knowledge, such as 
impermanence and suffering, as the mode of apprehension of the aspect of the application in 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
323 complete aspects that is a union of calm abiding  and special insight  or not? The latter is 
unreasonable, because  (i) it contradicts the proof by Arya [Vimuktisena] and Acarya 
[Haribhadra] that, as the antidote to the conception of the aggregates as permanent and so 
forth, one causes the aspects of impermanent aggregates and so forth to dawn and cultivates 
them, and (ii) the divisions of the aspects which are 173 that have been made would be 
meaningless. 
  In the first case, if a bodhisattva causes all the aspects without exception to dawn and 
cultivates them in his continuum through the application of a union of calm  abiding  and 
special insight , [this is unreasonable], (i) because it is inappropriate not to set down those 
divisions, and (ii) because it is inappropriate for Acarya Haribhadra to refute the explanation 
that a knower of bases includes the clear realizatio ns of solitary realizer aryas.  
 Having divided the yoga that is a union of calm abiding  and special insight  observing 
emptiness into the three doors of liberation, asserting that it exists from the path of 
accumulation until the tenth ground is a very grea t mistake, because a fully qualified special 
insight  observing emptiness, except for existing from the heat level of the path of 
preparation and above, cannot possibly be generated on the path of accumulation by those 
who are definite in the Mahayana linea ge. [The assertor], due to not understanding well 
even the demarcation where one transfers to the heat level of the Mahayana path of preparation, is simply imagining that he is making a presentation of the Mahayana paths.  
  Also, I do not think that the a ssertion that the aspects of the three exalted knowers are 
clearly presented in this chapter but not clearly presented in the first three chapters is correct, because (i) in the first chapter the aspects that are the topics of the principal cause and resul t are clarified, (ii) in the second chapter the aspects of the three paths are clarified, 
and (iii) in the third chapter the aspects of the knower of bases are clarified. Therefore, the 
meaning of collective cultivation is, as it has occurred in [the secti on 2B2C -2A1A above 
on] making a connection, collect ing all the aspects of the three exalted knowers in a practice 
that is a yoga by way of a union of calm abiding  and special insight .

2B2C -2A1B -1A1A- 1. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.1  The instances that know the bases, 
Called  “aspects” are the characteristics.  
Because the exalted knowers of all are of three types, 
Those are asserted to be of three types.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The instances of the exalted wisdom that know impermanence and so forth, the  bases  
included within the four noble truths, are exalted wisdoms tha t destroy the discordant 
classes by means of the dawning of the aspects opposite to the various discordant classes. 
Therefore, those called  “a bodhisattva’s exalted wisdoms that, through the aspects  of any of 
the three exalted knowers dawning well, cultiva te them ” are the antidotal characteristics  
in this context . Those  aspects that are exalted wisdoms posited as antidotes, when 
condensed into classes, are asserted to be of three types, because the exalted knowers of 
all are  definite in being of three types . 
 In this context, [Vimuktisena’s]  Illuminating the Twenty Thousand  explains:  
 
The instances are the characteristics that are antidotes to the discordant classes, for example, as in the case of impermanence [being an antidote] with respect to 
the conception of permanence, and so forth.  
 
Therefore, explaining [the instances] to be characteristics of the aspects is a wrong 
explanation of the meaning of the text by someone who has not understood what to do with 
this extensive explanation of the divisi ons of the aspects in this chapter, because [if his 
explanation were correct], the contextual meaning of the reply (that, since all three —bases, 
antidotes, and aspects —are included as the subject matter, through being characterized, the 
three exalted knowe rs are included as the subject matter) in the previous section on the  
rejections  of dispute regarding the statement of the purpose -and-relationship
132 would 
become lost. 
 Therefore, it means that “the exalted wisdom in the continuum of a bodhisattva that 
directly realizes the aggregates to be impermanent is an antidote to the superimposition that 
apprehends the aggregates as permanent, because, by means of the aspect of impermanent 
aggregates dawning well, it directly damages the other [the superimposition th at apprehends 
the aggregates as permanent].” This is to be similarly applied to all the aspects.    The antidote directly damaging the seeds that are objects of abandonment is the wisdom 
directly realizing emptiness. However, as the antidotes to as many su perimpositions which 
have erroneously engaged the aspects of the three exalted knowers as there are, one should 
cultivate all the apprehender aspects that are opposite classes of the aspects of the objects of 
                                             
 
132 See the dispute and reply under the section 2B2A -1: Having individually rejected doubts concerning a 
lack of purpose in  composing the treatise, explaining the general presentation of the subject matter, the three 
exalted knowers  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
325 observation of those [superimpositions] by mean s of a union of calm abiding  and special 
insight . 
 Someone asserts that what are to be cultivated through differentiation into the two —the 
object -aspects and the knower- aspects —are discordant and separate. This is without the 
slightest essence, because someone who asserts that the exalted wisdom realizing 
impermanence is to be familiarized with133 must also assert that impermanence is [to be] 
familiarized with; if someone asserts that impermanence is to be familiarized with but does 
not assert that the exa lted wisdom realizing that is [to be] familiarized with, then the bases 
would become empty.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1A- 2. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The entitinesses that are of the nature of the antidotes to the 
discordant classes apprehending p ermanence and so forth, the instances of the exalted 
wisdom observing impermanence and so forth, presented as the aspects themselves, are the characteristics. Furthermore, those, by way of the instances of the three exalted knowers of 
all, are asserted to be only of three types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a purpose for extensively explaining the divisions of the aspects in this chapter, 
because it is for the sake of knowing the antidotal characteristics. The subject —presenting 
the instances of the exalted wisdom  observing the aggregates as impermanent and so 
forth as the knower -aspects themselves —has a purpose, because it is for the sake of 
knowing the antidotal characteristics , the entitinesses that are of the nature of the 
antidotes to the discordant classes ap prehending the aggregates as permanent and so 
forth . The subject —furthermore, those knower -aspects, branches that are the antidotal 
characteristics —are asserted to be only of three types , because there are only three types  
by way of the instances of the three types of exalted knowers of all.

326 2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.2 Ranging from the aspect of non -existence until 
The aspect of the immovable , 
Those are explained as four with respect to each truth  
And as fifteen with respect to paths. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the sutra passages that indicate [the aspects] ranging from the aspect of non -
existence , “Bhagavan, this perfection of wisdom has gone to the other side that is non -
existence,”  until  the aspect of immovability , “this perfection of wisdom has gone to the 
other side that is immovability” —indicate the twenty -seven  aspects of a knower of bases, 
because those aspects of a knower of bases  are explained as four each with resp ect to 
each  of the first three  truths and as fifteen with respect to true paths.    
 Through these aspects, one should understand that the contemplations on (i) the 
drawbacks of impermanent aggregates that are sufferings of cyclic existence and (ii) the 
drawbacks of being tormented by the three types of sufferings and the like a re branches for 
training in the mentality of a being of middling capacity.  
 Regarding the exalted wisdom directly realizing the emptiness of a self of persons, there 
are divisions in to the first four aspects of true paths; from between (a) the exalted wisdom 
directly realizing the emptiness of apprehenders and apprehendeds being different substances and (b) the knowers of dualistic appearances as mistaken during subsequent 
attainment, there are divisions into the last eleven aspects of paths.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C. Commentary  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C1. Aspects of the first three truths 
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2. Aspects of true paths 
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C3. Summary of the enumeration [of the aspects]  
2B2C -2A1B-1A1B -1C1. Aspects of the first three truths 
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said.  Regarding that, in terms of the knower of all, the twelve aspects —
(1) not existing, (2) not produced, (3) devoid, (4) not oppressed, (5) non -abiding, (6) space, 
(7) not e xpressible, (8) without name, (9) without progression, (10) not appropriated, (11) 
not exhaustible, and (12) without production —are respectively the characteristics of 
impermanence and so forth of the three truths, sufferings and so forth. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding  the three types of knower- aspects that exist, there are twenty -seven  in terms of 
the knower of all . Sutra says: “ (1) Not existing  permanently, (2) true sufferings are not 
produced  ultimately, (3) devoid of a self that is a different object, and (4) not oppressed  
by a self that is its own entity.” These words indicating the four teach the aspects of true sufferings, because they teach respectively the four characteristics of true sufferings, impermanence and so forth.  
 The sutra words indicating the four —(5) non- abiding  in which craving, a cause of 
suffering , does not exist ultimately , (6) origins being like space  ultimately , (7) the causes of 
sufferings not being expressible  ultimately [through] investigation and analysis , (8) craving  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
327 and feelings, t he conditions for rebirth being  without name  ultimately —teach the aspects of 
true origins, because they teach respectively the four aspects of [true origins].  
 The sutra words indicating the aspects —(9) cessations being without progression  
ultimately , (10 ) the pacification of sufferings not being appropriated  by conditions , (11) 
sublime liberation not being exhaustible, and (12) cessation that is the dharmata free from 
stains being without production —teach the aspects of true cessations, because they teach  
respectively the four aspects of [true cessations].  
 
The twelve are  respectively the aspects of the three truths, sufferings and so forth , 
because, by way of the sutra passages, the knower- aspects are implied by the object -aspects, 
therefore both the obje ct-aspects and the knower- aspects are taught in sutra; regarding the 
assertion that only the object -aspects are indicated, it has been taught that it is well refuted 
by the Acarya Arya, because if that were to be the case, the contextual meaning would 
dege nerate . 
 The subject —the exalted wisdom of a bodhisattva directly realizing that sufferings do not 
exist permanently —is an aspect that observes the impermanent sufferings, because it is an 
exalted wisdom realizing that sufferings are impermanent.  Therefore, take the predicate 
of the probandum above as the sign for “It is an antidote to the superimposition that 
apprehends permanent sufferings.” Apply similarly to each [of the rest]. These [points] 
teach that these very knowers  of bases indicated on the occasion of the third chapter are 
cultivated by the applications in the continuum of a bodhisattva.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2. Aspects of true paths 
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2A. Brief indication of the divisions of true paths  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2B. Extensive explanation [of the divisions of true paths]  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2C. Summary [of the divisions of true paths]  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2A. Brief indication of the divisions of true paths  
COMMENTARY 
Because, as antidote to the afflictiv e obscurations, there is one uncontaminated path and, 
since solitary realizers are also included within the knower of all, as antidote to the knowledge obscurations there are the two: the contaminated path of meditation and the 
uncontaminated, there are th ree types of paths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are three types of true paths  of a knower of bases in this context, because, as 
antidote to the afflictive obscurations, there is one uncontaminated path of seeing and, 
as antidote to the knowledge obscurations of th ose solitary realizers, there are the two: 
the contaminated path of meditation and the uncontaminated path of seeing  
 There is no contradiction that the antidote to the knowledge obscuration s is taught in the 
context of indicating the aspects of the knower of bases, because, since the paths of solitary 
realizers are also included within the knower of all, it is necessary to indicate the antidote 
to the conception of apprehendeds [as external objects], which is a knowledge obscuration .  
 Since all the aspect s of the knower of bases are to be cultivated by bodhisattvas, the true 
path that is an antidote to the conception of apprehendeds [as external objects], which is a knowledge obscuration , is indicated in these true paths, and is explained here for the sake  of 
[making known that] bodhisattvas have generated it in their continua. However, for the sake 
of definitely understanding that the support of presentation of a knower of bases directly 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
328 realizing that apprehendeds are devoid of true existence is different , solitary realizers are 
mentioned; it is not for the sake of indicating that it is only a clear realization in the 
continuum of solitary realizers.  
 If one has not understood that the divisions of the path of the individual aryas are 
separate, one would n ot understand the existence of the many dissimilar purposes of 
cultivation for the sake of which bodhisattvas cultivate the aspects of the three exalted 
knowers.  
 Regarding this assertion of the great being Acarya [Haribhadra], a previous Tibetan 
asserted  that the clear realizations of solitary realizers are included within the knower of 
bases. This is unrelated, because these [points] indicate the objects of observation of a 
knower of bases that are included within true paths; there  is not even the slight est purpose  
for including the objects of observation of a knower of bases in a knower of bases; if such 
an inclusion in [a knower of bases] is necessary, then even the eight object- aspects of 
suffering s and origins belonging to [the objects of observation of a knower of bases] must 
be included in a knower of bases, [but] it cannot [include them].  
 
OPINION: If one takes these to explicitly indicate the entity of a knower of bases observing 
the aspects of true paths, then the aspects of true paths that are th e observed objects of a 
knower of bases would not have been explicitly indicated; if one were to accept that, it would contradict the contextual meaning, because this is the context of indicating the 
aspects of the four truths, the observed objects of a kn ower of bases. If all the paths that are 
the observed objects of a knower of bases are indicated, then it follows that the paths 
existing in the continua of the buddhas would also have been indicated.  
 
RESPONSE: The teaching that [the reply] “It is only t o be accepted” is to be analyzed. For, 
if the twenty -seven  knower -aspects that are in the entity of a bodhisattva’s knower of bases 
have not been explicitly indicated in this chapter, one would have to accept that all the 
aspects without exception of the k nowers of bases that are applications in complete aspects 
have not been explicitly indicated in the other chapters too; hence, in that case, if a 
bodhisattva has not cultivated all the aspects of the knower of bases without exception by 
means of the applic ations, then he would not generate in his continuum the clear realizations 
that are to be passed through having been viewed by knowing and seeing. That is included within the source of many fallacies, the branches of a knower of paths being incomplete and 
so forth. Therefore, it is outrageous to fling [such] a counterfeit repudiation to someone who 
possesses the non -erroneous instructions of the Ornament for Clear Realization  that has 
been transmitted from Venerable [Maitreya].

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2B -1. Dividing the antidotes to the afflictive obscurations into four  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding those, for the first, the four aspects of (1) absence  of agent, (2) absence  of 
knower, (3) absence  of transferer, and (4) absence  of taming  are respectively the 
characteristics of paths and so forth.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
329  
EXPLANATION  
Regarding those  three paths that exist , for the first path, there are four aspects ; the 
bodhisattva’s path of seeing that realizes with direc t cognition (1) the absence of a self of 
persons who is an agent  is the aspect of path of true paths, because it is a true path that 
proceeds to the city of nirvana.  
 Likewise, the bodhisattva’s paths of seeing, the aspects that directly realize (2) the 
absence  of a self of persons who is a  knower  of objects, (3) the absence  of a self of persons 
who is a transferer  from inferior states, and (4) the absence  of a self of persons who is a 
tamer  of afflictions, are respectively the characteristics of suitabil ity, achievement and 
deliverance of true paths and so forth , because they act as the direct antidotes to those 
conceptions of self of persons that have been explained. Similarly, apply as above to these the proof that they are the antidotes to the superimp ositions that wrongly engage the aspects 
of true paths. 
 Although the afflictive obscurations in the continuum of a bodhisattva are abandoned by 
the wisdom realizing emptiness, [the above aspects have been explained] for the sake of 
understanding the culti vation of the divisions of the classes of antidotes to the afflictive 
obscurations.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2B -2. The contaminated antidotes to the knowledge obscurations  
COMMENTARY 
For the second, the five aspects of (1) a dream, (2) an echo, (3) a double imag e, (4) a 
mirage, and (5) an illusion —respectively the characteristics of (1) being devoid of  
entitiness, (2) not having been produced, (3) not ceasing, (4) pacified from the beginning, 
and (5) having passed beyond sorrow by nature —are the antidotes to the knowledge 
obscurations in general . 
 
EXPLANATION  
For the second  path, the subject —the exalted wisdoms (realizing that apprehendeds are not 
external objects) which are included within a bodhisattva’s knower of subsequent 
attainment o f the path of meditation —are the antidotes to the knowledge obscurations 
that adhere to external objects, because (a) they realize tha t (1) forms and so forth being 
devoid of  entitiness  of external objects, like a dream, bear the characteristic of being a 
mere appearance that is a complete transformation of latencies; (2) forms and so forth, like 
an echo,  bear the characteristic of external objects not having been produced;  (3) [forms 
and so forth,] like a double  image, bear the characteristic of [external] objects not 
ceasing ; (4) [ forms and so forth,] like a mirage, bear the characteristic of [external] 
objects being pacified from the beginning ; and (5) [ forms and so forth,] like the aspect of 
an illusion , bear the characteristic of [external] objects  having passed beyond sorrow by 
nature , and (b) they realize through the force  of those awarenesses that, while the [external] 
objects do not exist, there are mere mistaken appearances as [external] objects.  
 The words “ in general ” [in the commentary above ] cause one to understand that each of 
them is also the antidote to the general conception of apprehendeds [as external objects].  
 
OPINION: If this path of meditation, the antidote to the conception of apprehendeds [as 
external objects] explained here , were included within the continua of solitary realizers, it 
would contradict the earlier explanation that it is generated in the continua of bodhisattvas. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
330 On the other hand, if it were included within the continua of bodhisattvas, since it has been 
explained above as a clear realization that is to be passed through having been viewed by 
knowing and seeing, it would be unreasonable to indicate its continuity, a path of meditation that acts to familiarize with it through diligent familiarization; otherwise, it w ould be similar 
with respect to a path bearing the aspect of impermanence and so forth.   
RESPONSE: There is no fault; it is merely explained that, although it is not familiarized 
with by means of diligent familiarization by a bodhisattva on the occasion o f the path of 
meditation, that path is produced in the continua of a bodhisattva on the path of meditation in general.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -1C2B -3. The uncontaminated antidotes to the knowledge obscurations  
COMMENTARY 
For the third, the six aspects of (1) the absence  of being thoroughly afflicted, (2) the absence  
of being completely pure, (3) the absence  of being sullied, (4) the absence  of elaborations, 
(5) the absence  of clinging, and (6) the immovable are respectively the individually 
ascertaining antidotes t o the conceptualizations, the knowledge obscurations: (1) the 
thoroughly afflicted, (2) the completely pure, (3) the latencies of afflictions, (4) the 
elaborations of forms and so forth, (5) one’s own realization, and (6) the degenerated. 
 
EXPLANATION  
For the third  path that is related to the explanation of the antidotes to the afflictive 
obscurations above , the six are respectively the individually ascertaining antidotes to 
the conceptualizations  of apprehendeds. The subject —the exalted wisdoms (directly 
realizing that apprehendeds are not external objects) in the continuum of a bodhisattva on 
the path of seeing —abandon the knowledge obscurations  that are  conceptions  of 
apprehendeds: having observed [apprehendeds] as external objects, adhering to (1) the 
thoroughly afflicted  due to grasping and so forth, (2) the completely pure  due to faith and 
so forth , (3) the latencies of afflictions, (4) the elaborations that are divisions of [external] 
objects, forms and so forth, (5) clinging that relishes the taste of one’s own realization  
that adheres [to them] as [external] objects , and (6) that very realization that was attained 
but has  degenerated , respectively (i) because they directly realize (1) the absence of being  
thoroughly afflicted  by external objects , and (ii) because they are aspects  that newly 
realize with direct cognition:  (2) the absence  of being  completely pure  due to external 
objects , (3) the absence  of being  sullied  by the latencies that are included within [external] 
objects , (4) the absence  of el aborations  that are divisions of [external] objects , (5) the 
absence  of clinging  that relishes the taste by a realization that adheres [to them] as objects , 
and (6) the absence  of other [external] objects, apprehendeds  of the immovable in which 
realization s attained do not  degenerate.

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.3  With respect to the causes and paths, sufferings and  
Cessations, “Respectively  
Those are eight, seven,  
Five, and sixteen” is declared.  
 
EXPLANATIO N 
There are thirty -six aspects of true paths, because with respect to  the paths of the causes, 
true origins and true paths,  and the results, true sufferings and  true cessations, 
“Respectively those  aspects are eight, seven, five, and sixteen” is declared  in sutra . It is 
taught in general in sutra: “Subhuti said, ‘Bhagavan, it is like this; this perfection of wisdom 
is a perfection that is separated from attachment’” until  “This perfection of wisdom is a 
perfection of the emptiness, the entitiness of non -things.”  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -2C. Commentary

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -2C1. General indication  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. That true origins and paths are the causes, and true sufferings and 
cessations the results (based upon the thoroughly afflicted and the other class) are indicated 
by means of the meaning. One should understand that true origins, paths, sufferings, and 
cessations are respectively “eig ht aspects” and so forth.   
 
EXPLANATION  
There are thirty -six aspects of the knower of paths, because one should understand that 
the aspects of the knower of paths that are based upon true origins, paths, sufferings, and 
cessations are respectively “eight, seven, five and sixteen aspects” and so forth.

332 QUALM: The sequence of the four truths here disagrees with explanations elsewhere. 
Hence it is flawed. 
RESPONSE: There is a special purpose for having indicated the sequence of the four truths 
in this manner, because they are indicated by means of  the stages of the  meaning of cause 
and result (i) for the sake of understanding the stages of entering cyclic existence, that true 
origins are the causes and sufferings are the results  based upon  the thoroughly 
afflicted  class,  and (ii) for the sake of understanding the stages of turning away from cyclic 
existence, that true paths are the causes and true cessations are the  results based upon 
the other class, the completely pure. Regarding the indication of sufferings  first elsewhere, 
[the four truths] are indicated in accordance with the stages of the object -possessors, the 
clear realizations, whereas here they are indicated in accordance with the stages of generation. Hence you should understand the purposes of the t wo [types of indication] from 
the respective points of view.

COMMENTARY 
With respect to that, regarding the aspects of (1) separated from attachment, (2) not abiding, (3) pacified, (4) the non -existence of attachment, (5) the non -existence of hatred, (6) the 
non-existence of ignorance, (7) the non -existence of the afflicted, and (8) the non -existence 
of sentient beings, respectively, there are three, three, one , and one serving as antidotes to:  
• those which are cause: (1) aspiration, (2) attachment, and (3) joy;  
• those which are origin: (4) attachment, (5) hatred, and (6) ignorance;  
• that which is strong production: (7) ideations
134; and  
• that which is condition: (8) strong adherence to sentient beings.  
In that way these are the  eight aspects of true causes.  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to that, the subject —the exalted wisdom directly realizing selflessness of 
persons in the continuum of a bodhisattva on the path of seeing —respectively:  
• is the antidote to those which are  the aspects of cause  of origins : (1) aspiration  that 
craves for future objects , (2) attachment that craves not to be separated , and (3) craving 
that takes joy in rebirth, because it is an exalted wisdom of a bodhisattva that: (1) is 
separated from the attachment of craving that desires to obtain the attributes of the 
desire realm , (2) doe s not abide  in the manner of adhering to not being separated from 
the attributes of the desire realm , and (3) has pacified  craving that takes joy in the body ;  
• serves as the antidote to those which are the aspects of origin: (4) attachment, (5) 
hatred, and  (6) ignorance, because it is a bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom directly 
realizing: (4) the non -existence of the object of adherence of attachment, (5) the non-
existence of the object of adherence of hatred, and (6) the non- existence of the object 
of adherenc e of ignorance;

333 • is the antidote to that which is the aspect of strong production: (7) the ideations  that

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -2C2B. Indicating true paths by way of thesis and  proof  
COMMENTARY 
The aspects of (1) immeasurable, (2) without being associated with  the two extremes, (3) 
not different, (4) without holding as supreme, (5) not conceptualizing, (6) without measurement, and (7) without  grasping are respectively:  
(1) the path that grants the opportunity to all sentient beings,  (2) how it grants the opportunity to all sentient beings,  
(3) that which is suitable,  
(4) how it is suitable,  
(5) that which is achievement,  
(6) how it is achievement, and  
(7) that which is del iverance.  
There are two, two, two , and one entitinesses of those. In that way these are the  seven 
aspects of true paths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject—the path of seeing of bodhisattvas —is superior to the true paths of hearers, 
because it is a path that grants  the opportunity of liberation  to all sentient beings . 
Alternatively, the subject —that [as above] —is the aspect of (1) a path that grants the 
opportunity of liberation  to all sentient beings , because it is a path that grants the 
opportunity of liberation t o (1) immeasurable  sentient beings.  
 
QUALM: (2) How  does that [path] grant the opportunity  of liberation to all sentient 
beings ?  
RESPONSE: It grants the opportunity of liberation to immeasurable sentient beings, 
because it grants the opportunity of libera tion to immeasurable sentient beings (2) without 
being associated with the two extremes  of samsara and nirvana. It is the aspect of (3) that 
which is suitable , because it is an exalted wisdom directly realizing that all phenomena are 
(3) not different in being empty of true existence. 
 
QUALM: (4) How is it suitable?  
RESPONSE: Taking the sign above as the predicate, [i.e. it is an exalted wisdom directly 
realizing that all phenomena are not different in being empty of true existence], because it is 
an exalted wisdom directly realizing freedom from elaborations (4) without holding the 
paths of hearers and solitary realizers as supreme.  It is the aspect of (5) that which is 
achievement, because it is an exalted wisdom directly realizing that all phenomena are 
devoid of  true existence and (5) does not conceptualize  them as truly existent. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
334  
QUALM: (6) How is it achievement?  
RESPONSE: It does not conceptualize them as truly existent, because it is an exalted wisdom directly realizing that all phenomena are (6) without measurement by valid 
cognition.  
 
It is the aspect of (7) that which is deliverance, because it is an exalted wisdom directly 
realizing the aspect of being  (7) without grasping  to all phenomena as existing ultimately.  
These are the seven aspects o f true paths , because there are two, two, two , and one 
entitinesses  of those  four aspects of paths  in that way.   
 They are explained like that, in a manner for the ease of understanding; actually the 
former seven are those which are to be explained while t he latter seven are those which act 
to explain. For the first three aspects of true paths, a thesis as well as a reason indicating 
each of path, suitability, and achievement have been explained, but for the aspect of 
deliverance merely a thesis ha s been ex plained ; a reason has not been indicated. This i s 
related to the mentality of the proximate trainees; it is not that a reason does not exist.  
 The seven aspects of true paths which have been explained in sutra  are: (i) the entities of 
the former [of each pair] of the first three [pairs] from among those which act to explain (in 
the explanation in that way by Acarya  Haribhadra), the questions, (ii) the reasons that are 
the latter [of each pair of the first three pairs from among those which act to explain], the 
explanations with respect to [those] questions, and (iii) the entity of the aspect of deliverance, a question. In summary, the following is explained:  
 
QUESTION: What is the aspect of path?  
ANSWER: “Immeasurable.”  
QUESTION: How is that the aspect of path?  
ANSWER: “Without being associated with the two extremes.”  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -2C2C. Explaining true sufferings by way of specific and general characteristics 
COMMENTARY 
The aspects of (1) impermanent, (2) suffering, (3) empty, (4) selfless, and (5) the fifth, the entitiness that is an aspect being devoid of  characteristics; in that way the aspects of true 
sufferings are five.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the five appropriated aggregates — 
(1) are impermanent because they disintegrate from the point of view of continuity as well 
as from the point of view of an instant of time, (2) are suffering  because they are objects to be discarded that do not concord with the 
aryas, (3) are empty  because they are devoid of a self who is an agent that is a different object, 
(4) are the aspect of selfless  because they are phenomena that are not established as the 
entity of a self of persons, and 
(5) are the fifth, the entitiness that is an aspect being devoid of  characteristi cs, because 
they are an aspect that is empty of a truly existent entity ultimately.  
In that way the aspects of true sufferings are five. A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom that 
directly realizes [each of] those aspects is an aspect of the knower of paths.

phenomena are adhered to as truly existent .

emerges” refers to the aspect of definitely emerging only to liberation in which the adherence to phenomena as truly existent has been ceased; it means that, with respect to 
acting to attain that definite emergence, one att ains it by ceasing the adherence to the true 
existence, which is mistaken regarding definitions, definienda , and time, and then 
familiarizing with that.

cessations, separations from adventitious stains. It is obvious that the meanings are similar 
regardless of whichever is done.

2B2C -2A1B-1A1B -3A2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.4  Those ranging from the close placements of mindfulness  
[Until ] the final aspect of buddha, 
Are divided into three [types of] exalted knowers of all  
In accordance with true paths ; therefore  
 
4.5  For learners, bodhisa ttvas, and  
Buddhas respectively, 
Thirty -seven, thirty -four, and  
Thirty -nine are asserted.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the final knower- aspects  ranging from the close placements of mindfulness , 
“Subhuti said, ‘Bhagavan, this perfection of wisdom has gone to the other side that is the 
close placements of mindfulness’; the Bhagavan says, ‘Because body, feelings, minds, and phenomena are unobservable,” until the final aspects of a buddha, “‘This Mot her has gone 
to the other side that is enlightenment; the Bhagavan says, ‘Because of being enlightened with respect to all phenomena in all ways,” —are the aspects of the  exalted knower of all 
aspects, because that there are divisions into thirty -seven, thi rty-four, and thirty -nine is  
asserted in sutra . 
  Even though [the aspects of the exalted knower of all aspects] are exalted wisdoms that 
are exalted knowers of all aspects, it is appropriate to divide them into three [types] by 
means of sets, because the supports of presentation are posited from the point of view of 
being respectively related to the learner  hearers, the bodhisattvas  and the buddhas . It is 
appropriate for the [aspects of the] exalted knowers of all aspects to be divided into three 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
338 [types] b y means of divisions of the lineage of the persons who are the supports, because the 
exalted knowers of all aspects are divided into three [types of] exalted knowers of all by 
means of lineage, therefore  there are three [types]. The reason is that the exal ted knowers 
of all aspects are divided into three [types] in accordance with the three [types of] 
dissimilar true paths  (that are included within the exalted knowers in the continua of the 
three [types of] persons) that exist.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3A3. Commentar y 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. It is said that those ranging from the close placements of 
mindfulness [ until] the final aspect of buddha, by means of being paths included within the 
three [types of] exalted knowers of all, because all arya persons  are included within the 
exalted knowers of all aspects, according to the enumeration are asserted to be thirty -seven 
aspects for hearers, thirty -four for bodhisattvas, and thirty -nine for buddhas.  
 
EXPLANATION  
It is said that the culminated exalted wisdoms  ranging from the close placements of 
mindfulness until  the final aspect of buddha, by means of being paths of the three 
[types of] exalted knowers of all, are asserted to be one hundred and ten in accordance 
with the thirty -seven aspects for hearers, thir ty-four for bodhisattvas, and thirty -nine 
for buddhas  that exist, because they are divided into the three [types of] exalted knowers of 
all by means of the different supports of presentation. It is appropriate for the exalted 
knowers of all aspects to be d ivided into the three [types of] exalted knowers of all, because 
the exalted knowers of all arya persons are included within  the exalted knowers of all 
aspects, in the manner of possession of progressive excellent qualities.  
 QUALM: Are not the close placements of mindfulness and the like explained to be the 
excellent qualities of the path of accumulation in the context of the extensive explanation? 
Why are the aspects of the  exalted knower of all aspects divided into three [ty pes]?  
RESPONSE: There is no fault. The  thirty -seven  harmonies with enlightenment are posited 
for the sake of understanding that these are in common with th ose of  hearers, since they are 
concordant in property in being paths of the seven sets, the paths th at fully investigate things 
and so forth. The six [sets], the path of antidotes and so forth, are posited as knowers of 
paths from the point of view of bodhisattvas, the persons who are the support, for the sake 
of understanding that they are concordant in  property with those of bodhisattvas.  
 Therefore, if the 110 aspects of the  exalted knower of all aspects had not been divided in 
accordance with the lower arya persons, one would not understand on which occasions of 
the lower paths those three exalted kn owers existing in the continuum of a buddha are 
initially generated, the reason for differentiating three exalted knowers, and so forth. Hence, they are divided into three sets from the point of view of the three persons.  
 At the time of making presentati on of three different exalted knowers from the point of 
view of different supports, although it is appropriate to posit the  thirty -seven  harmonies with 
enlightenment as a class of hearer’s realizations, in this context these modes of cultivating 
the close placements of mindfulness initially on the occasion of the path of accumulation are 
to be cultivated by bodhisattvas.

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -1. Close placements of mindfulness 
COMMENTARY 
Regarding those, for the knower of all, the paths that fully investigate things because one 
should engage in the four truths: full examination of body, feelings, minds, and phenomena, 
by way of specific and general characteristics, the four aspects of the close placements of mindfulness.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding those  three [types of] aspects of the three exalted knowers with respect to the 
exalted knower of all aspects , for th e knower of all, there are seven sets. First of all, 
regarding the paths that fully investigate  things, the body, minds, and so forth, there are  
the four aspects of the close placements of mindfulness , because with respect to that full 
examination  with wisdom by way of (i) specific characteristics: the  body  being unclean , 
feelings  being suffering , minds  being impermanent , and phenomena being selfless, and 
(ii) general characteristics : impermanence, suffering, emptiness, selflessness, and 
emptiness of true existence, there are four [types] acting to focus again and again through 
mindfulness . There is a purpose for examining them in that manner, because , having 
understood the reality of the four noble truths , one should engage in meditating on them . 
 By closely placing oneself through mindfulness on the body , one will engage true 
sufferings, because having understood that the body is in the nature of pervasive compounded suffering through that, one will generate the wish to be free from it. By closely 
placing oneself through mindfulness on feelings , one will engage true origins, because, 
having seen that craving is induced by feelings, one will generate the wish to abandon craving. By closely placing oneself through mindfulness on minds , one will engage  true 
cessations, because, having realized that the mind (that is free from a self) is produced and 
ceases momentarily through that, one will not have the fear that the self will be severed, so 
one will generate the wish to actualize cessations. By closely  placing oneself through 
mindfulness on phenomena , one will engage true paths, because, having realized that 
phenomena of the thoroughly afflicted class are to be abandoned and phenomena of the 
completely pure class are to be relied upon, one will develop the wish to cultivate the paths. 
 The reason for observing these, the body and so forth, at first is that childish beings 
conceptualize (i) the body as a thing which is the abode of a self, (ii) feelings as the [bases 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
340 of]136 utilizations of a self, (iii) min ds as a thing that is a self, and (iv) phenomena as the 
bases of a thoroughly afflicted [self] and a completely pure self. Therefore, for the sake of 
overcoming these, they —having realized with wisdom the specific characteristics, the body 
being unclean an d so forth, and the general characteristics, the body being impermanent and 
so forth, and empty of true existence —place themselves single -pointedly on them through 
mindfulness.   
 These are cultivated by bodhisattvas beginning from the occasion of the smal l level of the 
path of accumulation. Since the culmination of these are the aspects of the exalted knower 
of all aspects, there are many purposes for positing the close placements of mindfulness 
here: (i) to make known that the  thirty -seven  harmonies with enlightenment are generated in 
the continua of bodhisattvas, (ii) to make known that they belong to the class of hearer’s realizations at the time of differentiating among the classes of clear realizations from the point of view of the persons who are the supports, and (iii) having known that they 
transform into exalted knowers of all aspects at the time of culmination due to cultivation 
concordant with those by bodhisattvas, to realize that in an exalted knower of all aspects 
there is a knower of bases con cordant with that generated in hearers. The principal purposes 
are just these.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -2. Four correct abandonings  
COMMENTARY  
After that, because joyous effort with regard to engagement will arise, regarding the paths 
that arise from exertion:  non-virtue and virtue, that which has been generated and that which 
has not been generated, respectively the joyous effort that is the cause of: (1) correct 
abandonment, (2) non -generation, (3) increase, and (4) generation, the four aspects of the 
correct  abandonings.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Having explained the close placements of mindfulness, after these, regarding the paths that arise from exertion, there are  the four aspects of the correct abandonings. There is 
a reason for indicating them in such an order, becau se the joyous effort that is enthusiasm 
for discarding the objects of abandonment and adopting the antidotes with regard to 
engagement in discarding and adoption with respect to the four truths will arise. 
 Correct abandoning has four aspects, because there are four that are in the entity of the 
joyous effort that is the cause of: (1 -2) correct abandonment of  non-virtue , the 
thoroughly afflicted class that has been generated, and non -generation of that which has 
not been generated, and (3-4) increase and generation, respectively , of virtue , the 
completely pure class , that has been generated and that  has not been generated.   
 Further, it has been taught in sutra that, for the sake of (i) abandoning non -virtuous 
qualities that have been generated, and not g enerating those that have not been generated, 
and (ii) increasing the virtuous qualities that have been generated, and generating those that 
have not been generated, one generates aspiration, makes effort, initiates joyous effort, 
strongly holds the mind, and sets the mind. “Aspiration” refers to the foundation for joyous 
effort. “Effort” refers to joyous effort that pays attention to the reasons for calm abiding , 
strong holding and equanimity. “Initiating joyous effort” refers to initiating joyous effort i n 
generating the antidotes that act to eliminate laxity and excitement. The actual antidotes are 
                                             
 
136 Annotated in accordance with Jetsun Chokyi Gyeltsen’s Ocean of Sport . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
341 indicated by the last two: when there is laxity within the mind, as an antidote to that, one 
holds an uplifted mind through an attention that has become lucid, such as the 
contemplation on the excellent qualities of the buddhas; and when there is excitement, one sets the mind on the object of observation by withdrawing inwards the outwardly scattered 
mind through an attention that has become despondent, such as the contemplation on the 
drawbacks of samsara.  
 For the sake of understanding (i) the gaining of the four correct abandonings that have a 
special potency , and (ii) the results of cultivating the close placements of mindfulness, the 
four correct abandonings  are correlated with the middling level of the path of accumulation. 
However, the fully -qualified [correct abandonings] are generated even on the small level of 
the path of accumulation.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -3. Four aspects of the legs of magical emanation  
COMMENTARY 
Because possessing joyous effort makes the mind serviceable, regarding the paths that thoroughly train in meditative stabilization: the meditative stabilizations of (1) aspiration, 
(2) joyous effort, (3) intention, and (4) analysis are the four aspects of the legs of magical 
emanation possessing the applications that abandon.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The paths that thoroughly train137 in meditative stabilization are the four aspects of the 
legs of magical emanation. The reason for explaining them after the c orrect abandonings is 
because possessing joyous effort in discarding the objects of abandonment and adopting 
the antidotes  makes the mind serviceable  through meditative stabilization. There are four 
legs of magical emanation, because, as antidotes to the five faults, there are meditative 
stabilizations  possessing the eight applications  that abandon [the five faults] : [the 
meditative stabilizations of]  (1) contact with a single -pointed mind in dependence on the 
meditative stabilization of aspiration, (2) con tact with a single -pointed mind in dependence 
on joyous effort, (3) contact with a single -pointed mind in dependence on that attained 
from birth [due to] having previously cultivated meditative stabilization, the meditative 
stabilization of intention, and (4) contact with a single -pointed mind in dependence on 
discernment of phenomena that have been taught by others, the meditative stabilization of  
analysis.  
 The eight applications that abandon [the five faults] are:  
(1) aspiration,  
(2) exertion, (3) faith, 
(4) pliancy, 
(5) mindfulness, 
(6) introspection,  
(7) intention, and  
(8) equanimity. 
 
From among the five faults, that which acts as the hindrance to initially applying oneself in 
cultivation is laziness. Hence, the actual antidote acting to eliminate that  is exertion. Its 
foundation is aspiration; the cause of aspiration is faith; and the result of exertion is pliancy.  
                                             
 
137 Spyod emended to read sbyong  in accordance with the commentary above.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
342  At the time of applying oneself in the cultivation of meditative stabilization, since there is 
the fault of forgetting the instructions, a s antidote to it, there is mindfulness. When one 
engages in the actual [cultivation], since there are the faults of laxity and excitement, as the 
antidote to them, mainly there is mindfulness; however there is introspection which realizes 
[the presence of] laxity and excitement. When laxity and excitement occur, since there is the 
fault of not applying the antidote, as antidote to it, there is intention. When one engages in 
meditative stabilization naturally without needing to employ exertion, since there i s the fault 
of applying [the antidote], as antidote to it, there is equanimity [in which the mind is] set 
without exertion.  
  Since these meditative stabilizations are the main bases for accomplishing magical 
emanation, they are [known as] the legs of magi cal emanation. For the sake of 
understanding that these become more and more outstanding progessively over the three 
[levels of the] path [of accumulation], they are correlated with the great level of the path of 
accumulation. However, know that they are a ccomplished even from the occasion of the 
small level of the path of accumulation.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -4. Five aspects of the powers  
COMMENTARY 
Because, having thoroughly trained the mind, the preparations of the heat and the peak will 
arise, regarding the  paths that prepare for clear realization, the entitiness es of the heat and 
the peak: (1) faith, (2) joyous effort, (3) mindfulness, (4) meditative stabilization, and (5) 
wisdom, the five aspects of the powers. 
  
EXPLANATION  
After the legs of magical emana tion, regarding  the paths that prepare for clear 
realization  of the truth, there are  the five aspects of the powers . The reason for indicating 
them in such an order is because, having thoroughly trained the mind  by way of 
meditative stabilization , the prep arations of the heat and the peak that empower one with 
respect to the clear realization of the truth will arise. The subject —the powers that are the 
entitiness es of the heat and the peak —are of five, because there are the powers of: (1) 
faith  of convictio n in the truths , (2) joyous effort that is an enthusiasm for discarding [the 
objects of abandonment] and adopting [the antidotes] , (3) mindfulness  that does not forget 
the object of observation and aspect , (4) meditative stabilization  that is a single -pointed 
mind , and (5) wisdom  that acts to discern the mode of abidance of the truth . The rest will 
be explained below.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -5. Aspects of the strengths 
COMMENTARY  
Because having attained the heat and so forth, the forbearance and the supreme mu ndane 
quality will arise, regarding the paths that connect to clear realization, the entitinesses of the forbearance and the supreme mundane quality: (1) faith, (2) joyous effort, (3) mindfulness, 
(4) meditative stabilization, and (5) wisdom, the five aspe cts of the strengths.  
 
EXPLANATION  
After the powers , regarding  the paths that connect to clear realization , there are  the five 
aspects of the strengths . The reason for indicating them in such an order is because, 
having attained the heat and so forth that are in the entity of the powers , the entity of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
343 strengths, the forbearance and the supreme mundane quality will arise. This is because 
the five, faith and so forth, that are the entitinesses of the forbearance and the supreme 
mundane quality will arise. Further, they are the five  strengths of: (1) faith  of conviction 
in the truth s, (2) joyous effort for the sake of quickly realizing the truth , (3) mindfulness  
that does not forget the objects of observation and aspects of the truth , (4) meditative 
stabilization  that is a single -pointed mind , and (5) wisdom that individually discerns the 
aspects of the truth . 
 These five are governing factors that empower one with respect to clear realization of the 
truth. However, the difference between them and the  former [powers] is that, since manifest 
non-faith, laziness, forgetfulness, distraction, and corrupt wisdom are suppressed on this 
occasion, even during subsequent attainment when one does not cultivate faith and so forth, 
strong manifest non -faith and so  forth will not arise. During the occasions of the heat and 
the peak, although the discordant classes will not arise at the time when the five powers are 
manifest, they arise manifestly in a frequent manner during subsequent attainment. 
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -6. Aspects of the branches of enlightenment  
COMMENTARY 
Because having realized the four, the heat and so forth, the path that sees the truths will 
arise, regarding  the paths of clear realization: correct (1) mindfulness, (2) discernment of 
phenomena, (3) joyous effort, (4) joy, (5) pliancy, (6) meditative stabilization, and (7) equanimity, the seven aspects of the branches of enlightenment. 
 
EXPLANATION  
After the powers and strengths , regarding the paths of clear realization of the truth there 
are the seven aspects of the branches of enlightenment. The reason for indicating them in 
such an order is because, having realized and attained the four, the heat and so forth, the 
entities of the powers and the strengths, the path that sees the truths, the entity of the 
branches of enlightenment  will arise. Further, they are the branches of enlightenment  of: 
correct (1) mindfulness, the branch of abidance that acts not to be distracted to anything 
other than the observation of the truth, (2) discernment of p henomena  that newly realizes 
with direct cognition selflessness, the branch of nature , (3) joyous effort, the branch of 
definite emergence, (4) joy, the branch of benefit, benefitting the body and mind, and (5-7) 
pliancy, meditative stabilization, and equa nimity, the branches of basis, foundation and 
entity that are without affliction. 
 Further, by way of the branch of enlightenment of correct mindfulness: (i) through 
observing sufferings, one seeks to become devoid of them and abides in mindfulness without  forgetfulness; (ii) through observing origins, one seeks to be free from attachment 
to them and abides [in mindfulness without forgetfulness]; (iii) through observing cessations, one seeks to actualize them and abides [in mindfulness without forgetfulness ]; 
and (iv) through observing paths that act to cease sufferings, one seeks to cultivate them and 
abides in mindfulness without forgetfulness. Know the mode of cultivation in that way 
accordingly also for the remaining six.  
  Understand that, through observing the four noble truths by means of the seven branches 
in that manner, the branches that are the causes for hearer lineage -bearers and so forth to 
achieve their respective enlightenment are of seven types. Here, however, they are mainly to 
be cultivated  by bodhisattvas.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
344 2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B1A -7. Aspects of the branches of aryas’ paths  
COMMENTARY 
Because, having thoroughly known the seeing of the truth, the path of meditation will arise, 
regarding  the paths of pure deliverance: right  (1) view, (2) thought, (3) speech, (4) aim of 
actions, (5) livelihood, (6) exertion, (7) mindfulness, and (8) meditative stabilization, the eight aspects of the eight branches of aryas’ paths. 
 
EXPLANATION  
After having explained the branches of enlightenment , regarding the paths  of pure 
deliverance, the eight aspects of the eight branches of aryas’ paths  will be explained. 
The reason is because, having thoroughly known the seeing of the truth newly with 
direct cognition , the path of meditation will arise. 
 Further, these are the branches of aryas’ paths of: (1) right view, affirmation during 
subsequent attainment of the dharmata realized during meditative equipoise, (2) right  
thought,  the motivation for speech that reveals to others that which one has realized, (3) 
[right ] speech, teaching others the realizations as they have been realized by oneself,  and 
pure actions of speech, (4) right  aim of actions,  pure actions of body, abandoning killing 
and so forth,  (5) right  livelihood, pure actions of body and speech, abandoning  hypocrisy, 
flattery, and so forth, (6) right  exertion, exertion in the antidotes that abandon the objects to 
be abandoned by the path of meditation, (7) right  mindfulness that does not forget the 
reasons for calm abiding , strong holding and equanimity , an d (8) right  meditative 
stabilization , cultivation for the sake of achieving special excellent qualities, the 
clairvoyances and so forth .  
 Know that these eight branches are subsumed under (i) the four branches of affirmation, 
engendering of understanding,  engendering of belief in others, and antidote to the discordant 
classes, and (ii) also the three: training in ethics, training in the mind, and training in wisdom.

paths of the knower of all.

345 2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B2A -6. Paths of buddhas  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B2A -1. Paths of antidotes  
COMMENTARY  
Regarding the knower of paths, the paths of antidotes are: (1) the antidote to that produced 
by view, (2) the antidote to conceptualizations of the signs of that, and (3) the antidote to 
wishing for the three realms. Regarding the entitinesses that are lik e the aforesaid, 
respectively: (1) the entitinesses of the aspects of emptiness and selflessness are the first door of liberation, (2) the entitinesses of true cessations and paths are the second; and (3) 
the entitinesses of the aspects of impermanence and  suffering and of true origins are the 
third. In that way, these are the three aspects of the three doors of liberation.   
 
EXPLANATION  
From among the six regarding the knower of paths, the paths of antidotes are  divided 
into the three aspects of the three  doors of liberation , [and] also the three antidotes to the 
discordant classes. In that way: (1) the entitinesses of the aspects of emptiness and 
selflessness, the exalted wisdom directly realizing selflessness in the continuum of a 
bodhisattva arya  are th e first door of liberation , because they are (1) the direct antidotes 
to that produced by the view  of self by means of realizing with direct cognition the non -
existence of a self that is a different entity and object; the subject —the exalted wisdom 
directl y realizing (2) the eight entitinesses of the aspects of true cessations and paths —
are the second door of liberation, because they are (2) the direct antidotes to the 
conceptualizations that are improper apprehensions of the signs that bear the reason of 
that view of self; the subject —the exalted wisdom directly realizing (3) impermanence 
and suffering of true sufferings and the four entitinesses of the aspects of true origins —
are the third door of liberation, because they are (3) the antidotes to wishing for the 
three realms  as final objects to be attained .  
 Although the three doors of liberation are final exalted wisdoms that are exalted knowers 
of all aspects concordant with the true paths of bodhisattvas, classes of these exist in hearers and solitary realizers; they are included in the knower of paths for the sake of bodhisattvas 
cultivating the full complete path.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B2A -2. Paths of emanation  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding the paths of emanation: “Because of (1) not destroying and (2) destroying th e 
discrimination of form, respectively the internal (1) having form and (2) not having form 
views external forms,” these two liberations as antidotes to the obscurations to emanation, 
and (3) as antidotes to those thorough afflictions of respectively likin g and disliking to 
emanate attractive and unattractive forms, the door of liberation of the attractive, “Having manifested with  the body, having made it complete, one abides,” one liberation. In that way, 
these are the three aspects of the doors of liberat ion. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding the paths of emanation, the three aspects of the doors of liberation are  the 
three: (1 -2) these two, the liberation in which that having form views forms, and the 
liberation in which that not having form views forms, and (3) one  liberation  of the 
attractive in that way.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
346  “In dependence on the mind of a concentration or a formless absorption, the internal 
yogi having closely placed himself in the discrimination of himself having form , 
[generates] a concomitant meditative stabiliza tion that imagines forms —good and bad, big 
and small, and so forth —wherever he desires to emanate them; and the yogi having placed 
himself in the discrimination of himself not  having form , of merely the aggregates of the 
four names, emanates  external forms  as he desires, and  views them ”; these two 
liberations as antidotes to the obscurations to emanation  that [cause one to be] unable to 
emanate as one desires; in dependence on the mind of either a concentration or a formless 
absorption,  as antidotes to thos e thorough afflictions of respectively liking to emanate  
attractive forms and disliking to emanate unattractive forms , having familiarized with 
the relativeness of attractive and unattractive forms and with how they are subsequently 
related in the sense of  being mutually one essence, by eliminating the discrimination of the 
attractive and the unattractive as being absolutes and the discrimination of them as being 
established as different by entitiness, eventually imagining all appearances of forms as 
being one taste in being attractive is the door of liberation of the attractive. “Having 
manifested with the body  of wisdom , through having become freed from the obscurations 
to the first three liberations, having  made  it complete, one abides.”  
 Aryas frequently  abide by way of the two, this [door of liberation of the attractive] and 
the liberation of cessation. Hence with respect to these two, it has been taught in sutra, 
“Having manifested with the body , one abides completely”; the others are not taught.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B2A -3. Paths of abiding happily in the present life  
COMMENTARY 
With respect to the paths of abiding happily in the present life, regarding the nature of the 
path that abides in accordance with liberation, there are four aspects of the formless 
absorptions; and regarding the nature of the path that abides in peace, there is one aspect 
that ceases discrimination and feeling. In that way, there are five aspects.  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to the paths in which the body and mind abide happily in this present life, 
there are five aspects. There are four formless liberations and one liberation of cessation in 
that way. Regarding the nature of the path that acts as the cause for a path of liberation 
and abides in accordance with  uncontaminated liberation  without being polluted by the 
afflictions of its own level, there are four formless liberations, because they are objects that are freed from the obscurations to relishing the taste of their own levels. This in turn is 
because there are four aspects of the  formless absorptions  included within the actual 
[absorptions].  
 Regarding this, although bodhisattvas actually enter absorption for the sake of themselves 
abiding happily in that life for others’ welfare, the object of intent, they never actualize the 
fruitions of these [absorptions], (i) because bodhisattva aryas have  fully exhausted the 
branches for taking rebirth in cyclic existence through the force of contaminated karma of 
the formless realm, and (ii) because the formless realm is not a place for subduing sentient 
beings.  
 Regarding the nature of the path that abi des in peace  by having pacified coarse 
discrimination and feeling, the one liberation  that ceases discrimination and feeling  is 
actualized for the sake of becoming free from the obscurations to ceasing discrimination and 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
347 feeling. Regarding this, even heare r and solitary realizer aryas enter the absorption only for 
the sake of abiding happily in the present life.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B2A -4. Supramundane paths  
COMMENTARY  
Regarding the supramundane paths, there are nine: the four concentrations, the formless 
absorptions, and the absorption of cessation.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding the supramundane paths, there are nine  serial absorptions, because there are 
the nine : the four concentrations, the four  formless absorptions, and the absorption of 
cessation  that are in the  entity of supramundane paths. The eight liberations can be 
generated into the entity of supramundane paths of bodhisattva aryas, while the absorption of cessation is posited as a supramundane path since it is attained through the force of a 
supramundane p ath. 
 Regarding the explanation of the expression “serial absorptions,” they are called such 
because one enters absorption into the later one immediately after the previous one; later on 
when one has attained mastery over them, one enters them also in a le aping manner.   
 Other great trailblazers, interpreting the meaning of the Sutra on the Ten Grounds  
(Dasabhumikasutra ), assert that the absorption of cessation is one specific exalted wisdom 
of meditative equipoise that is placed in equipoise on dharmata, but it is clear that both 
Acarya  [Haribhadra] and Arya [Vimuktisena] assert that it is an attribute that is a non -
associated compositional factor in which the six collections of coarse [primary 
consciousnesses] along with their retinues have been stopped; someone’s assertion that it is 
the [state of] having stopped the seven collections along with their retinues upon the mind -
basis -of-all is unreasonable, because in Acarya [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary , the 
citation from Descent into Lanka Sutra ( Lankavat arasutra), “The eight primary 
consciousnesses and” has been explained; besides just that there is not even a hint of 
explanation that there is a mind -basis -of-all that is a different entity from the six collections. 
I shall explain the other meanings of th e absorption of cessation elsewhere.

aspects of the uninterrupted path , dharma forbearance, the object- possessors included 
within the objects, the four truths, the four characteristics that do not possess thorough 
afflictions.
