349 EXPLANATION  
Regarding those  aspects of the  exalted knower of all aspects, there are the ten aspects of 
the strengths. What are they? The ten aspects of: (1) the strength that knows sources and 
the opposites of those, non -sources, the arisal of happiness and suffering results from 
actions of virtue and non -virtue, (2) the strength that knows  the fruition of actions, the 
direct realization of all results without exception of minute actions, (3) the strength that 
knows the varieties of interests : interests regarding high status and interests regarding the 
paths of the three vehicles , (4) the strength that knows  the many classes that abide naturally 
and are transformed through  accomplishment  in the world systems, (5) the strength that 
knows superior and inferior faculties  that are the best, the middling, and trifling powers of 
faith and so forth , (6) the strength that is a knower that engages without grasping and 
obstruction138 in (i) the paths that proceed everywhere  to high status, such as meritorious 
and immovable actions, and to definite goodness, such as the three vehicles, and (ii) that 
which is opposite to those, (7) the strength that is a knower without grasping and obstruction 
with respect to (i) the thoroughly afflicted that are obscurations to the absorptions in the 
concentrations, liberations, an d so forth, and (ii) the completely pure, definite emergence 
from those, (8) the strength that remembers former states, all the past lives of oneself and 
others , (9) the strength that knows  death- transference and rebirth  that engages the future 
birth place s of all sentient beings , and (10) the strength that knows  the exhaustion of 
contaminations  that is the hearers’ and solitary realizers’ exhaustion of afflictive 
obscurations and the buddhas’ exhaustion of all stains .   
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B3B -1B. Four fearlessnesses  
COMMENTARY 
The four aspects of the fearlessnesses: oneself (1) attesting “I am enlightened”, (2) stating 
that attachment and so forth act as hindrances, (3) fully revealing the paths of the knower of 
all and so forth as definite emergence, and (4) attesting that one’s own contaminations have 
been exhausted, since there is no disputant.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are four aspects of the fearlessnesses:  
(1) in terms of one’s own welfare, the fearlessness regarding perfect realizations, oneself 
attesting “I am clearly and  completely  enlightened  in all ways with respect to all 
phenomena ” in the midst of a retinue, without  there being a dispute concordant with the 
Dharma  stating “You have not realized this phenomenon,”  
(2) in terms of others’ welfare, the fearlessness regarding revealing the hindering 
phenomena, stating that the afflictive obscurations, attachment and so forth , and the 
knowledge obscurations  act as hindrances  to liberation and omniscience, without  there 
being a dispute concordant with the Dharma  stating “Even though this object of 
abandonment is relied upon, it will not become a hindrance,”  
(3) [in terms of others’ welfare,] the fearlessness regarding revealing the path of definite 
emergence, fully revealing  the paths of the knower of all , the knower of paths, and so 
forth as definitely emerging  to liberation and omniscience, without  there being a dispute 
concordant with the Dharma  stating “Even though this path is relied upon, it is unable to 
cause definite emergence,”  and  
                                              
138 Chags thogs  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
350 (4) in terms of one’s own welfare, the fearlessness regarding perfect abandonment, attesting 
that all of one’s own contaminations without exception have been exhausted, since there 
is no dispute  concordant with the Dharma .   
 
Through [the Buddha] himself having attained happiness, and having attained intrepidity 
and fearlessness, that abode is pledged as an abode of the great hero. Also in the manner of 
a lion’s roar in the midst of a retinue, sutra says, “Having pledged that ‘O, I am clearly and  
completely enlightened with respect to these phenomena,’ I do not subsequently see the 
correct cause of such that will become a dispute concordant with the Dharma  in the world 
by a trainee -in-virtue, a brahmin, a god, a demon, or Brahma, or anyone e lse, saying that I 
am not clearly and  completely enlightened with respect to these phenomena  here.”  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B3B -1C. Four individual correct knowledges  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding (1) enumerations, (2) the characteristics of phenomena,139 (3) the languages of 
places, and (4) the objects of the classification of phenomena, respectively: (1) phenomena, 
(2) meaning, (3) terminology (definitive words), and (4) confidence, the four aspects of 
individual correct knowledges. 
 
EXPLANATION  
There are  the four aspects of individual correct knowledges , because there are the first 
two that are unobstructed regarding the objects to be known, and there are the later two that 
are unobstructed regarding that which explains : 
the meditative stabilizations and wisdoms that engage without grasping and obstruction the exalted knowers regarding (1) the many enumerations  of names of thoroughly afflicted and 
completely pure phenomena, and  (2) the specific and general characteristics of 
phenomena, are the individual correct knowledges of phenomena and meaning ; and  
the meditative stabilizations and wisdoms that engage without grasping and obstruction the 
exalted knowers regarding (3) the languages of places of all sentient beings that utters the 
language concordant with each place, and (4) the objects of the classification of the 
entities and aspects of phenomena, are respectively  the individual correct knowledges of 
terminology (definitive words) and confidence .   
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B3B -1D. Eighteen unm ixed qualities of a buddha  
In that way, the purities that are the eighteen qualities of a buddha unmixed with lower 
supports are the eighteen: (1) six included within behavior, (2) six included within 
realizations, (3) three included within exalted activit ies, and (4) three included within 
exalted wisdom.  
 
The six included within behavior:  
 
COMMENTARY 
The six aspects of not possessing (1) mistakes, (2) senseless sounds, (3) the degeneration of mindfulness, (4) non -equipoise of mind, (5) the discrimination o f difference, (6) the 
indifference of not examining individually;

the six aspects of a tathagata not possessing  (1) mistaken  behavior of body, such as

COMMENTARY

EXPLANATION

places where there are trainees , (9) mindfulness  that does not forget the method for 
subduing sentient beings , (10) meditative stabilization  that i s set in equipoise on the 
thusness of phenomena , (11) wisdom  that  discerns all phenomena , (12) liberation  in which 
the obscurations have been abandoned.   
 
The three included within exalted activity:

through the four types of behavior

speaking that which possesses the Dharma  and is meaningful , and (15) the exalted activities 
of the exalted mind, love, compassion, and so forth, are  (i) preceded by exalted wisdom 
and (ii) followed by exalted wisdom  due to moving along together with exalted wisdom in 
the middle .

2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B3B -2. Entity  
COMMENTARY  
The thusness explained by all the buddhas,   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the dharmata in the continua of the perfect  complete buddhas —is the aspect 
of thusness, because the thusness explained by all the buddhas  of the three times is 
distinguished by having extinguished all stains. 
 In terms of the object -possessor, the exalted wisdom that knows the modes in the 
continuu m of a buddha is the aspect of thusness, because it is an exalted wisdom that is set 
in equipoise on thusness in a manner of not arising again. Since a buddhas’s coming into the 
world is for the sake of clarifying the suchness of things to trainees, a budd ha who does not 
teach thusness does not exist at all. Therefore [thusness] is “explained by all the buddhas.”  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1B -3B3B -3. Support  
COMMENTARY 
the self- arisen that has mastered all Dharma , and  
 
EXPLANATION  
The very culminated principal perfection  of wisdom  is the aspect of the self- arisen that has 
mastered all Dharma , because it is an exalted wisdom that has attained mastery over 
turning all the wheels of Dharma  without exception to trainees. Further, the subject —those 
excellent qualities, the strengths and so forth explained above —are not based upon [just] 
anything that is self- arisen, because they are excellent qualities based upon the self- arisen 
that has mastered all Dharma .

is the realization of clear complete  enlightenment in all aspects  with respect to all 
phenomena .

2B2C -2A1B -1A1C. Summary [of the divisions of the aspects, the branches of knowers, the antidotes to all the 
discordant classes without exception]  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1C -1. Summary of the enti ties 
2B2C -2A1B -1A1C -2. Summary of the enumeration  
2B2C -2A1B -1A1C -1. Summary of the entities 
COMMENTARY 
With regard to that, the aspects of the knower of all, by the instances of hearers and 
bodhisattvas, are respectively without contaminations and also wit h contaminations.  
 
EXPLANATION  
With regard to that, the twenty -seven aspects of the knower of all, by the instances of 
those existing in the continua of hearers  and solitary realizers and those existing in the 
continua of bodhisattvas,  are respectively without  the contaminations  that are afflictions 
(due to [hearers and solitary realizers] having specifically  sought out the afflictive obscurations 
and intended to abandon them) and also with contaminations  (due to [hearers and solitary 
realizers] having specifically  sought out the afflictive obscurations and intended to abandon 
them) and also with contaminations (due to the opposite of the reason above). Regarding the 
above explanation of the solitary realizers’ paths as contaminated, they are with the 
contaminations (of the conception s of apprehendeds that are knowledge obscurations ) during 
subsequent attainment; bodhisattvas also cultivate in accordance with those.  
 
COMMENTARY 
The aspects of the knower of paths are only with contaminations because it is very much the 
case that bodhisattvas have not abandoned the afflictions.   
 
EXPLANATION  
That “ the thirty -six aspects of the knower of paths are only with the contaminations  that 
are categories of afflictions” is presented, because it is very much the case that bodhisattvas 
have not abandoned the afflictions  by way of taking the afflictions as the principal objects of 
abandonment . Although bodhisattvas have not abandoned the afflictions, they do not generate 
even the slightest fault of afflictions. Therefore  they are presented as being with the 
contaminations that are afflictions. Since hearers and solitary realizers take the afflictions as 
their principal objects of abandonment and abandon them, the knowers of bases in their 
continua are said to be uncontami nated.  
 
COMMENTARY 
The aspects of the paths of the exalted knower of all aspects are only uncontaminated because 
they have mastery over all phenomena  since the perfect complete buddhas have abandoned in 
all ways the afflictive and knowledge obscurations a long with their latencies.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the one hundred and ten final aspects of the paths of the exalted knower of all 
aspects —are only uncontaminated, because they have mastery over all phenomena.  That 
follows  since the perfect complete buddh as have abandoned in all ways the afflictive and 
knowledge obscurations along with their latencies.

ROOT TEXT  
4.6  Those who have acted exceptionally towards buddhas,  
Have planted roots of virtue with regard to them,  
And have been under the protection of spiritual guides  
Are vessels for listening to these. 
 
4.7 Because they have served buddhas, questioned, and  
The conduct of generosity, ethics, and so forth,  
The vessels who take, hold, and so forth  
Are asserted to be excellent beings.  
   
EXPLANATION  
The subject —bodhisattvas on the path of accumulati on who possess the characteristics 
which will be explained below —are vessels for listening to  these  three Perfection of 
Wisdom [Sutras] of the Mahayana, (i) because they have acted exceptionally towards 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
355 numberless buddhas  in the past, such as honoring them , (ii) because they have planted 
roots of virtue, such as offering alms, with regard to them, and (iii) because they have 
been under the protection of Mahayana  spiritual guides . The vessels who take  to mind 
the words of the Mothers , hold  the meanings witho ut forgetting them , complete the 
accomplishments, and so forth are asserted to be excellent beings, because , for the sake 
of those, they have served countless  buddhas, questioned all the words and meanings that 
they have doubts about , and practiced through the  proper  conduct of generosity, ethics, 
and so forth.  
2B2C -2A1B -1A2A- 3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Those who (1) have generally planted roots of virtue with respect to 
the buddhas who have arisen in the past and pre sent and trained in them, (2) have pleased 
the tathagatas by honoring them with body and so forth, (3) have fully questioned the meanings that they have doubts about, and (4) have practiced the ten perfections, generosity 
and so forth— having been blessed by the spiritual guides
—are accepted by the buddhas and 
so forth, respectively, as the very vessels (1) to listen to the scriptures of these Mothers bearing the characteristics of the aspects, (2) to hold, (3) to not forget the meaning, and (4) 
to proper ly take to mind . 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those [bodhisattvas on the path of accumulation who possess the 
characteristics which will be explained below] —(1) have generally planted roots of virtue  
initially, offering temples and so forth, with respect to the buddhas who have arisen in 
the past and present and trained in them  higher and higher , because of having been  
blessed by the spiritual guides . Respectively, they are accepted by  the buddhas and 
bodhisattvas  as the very vessels (1) to l isten to the scriptures of these Mothers  bearing 
the characteristics of the aspects  included within the 173 knower -aspects. “Having been 
blessed by the spiritual guides” is to be joined to the end of the three below, because it is to 
make known that the fu lly complete qualities of a buddha are contingent upon pleasing the 
spiritual guide.  Those accepted by the buddhas, great bodhisattvas, and so forth as the very vessels (2) to 
hold  in mind the words of these scriptures , (3) to not forget the meaning, and (4) to 
properly take to mind  acting to complete the achievings, respectively (2) have pleased the 
past tathagatas by honoring them with body and so forth,  because of having been 
blessed by the spiritual guides , (3) have fully questioned past buddhas regard ing the 
meanings that they have doubts  about,  [because of having been blessed by the spiritual 
guides] , and (4) have practiced the ten perfections, generosity and so forth  by having 
practiced the meanings that have been questioned , because of having been b lessed by the 
spiritual guides . By having taken the supports who collect the roots of virtue as the principal 
objects to be explained, the supports who act to  complete the achievings have also been 
indicated.

4.8  Because of not abiding in forms and so forth;  
Because of stopping the application to that;  
Because the thusnesses of those are profound;  
Because those are difficult to fathom; 
 4.9a  Because those are immeasurable;  
 
EXPLANATION  
The entities of the applications are as follows. By way of those two —(1) the application of 
not abiding in forms and so forth, cultivation through realizing that phenomena included 
within bases, paths, and aspects are not truly existent, and (2 ) the application of stopping 
the application , cultivation by way of  stopping the adherence  to that application stopping 
abidance as truly existent —(i) because of realizing the  thusnesses of those  bases, forms 
and so forth,  are profound, (ii) because of realizing that the dharmatas of those  phenomena 
included within paths are difficult to fathom, and (iii) because of realizing that the 
dharmatas of those  phenomena included within aspects  are immeasurable , [the latter three 
applications] are called (3 –5) “applications of the profound, of the difficult to fathom and of 
the immeasurable .” 
 Sutra says:  
“When a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, at the time of not abiding in forms, he performs the yoga with respect to forms.”  
“Whenever a great bodhisattva does not perform the application with respect to forms, at the 
time of not performing the application with respect to forms, he performs the yoga with 
respect to forms.”  
“Sariputra , because the thusness of forms is profound the perfection of wi sdom is 
profound.”  
“Sariputra , because forms are difficult to fathom the perfection of wisdom is difficult to 
fathom.”  
“Sariputra , because forms are immeasurable the perfection of wisdom is immeasurable.”  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
357 These very five applications are divided into the remaining [applications] by way of 
occasion and time. As explained above, a wisdom arisen from meditation observing 
emptiness does not exist prior to the heat level of the path of preparation. The repeated 
analysis of emptiness and being set single -pointedly on it while being set in equipoise on a 
fully -qualified calm abiding  exists even on the path of accumulation. Thus, know that both 
are included here.

2B2C -2A1B -1A2B -2C. Divisions by way of the applications [that are] the methods for completion  
The applications that are the methods for completing cu ltivation in the Mothers are as 
follows:  
 
ROOT TEXT  
4.10bcd  … because no increase and no decrease;  
Not seeing dharma, non -dharma, and so forth;

 4.11ab  Not conceptualizing forms and so forth,

EXPLANATION  
There is (14) the application realizing that there is no increase and no decrease  ultimately 
although conventionally faults and excellent qualities are increased and decreased;  sutra 
says, “Subhuti, when a great bodhisattva practices  the perfection of wisdom , at the time of 
perfectly not subsequently seeing phenomena ranging from forms until  the exalted knower 
of all aspects as increasing or decreasing, that great bodhisattva will thoroughly complete 
the cultivation of the perfection of wisdom.”   
 There is (15) the application of not seeing the wholesome, the unwholesome, and so 
forth as dharma, non- dharma, and so forth ultimately ; sutra says, “when a great 
bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, he perfectly does not subseque ntly see that 
called ‘dharma’; he perfectly does not subsequently see that called ‘non -dharma’...”  
 Not clinging to the aspects of inconceivable forms and so forth as truly existent is (16) 
the application of not seeing inconceivable forms and so forth; sutra says, “Subhuti, 
because forms are inconceivable, [they] are taught to be inconceivable.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
359  There is (17) the application of not conceptualizing the bases of characterization, forms 
and so forth, their marks , that which is suitable to be a form and so f orth, and the entities  
of their objects of characterization as truly existent ; sutra says, “Subhuti, when a great 
bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom , he does not conceive forms, does not 
conceptualize  them. He does not conceive the marks of forms, does not conceptualize  them. 
He does not conceive the natures of forms, does not conceptualize  them.”

sutra says, “Even when a son of the lineage or daughter of the lineage asks a question, he or she will quickly write it down.”  
2B2C -2A1B -1A2B -3. Commentary 
The five actual applications:  
 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The five applications of: (1) not abiding in forms and so forth, (2) 
not applying, (3) the profound, (4) the difficult to fathom, and  (5) the immeasurable, 
respectively, realizing in that way: (1) “not abiding in forms and so forth through absence  of 
entitiness,” (2) “merely not applying with respect to  those is applying,” (3 -5) due to forms 
and so forth themselves being the entitiness of thusness, “profound,” “ difficult to fathom,” 
“immeasurable,” and…  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalted wisdom of meditative equipoise of a bodhisattva on the path of 
preparation —is called, respectively, (1) the application of “not abiding in forms and so 
forth  ultimately , ” (2) the application of “ not applying, stopping adherence to object -
possessors as truly existent, (3 -5) the applications of “ the profound,  ” “the difficult to 
fathom, ” and “the immeasurable, ” respectively  (1) because it is an application realizing 
“not abiding in forms and so forth ultimately  through absence  of entitiness,” (2) 
                                              
141 Sbyang  emended to read byang.

360 because it is a bodhisattva’s application “applying with respect to  the meaning of the mode 
of abidance by merely not applying with respect to those forms and so fo rth,” (3 -5) 
because it is a bodhisattva’s application realizing  in that way, due to forms and so forth 
themselves being the entitiness of thusness, “ the dharmatas of bases are profound,” “ the 
dharmatas of paths are difficult to fathom,” and “the dharmatas of aspects are 
immeasurable.”  
 
COMMENTARY 
…fifteen types of applications: (6) attaining clear complete  enlightenment with great 
hardship over a long time, (7) receiving a predication, (8) irreversibility, (9) definite 
emergence, (10) being without hindranc es, (11) being close to perfect clear complete  
enlightenment, (12) quickly attaining clear complete  enlightenment, (13) others’ welfare, 
(14) no increase or decrease, (15) not observing dharma, non -dharma, and so forth, (16) 
ceasing the aspects of inconcei vable forms and so forth, (17) not conceptualizing things that 
are forms and so forth, (18) bestowing the precious result, (19) purity, (20) with regard to 
the boundaries, which respectively possess the accomplishment of those called: (6) fear 
regarding th e perfection of wisdom, (7) absence  of fear, (8) correctly  upholds, (9) abandons 
hindering phenomena, (10) always cultivates the Dharma , (11) the very support of new 
uncontaminated qualities, (12) manifestly achieves the resultant truth body, (13) thorough ly 
turns the wheel of Dharma , (14) sees neither increase nor decrease, (15) does not observe 
the constituents of desire, (16) does not cling to the aspects of inconceivable forms and so 
forth, (17) does not conceptualize forms, their marks, and their entit inesses, (18) sees the 
first result, (19) pure forms, (20) not discard manifestly striving for years: the twenty applications.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The applications of occasions:  
 The subject —the application of a dull -faculty bodhisattva abiding on the path of 
accumulation —respectively  is an application in the continuum of one (6) attaining clear 
complete  enlightenment with  great hardship over a long time  because it is an application 
in the continuum of a beginner in whom great manifest (6) fear regarding the perfection of wisdom  that is a realization of the meaning of the mode of abidance occurs.  
 The subject —the heat level of the Mahayana path of preparation —is the application of 
(7) receiving a predication , because it is an application that is distinguished  by possessing 
(7) absence  of fear  regarding the Mothers.  
 The subject —the peak level —is the application of (8)  irreversibility , because it is an 
application that (8) correctly upholds  hearing of the Mothers and so forth due to being 
superior to the heat l evel.  
 The subject —the forbearance level of the Mahayana path of preparation —is the 
application of (9) definite emergence , because it is an achieving that (9) abandons 
hindering phenomena  such as the attention adopted by the hearers and solitary realizers for 
the sake of oneself alone, due to having attained a forbearance that is unafraid of emptiness 
and so forth.  
 The subject —the supreme mundane quality level of the path of preparation —is the 
application of (10) being without hindrances , because it is the  fourth partial concordance 
with definite discrimination that  (10) always cultivates the Dharma  due to acting as the 
direct cause of the path of seeing.   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
361  The subject —the Mahayana path of seeing —is the application of (11) being close to 
perfect complete e nlightenment, because it is an application that is  (11) the very support 
of new uncontaminated qualities of the Mahayana paths. 
 The subject —the exalted wisdoms of the second ground until the seventh ground—are 
the applications of (12) quickly attaining clear complete  enlightenment, because they are 
applications that (12) manifestly achieve the resultant truth body  quickly.  
 The subject —the exalted wisdoms of the eighth ground —are the applications of (13) 
others’ welfare, (14) no increase or decrease, (15) not observing dharma, non -dharma, and 
so forth as existing ultimately, (16) ceasing the aspects of inconceivable forms and so forth, and (17) not conceptualizing the things th at are forms and so forth, because they are 
applications of the pure grounds that (13) thoroughly turn the wheel of Dharma  for others’ 
welfare due to having attained the ability to turn the wheel of Dharma  for all three lineage -
bearers, (14) see neither in crease nor decrease as existing ultimately, (15) do not observe 
any phenomena as the constituents of desire as existing ultimately, (16) do not cling to the aspects of inconceivable forms and so forth as truly existent, and (17) do not conceptualize 
the ba ses of characterization, forms, their marks, and the entitinesses of their objects of 
characterization as truly existent.  The subject —the exalted wisdom of the ninth ground —is the application of (18) 
bestowing the precious result, because it is a yoga of the pure grounds that (18) sees the 
first result of stream -enterer until unsurpassable enlightenment. 
 The subject —the exalted wisdom of the tenth ground —is the application of (19) purity , 
because it is a yoga of the pure grounds that cultivates (19) pure forms  until an exalted 
knower of all aspects.    The subject —a bodhisattva’s yoga that cultivates the Mothers quickly —is called an 
application (20) with regard to the boundaries , because it is an application that does (20) 
not discard manifestly striving i n cultivation through delineation of time into days, 
months, and years . The former five  and the later fifteen types  are the twenty applications .

362 2B2C -2A1B -1B1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.12ab  The fourteen types of excellent qualities:  
Overcoming the might of maras and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Through the force of cultivating the applications, the fourteen types of excellent 
qualities —overcoming the might of maras and so forth until definite attainment —will 
arise . 
2B2C -2A1B -1B1C. Commentary  
So the root text is said. The excellent qualities called: (1) overcoming the might of maras, 
(2) the buddhas paying attention and knowing, (3) directly perceived by the buddhas, (4) 
close to perfect complete enlightenment, (5) the great purpose, and so forth, (6) the place 
having been analyzed, (7) completing all uncontaminated excellent qualities, (8) a propounder being, (9) not being separated, (10) will produce roots of virtue not in common, 
(11) accomplishment of the meaning of the promise exactly, (12) full upholding of the 
extensive result, (13) accomplishment of the welfare of sentient beings, and (14) definite 
attainment —because, respectively, those called: (1) blessed by the buddhas, (2) attention, 
(3) seen by exalted wisdom, (4) become close, (5) g reat benefit, (6) enactment of the 
activities, (7) full completion of antidotal qualities, (8) expression of speech regarding the 
exalted knower of all aspects, (9) assist, (10) have generated extensive joy, (11) rejoice in 
the words of the promise of that , (12) stron g desire for the profound Dharma , (13) 
enactment of the welfare of sentient beings, and (14) finding the complete perfection of 
wisdom, will arise in those who take delight in the non -erroneous applications and will be 
attained —are the fourteen  excellent qualities.  
   
EXPLANATION  
The subject —in those  bodhisattvas who take delight in  cultivating the non -erroneous 
applications —the excellent quality of (1) overcoming the might of maras , without being 
harmed by hindrances with respect to cultivating  the applications, will arise , because, 
respectively, they are (1) blessed by the buddhas  with respect to cultivating the 
applications in that way . 
 Sutra says, “ Sariputra , as many countless, immeasurable buddhas as there are buddhas 
abiding and living in the countless, immeasurable world systems in all the ten directions pay 
attention to a great bodhisattva’s questioning, uphold ing, reading, teaching, recitation, 
proper attention, and cultivation of this profound perfection of wisdom. This is reality.”  
 (2) The  exalted minds of the buddhas  paying attention to  and knowing the cultivation 
of the applications will arise, because the exalted minds of the buddhas pay (2) attention  to 
the cultivation of the applications presently. In dependence on being  (3) dir ectly perceived 
by the buddhas , the applications become special, because the cultivation of the applications 
is (3) seen by exalted  wisdom  presently. They will (4) become very close to perfect 
complete enlightenment, because they (4) become close  to it pre sently.  
 Sutra says, “Know that: those sons or daughters of the Mahayana lineage will also 
become close to unsurpassable complete enlightenment.”  
  (5) The excellent qualities —the great purpose  of not being separated from the buddhas, 
the great benefit of being reborn in the happy migrations, the great result through becoming 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
363 a buddha, the great fruition through engaging in others’ welfare, and so forth— will arise, 
because they have attained the (5) great benefit of cultivating the applications presently. 
 Sutra says, “Whatever son or daughter of the lineage who writes and upholds this 
profound perfection of wisdom will attain the great purpose, the great result, and the great 
maturation.   
 (6) The benefit of the place having been analyzed,  such as the pla ce where they will be 
reborn having been predicted by the tathagatas, will arise, because they have attained the  (6) 
enactment of the activities  of the buddhas, such as teaching the Mothers to others.  
 Sutra says, “ Sariputra , after I pass into nirvana, thi s perfection of wisdom will spread to 
the southern ranges… By way of this profound perfection of wisdom  one will enact the 
activities of a buddha.”  
 (7) The benefit of completing all uncontaminated excellent qualities  will arise, 
because they have attained  the (7) full completion of some antidotal qualities  included 
within the path perfection of wisdom presently. 
 Sutra says, “Because those sons or daughters of the lineage will fully  complete the 
perfection of wisdom .”  
 (8) They will attain the state of a propounder being who teaches the Mothers to others, 
because they have attained the  (8) expression of speech regarding the exalted knower of 
all aspects  by the buddhas to them presently.   
 Sutra says, “Why? This is because I express speech regarding the po ssession of the 
exalted knower of all aspects to those sons or daughters of the lineage.”  
  (9) They will attain the state of not being separated from omniscience by the maras and 
so forth, because the buddhas (9) assist  them with respect to the cultivatio n of the 
applications presently.  Sutra says, “Those sons or daughters of the lineage will set themselves in meditative 
equipoise.”  
 (10) They will produce  roots of virtue not in common  with those of hearers and 
solitary realizers, such as setting many bei ngs in unsurpassable enlightenment, because they 
(10) have  generated extensive joy  with respect to the cultivation of the applications 
presently.  
 Sutra says,
 “Sariputra , those sons of the lineage or daughters of the lineage of the 
bodhisattva vehicle, upo n hearing this profound perfection of wisdom will generate great 
joy, faith, and satisfaction.”  
 (11) They will attain the accomplishment of the meaning of the promise to achieve 
others’ welfare exactly, because the buddhas (11) rejoice in the words of the  promise of that 
cultivation of the Mothers presently.  
 Sutra says, “I am subsequently pleased by the utterance of the words of those sons of the 
lineage or daughters of the lineage of the bodhisattva vehicle: view the mind with mind.  
  (12) They will atta in the full  upholding of the extensive result that is greater virtue in 
other lifetimes, because they have attained the (12) strong desire for the profound Mahayana Dharma  presently . 
 Sutra says, “They will fully uphold immense fruition by having manifestly composed 
immense roots of virtue.”  
 (13) They will attain the accomplishment of the welfare of sentient beings, such as 
giving away all possessions in other lifetimes, because they have attained the (13) 
enactment of the welfare of sentient bein gs, such as setting many creatures in 
enlightenment presently .

364  Sutra says, “Those sons of the lineage or daughters of the lineage, having remembered 
their rebirths, will find these six perfections.”   
 The excellent quality called (14) “definite attainmen t of the Mothers in other 
lifetimes” will arise, because  they will attain the excellent qualities called (14) “finding the 
complete words and meanings of the perfection of wisdom  in this life .” In that way these  
are the fourteen excellent qualities.

So the root text is said. The first set of ten are: (1) Will be attained with great hardship, (2) 
excessively quick confidence, (3) adopting negative states of the body, (4) adopting 
negative states of the mind, (5) performing recitations that h ave been generated 
inappropriately  and so forth, (6) holding reasons for turning away, (7) adherence to the 
causes and degeneration, (8) degeneration of relishing the taste of the sublime, (9) 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
365 degeneration of always upholding the supreme vehicle in all way s, and (10) degeneration of 
the constant object of intent;  
 
EXPLANATION  
The first set of ten are:  
• the two from the point of view of confidence:  
(1) producing ascertainment regarding the words and meanings of the Mothers will be 
attained over a long time with great hardship and  
(2) excessively quick confidence  quickly producing an ascertainment without decisive 
analysis.  
 
• the three from the point of view of adopting negative states:  
(3) adopting negative states of the body, such as questioning about the  Mothers while 
yawning and stretching ,  
(4) adopting negative states of the mind , such as questioning [about the Mothers] out of 
grasping , and  
(5) performing recitations that have been generated inappropriately and so forth, such 
as performing recitations  through having been motivated by [craving for] gain, honor, and 
the like.  
 
• the seven causes for degenerating from the Mahayana:  
(6) holding reasons for turning away from the Mothers ,  
(7) adherence to the causes  of omniscience  and degeneration of faith ,  
(8) degeneration of relishing the taste of the sublime  Mothers ,  
(9) degeneration of always upholding the Mothers, the supreme vehicle in all ways, 
seeking omniscience from the lesser scriptural baskets , and  
(10) degeneration of the constant object of in tent, having discarded the Mothers that have 
been found, seeking omniscience from the lesser scriptural baskets  
 
COMMENTARY  
the second set of ten are: (11) degeneration of the relation of cause and effect, (12) 
degeneration of that of which there is no hi gher, (13) the arising of a confidence that 
conceptualizes objects in many ways, (14) adherence to writing in letters, (15) adherence to 
non-things, (16) adherence to letters, (17) adherence to non -letters, (18) paying attention to 
places and so forth, (19 ) relishing the taste of gain, honor, and verses, and (20) seeking 
skillful means through incorrect paths;  
 
EXPLANATION

2B2C -2A1B -1B2C -1C. Three adverse conditions based upon others  
COMMENTARY  
there are another th ree: (44) application  of separation by the maras, (45) establishing the 
counterfeit, and (46) generation of joy towards an improper object. 
 
EXPLANATION  
There are another three:  
(44) application of separation  by the maras , the maras causing a rift between  the Dharma  
and the person, by coming to him in the guise of a trainee -in-virtue and saying, “The 
Mothers occur in my sutra scriptural basket ; they do not occur in the extensive, medium, 
and brief Perfection of Wisdom Sutras ,”  
(45) accomplishing  the count erfeit Mothers, teaching that meditating on ugliness and the 
like is meditating on the meaning of the mode of abidance , and  
(46) generation of joy towards an improper teacher, the  object of faith, producing faith in 
the maras (who have come in the guise o f buddhas) as buddhas.  
 If the master and the student were to get together by way of being free from these faults 
which have been explained, the proper way of relying upon a spiritual guide, the root of the 
path would  occur. Hence, the way to rely upon a s piritual guide is also indicated by 
implication. Indeed, having thought that the extensive explanation would be easy to understand, [Maitreya] did not include it in the explicit indication of the root text. This has 
been taught by the Acarya.

2B2C -2A1B -1C1A. Stating the systems of past acaryas  
QUALM: According to the assertions of the great Gomi, the characteristics of the 
applications are explained in the following manner. There are fo ur: the characterization143, 
the basis of characterization144, the object of characterization145, and the characteristic146. 
For example, fire arisen from hay is the basis of characterization; the entity attribute —the 
tangible object, heat —and the functional attri bute—that which burns —are the 
characteristics; that which is suitable to be affixed the convention “fire” is the object of characterization, and that very valid cognition which affirms the three —the characteristic, 
the object of characterization, and the b asis of characterization —is called “the 
characterization.” Likewise, here also the twenty applications themselves are the bases of 
characterization. Further, they are not factors of the knower of bases since they are 
uncommon [factors] that act to accomplish the truth body, and they are also not factors of 
the exalted knower of all aspects since they exist on the occasions of learners; they can only 
be either factors of the actual knower of paths or factors of a similitude of the knower of 
paths. If they are factors of only the knower of paths, what is the reason for dividing them 
into applications of the three exalted knowers?  
 
RESPONSE: They are divided into three from the point of view of the objects that observe the aspects of the three exalted knowers:   
• That observing emptiness and selflessness that are aspects of the knower of bases is (1)
147 
the application of stopping abidance. That observing the nineteen, the four aspects of cessations and the fifteen of paths of the knower of bases is (2) the applica tion that stops 
yoga. That observing the six: impermanence and suffering, and the four of origins of the 
knower of bases is (3) the application of the profound. The divisions of occasions of 
these three are the eight [applications]: (6 -13) [the application s of] realizing over a long 
time with great hardship, and so forth. The complete cultivations of these three are the 
four: (14 -17) [the applications of] no increase or decrease, and so forth. The results of 
these three are the two: (18 -19) [the application s of] bestowing the precious result and 
purity. The times of these three are: (20) the application of boundary. These eighteen  are

370 the applications of the knower of bases. 
• That observing the three, emptiness, selflessness and one portion of non -entitines s that 
are aspects of the knower of paths is (1) the application of stopping abidance. That 
observing the twenty- three, the seven of paths and the sixteen of cessations, is (2) the 
application that stops yoga. That observing the six —impermanence, suffering  and the 
four of origins —and one portion of non- entitiness is (4) the application of the difficult to 
fathom. The eighteen  by way of the divisions as above are the applications of the knower 
of paths.  
• That observing the 102 aspects that are property -possessors included within the aspects 
of the exalted knower of all aspects, the aspect of emptiness, the uninterrupted path with 
respect to sufferings, and a portion of the aspect of thusness is (1) the application of 
stopping abidance. That observing the three —the aspect of signlessness that is the 
dharmata of the 102 aspects of object -possessors, and the uninterrupted paths with 
respect to cessations and paths —and a portion of the aspect of thusness, is (2) the 
application that stops yoga. That observing the aspect of wishlessness that is the 102 aspects of the property -possessors, a portion of the uninterrupted path with respect to 
true sufferings, the uninterrupted path with respect to origins, and a portion of the aspect of thusness is (5) the applicati on of the immeasurable. The eighteen  [by way of] 
divisions of the three as above are the applications of an exalted knower of all aspects.  
 
The distinguishing characteristics and the functional characteristics do not characterize the 
object of characteriz ation as an application in complete aspects; they characterize it as an 
application that is an uncommon cause of the truth body. Further, it is indicated by the sixteen entitiness characteristics.  
 The characteristics of the applications characterize it as  an application that is an 
uncommon cause of the truth body; it is characterized by the collection of (i) the 
distinguishing characteristics that act to accomplish the fulfillment of one’s welfare and (ii) 
the functional characteristics that act to accompl ish the fulfillment of others’ welfare. When 
those are divided, there are sixteen distinguishing characteristics and the eleven functional characteristics.  
 The awareness that ascertains (a) the three —the characteristic, the object of 
characterization, and  the basis of characterization —and (b) the characteristic with respect to 
the basis of characterization is called the “characterization.” It exists either as a wisdom of 
hearing and contemplation prior to having engaged in the applications or as a knower 
subsequent to the applications. Having taken the twenty applications as the bases of 
characterization, and stated the distinguishing characteristics and the functional characteristics as reasons, it characterizes: “This is an application that is an uncommon  
cause of the truth body.”  
 The characterization is not a factor of the knower of bases or the exalted knower of all 
aspects; it is a factor of a similitude of the knower of paths. That characterization itself is 
indicated through implication by the three sets of sixteen knowledge characteristics; those 
are not actual characterization exalted wisdoms, because a characterization exalted wisdom 
observes the distinguishing characteristics and the functional characteristics, but the three 
sets of sixteen knowers do not observe them.  
 Therefore, the object -possessor is indicated by the objects through implication. It is said 
that a characterization exalted wisdom observes the two [types of] characteristics of the 
applications and the three sets of sixteen aspects  of knowledge.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
371  
It is also appropriate to take the twenty applications explained above to be the bases of 
characterization. However, according to the Acarya Haribhadra’s commentary on the 
intention of the root text, it is clear that both the bases of chara cterization and the objects of 
characterization are indicated by the sixteen entitiness characteristics, and likewise, it is very clear that the characteristics of the applications of the three exalted knowers are 
indicated by the three sets of sixteen asp ects of knowledge. Hence I do not think that the 
explanations —that a valid cognizer (that ascertains the characteristics with respect to the 
basis of characterization) is indicated and the like —are correct.  
 A past scholar, having taken the three exalted k nowers (in the continuum of a buddha that 
have been generated) as the characteristics, [explains] that they characterize the applications 
of the three exalted knowers as acting to generate buddhahood and so forth. However, he is unclear about the meaning o f the words of the commentaries of both Arya Vimuktisena and 
Acarya Haribhadra. Even though the three —knowledge, distinguishing and functional 
[characteristics] —have been taken as the reason for engaging the convention “Mahayana 
applications,” and so forth , it is difficult to posit what the purpose for explaining the 
characteristics is, what the mode of characterization is, and so forth.   
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B. Positing our own system  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -1. Bases of characterization  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -2. Objects of characterization  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -3. Characteristics  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4. Modes of characterization  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -5. Boundaries  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -1. Bases of characterization  
The bases of characterization are the sixteen yogas that are devoid of afflictions, sign s, and 
so forth.  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -2. Objects of characterization  
[With respect to the objects of characterization], from between the two, the generality and the instances, the first is the object that is the principal [object of characterization]. That, 
further, as explained above, has been indicated by the name “perfection of wisdom”; it has 
been indicated again and again in sutra by the three: textual, path and resultant Perfections 
of Wisdom, and its meaning has also been commentated on in the treatises. T he path and 
resultant Perfections of Wisdom themselves are differentiated into eight clear realizations.  The final object of attainment for a Mahayana lineage -bearer, from among the 
Perfections of Wisdom, is that very resultant perfection of wisdom, as it  has been taught, “ In 
that way , among wisdoms, the exalted knower of all aspects.” In order to proceed to it, the 
path perfection of wisdom itself must be well characterized as an object of valid cognition ; 
otherwise, one will enter a mistaken path. Hence, the applications referred to in the 
explanation of “the characteristics of the applications” here are the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom themselves. Their characteristics are called “the characteristics of the 
applications.”  
 Regarding the particu larities, the applications of the three exalted knowers are objects of 
characterization, also the applications that are superior to the applications of hearers and 
solitary realizers are objects of characterization, and the applications of the three exalte d 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
372 knowers possessing the special function of accomplishing others’ welfare are objects of 
characterization.   
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -3. Characteristics  
The characteristics are: (i) the knowers that are in the entities of the applications, (ii) the 
distinctions, an d (iii) the functions. 
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4. Modes of characterization  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4A. Mode of characterization of the generality 
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4B. Mode of characterization of the instances  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4A. Mode of characterization of the generality 
The bases of characterization —the sixteen bodhisattvas’ yogas that are devoid of afflictions, 
signs, and so forth —are characterized as the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom 
[because] they are bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms conjoined with a complete class o f special 
method and wisdom, such as great compassion and the wisdom realizing emptiness. 
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -4B. Mode of characterization of the instances  
The bases of characterization —the bodhisattvas’ yogas that have abandoned the four, the 
afflictions, sign s, and so forth, and that bear the aspect of observing any of the aspects of the 
knower of bases, knowing the arisal of a tathagata and so forth —are characterized as the 
bodhisattvas’ applications of the knower of bases, [because] they are bodhisattvas’ ex alted 
wisdoms bearing any of the aspects, knowing the arisal of a tathagata and so forth.  
 The bases of characterization —the bodhisattvas’ applications that are distinguished by 
the accomplishment of the difficult task of the armor of others’ welfare, and  so forth, and 
bear the aspect of observing any of the aspects of the knower of paths, emptiness, 
signlessness, and so forth —are characterized as the applications of the knower of paths, 
[because] they are bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms distinguished by any  of the aspects of 
knowledge of a knower of paths, emptiness, signlessness, and so forth.  
 The bases of characterization —the seven bodhisattvas’ yogas that observe the attributes 
included within bases and paths and so forth—are characterized as the applica tions of an 
exalted knower of all aspects, because they are bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms bearing the aspect of observing any of the aspects of the exalted knower of all aspects,
 the abiding in 
happiness of the present life of the tathagatas and so forth. Those are the ways in which the 
applications of the three exalted knowers are characterized by the three sets of sixteen 
knowledge characteristics.   
 The bases of characterization —the sixteen yogas of entitiness —are characterized as the 
applications that are superior to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers, because 
they are bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms distinguished by the inconceivable and so forth. 
That is the mode of characterization of the distinguishing characteristics.  
 Further, the ba ses of characterization —that [i.e. the sixteen yogas of entitiness] —are 
characterized as the applications possessing the special function of accomplishing others’ 
welfare, [because] they are bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms possessing the special functions of benefit, happiness, protection, and so forth.  
 Also the divisions should be known in that way: [the four entitiness yogas] are 
characterized as the applications of the knower of bases possessing the special function of 
accomplishing others’ welfare, [beca use they are] bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms possessing 
the special functions of benefit, happiness, protection; [the five entitiness yogas and the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
373 seven entitiness yogas respectively] are characterized as the applications of the knower of 
paths and the app lications of the exalted knower of all aspects possessing those such 
functions, [because they are] bodhisattvas’ exalted wisdoms distinguished by the seven [functions] and the one function.  
 Therefore, through the knowledge characteristics characterizing t hem in general as the 
applications of the three exalted knowers, [the respective bases of characterization] have 
been indicated; they have not been indicated through their having been distinguished by the 
isolates of being superior to the applications of h earers and solitary realizers. However, by 
having indicated the mode in which they are characterized by the distinguishing characteristics through having been distinguished by those [i.e. the isolates of being superior 
to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers], they are characterized in the manner of precluding that they [i.e. the distinguishing characteristics] are possessed by others.  
 The mere applications of the knower of bases cannot be distinguished by way of the self-
isolates [to be superior] to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers. Having indicated the mode of superiority of the two other [types of] applications [i.e. the 
applications of the knower of path s and of an exalted knower of all aspects] for the sake of 
realizing that they are superior also by way of the self- isolates of those two, that of the other 
one [ i.e. the mode of superiority of the applications of the knower of bases] is not indicated; 
from the point of view of substance, since the applications of the knower of bases in the 
continua of bodhisattvas are superior to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers, 
understand that they can be characterized as being superior.   
  Although th e functional characteristics along with the bases of characterization act to 
generate the buddhas’ fulfillment of others’ welfare, they do not characterize them  in that 
manner, because having ascertained with valid cognition the mere yogas of the path perfection of wisdom and the applications of the three exalted knowers, after that one 
decides that they are causes of omniscience.  In brief, having understood the divisions of the three, textual, path, and resultant 
Perfections of Wisdom, first a mere seeking [aspiration] that is a correct assumption regarding the resultant perfection of wisdom is generated. However, in order to ascertain 
with valid cognition the cause and result relationship, one must first ascertain with valid 
cognition the path perfection of wisdom prior to that. Know that, for the sake of understanding that, these characteristics of the generality and the instances  are explained.  
2B2C -2A1B -1C1B -5. Boundaries  
The boundaries [of the characteristics of the applications] are: they exist from the Mahayana 
path of accumulation until the end of the continuum; the assertion that distinguishing 
characteristics exist on buddh a ground and the like are clearly expressions of not having 
realized the contextual meaning of these characteristics. From those topics  characterizing 
the three exalted knowers that have been explained above [in the first three chapters], one is 
able to kn ow the mere three exalted knowers; in order to understand non -erroneously the 
self-isolates of the applications of the three exalted knowers, one must definitely know them 
in dependence on these [characteristics] that have been indicated in this chapter.

COMMENTARY 
Cultivating the applications by adopting and discarding respectively the excellent qualities 
and the faults is to be preceded by knowing the characteristics ; thus, after the faults, the 
characteristics of those:   
 
EXPLANATION  
After the faults  that act to hinder the cultivation of the applications , the characteristics  of 
those applications will be explained, because the reason is that, in order to cultivate the 
applications by adopting the excellent qualities and discarding the faults of the applications respectively , the applications must be known, and in order for that to occur, it 
is to be preceded by knowing the characteristics:

ROOT TEXT  
4.13  That which characterizes is made known  
To be a characteristic. Moreover, those are of three types:  
The knowledge, distinguishing, and functional.  
That which is an entitiness is also an object of characterization. 
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a purpose for explaining the characteristics of the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom here, because it is for the sake of making known that that characterizer
148 which is 
able to characterize  the yogas of the path perfection of wisdom  is a characteristic  of those. 
Moreover, those characteristics indicated here are divided into  three types, because the 
knowledge  characteristics which characterize them [as] applications of the three exalted 
knowers, the distinguishing characteristics whi ch characterize them as applications that are 
superior to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers , and the functional  
characteristics which characterize them as applications possessing the special function of 
accomplishing others’ welfare —are th ose.  
 There is a purpose for indicating the sixteen entitinesses of the applications, because it is 
for the sake of making known by means of indication through the term “characteristic of the 
applications” that that which is an entitiness  of an applicatio n is also an object of 
characterization  that is to be  characterized  and a basis of characterization.  
2B2C -2A1B -1C2B -1B. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Since these characterize [them as] the applications by establishing 
them as the agents , (1) knowledge, (2) distinction, (3) functional. And since they are objects 
                                              
148 Tibetan: mtshon by ed. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
375 to be characterized, by establishing them as the objects of action, (4) the entitiness 
characteristics —the characteristics are to be known as four types.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Since these characterize [them as] the generality or the instances [of]  the applications , 
they are called “characteristics of the applications”;  by the term “characteristics” 
establishing them  as the agents, the term “characteristics” engage these (1) knowledge, 
(2) d istinction, (3) functional  characteristics, because they characterize [them as] either the 
generality or the instances of the applications of the three exalted knowers. Since these  are 
objects to be characterized , by the term “characteristics” establishing  them as the 
objects of action , it is appropriate to affix the term “characteristics” to (4) the entitiness 
characteristics  of the applications, because those sixteen yogas are the bases of 
characterization and the objects to be characterized . The characte ristics , the bases for 
affixing the term “characteristics,” are to be known as four types , because there are those 
four as explained above .

2B2C -2A1B -1C2B -2A1C. Commentary  
So the root text is said. (1) The arisal of a tathagata, (2) the world is without disintegration, 
(3) the mental activities of sentient beings, (4) minds withdrawn and minds distracted, (5) 
the aspect of inexhaustible minds, (6 -8) minds together with attachment and so forth, and 
that included within the expression “and so forth”, minds free from attachment , (9) the 
extensive mind, (10) the mind that has become great, (11) the mind  cannot be measured , 
(12) the mind cannot be indicated, (13) the mind cannot be viewed, (14) fluct uating minds 
and so forth, (15) fluctuating and so forth bearing the aspect of thusness, (16) the realization 
of thusness by the Tathagata is taught to others and is imputed —through the applications of 
the knower of all being properly characterized by the sixteen instances that are the aspects 
of knowledge exactly, which are these aforesaid, these are the knowledge characteristics 
included within the knower of all.  
EXPLANATION  
These are the knowledge characteristics included within the knower of all. The bases of 
characterization —the four bodhisattvas’ yogas that are devoid of afflictions, signs, and so 
forth —are characterized as the applications of the knower of all  in the continuum of a 
bodhisattva. By what? By  the sixteen instances that are the aspects of knowledge  which 
are in the entities of the applications. How? Through being properly characterized by the 
characteristics [as] the objects of characterization; they are characterized exactly. 
 What are these characteristics? (1) A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom knowing the arisal of 
a tathagata in dependence on a perfection of wisdom that is an application of the knower of 
bases. (2) A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom conjoined with the exalted wisdom realizing that 
the world  is without disintegration  ultimate ly. Regarding the explanation [of the 
characteristics] bearing the aspect of emptiness in this context, that very path with which 
they are conjoined is indicated; know that the characterizers of the applications of the 
knower of bases bear the aspects of t he knower of bases.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
377  [A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom knowing]: (3) the many mental activities of sentient 
beings, (4) minds withdrawn into the selflessness of persons, and minds  distracted from 
that [selflessness of persons] , (5) the aspect of inexhaustibl e minds  like space , a 
bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom knowing:  (6-8) minds together with attachment, aversion, 
ignorance, and so forth, and that included within the expression  “and so forth” in the 
root text [4.15b] , minds free from attachment, and free from  aversion and ignorance , (9) 
the extensive mode of abidance of the mind, (10) the mind that has become great due to 
appearing like an illusion conventionally , (11) the mind  cannot be measured since 
supports that are individually ascertained do not exist  ultimately , (12) the mind cannot be 
indicated , saying “The sense consciousness is this ,” (13) the mind cannot be viewed  by 
the five eyes , (14) the  views of the (i) fluctuating, and so forth—(ii) collecting , (iii) 
spreading out, and (iv) gathering —of minds, arise in dependence on the basis of mistakes, 
the aggregates. The subject —a bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom knowing selflessness of 
persons —is a characteristic of the application of the knower of bases bearing such aspects in 
the continuum of a bodhisattva, b ecause it is an uncommon characterizer of that.   
 
Sutra says:  
(1) “Subhuti, this profound perfection of wisdom generates the ten strengths of the tathagatas.”  
(2) “The perfection of wisdom does not teach that these five aggregates disintegrate; it does 
not teach that they strongly disintegrate.”  
(3) “Subhuti, [the tathagatas] clearly know the minds and activities of immeasurable, 
countless sentient beings. They also are supported on and strongly know these Mothers.”  
(4) “The tathagatas clearly know, perfec tly and exactly, the minds of those sentient beings 
with the characteristics of being withdrawn and distracted.”  
(5) “The tathagatas clearly know, perfectly and exactly, the minds of those sentient beings in the aspects of being free from attachment, cessa tion and abandonment.”  
(6-8) “In dependence on the Mothers, [the tathagatas] clearly know, perfectly and exactly, 
the minds of those sentient beings that are with attachment are those called ‘minds with 
attachment. ’” “[The tathagatas] clearly know, perfect ly and exactly, those called ‘minds that 
are free from attachment .’”   
(9) “The tathagatas know, perfectly and exactly, the extensive minds of other sentient 
beings and persons are those called ‘extensive minds.’”  
(10) “[The tathagatas] know perfectly the great minds are those called ‘minds that have 
become great.’”  
(11) “[The tathagatas know] the immeasurable minds are those called ‘immeasurable 
minds.’”  
(12) “[The tathagatas know] the minds that cannot be indicated are those called ‘minds that cannot be  indicated.’”  
(13) “[The tathagatas know] the minds that cannot be viewed are those called ‘minds that 
cannot be viewed.’”  
(14) “The tathagatas, in dependence on these Mothers, (i) know the fluctuating, collected, 
spread out, and gathered minds of other persons and other sentient beings, and also those in 
that way, and (ii) clearly know ‘All of those varieties of scattered, collected, spread out, or 
gathered minds that will be produced and will arise will depend on forms, and will depend on feelings, disc riminations, compositional factors and primary consciousnesses, and will 
arise.’”   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
378  
With respect to decisive analysis here, some non -Buddhist sages, having summarized sixty -
two bad views into fourteen sections, posed questions to the Teacher [Buddha]. The y asked 
the questions based upon a self of persons, the basis of the attribute. They were not s uitable 
as vessels for teaching  the selflessness of persons if the Buddha had negated the basis of the 
attribute, and an attribute of a non -existent basis of the  attribute is not feasible. Therefore, 
[the Buddha] did not specify the answers to those questions. Hence they are called the “fourteen unspecified views.” There are four views each —based upon the past limit, based 
upon the future limit, and based upon nirvana—and two based upon the body and life.  
 
(1-4) The first [four based upon the past limit] are the views of the self of person s and the 
world as permanent, impermanent, both, and neither.  
(5-8) The second [four based upon the future limit] are the views  of [the self of person s and 
the world ] as possessi ng a limit, no tpossessing a limit, both, and neither. 
(9-12) The third [four based upon nirvana] are the views that a liberated self, after dying, 
will arise, will not arise, both and neither.  
(13-14) The fourth [two based upon the body and life] are the view of the body and life as 
one substance and the view of them as different substances.  
 
The view that the self of persons and the world are permanent, do not possess a limit, and 
enter a future rebirth, and the view that the Tathagata, after dying, will arise —are the views 
of the Samkyas, called the ‘fluctuating views’ since they engage their objects through an 
affirmative aspect.  
 The view that [the self of persons and the world] are impermanent, posses s a limit, and 
do not enter a future rebirth, and the Tathagata, after dying, will not arise —are the views of 
the Ayatas (Charvakas), called the “ collected views ” since they engage their objects through 
a negative aspect.   
 The views of both of those extr emes are the views of the Nirgranthas, called “spread  out 
views ” since they engage the affirmative and the negative in equal portions.  
 The views of inexpressibility as any of those extremes are the views of the Vatsiputriyas, 
called the “ gathered views ” since they engage through an aspect that negates both the 
affirmative and the negative. If one thinks about the mode of engagement of all of these, they are subsumed under the two, the fluctuating views and the collected views, since they 
are subsumed under  those bearing the aspects of either the affirmative or the negative. 
 Viewing the body and life as one substance is the view of the Vaisheshikas; viewing 
them as different substances is the view of the Samkyas. Those who view [the self of 
person s and the world] as both permanent and impermanent also view the nature as abiding 
permanently but its states as im permanent. The elimination of other qualms is of little 
necessity on this occasion, so I will not elaborate on it.   (15) A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom bearing the aspect of thusness,  the emptiness of 
true existence of the  fluctuating minds and so forth.  (16) A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom 
realizing that the realization of thusness by the Tathagata is taught to others as he
149 had 
understood it and is imputed . The natures of these aforesaid [exalted wisdoms ] are the 
knowledge characteristics of a knower of bases. 
 
                                              
149 Gyi emended to read gyis.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
379 Sutra says respectively:  
(15) “How does the Tathagata know forms in dependence on these Mothers? Thusness and 
unmis taken thusness and…”  
(16) “Having taken it as a basis of the realization of thusness, the understanding of thusness, the supreme knowing of thusness, and the correct indication of thusness, it is designated as 
thusness.” “In dependence on these Mothers, on e will attain clear complete  enlightenment 
[with respect to] this thusness.” “Due to having attained clear complete  enlightenment [with 
respect to] this thusness, one is called a ‘tathagata’ (‘one -gone -thus’).” “He taught it after 
having attained clear com plete  enlightenment.”

Therefore, what are those characteristics?  
 
• A bodhisattva’s exalted wisdom knowing in dependence on the path perfection of

characteristics  [ultimately] — 
are the characteristics of the applications of the knower of paths. They are not indicated

characteristics.  
 Sutra says:

characteristic of emptiness; it is signless; it is wishless.”

gods.”

either the composed or the uncomposed.”

tathagatas have attained clear complete  buddhahood without characteristics.”

other worlds.’”)

distinguished by the distinctions of the inconceivable, unequaled, and so forth —the sixteen

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
384 They are characterized by  the object- possessors of true sufferings and so forth that are 
distinguished by the disti nctions of understanding the inconceivable  dharmata of true 
sufferings , unequaled, and so forth—the sixteen characteristics of forbearances and 
knowledges of dharma and of subsequent knowledge  [dharma forbearance, dharma 
knowledge, subsequent forbearance, and subsequent knowledge] . Here the bases of the 
attributes are simply correlated with the path of seeing and indicated; know that the sixteen 
isolates of the attributes exist from the path of accumulation until  the end of [a sentient 
being’s] continuum.  
  Even the applications of the knower of bases in the continua of bodhisattvas are 
established to be greatly superior to the applications of hearers and solitary realizers. 
However, the manner of superiority has not been explicitly indicated here because, s ince the 
applications of the knower of bases exist also in hearers and solitary realizers, it should be 
known that they are not appropriate as the bases of attributes that are superior to any [of the 
applications of the three exalted knowers].

COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. (1) The inconceivable due to the strength of wisdom, well taken care 
of by the perfect complete buddhas and so forth, (2) unequa lled, (3) transcends 
comprehension, (4) transcends enumeration, (5) incorporating all arya persons, (6) those 
that are to be known by the wise beings, (7) thoroughly knowing the things that are not the 
objects of utility of the hearers and so forth, (8) kno wing very quickly the manifest 
knowledge that relies on one’s own system, (9) the entity of no decrease and no increase of 
all phenomena based upon the conventional and ultimate truths, (10) strongly accomplish ing 
the six perfections of generosity and so forth through purity of the three spheres, (11) correctly accomplishing  merit and exalted wisdom practiced over many eons by way of 
correct  application, (12) observing all phenomena without conceptualizing, (13) the support 
of bodhisattvas, the entitiness o f dharmadhatu, (14) the collection of causes that fully 
complete the perfections of prayer and so forth, (15) being taken care of by the method of a 
spiritual guide, and (16) not relishing adherence —the aforesaid natures of the sixteen are 
respectively the  distinctions that are the instants of true sufferings and so forth, by which the 
two—the knowers of paths and so forth of bodhisattvas and so forth —are superior to the 
paths of hearers and so forth, in being paths of distinction.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The applic ations of the knower of paths and the  exalted knower of all aspects that cultivate 
the two —the knowers of paths and so forth of bodhisattvas , buddhas,  and so forth —are 
the bases of characterization, applications superior to the lesser paths of hearers and so 
forth, in being paths of distinction. Further, they are characterized by the sixteen 
distinctions that are the instants , the object -possessors of true sufferings and so forth 
respectively.  
 Further, what are they? The four object -possessors of true sufferings are the four instants 
of forbearances and knowledges: (1) understanding the inconceivable dharmata of 
sufferings due to the strength of wisdom, well taken care of by the spiritual guides, the 
perfect complete buddhas , bodhisattv as and so forth, the empowering conditions , (2) 
realizing that it is unequaled due to the absence of anything alike , (3) realizing that 
dharmata perfectly transcends comprehension  by a valid cognizer of conventionalities , 
and (4) realizing that dharmata pe rfectly transcends enumeration  through counting. Sutra 
says, “Bhagavan, this perfection of wisdom abides closely due to being an inconceivable 
object, an unequaled object, an incomprehensible object, a non -enumerable object, and an 
object equal to the uneq ualed.”  
 The four object -possessors of true origins: (5) incorporating all  excellent qualities of all 
arya persons, (6) knowing those that are to be known by the wise beings  who know the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
386 modes and varieties , (7) thoroughly knowing the vast and profound things that are not 
the objects of utility of the hearers and so forth, and (8) knowing very quickly the 
manifest knowledge that relies on one’s own system , the Mahayana — having taken the 
four forbearances and knowledges of origins as the bases of the attribu tes, are the 
characterizers of superiority over the applications of hearers and solitary realizers .  
 Respectively, sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, this profound perfection of wisdom perfectly possesses the five perfections.”  
• “Bhagavan, this perfection of wisdom w ill be understood by those who are precise , fine, 
clever, and skilled.”  
• “That which is an abandonment and a knowledge of [persons] until  solitary realizers is a 
forbearance of a bodhisattva who has attained a forbearance with respect to the 
phenomenon that is not produced.”  
• “Those sons of the lineage or daughters of the lineage who, having heard this profound 
perfection of wisdom, asks questions about it, and having written it, gives its oral transmission and pays proper attention to it, should be asserted to attain nirvana very 
quickly.”  
 
 The four object -possessors of cessations: (9) realizing the entity of no decrease and no 
increase ultimately of all phenomena based upon the conventional and ultimate truths ; 
(sutra says, “This profoun d perfection of wisdom [does not] seek either decrease or 
increase.”); (10) strongly accomplish ing the six perfections of generosity and so forth 
through purity of the three spheres , the object of generosity, the act of generosity, and so 
forth being free from existing ultimately , (11) correctly  accomplishing  merit and exalted 
wisdom practiced over many eons by way of respectful continuous correct application ; 
(sutra says, “That bodhisattva having died and transferred from another buddha land or 
Tushita has  been born here.”),  and the exalted wisdom (12) observing all phenomena 
without conceptualizing  that is conjoined with method —characterize the four forbearances 
and knowledges of cessations as being superior to the applications of hearers and solitary 
realizers . 
 
The four of paths: (13)  possessing the lineage, the support of bodhisattvas’  paths , the 
entitiness of dharmadhatu, (14) possessing the collection of causes that fully complete 
the ten perfections of prayer and so forth, (15) being taken care of by the method of a 
spiritual guide  who reveals the complete path , and (16) not relishing adherence to any 
phenomenon of samsara or nirvana as truly existent. These four characterize the four 
forbearances and knowledges with respect to the Mahayana path as bei ng superior to the 
applications of hearers and solitary realizers . 
2B2C -2A1B -1C2B -2B3C -2. The reason for not explicitly indicating the objects to which they are superior  
COMMENTARY 
Because of that, [those] that are those are not explained because they are easy to understand by way of the characteristics of adherence and so forth being produced, which are separated 
from the distinctions that have been explained.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for not explicitly indicating the paths that are those  applications of 
hearers and solitary realizers here because they are easy to understand . The reason for that is 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
387 because they are easy to understand by way of the characteristics of being produced 
along with  adherence to true existence and so forth, which are separated from the 
distinctions  that have been explained.  Further, that is because the Mahayana path of 
seeing is explicitly indicated as possessing those attributes for the sake of proving that it is 
superior to the paths of hearers and solitary realizers.

aforesaid exactly according to the time, “not actualizing  the result of definite emergence of
