388 the three vehicles” —there are the seven functions of refuge and so forth of the knower of 
paths.  
 With the aim of accomplishing the revealing of all phenome na by150 an exalted knower of 
all aspects, there is the one deed that is supported on the exalted knower of all aspects.  
 In that way, the applications of the three exalted knowers of all are characterized by the 
aspects of function exactly; therefore they a re the functional characteristics.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In that way, the applications of the three exalted knowers of all are characterized by 
the bodhisattva’s exalted wisdoms included within the eleven aspects of function ( exactly  
as fire is characterized by tan gible object, heat) as applications that possess the special 
function of observing others’ welfare ; therefore they are the functional characteristics.  
 The functional characteristics of the applications of the knower of bases are the three 
functions of ben efit and so forth characterizing the applications of the knower of all  as 
being applications that possess special functions. What are they?  The functions of 
benefitting others with the aim of accomplishing the natures of (1) future benefit , 
placing sentient beings in the happiness of liberation , (2) the happiness of this life , such as 
pacifying suffering and mental unhappiness , and (3) the non -ripened state that is without 
suffering  by protecting them from the sufferings of samsara and causing them n ot to ripen 
[the sufferings of samsara] again. 
 Respectively, sutra says:  
• “Subhuti, the bodhisattvas will correctly engage in order to benefit the world.”  
• “The great bodhisattvas desiring unsurpassable complete enlightenment will liberate 
sentient beings  from suffering, mental unhappiness, and disturbance, and place them in 
nirvana, the truth of fearlessness.”  
• “The great bodhisattvas, having attained clear complete  buddhahood in the unsurpassable 
complete enlightenment will thoroughly protect sentient be ings from whatever 
sufferings that are experienced in cyclic existence.”  
 
The functions of the applications of the knower of paths  are the seven functions of refuge 
and so forth.  What are they?  The functions of benefitting others  with151 the aim of 
correctl y accomplishing : (1) the function of a refuge that places [sentient beings] in 
nirvana, the perpetual benefit, (2) the function of an abode that places [sentient beings] in 
the state that is  the opposite of the cause of suffering, (3) the function of a fri end that 
places [sentient beings] in realizing the equality of samsara and nirvana, (4) the function 
of an island, the entity of the support that realizes one’s and others’ welfare  by placing 
[sentient beings] in the liberation that is free from the moistu re of craving, which is 
concordant in property with dry [land] that is surrounded by water, (5) the function of a 
leader, accomplishing others’ temporal and final welfare, (6) the function of engaging in 
spontaneous accomplishment of the welfare of sentien t beings, and (7) these aforesaid 
exactly according to the time, “not actualizing  the perfect end, the result of definite 
emergence of the three vehicles , at an improper time .” 
 With the aim of accomplishing the revealing of all phenomena by an exalted kno wer 
of all aspects  in accordance with the mentality [of sentient beings] , there is the one deed

389 that is transitorily supported on an exalted knower of all aspects.  These are the eleven

abandoning their  signs, adoption of negative body stat es, (3) abandoning their  marks  
that are the causes , improper attention and so forth, and (4) distinguished by emptiness  
of conceptualizations of the objects to be adopted and objects to be discarded —the 
discordant classes, attachment and so forth, and the antidotes, non -attachment and so 
forth.

instances of attachment, aversion , and ignorance.”  
• “Those great bodhisattvas will become the nature of b eing devoid of the signs of 
attachment, aversion , and ignorance.”  
• “Those great bodhisattvas will become the nature of being devoid of the marks of 
attachment, aversion , and ignorance.”  
• “Subhuti, those great bodhisattvas who believe in this profound perfe ction of wisdom 
will become the nature of being devoid of the signs of attachment, aversion , and 
ignorance.”

(1) Hardship for sentient beings, who do not exist ultimately, to attain nirvana, (2) certainty

 (1) The application t hat undergoes hardship for infinite sentient beings, who do not exist 
ultimately, to attain nirvana  conventionally, (2) the application that accomplishes the 
certainty  of attaining buddhahood unequivocally with the characteristic of not falling 
into other vehicles, (3) the application that accomplishes  the three great objects of intent 
that are the supreme objects to be accomplished over a long time, (4) the application that realizes  phenomena that are the object of cultivation and the cultivator are not 
observed as existing ultimately , and (5) stopping all adherence to truly existent things —
five applications  like the aforesaid.  
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(1) “Although the great bodh isattvas seek the armor, saying, ‘I shall cause all sentient 
beings to fully attain nirvana,’ regarding that, they will undergo hardship with respect to 
sentient beings and those not observed as being imputed as sentient beings.”   
(2) “Those great bodhisa ttvas who seek the armor for sentient beings to attain nirvana do 
not fear the two states by way of hearers and solitary realizers.”  
(3) “The great bodhisattvas do not seek the armor for the sake of a trifling [number of] 
sentient beings. They seek the armor for the sake of all sentient beings fully attaining 
nirvana and the exalted wisdom that is an exalted knower of all aspects.”  
(4) “Bhagavan, the perfection of wisdom is profound; nobody meditates on it, nothing is meditated, and it is not meditated on in any way.”  
(5) “Some great irreversible bodhisattvas do not adhere to this profound perfection of wisdom. One should thoroughly investigate the great irreversible bodhisattvas.”

(1) observing the attributes of things that are included within  the knower of all and the

392 because there  are those like the applications realizing:  (1) observing  paths and bases, the

the thusness of forms.”  (7) “Bhagavan, these [phenomena] ranging from thusness  until the perfect end is profound.

2B2C -2A1B -2A1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. Skill in generating  in his continuum those ranging from the 
perfections of generosity and so forth until the exalted knower of all aspects, by wa y of 
knower -aspects observing [phenomena] as being devoid of  signs , is asserted as the partial 
concordance with liberation in this clearly and  completely  realizing all aspects.  
 
EXPLANATION  
It is asserted as the partial concordance with liberation that completes the type of path of 
attaining enlightenment over three countless eons  in this context of teaching the application 
that clearly and  completely realizes all aspects.  What [is asserted  as that partial 
concordance with liberation]?  
  It is the clear realization of the doctrine in the continuum of a person who is skilled 
in generating in his continuum those ranging from the perfections of generosity and so 
forth until  the exalted knower of all aspects due to practicing the achievings through 
realizati on with valid cognition, by way of knower -aspects that are  conjoined with the 
wisdom observing [phenomena] as being devoid of  signs of elaborations ultimately.  
 Having eliminated superimpositions by way of hearing and thinking regarding all types 
of clear realizations that are the twenty -one sets of uncontaminated exalted wisdom, which 
will be explained below, after that s by having practiced them, an experience with respect to 
all the clear realizations will be  produced. From then onwards, one is known to have 
initiated the beginning of the path of attaining enlightenment over three countless eons, 
entered the Mahayana path, and produced in the continuum a Mahayana partial concordance with liberation.  
 
QUALM: What is the criterion for having produced an exp erience like?  
RESPONSE: Through observing all sentient beings, one spontaneously engages in 
compassion bearing the aspect of desiring them to be utterly released from suffering. By 
having produced, for instance, that which is like kindness of a mother for her only son, it is 
said that one has elicited the experience of compassion. Inferring from this, one should 
understand the boundaries where one has elicited experience with respect to other paths.  
 With regard to the meaning of emptiness one familiarizes  oneself with it by way of a 
wisdom arisen from contemplation. Due to the imprint of that, when one views these things, 
they appear to the mental consciousness  like the reflection of a face in a mirror. By having 
attained stability [in it] with respect to the appearance to the mental consciousness , as 
though this present appearance would vanish if one were to blow on it, it is said that one has 
elicited an experience of the meditation on emptiness with a wisdom arisen from contemplation. However, a fully qu alified special insight observing emptiness exists [only] 
from the heat level of the path of preparation. For the sake of understanding that all the clear realizations in one’s continuum must be conjoined with the wisdom realizing emptiness, “ by 
way of kno wer-aspects observing [phenomena] as being devoid of  signs ” was mentioned 
[above].

2B2C -2A1B -2A2B -1A1. The  uncommon Mahayana going for refuge   
ROOT TEXT  
4.33a  Faith observing the buddhas and so forth;  
 
EXPLANATION  
Having initially generated a faith of conviction and a lucid faith observing all the causes of 
the Mahayana path as well as their results, the budd has and so forth, being skilled in them is 
called “skill in faith.” Like it has been said, “Furthermore, faith comes first, like a mother 
who gives birth,” know that faith is the root of all excellent qualities, and know that, having 
taken all the Mahayana  paths and results as objects of faith in one’s continuum, repeatedly 
familiarizing oneself with them establishes the root of the Mahayana path. Although refuge has been indicated as the principal meaning, skill in faith has been indicated first for the 
sake of identifying it as the root of the Mahayana path.   
 Sutra says, “Without separating from the perfection of wisdom and skillful means , 
contemplate in the mind the buddhas of the three times, their aggregates of ethics , and so 
forth, but do not take th em as signs. Know that if a great bodhisattva does not take the three 
doors of liberation as signs, he will attain the unsurpassable complete enlightenment without 
falling into those two, [the hearer’s vehicle and the solitary realizer’s vehicle], and with out 
dishonoring or degenerating [the bodhisattva vehicle].”

396 2B2C -2A1B -2A2B -1A2. The actual meaning  
ROOT TEXT  
4.33b  A mindfulness, the excellent attitude;  
 
EXPLANATION  
It has been mentioned that the excellent Mahayana attitude , the Mahayana generated mind, 
is “a mindfulness that is qualified by being a mindfulness that serves as an owner of 
omniscience.” For the sake of understanding that this is the criterion for being skilled in the entrance for entering into the Mahayana, the branch of skill in the specific mindfulness that 
is mindful of omniscience again and again has been mentioned. Sutra says: “Bhagavan, there is no doubt that any great bodhisattva who is not separated from the perfection of 
wisdom and skillful means will attain unsurpassable comp lete enlightenment.”

2B2C -2A1B -2A2B -1B2. Training in the last two perfections   
2B2C -2A1B -2A2B -1B1. Training in the deeds as a generality  
ROOT TEXT  
4.33c Joyous effort in the objects of utilization, generosity and so forth;  
 
EXPLANATION  
That qualified by (i) initiating joyous effort that takes the six perfections , generosity and 
so forth, as the objects of utilization, and (ii) initiating joyous effort in all Mahayana paths 
is called “skill in joyous effort.” Sutra says, “The great bodhisattvas, having initially generated the mind [of enlightenment], from then onwards, even though they perform 
generosity, do not take it  as a sign.”  
 The meaning of indicating “ training in the deeds through being qualified by joyous 
effort ” is as follows. It has been taught that even though one may have generated the mind, 
if one does not diligently initiate joyous effort in the deeds, the n that is simply a mere noble 
thought; one will not attain unsurpassable enlightenment. Therefore one should know that, when one trains in every division that is an instance of the deeds, one must qualify it with 
joyous effort.

4.33d  Non-conceptualizing meditative stabilization, and  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to common calm abiding, having observed any non -conceptual ized image, 
such as that of the exalted body of the Tathagata, by having familiarized with it in 
dependence on the nine steps of mental abidin gs, one practices until (i) one engages in it 
without exertion without need to rely upon the antidotes to laxity and excitement, and (ii) the bliss of physical and mental pliancy has been produced.   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
397  Uncommon concentration refers to meditative stabilizati on such as the Sky Treasury that 
does not conceptualize “It is this” with respect to the object of observation , in dependence 
on having previously achieved the above [common calm abiding], the perfection of 
concentration that is attained on the fifth groun d, and so forth. Sutra says, “Bhagavan, that is 
like this: the great bodhisattva, having generated the first mind [of enlightenment], from then onwards, does not observe that which will attain clear complete  enlightenment, that by 
which one will attain cle ar complete  enlightenment, and where one will attain clear 
complete  enlightenment —any phenomenon  ranging from forms [until ] the exalted knower 
of all aspects.”  
2B2C -2A1B -2A2B -1B2B. Training in the perfection of wisdom  
ROOT TEXT  
4.34ab  The wisdom that kno ws  
Phenomena in all ways and the five types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
In dependence on having attained a fully qualified calm abiding, one will be skilled in (i) the wisdom that knows the objects of knowledge, phenomena that are the modes and 
varieties, in all ways and (ii) the five types of objects . Both the special insights observing 
modes and observing varieties have been indicated here; the special insight observing 
varieties exists even from the path of accumulation and the special insight observing modes 
is attained from the heat level of the path of preparation. Know that it is attained once one is 
able to induce the bliss of pliancy through analysis with the wisdom that individually 
investigates the meaning of emptiness.  
 Sutra says, “Sons of the g ods, I have attained clear complete  enlightenment in all ways 
with regard to all phenomena; I have not observed any phenomena —that which will attain 
clear complete  enlightenment, that by which one will attain clear complete  enlightenment, 
and where one wil l attain clear complete  enlightenment.” Know the extensive presentation 
of these points from the stages of the paths of the perfections composed by  Je Rinpoche 
[Lama Tsongkhapa].

4.34cd  Complete enlightenment is asserted to be  
Easy to realize by the sharp, but difficult to realize by the dull.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding the intended trainees of the Ornament for Clear Realization , there are two types 
by way of level of faculty, because complete enlightenment is asserted to be easy to 
realize  and attain  by the sharp faculty bodhisattvas , but difficult to realize by the dull  
faculty bodhisattvas . 
 Sutra says, “In case I seek and contemplate the meaning of that taught by the Bhagavan 
in the teaching ‘It will be difficult for unsurpassable complete enlightenment to arise,’ then, Bhagavan, it will not be difficult to attain clear complete  enlightenment  in unsurpassable 
complete enlightenment” and “Venerable Subhuti, by that enumeration, it will be difficult to attain clear complete  enlightenment in unsurpassable complete enlightenment.”   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
398  The intended trainees of the Ornament for Clear Realization  are presented here as being 
definite as the two types: followers of faith and followers of Dharma; know that [the 
assertions that] those of middling faculty are explained separately, those of dull faculty 
apply themselves in the Hinayana, and the like have exp lained the meaning of this passage 
erroneously. The distinction in being dull or sharp in faculty, as explained above, lies in whether or not they have completed a fully qualified wisdom due to the degree of the force 
of their faith and the strength of joy ous effort observing all the stages of the Mahayana path.

2B2C -2A1B -2A2C -1. Distinctions in skill in  the five objects  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. Those that are not the entitinesses of the powers  bearing distinctions 
in: (1) faith, (2) joyous effort, (3) mindfulness, (4) meditative stabilization, and (5) wisdom, 
respectively are skilled in the five types  of objects: (1) the buddhas, (2) generosity, (3) 
perfect attitude, (4) not conceptualizing, and (5) full knowing of all aspects of all 
phenomena and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the bodhisattvas who are intended trainees of the Ornament for Clear 
Realization —are skilled in the five types  of objects, because respectively  they are skilled 
in the five types  of objects: (1) faith  observing all of the Maha yana path s and their results, 
the buddhas  and so forth , (2) joyous effort that is enthusiastic about all, generosity  and so 
forth , (3) mindfulness included within the generated mind, the perfect attitude, (4) the 
meditative stabilization that does not conc eptualize  with respect to all phenomena , and 
(5) full knowing exactly of all aspects of all phenomena that are modes and varieties and 
so forth.   
2B2C -2A1B -2A2C -2. Differences in progression on the paths of those of sharp and dull faculties  
COMMENTARY  
Although indeed in that way, all are not attaining unsurpassable enlightenment, because this 
is the nature: in this way, unsurpassable perfect complete enlightenment is easy to realize by 
the sharp ones, great faith and so forth, but it is difficult to rea lize by those dull ones.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Although both [types of] trainees of the Ornament for Clear Realization are indeed similar 
in being skilled in the five objects, faith and so forth, in that way, all trainees of the 
Ornament for Clear Realization are not similar in easily attaining unsurpassable 
enlightenment, because this is the nature: in this way, unsurpassable perfect complete 
enlightenment is easy to realize  and attain by the sharp ones  who abide in the  great  
vehicle, faith and so forth, but i t is difficult to realize and attain by those dull ones who 
abide in the  great vehicle .  
 Great faith and so forth is the great vehicle, while small and middling faith and so forth 
are the small and middling vehicles; know that, in dependence on the mere i mputation of the 
name “bodhisattva” to hearers in what is overtly seen in the sutras, delineating divisions of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects

explanation of the meaning of the passage.  
2B2C -2A1B -2A2C-3. Meanings that have been implied i mplicitly  
COMMENTARY 
Implicitly this is implied: “The middling realize the enlightenment of solitary realizers and 
the small realize the enlightenment of hearers.”   
 
EXPLANATION  
Implicit to what has been explicitly i ndicated in the root text, this is implied: “ Those on 
the path of accumulation abiding i n the  middling vehicle, middling faith and so forth,  
realize and attain the enlightenment of solitary realizers and those on the path of 
accumulation abiding in the  small vehicle, small faith and so forth, realize and attain the 
enlightenment of hearers.”  The meaning that has been explained is implicitly implied by 
the root text, because the superiority of difficult and easy attainment of unsurpassable 
enlightenment by t he sharp and dull bodhisattvas who abide in the great faith and so forth 
has been explicitly indicated and the attributes of the other two have not been explicitly 
indicated.  Know that it is erroneous and at variance with the Acarya’s intention to explain 
the explicit and implicit meanings differently  [from those presented above] .

So the root text is said. With re spect to this [application that] clearly  and completely  realizes 
all aspects, to observe sentient beings with five aspects: the minds of (1) equality, (2) love, 
(3) benefit, (4) absence of anger, and (5) non -harmfulness, and five other aspects: (6) the 
mind of father and mother, (7) the mind of brother and sister, (8) the mind of son and 
daughter, (9) the mind of friend and companion, and (10) the mind of relatives and kinsmen —is asserted as transformed -into-heat.

The subject —with respect to th is application that  clearly  and completely  realizes all 
aspects, transformed -into-heat—is asserted to observe sentient beings, because it 
observes sentient beings with five aspects: (1) the mind of equality  cultivating 
equanimity that, observing all sentie nt beings, stops prejudice due to grasping and aversion , 
(2) love  that bears the aspect of affection toward s all, (3) long -term benefit, (4) the absence 
of anger  due to a deluded mind, and (5) non- harmfulness due to being non- injurious , and 
because it observes sentient beings with  five other aspects: (6) the mind of father  and 
mother towards elderly men and women , (7) the mind of brother and sister  towards 
peers , (8) the mind of son and daughter  towards the juvenile , (9) the mind of mutually 
agreeable  friend and trustworthy companion, and (10) the mind of paternal relatives and 
maternal  kinsmen.  The individual delineations into small, middling, and great are merely a 
characterization; know that all observes all  
 These indicate (i) the cultivation of love bearing the aspect of affection prior to 
cultivating compassion, (ii) recognizing all sentient beings as having been one’s mother, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
402 remembering their kindness, and wanting to reciprocate, as its causes, and (iii) t he 
cultivation of equanimity prior to the three.   
2B2C -2A1B -2B1C -1B. Peak  
COMMENTARY 
In brief, by way of abandoning and adopting non -virtue and virtue respectively, by means of 
that bearing the essence of having turned  away and having engaged, there are t wo aspects 
connecting others to turning away from negativity and to abiding in virtue, and the two 
aspects  expressing praise for others who have engaged  similarly and concordant actions. 
Observing sentient beings with these “infinite aspects” by way of the  classification of 
properties is transformed -into-peak.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Transformed -into-peak  observes sentient beings . In brief, there are (i) two aspects : 
connecting others to turning away from negativity respectively through oneself abiding 
in having turne d away by way of abandoning non- virtue and connecting  others to 
abiding in virtue through oneself abiding by means of that  bearing the essence of having 
engaged by way of adopting virtue, and (ii) the two aspects : expressing praise for other  
persons  who ha ve engaged similar  to oneself without being urged  and cultivating joy in 
concordant  actions. Observing sentient beings  with these “infinite aspects” by way of 
the classification of properties  that are isolates  is peak .

COMMENTARY

by the aspects that connect exactly in accordance with the objects of observation and 
aspects, expression of praise, and concordance, observing the truths of suffering s and so 
forth of the supports of self and others is forbearance.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Due to the divisions of the supports who are oneself, the one acting to place, and others , 
the objects to be placed, on transformed -into-peak, by the aspects that connect others to 
virtue exactly in accordance with the objects of observation and aspects  that have been 
explained , expression of praise  of those who have engaged  without being urged , and 
concordance  due to being very joyous , observing all sentient beings  by means of placing 
the supports of others in knowing the four truths, through oneself abiding in knowing the 
four truths of suffering s and so forth is forbearance.  
 Although the divisions of the small, middling, and great have not been clearly correlated  
for the later three occasions, the complete meanings of objects of observation and aspects 
that are to be cultivated on all three occasions have been indicated.

2B2C -2A1B -2B2. Refuting wrong conceptions concerning the sequence of the six clear realizations  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2A. Repudiating incorrect positions  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2B. Presenting the accurate position  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2C. Rejecting contradictions  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2A. Repudiating incorrect positions  
Although it is suitable to slightly apply this passage as a rejection of [the criticism of] 
repetition of the three paths, explaining it as solely that is an explanation that has cast away 
the context meaning, because qualms regarding the last two of the e ight clear realizations, 
the subject matter of the eight chapters, are minor, and this is set forth by the Acarya for the sake of refuting wrong conceptions concerning the sequence of the first six clear 
realizations. This is because: (i) although it appea rs as though the sequence of the clear 
realizations in terms of unitary paths has been posited as our own system in the Condensed 
Commentary , having refute that system here, another sequence was asserted, and (ii) 
although it appears as though the system o f unitary paths with respect to the eight clear 
realizations has been asserted in the section on making a connection at the [explanation] of 
the truth body in [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary, that [each of the eight clear realizations 
] has the same meanin g as the full path explained here has been taught many times on other 
occasions of the Great Commentary. Further, I think that perhaps that explanation from the 
Condensed Commentary, not being the assertion of the Acarya himself, has been written 
merely as  a statement of the assertion of another previous master, because that system has 
been specifically  refuted in the two, the Great Commentary and the Small  Commentary.  
 Someone, having taken this passage solely as a rejection of [the criticism of] repetiti on, 
explained that, in this [chapter] and what comes below, the path of preparation and the path 
of seeing are imputed only to the exalted wisdom of the pure grounds. This is clearly either 
a repudiation of the Acarya’s assertion or an expression of not ha ving even slightly realized 
the explanation in the Great Commentary, because the refutation will be explained. When 
explaining the path of preparation explained in this [chapter] and that of the chapter on peak [application], if the path of preparation wer e designated by way of the mere explanation 
placing others in the finality of all excellences, destroying all dormancies, and fully completing the qualities of the three exalted knowers, then also the path of preparation of 
the first chapter would be  simil ar, because the purity of the three doors, pure signs, pure 
lands, the meditative stabilization of going heroically, prediction, the exhaustion of clinging, a non -conceptual meditative stabilization, and so forth have been explained.
  
 If one were to expla in those as merely abiding in the cause, cultivation through belief and 
conviction, hidden prediction and so forth, then the reply that the other is also similar would 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
404 have been eaten by a crow —non-existent! The conceptions that have been explained in the 
context of the path of preparation in the Great Commentary  have been explained to be the 
objects of abandonment of the path of seeing and path of meditation included within the 
peak application, (i) because, according to the assertion that it is designated , it follows that 
the objects of abandonment and the antidotes of peak application between the first and the seventh grounds would not be explicitly indicated on any occasion; if that were the case, it 
would contradict the Acarya’s assertion that has just been explained, and (ii) because, in the 
context of the supreme mundane quality that has just been explained, in response to the 
dispute that it contradicts the explanation (in the Hinayana scriptural baskets as well as the 
commentaries on their intention) that the supreme mundane quality is only one instant, the 
reply that there is a difference between the hearers’ accomplishment of their own welfare and the bodhisattvas’ accomplishment of others’ welfare through stages is given in [Haribhadra’s] Great Com mentary ; it has not been taught that “What has been explained in 
this chapter is not the path of preparation,” and at the juncture of complete irreversibility, the irreversible learner sangha has already been explained.  
 Now, also the teaching in [Haribhad ra’s] Great Commentary  that the characteristics of 
the non -learner sangha are to be explained would also be inappropriate, because it is 
incorrect to differentiate those who abide on the eighth ground into learners and non -
learners. There would be infinite  contradictions with the assertions of Acarya [Haribhadra], 
such as contradiction with the teaching that even in the context of the someone on the path 
of seeing who is irreversible, the instant of forbearance of subsequent knowledge with 
respect to true s ufferings is related to the subsequent knowledge.   
 In this [chapter] and what comes below, besides the Acarya proving that the path of 
preparation and path of seeing are fully qualified, there is not a mere bit of explanation by him explaining them to be  imputed. All these erroneous explanations are mistaken on 
account of not having understood how to explain well the meaning of the words of this passage; these will be explained.  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2B. Presenting the accurate position  
COMMENTARY  
By divisions of cultivating all aspects, paths, and bases, respectively regarding the three 
types of clear realizations, the exalted knower of all aspects and so forth , they are realized, 
the supramundane paths of seeing and meditation, through having been preceded by 
realizing the mundane partial concordance with definite discrimination. On the three, clearly  
completely realizing all aspects and so forth, through the distinctions of the occasions of higher and higher cultivation, since the uncontaminated exalted wisdoms i ncluded within 
the paths of distinction in all ways, are produced through the stages of the small, the  
middling, and the  great, in order to refute that they are produced together, that they have 
been taught with the convention “partial [concordance] with d efinite discrimination” and so 
forth should be known.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Since these refute wrong conceptions concerning the sequence of the six clear realizations, 
they should be known in this way; they should be known in the way  Guru Suvarnadvipa (i) 
has taugh t that the conventions of the earlier preceding mundane partial concordance with 
definite discrimination and the later partial [concordance with] definite [discrimination] and so forth are to be applied mutually to both the earlier and the later, and (ii) has taught that 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
405 the uncontaminated is empty of attachment and so forth. Having taught the convention 
“partial [concordance] with definite discrimination”  and so forth in the chapter on the 
exalted knower of all aspects  should be known as being purposeful , because it is in order 
to refute that the exalted knowers of all aspects that are the topics of principal causes and 
results are produced together —in which they are cultivated on a single continuity only 
during the occasions of the path of accumulation and  the path of preparation, and not 
cultivated from then onwards. One is able to refute that they are produced together, since 
they are produced through the stages of the small, the middling, and the great on the 
occasions of the three [paths of] preparation , seeing and meditation . That follows, because 
they are cultivated and  realized  on all three —the supramundane paths of seeing and 
meditation through having been preceded by realizing and attaining the mundane 
partial concordance with definite discriminatio n with respect to the occasion of the 
exalted knower of all aspects that are the topics of principal causes and results . Similarly, 
having taught the conventions “[paths of] preparation, seeing and meditation” in the chapters on the two types of clear real izations that are the knower of paths and the knower 
of bases —“and so forth” [in the commentary above] —is purposeful, because all three paths 
have been indicated  by divisions of cultivating all aspects  of the knowers  of paths and 
bases , impermanence and so  forth, on the occasions of all three paths. [The path of 
meditation] is the path of meditation implied in the chapter on the knower of bases.  
 Furthermore, that is also for the sake of refuting that a knower of paths is produced only 
from the first until  the sixth grounds and a knower of bases only on the seventh ground, 
together in one continuity. One should know that having taught the conventions that are the actual names, “partial [concordance] with definite discrimination” and so forth, on the 
three  occasions of types of clear realizations , clearly  completely realizing all aspects and 
so forth, is purposeful , because it is in order to refute production together in a single 
continuity in which the application in complete aspects is cultivated only on the  eight 
ground, and not cultivated elsewhere. It follows that one is able to refute production 
together on only those occasions, since the applications in complete aspects (that are 
without the contamination  of the afflictions with respect to the mode of ap prehension, 
exalted wisdoms included within the paths of distinction  that are superior in all factors 
and ways  to the paths of hearers and solitary realizers through the distinctions of the 
occasions of higher and higher cultivation  of the application in c omplete aspects)  are 
produced through the stages of the small, the middling, and the great included within 
the three [paths of] preparation, seeing, and meditation . 
  Likewise, the purpose of having taught all three fully qualified paths in the chapters of  
the peak application and the serial application is also for the sake of making known that they 
are cultivated on the occasions of all three paths, through refuting the wrong conception that 
the peak application is cultivated only on the ninth ground and t he first part of the tenth, 
while the serial application is cultivated only on the later part of the tenth ground, together 
in one continuity. Since there are no points of doubt regarding the instantaneous application 
and the truth body, they have not been mentioned.  
2B2C -2A1B -2B2C. Rejecting contradictions  
[Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
406 OPINION: Since conceptualizations that are of such aspects would have been abandoned 
previously, why are they indicated as objects to be abandoned on this occasion  of realizing 
the excellent finality?  RESPONSE: There is no fault, because the sutras such as the Sutra Unraveling the Thought  
teach that the thorough confusions (that hinder (i) the subsequent engagement in the subtle secrets and (ii) the great clairvoya nces) and their seeds are abandoned on the tenth ground.”  
 For this reason, masters of the past say that, since the causes of the latencies of the thorough 
confusions explained above are perfectly severed, due to being the entitiness that realizes 
the exc ellent finality, the bearers of the causes, such types of the four conceptualizations of 
apprehende ds and apprehende rs, are definitely abandoned on the clear realizations of peak 
application related to the ten grounds; on other occasions, [the abandonments ] are temporal 
abandonments. 
 It is clear that others have not even slightly understood the meaning of that passage, 
because each of the two thorough confusions is abandoned on each of the ten grounds, but 
the seeds of the two —the thorough confusion regard ing the state of the subtle secrets, and 
the thorough confusion that hinder the actions of the great clairvoyances —are the 
corresponding objects of abandonment of the tenth ground, and since they have been 
indicated to be exhaustively abandoned on that occ asion, there would not be even the 
slightest reason to explain that the peak application is only an exalted wisdom of the pure 
grounds. In some corrupted versions of the Great Commentary , it is said that “Both the 
earlier and the later, the tenth ground” and in some, “With respect to the former, it is also 
abandoned on the ten grounds.” These are not accurate. The version that says “the former is 
related to the tenth ground and the latter to the ten grounds” is correct.  
 Hence the meaning of this passage is as follows. The conceptualizations explained in the 
context of peak application were explained through being distinguished by the object of 
observation and the apprehension of an aspect . Therefore, these conceptualizations are 
simply just the manifest ones. The dispute is: “Have not most of these already been abandoned on the occasion  of the path of preparation? How are they presented here as 
objects of abandonment of the excellent final ity, the peak application of the path of seeing 
and the peak application of the path of meditation that are the excellent culmination? 
Because only the seeds are abandoned there.” The reply to that is as follows. The 
abandonment merely of the manifest [con ceptualizations by] the paths that are those is a 
temporal abandonment; it is not an abandonment by means of having come to bear the 
property of not being appropriate to be produced again. The peak applications of the path of 
seeing and of the path of medi tation act to destroy also the manifest [conceptualizations] by 
means of having abandoned the seeds of the conceptions. Further,  they are sequentially 
abandoned by the peak applications of the ten grounds, and the seeds of the last two 
thorough confusions are abandoned on the occasion of the tenth ground.  
 If the conceptualizations of the apprehendeds and the apprehenders have not been 
indicated through having been distinguished by the objects of observation and aspects, then 
one would not even realize of w hich object of abandonment they are seeds. Therefore, they 
are explained in that way.  
 The “excellent finality” is said with respect to the mere peak application; explaining it as 
solely an exalted wisdom of the tenth ground is inaccurate.   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
407 2B2C -2A1B -2C. Explanation of the stages in which the special clear realizations are produced in the continuum 
in dependence on those [previous stages]  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1. Explanation of the signs of irreversibility from unsurpassable enlightenment  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2. Explanation  of the special paths that act to achieve the three exalted bodies 
2B2C -2A1B -2C1. Explanation of the signs of irreversibility from unsurpassable enlightenment  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A. Meaning of the generality  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B. Meaning of the branches  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A.  Meaning of the generality  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -1. Divisions of irreversible persons 
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -2. Meaning of irreversibility  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -3. The mode of inference from the signs  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -1. Divisions of irreversible persons 
Even though s ome particular persons are of indefinite lineage, they  will not proceed to 
another path even slightly beginning from the Mahayana path of accumulation onwards, 
and, since it is not possible for them  to degenerate from the Mahayana generated mind, they  
will definitely attain unsurpassable enlightenment. Still, they  are not posited as possessing 
the signs of irreversibility. Likewise, [Maitreya’s  Ornament for the Great Vehicle Sutras 
(Mahayanasutralamkara )] says:  
 
Those who correctly practice  
Compassion, patience, beli ef and virtue  
Prior to the applications  
Are to be known as [possessing] the signs of lineage.  
 
Like it has been said, one who, prior to cultivating the applications, (i) possesses great 
compassion and vast patience observing sentient beings, and (ii) poss esses belief in the 
extensive Mahayana Dharma and the practice of extensive virtue such as generosity is one 
possessing the Mahayana lineage. Nevertheless, one is not posited to have attained a sign of 
irreversibility. Therefore, one is posited to have att ained irreversibility upon possessing 
those signs that will be explained. Regarding those, those of sharp faculty are presented to attain a sign of irreversibility from the heat level of the path of preparation, those of 
middling faculty from the path of s eeing, and those  [of dull faculty ], no matter how dull in 
faculty, from the eighth ground.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -2. Meaning of irreversibility  
The meaning of irreversibility is: (i) from the point of view of attitude, one has destroyed the opportunity for the production of a manifest attitude seeking liberation for the welfare of 
oneself alone, and (ii) from the point of view of application, one possesses a stable 
realization of special method and wisdom.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1A -3. The mode of inference from the signs  
From the sign of possessing special conventions of speech that reveal the complete class of 
Mahayana paths such as emptiness, one infers the presence of a special wisdom. From the 
sign of possessing special conventions of body that cannot bear the sufferings  of sentient 
beings, such as the body hairs standing on end and tears coming to their eyes, one infers the 
possession of a special Mahayana method in persons who possess them. From the sign of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
408 possessing those special method and wisdom, one infers that com plete enlightenment is 
suitable to arise due to the powerful potentiality of the cause by means of having destroyed 
the opportunity for the production of the manifest attitude explained above.  
 In relation to the sutra passage “A bodhisattva who possesses  those aspects, signs , and 
marks is to be held as one who is irreversible,” [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary  says:  
 
“[A bodhisattva who possesses those aspects, signs , and marks is] to be held [as 
one who is irreversible]” teaches inference in the manner of [Dharmakirti's] 
Commentary on [Dignaga's] 'Compendium of Valid Cognition'  
(Pramanavarttikakarika , stanza 1.9 ): 
 
That which is inferred to produce a result  
By way of a cause that is a potentiality, 
Because of not relying upon another object,  
Is described as being the nature.   
 
I shall explain the other  remaining oneselsewhere.

EXPLANATION  
The signs of irreversibility from complete enlightenment  of sharp faculty bodhisattvas 
abiding on the heat, the peak, the forbearance as well as the supreme mundane quali ty 
should be known, because they should be known by way of those twenty signs.  That 
follows because they should be known by way of having turned away from adherence to  
forms and so forth  as truly existent , until the activities that please the buddhas . 
 Sutra says:  
Subhuti asked, “Bhagavan, through how many aspects, signs and marks is a great 
bodhisattva irreversible from complete enlightenment?” In reply, [the Bhagavan said,] 
“Subhuti, you should know that when a great bodhisattva is irreversible with respe ct to 
[phenomena], from turning away from forms until unsurpassable complete enlightenment, 
he is irreversible.”  
“Subhuti, when a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, thinking this, he is 
not separated from those activities with respect t o training similarly to what has been taught 
by the tathagatas and, through those attentions possessing the perfection of wisdom, those activities do not degenerate from the perfection of generosity… regarding thinking not 
degenerating with respect to omni science, which bodhisattva is aware of the action of the 
maras? Thinking that he does not degenerate from unsurpassable complete enlightenment, 
Subhuti, you should know that the great bodhisattva who possesses those aspects, signs, and 
marks is irreversibl e.

EXPLANATION  
That those are the signs of irreversible sharp faculty [bodhisattvas] on the path of preparation should be known by way of these twenty signs , because by the eleven types  of 
signs of one abiding on the heat , the six types  of one abiding on the peak, the two types  of 
one abiding on the forbearance , and the one type  of one abiding on the supreme mundane 
quality , respectively, that those  are the signs that the sharp faculty bodhisattvas abiding 
on the heat, the peak, the forbearance, and the supreme mundane quality are irreversible from unsurpassable enlightenment should be known.  
 The ele ven signs of one abiding on the heat are as follows. The subject —a sharp faculty 
bodhisattvas abiding on the heat —is irreversible from unsurpassable enlightenment, because 
he has attained the signs of (1) having turned away from  some manifest adherence to  
phenomena, forms and so forth as truly existent , due to having realized that all 
phenomena are devoid of  entitiness  ultimately , (2) extinguishing manifest doubt as to 
whether or not the correct objects of refuge are so through having attained faith in the  
Three Jewels due to having understood them by the force of the wisdom arisen from 
                                             
 
153 Dzong -kha-pa’s Golden Rosary explains that “nature” ( chos nyid ) refers to “reason” ( rgyu mtshan ). 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
413 meditation that observes emptiness , (3) due to having fulfilled prayers to be reborn 
according to one’s wishes by the force of compassion, possessing the extinguishment of 
the eight states of non -freedom, namely, birth (a) with wrong views  deprecating actions 
and their results , (b-d) in the hells, as a hungry ghost, as an animal  by the force of karma ; 
and birth (e) not hearing a buddha’s words, and (f) in a remote land where there is no 
movement of the four types of retinue ; and birth (g) with incomplete faculties  in which 
the mental power and tongue sense power are  defective , with the properties of being 
stupid and mute, and (h) as a long- life god , one who is absorbed in With out 
Discrimination , (4) possessing  connection of oneself and others to virtuous qualities  due 
to being a bodhisattva who is disheartened  through compassion  that has been attained by 
the force of experience , (5) possessing  generosity and so forth thoroughly dedicated , 
“May the results ripen as objects of other sentient beings ,” due to practicing generosity 
and so forth through exchanging self and others, (6) not having a qualm even 
concerning  the profound meaning through having correctly realized the mode of  
abidance of phenomena with a wisdom arisen from meditation , (7) possessing  actions of 
body, speech, and mind that are motivated by a loving attitude due to having engaged in 
benefitting others  through the attitude that wishes all sentient beings to posses s happiness 
not degenerating , (8) not being associated with the five obscurations  such as the 
aspiration for the desire realm, due to having  attained the  fulfillment of the applications  
that strive resolutely for others’ welfare , (9) possessing the destruc tion of all dormancies 
of ignorance , bad views, and so forth due to having cultivated the antidotes  to those by 
the force of a wisdom arisen from meditation realizing emptiness , (10) possessing 
mindfulness and introspection in all behavior through constant  equipoise  by means of 
the mind never becoming distracted by an erroneous object at any time , and (11) being  
irreproachable, clean due to excellent utilization of dwelling, clothing , and so forth 
through familiarization with non -erroneous hygienic behavior .  
 The six signs of the peak are as follows. The subject —a sharp faculty bodhisattva abiding 
on the peak level of the path of preparation —(1) possesses non- occurrence  of the eighty 
thousand types of micro- organisms in the body, through having attained the roots of 
virtue that serve as a cause for non -abiding nirvana, which are  superior to  those of 
bodhisattvas of middling faculty abiding on the peak of  the mundane  and those below , (2) 
possesses absence of a crooked mind that is an attention to one’s wel fare motivated by 
deceit and dissimilation through pure bodhisattva’s behavior, roots of virtue, (3) 
possesses correctly adopting the excellent qualities of training —wearing rags taken 
from the rubbish dump and so forth—through disregard for gain and honor  and so 
forth  by especially relying upon the antidotes to attachment to gain and honor, such as food 
and clothing, (4) possesses absence  of miserliness, corrupted ethics , and the like 
contrary to those  six perfections  through practicing the special deeds o f the six 
perfections, generosity and so forth, (5) progresses to possess the yoga of the perfection 
of wisdom, that which does not contradict dharmata due to emptiness of true existence through correctly gathering all phenomena  in the emptiness of true ex istence with a 
wisdom arisen from meditation realizing emptiness , (6) desires hell for others’ welfare 
through having taken all migratory sentient beings  as one’s own,  like an only child, due 
to having especially familiarized with equalizing self and others and exchange of self and 
others .   
 The two signs of one abiding on the forbearance are as follows.  The subject—a sharp 
faculty bodhisattva abiding on the forbearance level —(1) possesses insusceptibility to

414 being led by other  teachers to other paths  because of possessing it through the nature154 
of conviction in the realization of the meaning of the mode of abidance , and (2) realizes 
that the maras who teach a fabricated counterfeit path such as fasting and relying on the 
five fires are maras through sk ill in knowing the methods that act to attain  buddhahood;  
 The one sign of one abiding on the supreme mundane quality is as follows. The subject —
a sharp faculty bodhisattva abiding on that [supreme mundane quality] —has attained (1) the 
pleasing of the buddhas in all activities of the three doors because of practicing the special 
deeds of the six perfections through purity of the three spheres.

2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2B2C -1A. Aspects of the instants of true sufferings 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Through emptiness of [existence by way of] their own character, 
having turned away from the conceptions of  phenomena such as forms, (2) through having 
been blessed by buddh as and so forth, stability of the minds of unsurpassable 
enlightenment, (3) through having accomplished the special Mahayana Dharma, having 
turned the mind away from the vehicles of the hearers and solitary realizers, and (4) through 
the strength of discerning phenomena, has perfectly and completely exhausted the link of 
birth in the concentrations and formless absorptions and so forth —these four are the aspects 
of sufferings.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —a middling faculty bodhisattva abiding on the path of se eing—(1) possesses 
having turned away from the  seeds of the acquired conceptions of phenomena such as 
forms  as truly existent , through having directly realized the emptiness of [existence by 
way of] their own character  ultimately , (2) possesses stability of the  ultimate and 
conventional minds of unsurpassable enlightenment, through having been blessed by 
buddhas and so forth to not degenerate from those two minds , (3) possesses having 
turned the mind away from the vehicles of the hearers and solitary reali zers, because 
he has destroyed the opportunity for producing an awareness seeking liberation for the sake of oneself alone through having accomplished the special Mahayana Dharma, and (4) 
has perfectly and thoroughly exhausted the link of potential that is  the cause for birth in 
the form realms and so forth through the force of that karma , even though he has attained 
the concentrations and formless absorptions, because he has exhausted it through the 
strength of discerning the mode of abidance of phenomena with a yogic direct 
perceiver— these four are the aspects of sufferings. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
417 2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2B2C -1B. Aspects of the instants of true origins  
COMMENTARY 
(5) By having separated from non -virtue, a light body and mind, (6) through the force of 
skillful means in su bduing sentient beings, utilization of desires without adherence, (7) 
through having seen the shortcomings of objects, always chaste conduct, and (8) by the 
nature of an excellent being, perfectly pure articles of livelihood —these four are the aspects 
of origin.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —a middling faculty bodhisattva abiding on the path of seeing —(5) possesses a 
light body and mind, the special bliss of pliancy, because he is distinguished by having 
separated  from the seeds of non -virtue, the objects of aba ndonment of the path of seeing , 
(6) possesses utilization of desires without adherence  that is craving for the objects of 
desire even though he abides within the household , because he abides in that way through 
the force of skillful means in subduing senti ent beings, (7) possesses always chaste 
conduct, because he abides in chaste conduct  through directly having seen  the 
shortcomings of objects  that are included within the attributes of the desire realm , and (8) 
possesses perfectly pure articles of liveliho od, because he has abandoned all wrong 
livelihoods by the nature of an excellent being who is empowered with respect to not 
forgetting the mind of enlightenment —these four are the aspects of origin.

2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2B2C -2. Eliminating qualms  
COMMENTARY  
In that way, when these instants of forbearances and knowledges which are included within 
the sixteen aspects have been correctly attained, since the pure mundane one bearing the 
aspect of not adhering to apprehendeds and apprehenders is included within th e mind of 
subsequent attainment, because the results concordant with it, having turned  away from 
discrimination of forms  and so forth, are generated, they are the characteristics of 
irreversibility of a bodhisattva abiding on the path of seeing. They are e xplained through 
designation  in order to make known that “the conventions of yogis are only concordant with 
these realizations at all [times] with the exception from the point of view of the purpose of taming sentient beings;” otherwise, how would the inst ants in the continua of yogis which 
are known by themselves be characteristics that produce conviction in others?  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM:  In that way, these instants of forbearances and knowledges  are the 
characteristics of irreversibility of a bodhisattva ab iding on the path of seeing.  Are 
these instants of forbearances and knowledges not meditative equipoise? How are they 
correct effect signs for inferring irreversibility in the perspective of the awareness of other 
persons?  
RESPONSE: The special conventions  of body and speech  are explained through 
designation  by the names “forbearances” and “knowledges.” There is a reason for imputing 
the special conventions of body and speech of a middling faculty bodhisattva abiding on the path of seeing in this manner, be cause they [the special conventions of body and speech] are 
generated in dependence on them [the forbearances and knowledges]. That follows because 
when these instants of forbearances and knowledges that are meditative equipoise which 
are included within the sixteen aspects have been correctly attained  during meditative 
equipoise , since the pure mundane subsequent attainment  bearing the aspect of not
156 
adhering to apprehendeds and apprehenders as truly existent even though  apprehendeds 
and apprehenders appear dualistically  is included within the mind of subsequent 
attainment, the results concordant with meditative equipoise itself, the special 
conventions of body and speech due to having turned away from discrimination that is 
the adherence of forms and so forth, are generated.    There is a purpose for imputing the special conventions of body and speech of one on the 
path of seeing with the name “meditative equipoise,” because it is in order to make known that “the conventions of body and speech of yogis who have attained a ground are only 
concordant with these realizations of meditative equipoise at all times  with the exception 
from the point of view of the purpose of taming sentient beings.”  
                                             
 
156 Ma added in accordance with Haribhadra’s commentary above.

420  The damage to fact is as follows. The subject —the instants  of meditative equipoise that 
exist  in the continua of yogis which are known by themselves  individually —how would 
they be correct effect signs, characteristics that produce conviction regarding 
irreversibility in the perspective of other  persons of intelligen ce inferior to themselves ? It 
follows that that would not be feasible, because it is not that they become direct objects of utilization of persons of intelligence inferior to themselves. You asserted the sign and the 
predicate. Alternatively, it follows th at these signs explicitly indicated here would be 
unsuitable to be correct effect signs for inferring irreversibility in the perspective of persons 
of intelligence inferior to themselves, because they are the meditative equipoise of the path 
of seeing.

After that are the characteristics of irreversibility of one abiding on the path of meditation.

EXPLANATION  
The signs of irreversibility of one abiding on the path of seeing hav e been explained. After 
that it is appropriate to explain  the characteristics of irreversibility of one abiding on the 
path of meditat ion. However, there is a reason for first explaining the  distinction of the 
path of meditation for the time being , because it occurs in the system of the yogi who 
has gained accomplishments, the great trailblazer, Acarya Dharmakirti, that “without 
apprehen ding the distinction, an awareness of  the possessor of distinction will not be 
produced,” therefore  the basis of distinction will be explained as a branch of realization.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1A -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.52  With respect to the path of meditation, profound,  
Profound emptiness and so forth.  
The profound is freedom from the extremes  
Of superimposition and deprecation.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
421 EXPLANATION  
The subject —with respect to the path of meditation —its mode of abidance is  profound,  
because its mode of ab idance is thusness that is qualified by profound emptiness and so 
forth. The subject —that mode of abidance —is profound, because it is a dharmadhatu that  
is freedom from the extremes of superimposition and deprecation.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1A -3. Commentary  
COM MENTARY  
So the root text is said. Since form does not exist in emptiness and so forth, and emptiness 
and so forth are not other than form, respectively that which is free from the extremes of 
superimposition and deprecation in emptiness and so forth is profound.  It is emptiness and 
so forth. Because of possessing the profound, the path of meditation is profound.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Respectively , [emptiness of form] is  free from the extreme of superimposition in 
emptiness and so forth, because form does not exist ultimately in emptiness and so 
forth ; [emptiness of form] is free from the extreme of deprecation, since emptiness and so 
forth are not an other that is a different entity than form. The subject —that which is 
thusness free from superimposition and deprecation —is profound , because  it is the sphere 
of emptiness and so forth. The subject —the Mahayana path of meditation —is profound, 
because of possessing the direct realization of the profound.  If thusness is profound, the 
exalted wisdom directly realizi ng it is [even] more profound .

4.54  That, because of being a continuity,  
Through the small, the middling, and the great  
Being divided into the small of the small and so forth,  
Is asserted to be of nine types by way of aspects.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
423 EXPLANATION  
That path of meditation of a Mahayana arya is asserted to be of nine types  by way of 
aspects through each of the small, the middling, and the great paths of meditation  being 
divided into the small of the small and so forth. It is suitable to divide it into nine , the 
small , the middling , [and so forth] , because it is a continuity  by way of the nine, the small , 
the middling , [and so forth] . 
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -1B2. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because “the afflictions of the bodhisattvas are the 
conceptualizations” occurs, through the analogy “very great darkness is dispelled by small 
illumination while the small is dispelled by the great,” because of the differences by  way of 
instances of the great, middling, and small of each of the great, middling, and small conceptualizations; because of the differences by way of instances of the small, middling, 
and great of each of the small, middling, and great antidotes to those also. In that way, by 
means of types , that is of the characteristic of emptiness ultimately, but, due to the divisions 
of antidotes to the conceptualizations, because sequentially nine types arise as a continuity 
on the nine levels —the desire realm and so forth —the path of meditation [arises as a 
continuity].  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the path of meditation  of a Mahayana arya —arises as a continuity, because 
sequentially nine types of the path of meditation arise as a continuity on the nine 
levels —the desire  realm and so forth. That follows because conventionally there are nine  
due to the divisions of antidotes to the conceptualizations that adhere to apprehendeds 
and apprehenders as truly existent by means of types, even though there are no divisions 
ultimat ely, since they are the nature that is of the  characteristic of emptiness ultimately . 
That follows because of the different divisions  by way of instances of the great, 
middling, and small of each of the great, middling, and small conceptualizations that 
adhere to apprehendeds and apprehenders as truly existent , and because of the different 
divisions  by way of instances of the small, middling, and great of each of the small, 
middling, and great paths of meditation of a Mahayana arya, the  antidotes to those a lso in 
that way.  
 It follows that there is no fault that the mode of correlating the divisions of the great, 
middling, and small  antidotes and objects of abandonment that has been explained is 
incorrect, because through the analogy “very great darkness is dispelled by small 
illumination while the small is dispelled by the great,”  it is correct that the great objects 
of abandonment of the path of meditation are  destroyed by the small path s of meditation, 
and the small objects of abandonment of the path of me ditation are destroyed by the great 
paths of meditation. Positing by way of divisions of the antidotes to the knowledge 
obscurations here has a purpose, because , in concordance with what occurs in the system of 
the great trailblazers, “the afflictions  of the bodhisattvas are the conceptualizations,” 
[they are posited] in terms of the antidotes to the main objects of abandonment. 
“Afflictions” has the significance of one needing to make effort for the sake of abandoning 
them.

the various Mother of the v ictors , the extensive, medium, and brief.

ROOT TEXT  
4.55  The teachings on countless and so forth  
Cannot bear up ultimately;  
The Subduer asserted those to be  
The concordant cause that is compassionate kindness  conventionally. 
 
EXPLANATION  
According to [Vimuktisena’s] Illuminating the Twenty Thousand , having s tated the 
objection, “ The teachings on the result, countless merit and so forth , cannot bear up 
ultimately ; hence they are incorrect,” [it states the reply that] it follows that there is no fault 
that they are incorrect conventionally, even though they are untenable ultimately, because 
the Subduer asserted those enumerations as indicated in the scriptures to be the  result of 
the concordant ca use that is the compassionate kindness  of the buddhas conventionally , 
even though they are untenable ultimately .

COMMENTARY 
To anticipate the qualms of beings of inferior intelligence thinking, “Is it not that 
familiarizat ion does not exist as that to be brought to perfection due to the characteristic of 
emptiness? Therefore, they are only not done even slightly :”   
 
EXPLANATION  
To anticipate the qualms of beings of inferior intelligence who (i) hold that generation of 
a result and the emptiness of true existence are contradictory and (ii) have not understood 
the meaning of emptiness and dependent -arising thinking, “Is it not that familiarization 
of the path of meditation  does not exist as that to be brought to perfection?  It follows that 
it is so [that there is no bringing to perfection], because it is not truly existent  due to the 
characteristic of emptiness. If you accept that, then  therefore, benefit and damage are 
only not done even slightly”:

ROOT TEXT  
4.56  It is inappropriate for properties that are not expressible  
To diminish and increase.  
What is diminished and what is attained  
By way of the path called “ meditation? ” 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —by way of the path called ‘meditation’—what object of abandonment is 
diminished and what antidote is attained? It follows that there is none, because it does not 
exist ultimately. It follows that there is pervasion, because it is inappropriate for 
properties of the path of meditati on that are not expressible  ultimately to diminish and 
increase.

2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -2B1B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.57  Exactly just as enlightenment,  
This accomplishes the desired aims. 
Enlightenment is the characteristic of thusness;  
Also that is asserted to be the characte ristic of that. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It follows that increase and decrease are unsuitable ultimately, because they cannot be 
expressed as either one or many ultimately. This reply of acceptance [relative to] the 
ultimate, although not explicitly included within the root text, has been implied . It follows 
that there is no pervasion that the objects of abandonment and the antidotes are untenable if 
increase and decrease do not exist ultimately, because exactly just as  the uncontaminated 
exalted wisdom of unsurpassable enlightenment enacts the we lfare of the trainees 
conventionally , even though increase and decrease do not exist ultimately , this Mahayana 
path of meditation accomplishes the desired aims , damaging the objects of abandonment 
and so forth conventionally,  even though increase and decrease do not exist ultimately . It 
follows that there is no fault that enlightenment and the path of meditation are not similar as 
analogy and meaning, because they are similar in enacting the special welfare 
conventionally, [an d] in the way  enlightenment bears  the characteristic of thusness , also 
that path of meditation is asserted to bear the characteristic of that thusness.

428 buddha that is the nature of the truth body and so forth , enacts the desired aims by means of

EXPLANATION

qualified  by increase or decrease , the nature of exalted wisdom without elaboration,

the special welfare concordant with the merit and exalted wisdom of the beings who 
are the trainees  ranging from ordinary beings with pure karma until bodhisattva aryas  by 
the mere mastery of a buddha that is the nature of the realization exalted wisdom truth 
body and so forth.  The expression “and so forth” subsumes the truth body  acting  as the 
actual empowering condition of the complete enjoyment body, and the complete enjoyment 
body [ acting as ] the empowering c ondition of the emanation body.

decrease and increase ultimately.

2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -2B2A -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.58ab  Enlightenment is not  
By an earlier mind, nor by a later one.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the conventional and ultimate minds  of enlightenment —it follows that they 
are not suitable to generate enlightenment, because it is not  suitable that enlightenment is  
generated  by an earlier , by a later one, nor  by many earlier and later ones arising together 
or arising serially . 
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -2B2A -3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said.  
QUALM: Because all objects of the exalted knower of all aspects and so forth that  
accomplish the enlightenment of a buddha do not appear to a single earlier or later mind, it 
is not su itable that enlightenment is by a single one. It is also not by merely many minds (to 
which the accomplishing of the desired aim  appears) arising together, because it is 
impossible, since it occurs, ‘Sentient beings are separate continua  of consciousness.’  It is 
also not  by merely many earlier and later arisen minds, the self- entities of the close 
placement s of mindfulness and so forth until the eighteen unmixed qualities of a buddha 
accomplishing the unsurpassable enlightenment of a buddha, that will be re alized arising 
serially, because there is no mutual relation, since as soon as that arises, [the earlier] 
disintegrates without accompanying it. Therefore, how is the function (by means of having 
generated the mind to which the special purposes appear) performed?  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: (1) It is not suitable that enlightenment is attained by just a single earlier or 
later conventional or ultimate mind of enlightenment, because all objects that are causes of 
an exalted knower of all aspects —the aspects of the exalted knower of all aspects , the 
knower of paths, the knower of bases, and so forth that accomplish the enlightenment of 
a buddha —do not appear  to just a single  earlier  or later mind.  
(2) It is also not generated by merely many minds (to which the accomplishing of the 
desired aim , omniscience , appears) arising together, because it is impossible for many to  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
430 arise together. That follows, since it occurs  in sutra, ‘Sentient beings are separate 
continua of consciousness.’  
 (3) It is also not  suitable for enlightenment to be generated  by merely many earlier  and 
later [instants of the]  two types of the mind  of enlightenment  arising , the self- entities of 
the close placements  of mindfulness and so forth until  the eighteen unmixed qualities 
of a buddha,  the causes accomplishing the unsurpassable enlightenment of a buddha, 
that will be realized  and attained  arising serially, because there is no mutual relation  
with respect to them generating a result. That follows, since as soon as that later one 
arises, the earlier one disintegrates without accompanying it. Therefore, how is the 
function  of generating enlightenment (by means of having generated the mind to which 
the special purposes , enlightenment and others’ welfare, appear) performed?  
 
SOMEONE FROM THE PAST: Although here [a n earlier mind and a later mind] arise 
serially, the earlier one abides until the later one is produced. Then , [by many earlier and 
later minds] gathering together, [enlightenment] is generated. That is the meaning of this 
third refuta tion.  
 A repudiation of that is as follows.  
A CERTAIN SCHOLAR:  If the modes of generating enlightenment are determined to be 
these three, since the three modes of generating enlightenment are untenable, it would be 
impossible to generate enlightenment; i f they are not determined [to be these three], then the 
Acarya would be unskilled in anticipating the above position.  
 
PROPONENT OF THE ABOVE POSITION: Are the proofs determined or not determined 
to be these three? If they are determined to be so, then the  generation of enlightenment 
would be untenable; if they are not determined to be so, then the Acarya would be unskilled in anticipating the above position.  
 This is solely a counterfeit repudiation, because (i) if the modes of generating enlightenment 
are determined to be these three, and none of the three is tenable, then it is necessary that 
enlightenment is not generated, and (ii) if the proofs for not generating enlightenment are determined to be these three, and none of the three is tenable, then it i s necessary that 
enlightenment is generated.

4.58cd  Through the mode of the analogy of an oil lamp, 
The eight aspects of profound dharmata.   
 
EXPLANATION  
It follows that there is no fault that, due to many earlier and later instants of the Mahayana 
generated mind arising sequentially, their results, the three exalted bodies , would not be 
generated, because —through the mode of the analogy of an o il lamp  in which, due to 
each potent later instant of an oil lamp  arising sequentially from a n earlier one, the wick 
with oil is completely burned —(i) due to many earlier and later instants of the Mahayana 
generated mind arising sequentially, their results, the three exalted bodies , are generated and 
(ii) the eight aspects of profound dharmata are also to be understood as tenable .

COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. During the first instant of contact between the flame and the core, 
because they do not create a speciality by being mutually produced by their own causes [in] 
simultaneous contact, without the second instant th e characteristics of cause and result, what 
acts to burn and what is burned, do not exist.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the first instant of contact between the flame and the core  with oil —
during  it, without the second instant arising, the characteristic of c ause, what acts to 
burn, and the characteristic of result, what is burned, do not exist, because the two  do 
not create a special ability (to burn the wick) that is distinguished by being mutually 
produced by their own causes [in] simultaneous contact.

COMMENTARY 
Similarly, also during the second instant , when the special flame and core are produced, 
because, since it follows that it would always exist and so forth, withou t the first instant it 
would not arise even conventionally.  The characteristics of cause and result, what acts to 
burn and what is burned, would not exist.

432  
EXPLANATION  
The subject—similarly, also during the second instant,  when the special  ability of the  
flame and core are produced —it follows that  it would always exist, because it arises 
causelessly. Alternatively, it follows that it would always not exist, because of that. You 
asserted the sign. If you accept that, then , since it follows that there would be that fault and 
so forth  due to elimination by a direct valid cognizer, it follows that without the first 
instant of it, the characteristic of cause, what acts to burn, and the characteristic of 
result, what is burned, would not exist, becaus e without the first instant of it, it would 
not arise even conventionally.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -2B2B -3A1B. [The analogy of] burning in reliance  
COMMENTARY 
Why? Because it is in that way. At any time, through the reality of dependent -arising of the 
nature of just this conditionality, by the strength of being related as  cause and result 
agreeably , solely without investigation, having relied on the first instant bearing the 
distinction of contact between both, if there is a second instant and so forth of specia l ability 
possessing culmination  created by that, then at that time although it has disintegrated 
without a cause, since the cause and the result are respectively observed by valid cognition to be  a result being produced and a cause disintegrating simultan eously, what acts to burn 
and what is burned exist.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Why? Because there is no burning without reliance on the earlier and later instants  in that 
way. However, what acts to burn and what is burned exist, since, ( at that time when the 
special a bility of the flame and core158 has been produced  in the second instant, although 
the first instant  has disintegrated without a cause  of disintegration arisen later), the cause, 
the flame,  and the result, the wick , are respectively observed  by valid cognitio n to be  a 
result  being produced and a  cause disintegrating simultaneously. That follows, because 
there is a second instant and so forth of special  ability possessing perfection created by 
that earlier [instant] having relied on the first instant bearing th e distinction of contact 
between  both  the flame and the wick . That follows, because what is burned and what acts 
to burn are tenable  at any time, through the reality of dependent- arising of the nature 
of159 just this appearing conditionality  that is not created by others such as Isvara , by the 
strength of being related as cause and result agreeably , solely without investigation  by 
a reasoning analyzing the ultimate.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C1C -2B2B -3A2. Applying the analogy to the meaning  
COMMENTARY  
That being the case, “the core is not burned by the first instant without relying upon the second instant of the flame, nor is the core burned by the second instant without relying 
upon the first instant of the flame.” Since the mode of the analogy of an  oil lamp 
emphatically makes known the correlation of the two earlierand later instants to a single object, through generating a later consciousness to which appears an object that is 
exceptionally  superior, devoid of the object that appeared to that, in d ependence on a n 
                                             
 
158 The core ( nying po) in this context refers to the wick.
