433 earlier consciousness —just like a n earlierinstant —to which appeared a measurable property 
acting to achieve enlightenment, the attainment of enlightenment is suitable.   
 
EXPLANATION  
That analogy being the case, the flame and the wick with oil do not burn without reliance

in reliance, the attainment of enlightenment in dependence on the Mahayana generated 
mind is suitable, through generating a later c onsciousness , a Mahayana generated mind,

earlier, devoid of the object that appeared to that former one , in dependence on a n 
earlier consciousness —just like a n earlier  instant of the flame and the wick with oil —to 
which appeared a measurable property , a small ability, that is a cause acting to achieve 
enlightenment. That follows, since the reality of an oil lamp  emphatically makes known 
the correlation  of benefit to a single  object , enlightenment  in which the two earlier  and 
later instants of Mahayana generated mind operate as abilities .

COMMENTARY 
QUESTION: In order to describe the characteristics of the irreversible bodhisattvas abiding 
on the path of meditation, with respect to which objects are the eight types of profundities?  
 
EXPLAN ATION  
QUESTION: In order to describe the characteristics that are signs of the irreversible 
bodhisattvas abiding on the path of meditation, with respect to which objects do the 
eight types of profundities  exist ?   
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.59  Profoundities with respect to production, cessation, 
Thusness, objects of knowledge,  
Knowledge, activity, non -duality, 
And skill in means.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
434 EXPLANATION  
The subject —the eighth ground bodhisattva —possesses the understanding of the eight types 
of profun dities, because he possesses the understanding of the profundities  with respect to 
(1) production , the profound mode of abidance of bases , (2) profound cessation, (3) 
thusness , the profound mode of practicing the deeds , (4) objects of knowledge, (5) 
knowle dge, (6) activity, (7) profound non -duality, and (8) skill in means , the profound 
mode of actualizing the result . 
 
Respectively, sutra says:  
(1) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, what do you think of that? What mind is ceased? Therefore, will it be produced?”  [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, it is not so.”  
(2) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, what do you think of that? Do you think whatever mind that has been produced possesses the property of cessation?” [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, it 
possesses the property of cessati on.” 
(3) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, what do you think of that? Will thusness abide only in 
that mode?” [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, it will.”   
(4) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, what do you think of that? Is thusness mind?” [Subhuti 
said,] “Bhagavan, it  is not.”  
(5) [The Bhagavan said,] “Is the thusness perfectly and subsequently seen by way of thusness?” [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, it is not.”  
(6) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, is that practice in that way a practice in the profound perfection of wisdom? ” [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, it is not.”  
(7) [The Bhagavan said,] “Subhuti, with respect to what is the practice of a great bodhisattva’s practice in the profound perfection of wisdom a practice?” [Subhuti said,] 
“Bhagavan, it is a practice with respect to  the ultimate that is immovability with respect to 
that.”  
(8) [The Bhagavan said,] “How does a great bodhisattva destroy the discrimination of the 
non-existence of the sign of signs?” [Subhuti said,] “Bhagavan, the great bodhisattva does 
not think, ‘The pr actice in the perfection of wisdom in this way should destroy signs; it 
should destroy non- signs.’”  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2C. Commentary  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2C -1. The entities of the profoundities  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2C -2. The way the realization of those becomes si gns 
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2C -1. The entities of the profoundities  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) “ Not by earlier or later instants, and also not through non -
entitiness, yet the very superior meaning to be realized through meditation will be generated” —dependent -arising, (2) “all things are without entitiness by way of nature since 
having arisen, but still cease conventionally” —cessation, (3) “although one is familiar with 
thusness on all occasions, one does not actualize it” —thusness, (4) “ one subsequently 
accomplish es many aspects, generosity and so forth, with respect to all phenomena of the 
nature of thusness” —objects of knowledge, (5) “by way of the entitiness of thusness, mere 
non-seeing is seeing” —knowledge, (6) “ the absence  of activity with respect to all by way of 
dharmata is activity” —activity, (7) “ one accomplish es all as entitiness being devoid of  
duality” —non-duality, and (8) “one thoroughly completes all accumulations , but one does 
not attain the result of that, buddhahood” —skill in mea ns…  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
435  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the eighth ground bodhisattva —:  
(1) understands  profound production, emptiness of true existence that is the meaning of the 
term “ dependent -arising,”  because he possesses the understanding that “ Enlightenment is 
not attained by earlier  or later instants  of the generated mind singly , and also not 
through non- entitiness  conventionally , yet the very superior meaning to be realized 
through meditation , enlightenment, will be generated.”  
(2) understands profound cessation , because h e has directly understood that the two truths 
are not contradictory, that  “All things are without entitiness by way of nature since [ the 
time of their] merely having arisen at the beginning , but still cease conventionally.”  
(3) possesses the understanding  of profound thusness , because he has understood the 
profound mode of realizing thusness, that “Although one is familiar with thusness on all 
occasions  of the learners’ paths , one does not actualize it at an improper time.”   
(4) possesses the understandin g of profound objects of knowledge , because he has directly 
understood “One subsequently accomplish es many aspects, generosity and so forth, 
with respect to all phenomena , although they are not established ultimately by way of the 
nature of thusness.”  
(5) possesses profound knowledge , because he has directly understood that  “If all 
phenomena were to exist ultimately by way of the entitiness of thusness  ultimately, such 
would be observable , but they are never seen ultimately ; mere non -seeing  of suchness is 
seeing  suchness. ”  
(6) possesses profound activity , because he has directly understood that  “The absence  of 
activity with respect to all phenomena  by way of dharmata that is the emptiness of true 
existence  is activity with respect to the meaning of the mode of abidance.”  
(7) possesses the realization of profound  non-duality , because he has directly understood 
“One a ccomplish es all paths as the entitiness being devoid of  duality  ultimately.”  
(8) possesses profound skill in means, because he has directly understood and familiarized 
with “One thoroughly completes all accumulations  of merit and exalted wisdom, but one 
will not attain the result of that, buddhahood , ultimately.”  
2B2C -2A1B -2C1B -2C2C -2. The way the realization of those becomes sign s 
COMMENTARY 
…by means of attaining the liberation that is not conceivable, through accomplishing meanings that are mutually contradictory, are the profundities.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The eight profundities are  understood  by means of attaining the liberation that is not 
conc eivable  by the awareness of dialecticians, great forbearance with respect to the 
phenomenon  that is not produced , through practicing and accomplishing meanings  that 
are mutually contradictory (in the perspective of those of inferior intelligence) as a 
common locus without contradiction .

COMMENTARY  
Because those who have attained the qualities of the assembly of learners make effort in 
order to attain buddhahood, the cause of attaining buddhahoo d: the equality of samsara and 
nirvana. 
 
EXPLANATION  
After having explained the signs of irreversibility, it is appropriate to explain the cause of 
attaining buddhahood: the application of the equality of samsara and nirvana, because 
those who have attaine d the signs of irreversibility, the qualities of the assembly of 
learners, make effort in the proximate cause in order to quickly attain buddhahood. The 
signs of irreversibility of those abiding on the eighth ground and above have been 
explained. However, to make a connection at this juncture, [Haribhadra’s] Great 
Commentary  says:  
 
Now, the learners who have been taught in the passage “The characteristics of non -
learning sangha shall be indicated” are on the seventh ground and those below. Upon attaining t he eighth ground, a bodhisattva needs to complete the special corpus of the 
path to achieve the three exalted bodies, and does not need to learn other paths, so he is called a “no -more -learner.” The way to complete the corpus of the path will be explained. 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2. Root text  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2A. Actual meaning  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B. Rejecting dispute s   
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2A. Actual meaning  
ROOT TEXT  
4.60ab  Because phenomena are like dream s,  
Not conceptualizing cyclic existence and peace. 
 
EXPLANATION  
[Sutra says,] “Sariputra asked, ‘Subhuti, if one has entered absorption into the meditative 
stabilization of the three doors of liberation in a dream, does the perfection of wisdom 
increase?’ Subhuti answered, ‘If it increases through meditation during the da y, it increases 
through meditation even during a dream. Why? There is no conceptuality during a dream or 
the day.’” Condensing its meaning, the subject —the eighth ground bodhisattva —has a 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
437 reason for not conceptualizing cyclic existence and peace  as truly e xistent, because, by 
means of having realized with direct cognition that cyclic existence and peace are devoid of 
true existence, he has exhaustively destroyed the opportunity for generating a manifest 
adherence to true existence even during the occasion o f subsequent attainment. Cyclic 
existence and peace are devoid of  true existence, because phenomena included within 
cyclic existence and peace are not ultimately existent, like a dream. 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B. Rejecting dispute s 
ROOT TEXT  
4.60cd  The response to dispute s such as “K arma does not exist ” 
Is just that which has been explained.  
 
EXPLANATION  
If all phenomena are not established ultimately, it follows that it is not that there is the fault 
of actions and results not being able to be posited, because the response to disputes  such 
as “It follows that karma does not exist ” is just that which has b een explained  in sutra , 
and those disputes can be rejected . 
 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1. Rejecting the consequence that it follows that accumulating karma even during 
daytime is infeasible  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2. Rejecting the consequence that it follows that a path o f action in a dream is complete

2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B4. Rejecting the ridiculous consequence 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1. Rejecting the consequence that it follows that accumulating karma even during daytime 
is infeasible  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1A. Dispute  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1B. Reply  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1A. Dispute  
It follows that accumulating karma of virtue or negativity even during daytime would be 
infeasible, because actions and results are not established ultimatel y, for example, as in the 
case of a dream. Fearing wordiness, I have not cited the words of sutra. I have summarized 
the meaning and stated it. That very disputeset forth by Sariputra to Subhuti was made by 
acting as if he had a doubt for the sake of takin g care of trainees who hold that the two 
truths are contradictory.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B1B. Reply  
SUBHUTI: It follows that the repudiation stated by Sariputra is not a correct repudiation, 
because (i) relative to the ultimate, the reply is “Accept,” and (ii) relative to the 
conventional, the reply is “The pervasion is indefinite.”   
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2. Rejecting the consequence that it follows that a path of action in a dream is complete  
Objection  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2B. Reply  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2A. Objection  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2A -1. Dividing the context of the dispute by Subhuti  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2A -2. Actual dispute by Sariputra  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
438 2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2A -1. Dividing the context of the dispute by Subhuti  
SUBHUTI: Having killed during the day and killed in a dream, if afterwards these are 
nourished by forceful adherence, saying, “That [person] has been killed by me. The killing 
of that [person] by me was well done!”, then they would be similar in having become paths 
of actions. Sariputra, what do you say about that karma?  
2B2C-2A1B -2C2A -2B2A -2. Actual dispute by Sariputra  
SARIPUTRA: Well, it follows that killing an object to be killed , giving an object to be 
given in a dream , and so forth  would be complete paths of action, because the waking state 
and a dream are similar. If you accept that, then it follows that the vows of a fully -ordained 
monk would be lost even due to dreaming that he killed a man in a dream.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B2B. Reply  
SUBHUTI: It follows that the repudiation by Sariputra is also not a correct repudiation, 
because the reply to that is “Accept.” For, the waking state and a dream differ in the degree 
of the strength of karma; if nourished by forceful adherence, even the k arma of a dream 
would become a path of action of virtue or non -virtue. The vows of a fully ordained monk 
would not be given up due to dreaming that he killed a man in a dream. That is presented 
from the point of view of a training stipulated by the Bhagava n, similar to the arisal of a 
downfall of unchaste conduct , as in the case of one who has produced a mind of 
concealment being posited as not being a fully -ordained monk, and one who has not 
produced a mind of concealment being posited as being a fully -ordained monk who is a 
person who has relinquished a training regarding a punishable action.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B3. Rejecting contradiction with scripture  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B3A. Dispute  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B3B. Reply  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B3A. Dispute  
SARIPUTRA: If there is  the accumulating of karma in both the waking state and a dream, 
then it would contradict what has been taught in sutra: “[They] are devoid [of] all karma and 
intentions.”  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B3B. Reply  
SUBHUTI: Sariputra, it follows that it would also not con tradict that, because what has 
been taught in sutra says that karma and intentions do not exist ultimately, but karma and 
results exist conventionally.  
 
Analyzing whether the dispute made once again by Sariputra in that manner is reasonable or 
unreasonabl e is clearly an expression of not having realized the meaning, because he acted 
as if he has doubt for the sake of trainees; do not wonder, “Would Sariputra have internal 
contradictions?”

439 2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B4A. Dispute  
SARIPUTRA TO SUBHUTI: It follows that the cultivation of the performance of 
generosity until wisdom by a bodhisattva in a dream would become the complete two 
accumulations if the ro ots of virtue are further dedicated to complete enlightenment, 
because there are complete paths of action in a dream. If you accept that, then there would 
be a ridiculous consequence, because a path of action in a dream is not even established as a

2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B4B -2. Making referral to Maitreya  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B4B -1. Subhuti’s own reply has already been given by implication  
Indeed we have already replied by implication; we are not able to give all replies by ourselves, because there is meaning in future buddhas arising continuously.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -2B4B -2. Making referral to Maitreya  
With regard to the teaching, “Sariputra ask ed Maitreya, and Maitreya said, ‘Did [the one 
with] the name “ Maitreya”  give a reply? ’…” someone’s assertion that Maitreya  did not 
reply is incorrect, because, as indicated by Subhuti above, he correctly indicated the meaning of the equality of cyclic exis tence and peace. Having indicated cyclic existence and 
peace as not existing ultimately, by implication he replied, “If one were to practice generosity and so forth in a dream and dedicate them to enlightenment, they would become 
accumulations; therefore t here would be no ridiculous consequence.” Maitreya’s  not 
explicitly completing the reply to that question indicates that the question and the answer 
are incomplete. The waking state and a dream characterize samsara and nirvana.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3. Commentar y 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3A. Actual meaning  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B. Rejecting the disputes 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3C. The meaning established by that  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3A. Actual meaning  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because samsara and the completely pure —the discordant and the 
antidote —are realized as being merely like dreams, due to being entities that are mere 
appearing images, since it does not conceptualize samsara and nirvana as differe nt, equality.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —an exalted wisdom of meditative equipoise of an eighth ground bodhisattva —
is an application of equality  of cyclic existence and peace, because, since it does not 
conceptualize samsara and nirvana as different , it is  a yoga of the pure grounds that 
realizes with direct cognition that they are similar in being empty of true existence. It realizes that cyclic existence and peace are empty of true existence, because the 
discordant , samsara,  and the antidote , the completely pure , are realized as (i) not 
existing ultimately and (ii) being  merely like dreams, due to being entities that are mere 
appearing images  conventionally . 
 Upon attaining the eighth ground, during the subsequent attainment, regardless of wha t 
objects of knowledge that are varieties appear, they appear like a reflection of a face in the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
440 mirror; objects that are not illusory appearances, like the appearances on some occasions on 
the seventh ground and below, do not appear even slightly. Even th ough one may not have 
abandoned exhaustively the seeds of the conceptions of true existence, due to having destroyed the opportunity for manifest conceptions of true existence to be produced, one is 
presented as “having attained the application of equality  of cyclic existence and peace.”  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B. Rejecting the disputes

2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2. Reply 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B1. Disputes  
COMMENTARY  
“If they are like dreams, by the ten non -virtues and generosity and so forth not exi sting, 
would it not become like on the occasion of being asleep, even on the occasion of not being 
asleep?” The reply to these disputes  … 
 
EXPLANATION  
“It follows that the ten non -virtues , killing and so forth, and virtuous actions, generosity 
and so forth would be pervaded by not existing, because all phenomena are empty of true 
existence; for example, they are like dreams. Therefore, would the collection of karma  not 
become non-existent like on the occasion of being asleep, even on the occasion of not 
being asleep?” The meaning is that, regarding “ these disputes ” mentioned, the reply  to all 
the disputes  that argue  that the two truths are contradictory, which are characterized by this 
dispute, should be characterized and known by this explanation below.

2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2A -1A. They themselves have accept ed the object to be killed and the killer as not 
established for an ultimate mind  
COMMENTARY  
In the tradition of propounders of external objects, they disintegrate without a cause due to momentarines s, because it occurs even in the tenets: “The diverse worlds arise from karma.” 
Ultimately the killing of someone by anyone does not exist, and not killing also does not; the robbing of anyone by someone does not exist, and not robbing also does not.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In the tradition of propounders of external objects —the Sutra Followers (Sautrantikas) 
and the Followers of the Great Exposition (Vaibhashikas)—it follows that ultimately , that 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
441 is, in the perspective of apprehension of an ultimate awareness, an  unmistaken sense 
consciousness,  the killing of someone by anyone does not appear as parts, part -possessor, 
and continuity -possessor , and the parts and part -possessor of the virtuous path of action, 
not killing, also do not  appear; in the perspective of ap prehension of that, the robbing of 
anyone by someone does not exist, and not robbing also does not  [exist] , because, in the 
perspective of apprehension of that, they do not appear as parts and part -possessor . That 
follows (i) because, in the perspective of apprehension of that, although infinitesimal final 
instants and minute particles appear, they disintegrate without a cause arisen later due to 
momentariness  in which things do not remain for a second period , and (ii) because it 
occurs even in our own tenets, [Vasubandhu’s] Treasury of Knowledge  (Abhidharmakosa) 
(4.1a): “The diverse worlds are produced from karma.”  This is because, by having 
asserted that the outer and inner environment and the inhabitants are produced from 
common and uncommon karma, ceasin g of life occurs through the force of exhausting the 
karma that propels life. Hence, it is because part -possessors that are common and 
uncommon karma as well as their results are also not established in the perspective of 
apprehension of an unmistaken awar eness.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2A -1B. They themselves have also accept ed them as established as objects of a conceptual 
conventional awareness 
COMMENTARY  
Regarding positions in the aforesaid and so forth, with respect to the property (contradictory 
to engaging t he continuity) that is generated, by means of realizing the killer and so forth, 
like possession of improper attention as non -virtues and so forth , killing and so forth  are 
presented.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Regarding positions  that assert in that way in the perspective of an ultimate awareness  in 
the aforesaid and so forth, with respect to the property (contradictory to engaging the 
continuity of the life that is the object to be killed ) that is generated  by the killing, by 
means of realizing the object to be killed, the killer , and so forth as objects of 
conventional awarenesses , killing and so forth  are presented  by they themselves. 
Likewise, the pervasion that it does not exist conventionally if it does not exist ultimately i s 
indefinite. It is like their possession of improper attention , covetousness, harmful intent, 
and so forth,  being  posited  as non -virtues and so forth. By “and so forth,” right view is 
posited as virtue and so forth.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2A -2. Indicating that as being similar also for the Middle-Wayers  
COMMENTARY  
“Similarly, although the properties are like dreams, to those who have not weaken ed all 
erroneous bonds, through adhering to the properties and so forth of the objects concordant 
with those.” That and  that replies in the aforesaid and so forth have been taught in other 
positions . One should understand the aforesaid. 
 
EXPLANATION  
“Similar  to that analogy , although the properties —the object to be killed,  the killing, and 
so forth —do not exist in the pers pective of a reasoning  [consciousness]  analyzing finality 
that comprehends ultimate truths, like dreams, it is not that they do not exist as objects of 
valid cognizers of conventionalities. Therefore, even though the object to be killed, the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
442 killing, and s o forth  do not exist ultimately, to the childish who have not realized emptiness, 
those who have not weakened  all erroneous bonds, the presentation of actions and results 
(through adhering to the objects of comprehension of valid cognizers of 
conventionali ties—the properties and so forth of the objects concordant with those , the 
object to be killed, the killing, the object to be given, the giving, and so forth —as the object 
to be killed, the killing, and so forth) is correct.” That reply of indefinite pervasion relative 
to the conventional and that  reply of acceptance relative to the ultimate in the aforesaid 
and so forth that the two truths are not contradictory have been taught in other positions 
in this small commentary [Haribhadra’s Clear Meaning ], the Perfection of Wisdom sutras, 
and the treatises of the Protector Nagarjuna . One should understand the aforesaid. This is 
the meaning that is to be realized from those in an extensive manner. 
 Although the reply is the word commentary  of “[The response] is just that which has 
been explained,” [4.60d] it should not be taken to have explained “other positions” in the 
sutras that the Proponents of Mind- Only  asserted to be definitive in meaning and so forth. 
Alternatively, regarding the me aning of applying the double expression “That and that,” the 
infinite responses that the two truths are not contradictory should be understood from the 
sutras and the extensive explanations given by the trailblazers.  
 To summarize, it is stated that “It follows that there is no pervasion that accumulating 
karma even during the daytime would be infeasible if actions and results are not truly 
existent, because, in the way  actions and results exist as objects of conventional 
awarenesses even though the three circles are not complete in the perspective of an ultimate 
awareness in your system, that of the Propounders of Objects, likewise also for us, the 
Middle -Wayers, actions and results are asserted to be objects of conventional valid 
cognizers even though the y do not exist in the perspective of that which comprehends the 
ultimate.”  
 In dependence on these words of the commentary, propounding that “The two hearer 
schools are pervaded by asserting that, if it is a gross object and a continuity, then it is 
empty of true existence,” is only erroneous. In that case, one would be asserting all three 
circles if that earlier debate itself were to counter itself. Hence, do not engage in deprecation 
since a skilled Propounder of Objects —who (i) explicitly repudiates his own root assertion 
by himself and (ii) asserts that itself to be a correct repudiation of others —is impossible.  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2B. Reply that the example is not established  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2B -1. The example is not established for the Middle-Wayers  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2B -2. The example is not established for the Propounders of Objects 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2B -1. The example is not established for the Middle-Wayers  
COMMENTARY  
In addition, since it has been said, “The dream mind is weakened by sleep, therefore re sults 
are not the same,” the example is not established.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In addition to the sign being indeterminate , the concordant example of there being no 
accumulation of karma during dreams is not established as correct for us, the Middle -
Wayers, because , since it has been said  in [Vasubandhu’s] The Twenty  (Vimsatika ), “The 
dream mind is weakened by sleep, therefore results are not the same,”  we also assert 
that the yielding of results [by] karma [accumulated during] dreams is merely weak in 
strength; the re is accumulation of karma .   
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
443  The Acarya accepts this to be concordant with the tenets of the Yogic Practitioners 
(Yogacara ). Therefore it is cited as the reason for the example not being established for 
ourselves; it is the reason for the example not be ing established for the Proponents of Mind -
Only  having taken them as the other party. For, it is inappropriate for the Propounders of 
Objects to fling the repudiation explained above to the Proponents of Mind- Only . Suppose 
one were to explain this as an ex ample that is not established for the Proponents of Mind -
Only ; even if those who dispute with the Middle -Wayers are explicitly stated as the two 
types of Propounders of Objects, since the Proponents of Mind- Only  also debate with the 
Middle -Wayers in the wa y the Propounders of Objects debate above, apply the reply of the 
example not being established for the Proponents of Mind -Only , the challenger. 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2A -3B2B -2. The example is not established for the Propounders of Objects 
COMMENTARY  
Similarly, regarding virtue or non -virtue being performed also in dreams, if one rejoices, 
“Oh! It was done. It was well done!” on the occasion of being awake , it becomes fully 
nourished by the nourishing of adherence with a mind of subsequent occasion, be cause of 
that. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It follows that, similar  to that example , the concordant example of there being no 
accumulation of karma during dreams is not established  as correct also for the Propounders 
of Objects themselves,  because  the Propounders of Objects themselves also assert that the 
karma [accumulated during] dreams definitely yield results if it is nourished by intense 
adherence:  if virtue or non -virtue is performed also in dreams , through  rejoicing, “Oh! 
It was done. It was well done!” on the occasion of awaking, it becomes fully nourished 
by the nourishing of adherence with a mind of subsequent occasion.

COMMENTARY 
Since also the example is not established, samsara and ni rvana are just equal. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Samsara and nirvana are established to be just equal  in being empty of true existence, 
because, since the sign and the concordant example that have been stated to damage that 
are [respectively] indeterminate and not estab lished, [the repudiation] is established to be a 
counterfeit repudiation .

2B2C -2A1B -2C2B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
4.61 Through establishing them,  
The world of sentient beings, and like wise 
The impure world of the environment as pure, 
The pure buddha land.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The eight h ground bodhisattva has attained the  application of pure buddha land where he 
will attain buddhahood, because —through having  accumulated the roots of virtue that 
establish  the impure world of sentient beings  as pure , and likewise the impure world of 
the environment as pure —he has attained the application of pure land . 
2B2C -2A1B -2C2B -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding the two types of buddha lands through the instances of the worlds of sentient beings and the environment, respectively, purity by means of 
generating purities the resources of gods, and golden grounds and so forth, as the antidotes 
to that which is impure due to hunger and  thirst, and stones and thorns and so forth, is pure 
buddha land.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Upon attaining the eighth ground, one possesses the application of pure buddha land. In 
dependence on having been preceded by extensive roots of virtue that purify the land, 
regarding the two types of buddha lands , the objects to be purified, the  purity of the 
land—through the instances of the worlds of sentient beings and the environment 
respectively, (i) by means of generating the possession of the resources  that are  
enjoyments  of gods, as the antidotes to the land of the inhabitants which is impure due to 
hunger and thirst and so forth, and (ii) [by means of generating] purities, golden grounds 
and so forth, as the antidotes to the land of the environment  which is impure due to  stones 
and thorns and so forth—is the possession of the application of pure land .

2B2C -2A1B -2C2C -2. Root text  
2B2C -2A1B -2C2C -3. Comment ary

After the application of pure land, the application of skillful means will be explained. The 
reason is that, having established one’s pure buddha land, it is necessary to enact the

The objects of the applications are [expressed by] “ objects .” The entity of the applications 
is [expressed by] “ this bearer that is  the application .” The divisions of the applications 
are the ten types of skillful means , beginning from “ Transcended the enemies .”   
2B2C -2A1B -2C2C -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Because of having correctly  crossed beyond obstructing 
phenomena, having transcended the maras, the gods and so forth, (2) because of having 
meditated upon the equality of all phenomena, abiding without abiding, (3) because of 
having accomplished prayer, being propelled by the force  of previ ous others’ welfare, (4) 
because of having familiarized very much with all difficult activities, not in common, (5) 
because of pure wholesome qualities, the absence of apprehending any phenomenon, (6) 
because of the emptiness door of liberation, not observ ing, (7) because of the signlessness 
door of liberation, signs do not exist, (8) because of the wishlessness door of liberation, wishes do not exist, (9) because of expressing the qualities  of irreversibility, having been 
previously questioned, the sign of  irreversibility, and (10) because of knowing all objects, 
immeasurable; the application that knows the proper time  and the improper time with 
respect to actualizing the ten types of objects of the perfection of wisdom is skillful means.  
EXPLANATION  
The a pplication  of the pure grounds  that directly knows the proper time regarding  
benefiting sentient beings and the improper time regarding  not benefiting sentient beings 
with respect to actualizing the ten types of objects of the perfection of wisdom , the thr ee 
doors of liberation and so forth, is the application of skillful means. What are they?  
 (1) The subject —the exalted wisdom of the tenth ground —has transcended the maras, 
the maras of the son of the  gods and so forth, because of having correctly crossed  
beyond obstructing phenomena,  the potential to be obstructed by maras and so forth, 
through the force of yoga. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                  Chapter 4: The Application in Complete Aspects  
 
446  What dies? The mara of the aggregates: the five appropriated aggregates. Through what 
does one die? The mara of the afflictions: the [root] afflictions and the secondary afflictions 
of the three realms. What is death? The mara of the lord of death: that which stops one’s life 
power beyond one’s control. That which hinders one from transcending death is the mara of 
the son of the gods (devaputra):  the Lord of Ecstasy160, the ruler of Controlling Others’ 
Emanations.  Due to hindering the attainment of the state of nirvana, they are called 
“maras.”  
 Hinayanists transcend the mara of the son of the gods upon attaining the path of seeing 
and transcend the mara of the afflictions  upon attaining the state of foe -destroyer. [Those 
who are] liberated from both factors transcend the mara of the lord of death  because of 
having attained control over the blessing of the composition factors of life. They transcend the mara of the aggregates  upon attaining nirvana without remainder. 
 Mahayanists transcend the mara of the son of the gods upon attaining a sign of 
irreversibility, and upon attaining the eighth ground they transcend the coarse [form of] the 
three other maras, but the destruction of all the four maras without exception occurs upon 
attaining buddhahood.  
 (2) [The exalted wisdom of the tenth ground] poss esses the application of abiding 
conventionally without abiding  ultimately  with respect to all phenomena , because of 
having meditated upon the equality of all phenomena in being empty of true existence.  
(3) It possesses [the application of] being propelled  by the force of previous  prayers for 
others’ welfare, because of having accomplished the  prayer : “May I enact others’ 
welfare spontaneously!”  (4) It possesses the application that is not in common with hearers 
and solitary realizers , because of having fam iliarized very much with all difficult 
activities  that enact others’ welfare. (5) It possesses the absence of apprehending any 
phenomenon as truly existent , because of being distinguished by the purity  that is free 
from the apprehending of wholesome  qualit ies (of the grounds and paths that have been 
familiarized with) as truly existent.  
 (6) It possesses the application that does not observe  the entities of the self of persons 
and of phenomena as truly existent  because of having familiarized with  the empti ness door 
of liberation  for many countless eons. (7) It possesses the application that realizes that 
signs that are causes do not exist  truly , because of having familiarized with  the 
signlessness door of liberation for many countless eons.  (8) It possesses the application 
that realizes that wishes for results do not exist  truly , because of having familiarized with  
the wishlessness door of liberation  for many countless eons.  
 (9) It possesses skill in the means of the sign of oneself being irreversible, because of 
possessing the special conventions that enable [others] to understand that oneself is 
irreversible, due to having  expressed the qualities of irreversibility that are concordant 
with the attainment of special signs of irreversibility with respect to  having been 
previously questioned by others based upon one’s irreversibility. (10) It possesses skill in 
the means of immeasurable  realizations , because of knowing all objects  that are methods 
for educing answers to questions , due to havi ng been made skilled in the five subjects of 
knowledge, and having directly familiarized with the mode of abidance for many countless eons. At this juncture in [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary , difference is made between the 
three exalted knowers and the ap plication in complete aspects as explained above.

2B2C -2A2A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
COMMENTARY 
Since, having attained the clear complete  realization of all aspects, the realization of 
excellent culmination will arise, because it causes easy  understanding, the clear realization 
of the peak being preceded by the explanation of the signs and so forth:  
 
EXPLANATION  
After having explained the application in complete aspects, there is a reason for explaining 
the clear realization of the peak , since, having attained the clear complete  realization 
of all aspects  of the three exalted knowers for the sake of gaining control over 
familiarization , the realization of excellent culmination that has gained self- power  over 
familiarization will arise. There i s a reason for explaining the peak by way of being 
preceded by the explanation of the signs of having attained the peak and so forth, 
because it causes easy understanding.

ROOT TEXT  
5.1 Viewing all phenomena as being like dreams  
Even in a dream and so forth,  
The twelve types of signs of the application  
That has gone to the peak are asserted. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
449 EXPLANATION  
The twelve types of signs of having attained the application that has gone to the peak 
are asserted in sutra, because the twelve types, viewing all phenomena with the meditative 
stabilization that is a union  of calm abiding and special insight as being like dreams even 
in a dream through the force of a wisdom arisen from meditation  and so forth,  are asserted  
in sutra . 
 Know that even if one is able to observe [all phenomena] in that manner just once in a 
dream through the force of having cultiva ted special insight observing emptiness on the 
occasion of being awake, that is a sign of having attained peak application. 
  Sutra says, “Subhuti, a great bodhisattva views all phenomena as being like dreams even 
in a dream… Bhagavan, a great bodhisattva who practices in that way is becoming close to 
omniscience.”  
2B2C -2A2B -1A1B. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Because of having very much familiarized ; viewing all 
phenomena as being like dreams and so forth even on the occasion of dreams ; (2) will not 
generate a mind of joy towards the grounds of hearers and so forth ; (3) seeing the tathagatas 
and so forth;  (4) observing emanations of the miraculous pow ers of the buddhas ; (5) 
generating the mind of teaching the excellent Dharma ; (6) having previously seen  sentient 
beings of the hells and so forth, recollecting to abandon bad migrations in one’s buddha land; (7) accomplishing the blessings of the truth th at pacify burning cities and so forth ; (8) 
accomplishing words of truth that annihilate non -humans, yaksas (harm -giver spirits), and 
so forth ; (9) through one’s clairvoyance, relying on a spiritual guide who outshines 
antagonists ; (10) training in the perfection of wisdom in all types ; (11) not having adherence 
to any phenomenon;  and (12) becoming close to a buddha’s enlightenment : the aforesaid 
twelve types are the special signs on the occasion of having attained the clear realization of 
the peak.  
 
EXPLANA TION  
The twelve types are the special signs on the occasion of having attained the clear 
realization of the peak. What are they?  
 The subject —a bodhisattva abiding on the heat level of the path of preparation —(1) 
possesses viewing all phenomena as being like dreams and so forth even on the 
occasion of dreams, because of having very much familiarized  with the union of calm 
abiding and special insight observing emptiness during waking time .  
 He has attained the peak application, because (2) he will not gen erate a mind of joy 
towards the grounds of hearers and so forth even in a dream through the force of having 
familiarized with the Mahayana generated mind.   
 He has attained the peak application, because he has attained the signs of the peak of:  (3) 
seeing  tathagatas surrounded by retinues consisting of hundreds of thousands [of trainees] 
and teaching the Dharma and so forth  even in a dream, through the force of having ripened 
the Mahayana roots of virtue to not be deprived of seeing the buddhas ; (4) observ ing in a 
dream  emanations of the miraculous powers of the buddhas : levitating into space and 
then teaching the Dharma and so forth ; (5) generating the mind of teaching the Dharma  
even in a dream, thinking, “Upon wakening, I shall teach sentient beings that  all phenomena 
are like dreams ;” (6) due to having previously seen sentient beings of the hells and so 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
450 forth  on the occasions of being awake and of a dream , recollecting to abandon bad 
migrations , thinking, “May even the names of bad migrations not exist  in my buddha 
land; ” (7) accomplishing the blessings of the truth that pacify burning cities and so 
forth  when dreaming and awake ; (8) accomplishing words of truth that annihilate non -
humans, yaksas (harm -giver spirits), and so forth  by expressing the power of the truth 
when a man or a woman is seized by them on the occasion of being awake ; (9) relying on a 
spiritual guide who outshines the actions of antagonistic maras  through oneself being 
without pride and one’s clairvoyance  knowing the actions of maras ; (10) training in the 
perfection of wisdom  for others’ welfare  in all types  of meditative equipoise and 
subsequent attainment ; (11) not having adherence to any phenomenon as truly existent 
through the force of having realized emptiness with a wisdom arisen from meditation ; and 
(12) becoming close to a buddha’s enlightenment due to abiding on the faultlessness of a 
bodhisattva and so forth.

The subject —a bodhisattva abiding on the peak application of the peak —has attained the 
natures of the sixteen increases  in merit, because he has attained that greatly exceeding 
the virtues of however many sentient beings there are in the billionfold world systems, 
the beings in Jambudvipa and so forth, making offerings to the buddhas and so forth, 
through analogies made in many ways.  
 Sutra says, “Suppose all the sentient beings, however many there are in Jambudvipa, 
without som e being earlier and the rest later, were to obtain human bodies and become 
buddhas, and, for as long as they live, a son of the lineage or daughter of the lineage were to 
revere them, honor them, and so forth. In comparison to that merit, if a son of the l ineage or 
daughter of the lineage, having taught others this perfection of wisdom, were to practice it with attention, his or her merit would have increased more... Suppose one, thinking ‘this,’ 
thinks, ‘This is the perfection of wisdom; through this perfe ction of wisdom I shall achieve 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
451 omniscience.’ It has been taught that, ‘If one has practiced in that way, one has not practiced 
the perfection of wisdom.’ ”

The subject —the entitinesses of the sixteen occasions of having attained the peak 
application of the peak —are the increases  in merit, due to the attainment of superiority  of 
each latter one over the former one , through analogies made in many ways, the virtues of 
making offerings to buddhas with flowers and so forth.  
 What are the sixteen?   
 The subject —a bodhisattva who has attained the peak application of the peak —possesses 
an increase in merit, because (1) he possesses merit greatly exceeding that of making 
offerings to tathagatas by however many sentient beings there are in the billionfold 
world systems, Jambudvipa and so forth.  
  He possesses an increase in merit, (2) becau se he possesses paying attention  to the 
perfection of wisdom  through possessing the two applications , merit superior  to that 
[former one] , (3) because he possesses having attained forbearance regarding emptiness, 
the phenomenon  that is not produced, superior to that [former one], (4) because, through 
the strength of the peak application, he realizes  that causal and resultant  phenomena—that 
which is to attain enlightenment and that which acts to attain enlightenment—are not 
observed as ultimately exist ent, superior to that [former one].  
 [The subject —a bodhisattva who has attained the peak application of the peak —
possesses an increase in merit, because he possesses:] (5) having attained great superiority 
over the attainment of the ten virtues and so fo rth, and the concentrations and formless 
absorptions  of sentient beings in Jambudvipa , (6) respectfully and without causing harm, 
all classes of gods come near  before he who strives in the perfection of wisdom of the 
peak , (7) outshining all maras  through the force of the perfection of wisdom of the peak , 
and (8) respecting  other bodhisattvas, beings  who have entered the same vehicle as himself,  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
452 as if they are the Teacher, and  abiding in similar  trainings .The aforesaid eight types of 
merit are mainly extern al due to relying on other persons.   
 Also, the subject —that [as above] —possesses an increase in merit of having attained the 
peak, because he possesses, (9) through skill in the means of the two accumulations  in all 
ways, pure trainings  by way of the wis dom realizing dharmata with a wisdom arisen from 
meditation .  
 He possesses an increase in merit of having attained the peak, (10) because he has 
attained  transformation into a bearer of buddha lineage  through the force of the 
perfection of wisdom of the peak , (11) because he has attained the generated mind that is 
concomitant with the applications and so forth, the cause of attaining the resultant 
buddhahood, (12) because he has attained the excellent quality of not producing 
miserliness and so forth, minds that are discordant classes of the perfection of wisdom , 
through the force of the perfection of wisdom of the peak , (13) because he has attained  not

three vehicles through the wisdom arisen from meditation , and (16) because he has attained

wisdom of the peak. The afores aid are eight other types of merit, different from those 
above, that are mainly internal .

ROOT TEXT  
5.3 Unsurpassable thorough completion  
Of the qualities of the three exalted knowers of all and  
The thorough non- forsaking of the welfare of sentient beings  
Is called the “stability.”  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
453 EXPLANATION  
The subject —the forbearance level of the Mahayana path of preparation —there is a reason 
for it  to be  called the “stability”  in sutra, because it is a parti al concordance with definite 
discrimination that (i) has attained unsurpassable  thorough completion of the classes of 
realizations that are qualities of the three exalted knowers of all included within 
realizations, one’s own welfare, and (ii) has attained  the thorough  non-forsaking of the 
welfare of sentient beings through compassion, others’ welfare, as explained in sutra. 
 [Asserting that one] connects to a pure ground through merely having attained the 
complete classes of realizations of the three exalt ed knowers leads to absurd consequences. 
If one were to take [completion of the classes of realizations] as referring to the completion 
of the entity of the realizations, [asserting that] it is attained from the ninth ground would be  
even more incorrect than that.   Sutra says, “By these roots of virtue, may persons of the bodhisattva vehicle, those 
observing the unsurpassable complete enlightenment, thoroughly complete the qualities of a buddha ; may they thoroughly complete the self- arisen qualities ; and may they thoroughly 
complete all uncontaminated qualities.”   
2B2C -2A2B -1A3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The characteristic of thorough non -forsaking of the welfare of 
sentient beings, on the occasion of realizing the non -conceptualizing through the entity of 
actualizing great compassion, through the strength of perfect skill in means in that way, the 
unsurpassable thorough completion of the topics  of the three exalted knowers of all that 
have been explained, the ge nerated mind and so forth mentioned above, is definite stability.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that [the forbearance level of the Mahayana path of preparation] —is the peak 
of definite stability , because it is the third partial concordance with definite discrim ination 
that has attained special method and wisdom. It has attained special stable method, because 
it has attained the characteristic of thorough non -forsaking of the welfare of sentient 
beings, even on the occasion of realizing the non -conceptualizing meditative equipoise 
by way of being conjoined with the entity of actualizing great compassion , which 
observes sentient beings , through the strength of perfect skill in means that accomplishes 
others’ welfare in that way. It has attained special stable wisdo m, because it has attained 
the unsurpassable thorough completion of the classes of realizations that are the thirty topics (of the three exalted knowers of all that have been explained previously, the 
exalted knower of all aspects and so forth), the genera ted mind and so forth mentioned 
above.

5.4 Through analogies made of the four continents and  
A thousand, a million, and the billion world systems,  
The meditative stabilization s is  
Proclaimed by immense merit.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The meditative stabilization s of the peak application of the supreme mundane quality is 
proclaimed  in sutra because, through analogies made of the amount of water in the four 
continents and a thousand, a million, and the billion world systems  being measurable 
with weighing scales, it i s indicated through being characterized in comparison with those 
by the immense  merit of rejoicing in the virtues of the four types of bodhisattvas. 
 Sutra says, “Rejoice from the bottom of your heart at the generated minds of those who 
have entered suprem e irreversible enlightenment. The weight of the Mount Merus of the 
billionfold world systems can be determined with weighing scales; that virtue of rejoicing is 
not like that.”   
2B2C -2A2B -1A4C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
By way of saying,  ‘The amount is measurable with weighing scales,’ by the four continents 
and so forth and the billionfold world systems, through individual analogies made of the 
merit and exalted wisdom of the meditative stabilization of the entitiness of all, the 
perfections and so forth, by immense merit superior and beyond measure compared to those, 
the characteristic of meditative stabilization described  is the always -abiding mind.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The amount of great earth and water in  the four continents and so forth and the 
billionfold world  systems can be determined respectively with weighing scales and with 
the number of drops that are taken up with the tip of a strand of hair, because they are 
measurable in that manner by way of saying, ‘The amount is measurable with weighing 
scales.’  
 The characteristic of the meditative stabilization (included within the collections of the 
merit and exalted wisdom of the meditative stabilization of the complete classes of the entitiness of all clear realizations, the perfections of generosity and so forth , in this context 
in sutra) described in sutra is the always -abiding mind, the peak application of the supreme 
mundane quality.  
 How is it described in sutra?  Through individual analogies made of the amount of the 
earth mandala and water in  the billionfold  world systems, the four continents and so 
forth , being determinable respectively with weighing scales and with the number of drops 
[that are taken up] with the tip of a strand of hair, the merit of rejoicing in the virtues of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
455 four types of bodhisattva s is indicated through being characterized by immense merit far 
more superior and beyond measure compared to that in those analogies. 
 It is characterized by indicating the merit of rejoicing in the virtues of the four—(1) one 
who actually generates the fi rst mind [of enlightenment], (2) one who has engaged in deeds, 
(3) one who is irreversible, and (4) one who is obstructed by one rebirth —being far more 
superior compared to that in those analogies explained above, and indicated.

After the partial concordance with definite discrimination is the path of seeing.  
 
EXPLANATION  
After having explained the peak of the partial concordance with definite discrimination , 
the peak of the path of seeing is  to be explained .

ROOT TEXT  
5.5 The conceptualizations of appre hendeds  
With respect to engagement and turning away  
Are to be individually known as the natures of nine,  
As the natures not as the objects are. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the conceptualizations of apprehendeds with respect to engagement and 
turning away that are the bodhisattvas’ objects of abandonment of the path of seeing —are 
to be individually known as the natures of nine, because  there are nine each through 
divisions by way of the objects. They are to be known as the natures apprehending what 
are not as the objects are , because they are wrong consciousnesses that are mistaken with 
respect to their objects of adherence . 
2B2C -2A2B -2B1A -1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Regarding engagement and turning away , the entitinesses that do not 
observe and observe, respectively, through divisions of the objects that are the discordant 
classes, like afflictions, conceptualizations with respect to apprehendeds called 
“apprehendeds by means of the dharmas of the bodhisattvas and the hearers and so forth 
being adopted and being discarded ,” the nine types that exist individually, because of 
appearing erroneously, since they engage without being related to reality, are to be known as the self- entities not exactly as the objects are.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The sub ject—the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing that are  conceptualizations 
with respect to apprehendeds called “ (1) the bodhisattvas ’ objects to be engaged in: the 
apprehendeds (that are paths as well as the results conjoined with the wisdom realizing that 
[phenomena] are not observed as truly existent) adopted  by bodhisattvas , and 
respectively, and (2) [the bodhisattvas’] objects to be turned away  from : the 
appre hendeds (by means of the dharmas of the hearers and so forth, the entitinesses 
observ ing [phenomena] as truly existent,) discarded  by bodhisattvas ” as truly existent —
are the discordant classes  of the peak application of the path of seeing , like the afflictions 
that are to be abandoned by the path of seeing , because they act to hinder the production of 
that. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
457   They are the nine types each that exist individually , because they exist in that way 
through divisions of the objects . They are to be known as the self -entities apprehending 
what are not exactly as the objects are, because their objects  appear erroneously.  That 
follows, since they engage by way of adhering to true existence without being related to 
reality.

5.7 If they are asserted to be the appreh enders of any,  
The apprehended objects in reality,  
Then those would be of the characteristic of

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
458 EXPLANATION  
Those are to be individually known as  the natures of nine  each, because  there are nine 
each through divisions by way of objects.  
 It follows that it is inappropriate that they are asserted to be the apprehenders of any  
object that accords with reality, because they are mistaken consciousnesses whose  
apprehended objects do not exist in reality . If that is accepted, then those  conceptions  
would be of the characteristic of being empty of the entities of unmistaken 
apprehenders in that way , because of that which is accepted .  
2B2C -2A2B -2B1A -2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Sinc e there exist apprehenders bearing the support of substantially 
and imputedly existent beings respectively of ordinary beings and arya persons, because the conceptualizations with respect to apprehenders act to hinder, they are the discordant 
classes. Nine  types individually exist through divisions of objects, at that time when “those 
which have become the things that are the objects of those do not exist as the entities of the 
apprehendeds according with the apprehended objects,” they are not apprehenders also of 
whatever, because of that, since they are entities devoid of the entities of apprehending, because of appearing erroneously, they are to be known as the self- entities not exactly as the 
objects are.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the  conceptualizations with respect to apprehenders  that are objects to be 
abandoned by the Mahayana path of seeing , since there exist apprehenders bearing the 
support of substantially and imputedly existent beings respectively in the continua of 
ordinary beings and arya persons  who are the individual supports —are the discordant 
classes  of the peak application of the path of seeing, because they act to hinder  the 
production of that. They are to be known as the entities apprehending what are  not 
exactly as the objects are, because  their objects  appear erroneously. That follows since 
they are consciousnesses that are entities devoid of the entities of apprehending  that 
according with the reality of the objects. That follows  because they are not apprehenders 
also of whatever  accordin g with the reality of the objects . That follows  because at that 
time “those which have become the things that are the objects of those conceptions do 
not exist as the entities of the apprehendeds according with how the apprehended  
objects are apprehended.”  It is that  nine types individually exist, because they exist in that 
way through divisions of objects.

460 EXPLANATION  
The subject—conceptualizations of the first type of apprehendeds, the nine types 
bearing the support of the class of engagement that adhere  to “objects to be engaged in as 
objects to be apprehended with respect to the Mahayana paths as well as results” as truly 
existent objects of utilization —are asserted to be objects to be abandoned by the 
occasions of the application of the Mahayana path of seeing , because they are conceptions 
that act to hinder the production of that  [Mahayana path of seeing] . 
 What are the nine? With respect to that, there are:  
A. The bodhisattvas’ objects of engagement as a generality 
B. The particularities of the bodhis attvas’ objects of engagement  
A. The bodhisattva’s objects of engagement as a generality 
(1) The subject —the Mahayana paths and results, the entitiness of attainment 
conventionally, even though unsurpassable enlightenment devoid  of ultimate existence is 
not realized and not attained ultimately by the six perfections  devoid  of ultimate 
existence —are the bodhisattvas’ objects of engagement, because they are their objects of 
practice.161  
B. The particularities of the bodhisattva’s objects of engagement  
B1. Cause s of the path s 
B2. Entities of the path s 
B3. Results of the path s 
B1. Cause s of the path s 
(2) The subject —bodhisattvas —become definite in the Buddha lineage, due to definitely  
entering the Immovable ground and so forth. 
B2. Entities of the path s 
B2A. Actual path s 
B2B. Objects of observation of the path s 
B2C. Ability to destroy the objects of abandonment  
B2A. Actual path s 
(3) The subject —they —perfectly accomplish the paths of seeing and so forth, due to 
realizing all phenomena as empty of true existence, like illusions. 
B2B. Objects of observation of the path s 
(4) [The subject—they —] observe  unmistaken objects of observation of consciousnesses, 
due to understanding all phenomena are exhausted in being mere appearing images. 
B2C. Ab ility to destroy the objects of abandonment  
(5) [The subject —they —] have attained a special ability to eliminate the objects of 
abandonment, because of having attained the ability to produce the antidotes to the 
knowledge obscurations and to destroy the di scordant classes, due to adopting that is 
preceded by having seen the excellent qualities and discarding that is preceded by 
having seen the faults.

forth in accordance with the mentality  of trainees.

with respect to  the results of placing all beings  who are the three lineage -bearers  in 
nirvana, because of acting in that way through the force of perfect skillful means  in 
enacting others’ welfare .

to encompassing all the Mahayana, to progress to the exalted knower of all aspects at the

antidote to all knowledge obscurations .

The first type of conceptualizations of apprehenders is to be understood as nine, because 
there are nine, adherence to that which upholds and discards posited as a person as a truly 
existent utilizer and so forth .  
 Sutra says, “Bhagavan, the perfection of wisdom does not uphold or relinquish any 
phenomenon... Subhuti, regarding this, the great bodhisattva has grasping, ‘Form,’ or ‘This is form,’…”  
2B2C -2A2B -2B1B -2A3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Upholding and relinquishing, like an i llusion conventionally, (2) 
attention without paying attention  to the perfect, (3) closely related to the three realms 
together through  the nature, (4) abidance through not abiding in emptiness, (5) through not 
adhering to things, adherence to all, (6) all  phenomena are imputations  since they are devoid 
of substantial existence, (7) grasping that precedes non -adherence  through non- grasping that 
knows such ness, (8) through the mere antidote that cultivates equality, the antidote, and (9) 
through not knowing perfectly the perfection of wisdom, obstructed with respect to progress 
as desired: through adhering to the aforesaid as ultimate things, conceptualizations of the 
first type  with respect to apprehenders, the nine types, are asserted  to be objects to be 
abandoned by the occasions of the application of the path of seeing.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —through adhering to apprehenders belonging to ordinary beings as things  
that are ultimate utilizers , conceptualizations of the first type with respect to 
apprehende rs, the  nine types  included within acquired knowledge obscurations —are 
asserted to be objects to be abandoned by the occasions of the application that is the 
peak application of the path of seeing , because they are directly destroyed by it . 
 In terms of ab andoning the seeds, the names of the antidotes have been merely 
individually explained from the point of view of the objects of abandonment; in fact, the 
seeds of all the four conceptualizations of apprehendeds and apprehenders, the objects to be 
abandoned by the path of seeing, by that very uninterrupted path of the peak application of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
466 the path of seeing are abandoned simultaneously. Know that the assertion that they are 
abandoned sequentially is damaged by many reasonings. 
 Although it has been explained that the first two types of conceptions of apprehendeds 
and conceptions of apprehenders are abandoned by paths that are preparations of the applications, since a little [degree] of those two are easily abandoned from the point of view 
of the manifest, they  have been posited in that way; it does not indicate that there are two 
stages of antidotes that abandon the seeds prior to the liberated path on the peak application of the path of seeing.  
 What are the nine?  
A. Degeneration from the causes of the exalted  knower of all aspects  
B. Degeneration from the result, the exalted knower of all aspects  
A. Degeneration from the causes of the exalted knower of all aspects  
A1. Adherence to behavior  
A2. Adherence to objects of knowledge 
A3. Adherence to objects of abandonment and antidotes

ROOT TEXT  
5.15 Not definitely emerging as intended,  
Definitely apprehending a path as not being a path,  Production as well as cessation,  
Possession and non- possession of properties,  
 
5.16 Abiding, destroying the lineage,  
Seeking, lacking the cause, and  
Observing antagonists:  
These are the other conceptualizations that apprehend.  
 
EXPLANATION  
These are the nine other conceptualizations that apprehend  those—from not definitely 
emerging exactly to the objects of intent, until observing antagonists  such as maras —
imput ed as beings as truly existent utilizers, because there are nine through divisions by way 
of objects .  
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, it is like this. A wheel -turning king rides on a chariot drawn by four 
pairs of horses and travels evenly on the road… Subhuti, it  is like this. For example, a 
master of combat who wields the bow of proficiency and endowment is difficult to defeat 
by enemies and antagonists. Likewise, a great bodhisattva who practices the perfection of 
wisdom is difficult to defeat by maras or the go ds belonging to the class of maras…”  
2B2C -2A2B -2B1B -2B3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Due to definite  emergence of the hearers and so forth, not 
definitely emerging exactly to the objects of intent, (2) since the path of one’s desire  does 
not exist, definitely apprehending the other path as not a path, (3) through the conventional 
entities of causes and results, producing and ceasing, (4) through appearing as being without 
obstacle and the other, possession and non -possession of all t hings, (5) through likeness 
with a bird that abides in the sky, abiding on forms and so forth, (6) by means of the 
generated mind of enlightenment and so forth, destroying the lineage of hearers and so 
forth, (7) since qualities superior to thusness do not  exist, without desire, (8) through 
dependence upon ultimate truth, lacking the  cause, and (9) through the nature of having 
immensely familiarized with miserliness, observing things that are antagonists such as the 
maras: conceptualizations of the second t ype that apprehend through adherence to the 
aforesaid imputed things, nine types, are asserted to be objects to be abandoned by  the 
occasions of engaging the mind and mental factors of the path of seeing.  
 
EXPLANATION

2B2C -2A2B -2B1C. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
In that way, through nine interceding stanzas, due to the four types, there are individually nine types of discordant classes of the path of seeing. One should understand also that  the 
four types of antidotes to abandon those are similarly different by way of divisions.  
 
EXPLANATION  
In that way, through nine interceding stanzas, due to the four types of conceptions , 
there are individually nine types of discordant classes of the  peak  application of the  path 
of seeing. One should understand also that the four types of antidotes to abandon those 
objects of abandonment  are similarly different by way of divisions .  
 With respect to the conceptualizations that are objects of abandonment, the presentation 
of the four possibilities by means of objects is as follows. (1) That which is a conception of 
apprehendeds but not a conception of apprehenders is: adherence to forms and so forth as 
truly existent objects of utilization. 
(2) That which is  a conception of apprehenders but not a conception of apprehendeds is: 
adherence to a single instant of consciousness as a truly existent utilizer. It is not an 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
471 apprehended object [because] it has been posited that it cannot be realized by a direct 
perceiv er of an ordinary being.168 
(3) The possibility that is both a conception of apprehendeds  and a conception of 
apprehende rs is as follows. With respect to  consciousness that possesses continuity , 
adherence to it as an object of utilization is a conception of apprehendeds, while adherence 
to it as a utilizer is a conception of apprehende rs.169 
(4) That which is neither is: the non -dual perfection of wisdom.

ROOT TEXT  
5.17 With respect to enlightenment, teaching others,  
Thoroughly bestowing the causes of that, and  
The uninterrupted cause for attaining that, 
The characteristics of immense merit.  
 
EXPLANATIO N 
In order to generate the peak  application of the path of seeing, the cause that generates final 
great enlightenment, one transforms it from the possession of the two causal accumulations: 
(i) for the sake of placing  other  Mahayana lineage -bearers in enlightenment , teaching  the 
method for that , (ii) thoroughly bestowing the words and meanings of the perfection of 
wisdom, the causes of that enlightenment , and (iii) the possession of the uninterrupted 
cause for attaining that  peak application of the path of seeing , the characteristics of 
                                              
168 The reason seems faulty. The fact that it cannot be realized by a direct perceiver of an ordinary being does

472 immense merit, cultivating the perfection of wisdom during meditative equipoise by 
oneself and so forth . 
 The causes of the final great enlightenment are indicated. However, this is only to be 
definitely applied to indicating the causes of the temporal gre at enlightenment, the peak 
application of the path of seeing.  
 Respectively, sutra says:  
“Ananda, these 6,000 bhiksus will attain clear complete  buddhahood in unsurpassable 
complete enlightenment in the star- like eon.”  
“Ananda, therefore I bestow upon you  this profound perfection of wisdom.”   
“Ananda, therefore, if a person of the hearer vehicle teaches Dharma possessing the 
perfection of wisdom to a great bodhisattva one day, when small, his merits will increase 
greatly.”  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2A -3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Placing others in enlightenment, which bears characteristics that will 
be explained, through teaching the path of seeing and so forth, is one. By means of perfect 
words, meanings, and so forth, as the sole causes of enli ghtenments, bestowing the 
perfection of wisdom perfectly and individually to those, is the second. The uninterrupted cause that acts to attain enlightenment, the characteristics of much more merit of cultivation 
of the perfection of wisdom by oneself and s o forth, is the third. 
 
EXPLANATION  
A. Placing in enlightenment   
B. Placing in the causes of that  
C. Cultivating one’s uninterrupted cause  
A. Placing in enlightenment   
The subject —placing other  trainees  in enlightenment, which bears characteristics that 
will be explained  below , through teaching the path of seeing and so forth,  by a 
bodhisattva on the path of preparation —is one cause of the peak application of the path of 
seeing, because it is included within the accumulation of merit that observes the final  great 
enlightenment, a cause for generating it .   
B. Placing in the causes of that  
By means of perfect words, meanings, and so forth  that teach the mode of abidance, as 
the sole causes of temporal and final enlightenments, bestowing the perfection of wisd om perfectly and individually to those trainees by a bodhisattva on the path of 
preparation, is the second  cause that generates the peak application of the path of seeing, 
because it is included within the accumulation of merit, a cause for generating it, [and]  it 
places others in the causes of it .  
C. Cultivating one’s uninterrupted cause  
The subject —the exalted wisdom of meditative equipoise of the path of preparation 
possessing  the characteristics of much more merit of cultivation of the perfection of 
wisdom during meditative equipoise by oneself, teaching it to others during subsequent 
attainment, and so forth, the uninterrupted cause that acts to attain the enlightenment  
included within the peak application of the path of seeing —is the third  cause of the peak 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
473 application of the path of seeing, because it is other than the two previous [causes and] it is 
the substantial cause of that .

2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
5.18 The e xalted wisdom of the extinction and  
Non-production of stains is described as “enlightenment.”  
Because they are devoid of extinction and are devoid of production,  
Those are to be known respectively.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for indicating in sutra that “Obscurations are devoid of extinction 
ultimately and are devoid of  production ultimately,” because they  are established in 
dependence on a correct sign to be devoid of  extinction and be devoid of production 
ultimately. Those final exalted wisdoms directly knowing that they are devoid of extinction 
and production  ultimately are to be known respectively  as the great enlightenments 
knowing extinction and non -production, because the final exalted wisdom directly 
knowing the ultimate  extinction and non -production of stains is described  in sutra  as the 
“great enlightenment”  and is suitable in that way . 
  Sutra says, “Ananda, those who assert the extinction of the perfection of wisdom just 
assert the extinction of space,” and “Do those phenomena have p roduction? If they do not 
exist, how can they have extinction?”  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The stains that are afflictive and knowledge obscurations that have been produced and that have not been produced, imputed, since “Not belonging to 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
474 dharmadhatu, in this way phenomena do not exist” occurs, the entitinesses of dharm adhatu, 
like space, because they are devoid of  cessation and production; or as there do not exist 
feasible  things by way of the entitinesses of one and many, and the signs of causes and 
results and so forth, like a lotus in the sky, because of being devoid  of cessation and 
production. Respectively, the knowledge of the extinction and non -production of stains, the 
characteristic of realizing all phenomena non -erroneously, the nature of exactly the truth 
body and so forth, is described as being the great enli ghtenment. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the stains that are afflictive and knowledge obscurations that have been  
produced  and are past now, and that have not been produced  and are future, imputed as 
truly existent by the Propounders of Things —are devoid of  true existence , because of being 
devoid of  cessation and production ultimately . There is pervasion, because of the 
entitinesses of dharmadhatu, the emptiness of true existence, like space, of all 
phenomena, which are empty of true existence, since , also in th at way  “Not belonging to 
dharmadhatu, in this way phenomena do not exist” occurs  in [Maitreya’s] 
Differentiation  of the Middle and the Extremes  (Madhyantavibhaga). Mentioning “ or” has 
the significance of either sign being established.  
 The subject —the obscurations —are devoid of  true existence, because they are free from  
the entitinesses of truly existent one and many.  The subject —those —are devoid of  
production ultimately, because they are not produced ultimately : (i) from a cause that is self, 
other, or bot h, or (ii) causelessly. [This is] because with respect to the above predicate and 
subject, ultimately existent results are not produced [and] also those that are non -things are 
not produced ultimately , like a lotus in the sky. The stains are devoid of extinction and 
production  ultimately, because they are devoid of  cessation and production  ultimately . 
That follows, as there do not exist feasible things ultimately  by way of the signs and so 
forth  that have been explained.  
 The subject —the exalted knower of all aspects, the characteristic of realizing the mode 
of abidance of all phenomena non- erroneously, the nature of exactly the three exalted 
bodies, the truth body and so forth—is described as being the great enlightenment,  
because it is the final exalt ed wisdom  respectively knowing the ultimate  extinction and 
non-production of stains .
