ROOT TEXT  
5.19 With respect to the nature that is without cessation,  
By the path called “seeing”  
What type of conceptualization is extinguished?  What aspect of non -production is attained?  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —by the Mahayana path called “seeing” —what previously existing  truly 
existent type of conceptualization  that is a knowledge obscuration  is extinguished, and 
what true cessation that is the aspect of non -production  that is a severance of the 
continuation of production of a truly existent [type of conceptualization that is a knowledge 
obscuration]  is attained?  It follows that [kind of true cessation] it is not attained, because it 
[i.e. the Mahayana path of seeing] is  of the nature that is without a cessation  in which 
previously existing  truly existent stains are newly stopped. That follows, because truly 
existent stains have never existed previously. You accepted the former consequence.

abandoned the conceptualizations that are afflictive and knowledge obscurations in all 
ways, in the manner of having abandoned the seeds, is a state that is greatly amazing , 
since a contradiction has been asserted . The a foresaid has been taught through an 
interceding stanza:   
 
EXPLANATION  
If it is asserted that they are not empty of true existence like that, that is, phenomena exist 
perfectly, then, according to that asserted, the assertion that the Bhagavan has abandone d 
the conceptualizations that are afflictive and knowledge obscurations in all ways, in the 
manner of having abandoned the seeds, is a state that is greatly amazing, since a 
contradiction has been asserted. The aforesaid has been taught through an interced ing 
stanza:   
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
5.20 Any statement that the Teacher  Has extinguished the obscurations also with respect to objects of 
knowledge,  
Even though phenomena of others exist,  
I deem that as amazing! 
 
EXPLANATION  
I, Maitreya, deem that— any statement that the Teacher has extinguished the 
obscurations of confusion also with respect to objects of knowledge , even though outer 
and inner  phenomena ultimately exist in the system of others , the Propounders of 
Things —as amazing , because if things were to exist ultimately, it would be unsuitable for 
obscurations to be abandoned!  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3. Commentary  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3A. Presentation of the generality 
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B. Meaning of the words  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
477 2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3A. Presen tation of the generality  
It is incorrect to link the first two of these yogic grounds to only those of the hearers schools 
and the Proponents of Mind- Only, because the uncommon meanings imputed by them are to 
be refuted in this context. 
 Further, someone, having taken the yogic grounds to be four, explained that the second 
accords with the view of solitary realizers, and the third accords with the view of Mind -
Only. This is also incorrect, (i) because the views of solitary realizers and Mind -Only do not 
differ in being higher or lower, (ii) because , although it is propounded that “Since solitary 
realizers assert that apprehenders are truly existent, whereas the Proponents of Mind -Only  
assert consciousnesses (in which apprehendeds and apprehenders are non -dual) exist 
ultimately, [their views] are different,” that is merely agreeable only in the absence of 
having undertaken investigation, and (iii) because the view based upon a sign of definite 
concurrent observation by the second yogic ground has been indicate d, and there is no 
Proponent of Mind- Only who has asserted a way to determine a view that is higher than 
that.  
 The assertion of the “four yogic grounds” is also something to be reflected upon, 
[because] the stages of a person —who, having refuted that blu e is a different substance 
from the consciousness to which blue appears, does not refute that (apprehendeds and 
apprehenders are different substances in which) the consciousness to which blue appears is 
a different substance from blue —are incorrect.  
 OPINI ON: Even though it has been refuted on the second yogic ground that blue is an 
external object, an apprehended being merely another substance relative to an inner consciousness has not been refuted. Therefore, the emptiness of apprehendeds and 
apprehenders  being mere different substances is indicated by the third yogic ground.  
RESPONSE: That is also very incorrect, (i) because, like it has been said in [Dharmakirti's] 
Commentary on [Dignaga's] “Compendium of Valid Cognition”  (Pramanavarttikakarika ) 
that “Th e argument is similar for that,” apprehendeds and apprehenders being different 
substances relative to an inner consciousness has also been refuted in dependence on that very previous reasoning, and the levels of higher or lower tenets with respect to that have 
not been taught by any of the great trailblazers, and (ii) because it follows that the 
Proponents of Mind- Only  have not refuted apprehendeds and apprehenders being mere 
different substances [from the point of view of] a sense consciousness in dependen ce on a 
sign of definite concurrent observation; a sign of definite concurrent observation that 
accords with the Proponents of Mind- Only  has been indicated by the second yogi c ground, 
but, according to you, it is necessary to refute apprehendeds and appreh enders being mere 
different substances in dependence on the third yogi c ground above that [second yogic  
ground].  
 With respect to the determining of the view of the Proponents of Mind- Only , it is realized 
without exception in dependence on a sign of the definite concurrent observation; that which accords with it is just what has been indicated as the second yogic ground. Since 
there is no other view above that besides the view of the Middle -Wayers, the “four yogic 
grounds” are only something to be reflect ed about.  
 In this Ornament for Clear Realization , only three levels of vehicles from the point of 
view of views have been indicated: (1) the small, the common realization of selflessness of 
persons ; (2) the middling, the realization of apprehendeds and ap prehenders being empty of 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
 
478 different substances ; and (3) the great, the realization of consciousnesses as not being truly 
existent. 
  QUALM: They seem to have been indicated sequentially in this chapter. Why is this the case?  
RESPONSE: This is to be analyzed. These explanations on the yogic grounds here indicate 
the stages of meditation on the essential meaning in the way it has been taught in the 
Ornament for Clear Realization . Further, one initially meditates on the  common [realization 
of] selflessness of persons. Having been preceded by that, after that one pays attention to apprehendeds and apprehenders being empty of different substances, in which there is an absence of external objects, the mode of Cognition -Only  conventionally. Since the Acarya 
made a presentation of the absence of external objects concordant with that of the Yogic Practitioners (Yogacara ) conventionally, the sign of definite concurrent observation has 
been mentioned here; the occasion for determ ining the uncommon tenets of Mind -Only has 
not arrived , and they are not asserted [here] since it would be inappropriate.  
 With respect to that, the indication according to two levels is as follows. In terms of 
realizing subtle emptiness, when one directly realizes the emptiness of true existence during meditative equipoise, during subsequent attainment one realizes that dependent -arisings are 
illusions through the force of experience. Like that, when one directly realizes the emptiness of apprehendeds and apprehenders being different substances during meditative equipoise, 
during subsequent attainment one turns away the apprehension of sense consciousness es to 
which blue appears and so forth as unmistaken other- powered natures, and realizes that they 
are m istaken other- powered natures through the force of experience. Therefore, the stages of 
the modes of realization of meditative equipoise and subsequent attainment have been merely indicated; the assertion that the sequence of higher and lower views has bee n 
indicated is only inappropriate since it cannot withstand analysis. I shall explain the remaining points from time to time.

2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1A1. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1A2. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation    
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1A3. The result of meditation     
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
 
479 1. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation  
The subject —with regard to that,  the appropriated aggregates —are empty of a person who 
is not merely imputed upon the collection or the continuity, since they are produced and 
disintegrate. There is pervasion,  because whatever is a self of persons is empty of 
production and disintegration.  
2. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation    
In that way, in dependence on calm abiding, [one meditates] during meditative equipoise, 
thinking, “A self of persons does not exist.”  
3. The result of meditation   
Through having meditated in that way, the manifest acquired  adherence to a self of 
persons has been abandoned and also the manifest innate [adherence to a self of persons] 
has been suppressed . Having completely cast that away, because aggregates and so forth 
devoid of that  self are dependent -arisings,  one perfectly observes the bearers of the 
properties of production and disintegration during subsequent attainment . 
 One acts to abandon the adherence to the continuity as substantially existent. Meditation 
on this through a union of calm abiding and special insight exists from the path of preparation until the path of seeing. However, the meditation in dependence on mere calm 
abiding exists even on the path of accumulation. The purpose is, having destroyed the glory of the afflictions on the occasions of the paths of accumulation and preparation, fo r the sake 
of suppressing the classes of sufferings of cyclic existence. 
 Further, if one has not abandoned the cyclic existence that is a result of karma and 
afflictions, let alone accomplishing others’ welfare, one will not [be able to] accomplish 
even one’s welfare. Hence it has been taught that bodhisattvas have despondency with respect to cyclic existence that is a hundred thousand times stronger than that of hearers. This is the stage of meditating on that very knower of bases that has been explained as the subject matter of the third chapter above. There is a purpose for cultivating the yogic ground that observes selflessness, because it is for the sake of (i) suppressing the manifest 
afflictions and (ii) passing through the grounds of hearers having viewed them by knowing 
and seeing.

2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1B1A. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1B1B. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
 
480 2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1B1C. The result of meditation   
 
A. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation 
The subject —blue —it and the awareness of it, that is, [the awareness] to which  it appears, 
are not different substances, because of definite concurrent observation. An awareness 
that is based upon the aforesaid sign is a reasoning  of the wisdom arisen from 
contemplation. 
 
B. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation  
During meditative equipoise, one  pays attention, thinking, “This blue is the nature of only 
mere mind; there is no external object,” and meditates on that.  
   
C. The result of meditation   
Even though one has not observed subtle emptiness  through not having completely cast 
away adherence to the mind bearing the aspect of an apprehender  as truly existent , one 
has abandoned adherence to external objects; one understands dualistic appearances are mistaken . 
 
Since the Acarya made a presentation of conventionalities concordant with that of the Yogic 
Practitioners (Yogacara ), he asserted that apprehendeds  and apprehenders are empty of 
being different substances even conventionally and a knower of emptiness of duality is established as a mere conventionality. The [assertion of the] mere non -existence of external 
objects even conventionally is common to the Proponents of Mind- Only; the true existence 
of a knower of non -duality should not be asserted in any way in this context.  
 There is a purpose for meditating on apprehendeds and apprehenders being empty of 
different substances, because it is for the sake of (i) abandoning the adherence to 
apprehendeds and apprehenders being different objects and (ii) passing through the grounds of solitary realizers having viewed them by knowing and seeing. This is the stage of meditating on the points that have been indicated in the second chapter and on the occasion of the aspects of the knower of bases. Further, it is meditated on the Mahayana path of accumulation until the path of seeing.  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1B2. The stages of attention during subsequent attainment  
COMMENTARY  
By definitely thinking, “If apprehendeds do not exist, apprehenders do not exist,” having eliminated  even that mere cognition, the characteristics of the aspect of apprehender, [and] 
having ascertained that “Solely this exalted wisdom of non -duality is a correctly  existing 
entity.”  
 
EXPLANATION 
By definitely thinking  during the occasion of subsequent attainment , “If apprehendeds do 
not exist  as another substance , unmistaken apprehenders to which apprehendeds  and 
apprehenders appear to be of different substances  do not exist,” having eliminated even 
that mere apprehension of the characteristics of the aspect of apprehender as unmistaken cognition, [and] having ascertained that “Solely this  exalted wisdom of non-
duality of the apprehendeds and apprehenders is a correctly  existing entity,” one pays 
attention to that . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
 
481  This is the meaning of not having abandoned the conception of true existence with 
respect to  a knower of non -duality with respect to the imprint of that realization; it is 
inappropriate [to interpret it as] (i) adherence to true existence that is induced by its own 
force and (ii) establishment as truly existent as the meaning of the formulated tenets. (a) Understanding that apprehenders  to which appr ehendeds and apprehenders appear to be of 
different substances are mistaken and (b) counteracting the apprehension that they are 
unmistaken as explained above indicate the occasion of subsequent attainment.  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1C. Yogic ground that realize s all phenomena are empty of true existence  
COMMENTARY 
Furthermore, that, because it is a dependent -arising, like an illusion, is devoid of  entitiness. 
Through having meditated, thinking, “Perfectly and solely, [phenomena are] free from the 
imputed entities as the thing, the absence  of the thing, and so forth,” when the strength of 
meditation is accomplished, like the knowing of gems, silver, and so forth in someone, all signs that mistake have  been abandoned. If the non -conceptualizing awareness to which the 
nature of being like an illusion  appears  is perfectly produced like whatever that is to be 
cognized by the individual itself, the yogi perfectly abandons the knowledge obscurations.  
EXPLANATION 
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1C1. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1C2. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation    
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -1C3. The result of meditation   
1. Reasoning of the wisdom arisen from contemplation  
The subject —furthermore, that knower of non -duality —is devoid of  entitiness ultimately , 
because it is a dependent -arising, for example, like an illusion, since, by way of the 
reasoning, “When the true existence of things has been negated, since the object of negation does not exist, it is clear that the negation does not exist perfectly,” that the negation is truly existent is also refuted. 
2. Yoga of the wisdom arisen from meditation  
Through having meditated, thinking, “Perfectly and solely, [phenomena are] free from 
the imputed entities as the thing  that is true exis tence , the truly existent  absence  of the 
thing (that is true existence) in which that has been negated , and so forth,” one abandons 
the adherence to true existence. 
3. The result of meditation  
Through having meditated on the emptiness of all phenomena,  when the strength of 
meditation is accomplished, [that is,] the clear appearance with respect to dharmata is fully 
completed, the yogi perfectly abandons the afflictive obscurations and the knowledge 
obscurations. The reason is because, at that time, all sign s that  mistake  the aggregates as  
truly existent, the seeds of the acquired knowledge obscurations, have been abandoned, 
[and] the non- conceptualizing awareness (of meditative equipoise to which the awareness 
itself, although appearing, is empty of true exi stence, appears as the nature of being like 
an illusion)  is perfectly produced like whatever that is to be cognized by the  individual 
meditative equipoise itself, for example, like the knowing of the forms of crystal gems, 
silver, and so forth  individually  without mixing them up  in someone  who is an expert.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
482  An “awareness to which the nature of being like an illusion  appears” [in the commentary 
above] i s illusion -like meditative equipoise, because that has been taught on many occasions 
in [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary . Regarding the assertion that dependent -arisings 
appear as illusions in the perspective of a learner arya’s meditative equipoise that di rectly 
comprehends ultimate truths, since it lacks even the mere scent of the Middle -Wayers, know 
that as erroneous propounding while not having realized the Acarya’s intention. The 
assertion by someone in the past that that the Acarya established illusion s through reasoning 
is also just a deprecation of the Great Middle -Wayer.  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -2. Explaining the mere meaning of the words of the root text  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -2A. Demonstration of damage to ultimately existent obscurations  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B-2B. The contradiction between accepting that and accepting the assertion that the 
seeds of knowledge obs curations can be abandoned  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -2A. Demonstration of damage to ultimately existent obscurations  
COMMENTARY 
It is not like that, becau se in the way  space, the entity of the mere absence  of substance, is 
apprehended at all times, a self -entity in which there is no generation and there is no 
abandonment is apprehended. If the entities of mere dharma knowledge that are the instants 
and the characteristics of the objects of knowledge exist ultimately, then at that time, by 
meditation on the antidotes —like  space —they do not act even slightly on those. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It follows that a self- entity in which there is no new generation of antidotes t hat act to 
abandon the afflictive obscurations and knowledge obscurations and there is no abandonment  of obscurations is  apprehended, because  the obscurations are  not empty of 
true existence like that, that is, they exist ultimately, in the way,  for example, 
uncompounded space, the entity of the mere absence  of obstructive substance, is 
apprehended at all times. If you accept that, then  it follows that at that time , when  the 
objects of abandonment and the antidotes exist ultimately, by meditation on the 
antidotes —like  one is not able to produce benefit or damage to  space —the antidotes  do 
not act even slightly  to damage those  objects of abandonment, because the entities of 
mere dharma knowledge that are the instants and the obscurations to the 
characteristics of the objects of knowledge exist ultimately.  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -2B3B -2B. The contradiction between accepting that and accepting the assertion that the 
seeds of knowledge obs curations can be abandoned  
COMMENTARY  
Because of that, since others do not  turn away the errors of adherence to things, one —who 
[accepts] that knowledge obscurations have been abandoned in all ways by the Bhagavan 
and who accepts also that phenomena exist —by accepting mutually contradictory meanings, 
I deem as amazing!  
 
EXPLANA TION  
One—who [accepts] that knowledge obscurations have been abandoned in all ways by 
the Bhagavan and who accepts also that phenomena that are objects of abandonment and 
antidotes exist ultimately —is pervaded by accepting mutually contradictory meanings , 
since others, the Propounders of Things, (a) have accepted that knowledge obscurations 
have been abandoned by the Bhagavan, and (b) have accepted that objects of abandonment 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
483 and antidotes exist ultimately, and do not turn away the errors of adherence to ul timately 
existent things  if they exist ultimately. Because of that established above in that way , I, 
Maitreya, deem that as amazing!

2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -3B1. The mode of abidance of things that are the bases  
ROOT TEXT  
5.21a  With respect to those, there is no object to be eliminated,  
 
EXPLANATION  
With respect to those constituents that are pure by nature , there is no previ ously existent 
object— ultimately existent (i) objects of adherence of the conceptions of self of persons 
and of phenomena and (ii) afflictions —to be newly eliminated, because ultimately existent 
objects of adherence of the two conceptions of self and affli ctions have never existed from 
the beginning.  
 Through this, (i) the superimpositions that adhere to any phenomenon as truly existent 
and have deviated from the meaning of the mode of abidance, and (ii) their objects of 
adherence, have been indicated to no t be established from the beginning. Although the 
conception of afflictions as ultimately existent is a conception of self of phenomena, explaining that itself separately is with purpose.  
 
ROOT TEXT  
5.21b There is no object to be posited slightly.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
484 EXPLA NATION  
With respect to these  constituents, there is no previously non -existent object—the two 
selflessnesses —to be newly posited even slightly, because they are the natures of being 
devoid of a self of persons and of phenomena .   
 Through this, the refutat ions of (i) the deprecations that wrongly engage the meaning of 
the mode of abidance, saying, “the two selflessnesses do not exist,” and (ii) their objects of 
adherence, have been indicated to not be newly posited. 
 Explaining in general that there is no stain to be eliminated and there is no antidote to be 
generated —without affixing qualifications to the objects of negation —is a deprecation.  
 Furthermore, that very explanation by way of the first two lines [5.21ab] is correlated to 
the meaning established : if it is established that there is no previously existent affliction that 
is truly existent to be newly [eliminated], then it is established that there is no previously 
non-existent affliction that is empty of true existence to be newly posited. 
 That is  an explanation in terms of the explicitly indicated self- isolates; if the emptiness of 
true existence, ultimate truth, is established (since, with respect to these —the person and the 
aggregates —there is no previous existent that is truly existent to be ne wly eliminated,) then, 
(since it is established that there is no previously non -existent illusion -like falsity that is 
empty of true existence to be newly posited even slightly,) with respect to objects that are 
empty of being established by nature ultimat ely, conventional truths —in which all actions 
and agents are feasible —are established. 
 In summary, the union of the two truths —that is permitted due to our system’s 
acceptance of (i) ultimate truth, the emptiness of true existence in which the focal objec t of 
the conception of true existence does not exist even slightly, and (ii) all presentations of 
objects and agents —has been indicated. 
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -3B2. The view that realizes it  
ROOT TEXT  
5.21c  It correctly views the perfect.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The wisdom realizing the absence  of nature ultimately, which correctly views  
the perfect—the emptiness of the person and the aggregates being established by nature 
ultimately —in that way, is  the view that understands the meaning of the mode of abidance .   
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B-3B3. Result of familiarization with the view  
ROOT TEXT  
5.21d Having seen the perfect, freedom . 
 
EXPLANATION  
If, having directly seen the perfect meaning, (conjoined with the extensive factor of 
method, the class of deeds,) one familiarizes with it aga in and again , freedom , the state of a 
perfect complete buddha, will be attained . 
2B2C -2A2B -2B2B -3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because of what? Because to be liberated while  adhering  to things is 
incorrect. Any phenomenon, without having been  eliminated or posited by the entities of 
deprecation and superimposition, regarding dependent -arisings, forms and so forth, these 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
485 very entities that exist perfectly conventionally, should be inve stigated as entities being 
devoid of  nature and so forth. In that way, since one turns away errors, like an illusory 
elephant defeating another illusory elephant, when one sees suchness  one will be completely 
liberated. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —dependent- arisings, forms and so forth, these entitiness es that exist 
perfectly , that is, by way of their own character, conventionally —should be investigated 
and meditated upon as entities being devoid of  nature ultimately and so forth by those 
desiring to attain om niscience; because of what? Because to be liberated  while  not having 
abandoned adher ing to things as existing ultimately is incorrect. They should view the 
mode of abidance of conventionalities that are the bases in the manner of a union of the two 
truths, because any phenomenon should be known as not existing ultimately and as 
existing conventionally , without having been (i) eliminated by the entity of deprecation , 
saying “It does not exist even conventionally ,” or (ii) posited by the entity of 
superimposition , saying “It exists ultimately .” When, through having familiarized with it 
in that way,  one sees suchness with direct cognition, one will be completely liberated, 
since , if one familiarizes with it in that way, one turns away errors , falsities that are  
objects of abandonment, by way of falsities that are antidotes , like an illusory elephant 
defeating another illusory elephant.

The subject —that uninterrupted path included within the forbearance of dharma

The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
486 perfections mutually included in each of generosity and so forth —is the  peak application 
of the  path of seein g in this  context, because it is a clear realization of the truth that acts to

are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing . 
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, with res pect to this, when a great bodhisattva performs generosity, 
he completely dedicates it to omniscience and performs generosity.”

COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. That which has been produced bearing the thirty -six aspects, 
distinguished by the pure three circles, included within the forbearance of dharma 
knowledge with respect to sufferings, the same instant as that which is the six perfections, 
generosity and so forth, mutually included as above in each entity of generosity and so forth 
individually is the path of seeing with respect to this clear realization of the peak. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that uninterrupted path  which has been  produced bearing the thirty -six 
aspects that are divisions by way of isolates distinguished by the pure three circles  with 
respect to all six perfections, that included within the forbearance of dharma knowledge 
with respect to sufferings, the same instant as that which is the six perfections, 
generosity and so forth, mutually included (as explained in the context of achieving of 
armor170 above ) in each entity of generosity and so forth individually—is the path of 
seeing with respect to this c lear realization of the peak. The sign is like [that in the 
explanation of] the root text. 
 That very uninterrupted path with respect to directly destroying the seeds that are the 
knowledge obscurations has been indicated as being produced in the nature of infinite clear 
realizations; if it were not distinguished by infinite accumulations of merit, it would not be able to abandon the seeds that are the knowledge obscurations.

2B2C -2A2B -2B2C -2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
5.23 Then , through having absorbed into the meditative stabilization  
Of the loftily looking lion,  
He realizes the forward order and  
Reverse order of dependent -arising.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Having indicated the mode of abandoning the objects to be abandoned by the peak 
application of the path of seeing, then, the subject —the yogi who cultivates the peak 
application of the path of seeing —possesses a special function that acts to gain control o ver 
the abandonment, because, due to having absorbed into the meditative stabilization of 
the loftily looking lion  during meditative equipoise , during subsequent attainment he 
realizes the forward order and reverse order of dependent- arising.  
 The assertio n—that the yoga (that is cultivated on the occasion of the path of meditation 
after the peak application of the path of seeing has been cultivated) has been indicated by the “then” mentioned [in the root text 5.23a]—is unrelated, because the Acarya very cl early 
indicated that [it refers to] the cultivation by that very [yogi] abiding on the peak application 
of the path of seeing.  
 Even if they were correlated to the earlier and later stages with respect to ascertainment, 
one should explain that the uninterrupted path has been indicated by the earlier passage, and 
the liberated path (in which fear of the two obscurations has been extinguished) has been 
indicated by this [passage].   
 Sutra says, “One differentiates among the eight liberations and the nine serial absorptions 
and absorbs into the meditative stabilization of the loftily looking lion,” and “One 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
488 individually investigates the twelve links of dependent -arising in forward order and in 
reverse order.”  
 
OPINION: The clear realization of the truth is one  instant; since that itself is not to be 
repeatedly cultivated, that contradicts the words in [the above section on] “Making a connection.”  
RESPONSE: There is no fault. (i) Since there are two earlier and later occasions of uninterrupted path and liberated  path, it is explained to be “repeatedly cultivated,” and (ii) 
further, not only is it cultivated during meditative equipoise, during subsequent attainment the investigation with respect to dependent -arising  is explained to be “repeatedly 
cultivated.” Henc e there is no contradiction. Correlating these [paths] explained here with 
the exalted wisdoms of the pure grounds has already been explained above to be unrelated.

2B2C -2A2B -2B2C -2C1. Peak application of the path of seeing included within meditative equipoise  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The yogi who has attained the path of seeing, because he does not have fear of the afflictive and knowledge obscurations, through
 having absorbed into the 
meditative stabiliz ation of the loftily looking lion, …  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the yogi who has attained the  peak application of the  path of seeing— has 
absorbed into the meditative stabilization of the loftily looking lion, because he is a 
person abiding in the clear real ization of the truth who does not have fear of the afflictive 
and knowledge obscurations  that are the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing by 
means of having directly abandoned the seeds of the acquired knowledge obscurations.  
“Through” leads to w hat comes later.  
2B2C -2A2B -2B2C -2C2. Peak application of the path of seeing (that is to be passed through having been viewed 
by knowing and seeing ) that is attained subsequent to that  
COMMENTARY 
… afterwards, with regard to dependent -arising, he correctly investigates in forward order, 
“Through the condition of ignorance, compositional action,” and so forth, and in reverse order, “Through having ceased birth, aging and death cease,” and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
From the meditative equipoise on ultimate truth, afterwards, with regard to dependent -
arising, he correctly  investigates:  
(i) the thoroughly afflicted in forward order : from “Through the condition of ignorance, 
compositional action ; through the condition of compositional action, consciousness ,” until 
“Through the condition of birth, aging and death,”  
(ii) the completely pure in forward order:  from “Through having ceased ignorance, 
compositional action ceases” until  “Through having ceased birth, aging and death 
cease,” and so forth,  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
489 (iii) the thoroughly afflicted in  reverse order : from “Aging and death arise from birth,” 
until “Compositional action arises from ignorance,” and  
(iv) the completely pure in reverse order,  when one wonders, “Through having ceased what 
do aging and death cease?”: from “Through having ceased birth, aging and death cease,” 
until “Through having ceased ignorance, compositional action ceases,” and so forth.  
 
There is a purpose for investigating  in that way, because it is for the sake of understanding 
the Four Noble Truths exactly. Also, (a) through having investigated the thoroughly 
afflicted in reverse order, having understood the nature of sufferings, one produces the 
desire to be separated from them, and (b) through having investigated [the thoroughly 
afflicted] in forward order, having understood the causes of sufferings, one produces the 
desire to abandon origins. If one has established with valid cognition that origins can be abandoned, (c) through having investigated the completely pure in reverse order, one will 
understand that sufferings can be ceased, and (d) through having investigated [the completely pure] in forward order, one will understand that the true paths that act to cease 
ignorance are to be relied upon in one’s continuum. 
 This explanation here is the explanation of that very path of seeing ( that is to be passed 
through having been viewed by knowing and seeing)  that is to be generated in the continua 
of bodhisattvas.

171 Refer to the chart on leaping absorptions.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
490 2B2C -2A2B -3A2A. Presentation of the generality  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 1. The manner in which it has been indicated in sutra  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 2. The manner in which it has been summarized by the treatise  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 3. Explaining the nature of the leaping [absorption]  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 1. The manner in which it has been indicated in sutra  
With respect to the nine serial absorptions, the following have been taught:  
the preparation for the absorptions in forward order and in reverse order: the meditative 
stabilization of the loftily looking lion,   
an ascending process without inserting anything in -between,  
an ascending process by alte rnating the four concentrations and the four formless 
absorptions with the [absorption of] cessation, and  
a descending process by alternating the nine serial absorptions with the desire realm mind. 
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 2. The manner in which it has been summari zed by the treatise  
Having indicated the meditative stabilization of the loftily looking lion by the first two lines 
[5.24ab], and a mere summary of the descending process [involving] alternation with the 
desire realm mind by the remaining lines [5.24cd —5.25], [Maitreya], having thought that 
the two middle ones are easy to understand, [indicated them] through implication.  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2A- 3. Explaining the nature of the leaping [absorption]  
The bases of alternation, the four concentrations and the four formle ss absorptions, are 
alternated with the alternated property, the [absorption of] cessation. At the time when the nine serial absorptions have been taken as the bases of alternation, the alternated property is 
the desire realm mind . Therefore , when one is a ble to enter into absorption having 
performed alternation in that way, one will have complete d the dexterity in meditative 
stabilization.  
 Regarding the ascending process without inserting anything in -between, since merely the 
loci of the series is indica ted, it is not an actual leaping [absorption], because proceeding 
through alternating with that which is not similar to the bases of alternation has been taught to be a leaping [absorption]. If one has not previous ly entered a Hinayana path, this is 
attain ed on the sixth ground and above.  
2B2C -2A2B -3A2B. Meaning of the words  
ROOT TEXT  
5.24abc  Having ascended and descended in two ways  
In the nine absorptions that are along with the [absorption of] cessation,  
The leaping absorption.  
 
EXPLANATION  
[This] is t he leaping absorption here, because it absorbs by means of the two, the 
preparation and the actual. It is an absorption preceded by a preparation, because, with 
respect to the first actual [absorption], having ascended and descended in two ways —in 
forward order and in reverse order—in the nine absorptions that are along with  the 
[absorption of] cessation, after that one initiates the actual [absorption]. 
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, what is the meditative stabilization of the loftily looking lion of a 
great bodhi sattva? Subhuti, regarding this, a great bodhisattva —who is isolated from desire, 
isolated from non -virtuous qualities that are negativities, together with investigation, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
491 together with analysis, and bears the joy and the happiness that are produced from 
isolation —will accomplish the first concentration and abide in it.”  
 
ROOT TEXT  
5.24d  Taking the demarcation of the non -equipoise  
 
5.25abc  Consciousness included in the desire realm,  
Having leaped over one, two, three,  Four, five, six, seven, and eight,  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a purpose of taking the demarcation of the non -equipoise consciousness 
included in the desire  realm  and abiding in it, because it is for the sake of alternating the 
nine serial absorptions with the desire realm mind in the descending process. The subject —
the eighth -grounder —is able to alternate the nine serial absorptions with the desire realm 
mind, bec ause he is able to enter into absorption:   
1. first, having entered into the [absorption of] cessation, actualizing the desire realm mind;  
2. then, having leaped over only one, the [absorption of] cessation, actualizing the Peak 
of Existence, and then actualizin g the desire realm mind;   
3. then, having leaped over two, the [absorption of] cessation and the Peak of Existence, 
actualizing Nothingness and then actualizing the desire realm mind;  
4. then, having leaped over three, the previous two  [—the absorption of cessation and 
the Peak of Existence —and Nothingness], actualizing Infinite Consciousness and then 
actualizing the desire realm mind;  
5. similarly, having leaped over four, actualizing Infinite Space and then actualizing the 
desire realm mind;  
6. then, having lea ped over  five, actualizing the fourth concentration and then 
actualizing the desire realm mind;  
7. then, having leaped over six, actualizing the third concentration and then actualizing 
the desire realm mind;  
8. then, having leaped over seven,  actualizing the se cond concentration and then 
actualizing the desire realm mind; and   
9. then, having leaped over eight, actualizing the first concentration and then actualizing 
the desire realm mind. 
 
ROOT TEXT  
5.25d  Proceeding dissimilarly, until  the [absorption of] cessat ion. 
 
EXPLANATION  
That absorption that has been explained is called a “leaping absorption,” because it is an 
absorption that proceeds by means of alternating dissimilar  equipoise and non -equipoise 
[minds] , [alternating ] the four concentrations and the four formless absorptions, until  the 
[absorption of] cessation , [with the desire realm mind] .

meaning of the words.  
2B2C -2A2B -3A3B. Actual  
Regarding the ascending process without inserting anything in -between that was explicitly 
indicated in sutra, [Haribhadra], having thought that it was easy to understand, also did not

COMMENTARY 
Again, having entered into absorption on the first concentration, having arisen from that, 
cessation; similarl y, from [the second concentration]  until the source Without 
Discrimination Not Without Discrimination, they enter into absorption on cessation. Having 
arisen from that, they enter into the absorption immediately; observe. 
  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those [b odhisattvas who have attained the sixth ground and above] —there is a 
reason for them to enter into the actual leaping absorption, because it is for the sake of 
training in the dexterity of meditative stabilization: again, having entered into absorption 
on the first concentration, having arisen from that, [they enter into absorption on] 
cessation; similarly, having arisen from [the second concentration] until  the source 
Without Discrimination Not Without Discrimination, they enter into absorption on 
cessatio n. Having arisen from that, they enter into the absorption immediately  
following the absorption on cessation ; there is a reason for them to observe the Peak of 
Existence and enter into absorption, because having actualized the desire realm mind after 
that, it is for the sake of alternating with the desire realm mind in the descending process.   
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
493 OPINION: The purpose for entering into the absorption on the Peak of Existence after the 
[absorption of] cessation is that, since one is unable to arise from the [a bsorption of] 
cessation into the [absorption of] cessation, one re gresses  slightly from  it. 
RESPONSE: Even if you have thought that in the ascending process the bases of alternation 
are all nine absorptions, that is inappropriate, (i) because, at the time when the [absorption 
of] cessation has been taken as a basis of alternation, it is incorrect to take the [absorption 
of] cessation as the alternated property, and (ii) since all nine absorptions are present in the 
bases of alternation in the descending pro cess, the [absorption of] cessation would be 
missing in the enumeration in this commentary.  
 Therefore, although one is able to arise into an uncontaminated path after the [absorption 
of] cessation, know that one enters into the absorption on the Peak of E xistence for the sake 
of alternating the desire realm mind in the descending process, through  actualiz ing the 
desire realm mind after that. Hearers, due to a lesser method, are distant by way of the four 
types of distances: support, object of observation, aspect, and absorption.  
 
COMMENTARY 
Having set himself on the entity of the demarcation consciousness that operates  in the desire 
realm, arisen through the strength of skillful means, a non -equipoise consciousness. Having 
actualized like that, after that, into cessation. Then into non -equipoise. After that, casting 
away only one, cessation, the source Without Discrimination Not Without Discrimination. 
Then into non- equipoise. Then casting away two, the source Nothingness. Then into non -
equipoise. Similarly, having cast away up until eight, into the first concentration. Then into 
non-equipoise.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Although [hearers] are unable to actualize a desire realm mind immediately after the Peak of Existence, a bodhisattva, arisen from the Peak of Existence, through the strength of 
skillful means, having actualized  a desire realm mind  like that, a non -equipoise 
consciousness to which forms and so forth of the desire realm appear, having set himself 
on the entity of the demarcation consciousness that operates i n the desire realm, 
initiates the descending process. Then, after that, [he enters] into cessation. Then [he 
enters] into non- equipoise. After that, casting away only one, the [absorption of] 
cessation, [he enters] the source Without Discrimination Not Without Discrimination. 
Then [he enters] into non- equipoise. Then casting away two, [he enters] the source 
Nothingness. Then [he enters] into non -equipoise. Just understand that. Similarly, having 
cast away [three] up until  eight—[the eight] from cessation until  the second 
concentration —[he enters] into the first concentration. Then [he enters] into non -
equipoise.

ROOT TEXT  
5.26 The brief, the extensive, that not held  By the aid of the buddhas,  
The non- existent qualities of the three times,  
The three types of paths that are excellent : 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
495  
5.27ab  This one type of conceptualizations of apprehendeds  
Are possessors of objects of utilization of the aspects of the applications.  
 
EXPLANATION  
There are nine by way of objects, because there are the conceptions —from the adherence to 
the brief and the extensive  scrip tures as truly existent objects to be engaged in, until the 
adherence to the three types of paths that are excellent as truly existent objects of 
utilization. 
 The entity of the conceptions is  this one type of conceptualizations  of apprehendeds  
that adhere  to (i) the scriptures and (ii) their subject matter, the bodhisattvas’ paths and 
results, as truly existent objects to be engaged in. The antidotes are the occasions of the 
preparations of the applications, possessors of objects of utilization of the aspe cts of the 
applications.   
 Respectively, sutra says:  
(1-2) “Bhagavan, how will a great bodhisattva seek the brief and the extensive of all the 
Dharma?”  
(3) “If one learns the perfection of wisdom that has been taught...”  
(4) “Great bodhisattva, this one w ho abides in the perfection of wisdom that has been taught 
in that way is to be known as irreversible.”  
(5) “Accomplish the perfection of wisdom...”  
(6) “One meditates on the perfection of wisdom; it is to be known to have these benefits.”  
(7) “Since depen dent-arising has no essence, practice the perfection of wisdom.”  
(8) “By accomplishing the emptiness of space, accomplish the perfection of wisdom.”  
(9) “By meditating on the emptiness of space, meditate on the perfection of wisdom.”  
2B2C -2A2B -3B2A -1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) The brief Dharma that takes care of sentient beings who like the 
brief, (2) the extensive Dharma that takes care of sentient beings who like the extensive, (3) 
due to not being persistent regarding the meani ngs as they have been explained, not being 
aided , that is, held by the buddhas, (4) by the ceasing of arisal, the excellent qualities of the 
path of preparation do not exist, (5) by not possessing the cause of perfectly producing, the excellent qualities o f the path of seeing do not exist, (6) by the future not existing, the 
excellent qualities of the path of meditation do not exist, (7) by pacification of errors and so 
forth, the path of preparing to attain nirvana, (8) by the accomplishment of emptiness, the 
path of seeing, and (9) by meditating upon the absence  of nature, the path of meditation. 
Regarding the aforesaid, the first type of conceptualizations of apprehendeds, since they are the objects to be abandoned on this occasion, are the nine types of object -possessors on the 
occasion of the application of the path of meditation.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the first type of conceptualizations of apprehendeds —are  of nine types , 
because there are  nine objects to be abandoned by the  object- possessors on the occasion of 
the application that is the peak application of the  path of meditation.  That follows, since 
the nine divisions by way of objects  are the objects to be abandoned on this occasion  of 
the preparation s of the applications . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
496  What are the nine? (1) The subject —the conception included within innate adherence to 
the brief Dharma scriptures that take care of sentient beings who like the brief as truly 
existent objects to be engaged in —is an object of abandonment of the preparati ons of an 
application of the path of meditation of a Mahayana arya, because it is to be directly 
destroyed by them . Apply the sign and the predicate similarly to the ones below. 
 (2) The subject —that included within innate adherence to the extensive Dharma  
scriptures along with the subject matter that takes care of sentient beings who like the 
extensive as truly existent objects to be engaged in —[is an object of abandonment of the 
preparation s of an application of the path of meditation of a Mahayana arya, because it is to 
be directly destroyed by th em.] The meanings are as follows. The former is adherence to the 
subject matter of the scriptures, the knowledge of the general characteristics of thusness and 
so forth, as truly existent objects to be engaged in , and the latter is adherence to the 
extensive Dharma, the knowledge of the individual specific characteristics, as truly existent 
objects to be engaged in.   
 (3) The one in terms of behavior is the adherence to the attention that is a yoga of the 
perfect ion of wisdom, (an antidote to not being aided, that is, held by the buddhas 
through them acting as protectors , due to [oneself] not being persistent regarding the 
meanings of the brief and the extensive as they have been explained,) as a [truly existent] 
object to be engaged in. “Aided172” is a synonym of “assisted” and “protected”; explaining 
it to mean “sent out173” is a case of erroneous terminology.  
 
 (4-6) The three in terms of the excellent qualities of the paths are adherences to:  
• the excellent qualiti es of the path of preparation, which  do not exist  ultimately but 
exist conventionally , by the ceasing of arisal , that is, reliance on the path of seeing ,  
• the excellent qualities of the path of seeing , which  do not exist  ultimately but exist 
conventionally , by not possessing the cause of perfectly producing  them as the four 
extremes , and  
• the excellent qualities of the path of meditation , which  do not exist  at the time of the 
path of seeing but exist at the time of the path of meditation, by not existing as a future  
substance,  
as truly existent objects to be engaged in.  
 
 (7-9) The three in terms of the entity of the paths are: the subject —the innate conceptions 
that are adherences to:  
• the path of preparing to attain nirvana, by pacification of errors , the conception of 
permanence and so forth, and realizing impermanence  and so forth,  
• the path of seeing, by the accomplishment of, that is, meditation on the new realization 
of emptiness, and  
• the path of meditation, by meditating upon the absence  of nature ultimately that has 
already been realized with direct cognition on the path of seeing , 
as truly existent objects to be engaged in —[are object s of abandonment of the preparation s 
of an application of the path of meditation of a Mahayana arya, because they are  to be 
directly destroyed by th em.]

5.29 Meditating,  not meditating,

EXPLANATION  
Regarding the antidotes, the second type of conceptions of apprehendeds, the conceptions 
of apprehendeds to be turned away from, that are objects to be abandoned by the Mahayana path of meditation, are not abandoned by any antidote, because they are asserted to be 
abandoned by the uninterrup ted paths that are the object- possessors engaging minds and 
mental factors  included within the Mahayana path of meditation . 
  Know that there are nine conceptualizations that are to be abandoned on the occasions of 
the uninterrupted paths of the path of me ditation of a Mahayana arya, because there are nine 
divisions by way of the objects to be turned away from, not producing the mind  of 
enlightenment and so forth.  
 Sutra says, “Having generated the first mind, hold it close,” and “Practice the perfection 
of wisdom until abiding in the essence of enlightenment.”   
2B2C -2A2B -3B2A -2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Due to being separated from the spiritual guide and so forth, not 
producing the mind of enlightenment, (2) due to being devoid of the superior merit of the 
buddhas, not paying attention to the essence of enlightenment, (3) because of being a hearer lineage -bearer, paying attention to the vehicle of that, (4) because of being a solitary realizer 
lineage -bearer, actualizing the vehi cle of that, (5) due to lacking achievement of the 
perfection of wisdom, not paying attention to perfect complete enlightenment, (6) due to 
being together with the observation, meditating, (7) due to lacking the observation, not 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
498 meditating, (8) due to lack ing the observation and also lacking non -observation, lacking 
meditation and also not lacking meditation, and (9) since erroneously adhering, not exactly 
according with the meaning. Regarding the aforesaid, the second type of conceptualizations 
of apprehen deds, since they are the objects to be abandoned on that occasion, are the nine 
types of object -possessors on the occasions of engaging minds and mental factors of the 
path of meditation.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the second type of conceptualizations of ap prehendeds  included within 
innate objects of abandonment, the conceptions of apprehendeds to be turned away from —
are the  nine types of object- possessors, because there are  nine by way of objects. They are 
to be destroyed  on the occasions of the uninterrupt ed paths engaging minds and mental 
factors of the path of meditation  of a Mahayana arya , since they are the objects to be 
abandoned on that occasion.  
 What are the nine?  
 (1) The subject —someone on the hearer’s path of accumulation who proceeds to solitary 
peace —does not produce the mind of complete enlightenment as long as he has not 
attained the result of hearer foe -destroyer, due to being separated from the spiritual guide 
and so forth who guides him in that way.  
 The subject —the innate conception that is a bodhisattva’s adherence to that as a truly 
existent object to be turned away from, since the Mahayana generated mind is not produced on a hearer’s path of accumulation —is an object of abandonment of the uninterrupted path s 
of the Mahayana path of meditation, because it is to be directly destroyed by th em. Apply 
the sign and the predicate similarly to the ones below. (2) The subject —the former 
[someone on the hearer’s path o f accumulation who proceeds to solitary peace] —does not 
pay attention to the essence of enlightenment, due to lacking the superior merit of 
observing the buddhas . 
 (3) The subject —someone proceeding to solitary peace who abides on the hearer’s path 
of prep aration —pays attention to the vehicle of that  hearer, because of being a hearer 
lineage -bearer . 
 (4) The subject —someone on the path of preparation who is definite in the solitary 
realizer’s lineage —actualizes the vehicle of that solitary realize r, because  of being a 
solitary realizer lineage -bearer .  
 (5) The subject —someone on the hearer’s path of seeing who proceeds to solitary 
peace —does not pay attention to perfect complete enlightenment as an object to be 
attained in his continuum for the time being , due to lacking diligent achievement of the 
perfection of wisdom . 
 (6) The subject —someone on the hearer’s path of meditation — meditates on a reflection 
of the perfection of wisdom , due to being together with the direct observation  of 
impermanence and so forth without being conjoined with the wisdom realizing emptiness.   
 (7) The subject —a hearer foe -destroyer— does not meditate  on the meaning of the mode 
of abidance , due to lacking the observation  of that. 
 (8) The subject —that —(i) lacks meditation  on the meaning of the mode of abidance , 
due to  lacking the observation  of that , and (ii) also does not lack meditation, due to also 
lacking non -observation of impermanence and so forth.  
 In terms of the object, they are explained in that way; the subject —the innate conception 
that is adherence (to the four possibilities, (i) adherence to meditation as truly existent, (ii) 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
499 adherence to non -meditation as truly existent, (iii) [adherence to] both meditation and non -
meditation [as truly existe nt], and (iv) [adherence to] neither [as truly existent], as truly 
existent) as a truly existent object to be turned away from —[is an object of abandonment of 
the uninterrupted path of the Mahayana path of meditation, because it is to be directly 
destroyed  by that.] The antidote s to that is the uninterrupted path s of the path of meditation 
realizing freedom from the four possibilities in suchness.  
  (9) The subject —hearers and solitary realizers —adhere to that which is not exactly 
according with the meaning  of the mode of abidance, since they erroneously adhere  to 
the meaning of the mode of abidance . The subject —the innate conception that apprehends 
that as a truly existent object to be turned away from, since hearers and solitary realizers erroneously adher e to the meaning of the mode of abidance —[is an object of abandonment 
of the uninterrupted path s of the Mahayana path of meditation, because it is to be directly 
destroyed by them .]

As explained on the occasion of the objects to be abandoned by the path of seeing above, 
the conceptualizations with respect to apprehenders of substantial existence included 
within innate objects of abandonment are the nine types of object- possessors, because 
there are nine divisions by way of objects, the objects of abandonment on the occasion of 
an application of the path of meditation.

be directly destroyed by th em. Apply the sign and the predicate similarly to the remaining 
ones.174 (2) The five aggregates are  just merely imputed  upon as phenomena,  because 
phenomena that are forms and so forth are  just mere appearances  to a knower of 
conventionalities .175 (3) Phenomena of the exalted knower of all aspects and so forth are 
not empty,  i.e. not non -existent, conventionally because [the exalted knower of all aspects 
and so forth] progress as comprehenders to all bases, paths, and aspects . The subject —the 
innate conception that adheres to phenomena of the  three exalted knowers  posited as a 
person  as a truly exis tent utilizer—[ is an object of abandonment of the preparation s of an 
application of the path of meditation of a Mahayana arya, because it is to be directly 
destroyed by them .]  
 The two in terms of view are as follows. (4) The subject —an ordinary being —gra sps to 
phenomena  as truly existent , because of adhering to true existence due to not having 
abandoned adherence in any way. (5) The subject —a bodhisattva —discerns dharma that

The subject —as above, the i nnate conceptions of apprehenders of imputed existence that 
are the last conceptualizations of apprehenders — are asserted to be the nine types of 
object- possessors,  because there are nine differentiated by way of objects. They are objects 
of abandonment on the occasions of the uninterrupted paths engaging minds and mental 
factors of the path of meditation , because they are to be directly destroyed by them . 
 What are the nine? The three types of confusion about the objects of observation of the 
three exalt ed knowers are as follows. (1) The subject —someone on the hearer’s path of 
accumulation —is confused about the abandoning of  the obscurations to the exalted 
knower of all aspects, because of confusion due to not fully knowing all aspects that are 
modes and varieties. (2) [The subject —someone on the hearer’s path of accumulation] —is 
confused about the abandoning of  the obscurations to the knower of paths, because of 
confusion due to not fully knowing all paths  of the three vehicles. (3) The subject —a 
common o rdinary being —is confused about the abandoning of  the obscurations to the 
knower of all, because of confusion due to not fully knowing all bases. The subject —the 
innate conception s that adhere to that which are imputed as being s [who are confused] about 
the abandoning of the three types of obscurations as truly existent utilizer s—[are object s of 
abandonment of the uninterrupted path s of the path of meditation, because they are  to be 
directly destroyed by th em.]176 Due to confusion about the objects of observa tion of the 
three paths, one is confused about the abandoning of the obscurations of those three [exalted 
knowers].

504  The one confusion about the entity of the path is as follows. (4) The subject —that —is

being associated [with thusness and so forth], the two truths being different entities ,

of the uninterrupted path s of the path of meditation, because it is to be directly destroyed by 
them. Most of these are indicated by way of manifest conceptions, but know that  the 
principal objects of abandonment are the seeds [of the conceptions].   
2B2C -2A2B -3B3. Summary  
COMMENTARY 
To abandon those four types of discordant classes of the path of meditation, understand that, “Also the four types of antidotes, by the divisions of those, are different ones.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
Understand that, “Also the four types of antidotes to the four types of conceptualizations of apprehendeds and apprehenders, by the divisions of those  different isolates , are thirty -
six different ones ,” because t hey serve to  abandon those four types of discordant classes 
of the path of meditation , the thirty -six that exist. 
                                             
 
177 Sanskrit: dharmin  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
505 2B2C -2A2B -3C. The benefits of having abandoned the objects of abandonment by the antidotes  
Regarding the excellent qualities that are attained  through having abandoned the objects to 
be abandoned, there are:  
 
2B2C -2A2B -3C1. Making a connection  
2B2C -2A2B -3C2. Root text  
2B2C -2A2B -3C3. Commentary  
2B2C -2A2B -3C1. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY 
Since, at just the same time as the 108 conceptualizations as above are abandoned, the 108 
afflictions included within those are abandoned, all the fulfillments of excellent qualities are 
supported upon the bodhisattva abiding on the path of meditation. The aforesaid is taught 
through intercedin g stanzas:  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject—the bodhisattva arya abiding on the path of meditation —all the 
fulfillments of the classes of excellent qualities  of realizations of the three vehicles are 
supported upon him, since, at just the same time as the 108 conceptualizations of 
apprehendeds and apprehenders that are objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation 
(as explained in the context of the peak application of the path of seeing above ) are 
abandoned, the 108 afflictions included within those conceptions are abandoned.  
 Although it is suitable to explain that the afflictive obscurations (that are objects to be 
abandoned by the path of meditation) are abandoned at the same time as when the knowledge obscurations  (that are objects to be abandoned by the path of meditation) are 
abandoned, according to the explanations in other contexts of [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary ,  it is clear that it is asserted that the manifest conceptions and the seeds that 
are knowledge  obscurations are abandoned at the same time, and even though the manifest 
knowledge obscurations do not occur on the eighth ground and above, there is no 
contradiction with the explanation in that way.

COMMENTARY 
After the path of meditati on, since it is the uninterrupted path, the uninterrupted meditative 
stabilization:  
 
EXPLANATION  
After the peak [application] of the path of meditation, the uninterrupted meditative 
stabilization will be explained, since this very peak  is the uninterrupted  path of 
buddhahood. It has been explained that the peak application of the path of meditation is the generality, and the uninterrupted peak application is a particularity of it. Alternatively, those 
that have been explained on the occasion of the peak app lication of the path of meditation 
are together with the seeds of the conceptions, whereas at the time of the uninterrupted peak application, the seeds are not even slightly present, therefore they are very different.

5.37 Through analogies made of the virtues of setting  
The beings of the billionfold world systems in the excellent realizations  
Of hearers and the rhinoceros -like, 
And in the faultlessness of the bodhisattvas,   
 
5.38 By immense merit,  
That uninterrupted meditative stabilization,  
Is without interruption to buddhahood,  
The exalted knower of all aspects.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that uninterrupted meditative stabilization  at the end of the continuum —is 
the uninterrupted peak application, because it is the final direct cause that is without interruption to buddhahood, the nature of the exalted knower of all aspects. The 
uninterrupted peak application is characterized by means of the immense merit of its result and indicated in sutra, because, through analogies made of the great merit of the virtues 
of setting the beings of the billionfold world systems in [the excell ent realizations], from 
hearers [until] the first ground,  the faultlessness of the bodhisattvas, it has been 
characterized through comparison  by the immense merit of the result of the uninterrupted 
peak application, buddhahood, and indicated . The immense m erit of the direct cause is 
characterized by the immense merit of the direct result.

realizers, and the faultlessness of bodhisattvas, by more merit superior to that, since that which is the exalted knower of all aspects is buddhahood, that which is the uninterrupted 
meditative stabilization  that will attain buddhahood is called the uninterrupted meditative 
stabilization here.

indicated in sutra, because, through analogies made of more meri t [than] that merit that 
is generated by someone through setting the sentient beings of the great thousand of the billionfold world systems in [the excellent realizations of] hearers, solitary realizers, and 
the first ground, the faultlessness of bodhisatt vas, it has been characterized  through 
comparison  by the direct result of the uninterrupted peak application, buddhahood, being of

result of the uninterrupted peak application.   The subject —that which is the uninterrupted meditative stabilization  at the end

the final application that will attain perfect complete buddhahood.

ROOT TEXT  
5.39 The objects of observation of this are devoid of  the thing.  
The empowering is asserted to be mindfulness.   
The aspect is pacification.  
Regarding these, the propounders dispute continuously.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —all illusory phenomena devoid of  the thing  that is true existence —are the 
objects of observation of this  uninterrupted peak application, because they are the bases 
for eliminating superimpositions of this. 
 The subject —the Mahayana generated mind, the  mindfulness  that is included within th e 
path that is the application at the end of the continuum on the tenth ground —is asserted to 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
509 be the empowering  condition of the uninterrupted peak application, because it is the 
condition that is self- powered with respect to generating that. 
 The subject —the uninterrupted peak application —bears the aspect of pacification  by 
nature, because it directly realizes all phenomena as being empty of true existence. 
 These states of the objects of observation and aspect of the uninterrupted peak application 
are very profound, because , regarding these  objects of observation and aspect , the 
propounders of the two truths as contradictory pass from one to another, and dispute , 
saying , “If it is an observed object condition, it is truly existent; if it is devoid of  true 
existence, then that contradicts it being an observed object condition .” 
 Sutra says,  “Subhuti, the objects of observation of the exalted knower of all aspects are 
devoid of  the thing. The empowering is mindfulness. The aspect is pacification. The 
characte ristic is the absence  of characteristic.”

2B2C -2A2B -4A2B -2B. Indicating these states as profound  
2B2C -2A2B -4A2B -2A. Actual  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The observed object conditions of this  uninterrupted meditative 
stabilization are all phenomena observed as being devoid of  the thing. The empowering 
condition is mindfulness. The aspect is pacification by nature. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Compounded phenomena are the observed object conditions of this uninterrupted 
meditative stabilization , because all phenomena are observed by it  as being devoid of  
the thing  that is true existence . The empowering condition and the aspect are as 
[explained in the  explanation on] t he root text. 
2B2C -2A2B -4A2B -2B. Indicating these states as profound  
COMMENTARY 
Regarding these states, because they are difficult to fathom, the argumentative disputes of 
the propounders who have not realized skill in means  arise continuously, those are t o be 
eliminated through skill in means. By this, there is the thought thinking, “The profundity of 
these is explained.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for the argumentative disputes of the propounders (deprived of skill in 
the means of the divisions of th e two truths) arising continuously, because, regarding 
these states of the objects of observation and aspect of the uninterrupted peak application , 
since the divisions of the two truths must be understood, they are  difficult to fathom. By 
this indication o f the argumentative disputes arising continuously , there is the thought 
thinking, “The profundity of these  objects of observation and aspect of this uninterrupted 
peak application  is explained,” because, since the arguments arise continuously, those 
argume nts have been indicated to be eliminated through skill in the means of the divisions 
of the two truths .

wrong conceptions will that meditative stabilization be generated? ”

ROOT TEXT  
5.40 Feasible objects of observation,  
Their entitiness definitely apprehended,  
The exalted wisdom that is the exalted knower of all aspects,

 5.41 The application, the Three Jewels,
