5.42 Characteristics and meditation:  
The wrong c onceptions of the propounders  
Bearing the support of the exalted knower of all aspects  
Are asserted to be of sixteen types.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The wrong conceptions of the propounders of the two truths as contradictory (bearing the support of the objects of observation and aspects of the exalted knower of all aspects 
and the uninterrupted peak application, its cause,) are asserted to be of sixteen types , 
because they are asserted to be sixteen by way of objects . 
 What are the sixteen?
 They are:  
(1-2) two wrong conceptions with respect to the objects of observation and aspect of the 
path meditated upon;  
(3) one wrong conception with respect to the result of meditation;  
(4-5) two disputes with respect to the infeasibility of conventional and ult imate truths, the 
modes of abidance of the bases;  
(6) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of conduct;  (7-9) three consequences with respect to the infeasibility of going for refuge that is the 
support of conduct, as well as its objects;  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
511 (10) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of the attribute of conduct;  
(11-12) two consequences with respect to the infeasibility of clear realizations and their 
objects of abandonment;  
(13) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of the entity of the path;  
(14) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of divisions of the objects of 
abandonment and the antidotes;  
(15) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of specific and general characteristics 
that are to be med itated upon;  and 
(16) one consequence with respect to the infeasibility of meditating upon those.  It has been extensively taught in sutra: “Bhagavan, why? Is it that only an exalted knower 
of all aspects is devoid of  the thing? Or is it that forms are dev oid of  the thing?.…”  
2B2C -2A2B -4B3. Commentary  
2B2C -2A2B -4B3A. Identifying the wrong conceptions that are to be eliminated  
2B2C -2A2B -4B3B. Instructions on generating the uninterrupted peak application after having eliminated 
them   
2B2C -2A2B -4B3A. Identify ing the wrong conceptions that are to be eliminated  
COMMENTARY  
(1) Due to compounded and un compounded constituents being devoid of  the thing, objects 
of observation are feasible, (2) because  of being devoid of  nature in all ways, the entitiness 
of the obj ect of observation is definitely apprehended, (3) due to things and non -things not 
being observed, the exalted wisdom that is the exalted knower of all aspects, (4 -5) due to the 
entitiness of thusness, the two , conventional and ultimate truths, (6) due to generosity and so 
forth not being observed, the application, (7) due to there being no object to be realized, the 
Buddha Jewel, (8) due to just being merely imputed in name, the Dharma Jewel, (9) due to 
the observation of forms and so forth being stopped, the Sangha Jewel, (10) due to 
generosity and so forth not being observed, skill in means, (11) due to the realization of 
entities of the things, non -things, and both being stopped, the clear realizations of the 
tathagatas, (12) due to impermanence that pre sents elaborations, errors regarding 
permanence and so forth, (13) due to the result of meditating upon the path  not being 
actualized, the path, (14) due to discarding and adopting not existing, discordant classes and antidotes, (15) due to the absence  of the property -possessors, the characteristics of 
phenomena,  and (16) due to specific and general characteristics being infeasible, meditation. Regarding the aforesaid, the wrong conceptions of all propounders, beings who 
do not know the skill in means of t he bodhisattvas, through having established  mutually 
contradictory meanings, due to being infeasible, the entity of doubt by the object -possessors 
that have been explained, sixteen wrong conceptions bearing the support of the exalted 
knower of all aspects.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —those  wrong conceptions of all propounders, beings who do not know the 
skill in means of the divisions of the two truths  of the bodhisattvas  explained above —are 
not causes of correct repudiations, because they do not transcend the entitiness of doubt  
when they are well examined. That follows, because they are awarenesses that conceive erroneously due to thinking that subsuming (i) the objects of observation of the exalted 
knower of all aspects and (ii) the emptiness of true existen ce into a common locus is  
infeasible, through having established that (a) being an object of observation of the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
512 exalted knower of all aspects and (b) the emptiness of true existence are mutually 
contradictory meanings  although they are not contradictory. T here are sixteen wrong 
conceptions , because, by the object- possessors that have been explained, the objects of 
observation of the exalted knower of all aspects being infeasible and so forth , there are 
sixteen  bearing the support of the objects of observati on and aspects of the uninterrupted 
peak application and  the exalted knower of all aspects  being infeasible and so forth . 
 What are the sixteen? The wrong conception with respect to the objects of observation is 
as follows. (1) It follows that the objects of observation of the uninterrupted peak 
application and the exalted knower of all aspects are not feasible, due to compounded and 
uncompounded constituents being devoid of  the thing  that is true existence.179 [Those 
propounders] see that the compounded and so forth are not feasible as observed  object 
conditions from their own side if one has refuted that all phenomena are established from 
the side of the objects’ mode of subsistence without being posited throu gh the force of 
appearing to an awareness; and having seen that (i) [the compounded and so forth] are posited as observed  object conditions from their own side and (ii) the meaning of true 
existence that has been explained is not negated, they hold that th e two truths are 
contradictory. When the conventional has been asserted in our system, the observed focal 
objects of the conception of true existence would not become non -existent; if it were to 
become like that, it would be awkward to assert the conventio nal in our system. This is a 
wrong conception with respect to  the objects of observation of the two accumulations.  
 Regarding this, having stated the Middle -Wayers’ assertion that posits the ultimate reality 
as the sign, disputing that the conventional is not feasible is an argument bearing the door of 
the ultimate. If the qualification “ultimate” has been explicitly affixed to the sign, one 
replies that the pervasion is not established, and if it has not been explicitly affixed, one 
replies that the sign is not established, in which case the pervasion with respect to the accuracy of the sign is not established.  
 The wrong conception with respect to the aspect is as follows. (2) It follows that the 
aspect in which the  entitiness of the object of observation is definitely apprehended  as 
being free from elaborations is infeasible , because all phenomena are devoid of  nature  in 
all ways.  
 This also is an argument bearing the door of the ultimate and one should reply that the 
sign is not established. Applying the reply “Conventionally the sign is not established” is 
foolish, since, if the sign were  correct, the n [the reply ] would be “No pervasion. ” If the 
dispute is carried out having explicitly affixed the qualification “ultimate” to the sign, then 
one replies that the pervasion is not established. 
 If (i) someone says, “It follows as being that ultimately,” and one replies “A ccept,” (ii) 
someone says, “It follows as being that conventionally, because of being that ultimately,” 
and one replies that the pervasion is not established, and (iii) someone says, “It follows as 
being that conventionally, because of being that conventio nally,” and one replies that the 
sign is not established, then these are expressions of not keeping in mind the dispute on the two truths.   
 The one wrong conception with respect to the result is as follows. (3) It follows that the 
result, the exalted wis dom that is the exalted knower of all aspects , is infeasible , due to 
things and non- things  not being observed . 
                                             
 
179 This reason means that compounded and uncompounded constituents  do not exist ultimately.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
513  The two  wrong conceptions with respect to the two  truths are as follows. (4 -5) It follows 
that forms and so forth existing conventionally is infeasible, due to the entitiness of 
thusness,  the emptiness of true existence. It follows that ultimate truth is infeasible, due to 
thusness, the emptiness of true existence of all phenomena. 
 With respect to both of those  [(4) and (5)] , the reply is “N o pervasion. ” [The 
propounders] think, “ If it is an ultimate truth, it is truly existent.” The two disputes and 
these two are to be individually explained. Hence the Acarya said “ the two” [in the 
Commentary] . 
 The wrong conception with respect to the entity of conduct is as follows. (6) It follows 
that the application that is a union of calm abiding and special insight is infeasible, due to 
generosity and so forth not being observed. 
 The three wrong conceptions with respect to going for refuge that is the su pport of 
conduct, as well as its objects, are as follows. (7) It follows that the Buddha Jewel is 
infeasible, due to there being no object to be realized  that expands the awareness with 
respect to object s of knowledge.  
(8) It follows that the Dharma Jewel  is infeasible, due to all dharma  just being merely 
imputed in name. 
 (9) It follows that the Sangha Jewel  is infeasible, due to the observation of forms and 
so forth , the bases for the imputed “Sangha ,” being stopped.  
 The wrong conception with respect to the attribute of conduct is as follows. (10) It 
follows that skill in means  is infeasible, due to generosity and so forth that are conjoined 
with not conceiving the three circles  not being observed .   
 The wrong conception with respect to clear realization s is as follows. (11) It follows that 
the clear realizations of the tathagatas  are infeasible, due to the realization of entities of 
the things, non -things, and both that are to be realized  being stopped.  
 The wrong conception with respect to objects of abandonment is as follows. (12) It 
follows that the four erroneous  conceptions  regarding permanence and so forth  of the 
aggregates are infeasible, due to the non -erroneous conceptions  regarding  impermanence 
and so forth, that purify  elaborations  being infeasible . Here also the sign is not established. 
 The wrong conception with respect to the entity of the path is as follows. (13) It follows 
that the entity of the path is infeasible , due to enlightenment, the result of meditating 
upon the path, not being actualized.  
 The wrong conception with respect to the divisions of the objects of abandonment and 
the antidotes is as follows. (14) It follows that the divisions of discordant classes and 
antidotes  are infeasib le, due to the objects to be discarded and the objects to be adopted  
not existing.  
 The wrong conception with respect to the divisions of the characteristics of phenomena is 
as follows. (15) It follows that the specific and general characteristics of pheno mena  are 
infeasible , due to the absence of the property -possessors that are bases of 
characterization.  
 The wrong conception with respect to meditating upon specific and general 
characteristics is as follows. (16) It follows that meditation  upon the specific and general 
characteristics of phenomena is infeasible, due to specific and general characteristics  that 
are to be meditated upon  being infeasible. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
514 2B2C -2A2B -4B3B. Instructions on generating the uninterrupted peak application after having eliminated the m 
COMMENTARY  
Because of that, regarding the arguments bearing the doors of the conventional and the 
ultimate, since it is necessary to reply respectively by means of the ultimate and the 
conventional, the bodhisattvas desiring virtue, having perfectly gen erated in all ways the 
ascertainment that precedes dispelling in a thousand ways, through skill in means that is based upon the two truths, should generate the uninterrupted meditative stabilization.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the bodhisattvas desiring virtu e, the fulfillment of one’s and others’ 
welfare s—having been preceded by skill in the divisions of the two truths, should generate 
the uninterrupted meditative stabilization, because, having perfectly generated in all 
ways the non-erroneous ascertainment of the objects of observation and aspects of the 
uninterrupted peak application and the exalted knower of all aspects  that precedes 
dispelling in a thousand ways  the wrong conceptions that have been explained , through 
skill in means that is based upon both truths, the objects of the two accumulations , they 
should generate the uninterrupted meditative stabilization.  
  They should be skilled in the divisions of the two truths, since, (i) regarding the 
arguments bearing the doors of the conventional  that act to  repudiate the ultimate 
through having stated the Middle -Wayers’ assertion of the conventional as the sign , it is 
necessary to reply respectively by means of the ultimate, (ii) and regarding the 
arguments bearing the doors of the ultimate  that act to repudiate the conventional through 
having stated the Middle -Wayers’ assertion of the ultimate as the sign , it is necessary to 
reply by means of the conventional.  That follows, because  it is necessary to reply that the 
two truths are not contra dictory in response to the continuously arising arguments that the 
two truths are contradictory. As a branch of completing the accumulations of merit in one’s 
continuum, it is appropriate to eliminate the wrong conceptions that apprehend the two 
truths as contradictory in others’ continua. However, mainly one should extinguish the 
manifest conceptions as well as their seeds in one’s continuum.  
 (i) The conception of the two truths as contradictory posited by tenets is exhaustively 
abandoned on the path of seeing, and (ii) the manifest conception of true existence that apprehends contradiction with the conventional operates until the seventh ground , and its 
seeds are exhaustively abandoned by the uninterrupted peak application.  
 In summary, suppose one (i) r efutes that the generation of a sprout by a seed is 
established from the side of the objects’ uncommon mode of subsistence without being posited through the force of appearing to an awareness, and (ii) accepts that a seed generates  
a sprout from its own si de; [those propounders], having seen those two as contradictory, 
dispute that they are infeasible. If the meaning of true existence has been negated, then the 
generation of a sprout by a seed is just posited to be [like] thinking, “In the perspective of 
the conception apprehending a snake upon [the basis of] a mottled rope, a snake exists.”  
 Saying “It follows that the generation of a sprout by a seed from its own side is 
infeasible, because the generation of a sprout by a seed is just posited through the force of 
an appearance to an  awareness” is an argument bearing the door of the ultimate, but the 
reply that the pervasion is not established is approp riate; it is not necessary to only reply 
with the sign not being established relative to the conventional in response to an argument bearing the door of the ultimate.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence                Chapter 5: The Peak Application  
 
515  Furthermore, saying “ It follows that the generation of a sprout by a seed is established  
from the side of the sprout’s uncommon mode of subsistence without being posited through 
the force of appearing to an awareness, because the sprout is generate d by a seed from its 
own side ” is an argument bearing the door of the conventional , but the repl y that the 
pervasion is not established is appropriate . 
 These are the modes of reply by means of the two truths that agree with the assertions of 
the Acarya; if one were to look at how the intention of the Protector Nagarjuna ha s been 
commentated upon by Acarya Buddhapalita and Acarya Chandrakirti, [one would see that] the modes of reply of the two truths based upon that, due to the object of negation being 
narrower than that, have been explained differently.

2B2C -2B1B. Explaining the text of the chapter  
2B2C -2B1C. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -2B1A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
COMMENTARY 
Since one who has attained the clear realization of the peak, having taken cognizance of the 
meanings that are realized individually and together  in accordance with the sequence, 
thoroughly cultivates, because it is to make it stable, the clear realization  of the serial:  
 
EXPLANATION  
After having explained the clear realization of the peak, the clear realization of the serial 
will be explained. The reason is because, since  a person who has  attained the clear 
realization of the peak, having taken cognizance of  the meanings —(i) the aspects of the 
three exalted knowers that have been indicated in the first three chapters that are realized  
individually and (ii) the aspects of the three exalted knowers that have been indicated in 
the [fourth] chapter on [the clea r complete  realization of] all aspects, from the aspects until 
the characteristics, [that are realized] together —in accordance with the sequence  of the 
modes of production , thoroughly  cultivates [them] , it has been indicated for the sake of 
that. There is a reason for cultivating [them] progressively, because it is to make the  
cultivation  stable. 
2B2C -2B1B. Explaining the text of the chapter  
2B2C -2B1B -1. Root text  
2B2C -2B1B -2. Commentary  
2B2C -2B1B -1. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
6.1 Those with generosity until wisdom,  
Remembering the Buddha and so forth, and   
Phenomena, entitinesses devoid of  the thing,  
Are asserted to be the activities of the serial.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Those  progressive cultivations of the aspects of the three exalted knowers by means of 
being conjoin ed with these thirteen topics are asserted to be the activities of the serial.  
What are the thirteen? [Those cultivations are] conjoined with  (1-6) the six perfections, 
from generosity until  wisdom, (7-12) the six recollections  remembering the Buddha and 
so forth, and (13) the wisdom realizing all phenomena  as entitinesses devoid of  the thing  
ultimately.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 6: The Serial Application  
 
517  Sutra says, “Bhagavan, a great bodhisattva practices serially, trains serially, and with 
diligence performs serially with respect to all, the entitinesses  devoid of  the thing.”  
 Someone’s interpretation that the cultivation of these thirteen topics progressively is the 
mode of cultivation of the serial contradicts the Acarya’s text, because in the commentary 
below where the summarized meanings have been ex plained as six180, he explained [the 
mode of cultivation of the serial] to be the cultivation of the aspects of the three exalted 
knowers progressively. Although “ the meanings that are realized individually and 
collectively ” [mentioned] in the section on making a connection of this [point above] are 
cultivated progressively, it is very clear that (i) the indication of the aspects of the three 
exalted knowers individually in the first three chapters, and the occasions of train ing in the 
aspects of the three exalted knowers and not completing [the training] slightly are [the 
significances of] “individually” and (ii) the  indication of the 173 aspects of the three exalted 
knowers completely in the [fourth chapter on the clear comp lete realization of] all aspects is 
the significance of “ collective ;” cultivation of those [173 aspects] completely is the meaning 
of collective cultivation. One must definitely assert that the cultivation of all the aspects of 
the three exalted knowers wi thout exception in a single series having definite sequence, like 
a horse galloping, is the mode of cultivation of the serial.  
 Further, by means of the cultivation in that manner being conjoined with the thirteen 
topics, (i) cultivation on the occasion of the path of accumulation is serial activity, (ii) 
cultivation on the occasion of [partial concordance with] definite discrimination is serial 
training, and (iii) cultivation on the occasions of the path of seeing and the path of 
meditation is serial achie ving. The mode of acting to be conjoined with the thirteen topics is 
to be just understood as the mode of being conjoined with the realization [of phenomena] as entitinesses devoid of  the thing, and one trains through: (a) not being separated from the 
recollection of the Three Jewels, the support, (b) recollecting ethics that is characterized as 
the turning away from discordant classes, (c) recollecting giving that is characterized as the 
engaging in virtue, and (d) recollecting the gods and the gurus as witnesses; one also 
practices all of those through being conjoined with the six perfections.  
2B2C -2B1B -2. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1 -6) Because the four perfections are included within the perfection 
of wisdom, by completing in all ways the six, the perfection of generosity and so forth, 
distinguished by the purity of the three circles, perfectly achieving the ten grounds; (7)(a) 
the close placements of mindfulness and so forth, (b) the aspects of the branches of 
enlightenment, and (c) the eight branches of aryas’ paths, recollection of the Buddha with 
the characteristic of not remembering as existing ultimately, the three types respectively that 
act to describe the partial concordance with definite discrimination, and the paths of se eing 
and meditation; (8) similarly, recollection of virtuous, non -virtuous, and unspecified 
phenomena; (9) like the above, recollection of the bodhisattva arya irreversible sangha; (10 -
12) similarly, recollections of ethics, giving, and the gods; and (13) the understanding of all 
phenomena, forms and so forth, as entitinesses devoid of  the thing. Those which are 
realizations by way of the aforesaid are asserted to be the activities of the serial.  
 
                                              
180 See below: 2B3A. Summarizing into six meanings  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 6: The Serial Application  
 
518 EXPLANATION  
Those which are the  activities of realizing progressively all the aspects of the three exalted 
knowers without exception by way of the first twelve former topics and (13) the 
understanding of all phenomena, forms and so forth, as entitinesses devoid of  the thing  
ultimately are asserted to be the activities of the serial  here.  
 What are the twelve? (1 -6) At the time of cultivating the aspects progressively, one 
should cultivate them by means of being conjoined with the conduct [of] the six perfections, 
because one must cultivate them by means of bein g conjoined with that perfectly achieving 
the ten grounds, by completing  in all ways the six : the perfection of generosity, 
distinguished by the purity of the three circles  in which one does not observe the gift, the 
giver, and the giving to be ultimately existent, and so forth.   
 
QUALM: In order to complete the ten grounds, does not one need the ten perfections?  
RESPONSE: There is no t the fault of the remaining four perfections not being included 
within them, because the four perfections of skillful means, prayer, power, and exalted wisdom are included within the perfection of wisdom.  
 
The way of acting to be conjoined with the excellent attitudes [of] the six recollections is as 
follows. (7) Initially one performs a cultivation by means o f not being separated from the 
recollection of the Buddha, because one performs recollection of the Buddha  by means of 
the causes : the three types respectively , (a) the close placements of mindfulness and so 
forth  [shown] in sutra as acting to indicate  the partial concordance with definite 
discrimination, (b) the cultivation of the aspects of the branches of enlightenment shown 
in sutra as acting to indicate  the path of seeing, and (c) the eight branches of aryas’ paths  
shown in sutra  as acting  to describe  the path of meditation , and so forth .  
 (8) One cultivates through being conjoined with the recollection of  the Dharma, because 
one performs cultivation by means of similarly (i) not remembering virtuous, non -
virtuous, and unspecified phenomena as existing ultimately , and (ii) recollecting exactly 
the characteristic of having adopted, discarded, and acted in equanimity [respectively with 
respect to  virtuous, non- virtuous, and unspecified phenomena] conventionally. 
 (9) One performs cultivation by means of r ecollection of the Sangha , because, like the 
above, one cultivates without being separated from the recollection of the bodhisattva 
arya irreversible sangha  as assistants. (10-12) One cultivates similarly  through (i) not 
remembering those recollections of ethics  that are characterized as the turning away from 
discordant classes , giving  that is characterized as the engaging in virtue , and the gods , the 
witnesses, as existing ultimately, and  (ii) having remembered exactly the characteristics [of 
their] existi ng conventionally.

Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
2B2C -2B2. The instantaneous application that has attained stability   
2B2C -2B2A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
2B2C -2B2B. Explaining the text of the chapter  
2B2C -2B2C. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -2B2A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
COMMENTARY 
Because of thoroughly cultivating, that is, greatly familiarizing, with the clear realization of 
the serial, since one cultivates those by way of a single instant , there is the clear complete  
enlightenment of a single instant; further, that, since there are four types by way of the 
characteristics:  
 
EXPLANATION  
After the serial, it is appropriate to explain  the clear complete  enlightenment of a single 
instant, since —when one has progressively thoroughly cultivated , that is, excellently 
culminated the familiarization with, the aspects of the three exalted knowers, the clear realization of the serial —one is able to cultivate tho se by way of a single instant . The 
reason for that is because of having greatly familiarized with  them for many countless 
eons. The subject —further, that—is of four, since there are four types by way of the 
characteristics  in which one entity has been differentiated by way of isolates.

COMMENTARY 
The first, the  clear complete  enlightenment of a single instant with the characteristic of all 
uncontaminated qualities that are non -ripened in a single instant:   
 
EXPLANATION  
The first of the four instants , the clear complete  enlightenment of a single instant with 
the characteristic of actualizing all uncontaminated qualities that are non- ripened  
(since they have been accomplished with exertion on the seventh ground and below) in a 
single instant:  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
 
521 2B2C -2B2B -1A2. Root t ext 
ROOT TEXT  
7.1 Because all uncontaminated qualities are  
Included within each of generosity and so forth,  
This realization of a subduer  
In a single instant is to be understood.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —this realization in which the uncontaminated non -ripened paths are actualized 
in a single instant  in the continuum of a subduer  abiding on the uninterrupted path at the 
end of the continuum —is to be understood  as the first instantaneous application, because it 
is a final application in which the uncontaminated non -ripened classes are actualized. At 
that time, when a single uncontaminated exalted wisdom has been actualized, one is able to actualize the others, b ecause all qualities that are uncontaminated paths are included 
within  each of generosity and so forth.  
 Sutra says, “Great bodhisattva, practicing the Mother in that way acts to fully complete 
the six perfections with a single generated mind.”   
2B2C -2B2B -1A3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because it occurs in the system in the self- entity of dharmadhatu: 
“One thing is the entitiness of all things. All things are the entitiness of one thing. 
Whichever sees the thusness of one thing sees  the thusness of all things.” It is not exhausted 
only in one being included within many; rather, a single instant that observes the exalted wisdom of generosity and so forth, through the entity of being separat ed from the errors of 
definitely apprehending  things as individuals, because of including all phenomena with the 
characteristics —from generosity and so forth, until the eighty excellent exemplifications —
the realization of the subduer bodhisattva should be known as “the clear complete  
enlightenment in  a single instant.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the realization of the subduer bodhisattva abiding on the uninterrupted path 
at the end of the continuum —should be known as “the clear complete  enlightenment in a 
single instant,”  because it is not exhausted onl y in one being included within many , 
such as an a soka tree being included within a forest ; rather, it is a final application in which 
that very exalted wisdom at the end of the continuum (cultivating  a single instant that 
observes the exalted wisdom of gen erosity and so forth ) cultivates by way of including 
generosity and so forth, until  the eighty excellent exemplifications. At the time of 
cultivating one instant of uncontaminated exalted wisdom, one is able to cultivate all uncontaminated paths, because a t the time of the uninterrupted path, one is able to do that 
through  (i) the conducive conditions for cultivating in that way being complete, and (ii) the 
entity of being separated  from all the adverse conditions , the errors of definitely 
apprehending thin gs that are the two truths as individual  entities. At the time of the 
uninterrupted path at the end of the continuum, although one has not exhaustively 
abandoned the stains of negative states, know that there is not even th e slightest stain at that 
time.  
 There is a reason for being able to extinguish the stains that apprehend the two truths as 
different entities, because it occurs in the system of the great trailblazer Acarya Aryadeva : 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
 
522 (i) having thought that all phenomena are the same in the entity of dha rmadhatu that is the 
emptiness of true existence, it is said, “One thing is the entitiness of all things ,” and (ii) in 
the perspective of the seer that is an arya’s meditative equipoise, the dharmata of all things 
appear as only one aspect; they do not app ear as dissimilar, [so] it is said, “All things are 
the entitiness of one thing.” Further, since they appear in that way, “Whichever person 
directly sees the thusness of one thing sees the thusness , the dharmata, of all things.”

ROOT TEXT  
7.2 Like all are set in motion together  
When the apparatus of a water mill  
Is propelled by a being with a single stride,  
Likewise it knows in a single instant.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The exalted wisdom at the end of the continuum is able to  know  all uncontaminated exalted 
wisdoms  in a single instant , because, like in the analogy that all are set in motion 
together when the apparatus of a water mill is propelled by a being with a single 
stride, it is able to likewise  know . 
2B2C -2B2B -1B3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. It is like, for example, when propelled by a being with a single 
stride, through the strength of previous excellent construction by a skilled craftsman, all of 
the apparatus  of a water mill is set in motion together at one time. Due to t he projection of 
former prayers and the force of dharmadhatu, in only a single instant, when a single uncontaminated wisdom is observed, all those concordant in type are actualized.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuu m—when a single 
uncontaminated wisdom , such as a branch of enlightenment, is observed in only a single 
instant, all those concordant with it in type are actualized, because one is able to do that 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
 
523 due to the projection of former prayers , thinking, “May I be  able to actualize all in one 
instant,”  and the force of dharmadhatu in which all phenomena are the same taste in the 
nature of thusness . It is like, for example, when propelled by a being with a single stride, 
through the strength of previous excellent co nstruction by a skilled craftsman, all of 
the apparatus of a water mill is set in motion together at one  time.

Exalted wisdom in a single instant.

produced, the exalted wisdom realizing the uncontaminated [qualities] that are  the state s of 
the ripened in just a single instant is the clear complete  enlightenment  in a single instant, the 
perfection of wisdom.

7.4 Through abiding on all phenomena as being like dreams,  
By means of the conduct of generosity and so forth,  
It realizes, in a single instant,  
That phenomena are devoid of  character.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuu m—is the characterless 
instantaneous application, because it is a final application that directly realizes, in a single 
instant, that phenomena of the thoroughly afflicted class and completely pure class are 
devoid of  character  ultimately. It is able to re alize that, because it is a final application 
that, through abiding on the realization of all phenomena as being like dreams, by 
means of the conduct of the six perfections, generosity and so forth,  has familiarized with 
that. 
 Sutra says, “When a great bo dhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, he abides on 
the dream -like five appropriated aggregates and practices generosity … cultivates wisdom.”  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
 
525 2B2C -2B2B -3C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. By previous familiarization with all pheno mena as being like 
dreams, having experienced the two accumulations, in the state of realization, abiding with 
respect to all phenomena with the entitiness  of dreams, the appropriated aggregates and so 
forth, through achieving the six perfections, generosi ty and so forth, through just a single 
instant, bearing the aspect that definitely realizes the entitinesses of generosity and so forth, 
since it knows “All phenomena are without character,” phenomena with the entities of the 
thoroughly afflicted and the c ompletely pure are just without  character, it is the clear 
complete  enlightenment in a single  instant.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that —is the  third clear complete  enlightenment in a single instant, since it 
is a final application bearing the aspect of definitely realizing the entitinesses of 
generosity and so forth  that, through just a single instant, knows “All phenomena are 
without character  ultimately ,” thoroughly afflicted and the completely pure 
phenomena are just without character  ultimately . The tenth ground bodhisattva at the end 
of the continuum is able to know that, because —(i) abiding  through realizing in that way 
with respect to all phenomena with the entitiness of dreams, the appropriated 
aggregates and so forth  that appear but are devoid of  nature , and (ii) abiding through 
achieving the six perfections, generosity and so forth— he has familiarized with them for 
three countless eons. That follows, because , by previous familiarization with all 
phenomena as being like dreams  on the ninth ground and below , having experienced the 
two accumulations, he is preceded by having attained and familiarized with  the state of 
realization.

7.5 Like not seeing a dream and tha t which sees it  
In a manner of duality,  
It sees in a single instant  
The thusness that is the non -duality of phenomena. 
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence              Chapter 7: The Instantaneous Application  
 
526 EXPLANATION  
The subject —the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuum —is the non -dual 
instantaneous application, because it is a final yoga that sees in a single instant the 
thusness that is the non -duality, the emptiness of being different substances,  of 
phenomena  that are apprehendeds and apprehenders, for example, like not seeing (i) the 
mountains, houses, and so forth of a dream  and (ii) the consciousnesses that see them —in 
a manner of different substances . 
 Sutra says, “When a great bodhisattva practices the perfection of wisdom, he does not see 
dreams and does not see the seeing of dreams.”  
2B2C -2B2B -4C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. For a long time, through having readily  familiarized with 
uninterruptedly abandoning dualistic appearances, the bodhisattva who has uprooted the 
latencies of dualistic appearances, when not seeing dreams as apprehendeds and the s eeing 
of dreams as apprehenders, in a manner of apprehendeds and apprehenders, since, through 
realizing in just a single instant, “All phenomena are also of such a nature,” the suchness of 
phenomena, the absence  of duality, it is the clear complete  enlight enment in a single instant. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —that —is the  fourth clear complete  enlightenment in a single instant, 
because it is a final application  through directly realizing in just a single instant, “All 
phenomena that are apprehendeds and appreh enders are also of such a nature of being 
empty of being different substances ,” the suchness of phenomena, the absence  of duality  
between apprehendeds and apprehenders. The bodhisattva who  abides at the end of the 
continuum is able to realize that, because  he has uprooted the latencies of mistakes in 
which apprehendeds and apprehenders appear dualistically. That follows because it is a 
final [application] through having readily familiarized with uninterruptedly abandoning the mistakes of dualistic appearanc es for a long time, for example, like not 
seeing (i) the mountains, houses, and so forth of  dreams as apprehendeds that are other 
objects and (ii) the seeing of dreams as apprehenders  that are different substances —in a 
manner of apprehendeds and apprehende rs that are different substances .

Chapter 8: The Truth Body   
2B2C -3. Explaining the truth body extensively   
2B2C -3A. Making a connection by stating the relationship  
2B2C -3B. Explaining the text of the chapter  
2B2C -3C. Summarizing the chapter  
2B2C -3A. Making a co nnection by stating the relationship  
COMMENTARY 
In the second /next  instant of having thoroughly cultivated the clear complete  enlightenment 
in a single instant is the clear complete  enlightenment as the truth body, since that, further, 
by way of the divisi ons of the nature body and so forth, is of four types:  
 
EXPLANATION  
After the instantaneous application, there is a reason for explaining the truth body, because 
in the second instant of having thoroughly cultivated the application of the clear 
complete  enlightenment in a single instant with respect to all phenomena , the clear 
complete  enlightenment as the truth body  arises.  
 The subject —that  truth body, further —is of four types , since it is of four types by way 
of the divisions of the nature body and so fo rth.

2B2C -3B1A -1. Demarcati on in the time of accomplishing the result  
While that very uninterrupted path at the end of the continuity of the ten grounds is placed 
in meditative equipoise on thusness in [a manner of] water placed  in water, at the time of 
that uninterrupted path, in t he perspective of the seer that is that meditative equipoise, not 
even the slightest aspect that is an object of knowledge that is a variety181 dawns, because it 
has not exhaustively abandoned the stains that apprehend the two truths to be different entities . However, at that time, it abides in the nature of having attained all the classes of 
clear realizations that are the twenty -one sets of uncontaminated exalted wisdom without 
exception.  
 Then, in the second instant, when the liberated path is produced, d ue to dualistic 
appearances having vanished with respect to objects of knowledge that are modes, in the 
perspective of the seer that is that [liberated path], which has become [like] water placed in 
                                             
 
181 “An object of knowledge that is a variety ” refers to a conventional truth.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
528 water, all the objects of knowledge that are varieties ar e directly seen like an amalaki [fruit] 
placed on the palm of the hand. At that time, it is said that “the knowledge obscurations 
have been exhaustively abandoned, the perfect end has been actualized, the truth body has 
been actualized, and one has attaine d clear complete  enlightenment in all ways with respect 
to all phenomena.”  
 At the time of attaining the truth body , one achieves a complete enjoyment body, which 
possesses the five characteristics, the five excellent qualities, and the five certainties —a 
later similar type of that very exalted body that is adorned with the signs and 
exemplifications when one is abiding at the end of the continuity of the ten grounds. 
Through the complete enjoyment body acting as an empowering condition, [one achieves] a 
supreme emanation body that takes care of trainees by means of the twelve deeds. At that 
time, although they do not exist as a composite, underst and that some instances of a 
buddha’s emanation body are attained in a manner of having established a relationsh ip with 
the complete enjoyment body in dependence on a single set that is a composite of direct 
causes.  
 You should know from elsewhere the mode of accomplishing the form body by 
traversing the path of the Paramitayana (Perfection Vehicle) for up to three countless eons, 
and augmenting it with the path of Mantra [Vehicle] after that.  
2B2C -3B1A -2. Identifying the entity of the three exalted bodies  
(i) The factor of purity with respect to the inherent stains  distinguished by the purity with 
respect to advent itious [stains] of the form bodies and the exalted wisdom truth body, and 
(ii) the factor of purity with respect to adventitious [stains] in regard to those [the form 
bodies and the exalted wisdom truth body ] are the uncompounded bodies and are called “the  
nature bodies.” In the statement “Because of being the entity of a follower of reasoning” in the Two Truths  by Acarya Jñanagarbha, that which has been taught to be the object found 
by a reasoning consciousness  analyzing finality is that very factor of purity with respect to 
the nature.  
 Suppose one thinks, “If that very body possessing the two purities, without existing in the 
continua of sentient beings since beginningless time, were to arise only at the time of buddhahood, then it would necessarily be i mpermanent.” This is a mistake due to (i) not 
differentiating between the permanence that is renowned to the wise and [the permanence 
that is renowned to] the foolish, and (ii) not differentiating between occasional functioning 
things and occasional uncomp ounded phenomena.  
 If it were not like that, it would be necessary to accept that the disintegratedness of a 
house in the morning has existed since beginningless time, and it would be necessary to 
accept that the new disintegratedness of a house in the ev ening has existed in the morning. 
 The exalted wisdom truth body included within the twenty -one sets of uncontaminated 
exalted wisdom has been taught to be the fourth exalted body from the point of view of the 
Mantra [Vehicle]. Taking it also to be the fou rth exalted body by the victors’ son, 
Haribhadra, is merely a way of categorization; it is not that he asserts that it is not subsumed 
under the three exalted bodies. 
 
OBJECTOR: It is inappropriate for that [exalted wisdom truth body ] to be included in the  
three exalted bodies because it is neither a nature body nor either of the other two exalted 
bodies.   
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
529 RESPONSE: It follows that it would be inappropriate for it to be included in the four 
exalted bodies because, for example, the copper- colored nails of the complete enjoyment 
body are not any of the four exalted bodies that you assert. If that is said, what reply do you have?  
 OBJECTOR: The sign is not established. [The copper -colored nails not being any of the 
four exalted bodies is not established becau se] they are the complete enjoyment body.  
 
RESPONSE: In that case, what contradiction would there be in accepting that the other [the 
exalted wisdom truth body ] is the nature body?  
 OBJECTOR: It follows that [the exalted wisdom truth body ] would be an unc ompounded 
phenomenon.  
 RESPONSE: It follows that the other, [the copper- colored nails], would also have the 
complete thirty -two excellent signs and eighty excellent exemplifications. If you accept that, 
then, since you would accept the other, they are similar in all ways. 
 
OBJECTOR: [The copper -colored nails] are only one part of the complete enjoyment body; 
they are not an actual complete enjoyment body since [the complete enjoyment body] is 
imputed upon the composite of all the signs and exemplifications without exception.  
 RESPONSE: Also [the exalted wisdom truth body ] is a part of the nature body. It is not an 
actual [nature body] because [the nature body] is imputed upon the composite possessing the two purities in regard to the exalted wisdom truth bod y.  
 Therefore, having seen that the exalted wisdom truth body  is together with an exceptional 
purpose, [Haribhadra] presented it as an exalted body and so forth. The complete enjoyment 
body has already been explained. The supreme emanation body is the exalted wisdom truth 
body  itself that (i) appears to some ordinary beings as a form body adorned with the signs 
and exemplifications, and (ii) shows the manner of attaining clear complete  enlightenment 
at the foot of the bodhi tree through the stages of desce nding from the abode of the Tushita 
and so forth.  
2B2C -3B1A -3. Eliminating wrong conceptions regarding that  
Asserting that the nature body of a buddha is not a fully qualified buddha is a negativity 
greater than other negativities because it deprecates t he exalted body possessing the two 
purities. If that were the case, it follows that it would not even be a mere excellent quality, because it would neither be a final excellent quality nor a temporal excellent quality that is 
not that. If [you were to say that] the former [part of the] sign [i.e. that it is not a final 
excellent quality] is not established, then your position that it is not a buddha would 
degenerate. If you accept that [it is not even a mere excellent quality ], if it were  not the 
excell ent quality of final abandonment, then it would be similar in also not being the 
excellent quality of final realization.  
 OBJECTOR: The two form bodies are mere other- appearances of the trainees; they do not 
exist with respect to the self- appearance of bud dhas and they are not included within the 
continua of buddhas themselves.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
530  
RESPONSE: Saying that is a very grave misdeed, because it follows that a form body of a 
buddha does not exist at all because it is not directly seen by an omniscient knower. If [you  
were to say that this sign] is not established, it would contradict [your opinion above that form bodies] do not exist with respect to the self- appearance of buddhas.  
 
OBJECTOR: The buddhas are always in the meditative equipoise on thusness; that is the 
meaning of [the form bodies] not being established in the perspective of that seer.  
 RESPONSE: It has already been explained previously that also that is incorrect.  
 Do you assert that the two form bodies are not included within the continuum of any 
person , or that they are included within the continuum of a sentient being? In the first case, 
it follows that it would be impossible for bodhisattva aryas to attain the final result through 
having familiarized with performing generosity, guarding  ethics , and so  forth in their 
continua, because it is impossible for the final result of familiarizing with the accumulation 
of merit to be included in the continuum of any person. 
 
OBJECTOR: In that case, if, in dependence upon having familiarized with the practices of 
generosity and so forth that are included in one’s own continuum until the end of the 
continuity of the ten grounds, one were to become a buddha, then there would only be the 
nature of total purity, thusness, due to the continuity of cognition having been  severed.  
 
RESPONSE: In that case, you would not be able to demonstrate even the slightest damage 
to the [assertion] by the Ayatas that, although cognition occurs until one is about to die, 
upon death it becomes non -existent. Therefore, do not propound tha t the very system of the 
Charvakas is the thought of the Muni! Know that the assertion that there is no awareness in an arya’s meditative equipoise is also refuted through this. 
 
OBJECTOR: The two form bodies are merely included in the continuum of a senti ent being.  
 RESPONSE: Suppose the person Devadatta’s cultivation of the intention to perform 
generosity, the intention to abandon [misdeeds], and the like has been excellently 
culminated. Asserting that Yajjadatta, a person of a different continuum from h im, (i) has 
culminated long life such that henceforth he does not display the manner of death in the 
perspective of appearance to trainees, (ii) has culminated great resources, and so forth, is to 
be understood as the culmination of erroneous speech propou nding actions and results, in 
which actions done are wasted and actions not done are met with.   Someone’s propounding that there is not even the slightest compounded excellent quality 
included within the continuum of a buddha is to be known as the erroneo us system of others 
who assert the extinction of awareness and the extinction of phenomena. Know that these [points] also refute the assertion that the two form bodies of a buddha are not fully qualified 
buddhas.  
 As a proof for that, someone says that the  Bhagavan taught [in the Vajra Cutter Sutra 
(Vajracchedika)] that, “Some see me as form” and so forth. Taking that quotation as a proof 
is a case of an insincere mind. Did you, who propounded in that way, not assert that they are 
not buddhas? Therefore, th rough taking the exalted wisdom of a buddha as the basis of 
designation, it is presented as the form bodies; one should understand that there is not even 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
531 the slightest body that is an agglomeration of very subtle particles that are included within 
matter.

2B2C -3B1B -1B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.1 The nature body of the Subduer  
Has attained th e uncontaminated qualities,  
Is pure in all ways, and  
Bears the characteristics of the nature of those.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the nature body of the Subduer —is distinguished by three attributes, 
because it is an exalted body that (i) has attained the t wenty -one sets of uncontaminated 
qualities, the close placements of mindfulness and so forth: the attribute of possession; and 
possesses the two: (ii) purity in all ways  in being free from the two obscurations along with 
their latencies: the attribute of s eparation , and (iii) purity in bearing the characteristics of  
being empty of true existence, the nature of those  uncontaminated exalted wisdoms: the 
attribute of entity .   
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, the uncontaminated qualities are like dreams. They do not possess 
the thing, are empty of the characteristic of bearing the nature of non -thing, and are 
completely pure in all ways. This unique characteristic, that which is the nature of all of 
those, the absence of characteristic, is to be understood as the tath agata, foe -destroyer, 
perfectly complete buddha.”

532 2B2C -3B1B -1C1. Actual  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. The nature body of the Muni Buddha Bhagavan: (1) uncontaminated 
through the entitiness of supramundane dharmadhatu, the nature of exalted wisdom, the 
close placements of mindfulness and so forth; (2) since stains are a dventitious, pure in all 
ways; and (3) the entitiness of the self of those which are the characteristic of being devoid of nature, the entitiness of absence  of production is this. Since it is an object that is not 
contrived, it is attained through a supram undane path; since it is not created, it is attained 
through the realization of all phenomena by the illusion -like consciousness. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the nature body of the Muni Buddha Bhagavan —is distinguished by three 
attributes, because of the foll owing. It is distinguished by (1) the attribute of possession: 
being uncontaminated through the entitiness of supramundane dharmadhatu, the 
nature of uncontaminated exalted wisdom, the close placements of mindfulness and so 
forth . It is [distinguished by] (2) the attribute of separation: being  pure and devoid in all 
ways  in being free from the two obscurations along with their latencies; the obscurations are 
suitable to be utterly extinguished by antidotes, since stains are adventitious . (3) The 
attribute o f entity: the entitiness of absence  of production ultimately is this, because it is 
the entitiness of the nature that is the mode of abidance  of those which are the 
uncontaminated exalted wisdoms that are of the characteristic of being devoid of nature  
ultimately .  
 It is not to be newly produced by causes, since it is an object that is not contrived  by 
causes and conditions . It is not attained by any method, a mere mundane path and so forth, 
because  it is attained through a supramundane path; since it is n ot a mere mundane path 
and [is not] created  by conditions, it is attained through (i) being  ornamented by extensive 
method  and (ii) the realization of all phenomena as being free from elaborations by the 
illusion -like consciousness that directly realizes e mptiness . 
2B2C -3B1B -1C2. The purpose of positing the three exalted bodies that are property -possessors as different 
from the dharmata body  
COMMENTARY 
The remaining three exalted bodies, the entities of dharmata ultimately, distinguished by 
beliefs in accordance with how they appear as real conventionalities, because “They are 
presented as the objects of utilization of buddhas, bodhisattvas, hearers, and  so forth” has 
been indicated. Because it occurs in the system, “Why? Voidness is asserted as merely not 
different from that which is devoid.” Although not different from that, those are presented 
as different.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the remaining thre e exalted bodies  that are property -possessors—are not 
different entities from the nature body, because they are exalted bodies that are of the entities of dharmata , emptiness of true existence, ultimately. Although not different in 
entity from that, those  three  are presented as different, because, although not different 
from that in entity, they are posited and  distinguished by different beliefs in accordance 
with  how they appear  dissimilarly  as real conventionalities.  
 There is a purpose for positing them as three different [exalted bodies], because “They 
are presented differently as the direct objects of utilization of only buddhas  mutually 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
533 among themselves , and as the objects of utilization of the bodhisattvas, hearers, and so 
forth” has been indicated.  
 They are not different entities from the nature body, because it occurs in the system  of 
the great trailblazers , “That called ‘emptiness’ does not exist whatsoever [as being] other 
from consciousness. Why? Voidness of true existence is asserted as merely not a 
different entity from the basis of emptiness, that which is devoid  of true existence .” Those 
who assert that the two form bodies are not able to perform functions in the way they appear 
[and] are unreal conventionalities are simply separated from the  fortune of seeing these 
stainless commentaries.

So the root text is said. (1) From the close placements of mindfulness and so forth until the 
eight branches of aryas’ paths: the harmonies with enlightenment; (2) like the above, love 
and so forth: the Brahma  abodes, the immeasurables; (3) by the internal discriminating as 
having form and not having form, viewing external forms: two; liberation of the attractive manifested by the body, having completed, abiding: one; the sources Space, Consciousness, Nothingne ss, and Without Discrimination Not Without Discrimination: four; and ceasing 
discrimination and feeling: one; in that way the eight liberations;   
EXPLANATION  
(1) From the close placements of mindfulness and so forth until  the eight branches of 
aryas’ paths: the harmonies with enlightenment, taken as one set; (2) like the above, 
love and so forth: the Brahma abodes, the  four immeasurables, taken as the second set; 
(3) the third set is: (i -ii) by the internal , the yogi, being placed in the discrimination  of 
himself as having form and being placed in the discrimination  of the mere aggregates of 
the four names without having form, viewing external forms  in the aspect of 
unattractiveness : two; (iii) imagining eventually that all appearances of forms are one taste 
in being attractive, liberation of the attractive manifested by the body, having 
completed, abiding: one ; (with respect to the first two [liberations], although [the yogi] has 
previously separated from attachment to the forms of the desire realm, sin ce he has not 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
535 attained mastery over them as he desires, he observes them again for the sake of attaining it; 
when the continuum is discouraged due to having meditated on unattractiveness through the 
first two, the third liberation is (a) for the sake of up lifting it, and (b) for the sake of 
examining whether or not the first two liberations have been accomplished; if adherence is not produced even though one pays attention to the attractive, then the first two liberations 
have been accomplished);  (iv-vii) t he four liberations of the formless : [Infinite] Space  and 
so forth are for the sake of being liberated from the obscurations of relishing the taste and [for the sake of] keeping them distant ; and (viii) for the sake of being liberated from the 
obscurations  of coarse discrimination and feeling, ceasing discrimination and feeling: 
one; in that way , the eight liberations.   
 It has been explained in [Asanga's]  Compendium of Knowledge 
(Abhidharmasamuchchaya)  that also the first two liberations are supported on the final 
fourth concentration. However, [Vasubandhu’s] Treasury of Knowledge  (Abhidharmakosa) 
explains that “The first two of the eight liberations, unattractivenesses, exist in two 
concentrations” [8.32ab] . It is not that it is inappropriate for someone who has asserted the 
former to assert the latter.

The fifth set, the totalities:

A. Actual meaning  
COMMENTARY 
(5)(a) earth, (b) water, (c) fire, (d) wind, (e) blue, (f) yellow, (g) red, (h) white, (i) space, and (j) consciousness: the ten types of totalities;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(5) The entity is that which is concomitant with a meditative stabilization and wisdom that 
observes the ten phenomena. The divisions are the ten types of totalities observing (a–d) 
the four elements, earth  and so forth, (e–h) the four that have arisen from the elements, 
blue and so forth, (i) [Infinite] Space , and (j) [Infinite] Consciousness . With respect to the 
mind support,  the first eight are supported on the fourth concentration, and the last two on 
[Infinite] Space and [Infinite] Consciousness.  
 With respect to the manner of training,  if one is preceded by familiarization in which one 
has trained in the past, then, by merely seeing the special support of observation, one will 
attain a meditative stabilization of totality. At the time of generating it newly, fill a vessel 
with clear water free from impurities. At a favorable place —at the fo ot of a tree, at a 
guesthouse, and so forth —pile up logs of wood that have oil and sap. Encircle it with a wall 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
536 and in it make a hole of diameter measuring one finger span182 and four fingerbreadths. 
Having lit the fire, there is the wood below, the smoke ab ove, and the fire in the middle. In 
any of the intermediate directions, make a window or hole in the wall that can be contacted 
by the wind. Take as the support of observation the colors blue, yellow, red, white, and so 
forth.  
 Place a butter lamp in a va se. Having made a hole in it that is not too big, through its 
light, an orb of illumination that strikes the surface of a wall will remain for a long time. At 
a location where the wall is covered by either a piece of matted fabric or woolen cloth, make 
a hole of diameter measuring one finger span and four fingerbreadths, and take this as the 
object of seeing.  Through taking these as the supports of observation, in dependence on 
a concentration, one observes the mere individual names, “Earth, earth, water, w ater,” and 
familiarizes with them again and again.  
 Having familiarized with them in that way, when one is able to emanate as one desires to 
pervade all without exception with earth, water, and so forth, and is able to gather them back and so forth, then [ the totalities] have been accomplished. However, if they appear in 
the aspect of water with bubbles, the fire dying out, falling, and the like, and one is not able 
to emanate as one desires, then [the totalities] have not been accomplished.  
 The sign of the totality of earth has not been explained because [Haribhadra] had thought 
that it was easy to understand; it is not that it does not exist. The appearance of the earth 
having patches of salt and so forth is a sign that [the totality of earth] has not bee n 
accomplished. Being able to emanate [earth] as one desires —shiny, level, and the like —is a 
sign that [the totality of earth] has been accomplished. 
 The function of meditating in that way is that,
 through the eight types of observation with 
respect to th at having form, one is able to accomplish emanation and the magical emanation 
of transformation. In dependence on the totality of space, one travels in space. In 
dependence on the totality of consciousness, one accomplishes the excellent qualities: abandon ment of the afflictions, the four individual correct knowledges, and so forth.  
 From among the twelve sources, these totalities are posited only with respect to four of 
them: the four colors with respect to the form source, the four elements with respect to the 
source of object of touch, space with respect to the phenomena source, and consciousness 
with respect to the mental source.  
 With respect to the meaning of the name “totality,” “totality” has the significance of that 
acting to pervade and spread to  all without exception. The presentation of the generality of 
these will be explained; other modes are just to be understood.  
 
QUALM: Why is anything other than these not posited as a totality?  
RESPONSE: It is because a totality is that which acts to perv ade [all]; anything other than 
these is not able to pervade [all]. That in which the class of it is present can be pervaded by 
it. (i) Because the class of the five sense powers is not present in the forms of the 
environment, (ii) because the class of odors and tastes is not present in the forms of the form 
realm, and (iii) because sounds do not have a continuity of similar class, thereby they cannot 
be emanated to pervade [all] —they are not posited as totalities.  
                                             
 
182 A finger span is the distance from the tip of the thumb to the tip of the middle finger . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
537 B. Differences among the liberations, the m asteries, and the totalities  
The causes that produce the liberations, the masteries, and the totalities are the respective 
previous [sources]. The methods that act to purify are the respective later [sources]. 
Therefore, the sources that are the masteries  and the totalities are to be known as special 
paths that act to purify the liberations.  
 
QUALM: If one meditates on the sources that are the masteries for the sake of training in 
the observed object s of the liberations, why is there an explanation of a m astery that trains 
in the liberation of having form, but no explanation of [that of] the liberation of the 
formless?  
  
RESPONSE: It is because it is difficult to master form. That is because when one masters it, 
one will also attain the power to master the  formless. Through the liberations, one imagines 
other aspects of emanated forms: small natural forms and so forth. Through the masteries, by means of attaining power, one masters the previous [forms].  
 In general, the masteries are of five types:  
(i) the great mastering the small;  
(ii) the powerful mastering the weak;  
(iii) mastery through making that which was previously visible invisible;  
(iv) the mastery through repudiation, as in the abandonment of the afflictions; and  
(v) the mastery of co ntrol, as in a lord who controls his servant.  
Here, [those indicated are] the masteries included within the third and the fifth.   
 The sixth set, [the masteries]:  
COMMENTARY  
(6)(a) through the internal discriminating as form and formless, individually v iewing 
external forms in the aspects of great and small, and having mastered those, knowing: four, and (b) through only the internal discriminating as formless, having mastered blue, yellow, 
red, and white, seeing: four types; in that way, the eight types of sources that are masteries;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(6)(a) through the internal , the yogi, discriminating himself as form and discriminating 
himself as formless, individually with respect to each, viewing external forms in the 
aspects of great and small, as beaut iful and not beautiful, and as good and bad, and 
having mastered those  forms , knowing them through calm abiding and seeing them 
through special insight : four  masteries of shapes , and (b) through only the internal , [the 
yogi,]  discriminating [himself] as fo rmless, having mastered  the colors, blue, yellow, 
red, and white, knowing and seeing: four types. The first four [masteries] arise from 
causes that are the first two liberations, while the latter four arise from causes that are the 
liberation of attractive ness; in that way, [those are] the  eight types of sources that are 
masteries.  
 Regarding the latter four, they are as taught in sutra: “The internal discriminating 
[himself] as formless views external forms as blue, as blue color, as displayed like they a re 
blue, and as emitting blue light. For example, he views a flower of the flax plant or the fine 
cloth of Varanasi as blue, as blue color, as displayed as if it is blue, and as emitting blue 
light. Likewise, the internal discriminating [himself] as formle ss views external forms as 
blue, blue color, displayed like they are blue, and as emitting blue light. Having mastered 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
538 those forms, he knows them. Having mastered them, he sees them. Discriminating in that 
way is the fifth source that is a mastery.”   
 Similarly, apply [the above] to the three other [colors] through connecting the karnikara 
flower to yellow cloth, the vandujipaka flower to red [cloth], and the planet Venus to white 
[cloth].  
 With respect to that, blue is a brief indication; blue color is in nate; that displayed like it is 
blue is manufactured; regarding emitting blue light, there is clear light with respect to both 
[the innate and the manufactured]. A flower is an example of the innate, while a cloth is an 
example of the manufactured.  
  
The seventh, the meditative stabilization without afflictions:  
COMMENTARY  
(7) that which is “the meditative stabilization in which the continuity of the foes, the afflictions, included in the continua of others is uprooted”: without afflictions;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(7) When hearer foe -destroyers go to a town, they merely do not act as an observed object 
condition for the afflictions (related to the desire realm in the continua of others) that are to 
be abandoned by the path of meditation. That which is “the meditative stabilization in 
which, through the force of the tathagatas’ meditative stabilization without afflictions, not 
only do they merely not act as the observed object condition for the production of afflictions, the continuity of the foes, the afflictions, included in the continua of other  
sentient beings  is uprooted” is  the meditative stabilization without afflictions .  
 
The eighth:  
COMMENTARY 
(8) through perfectly separating from all signs, destroying grasping, and accomplishing t he 
prayers that eliminate doubts, engagement during the occasions as long as samsara exists 
and until liberation: the exalted knower of wishes;   
 
EXPLANATION  
(8) Through (i) spontaneously enacting others ’ welfare by means of perfectly separating 
from and pacifying all signs of exertion that motivates correct actions of body and speech , 
(ii) destroying grasping to all objects of knowledge as truly existent , and (iii) 
accomplishing the prayers that eliminate others’ doubts  by means of the exalted knower 
without obstructions , engagement of the exalted knower without grasping and obstructions 
during the occasions as long as samsara exists and until the attainment of liberation  by 
all sentient beings is  the exalted knower of wishes .

wisdom , due to knowing all objects of knowledge without obstructions , and (j) power over

2B2C -3B1B -2A2B. The assertion that, although two dissimilar exalted bodies have been individually shown, 
there is not the fault of not having engaged the meaning of the words  
2B2C -3B1B -2A2A. Describing the system that asserts the earlier and later stanzas above as the brief indication 
and extensive explanation that indicate the nature body  
COMMENTARY 
The nature body of the Subduer  
Has attained the uncontaminated qualities , 
Is pure in all ways, and  
Bears the characteristics of the nature of those.  
 
Due to others having asserted only the supramundane uncontaminated qualities in 
accordance with a literal reading of the above, the nature body bears the characteristic of 
that w hich is not being produced, the nature of those. It has been explained that those 
themselves also are the dharmata body, but having made non -manifest the condition of 
entity (or nature), the “truth body” has been indicated. If it is asked, “What are those 
uncontaminated qualities, such that the truth body bears the characteristic of the natures of 
them?” the stanzas “The harmonies with enlightenment; the immeasurable [8.2a]” and so 
forth engage them.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Due to others —Arya Vimuktisena and so fort h—having asserted that only the 
supramundane uncontaminated qualities included within compounded phenomena have been explicitly indicated by “the uncontaminated qualities” [in 8.1b]  in accordance with a 
literal reading of the first stanza “The nature body of the Subduer…,” [8.1], the nature 
body bears the characteristic of that which is the nature of not being produced  
ultimately , the nature of those  exalted wisdoms .  
 
OBJECTION: In the context of the extensive explanation it would be reasonable to say “the  
dharmata body (dharmatakaya),” but instead ‘Are called the “truth body (dharmakaya)”’ 
occurs [in 8.6d]. Therefore, although the earlier stanza [8.1] has been asserted to indicate the 
nature body, that the later stanzas [8.2 –8.6] indicate the exalted wisdo m truth body  as an 
exalted body that is other than the nature body is an assertion of Venerable [Maitreya]; it is incorrect to take the earlier stanza and the later stanzas [respectively] as the brief indication 
and the extensive explanation.  
 RESPONSE: In  the context of completing the conclusion, it has been explained that it 
would have been reasonable that those dharmatas of the twenty -one uncontaminated exalted 
wisdoms themselves also ‘Are called the “dharmata body” (dharmatakaya)’ to occur, but 
having a bbreviated the words from the perspective of metered stanzas, the “truth body” 
(dharmakaya) was said. Having erased, that is, made non- manifest “ta,” the condition of 
entity (or nature) in “dharma ta
kaya,” ‘Are called the “truth body  (dharmakaya)” ’ [8.6d]  
has been indicated.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
543 QUALM: Are those  twenty -one sets of uncontaminated exalted wisdoms asserted as the 
dharmata body? Otherwise, they would not be relat ed [to each  other] and the passages that 
assert four exalted bodies would be established.  
 
RESPONSE:  There is no fault  [that the twenty -one sets of uncontaminated exalted wisdoms 
and the dharmata body would not be relat ed to each  other and the passages that assert four 
exalted bodies would be established ], [because] since they [the twenty -one sets of 
uncontaminated exalted wisdoms ] are excellent qualities of the nature body183, they are to 
be included in that. There is also no fault that the earlier and the later are not related, 
[because] the truth body bears the characteristic of the natures of the uncontaminated 
minds and mental factors. Hence, if it is asked, “What are those  uncontaminated 
qualities?”  the stanzas “The harmonies with enlightenment; the immeasurable [8.2a]” 
and so forth engage  in explaining them .  
 
QUALM: Do those (who do not assert the  earlier stanza and the later stanzas as the brief 
indication and the extensive explanation) not accept the words  “the uncontaminated 
qualities” [in 8.1b]  in accordance with a literal reading [ of the first stanza ]?  
 RESPONSE: They do not  [accept the words  “the uncontaminated qualities”  in accordance 
with a literal reading of the first stanza  to refer to only the supramundane uncontaminated 
qualities included within  compounded phenomena ], because they explain “uncontaminated” 
by way of the entitiness of dha rmadhatu; otherwise, they would definitely have to accept 
[the earlier stanza and the later stanzas] as the brief indication and the extensive 
explanation. 
2B2C -3B1B -2A2B. The assertion that, although two dissimilar exalted bodies have been individually sh own, 
there is not the fault of not having engaged the meaning of the words  
COMMENTARY 
OTHERS: According to those, by means of generating the appearances, the special conventional welfare of the yogi, having become [uncontaminated] due to the states having 
transformed, enact the deeds of welfare, teaching the Dharma and so forth, non -dual minds 
and mental factors, how are those that must surely be asserted included?  
 
EXPLANATION  
OTHERS  WHO ASSERT FOUR EXALTED BODIES : According to those  who do not 
assert those uncontaminated exalted wisdoms to be other than the three exalted bodies, the subject —those exalted wisdoms possessing the three attributes  that must surely be 
asserted on the buddha ground—how are  they included  within the buddha ground ? It 
follows that it is incorrect that they are included, because they are not included within any 
of the three other exalted bodies. You accepted the pervasion.   
 [Those exalted wisdoms] possess the three attributes, because they  (1) enact the deeds of 
welfare, teaching the Dharma, radiating light rays, and so forth, by means of generating 
                                             
 
183 “Nature body” and “dharmata body” seem to be have been used interchangeably here. While such an 
usage would have been justified in the Consequence School, it is problematic in the Autonomy School, since 
the dharmata body in the Autonomy School is only the nature body that is the factor of purity with respect to 
inherent stains, and does not include the nature body that is the factor of purity with respect to adventitious 
stains.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
544 the appearances of the two form bodies that are their results , the special conventional 
welfare of the yogi : the attribute of the function, and  because they  are distinguished by (2) 
having become  uncontaminated due to the contaminated states having transformed : the 
attribute of abandonment, and (3) minds and mental factors in which apprehendeds and 
apprehenders are non- dual : the attribute of enti ty. 
 
OBJECTION: There is no fault that [those uncontaminated exalted wisdoms] are not 
included within any of the three exalted bodies, because, since a property -possessor 
(dharmin) is included within the dharmata, they are included within the nature body.  
RESPONSE: It follows that it is also incorrect to indicate the complete enjoyment body and 
the emanation body as different bodies, because the reasoning is similar also for them. If you are saying that I must definitely assert the divisions of the three e xalted bodies since 
three exalted bodies have been taught in the scriptures and [Maitreya’s] Mahayana Treatise on the Sublime Continuum  (Mahayanottaratantrasastra ) and so forth, then it follows that it 
is reasonable to also posit the exalted wisdom truth b ody as another division of the exalted 
bodies, just like the complete enjoyment body and emanation body, because the bodies have 
been taught also as four in some scriptures and valid commentaries.

interceding stanza:
