546 2B2C -3B1B -2B1B. Root text  
ROOT TEX T 
8.7 The hearers’ without afflictions  
Abandons the afflictions of people who look;  
The victors’ without afflictions cuts the continuity  
Of the afflictions of those in towns and so forth. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The buddhas’ meditative stabilization without afflict ions is greatly superior to the hearers’ 
meditative stabilization without afflictions, because the  hearers’ meditative stabilization 
without afflictions simply abandons acting as the observed object condition of the 
afflictions of people who look  at them i n towns and so forth; whereas the victors’ 
meditative stabilization without afflictions cuts from the root the continuity of the 
afflictions of those  people and so forth who observe them when they go to  towns , cities,  
and so forth.  
 Before hearers and solitary realizers go on alms round and so forth in towns, having 
investigated whether or not afflictions will be produced in others who observe them, if they 
know that [afflictions] will be produced, then they will absorb into the medit ative 
stabilization without afflictions in dependence on a final concentration. However, the tathagatas [absorb into the meditative stabilization without afflictions] by merely relying 
upon their wish.  
2B2C -3B1B -2B1C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root te xt is said. The meditative stabilization without afflictions of the hearers and so 
forth, abandons the production of afflictions in people: “It would be inappropriate if 
afflictions will be produced in some through seeing me.” The tathagatas’ meditative 
stabilization without afflictions uproots the continuity of afflictions in all beings in towns 
and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The meditative stabilization without afflictions of the hearers and so forth, abandons 
the production of afflictions in people: “It woul d be inappropriate if afflictions will be 
produced in some through seeing me.” The tathagatas’ meditative stabilization 
without afflictions uproots the continuity of afflictions in all beings in towns and so 
forth. It is as in the [explanation of the] root  text.

547 EXPLANATION  
Because the tathagatas’ exalted knower of wishes has been shown as distinguished 
from the knower of wishes of the hearers an d so forth, an interceding stanza:  
2B2C -3B1B -2B2B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.8 The buddhas’ exalted knower of wishes is asserted  
To be spontaneous, to have abandoned grasping,  
To be unobstructed, to abide permanently, and  
To answer all questions. 
 
EXPLANATION  
The buddhas’ exalted knower of wishes  is greatly superior to the hearers’ knower of 
wishes, because that of the buddhas engages naturally, is non -erroneous, pervades all 
objects of knowledge, does not discontinue, and acts to eliminate all doubts, whereas  the 
hearers’ knower of wishes is greatly inferior to that. 
2B2C -3B1B -2B2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The tathagatas’ exalted knower of wishes is asserted to: (1) engage naturally without the signs; (2) be separated from grasping to forms and so forth, through 
not adhering to things; (3) be unobstructed with respect to all objects  of knowledge, since 
afflictive and knowledge obscurations along with their latencies have been abandoned; (4) abide permanently, because of abiding as long as samsara exists; and (5) answer questions, 
due to having acquired the individual correct knowledg es. That of the hearers and so forth, 
due to being the reverse, is not like that.  
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the tathagatas’ exalted knower of wishes —is greatly superior to the 
hearers’ knower of wishes, because it is asserted to: (1) engage naturally without the 
signs  of exertion ; (2) be separated from grasping to forms and so forth, through not 
adhering to things  as truly existent ; (3) be unobstructed exalted wisdom with respect to 
all objects of knowledge, since the two  obscurations along with their latencies have 
been abandoned;  (4) abide permanently, because of the action of the meditative 
stabilization abiding as long as samsara exists;  and (5) answer  all questions, due to 
having acquired the culmination of the four individual correct knowledges.   
 The subject —the exalted knower of wishes  of the hearers and so forth —is not even 
similar in part to a buddha’s exalted knower of wishes like that, due to being an exalted 
knower of wishes that is  with exertion and so forth , the reverse  of that distinguis hed by the 
five attributes .

“Even when residing permanently i n the truth body with the entitiness of great exalted 
compassion, why does he not enact welfare permanently?” In order to reply to the aforesaid, 
an interceding stanza:  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the Subduer Buddha Bhagavan —why does he not enact the welfare of all 
sentient beings permanently by means of directly displaying the appearances of exalted 
bodies ? He should. It follows that it is incorrect for him not to enact the welfare [even] 
occasionally by means of the exalted bodies directly appearing to certain sentient beings,  
because he  resides permanently  in the truth body with the entitiness of great exalted 
compassion that wishes all sentient beings to be free from suffering as long as cyclic 
existence exists. In order to reply to the aforesaid, an interceding stanza:  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B. Reply  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1. Reliance on the attribute of the accumulations in sentient beings’ own continua  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -2. Explanation through an analogy that the exalted body does not directly appear if the 
conditions i n sentient beings’ own continua are not complete  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1. Reliance on the attribute of the accumulations in sentient beings’ own continua  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1A. Root text  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1B. Commentary  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1A. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.9 Through the thorough ripening of the causes,  
It appears as this and that  
Beneficial activity  
For anyone, anywhere, at any time. 
 
EXPLANATION  
It follows that the enactment of sentient beings’ welfare through a buddha’s exalted body 
directly appearing to them relies on the attribute of they themselves having gathered the 
accumulations, because in dependence on the thorough ripening of the roots of virtue —
the causes  for hearing the Dharma directly from a buddha, the cultivation of faith through 
having obser ved buddhas in the past and so forth —the exalted body appears as this and 
that benefit through performing the activity of directly teaching the Dharma for any  such 
trainees , anywhere, at any time, and performs activities; and it does not appear to those 
who have not gathered the accumulations . 
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -1B. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. The seeds of the roots of virtue generated in the past through having 
met the spiritual guide and so forth, the causes of having observed  the buddhas and so forth, 
having become fully developed, at that time when, if the Dharma is taught to whichever 
sentient beings at whatever time, it will be agreeable for a long time. Since they have 
accomplished  past prayers in order to enact the welfare of those, s ince the bhagavans 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
549 perform activities of welfare appropriately in accordance with appearing  at this and that, 
although abiding nearby like the wish- granting gem at all times, due to the causes being 
incomplete since it is generated through the shortcomings  of their karma, the results do not 
appear to ripen. Thinking the aforesaid is the intention.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Hearing the Dharma directly from a buddha relies on the two special outer and inner 
conditions, (i) because it must arise in dependence upon the inne r condition: the seeds of 
the roots of virtue of faith and so forth generated in the past through having met  the 
Mahayana spiritual guide and so forth in the past , the  causes that are generated through 
having observed the buddhas and so forth, having becom e fully developed powerful 
potentials , and (ii) because of the outer condition: performing activities of directly teaching 
the Dharma at that time when, if the Dharma is taught to whichever  sentient beings at 
whatever time, it will be agreeable and benefic ial temporally and for a long time  through 
the attainment of liberation . 
 Although the subduer buddha bhagavans’ capacity to enact others’ welfare abides 
nearby like the wish -granting gem at all times, the results  of that do not appear to  
directly ripen  certain sentient beings. It is not due to the capacity of the buddhas’ exalted 
compassion having degenerated; rather it is due to the causes for the buddhas directly appearing  being incomplete, since it is generated through the shortcomings of the  
karma  of sentient beings themselves who especially committed negativities . They abide  
like the wish -granting gem at all times, since the bhagavans  perform activities of welfare 
in accordance with appearing at this and that  place and time wherever and whenever 
there are trainees who have gathered the accumulations. That follows , since they have 
accomplished past prayers in order to directly enact the welfare of those  trainees .

8.10 Just as, even though the king of gods might have sent down rain,  
An unsuitable seed does not grow,  
Due to lacking fortune, they do not experience the excellences  Even though the buddhas arise.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Even though the buddhas actually arise  in the world, there is a reason for certain sentient 
beings  not directly meeting them, because, due to lacking the fortune to directly see the 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
550 buddhas, they do not experience the excellences  of hearing the excellent Dharma and so 
forth, just as , for example, even though the king of gods might have sent down rain, with 
respect to that which is unsuitable as a seed due to having been burnt by fire and so forth, a 
green sprout does not grow.  
2B2C -3B1B -2C1B -2C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. For example, even though the king of gods might have sent d own 
rain, sesame seeds and so forth that have become unsuitable through the entity of rot and so 
forth do not grow. Similarly, even though the buddhas who are skilled in fully completing 
all hopes in the mind arise, since lacking fortune, they will not att ain the excellences of 
hearing the excellent Dharma and so forth.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Even though the buddhas who are skilled in fully completing all temporal and ultimate 
hopes in the mind arise, certain sentient beings will not attain the excellences of hearing 
the excellent Dharma and so forth, since certain [sentient beings]  lack the fortune  for 
that. For example, even though the king of gods might have sent down rain, sesame 
seeds and so forth that  have become  unsuita ble through the entity of rot and so forth 
do not grow.

2B2C -3B1B -2C2A. Question 
COMMENTARY 
QUALM: The truth body with the nat ure of exalted wisdom exists in the supports that are 
the continua of definite individual yogis and arises in each instant. Why is it described as 
“pervasive” and also “permanent?”  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: The subject —the truth body with the nature of uncontam inated exalted 
wisdom  included within the compounded —why is it described as “pervasive?”  It follows 
that it is unreasonable, because it  exists only in the supports that are the continua of 
definite individual yogis who abide in Akanistha  and so forth. And  because it is produced 
and arises in each instant , why is it also described as  “permanent?”

551 2B2C -3B1B -2C2B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.11 Because the activities in that way are extensive, 
The Buddha is definitely described as “ pervasive;”   
Because that itself is without extinction,  
He is also described as “permanent.”   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the Buddha —there is no contradiction that he  is definitely described as 
“pervasive ,” because the activities in that way explained above  are extensive , due to 
engaging all sentient beings ; in consideration of the continuity,  he is also described as 
“permanent,” because  that entity of him itself is without extinction  even though it enacts 
the welfare of sentient beings and resides as long as  cyclic existence exists . 
2B2C -3B1B -2C2B -3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Because of his extensive performance of activities by means of 
appearing at all through the mode that has been explained in that way and because the 
Bhagavan does not have extinction even though he resides continually as long as cyclic 
existence exists, respectively the Buddha is clearly described as “pervasive” and 
“permanent.”  
 
EXPLANATION  
There is a reason for respectively describing the Buddha as “pervasive,” because of his 
extensive performance of activities of others’ welfare by means of the exalted body  
directly appearing at all opportune times to directly subdue the trainees  through the mode 
that has been explained in the context of “Through the thorough ripe ning of the causes” 
[8.9a] in that way; in consideration of  the continuity, he is clearly  described as 
“permanent,” because the Bhagavan does not have extinction in continuity even though 
he resides continually as long as cyclic existence exists.

feet that abide very [evenly] similar to the tortoise;

the possession of these results.

time of a learner in the past. Also apply in that way with respect to the root text.

the past.

EXPLANATION  
QUESTION: What are the causes of the signs?  
RESPONSE: After that, through interceding stanzas: 
2B2C -3B1B -3B1C -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.19 Seeing gurus off and so forth;  
Firm perfect adoption;  
Relying on gathering; giving  
Sublime thing s; liberating   
 
8.20 That to be killed; perfectly adopting  
Virtue; advancing it and so forth : 
The characteristics of the causes that act to accomplish them  
Are as they have been explained in the sutras. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Through having familiarized with those causes of the signs  explained above, one will 
definitely accomplish the resultant signs, because one will definitely accomplish the resultant signs through having familiarized with those  as they have been explained in the 
sutras  that are the Mothers: (i) the seven explicitly  indicated here —seeing gurus off and so 
forth; firm perfect adoption and so forth —and (ii) the twenty -five included in “ and so 
forth ” [in 8.20b]. 
2B2C -3B1B -3B1C -3. Commentary 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. Since these two stanzas have already been commentated upon in the explanation of the signs above, I shall not write on them again.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The reason for not explaining them extensively here is as follows. The subject —these two 
stanzas —I shall not write extensively on them again here, because  I shall not write on 
them  here since they have already been commentated upon in the explanation of the 
entity of the signs above.  They have been explained there since the explanation is simple 
and easy to realize by others due to the causes and results ha ving been correlated.

EXPLANATION  
Join “ The Subduer’s ” to all below, and join also the last line [“ Asserted to be the 
Buddha’s excellent exemplifications ”] to the end of all the above. The attri butes of the 
signs of the exalted body that appear externally to others cause the internal excellent qualities that will be explained to be understood. Due to causing understanding in others’ 
continua, they are [called] exemplifications.   
2B2C -3B1B -3B2C. Commentary  
From among eight [sets], the first set of ten:  
 
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Due to having separated from attachment to all compounded 
phenomena, nails that are copper colored; (2) due to the extraordinary thought of benefiting 
all sentient beings, nails that are glossy; (3) due to having been born in the best of classes, 
nails that are raised; (4) due to the modes without  misdeeds, digits that are rounded; (5) due 
to the collection of roots of virtue, digits that are well -developed;  (6) due to perfect 
engagement serially or individually, digits that are tapered; (7) due to having guarded  
against actions of the body and so forth and livelihood, veins that are not prominent; (8) due 
to having untied the knots of the afflictions, veins that are without knots; (9) due to 
possessing intelligence with respect to very hidden phenomena, ankle bones that do not 
protrude; (10) due to having acted to liberate beings from all locations of difficult 
migrations, legs that are not unequal;  
 
EXPLANATI ON 
(1) The complete enjoyment body possesses nails that are copper colored, due to having 
culminated separation from attachment to all compounded phenomena.  It is said, “As a 
sign that one has such an inner excellent quality, one possesses the sign of the exalted 
body.” 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
562 (2) He possesses nails that are glossy, due to possessing the culminated extraordinary 
thought of benefiting all sentient beings  in the long -term.  
(3) He possesses nails that are raised  without any unevenness , due to having been born 
in the best of human classes.  
(4) He [possesses] digits that are rounded, due to  having culminated the modes of 
practice of the achievings without misdeeds.   
(5) He possesses digits that are well -developed, due to having culmina ted the collection 
of roots of virtue  in the past . 
(6) He possesses digits that are tapered, due to having culminated  perfect engagement 
serially or progressively in the classes of realizations of the three vehicles . Affix “having 
culminated” in accordance  with what is suitable in the context.  
(7) He possesses veins of the body that are not prominent  from the outside , due to having 
guarded against the ten non -virtuous paths of actions of the body and so forth and wrong 
livelihood.  
(8) He possesses veins tha t are without knots, due to having untied the knots of the 
afflictions  and having become freed . 
(9) He possesses smaller ankle bones that do not protrude, due to possessing intelligence 
with respect to very hidden phenomena  since he has realized the profoun d secret point .  
(10) He possesses legs that are not unequal since they do not differ in length , due to 
having acted to liberate beings from all locations of difficult migrations  such as cyclic 
existence .

COMMENTARY 
(11) due to skill in outshining human beings, the gait of a lion; (12) due to skill in

EXPLANATION  
(11) He possesses the gait of a lion, due to skill in outshining human beings.  
(12) He possesses the gait of an elephant, due to skill in outshining evil nagas  and so 
forth .  
(13) He possesses the gait of a swan, due to skill in  traveling through space  like a bird .  
(14) He possesses the gait of a leader, due to skill in being a leader of beings , sentient 
beings’ guide to the desired destinations .  
(15) He reverses and proceeds in a clockwise manner, due to proceeding in accordance 
with the path of circumambulation.  
(16) He proceeds beautifully, due to skill in being attractive and beautiful  to behold when 
he is proceeding .  
(17) He proceeds without swaying,  due to always not having a crooked mind that pays 
attention to two loci .  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
563 (18) He possesses a body that is elegant  since it is well -developed and dignified , due to 
perfectly expressing others’ excellent qualities.  
(19) He possesses a body that is as though cleansed since it is clean and pure , due to not 
being defiled by qualities of negativities  of the three doors .  
(20) He [possesses] a body that is well proportioned  since the height, girth, and so forth 
are precise  and not mixed- up, due to having taught Dharma in accordance with the  
minds of the trainees.

COMMENTARY

and so forth that was clean.  
(22) He possesses a body that is soft, because of a compassionate mi nd. 
(23) He possesses a body that is pure, because of a pure mind  free from stains .  
(24) He possesses signs that are complete, because of having thoroughly completed all 
the subduing qualities  that destroy the afflictions and conceptualizations in his con tinuum .  
(25) He possesses a body that is well -built due to it being broad and comely  in features , 
because of having expressed to others  excellent qualities that are (i) vast due to being 
extensive and (ii) beautiful  due to being pleasing .  
(26) He possess es strides that are even  since they do not differ in  span, because of an 
equanimous mind towards all sentient beings .  
(27) He possesses two eyes that are clear  since there are no specks and the like , because of 
having taught the pure Dharma  that generated  only excellent qualities .  
(28) He possesses a body that is very youthful -looking, because of having taught a 
Dharma along with examples  that is easy to understand.  
(29) He [possesses] a body that is not physically feeble, because of a mind that is always 
dauntless  regarding difficult matters .  
(30) He [possesses] a body that is physically well-developed, because of roots of virtue 
superior  to all worlds .   
 
The fourth set of ten:  
COMMENTARY 
(31) due to having extinguished rebirth, a body that is ve ry firm; (32) due to having very 
distinctly taught dependent- arising, primary limbs  and secondary limbs that are very

564 students, a waist that is round; (35) due to not being defiled by the faults of cyclic existence,

EXPLANATION  
(31) He possesses a body that is very firm  since the flesh is not flabby , due to having 
extinguished the karma for taking rebirth.  
(32) He possesses primary limbs , such as han ds and legs,  and secondary limbs , such as 
digit joints, that are very distinct since the components are differentiated , due to having 
very distinctly taught the forward and reverse cause and effect processes of dependent-
arising  for the sake of freeing tra inees from cyclic existence . 
(33) He possesses sight that is without visual aberration of adventitious defilements and 
is clear, due to having completely taught the meaning of the words based upon high 
status and definite goodness that are very pure. (34) He possesses a waist that is round, due to having caused excellent ethics in his 
students. 
(35) He possesses a waist that is moderately sized  since the waist is appropriately sized , 
due to not being polluted and not being defiled by the faults of cyclic ex istence  since he 
has destroyed them with antidotes . 
(36) He possesses a waist that is without depressions  since the waist is not too long , due 
to having destroyed the inflatedness of pride.    
(37) He possesses a belly that is flat without highs and lows , due to having turned away 
from the limited and taught unlimitedly the Dharma  that is to be known . 
(38) He possesses a navel that is deep, due to having realized the profound Dharma.  
(39) He possesses a navel whose creases are  curled clockwise, due to having caused his 
students to uphold the instructions in accordance  with himself.  
(40) He possesses beautiful  conduct, due to having caused beautiful behavior of the three 
doors  in all his retinue. 
 
The fifth set of ten:  
COMMENTA RY 
(41) due to a pure mind, conduct that is pure; (42) due to having separated from subduing

EXPLANATION  
(41) He possesses conduct of the three doors that is pure, due to a pure mind  since it is 
stainless . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
565 (42) He has  no moles or black marks on the body, due to having separated from (i) 
teaching a wrong path and (ii) teaching subduing Dharma that is untimely,  such as 
subduing trainees at an inopportune time .   
(43) He possesses hands that are very smooth like cotton, due to having taught the 
Dharma that becomes a cause for attaining well -being of the body, speech, mind, and so 
forth.  
(44) He possesses lines of the hands that are clearly radiant, due to having equalized his 
own being and other be ings and  attained the status of a great splendid trainee -in-virtue. 
(45) He possesses lines of the  hands that are deep  that are visible from a long distance , 
due to having abided in the culmination of realizing the profound Dharma.  
(46) He possesses lines of the  hands that are long, due to granting happiness in the short -
term and benefitting in the long -term, and perfectly giving pure teachings again and again 
also in the future. 
(47) He possesses a face that is not too long, due to having taught plenty of trainings in 
accordance with the many levels of the trainees . 
(48) He possesses lips that are as red as the bimba fruit  in which reflections of forms can 
appear, due to having realized that all worlds of environments and inhabitants are like a 
reflection. 
(49) He possesses a tongue that is supple, due to having subdued all trainees with 
smooth words.  
(50) He possesses a tongue that is very slender, due to having taught  abundant logic of 
reasoning that is an  excellent quality.  
 
The sixth set of ten:  
COMMENT ARY  
(51) due to having taught a subduing Dharma that is difficult to fathom by childish beings

EXPLANATION  
(51) He possesses a tongue that is red, due to having taught a subduing Dharma for 
destroying afflictions that is difficult to fathom by childish beings who have grasping to 
I and mine . 
(52) He possesses a thunderous voice, due to not having had any trepidation, 
apprehensiveness,  and fear [thinking,] “I shall conceal shortcomings.”  
(53) He possesses a voice that is pleasant, supple, and smooth, due to having spoken 
pleasantly and so forth through speech that is delightful to the ears . 
(54) He possesses canine teeth that are round, due to having exhaustively restrained the 
nine entwinements with samsar a: subsequent grasping and so forth.  
(55) He possesses canine teeth that are sharp, due to having tamed the strong afflictions 
in beings who are difficult to tame. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
566 (56) He possesses canine teeth that are white, because of having acted to destroy with the 
very wholesome subduing Dharma that destroys afflictions . 
(57) He possesses canine teeth that are even  since they do not differ in length , due to 
having abided on the ground that realizes the equality  of samsara and nirvana . 
(58) He possesses canine teeth that are tapered, due to having perfectly taught the 
serial clear realizations  of the three vehicles through their stages . 
(59) He possesses a nose that is prominent, due to having abided in the supreme 
wisdom  realizing emptiness that is complete in the fac tor of method . 
(60) He possesses a nose that is clean  since it is free of snot and the like , due to beings  
(who are the trainees) possessing excellent pure  conviction .

COMMENTARY 
(61) because of the very extensive Dharma, eyes  that are wide; (62) due to having led

female migrating beings, eyes with the white and black parts defined, like the lovely petals

EXPLANATIO N 
(61) He possesses eyes that are wide, because of having taught the very extensive 
innumerable Mahayana Dharma.  
(62) He possesses eyelashes that are thick, due to having led limitless multitudes of 
sentient beings  through having abided in cyclic existence . 
(63) He possesses eyes with the white and black parts defined  since they are unmixed , 
like the lovely complete petals of a lotus, due to having pleased all young female god, 
anti-god, and human migrating beings  who are difficult to please, since he is wo rthy of 
praise by them . 
(64) He possesses eyebrows that are long, due to constantly watching migrating beings 
in the future , through looking, “How will they be in the future?”  
(65) He possesses eyebrows that are smooth, due to being skilled in the gentle s ubduing 
Dharma  through subduing with the gentle Dharma that does not rely on the five fires and 
the like .  
(66) He possesses eyebrows that are glossy, due to the mental continuum having been 
thoroughly moistened by virtue.  
(67) He possesses hairs of the e yebrows that are even  since they do not differ in length , 
due to seeing the faults  of afflictions . 
(68) He possesses arms that are long and well -developed, due to having turned away 
the worst harm  of sentient beings: the causes of general cyclic existence and of bad 
migrations [in particular].  
(69) He possesses ears that are equal  since they do not differ in length or size , due to 
being victorious over attachment and so forth  that are difficult to conquer . 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
567 (70) He possesses ear sense powers that are unimpaired  such as ears that are unimpaired 
in engaging sounds , due to having set all sentient beings (who are the trainees) in 
happiness without  degenerating their continua.  
 
The eighth set of ten:  
COMMENTARY 
(71) due to not having been influenced by all that is fabricated by views, a forehead that is 
well-defined; (72) due to having defeated all propounders, a forehead that is broad; (73) due 
to having completed the best of prayers, a head that is v ery well- formed; (74) due to having 
turned away from the joy of objects, hair that is as black as bees; (75) because of having 
abandoned those dormancies that are to be abandoned by the  path of seeing and the  path of 
meditation, hair that is dense; (76) du e to having known the teachings with a smooth mind, 
hair that is smooth; (77) due to a mind that is not disturbed by attachment and so forth, hair 
that is untangled; (78) due to words that are always not harsh, hair that is not bristly; (79) 
due to having scattered the flowers of the branches of enlightenment, hair that is fragrant; 
(80) due to having been beautiful in all ways, hands and feet that are adorned with shrivatsas, svastikas, and nandyavartas;   
 
EXPLANATION  
(71) He possesses a forehead that is well -defined  and a hairline that stands out well , due 
to not having been influenced by all that is fabricated by views  holding to extremes, 
such as the sixty -two bad views, which have not been able to change his original [view] . 
(72) He possesses a forehea d that is broad, due to having defeated all wrong 
propounders.  
(73) He possesses a head that is very well -formed  like the parasol , due to having 
completed the best of prayers in which one prays to attain buddhahood for others’ welfare .  
(74) He possesses hair that is as black as bees, due to having turned away from all 
craving to objects —forms, sounds, and so forth —the joy of objects. 
(75) He possesses hair that is dense  without intermediary gaps , because of having 
exhaustively abandoned those dormancies that are to be abandoned by the path of 
seeing and the path of meditation.  
(76) He possesses hair that is smooth, due to having known the teachings with a smooth 
mind that is without the roughness of holding to extremes . 
(77) He possesses hair that is untangled, due to a mind that is not disturbed by 
attachment and so forth. 
(78) He possesses hair that is not bristly  and not stiff, due to words that are always not 
harsh since he has exhaustively abandoned harsh words . 
(79) He possesses hair that is fragrant, due to having scattered the flowers of the 
branches (that serve as the causes of enlightenment) before the [Three] Jewels . 
(80) He possesses hands and feet that are adorned with shrivatsas, vajra -like patterns on 
the thumbs and the big toes,  svastikas that are squarish patterns on the palms of the hands 
and the soles of the feet , and,  on their edges, nandyavartas  of seven patterns swirling 
clockwise on the feet on the fourth toes and so forth , due to the exalted body having been 
beautiful in all ways. It has also been taught that there is a shrivatsa at the heart.  
  Regarding these exemplifications, it has been taught that, due to the presence of a certain 
inner excellent quality, a certain sign dawns externally, and that [the inner excellent quality 
and the external sign] are a pair of cause and result.

EXPLANATION  
In that way, having shown three exalted bodies, the fourth, the emanation body, in

2B2C -3B1B -4B. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
8.33 The exalted body through which  
The various benefits to migrating beings are simultaneously enacted  
As long as cyclic existence exists  
Is the uninterrupted emanation body of Muni (the Subduer). 
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalt ed body that does not [possess] the five certainties through which 
the form bodies, such as the form body of Shakyamuni , uninterruptedly enact  exalted 
activities —is the emanation body , because it is an exalted body that simultaneously 
enacts the various be nefits of high status and definite goodness by directly appearing to 
pure and impure migrating beings as long as cyclic existence exists.  
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, moreover, someone who trains in this Mother, having realized those 
dharmas, will attain buddhah ood in unsurpassable complete enlightenment and then enact 
the welfare of all sentient beings through various emanations of a perfectly complete 
buddha at all times in the innumerable world systems of the ten directions. A great 
bodhisattva should train in  the Mother in that way.”   
2B2C -3B1B -4C. Commentary  
COMMENTARY  
So the root text is said. The exalted body through which the forms of Shakyamuni and so 
forth simultaneously enact the desired aims of sentient beings in all world systems as long 
as cyclic e xistence exists, continually, is the uninterrupted emanation body of the Buddha 
Bhagavan.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —the exalted body through which the forms of Shakyamuni and so forth 
simultaneously enact the desired aims of pure and impure sentient beings by directly 
appearing —is the emanation body of the Buddha Bhagavan , because it is a final form 
body that does not [possess] the five certainties.  It uninterruptedly enacts  exalted 
activities, because it continually  enacts  exalted activities  in all world sy stems as long as 
cyclic existence exists.

COMMENTARY 
In that way, the essence  of the nature body arise through the strength of cultivating, as the 
objects of buddhas and so forth , in reliance  upon exalted wisdom and so forth , having 
indicated the three exalted bodies that have been imputed,…   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subject —in that way,  the remaining three exalted bodies —are  in the essence  of the 
nature body, because that is the dharmata  of those. They are not causeless and do not arise 
from discordant causes, because they  arise  through the strength of cultivating  the two 
accumulations. The three exalted bodies that have been imputed  as real conventionalities 
do not arise causelessly, because (i) the exalted wisdom truth body that is a direct object of 
utilization of only buddhas  arises in reliance upon having gathered the accumulation of 
exalted wisdom , its cause , (ii) the complete enjoyment body that is a dire ct object of 
bodhisattva aryas and above who are [included within the expression] “ and so forth” arises 
in reliance upon having gathered the accumulation of merit, its cause, [which is included 
within the expression] “ and so forth, ” and (iii) the supreme e manation body arises from 
having gathered factors of both accumulations.  
 Regarding the meaning of this passage, someone explained that the four exalted wisdoms 
had been indicated as the basis of designation of the three exalted bodies. Another person, having refuted that, explained that the three, (i) the twenty -one uncontaminated exalted 
wisdoms, and included within the expression “and so forth” —(ii) the exalted body adorned 
with the signs and exemplifications that resides only in Akanistha , and (iii) the  exalted body 
that appears as a reflection of that —had been indicated as the remaining three exalted 
bodies. These [explanations] lie outside of the assertions of Acarya [Haribhadra], because in 
his Great Commentary  in the context of the presentation of th e corpus, the brief indication 
of this passage, he has clearly indicated that the remaining three exalted bodies respectively arise from the accumulation of exalted wisdom, the accumulation of merit, and factors of 
both. In that way, having indicated the n ature body and the remaining three exalted bodies, 
the exalted activities will be explained.  
2B2C -3B2A -1B. Indicating that the exalted activities will be explained  
COMMENTARY  
… since, by means of only the exalted wisdom conventionally generating appearan ces of 
the complete enjoyment body and so forth, activities of welfare are enacted, the activities 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
570 (based upon the appearances to beings who are the trainees) that arise in dependence on the 
owner who is that are of the truth body.   
 
EXPLANATION  
The subje ct—the  exalted activities (based upon the appearances to pure and impure 
beings who are the trainees) that arise in dependence on the owner who is that exalted 
wisdom truth body—one should know that they are exalted activities of the exalted wisdom 
truth body, since —by means of only the exalted wisdom truth body of realizations  
conventionally generating appearances of the complete enjoyment body , the emanation 
body,  and so forth —activities of welfare, those [the subject] and so forth, are enacted.  
2B2C-3B2A -2. Root text  
2B2C -3B2A -2A. Brief indication by means of duration  
2B2C -3B2A -2B. Extensive explanation by means of divisions 
2B2C -3B2A -2C. Summary by means of enumeration  
2B2C -3B2A -2A. Brief indication by means of duration  
ROOT TEXT  
8.34ab  Similarly , as long as cyclic existence exists,  
The activities of this are asserted to be uninterrupted.  
 
EXPLANATION  
The truth body is uninterrupted.  Similarly, as long as cyclic existence exists, the exalted 
activities of this truth body are asserted to be uninterrupted , because it has been 
preceded by having culminated the accumulation of causes for being uninterrupted in that way. In terms of the relationship in the root text, “Similarly” is related to the emanation 
body. However, Acarya [Haribhadra] interpreted it to be [related to] the truth body.

ROOT TEXT  
8.34c  The activities in which migrations have been pacified;  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
571 EXPLANATION  
(1) He initially sets [trainees] in the excel lent attitude, because he enacts the exalted 
activities  of [setting trainees in] generating the attitude that seeks liberation in which [the 
aspirations] seeking mere migrations that are happy migrations and bad migrations have 
been pacified . Through this,  setting [trainees] in a support of the path has been implied, but, 
explicitly, setting [trainees] in the attitude seeking liberation has been indicated.  
 Sutra says, “Subhuti, from here, when the eyes of the Buddha look at the worlds, from 
the grains of sand of the Ganges Rivers in the world systems in the eastern direction, great 
bodhisattvas who intentionally think, ‘Sentient beings have gone to the great hells, and 
having pacified the sufferings of the hell beings…’”

EXPLANATION

means of gathering [disciples]:  generosity, speaking pleasantly, conduct of welfare, and 
according with the meaning. 
2B2C -3B2A -2B1A -3. Setting in the view that realizes the four truths  
ROOT TEXT  
8.35ab  The realization of the thoroughly afflicted  
And the completely pure;   
 
EXPLANATION  
(3) He sets [trainees] in the wisdom that realizes the four truths, because he sets [trainees] in  
the realization that (i) sufferings and origins that are  the thoroughly afflicted causes and 
results are to be discarded and (ii) paths and cessations that are  the completely pure  causes 
and results are to be adopted . 
 Sutra says, “Such a transcendence of the cyclic existence of the five migrations that is 
either thoroughly afflicted or completely pure has never existed.”

2B2C -3B2A -3A1A- 1. Setting in seeking liberation, the attitude of middling beings  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) Initially, having enacted the exalted activity in which migrations 
have been pacified, the characteristic of abiding without adherence to good migrations and 
poor migrations;   
EXPLANATION  
(1) After having set [trainees] in high status, he acts to set them initially  in the attitude 
seeking liberation, because he enacts the  exalted activity of [setting trainees in the attitude] 
seeking liberation in which the attitude of seeking migrations  in cyclic existence  have 
been pacified, the characteristic of abiding without adherence to happy migrations that 
are good migrations  and bad migrations that are  poor migrations  as objects to be attained . 
Through this, setting [trainees] in a support of the path has been indicated, but, explicitly, 
the exalted activity of setting [trainees] in seeking liberation has been indicated.   
2B2C -3B2A-3A1A- 2. Setting in the four means of gathering [disciples]  
COMMENTARY 
(2) setting in the four properties of gathering [disciples], generosity and so forth;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(2) It is not limited to just that, because he enacts the exalted activity of settin g [trainees] in 
the four properties of gathering [disciples]: generosity that acts to gather sentient beings 
and so forth.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
579 2B2C -3B2A -3A1A- 3. Setting in knowing the four truths  
COMMENTARY 
(3) having caused to realize the discordant classes and the antidotes  from the point of view 
of the objects to be discarded and to be adopted, through knowing that is arisen from 
hearing and so forth;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(3) It is not limited to just that, because he causes  [trainees] to realize the discordant 
classes , sufferings and origins, and the antidotes , cessations and paths, respectively from 
the point of view  of the objects to be discarded and to be adopted, through knowing 
that is arisen from hearing , arisen from contemplation and meditation, and so forth.  
2B2C -3B2A -3A1A- 4. Setting in the attitude of other’s welfare 
COMMENTARY 
(4) setting in the welfare of sentient beings, that is, the nature of such characteristics of love 
and so forth that are others’ welfare, through being devoid of subsequent grasping an d so 
forth, like an illusionist;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(4) It is not limited to just that exalted activity, because he then enacts the exalted activity of 
setting [trainees] in the welfare of sentient beings, that is, the nature of such 
characteristics of the four immeasurables, love and so forth that are others’ welfare, 
through being devoid of subsequent grasping, anger, and so forth with respect to 
sentient beings , like an illusionist.   
2B2C -3B2A -3A1A- 5. Setting in the conduct  
COMMENTARY 
(5) after that, in famil iarizing with the six perfections, distinguished by the purity of the 
three spheres, one’s own welfare;  
 
EXPLANATION  
(5) He sets [trainees] in special conduct, because, after that, he sets [trainees] in 
familiarizing with the six perfections, distinguished  by realizing the purity of the three

2B2C -3B2A -3B. Summary  
COMMENTARY  
Therefore, just as the truth body, also the twenty -seven types  of exalted activities of this, 
since it is asserted as long as cyclic existence exists, are enacted.   
 
EXPLANATION  
Therefore, just as the truth body  is uninterrupted , also the twenty -seven types of exalted 
activities of this  truth body are uninterrupted , because, since it is asserted to enact the 
welfare of sentient beings as long as cyclic existence exists, the  exalted activities are 
enacted  [uninterruptedly] . 
2B2C -3B2B. The sequences of the other topics of the words should also be understood in depende nce on the 
first and last of the seventy topics  
COMMENTARY 
Through the stages of having indicated the sequences in which the first generated mind and 
the activities that is the last  have been indicated, the sequences of the remaining interceding 
topics of the words are also to be understood. Fearing wordiness, I have not written them 
down.  
 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
586 EXPLANATION  
DISPUTANT: Having explained the individual boundaries on the occasions of indicating 
the divisions of the generated mind and the exalted activities, why are the boundaries of the 
sixty -eight topics of the words not explained?  
 RESPONSE: Since it is the occasion to mostly indicate the attributes of the general path, the 
boundaries are not made definite. In some cases the boundaries have been clearly indicated; 
in those cases where the boundaries have not been clearly indicated, also [the boundaries] 
need to be realized by following the two: the first and the last. There is not the fault of not 
realizing the sequences , that is, the boundaries, of the remaining  sixty -eight interceding 
topics of the words in dependence upon this treatise, because, through the stages of 
having indicated  the sequences , that is, the boundaries, in which the generated mind , the 
first of the seventy topics, and the exalted activities that is the last have been indicated, 
the boundaries of the remaining ones are also to  be understood  in dependence on those .  
 
DISPUTANT: Although they have not been explicitly indicated in the root text, they must 
definitely be explained in the commentary . 
 
RESPONSE: I, Haribhadra, have not written down  the boundaries of the remaining topics 
of the words extensively in this small commentary, because, fearing wordiness, I have not 
written them down.   
 
DISPUTANT: You should have explained it in this way: “If it were appropriate to explain 
the remaining topics of the words as it has been done for the first and the last, then, since the 
complete stages of the Mahayana path must have been indicated seventy times, there would 
be the fault of repetition.”  
 
Respon se: According to you, is the path complete by merely that explicitly explained on the 
occasions of the generated mind and the exalted activities? This is not correct because even 
those that have not been explicitly indicated there but have been explicitly indicated on 
other occasions are definitely necessary as branches of the stages of the paths of clear 
realizations. If those that have been indicated on other occasions are explicitly indicated on 
those two occasions, then since [Maitreya] has simply writt en down the entire Ornament for 
Clear Realization  there, it follows for you that there would be a huge fault of repetition. If 
the other clear realizations have not been indicated on any occasion, there would be the fault 
that they would not be realized.  
 Therefore, although a certain portion of the  structure  and the coarse excellent qualities 
existing from the ground of a beginner until  the buddha ground has been indicated on the 
occasions of the generated mind and the exalted activities, other clear reali zations that have 
not explained there have been explained through connection with the [topics on] 
instructions, the path of preparation, and so on. Since the meanings are similar for the path 
of preparation, they must be complete on the occasion of that, b ut there are infinite 
differences in whether or not a single term is able to explicitly indicate [them].  
 It seems that many scholars have made mistakes due to not having eliminated qualms 
regarding this engagement through an eliminative expresser. Althou gh it is a single isolate, 
since it has been indicated many times by way of the differences that are dissimilar 
purposes, how could there even be a mere whiff of the fault of repetition? For, explaining an 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence            Chapter 8: The Truth Body  
 
587 isolate during the two occasions of the path of ac cumulation and the path of preparation is to 
make it known that it exists and is cultivated on both those occasions. All types of clear 
realizations are cultivated on the occasion of a single path of accumulation, but if all the 
clear realizations were ind icated there, then the author of the treatise that indicates the 
complete stages of the path would be unskillful. It seems that a single isolate has been indicated with many dissimilar names. It is for the sake of becoming skilled in the 
enumeration of nam es (synonyms); we should know them through analyzing their 
definitions. Since these points of doubt are difficult to realize and are of very great purpose, 
those who are skilled should think about them in detail.

Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization.

Essence, which is an explanation of the commentary on the Treatise of Quintessential 
Instructions of the Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization .

2B3A -1. Making a connection  
COMMENTARY 
Having explained by means of the eight meanings in t hat way to benefit sentient beings with 
liking for the extensive, they are also explained in another way:  
  
EXPLANATION  
Having explained the summarized meanings by means of the eight meanings in that way 
in order to benefit sentient beings with faith in th e extensive  elaboration of the meanings , 
the summarized meanings are also explained in another way, other than that, through 
having been summarized into six, for persons with faith in the middling.  
2B3A -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
9.1 The characteristics; the application of those;  
That highly; the sequence of those;  
The final; the fruition:  
Another set of summarized meanings is the set of the six types. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Another set of summarized meanings of the Ornament, other than the set of the eight, is to be known as the set of the six types , because the following six are to be known as the 
middling summarized meanings of the Ornament:  
(1) the characteristics  that are the entities of the three exalted knowers ; 
(2) the application  in complete aspects that is the practice of those  three exalted knowers ; 
(3) the peak [application] that is highly  transformed from that  application [in complete 
aspects] ;  
(4) the serial sequence of the practice of those  three exalted knowers ; 
(5) the instantaneous final  application of those three exalted knowers ; and   
(6) the truth body, the fruition  of the three exalted knowers.  
2B3A -3. Commentary 
2B3A -3A. Individual explanations  
2B3A -3B. Having summarized, making referral to the extensive meaning of the words above  
                                              
186 Here the outline is st ated slightly differently from before in the Tibetan text: Explaining other summarized 
meanings  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
589 2B3A -3A. Individual explanations  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) First of all, the three exalted knowers of all, the exalted knower 
of all aspects and so forth, since they are states that are characterized, are characteristics.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) First of all, the subject —the three exalted knowers of all, the exalted knower of all 
aspects and so forth —since whatever is an object of cultivation of the applications needs to 
be characterized as an object of valid cognition at the start, they are characteristics  due to 
acting to characterize this . 
 Since they are states that are characterized  by means of the aspects of the three 
exalted knowers , it is reasonable to explain them as states that  are characterized by the thirty 
topics. Howeve r, they are not to be explained as above, on the occasion of the presentation 
of the corpus. According to what is overtly stated in the commentary of Arya 
[Vimuktisena], that which acts to characterize is to be taken as a characteristic.  
 
COMMENTARY 
(2) Ne xt, because of gaining control, it applies the cultivation of the three exalted knowers 
of all through this, the clear complete  realization of all aspects  is an application of the three 
exalted knowers of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(2) Next, the subject —the application of the clear complete  realization of all aspects —is 
an application that practices the aspects  of the three exalted knowers of all, because  it 
applies the cultivation of the aspects of the three exalted knowers of all through this  
bodhisattva’s yoga that is a union of calm abiding and special insight , therefore it is an 
application that cultivates . There is a purpose for the cultivation, because it is for the sake 
of gaining control  over the aspects of the three exalted knowers . 
 
COMMENTARY  
(3) Next, because it progresses to magnificence through great familiarity, the clear 
realization of the peak is the occasion of the excellence of the three exalted knowers of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(3) N ext, the subject —the clear realization of the peak —is the occasion of the excellent 
familiarization with  the aspects of  the three exalted knowers of all, because it is an 
application that progresses and has progressed to magnificence  by way of being conjoi ned 
with the wisdom arisen from meditation realizing emptiness  through great familiarity  with 
the application in complete aspects previously . 
 
COMMENTARY  
(4) Next, because of ascertaining the properties  that have been realized, through 
straightening up th e meanings cultivated individually and altogether, the clear realization of 
the serial is the occasion of the sequence of the three exalted knowers of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(4) Next, the subject —the clear realization of the serial —is the occasion of the seque nce 
of the aspects of the three exalted knowers of all, the sequence of cultivating them in a 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
590 single series as property -possessors,  because it is an application that cultivates  through 
straightening up (in a single series that bears order) the meanings  cultivated: (i) 
individually,  the aspects of the three exalted knowers indicated on the occasion of the first 
three chapters  and (ii) altogether , the aspects that have been indicated on [the occasion of 
the fourth chapter on the application of clear complete  realization of] all aspects . There is a 
purpose for cultivation in that way, because it is for the sake of ascertaining  in an instant 
the properties that have been realized  previously .   
 
COMMENTARY 
(5) Next, since lacking progression to distinction, perfect clear complete  enlightenment in a 
single instant is the occasion of the finality of the three exalted knowers of all.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(5) Next, the subject —the application of perfect clear complete  enlightenment in a single 
instant —is the occasion of the final application of the three exalted knowers of all , 
because it is a final application  since realizations of the learner’s path lack progression to 
distinction  beyond it .  
  
COMMENTARY 
(6) Next, si nce it is the result of those, the truth body along with the activities is the fruition 
of the three exalted knowers of all. 
 
EXPLANATION  
(6) Next, the subject —the truth body along with the activities —is the occasion of the 
fruition  of the realizations of the three exalted knowers of all, since it is the  final result 
of those  applications . 
2B3A -3B. Having summarized, making referral to the extensive meanings of the words above  
COMMENTARY 
Like that, another set of summarized meanings of all the Mothers, the set of the six types, are to be explained in accordance with only the above.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Another set of summarized meanings of all the three Mothers, other than the set of the 
eight, the set of the six types, is like that explained above. The meanings of the words of 
that set of summarized meanings are also to be known , because the meanings of the words 
are to be explained in accordance with only the occasion of the eight above.

2B3B -2. Root text  
ROOT TEXT  
9.2 The three types of objects; the cause s, 
The natures of the four a pplications; and  
The result, the truth body along with the exalted activities:  
Another set of summarized meanings is the three types.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) The subject —the aspects of the three types of exalted knowers —are the objects of 
cultivation of the appl ications, because they are the objects  of practice of those.  
 Just as generating the mind into the entity of compassion is called “cultivating 
compassion,” one cultivates the aspects of the three exalted knowers by generating them 
into the entity of the a pplications; it is not like looking at a visual form with an eye 
consciousness.  
 The Great Lama Lotsawa explained that, due to having been summarized into three 
objects that are to be known by hearing and contemplation, they are called “objects”. Here they are subsumed under three main objects to be cultivated through the applications, hence 
they are said to be “the three types of objects” [9.2a].   
 (2) The subject —the natures of the four applications —are the causes  for those final 
three exalted knowers, because they are the methods that act to attain them. (3) The 
subject —the truth body  along with  the exalted activities —is the result of the 
applications of the three exalted knowers, because it is the final object th at is to be attained 
through the strength of those [applications]. Another set of summarized meanings  of the 
three Mothers, other than the two above, is the three types : objects, causes, and result.  
2B3B -3. Commentary  
COMMENTARY 
So the root text is said. (1) First of all, the entitiness of the three exalted knowers of all, the 
exalted knower of all aspects and so forth, are the causes that are the objects that are 
engaged in.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(1) First of all, the subject —the entitinesses  of the three exalted knowers of all, the 
exalted knower of all aspects and so forth —are the causes that are the objects that are 
engaged in  with respect to cultivating the applications, because one engages in cultivating 
the applications by taking them as the objects to be kno wn, the objects to be cultivated, and 
the objects to be attained, with respect to the applications . 
 
COMMENTARY 
QUALM: How are those applied?  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
592 RESPONSE: (2) After that, the four types of clear realizations, the clear complete  
realization of all aspects and so forth, are the applications.  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: How are those three exalted knowers (i) cultivated  through  the applications and 
(ii) applied  as objects of the applications ? 
RESPONSE: (2) After that, the subject—the four types of clear realizations, the clear 
complete  realization of all aspects and so forth —are the applications  that cultivate the 
aspects of the three exalted knowers, because they are yogas that are a union of calm abiding and special insight in th e continuum of a bodhisattva that cultivate those .  
 
COMMENTARY 
QUALM: What is the result of those causes that possess such applications?  
RESPONSE: (3) After that, the truth body along with the exalted activities is the result.  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: What is  the result of those applications, the causes that possess such 
applications  of the three exalted knowers ? 
RESPONSE: (3) After that, the subject —the truth body along with the exalted 
activities —is the result of the applications of the three exalted knowers , because it is a 
final object of attainment of those [applications].  
 
Summary:  
COMMENTARY 
Therefore, another set of summarized meanings of the three types of Mothers of the victors, 
the set of the three types, are to be explained in accordance with only t hat. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Therefore, another set of summarized meanings of the three types of Mothers of the 
victors , other than the two above , is the set of the three types . The meanings of the words 
of that are also to be known , because they are to be explained in accordance with only 
that explanation [of the summarized meanings] as eight . 
 
DISPUTANT: If the need to indicate a complete extensive explanation of the branches of 
the meanings of the words to persons with faith individually in the three sets of summarized 
meanings were uncertain, then it would contradict the explanation above with respect to the 
indication. If it were necessary to explain that extensively to all [three types of persons], 
then the differences among the three —the extensive, the m edium , and the brief—would be 
incorrect, because any sutra or treatise would be easy. If there is no difference in needing to explain them completely [to all three types of persons], then the divisions individually into 
persons with faith in the extensive, middlin g, and brief would be incorrect, yet there exists 
differences in sharpness or dullness of faculty. Also, since (i) that is similar with the 
probandum [that there are the three sets of summarized meanings], and (ii) it has been 
taught that the all three set s of summarized meanings are differentiated with respect to each 
of the three Mothers, you would definitely have to accept nine levels of sharpness or 
dullness in faculty.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
593 RESPONSE: That is true, but, although this meaning seems to be a point that is very  
difficult to understand, I have not seen the analysis performed by any scholar. Someone said 
that there are differences that distinguish the definite entity, the definite number, and the 
definite sequence of the clear realizations, but that is difficult t o posit, because there is no 
difference in the need to indicate the meaning of the words extensively [to all three types of persons].  
 OPINION: The three —the extensive, medium, and brief Perfection of Wisdom Sutras —
have been taught for the sake of the thre e: trainees of sharp, middling, and dull faculty with 
liking for extensive, middling, and brief words. Differentiating three  sets of summarized 
meanings with respect to each of the Mothers has been done not in terms of differences of sharpness or dullness in faculty, but in terms of trainees with liking for extensive, middling, 
and brief elaboration of the meanings.  
 RESPONSE: However, it would be difficult to posit that separated from the consideration of 
the levels of sharpness or dullness in faculty.  
 If the sets of summarized meanings amount to nothing more than mere differences in the 
quantity of words in the presentation of the corpus, then why are the sets of summarized meanings not summarized into the two: the path perfection of wisdom and the resul tant 
perfection of wisdom? Therefore, I thought that it is like this: all three types of trainees of, 
for instance, the Eight Thousand Stanza Perfection of Wisdom Sutra, do not differ in 
needing an explanation of the complete meaning of its words, and furt her, they act to 
generate ascertainment of all the meanings of the words by way of eliminating 
superimpositions through reasoning.  
 Regarding the three dissimilar sets of summarized meanings that have been explained, 
initially when just the summarized mean ings have been explained, one should know the 
differences in whether or not [the trainees] are able to induce the ascertainment of the coarse 
meaning of the extensive explanation of the branches through correct assumption. For 
example, it is like the diffe rence between (i) thinking that there is water in the old well 
while not seeing a reason when dwelling at home, and (ii) realizing the water with a valid cognizer. Likewise, this is also to be known with respect to the treatise. [The three sets of 
summariz ed meanings] do not differ in the quantity of words in the presentation of the 
corpus; they are not different with respect to the extensive explanation of the branches.  
OBJECTION: Well then, [the treatise] would be similar in having this fault of the corp us 
being summarized into two.   
 
RESPONSE: No, it is not similar, because, if one is able to induce correct assumption with 
respect to the coarse meaning of the words of the extensive explanation of the branches through merely those [summarized meanings of the Ornament ], then there would be no need 
to rely on the extensive explanation and the summarized explanation of a treatise such as 
this one. Thus, scholars who analyze the meanings in that way should understand them by 
having contemplated them in detail .  
 These commentaries on the root text have not been correlated with the sutras in detail and 
have not extensively explained the details of the disputes and replies. They have been composed for the sake of realization by even those who wish to engage in t he perfection of 
wisdom merely by abiding with their legs stretched. The way to practice the extensive and 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
594 brief stages of the paths of clear realizations should be known from explanations stated 
elsewhere.

2C1A. Differences in the objects to be explained  
COMMENTARY  
By way of the meaning in some, the words in others, and   
Names that are enumerations in others:  
Those who are skilled should know just those summarized meanings  Also with respect to all the Mothers.  
 
EXPLANATION  
Those who are skilled in the meanings of the sutras and the treatises should know just those  eight, six, and three  sets of summarized meanings of the subject matter also with 
respect to all the three Mothers , because (i) some of them have been taught by way of the 
complete meaning explicitly and implicitly  in the Arya Eight Thousand Stanza Perfection 
of Wisdom , (ii) others have been taught by way of  the complete words that explicitly 
indicated the meanings in  the m edium  Mother, and (ii) others have been taught in the 
extensive [Mother]  by way of  names that are many enumerations  for each meaning, that 
is, by way of many  synonyms for each meaning.  
 
COMMENTARY  
If not like that, the levels of all  
Would not have been well summarized;  
They simply have differences only through  Classifications of brief and so forth.  
 
EXPLANATION  
QUALM: Since there are differences in the wo rds, are there not differences in the 
meanings?  
 RESPONSE: [If there were differences in the meanings, then] it follows that all  three levels 
of meanings of the subject matter of all the three Mothers  would not have been well 
summarized  in a manner of abse nce of differences by this treatise, because the three 
Mothers would not  be similar in subject matter like that.  
  
QUALM: Then, what is the point of having taught three dissimilar texts with respect to a single similar subject matter?  
                                             
 
187 Here the outline is stated slightly differently from that before in the Tibetan text: The activities for 
completing the explanation. 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings

RESPONSE: There is n ot the fault of repetition in the three Mothers, because they simply 
have differences only through classifications of brief,  middling, extensive words, and so 
forth. These [discussions] follow from “[To the perfection of wisdom respectfully I pay 
homage in order to thoroughly reveal its versified Ornament] as serving as an ornament of all” [at the beginning of Haribhadra’s commentary  Clear Meaning ].
188  
2C1B. How they h ave been explained in dependence on what  
COMMENTARY  
By means of having seen the meanings  Through the force of Arya Vimuktisena,   
For ease in understanding,  
Without having correlated the words, the phrases, and so forth,  
 
Having paid homage to the excellent guru,  
The perfect abbot, Vairocana,  
This clear commentary on the versified  
Was composed by Haribhadra. 
 
EXPLANATION  
This Clear Meaning,  a commentary on the versified Ornament for Clear Realization  was 
composed by the victors’ son, Haribhadra. In dependence on what  did he compose it? He 
did not compose it without the instructions of the past acaryas, because (i) he composed it 
by means of having seen the meanings through the force of having contemplated well the 
comment ary of Arya Vimuktisena, and, (ii) having first explicitly paid homage to the 
excellent guru  who had understood all the meanings of the treatise , the perfect abbot, 
Vairocana, he composed it after having asked him for permission . 
 How did he compose it? He  did not explain [the Ornament] elaborately in this 
commentary, because he clearly explained just the meaning without having explicitly correlated the words  of the sutras , the phrases  of the treatises , and so forth. 
Alternatively, it means that he composed  it through summarizing without having correlated 
it with  the words of sutras, without having correlated  it with  the words of the disputes and 
replies, and without having correlated it with  the citations from other sources and so forth. 
There is a reason f or composing it through summarizing the meanings without having 
correlated it extensively with the sutra words , because it is for the sake of ease in 
understanding.  
2C2. Dedicating the virtues of composition  
COMMENTARY 
Due to whatever virtues arisen from c ompiling that  
Attained by me,   
May all beings  
Attain the wisdom of a Sugata.

2C3. Discarding arrogance,  and the reason for enthusiasm in composing the commentary  
2C3A. Discarding arrogance  
2C3B. Requesting forgiveness for mistakes 
2C3C. The reason for enthusiasm in composing the comm entary  
2C3A. Discarding arrogance  
2C3A -1. The inferiority of his own mind  
2C3A -2. The meanings of the texts are difficult to realize 
2C3A -1. The inferiority of his own mind  
COMMENTARY 
Unclear wisdom, all the treatises  
Lie beyond; how can they be mine?  
 
EXPLANATION  
How can all the meanings of the treatises be  the objects of utilization of my direct 
cognition ? For, (i) since my wisdom  is unclear, I engage by way of generalities in order to 
express the treatise, and (ii) all the meanings of the treatises li e beyond  the objects of my 
direct cognition. Alternatively, “For they are the natures that lie beyond” is explained. 
2C3A -2. The meanings of the texts are difficult to realize 
COMMENTARY 
The meanings of the treatises, objects of utilization of minds of ary as,  
The great beings; how can they be mine?  
 
EXPLANATION  
How can all the meanings of the treatises without exception be  the objects  of my direct 
cognition ? For, since  they are objects of utilization of the direct cognitions  that are minds 
of aryas, the gr eat beings , they are difficult to realize.  
2C3B. Requesting forgiveness for mistakes 
COMMENTARY 
Nevertheless, not being objects of utilization,  
May those skilled beings forgive the mistakes,  
Composed by me with effort  In the perspective of one’s and others’ welfares.  
 
EXPLANATION  
They are not objects of my direct cognition. Nevertheless, not being objects of utilization, 
if there are lots of mistakes  in the commentary that was composed by me with effort, may 
those skilled beings who possess Dharma eyes forgive them.  There is  a purpose for having 
composed it with effort, because it was done in the perspective of one’s and others’ 
welfares. [This discussion] follows from “Although not my object of utilization in all ways, 
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
597 [because it accords with achievi ng merits, I generated enthusiasm, as I desire to benefit 
myself and others]” [at the beginning of Haribhadra’s commentary  Clear Meaning ].189 
2C3C. The reason for enthusiasm in composing the commentary 
COMMENTARY 
Oh! My resting place from fatigue  
Through th e various textual systems,  
The system of the perfection of wisdom,  I saw a long time ago. 
 
EXPLANATION  
From among the four cases: (1) disparagement, (2) wishing, (3) discouragement, and (4) 
amazement, here the exclamation “ Oh!” is a case of the last one. I realized and saw  this 
system of the perfection of wisdom, a special realization that is  the resting place  of my 
mind from the result of weariness and fatigue through having viewed the various textual 
systems that are the complete upper and lower tenets that I had trained well in , therefore it 
was appropriate for me to have composed the commentary. Further, I first pleased my virtuous spiritual guide for a long time and underwent much hardship, whereby I saw  it a 
long time ago. [This discussion] follows from “In that way, [scholars illuminated] all of 
these treatises [not found by just some; these which I found accordingly are amazing]” [at the beginning of Haribhadra’s commentary  Clear Meaning ].

3A. Author of the treatise  
ROOT TEXT  
The Treatise of Quintessential Instructions of the Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for 
Clear Realization , composed by the Protector Venerable Maitreya, is completed.  
 
COMMENTARY 
The commen tary on the Treatise of Quintessential Instructions of the Perfection of 
Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization , composed by Acarya Haribhadra, is completed.  
 
EXPLANATION  
(i) The commentary on the Treatise of Quintessential Instructions of the Perfection of 
Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization , the stages of the path of the paramitas in 
particular, that has gone to the other side of the ocean -like tenets of oneself and others, 
composed by Acarya Haribhadra who possesses the complete instructions of 
unint errupted excellent beings beginning from Venerable Maitreya , and (ii) the Ornament of 
the Essence , an explanation of that, [composed by Gyel- tsab Dar- ma Rin -chen,] are 
completed.

190 See the outline “ 2A2B. Proving that [Haribhadra] possesses the inner condition of wisdom realizing all 
the meanings of  the treatise .”

598 3B. How it was translated by whom  
COMMENTARY  
Translated, revised, and sett led upon by the Indian Abbot Vidyakara prabha and the reviser-
translator Bandhe (Monk) Pal -tseg. Excellently settled upon by the later pandit, Glorious 
Gomi (Honorable) Chi -me, and so forth, and the translator Bhiksu Lo -den She -rab. 
 
EXPLANATION  
Translated, revised, and , through explaining and hearing,  settled upon by the Indian 
Abbot Vidyakaraprabha and the reviser -translator Bandhe (Monk) Pal -tseg. 
Translated and, through explaining and hearing,  excellently settled upon by the later 
Indian Abbot  Glorious G omi (Honorable) Chi -me and so forth, and the translator Lo-
den She -rab. 
 Furthermore, through having looked well at (i) [Haribhadra’s] Great Commentary on the 
Eight Thousand  translated by the Indian abbot Subhutisri and the Great Translator initially, 
(ii) the teaching [on that Great Commentary ] given by Lord Atisha and translated by the 
Great Translator, (iii) the teaching [on it] given by that pandit translated by Drom -ton-pa, 
and (iv) all the later new translations, you should know them by checking the m eanings 
carefully. 
 
The one path traversed by all the victors of the three times, 
The meaning asserted by the speech of the excellent regent (Gyel -tsab), 
The system of the good son of the victors, the scholar among the scholars, Has been explained with speech that is clear, unmixed, and easy to understand.  
 
That which is transmitted from one to another, Dimpamkara and so forth, who possess the complete  
Magnificent essential meanings of all the scriptures and  
The instructions of the stages of the Mahayana path, has come.  
 
Furthermore, most of the beings who have entered into this mode  
Are dominated by thoughts of gain, honor, and deceptiveness, And have strayed from the proper accomplishing of the meaning of the Mothers, 
Therefore  they are limited to mere faith in this supreme text. 
 
Saying “This commentary of this root text,”  
Some, even with just that, are inflated with haughtiness. 
Others have arranged a string of praise for themselves and abuse for others;  
Realizing just a porti on of this system, they do not see it. 
 
Hence, the entity, enumeration, sequence, and so forth of the paths  
Have come excellently from the paths of correct knowledge;  
Having rejected all contradictions, lack of relation, and repetition,  
They have been expl ained well through the kindness of the venerable gurus. 
 
Having abandoned the minds of falling into the directions of hatred and desire, And having exceptional kindness for those who are less than excellent, 
Upholders of the excellent dharma of all the vic tors; 
I pay homage to the gurus.  
The Explanation: Ornament of the Essence       The Indication of Other Summarized Meanings  
 
599  
Due to whatever effort and wholesome virtues created here,  
May the wretched beings bound in the prison of cyclic existence,  
Having cut all lassos of craving to objects, 
Enter the city of the omniscient victors.  
 
Henceforth, in all lifetimes,  
May I never be separated from this excellent path, and  
Having pleased the virtuous spiritual guides of the supreme vehicle,  
Uphold all the excellent Dharma of the victors without exception.  
 
Having heard much this system of t he perfection of wisdom, the great tradition of the 
victors, many times from the Venerable Excellent Kumaramati, Kab -shi Dzin -pa Do -je, and 
so forth, and later,  having requested again and again Je Rinpoche, the Omniscient Losang 
Dragpa Palzangpo [Lama Tson gkhapa] who is non -different from Venerable Manjusri, in 
dependence on the force of having obtained many times the complete instruction of the paramitas, through being urged by many virtuous spiritual guides, the composition of this  
Ornament of the Essence , an explanation of the root text of the  Treatise of Quintessential 
Instructions of the Perfection of Wisdom: Ornament for Clear Realization , along with its 
commentary, by Dar- ma Rin -chen, one who abides in the modes of conduct of the supreme 
vehicle, at G eden Nampar Gyelwe Ling, is completed. 
 Sarva mangalam.  
 The indication of other summarized meanings is completed.
